PN CN114180610-A
TI Preparing barium carbonate with large particle size comprises adding barium sulfide solution into first and second tower, introducing carbon dioxide, adding barium sulfide solution to fourth tower, introducing hydrogen sulfide gas generated in three towers into fourth tower, discharging material.
AU LU F
   JING Y
   LI Q
   ZENG K
   WAN Y
   LIANG Q
   LI M
AE GUIZHOU REDSTAR DEV CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2022430889
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing barium carbonate with large particle size involves (a) adding barium sulfide solution to a first tower and a second tower, introducing carbon dioxide to the first tower, and introducing the hydrogen sulfide gas generated by the second tower into the third tower, (b) adding the barium sulfide solution to the third tower, and introducing the hydrogen sulfide gas produced in the first tower into the third tower through the second tower, and (c) adding the BaS solution to the fourth tower, introducing the hydrogen Sulfide gas generated in the first three towers into the fourth tower, and discharge the material from the first tower.
   USE - Method for preparing large particle size barium carbonate is used in electronic, glass, and ceramic industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method satisfies the product particle size requirement, at the same time, four tower series process better promotes process control, keeping the tail gas (dispersed) H2S concentration stable, promoting downstream process safety the production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a large particle size barium carbonate prepared by the above-mentioned method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of four towers connected in series.
   Chemical reaction (1)
   Initially generating chemical reaction (2)
   Continuously generating chemical reaction (3)
   Positive generating pre-carbonization reaction (4)
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E34-D03D; E11-W

TI Fermentation device of organic fertilizer strain comprises fermentation tank and lower base, where side end of fermentation tank is provided with blower connecting bracket, and blower is connected with outer fixedly connected with bracket.

AE YUANBAOTIAN BEIJING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022443862

The organic fertilizer strain fermentation device comprises a fermentation tank and a lower base, the side end of the fermentation tank is provided with a blower connecting bracket, the blower is connected with the outer fixedly connected with of the bracket blower, the air outlet of the blower is fixedly connected with the three-way air inlet pipe, the inner part of the three-way air inlet pipe is rotatably connected with an air pipe, one end of the air feeding pipe close to the three-way air inlet pipe is fixedly connected with with a rotating belt wheel. The organic fertilizer strain fermenting device is provided with a blower, a three-way air inlet pipe, an air feeding pipe, an air inlet pipe, a first driving wheel, a second driving wheel and a driving motor, under the rotating action of the driving motor drives the two air feeding pipes to synchronously rotate at the same speed, so that the carbon dioxide gas can be uniformly introduced into the fermentation tank from the air inlet hole, so as to react with the organic fertilizer strain in the fermentation tank, so that the reaction of the carbon dioxide gas and the strain is more uniform, The time needed for fermentation is shorter.

PN CN114106925-A
TI Refining cod oil comprises e.g. mixing cod and water, crushing to obtain cod mixed liquid, enzymolyzingcod mixed liquid in enzymolysis liquid e.g. keratinase, papain, alkaline protease, sodium hydroxide and water, and purifying and extracting crude cod oil with supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU HUANG S
   GUO H
   ZHOU J
AE HAINAN HUAYAN COLLAGEN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 2022379357
AB    NOVELTY - Refining cod oil comprises (i) pre-treating by cleaning the cod, mixing the cod and water, and using a wall breaking machine for crushing to obtain a cod mixed liquid, (ii) performing enzymolysis by placing the cod mixed liquid into enzymolysis liquid, soaking at low temperature, where enzymolysis liquid comprises 2-4 wt.% keratinase, 1-3 wt.% papain, 5-9 wt.% alkaline protease, 10-15 wt.% sodium hydroxide and water (remaining amount), and performing enzymolysis to obtain cod mixed liquid, (iii) pressurizing by placing the cod mixed liquid after enzymolysis of the step (ii) into an ultra-high pressure container to obtain crude cod oil, (iv) purifying and extracting crude cod oil with supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain the crude cod oil product, and (v) refining the purified cod oil by using a chromatography column to obtain the finished product.
   USE - The method is useful for refining cod oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: removes the fishy odor in fish oil; and prepares fish oil with light yellow color, which reaches the first-class standard of refined fish oil. The cod oil: has high extraction rate of 79.53%, eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) content of fish oil extracted from cod fish meat as high as 29.14%, and high docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) content of 25.66%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), setting ultra-high pressure container parameter as: firstly raising the pressure to 350-400 MPa for 30-50 minutes, and then raising the second pressure to 500-530 MPa for 10-20 minutes. In the step (ii), soaking at low temperature of 5-8degrees Celsius for 5-7 hours. In the step (iv), the extraction pressure in the carbon dioxide is 15-20 MPa, the extraction temperature is 60-70degrees Celsius, the carbon dioxide flow is controlled at 80-85 l/hour, and the extraction time is 1-2 hours.Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the volume ratio of the cod mixed liquid and enzymolysis liquid is 1:5-7. In the step (v), the chromatographic column filler is silica gel. The mass ratio of the silica gel and purified cod oil is 3:4:1.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D10-A02; D10-A01; D05-C19; D05-A02C
IP C11B-001/02; C11B-001/10; C11B-003/10
PD CN114106925-A   01 Mar 2022   C11B-001/02   202246      Chinese

PN CN114047297-A
TI Method for monitoring oxygen in workshop, involves adjusting oxygen-containing alarm signal sent to workshop oxygen alarm system according to oxygen-carbon ratio difference value, so as to adjust alarm state of alarm system.
AU LI D
AE HUIZHOU KAIMEITE GASES CO LTD (HUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2022300920
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (S100) an oxygen-carbon ratio of the workshop. A ratio difference process is performed (S200) on the oxygen-to-carbon ratio and a preset ratio to obtain an oxygen-to-carbon ratio difference. The oxygen-containing alarm signal sent to the workshop oxygen alarm system is adjusted (S300) according to the oxygen-carbon ratio difference, so as to adjust the alarm state of the workshop oxygen alarm system.
   USE - Method for monitoring oxygen in workshop, used in dry ice production plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables comparing the content of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the workshop to obtain oxygen-carbon ratio, determining the ratio of the oxygen and the carbon dioxide, and determining whether the oxygen carbon ratio reaches the alarm ratio, so as to control the alarm state of the workshop oxygen alarm system, thus enabling to timely send the alarm signal when there is an asphyxia environment in an efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a workshop oxygen-containing monitoring device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the method for monitoring oxygen in workshop. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Step for obtaining oxygen-carbon ratio of workshop (S100)
   Step for performing ratio difference process on oxygen-to-carbon ratio and preset ratio (S200)
   Step for adjusting oxygen-containing alarm signal sent to workshop oxygen alarm system (S300)

PN CN113979653-A
TI Preparation method of steel slag cementitious material, involves continuously grinding for specific range of degrees centigrade to obtain steel slag gel material after introducing wet smoke gas containing carbon dioxide.
AU WANG S
   ZHANG L
   ZHANG Y
   WANG Q
   YAO S
   FANG Y
AE UNIV SHENYANG JIANZHU (USYJ-C)
GA 202223553R
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method involves crushing the raw steel slag to 0.05-4.0 mm and placing in the grinding equipment, adding liquid admixture and crystal form control agent and mixing evenly, the weight ratio of admixture to steel slag is 1-10%, the weight ratio of crystal form control agent to steel slag is 0.1-1%, introducing wet flue gas containing carbon dioxide and continuously grinding for 1-60 minutes at a temperature of 20-80?oC to obtain a steel slag cementitious material when the evenly mixed steel slag is ground to a specific surface area of not less than 200 m^2/kg. The raw materials of the steel slag are the steel slag after cooling in the steel mill and the steel slag stored for a certain period of time.
   USE - Preparation method of steel slag cementitious material used for preparation of building materials (all claimed) used in cement and steel industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare steel slag gel material, which improves the hydration activity of the slag in hydration process, and promotes the large-scale application of the steel slags in the building material field, and has important social, economic and environmental benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: (1)a steel slag cementitious material; and (2)an application of steel slag cementitious material used for preparation of building materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid admixture is polyethylene glycol, sorbitol, diethanol monoisopropanolamine, polycarboxylic acid, ethyl cellulose, polyvinyl alcohol, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, Span 20 (RTM: Sorbitan monolaurate), and Tween 20 (RTM: polyoxyethylene sorbitol ester that belongs to the polysorbate family), where the concentration of the liquid admixture is not more than 10%.The crystal form control agent is acrylic acid, maleic acid, tartaric acid, malic acid, succinic acid, citric acid, malonic acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, zinc sulfate, aluminum chloride, sodium chloride, barium chloride, amino acid-based control agents and protein-based control agents.

PT P
PN CN113809199-A
TI Preparation of laser-induced nano bismuth-three-dimensional porous graphene plasma enhanced composite photo-electrode used in e.g. photoelectric catalysis, involves dissolving bismuth ion precursor into mixed solution of N, N-dimethyl formamide and dimethyl sulfoxide.
AU UNANNOUNCED I
AE UNIV QINGDAO AGRIC (UNQA-C)
GA 202203933D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of laser-induced surface plasmon resonance (SPR)-enhanced nano-bismuth three-dimensional (3D)-porous graphene composite photoelectrode based on bismuth ion-doped polysulfone polymer film comprises dissolving bismuth ion precursor needed for preparing laser-induced nano bismuth into N,N-dimethyl formamide (DMF) and dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO); adding polysulfone polymer for continuously magnetic stirring until it is dissolved to obtain viscous solution to surface of indium tin oxide conductive glass; vacuum drying; forming bismuth ion-polysulfone polymer composite film on surface of indium tin oxide conductive glass; and using carbon dioxide infrared laser engraving machine to engrave pre-designed light electrode pattern to surface of bismuth ion-polyether sulfone composite film to prepare SPR enhanced nano-bismuth-3D-porous graphene composite photo-electrode that can widen light response range of cadmium sulfide semiconductor.
   USE - Laser-induced preparation of nano-bismuth surface plasma enhanced composite photo-electrode used in photoelectric catalysis, environment treatment and biological sensing.
   ADVANTAGE - The laser-induced SPR-enhanced nano-bismuth-3D-porous graphene plasma enhanced composite photoelectric material can be used for expanding light response range of semiconductor. The photo-thermal effect of carbon dioxide laser, local instantaneous temperature of bismuth ion-doped polysulfone polymer film is increased to close to 3000degrees Celsius.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bismuth ion precursor needed for preparing laser-induced nanometre bismuth is bismuth nitrate pentahydrate, tri-o-tolyl bismuth chloride, or triphenyl bismuth.Preferred Method: The method preferably involves adding 0.12-0.18 g bismuth ion precursor into 20-30 ml DMF and DMSO mixed solution; stirring to completely dissolve; adding 2.0-3.0 g polysulfone polymer for 3-5 times (time interval is 20- 30 minutes) into obtained solution; continuously stirring to obtain polysulfone polymer solution containing bismuth ion with certain viscosity; spin-coating bismuth-containing ion polysulfone polymer solution to surface of cleaned indium tin oxide conductive glass at 2000-3000 revolutions/minute for 80-100 seconds to form uniform bismuth ion containing polysulfone polymer solution film on surface of indium tin oxide conductive glass; obtaining bismuth-containing ion polysulfone polymer solution film modified indium tin oxide conductive glass at 75-100degrees Celsius vacuum heat for 1-2 hours; volatilizing solvent in tin indium oxide conductive glass surface to prepare bismuth ion doped polysulfone polymer film; cooling bismuth ion doped polysulfone polymer film at room temperature; putting carbon dioxide laser carving machine working platform; using carbon dioxide laser to scan bismuth ion doped polysulfone polymer film surface; directly and in situ depositing bismuth ion doped polysulfone polymer film on surface of indium tin oxide conductive glass; and synchronously converting into SPR enhanced nano-bismuth-3D-porous graphene composite nano material.

GA 2021A42874
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nitrogen-doped titanium-based aerogel catalyst comprises mixing resorcinol, formaldehyde, nitrogen source, ethanol and acid at molar ratio of 1:2-3:0.04-0.1:8-10:0.04-0.06 to obtain nitrogen doped with resorcinol-formaldehyde N-RF sol, mixing 13-15 mol ethanol and 4-6 mol deionized water to obtain solution 1, mixing 1-2 mol titanium source, 0.4-0.6 mol nitric acid and 13-15 mol to obtain solution 2, mixing solution 1 and solution 2 to obtain titanium dioxide sol, mixing N-RF sol and titanium dioxide sol to obtain N-RF/titanium dioxide sol, reacting N-RF/titanium dioxide sol at 50-70 degrees C for 6-8 hours to obtain N-RF/titanium dioxide wet gel, performing solvent replacement and carbon dioxide supercritical drying on wet gel to obtain N-RF/titanium dioxide composite aerogel, heating N-RF/titanium dioxide composite aerogel at 900-1500 degrees C in inert atmosphere for 5-10 hours to obtain nitrogen-doped titanium-based aerogel material, cooling, grinding and sieving.
   USE - The preparation of nitrogen-doped titanium-based aerogel catalyst is used for oxygen reduction reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of nitrogen-doped titanium-based aerogel electro-catalyst with high controllability and low reaction resistance.

The hyperbranched                polyester phosphate resin contains terminal                hydroxyl groups and terminal phosphate groups. The                ionic liquid contains 1 octyl 3 methylimidazole                hexafluorophosphate, 1 butanesulfonic acid 3                methylimidazole trifluoroacetate and N butyl N                methylpyrrolidine bis-(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)                imide salt. The adhesion promoting material is                prepared by dispersing each component under                supercritical carbon dioxide conditions.
   USE - Adhesion promoting material used in                water-based paint (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adhesion promoting material is applied to                water-based coatings, and can significantly improve                paint film adhesion, salt spray resistance,                abrasion resistance, hardness, artificial hand                sweat resistance and impact resistance.

   (1) a method for preparing adhesion promoting                material, which involves adding 50-60 pts. wt.                epoxy phosphate resin, 8-15 pts. wt. hyperbranched                polyester phosphate resin, 2-5 pts. wt. ionic                liquid, 18- pts. wt. graphene 22 parts, 5-10 pts.                wt. polyvinylpyrrolidone, where hyperbranched                polyester phosphate resin contains terminal                hydroxyl groups and terminal phosphate groups,                ionic liquid contains 1 octyl 3 methylimidazole                hexafluorophosphate, 1 butanesulfonic acid 3                methylimidazole trifluoroacetate and N butyl N                methylpyrrolidine bis- (Trifluoromethanesulfonyl)                imide salt;fully dispersing raw materials under the                supercritical carbon dioxide condition to prepare                the adhesion promoting material; and
   (2) a water-based paint, which comprises                adhesion promoting material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The epoxy phosphate resin                  is prepared by stirring 60 pts. wt. E44 epoxy                  resin, 5 pts. wt. ethylene glycol butyl ether, 10                  pts. wt. propylene glycol methyl ether, and 15                  pts. wt. n-butanol uniformly, adding 10 pts. wt.                  polyphosphoric acid dropwise, and controlling the                  dropping temperature at 70-80 degrees C, keeping                  warm for 4-6 hours to prepare epoxy phosphate                  resin. The hyperbranched polyester phosphate                  resin is prepared by adding 5 pts. wt. xylene, 15                  pts. wt. pentaerythritol, 50 pts. wt.                  dimethylolpropionic acid, and 0.1 pts. wt.                  stannous octoate to the reaction kettle, then                  passing in nitrogen, raise the temperature to 140                  degrees C, and stirring to react, until the acid                  value is lower than 20mgKOH/g, vacuumizing to                  remove xylene and water, naturally cooling to                  room temperature to obtain a polyhydroxy                  hyperbranched polyester compound, adding 10 pts.                  wt. ethylene glycol butyl ether, 20 pts. wt.                  propylene glycol methyl ether, 5 pts. wt.                  phosphoric acid and 0.05 pts. wt. dibutyltin                  dilaurate at 110 degrees C. The reaction is                  stirred until the acid value is lower than 20                  mgKOH/g, and it is naturally cooled to room                  temperature to obtain a hyperbranched polyester                  phosphate resin. The mass ratio of ionic liquid                  contains 1 octyl 3 methylimidazole                  hexafluorophosphate, 1 butanesulfonic acid 3                  methylimidazole trifluoroacetate and N butyl N                  methylpyrrolidine                  bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide salt is 2:5:3.                  The preparation process of the epoxy phosphate                  resin includes 60 pts. wt. E44 epoxy resin, 5                  pts. wt. ethylene glycol butyl ether, 10 pts. wt.                  propylene glycol methyl ether, and 15 pts. wt.                  n-butanol uniformly, adding 10 pts. wt.                  polyphosphoric acid dropwise, control the                  temperature at 70-80 degrees C, keeping warm for                  4-6 hours to prepare epoxy phosphate resin. The                  preparation process of the hyperbranched                  polyester phosphate resin includes adding 5 pts.                  wt. xylene, 15 pts. wt. pentaerythritol, 50 pts.                  wt. dimethylolpropionic acid, 0.1 pts. wt.                  stannous octoate to the reaction kettle and then                  introducing nitrogen gas, raising the temperature                  to 140 degrees C, stirring the reaction until the                  acid value is lower than 20mgKOH/g, applying                  vacuum to remove xylene and water, naturally                  cooling to room temperature to obtain a                  polyhydroxy hyperbranched polyester compound,                  adding 10 pts. wt. ethylene glycol butyl ether,                  20 pts. wt. propylene glycol methyl ether, 5 pts.                  wt. phosphoric acid and 0.05 pts. wt. dibutyltin                  dilaurate, stirring and reacting at 110 degrees                  C, until the acid value is lower than 20mgKOH/g,                  naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain a                  hyperbranched polyester phosphate resin.

TI Compressor with helical siphons for isothermal boiler, carbon dioxide extractor, heat engine, and dehumidifier, has pockets of gas isolated by translating bells that undergo pressure gradient of column of liquid under diopter.
AU LANDIECH P
AE LANDIECH P (LAND-Individual)
   LANDIECH P (LAND-Individual)
GA 2021358583
AB    NOVELTY - The compressor has bells which are determined by a cylindrical wall of radius, and by a central cylindrical channel of radius, and by a helical wall between the central channel and the wall. A horizontal surface inside a level line rests on the upper point of the intersection between the central channel and the helical wall. The suction bells are full of gas, in which a rotation of the compressor translates the gas downwards until the level line reaches the diopter and isolates the pockets of gas they contain. The pockets of gas isolated by the translating bells undergo the pressure gradient of the column of liquid under the diopter via the surface inside the line of level and are compressed accordingly.
   USE - Compressor with helical siphons for isothermal boiler, carbon dioxide extractor, heat engine, and dehumidifier.
   ADVANTAGE - Reduces the overall size of the device and to transmit the drive movements without any sealing device comparable to a valve seal, a piston ring or a stuffing box. Reduces the energy cost of compressing a gas, since the major portion of which is decompressed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an evaporator-condenser.

PN CN110965075-A
TI Preparing electrode used as electro chemical reduction of carbon dioxide reaction, involves putting cattail velvet into reaction kettle, adding water to reactor volume, and rinsing product with distilled water, after hydrothermal treatment.
AU ZHANG H
   YAO P
   LI X
   QIU Y
   ZHANG T
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2020289287
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing an electrode involves putting cattail velvet into the reaction kettle, adding water to 1/3-2/3 of the reactor volume, cattail velvet content is 0.05-0.10 gram per millilitre, and performing hydrothermal treatment at 160-180 degrees C for 24 hours. The product after hydrothermal treatment is first rinsed with distilled water for more than three times, and then dried for 12-24 hours. The sample is taken, after drying and added alkaline solution to activate, the concentration of the sample is 0.1-0.3 gram per millilitre, and then freeze-dried for 12-24 hours. The freeze-dried sample is calcined under high temperature ammonia gas, and then treated with acid for 5-10 hours to obtain a catalyst. The 2-5 milligram catalyst and a certain amount of 5-10 wt.% nafion solution are added to 200-500 micro liter ethanol solution, ultrasonic for 0.5-2 hours, and then apply it to the surface of glassy carbon electrode. The volume ratio of Nafion solution to ethanol solution is 1/10-1/20.
   USE - Method for preparing electrode used as electro chemical reduction of carbon dioxide reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare electrode which is simple/easy to control, cost-effective, and has excellent carbon-dioixde to prepare Faraday efficiency and stability of carbon mono oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The cattail velvet is dry cattail velvet. The alkaline solution is 4-6 mole per liter potassium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide solution. The acid is hydrochloric acid or nitric acid, and the mass concentration is 10-20 wt.%. The sample is calcined with ammonia gas at a high temperature of 800-1000 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and then immersed in acid treatment for 5-10 hours. The sample concentration in acid is 0.04-0.08 gram per millilitre.

PN WO2020061703-A1; CA3114249-A1; EP3856218-A1; US2022000158-A1; EP3856218-A4
TI Making cannabinoid-containing product e.g. beverage for human consumption, involves extracting cannabinoid and waxes from cannabis plant material with carbon dioxide, and adding emulsifier to extract to make cannabinoid containing emulsion.
AU ALSAYAR M
   ELVIRA G
AE HEXO OPERATIONS INC (HEXO-Non-standard)
   HEXO OPERATIONS INC (HEXO-Non-standard)
   HEXO OPERATIONS INC (HEXO-Non-standard)
GA 202024981T
AB    NOVELTY - Making a cannabinoid-containing product for human consumption, comprises (a) extracting a cannabinoid and waxes from Cannabis plant material with carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions to obtain an extract containing the cannabinoid and waxes, and (b) adding an emulsifier to the extract containing the cannabinoid and waxes to make a cannabinoid-containing emulsion.
   USE - The process is useful for: making cannabinoid-containing product for human consumption; and making a beverage including drinking water, dairy milk, non-dairy milk, juice, a smoothie, coffee or a caffeinated beverage, tea, herbal tea, an energy drink, a fermented beverage, non-alcoholic beer, or a cocoa beverage, preferably a non-alcoholic drink, a sparkling water, a non-alcoholic spirit or cider (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is free of a step to remove waxes; and improves the taste profile, without requiring addition of taste masking compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) an undistilled cannabinoid-containing concentrate for mixing with an emulsifier to make a cannabinoid- containing emulsion for human consumption, where the cannabinoid contains concentrate which is free of winterization ethanol;
   (2) a cannabinoid-containing concentrate for mixing with the emulsifier to make a cannabinoid-containing emulsion, and comprising plant waxes; and
   (3) an edible comprising at least 0.002 mg/ml of an emulsified cannabinoid, and having a bitterness intensity of 7 based on a quinine sulfate standard solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The step of decarboxylation is performed before or after step (a) and before step (b) to obtain an activated cannabinoid. The step (b) includes adding the emulsifier in a ratio of emulsifier:extract of 1:3-1:10. Preferred Components: The beverage includes at least 0.002 mg/ml of the cannabinoid. The emulsifier comprises a polysaccharide-based emulsifier, a protein-based emulsifier, and/or a small-molecule surfactant. The cannabinoid includes tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) and/or cannabidiol (CBD). The edible includes: less than 1 wt.%, preferably less than 0.6 wt.% emulsifier; and 0.04-0.65 wt.% emulsifier.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the cannabinoid-containing product is by oral route. No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - 1000 mg Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC)-containing Cannabis oil was mixed with 50 mg poly(ethylene glycol) monooleate with an appropriate amount of ethanol in a container to obtain an oil phase mixture. The oil phase mixture was heated at 50 degrees C until a liquid oil phase was obtained. In a separate container, 50 mg of sodium oleate were dissolved into 20 ml of deionized water to form an aqueous phase mixture. The oil phase mixture was added to the aqueous phase mixture and the combined mixture was mixed with a high shear mixer to obtain a coarse emulsion. T25 at 8000 rpm for 5 minutes can be used here. The coarse emulsion was mixed with a microfluidizer to further homogenize the emulsion and obtain the first microencapsulation composition containing THC.

PN US10427138-B1
TI Producing synthesis gas from methane involves providing a catalytic reactor containing a metal ferrite catalyst bed, and delivered one continuous gas stream containing methane into catalytic reactor.
AU SIRIWARDANE R V
AE US DEPT ENERGY (USAT-C)
GA 201982675J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing synthesis gas from methane involves providing a catalytic reactor containing a metal ferrite catalyst bed comprising MzFexOy, where 0 not less than x less than or equal to 4, z not greater than 0 and 0 not less than y less than or equal to 5, and where M is one group II alkali earth metal. The MzFexOy comprises 30 wt. % of metal ferrite oxygen carrier, and delivered one continuous gas stream containing methane comprising not greater than 1 vol.% of one continuous gas stream into catalytic reactor. The another continuous gas stream containing carbon dioxide delivered comprises not greater than 1 vol.% of the other continuous gas stream into catalytic reactor. The catalytic reactor is maintained at reaction temperature, where reaction temperature is sufficient for the reaction of methane and carbon dioxide. The continuous stream of gaseous products is generated containing hydrogen (H2) and carbon monoxide (CO) gas and withdrawn a product stream from a fuel reactor. The gaseous products comprise the product stream, and where at least not greater than 50 vol.% of the product stream consists of CO and H2.
   USE - Method for producing synthesis gas from methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce synthesis gas from methane, that offers various advantages such as mitigation of greenhouse gasses CO2 and methane and converting these gasses into valuable syngas with a H2/CO ratio 1 which may be used to produce valuable chemicals downstream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Group II alkali earth metals comprise one of magnesium (Mg), calcium (Ca), barium (Ba), strontium (Sr) and/or combinations. The inert support contains one of alumina, silica, zirconia, clay, alumina silicates, titania, monolith and/or combinations. Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature ranges from about 500-1100 degrees C. The metal ferrite catalyst further comprises an inert support, where the inert support comprises from about 5-70 wt.% of the metal ferrite oxygen carrier. The catalytic reactor containing a metal ferrite catalyst bed may be diluted with an inert material comprising one of alumina, silica, zirconia, clay, titania and/or alumina silicates. The heat for the method and one of the CO2 gas streams may be provided by combustion of a fuel or chemical looping combustion of a fuel with an oxygen carrier. The Group II metal ferrite may be in an oxidized form or a reduced form.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109775735-A; CN109775735-B
TI Preparing high-purity ultrafine magnesium            carbonate useful for battery material comprises e.g.            smelting raw material magnesium hydroxide, or magnesium            oxide, sieving, mixing first powder with water, and            removing ferromagnetic impurities.
AU YIN Q
   BI L
   XING X
   ZHAI L
   SUN M
   DI L
AE HEBEI MEISHEN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
   HEBEI MEITAI MAGNESIUM MATERIAL CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 201948288B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity ultrafine magnesium                carbonate comprises (a) smelting raw material                magnesium hydroxide, or magnesium oxide, and                sieving the first powder having particle diameter                of less than 5 mu m, (b) mixing first powder with                water, stirring to obtain suspension, and further                removing ferromagnetic impurities by grid type iron                remover to obtain first reaction liquid, (c)                passing carbon dioxide into first reaction liquid,                turning on heating, and stirring to obtain second                reaction liquid, removing the ferromagnetic                substance in second reaction liquid by grid type                iron remover again, then continuously passing                carbon dioxide, and stirring until the pH of the                solution is constant to obtain the third reaction                liquid, (d) dehydrating third reaction liquid,                drying to obtain filtrate, crushing filtrate, and                sieving second powder having particle diameter of                not greater than 10 mu m, and (e) subjecting the                second powder to removal of ferromagnetic substance                by a strong magnetic field to obtain the                product.
   USE - The high-purity ultrafine magnesium carbonate                is useful for battery material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has ability to remove only a small                amount of ferromagnetic impurities remaining in the                powder; controls content of magnetic substance in                the magnesium carbonate product; meets the                requirements for preparing battery; greatly reduces                energy consumption in the entire preparation                process; and is excellent for saving production                costs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (b), the                  mass ratio of first powder and water is                  1:12-1:25. The grid type iron separator comprises                  circulation pipe that is connected with reaction                  kettle at both ends, and magnetic mesh grille                  provided at middle portion of circulation pipe.                  The mesh grid comprises multiple strip-shaped                  housings staggered in mesh shape and having                  cavity, and electromagnet provided in cavity. The                  outer wall of the strip housing is smooth and has                  ferromagnetism. A sewage pipe is provided at a                  portion of circulation pipe on downstream side of                  mesh grid. A control valve is provided on the                  sewage pipe. Preferred Method: In the step (b),                  introducing clean carbon dioxide at the rate of                  30-50 m grams/hour, and stirring at 30-100                  degrees C, placing first powder and water in                  reaction kettle, and stirring for 1.5-3 hours to                  obtain suspension. In the step (c), circulating                  first reaction liquid through grid type separator                  at the speed of 4-10 m grams/hour, passing first                  reaction liquid through iron remover, returning                  to reaction kettle for continuously carbonizing,                  and pyrolyzing, and stopping the reaction until                  the pH of the solution is constant. The first                  reaction liquid flows into circulation pipe from                  reaction kettle, passing through the mesh grid,                  and flowing back to the reaction kettle. In the                  step (d) injecting second reaction liquid into                  plate and frame filter press for dehydration                  until the moisture content of the filter cake is                  less than 70%, and then placing filter cake in a                  rotary flash dryer to be dried to a moisture                  content of less than 2%. In the step (e),                  continuously passing second powder through strong                  magnetic field, applying ultrasonic waves or                  laser waves to second powder located in strong                  magnetic field, ans where the strong magnetic                  field comprises at least one magnetic pole for                  adsorbing ferromagnetic substances.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. AU YANG D

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a hydrocarbon reactor including a reaction shell (1) resistant to high temperature and pressure, and closed. An over-liquid substrate is formed in the reaction housing, which is divided into an upper portion into a hydrocarbon-forming reaction chamber (2) and a lower portion as a reaction product chamber (3). The liquid passing substrate (4) is provided with a liquid passage hole for allowing the reaction product to enter the reaction product chamber. A raw material introduction end (5) is arranged on the reaction housing corresponding to the hydrocarbon reaction chamber. A reaction product deriving end (6) is arranged on the reaction shell corresponding to the lower portion of the reaction product chamber. A light energy focusing layer focuses the illumination of the solar energy into the hydrocarbon reaction chamber.

PN US2019072236-A1; US10551005-B2
TI Inflatable structure for storing carbon dioxide, has outer inflatable wall separated from inflatable bladder by pressurized intermediate space containing gas different from storage gas, and providing protection from elements.
AU PTASZEK W
AE PTASZEK W (PTAS-Individual)
GA 201921763N
AB    NOVELTY - The structure has an inflatable bladder (14) containing storage gas made of flexible material impermeable to the storage. An outer inflatable wall (12) is separated from the inflatable bladder by a pressurized intermediate space (20) containing a gas different from the storage gas, and provides protection from elements. A variable speed blower (30) is operatively connected to the intermediate space to pressurize the intermediate space. A sensor and a controller are arranged responsive to pressure in the intermediate space and pressure in the bladder to maintain pressure in the intermediate space higher than the pressure in the bladder.
   USE - Inflatable structure for storing gas i.e. carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure ensures that pressure in the space between the bladder and the outer structure is greater than the pressure in the bladder to minimize gas leakage and facilitate pumping gas out of the bladder. The structure can be adapted to be installed on a ground with minimal foundation requirements, and provides for storage of large volumes of gas in different environment conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a system for storing gas
   (2) a method for storing gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross sectional view of a storage structure comprising an earth berm and a ground liner.
   Gas storage system (10)
   Outer inflatable wall (12)
   Inflatable bladder (14)
   Pressurized intermediate space (20)
   Variable speed blower (30)

TI Method for de-bottlenecking hydrogen generating plant, involves using recovered hydrogen to form additional hydrogen stream that is compressed to pressure in predetermined range and sending wholly or partly to pressure swing adsorption unit.
AU ALLIDIERES L
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE ETUDE & EXPL PROCEDES CLAUDE (AIRL-C)
GA 2019023340
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating a synthesis gas (6) by reforming from light hydrocarbons, enriching hydrogen and carbon dioxide by conversion of carbon monoxide contained in the synthesis gas with steam, purifying hydrogen and producing high pressure gas stream ultra pure hydrogen with associated production of low pressure gas residual waste whose two major constituents are carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The electrochemical hydrogen purification cell is installed on low-pressure gaseous waste gas of pressure swing adsorption (PSA) unit (12), to separate hydrogen and waste cell. The recovered hydrogen is used to form an additional hydrogen stream which is compressed to a pressure of between 8 and 25 bar and sent wholly or partly to the inlet of PSA unit to increase the production of hydrogen of plant, while maintaining the purity of hydrogen produced by PSA.
   USE - Method for de-bottlenecking hydrogen generating plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is simple, and all hydrogen present in synthesis gas is optimized, without increase in additional cost. The purification is simplified through PSA unit. The high purity of produced hydrogen is maintained.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an installation for de-bottlenecking hydrogen generating plant.

   Reactor (4)
   Synthesis gas (6)
   Condensate (10)
   PSA unit (12)

PN CN109023222-A
TI Treating automobile brake disc surface involves sanding surface of brake disc of automobile, pickling and polishing surface of brake disc of automobile then washing and drying, performing carbonitriding treatment on automobile brake disc.
AU TAO J
AE TAO J (TAOJ-Individual)
GA 2018A5126V
AB    NOVELTY - Treating automobile brake disk surface involves sanding surface of brake disk of the automobile by using 200 mesh, 600 mesh, and 1800 mesh sandpaper in sequence, pickling and polishing surface of the brake disk of the automobile then washing and drying, performing carbonitriding treatment on the automobile brake disk which is pickled and washed with water and dried. The carbonitriding treatment is performed by putting automobile brake disk which is pickled and washed and dried to be vacuumed and heating pre-oxidation is performed in furnace. The pressure in the furnace is maintained at 0.5-0.8 Pascals, the heating temperature is 300 plus minus 10 degrees C, the heating time is 1-2 hours, then the temperature is raised to 420 plus minus 10 degrees C, and the vacuuming device is started to vacuum the furnace into carbon dioxide and ammonia.
   USE - Method for treating automobile brake disk surface.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat automobile brake disk surface which greatly extends service life of brake disk of the automobile.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon dioxide flow rate is controlled at 1.1-1.2m3/hours, the flow rate of ammonia gas is controlled at 21-24m3/hours, carbon and nitrogen infiltration, infiltration time 3-8hours. The car brake disk after carbonitriding is cooled to 200 degrees C with the furnace, and then cooled to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pickling is performed by washing with sulfuric acid having a concentration of 5% by mass for 5 minutes. The cooling is gradually performed at a temperature decreasing rate of 10-15 degrees C per minute.

TI Preparing instant chopped chili clam comprises chopping chili, pre-treating clam, mixing with chopped chili, and processing clam meat.
AU GAO C
AE GAO C (GAOC-Individual)
GA 2018075983
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing instant chopped chili clam comprises e.g. chopping chili, pre-treating the clam, mixing with chopped chili, and processing clam meat, where preparation of the chopped chili comprises chopping traditional chili, pressurized sealing and allowing to stand for 5-8 days after treatment, the filled gas is high purity nitrogen or carbon dioxide, and the pressure of the pressing is 10-50 KPa, the mixing of chopped chili and clam meat comprises traditional heat pressing or normal temperature ultrahigh pressure processing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing instant chopped chili clam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: completely maintains nutrient content and fresh spicy crispy in food; prolongs the clam meat shelf life; and suitable for industrial production. The instant chopped chili clam has good mouth feel and taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing instant chopped chili clam comprises chopping chili, pre-treating the clam, mixing with chopped chili, and processing clam meat, where preparation of the chopped chili comprises chopping traditional chili, pressurized sealing and allowing to stand for 5-8 days after treatment, the filled gas is high purity nitrogen or carbon dioxide, and the pressure of the pressing is 10-50 KPa, the mixing of chopped chili and clam meat comprises traditional heat pressing or normal temperature ultrahigh pressure processing, the traditional hot pressing is at 121 degrees C for 15-20 minutes, the normal temperature pressure is 100-600 MPa, and the treating time is 10-20 minutes. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for instant chopped chili clam, prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The clam includes yellow clam, white clam or flower clams. Preferred Composition: The chopped chili comprises 500 g hot chili, 30-50 g garlic, 10-20 g ginger, 25-45 g salt, 5-15 g sugar, 10-25 ml spirit and lactic acid bacteria. Preferred Method: The mass ratio of the chopped chili and the clam meat is 1:3-1:1. The placing is placed under the 15-30 degrees C temperature condition. The room temperature of ultrahigh pressure treatment is less than 30 degrees C. The method specifically comprises (i) chopping traditional fresh chili, subjecting to pressurized treated by introducing gas into a sealed container for 5-8 days, (ii) frying clam meat, (iii) mixing step (i) chopped chili and step (ii) treated clam meat, placing into bottle, sterilizing at 121 degrees C for 15-20 minutes to obtain chopped chili, or mixing the step (i) chopped chili and step (ii) processed clam meat, placing into aluminum foil bag, performing normal-temperature and ultra-high pressure processing to obtain the finished product, where pressurizing pressure is 100-600 MPa, and the treatment time is 10-20 minutes. The method for pre-treating clam meat comprises passing high-temperature steam through the water washed clam, using razor separating clam skin, removing skin, cutting, placing meat into the drum, soaking the drum in razor's soup, rotating the drum, raising the drum, centrifugal filtering the soup, rotating the filtrate submerged drum and drum, filtering, repeating the operation until the clam meat is without sand. The preparation of the chopped chili comprises cleaning chili, garlic and ginger, draining water, spreading and drying, crushing the chili, garlic and ginger in crusher, where chili crushing to 0.5-1 cm, crushing garlic and ginger to 0.5 cm, adding salt and sugar into the crushed chili, garlic and ginger mixture, stirring uniformly, adding white spirit and lactic acid bacteria into the chopped chili, mixing uniformly and stirring, processing by pressurized gas, sealing and storing under 15-30 degrees C.

PT P
PN KR2021092350-A; KR2347742-B1
TI Composition useful for e.g. preventing acne and alleviating inflammation, comprises wheat germ extract obtained by solvent extraction method and natural extract including pine needles extract, husk extract and green tea extract.
AU KANG Y S
   KANG J S
   SUH J
AE EUGNE BIOTECH CO LTD (EUGN-Non-standard)
GA 202187475J
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises: wheat germ extract, obtained by solvent extraction method, carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method, hot water extraction method, steam distillation method, microwave extraction method and/or percolation extraction method; and a natural extract including pine needles extract, husk extract and/or green tea extract.
   USE - The composition is useful for improving skin by preventing skin diseases including acne, alleviating inflammation, suppressing sebum production, suppressing oil and improving skin moisturizing properties.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is economical to prepare and is suitable for many people suffering from acne to have access to variety of products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises: 0.1-5 wt.% wheat germ extract; 0.1-10 wt.%, preferably 0.1-1 wt.% natural extract; and additives including essential oils, emulsifiers, thickeners, fragrances, preservatives, solvents, fillers, humectants, vitamins and/or dyes. Preferred Components: The composition is prepared into solutions, gels, emulsions, suspensions, microemulsions, microcapsules, creams, skins, lotions, powders, ointments, sprays and cone sticks.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Antiseborrheic; Dermatological. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Wheat germ was ground. 2.5 l secondary distilled water was added to 100 g milled wheat germ and stirred for 1 hour. 1 l chloroform solution was added to the solution, stirred again for 30 minutes and centrifuged. The solvent layer was recovered through separation. The chloroform layer was removed and the remaining filtrate layer was again subjected to chloroform extraction in the same manner three times. The chloroform layers were collected, washed with distilled water and concentrated at 40 degrees C using a vacuum evaporator. 6.2 g concentrated material was redissolved in chloroform, and then filtered through 0.45 mu m polytetrafluoroethylene filter to obtain wheat germ extract. Dried pine needles, green tea, and husk were mixed with ethanol in a weight ratio of 1:10, respectively, and then heated at 30 degrees C for 24 hours, then immersed, filtered and the filtrate was concentrated under reduced pressure. The pine needle extract, green tea extract and husk extract were mixed to obtain a mixture. The wheat germ extract and the mixture were mixed in a weight ratio of 1:1 to obtain the composition.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. TI Preparation of sheet metal involves using metal            material base and alloy powder reinforcing material,            cold spraying to deposit alloy powder onto base,            forming deposited layer, and performing rapid cooling            and friction stir processing.
AU WANG K
   WANG J
   QIAO K
   WANG W
   ZHANG S
   XIE H
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
GA 202176031V
AB    NOVELTY - A sheet metal is prepared by using metal                material as base and alloy powder as reinforcing                material, depositing alloy powder on the base                surface by cold spraying to form a deposited layer,                and performing rapid cooling and friction stir                processing on the deposited layer by spraying                coolant including liquid carbon dioxide or liquid                pentane.
   USE - The method for preparing sheet metal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare metal plate in a                simple and eco-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the sheet                metal preparation device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The base is magnesium                  plate or aluminum plate metal material with a                  purity of not less than 99%. The alloy powder                  includes aluminum, magnesium, zinc and/or silicon                  powder with particle size of 10-100 mu m.                  Preferred Method: The sheet metal is prepared by                  determining alloy powder ratio according to the                  alloying design, selecting metal powders with                  particle size of 10-100 mu m, mixing to obtain                  the alloy powder, cold spraying alloy powder onto                  base surface using nitrogen powder feeding at                  preheating temperature of 500-700 degrees C,                  preferably 600 degrees C at powder feeding rate                  of 25-35 g/minute, preferably 25 g/minute,                  spraying distance of 20-30 mm, preferably 25 mm,                  and cooling rate of 40-50 K/seconds, preferably                  40 K/s to form deposited layer with thickness of                  1-4 mm, preferably 4 mm, and performing rapid                  cooling and friction stir processing on the                  deposited layer by spraying coolant including                  liquid carbon dioxide or liquid pentane at                  injection distance of 2-4 mm, stirring head speed                  of 300-1500 revolutions/minute (rpm), preferably                  1200 rpm and stirring head forward speed of                  20-200 mm/minute, preferably 25 mm/minute.

AU RABIE A

   CHINTA S
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   RABIE A (RABI-Individual)

   ALLADA R (ALLA-Individual)
   PANT A (PANT-Individual)
   NARAYANASWAMY R (NARA-Individual)
   CHINTA S (CHIN-Individual)
GA 202067183W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen enriched syngas involves reacting catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reaction, a CPO reactant mixture in a CPO reactor to produce the hydrogen enriched syngas, where the CPO reactant mixture comprises hydrocarbons and oxygen. The CPO reactor comprises a CPO catalyst. The hydrogen enriched syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water, and unreacted hydrocarbons. The hydrogen enriched syngas is hydrogen to carbon monoxide (H /CO) in ratio of greater than about 2.0.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen enriched syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce hydrogen enriched syngas that has endothermicity, and increases the hydrogen content of the syngas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbons comprise methane, natural gas, natural gas liquids, associated gas, well head gas, enriched gas, paraffins, shale gas, shale liquids, fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) off gas, refinery process gases, stack gases, fuel gas from fuel gas header, or combinations. The operational parameter comprises a CPO pressure of less than about 30 barg. The operational parameter comprises a C/O molar ratio in the CPO reactant mixture of equal to or greater than about 2:1. The operational parameter further comprises a CPO pressure of less than about 30 barg and/or a CPO effluent temperature of equal to or greater than about 750 degrees C. Preferred Process: The portion of the carbon is reacted with water to produce carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The process further involves recovering a WGS reactor effluent from the WGS reactor, where the WGS reactor effluent comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water, and unreacted hydrocarbons, and where the WGS reactor effluent is characterized by an M ratio of the WGS reactor effluent, and removing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the WGS reactor effluent to yield the hydrogen enriched syngas, and where the hydrogen enriched syngas is characterized by an M ratio that is greater than the M ratio of the WGS reactor effluent. The portion of the carbon monoxide in the CPO reactor undergoes a water-gas shift (WGS) reaction, and increases the amount of hydrogen in the hydrogen enriched syngas. The hydrogen enriched syngas is compressed in a single compression stage to introducing at portion of the hydrogen enriched syngas to the methanol reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN110937612-A; CN110937612-B
TI Preparing high-quality heavy soda ash comprises            mixing crude sodium bicarbonate and water,            steam-stripping, evaporating and concentrating            stripping completion liquid, and separating and drying            sodium carbonate monohydrate crystals.
AU HU B
   FENG Q
   HU S
   ZHANG M
   CHEN X
   LI L
AE CHINA TIANCHEN ENG CORP (CNCE-C)
   CHINA TIANCHEN ENG CORP (CNCE-C)
GA 2020275542
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-quality heavy soda ash                comprises (i) mixing crude sodium bicarbonate and                water to obtain sodium bicarbonate solution or                slurry, and steam-stripping sodium bicarbonate                solution or slurry in steam stripping tower for                decomposing sodium carbonate, carbon dioxide and                water to obtaining stripping completion liquid;                (ii) evaporating and concentrating stripping                completion liquid to obtain sodium carbonate                monohydrate crystals; and (iii) separating and                drying sodium carbonate monohydrate crystals.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                high-quality heavy soda ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical; and improves the                product quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The crude sodium                  bicarbonate is prepared by (a) evaporating                  high-salt alkali halide into crystallization                  point of sodium chloride to obtain the                  concentrated product; (b) evaporating                  concentrated product, precipitating sodium                  chloride and separating sodium chloride product                  and alkali halide; (c) filtering and preheating                  alkali halide, reacting with carbon dioxide for                  converting sodium carbonate into sodium                  bicarbonate and precipitating sodium bicarbonate                  crystals by cooling; and (d) separating the                  product obtained in step (c) by using separating                  device to obtain sodium bicarbonate filter cake.                  The crude sodium bicarbonate is crude heavy                  alkali obtained by carbonization and filtration                  in the process of synthesizing sodium bicarbonate                  by double alkali method or the ammonia-alkali                  method. In the step (i), the sodium bicarbonate                  solution or slurry is prepared by produced steam                  condensate by vaporization on top of stripping                  tower exhaust gas at 69-160 degrees C under                  stripping pressure of 0.88-6 barA and sodium                  carbonate monohydrate crystallizer, where the                  solid content of sodium bicarbonate slurry does                  not exceed 35 wt.%, and neutralizing the                  stripping completion liquid by using caustic                  soda. In the step (ii), the temperature of sodium                  carbonate monohydrate crystallizer is 35-109                  degrees C and the sodium carbonate monohydrate                  crystallizer continuously discharges the mother                  liquor. In the step (b), the high-salt alkali                  halide is halide collected from underground                  natural alkali ore or directly drawn from                  underground, where the high-salt alkali halide                  sodium chloride salt is greater than 10 wt.% and                  total alkalinity is lesser than 4 wt.%. In the                  step (c), the carbon dioxide required for                  carbonization reaction partially comes from                  stripping tower, where the carbonization reaction                  carried out in carbonization reactor. The                  carbonization reactor is single carbonization                  reactor or multi-stage series reactor and the                  carbonized halide and alkali halide exchanges                  heat for cooling down. In the step (d), the                  thickener or hydrocyclone is used for                  concentrating the halide containing sodium                  bicarbonate crystals before separation.

PT P
PN CN110724047-A; CN110724047-B
TI Preparing aromatic carboxylic acid compound            comprises e.g. heating mixture containing silane            compound, copper catalyst and organic solvent 1, and            reacting with organic solvent 2, aromatic halide,            palladium catalyst and alkali.
AU LI C
   LU S
   PARIDARA K
   WANG M
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2020105524
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aromatic carboxylic acid compound                comprises (i) heating mixture containing silane                compound, copper catalyst and organic solvent 1 in                carbon dioxide-containing atmosphere to obtain                system I, and (ii) adding organic solvent 2, raw                material containing aromatic halide, palladium                catalyst and alkali into the system I, heating,                reacting, and separating to obtain final                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aromatic                carboxylic acid compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes low cost, stable, simple                and easy available raw materials, and commonly                available and stable catalysts; and has mild                reaction conditions, simple post-treatment and high                product yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silane compound in                  step (i) is at least one of compounds containing                  silicon-hydrogen bonds, preferably poly(methyl                  hydrogen siloxane), triethoxysilane and/or                  triphenoxysilane. The organic solvent 1 is                  1,4-dioxane, tetrahydrofuran and/or cyclopentyl                  methyl ether. The copper catalyst is at least one                  of complex formed by copper salt and                  bisphosphonate ligand compound 3. The copper salt                  is inorganic salt of copper, carboxylic acid salt                  of copper, alkoxide of copper and/or ketone salt                  of copper, preferably copper acetate, copper(II)                  acetylacetonate and/or copper(II) methoxide. The                  bisphosphonate ligand compound 3 is at least one                  substituted 1,2-diphosphinobenzene compound of                  formula (A), preferably 1,2-bis                  (diphenylphosphino) benzene,                  1,2-bis(bis(3,5-di-tert-butylphenyl)phosphino)benzene,                  1,2-bis(dicyclohexylphosphino)benzene and/or                  1,2-bis(isopropylphosphino)benzene. The molar                  ratio between copper salt and bisphosphonate                  ligand compound 3 is 1:1-6. The molar ratio                  between copper catalyst, carbon dioxide and                  silane compound in step (i) is                  1:100-400000:50-10000. The number of moles of the                  copper catalyst is calculated based on the number                  of moles of the copper element contained in the                  copper catalyst. The number of moles of carbon                  dioxide is calculated by the number of moles of                  carbon dioxide itself. The number of moles of the                  silane compound is calculated based on the number                  of moles of the silane compound itself. In step                  (i), the heating temperature is 50-70 degrees C,                  and the heating time is 0.5-24 hours. In step                  (ii), the organic solvent 2 is toluene, xylene                  and/or benzene, the aromatic halide is                  halogenated aromatic compound and/or halogenated                  heteroaromatic compound. The aromatic halide is                  at least one substituted aryl compound of formula                  (II), substituted alkene compound of formula                  (III) and substituted alkene compound (IV),                  preferably bromobenzene, 2-bromonaphthalene,                  4-methoxybromobenzene, 4-nitrobromobenzene,                  3-bromothiophene, 3,5-dimethoxybromobenzene,                  3-iodobromo, 3-nitrobromobenzene,                  3-fluoro-4-methylbromobenzene,                  4-bromobenzaldehyde, 4-formylchlorobenzene,                  4-chlorobromobenzene, 4-bromoacetophenone,                  4-bromo-1,2-methylenedioxybenzene,                  N-boc-5-bromoindole, 3-bromoquinoline,                  2-chloroquinoline, 6-bromoquinoline,                  3-bromoiodobenzene, 4-chlorobromobenzene,                  p-dichlorobenzene, 1,4-dibromobenzene,                  1,3-dibromotoluene, 4,4'-dibromobiphenyl,                  2,6-dibromonaphthalene, 1,3,5-tribromobenzene,                  2,6-dichloropyridine, 2-bromovinylbenzene,                  1-bromovinylbenzene and/or 2-bromoindene. The                  palladium catalyst in step (ii) is palladium salt                  and/or bisphosphonate ligand compound 4. The                  palladium salt is inorganic salt of palladium,                  carboxylate of palladium, alkoxide of palladium                  and/or ketone salt of palladium, preferably                  palladium acetate, palladium chloride and/or                  palladium(II) acetylacetonate. The bisphosphonate                  ligand compound 4 is at least one compounds                  containing bis-phenyl bisphosphonate moieties of                  formula (G), preferably                  4,5-bis(diphenylphosphino)-9,9-dimethylxanthene,                  1,1'-bis(diphenylphosphino)ferrocene and/or                  1,3-Bis(diphenylphosphino)propane. The molar                  ratio between palladium salt and bisphosphonate                  ligand compound 4 is 1:1-5. The alkali in step                  (ii) is at least one organic amine preferably                  triethylamine, diisopropylethylamine and/or                  tributylamine. The molar ratio between palladium                  catalyst, alkali and aromatic halide is                  1:1-4:10-1000. The number of moles of the                  palladium catalyst is calculated based on the                  number of moles of the palladium element                  contained in the palladium catalyst. The number                  of moles of the alkali is calculated based on the                  number of moles of the alkali itself. The number                  of moles of the aromatic halide is calculated                  based on the number of moles of the aromatic                  halide itself. In step (ii): the reaction                  temperature is 60-150 degrees C, and the reaction                  time is 3-72 hours. The separation in step (ii)                  comprises adding a alkali aqueous solution into                  the reaction system to separate the reaction                  system, taking the aqueous layer, adding acidic                  aqueous solution for neutralization, extracting                  the organic layer with organic solvent 5 to                  obtain final product. The alkaline aqueous                  solution is sodium hydroxide solution, potassium                  hydroxide solution, sodium carbonate solution,                  potassium carbonate solution and/or sodium                  bicarbonate solution. The acidic aqueous solution                  is hydrochloric acid solution and/or citric acid                  solution. The organic solvent 5 is ethyl acetate,                  dichloromethane, toluene and/or methyl tert-butyl                  ether.R1-R4=2-18C alkyl or aryl;A1, A2, A3=optionally substituted aryl or                  optionally substituted heteroaryl;X1, X1=Cl, Br or I;A2=optionally substituted aryl or optionally                  substituted heteroaryl;R5, R6, R7, R8=H, or 1-3C alkyl; andn, l, q=0, 1, 2, 3, 4 or 5.

TI Preparing sparkling rice wine involves cooking rice into rice, cooling rice rice to first temperature, and then adding rice koji to rice rice to obtain rice rice containing rice koji, and fermenting rice rice containing rice koji.
AU WENG L
AE SHANGHAI JINGCONG LIQUOR IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201981220M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sparkling rice wine involves cooking rice into rice, cooling rice rice to the first temperature, and then adding rice koji to rice rice to obtain rice rice containing rice koji, fermenting the rice rice containing rice koji to obtain a liquor brewed from rice and separating the liquor, and then sterilizing the liquor, and adding carbon dioxide gas to obtain the sparkling rice wine.
   USE - Method for preparing sparkling rice wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare sparkling rice wine that is clear, and in cost effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The method further involves immersing the rice for a predetermined period of time according to the weather temperature. The predetermined time period is 5-15 days. The fermenting the rice rice containing rice koji involves first fermenting the rice rice containing rice koji in a first open container for a first predetermined period of time, fermenting the rice containing rice rice in the first open container in the second open container for a second predetermined period of time, where volume of the first open container is greater than the volume of the second open container. The liquor is separated liquor by pressing and heating the liquor to 80-100 degrees C and holding for 10-30 minutes. Preferred Components: The raw material for preparing the sparkling rice wine comprises rice, water, rice koji and carbon dioxide. The rice comprises one or more of glutinous rice, glutinous rice, glutinous rice, or black rice.

TI Producing iron-metal-organic skeleton, comprises dissolving organic linker having amine group in solvent to form organic linker solution, adding glycidyl compound into organic linker solution, and adding iron metal precursor.
AU NOH D G
   ROH D K
AE KOREA INST CERAMIC ENG & TECHNOLOGY (KOCE-C)
GA 201962425Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing iron-metal-organic skeleton, comprises (a) dissolving organic linker having amine group in solvent to form organic linker solution, (b) adding glycidyl compound into the organic linker solution containing organic linker, and (c) adding iron metal precursor into the organic linker solution and reacting.
   USE - The method is useful for producing iron-metal-organic skeleton.
   ADVANTAGE - The iron-metal-organic skeleton has excellent separation/adsorption performance of carbon dioxide through amine functionalization and catalytic properties for specific molecules. The method controls size of ferrous metal-organic skeleton and is simple.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron metal precursor includes tetrachloroferrite, ferric chloride, ferric chloride of formula ((FeCl3.nH2O) (I), ferric citrate, citric acid ferric hydrate of formula (FeC6H5O7.nH2O) (II), ferrous sulfate heptahydrate, iron oxalate dehydrate, iron acetylacetonate, ferric hydroxide, ferric nitrate and/or iron acetate.n = 1-12.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises reacting organic linker and glycidyl-based compound while stirring at 40-90 degrees C. The reaction is carried out using sonication at 60-120 degrees C. The method comprises controlling the molar ratio of the glycidyl-based compound and organic linker to control the size of the ferrous metal-organic skeleton. Preferred Components: The organic linker having amine group includes 2-aminoterephthalic acid, 4-aminophthalic acid, 4-aminoisophthalic acid, and/or 5-aminoisophthalic acid. The solvent includes methanol, ethanol, butanol, dimethylformamide, diethylformamide, dimethylsulfoxide, deionized water, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone and/or dimethylacetamide. The content of the organic linker contained in the organic linker solution is 0.1-2 wt.%. The glycidyl-based compound includes polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, poly(propylene glycol)diglycidyl ether and/or polyethylene glycol-epoxide. The molar ratio of glycidyl compound and organic linker is 0.03-1:1. The molar ratio of organic linker and iron metal precursor is 0.66-1.5:1.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   USE - Manufacturing methanol from organic waste.

AE SEO GYUNG IND CO LTD (SEOG-Non-standard)
GA 201925650R
AB    NOVELTY - Calcium carbonate and iron oxide production involves crushing molten steelmaking slag by quenching molten steelmaking slag into water to obtain a preformed and nonmetal slag, removing the whisker, and then pulverizing the non-metallic slag for blowing carbon dioxide-containing gas into mixture of the non-metallic slag and water to prepare calcium-eluting solution. The precipitated calcium carbonate and iron oxide are separated from the calcium-eluting solution.
   USE - Method for producing calcium carbonate and iron oxide from steel making slag.

The carbon dioxide-containing gas comprises carbon dioxide or a carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gas. The non-metallic slag is added to calcium solvate selected from saccharide-based materials, polyhydric alcohols and their mixtures. The non-metallic slag and the calcium-leaching solution preparation are pulverized.

PN CN109251892-A
TI Preparing HANK cell culture supernatant useful for inhibiting formation of skin melanin particles, comprises e.g. collecting autologous or allogeneic healthy human anticoagulated peripheral blood or umbilical cord blood, and centrifuging.
AU ZHANG M
AE SHENZHEN HANK BIOLOGICAL ENG CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201910424N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing HANK cell culture supernatant comprises (i) collecting autologous or allogeneic healthy human anticoagulated peripheral blood, or umbilical cord blood, (ii) separating mononuclear cells with lymphocyte separation solution, (iii) suspending the obtained monocytes in NK cell serum-free medium, adding IL-2 and membrane chimeric cytokines, culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 10-16 days, centrifuging the HANK cells and tsupernatants at room temperature, collecting separately the HANK cells and culture supernatant.
   USE - The supernatant is useful for inhibiting formation of skin melanin particles (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The serum-free medium is X-Vivo15 serum-free medium. The cultured HANK cells are greater than or equal to 4x 1010 per liter. The cultured CD56+CD3-NK cells have purity of greater than or equal to 80%. The cultured HANK cells having killing rate of K80 target cells of greater than or equal to 80% and target ratio of 10:1. The HANK cell culture supernatant comprises auxiliary agent. The preparation is in the form of water agent and lyophilized powder. Preferred Method: The water agent is prepared by (i) storing the supernatant of HANK cell culture in the refrigerator below -20 degrees C for less than half year in the refrigerator at -80 degrees C for more than one year, (ii) thawing, centrifuging at 4 degrees C at speed of 4000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 20 minutes, further removing particulate matter from the supernatant, as well as flocs formed during storage and freeze-thaw, (iii) sterilizing by 0.2 mu m filter membrane, (iv) packing in appropriate containers according to different application needs, (v) storing in the refrigerator below -20 degrees C for less than half a year, and then storing in a refrigerator at 80 degrees C for more than one year, and(vi) thawing, storing in refrigerator at 4 degrees C and use up within one week, otherwise it will be invalid. The lyophilized powder is prepared by (i) taking HANK cell culture supernatant, centrifuging at speed of 4000 rpm at 4 degrees C for 20 minutes, and collecting the supernatant, (ii) adding 5-15 wt.% mannitol as auxiliary agent, (iii) sterilizing by 0.2 mu m filter membrane, (iv) dispensing in glass bottles as large packaging material or in a vial, dissolving in a solvent, (v) pre-freezing at -20 degrees C under sterile conditions until the supernatant solidifies, evacuating the solidified supernatant, subliming and drying to remove ice crystals, and sealing the stopper, (vi) storing the lyophilized powder at -20 degrees C.

PN CN107903209-A; CN107903209-B
TI Preparing 2-amino-5-fluoropyridine-3-methyl formate comprises e.g. reacting N- tert-butyloxycarbonyl-2-amino-5-fluoropyridine as raw material with butyl lithium and then carrying out deprotonation of N,N,N'-trimethylethylenediamine.
AU GAO F
   CENG S
   JIANG J
   ZHANG H
   JIANG D
   ZENG S
   ZHANG X
AE SHANGHAI JINLU PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   NINGXIA ZHONGTONG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2018300886
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2-amino-5-fluoropyridine-3-methyl formate comprises reacting N-tert-butyloxycarbonyl-2-amino-5-fluoropyridine as raw material with butyl lithium, carrying out deprotonation of N,N,N'-trimethylethylenediamine, passing carbon dioxide gas to obtain N-tert-butyloxycarbonyl -2-amino-5-fluoropyridine-3-carboxylic acid and then esterifying in presence of thionyl chloride or phosphorus oxychloride to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 2-amino-5-fluoropyridine-3-methyl formate.
   ADVANTAGE - The 2-amino-5-fluoropyridine-3-methyl formate: has simple preparation process and high reaction selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) mixing organic solvent I, N-tert-butyloxycarbonyl-2-amino-5-fluoropyridine and N,N,N'-trimethylethylenediamine, reacting with n-butyllithium at -78 degrees C to -40 degrees C, carrying out proton exchange, passing carbon dioxide gas, quenching, recrystallizing from a mixed solvent to obtain N-tert-butyloxycarbonyl-2-amino-5-fluoropyridine-3-carboxylic acid, and (ii) adding product obtained in step (i) into methanol, adding thionyl chloride or phosphorus oxychloride, refluxing reaction, cooling, concentrating the solvent, adding ethyl acetate and basic aqueous solution, layering, organic layer, evaporating to obtain crude product, adding organic solvent II and then recrystallizing to obtain final product. Preferred Components: In step (i), organic solvent I is tetrahydrofuran, 2-methyltetrahydrofuran, cyclopentyl methyl ether or toluene. The molar ratio between N-tert-butyloxycarbonyl-2-amino-5-fluoropyridine, N,N,N'-trimethylethylenediamine and n-butyllithium is 1:1-1:1.2:2-2.2. The recrystallization mixed solvent is methanol or ethanol and ether, triisopropyl ether or methyl tert-butyl ether. In step (ii), molar ratio between N-tert-butyloxycarbonyl-2-amino-5-fluoropyridine-3-carboxylic acid, thionyl chloride and phosphorus oxychloride is 1:1-2. The alkaline aqueous solution is sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate or potassium bicarbonate. The beating organic solvent II is n-heptane or n-hexane; and recrystallized organic solvent II is methanol/water mixed solvent.
