TI Atmospheric nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide            isotope sampling and analysis device comprises sampling            needle and needle-containing helium gas tube matched            and inserted to sealed sample bottle and purge source            provided at insertion position.
AU SUN W
   ZHANG Y
   YU H
   WANG Y
   HE J
   WU X
AE COLD & ARID REGIONS ENVIRONMENTAL &              ENG (CAHH-C)
   NORTHWEST ECO-ENVIRONMENT & RESOURCES (NWEC-Non-standard)
GA 2020441525
AB    NOVELTY - Atmospheric nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide                isotope sampling and analysis device comprises                sampling needle and needle-containing helium gas                tube matched and inserted into sealed sample                bottle. A purge source is provided at insertion                position. The sampling needle is connected to A                port of adsorption trap via pipeline. The port of                the first switching valve is connected to second                purge tube, needle-loaded helium gas tube, third                purge tube, first vent tube, second vent tube,                fifth purge tube, first connection hole of second                conversion valve, A port of liquid nitrogen cold                trap, inlet of Plot-Q chromatography column, fourth                purge tube, B port of liquid nitrogen cold trap and                B port of adsorption trap. The A port of liquid                nitrogen cold trap is connected to inlet of Plot-Q                chromatography column. The fourth purge tube is                connected to B port of liquid nitrogen cold trap                and the end of hydrophobic trap connected with                sample gas-open splitter via pipeline.
   USE - Used as an atmospheric nitrogen, oxygen,                carbon dioxide isotope sampling and analysis                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device avoids influence of the test                environment on the analysis results; achieves                simultaneous detection of three gas stable isotopes                in one sampling; improves accuracy of data and                detection efficiency; has simple and convenient                one-key operation of the entire device testing                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                sampling of atmospheric nitrogen, oxygen and carbon                dioxide isotope sampling and analysis device                comprising (a) preparing sampling by placing first                switchover valve in inject state, opening purge                source to purge the sample bottle sampling port to                maintain the helium environment of the sample                bottle before needle insertion, simultaneously                introducing helium from second purge tube,                discharging by the sampling needle after passing                via adsorption trap, maintaining smoothness of                sampling needle and connected pipeline, introducing                the helium gas from third purge tube and                discharging through needle-carrying helium gas tube                to maintain the needle-carrying helium gas tube and                its connecting pipeline clean, (b) needle sampling                by switching first switching valve to the load                state, placing the second switching valve in the                load state, placing sampling needle and                needle-carrying helium gas tube in sample bottle                and the needle, turning off purge source, filling                the second purge tube with helium gas, conveying                needle-carrying helium gas tube into the sample                bottle under the pressure of helium, where sample                gas follows helium flow from the sampling needle                via adsorption trap and conveying liquid nitrogen                cold trap, storing carbon monoxide in the sample                gas composition in frozen condition in liquid                nitrogen cold trap, conveying other gases into                quantitative loop, discharging the excess gas from                the third vent tube after the metering ring obtains                the rated amount of gas composition, (c) separating                nitrogen and oxygen by switching second switching                valve to inject state, introducing helium gas from                the seventh purge tube to enter the dosing loop to                push the gas in the dosing loop, conveying into the                molecular sieve column for separation, drying                through the hydrophobic trap, flowing into the open                splitter of the sample gas, sequentially flowing                separated oxygen and nitrogen out of the open                splitter to complete the separation of oxygen and                nitrogen, (d) separating carbon dioxide isotope                separation by completing detection of nitrogen and                oxygen isotopes After the completion of separation                of oxygen and nitrogen and switching first                switching valve to inject state, raising the liquid                nitrogen cold trap and introducing helium from the                fourth purge tube, conveying liquid nitrogen cold                trap and the carbon dioxide gas in the liquid                nitrogen cold trap under the helium flow enters the                Plot-Q chromatography column to be separated,                conveying carbon dioxide into an open splitter of                the sample gas through the water trap, and flowing                out of the open splitter and (e) turning on purge                source, pulling out sampling needle and the                needle-carrying helium gas tube and maintaining                flow of helium carrier gas and its communication                pipeline in all purge tubes.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the atmospheric nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide                isotope sampling and analysis device.

AE CHONGQING INST GEOLOGY & MINERAL RESOURC (CNMO-Non-standard)
GA 201958356Q
AB    NOVELTY - A shale organic carbon determining method involves grinding the shale to be tested, placing the grounded shale in a porcelain boat that has been burning constant weight, adding an excess hydrochloric acid solution to the grounded shale until there are no more bubbles in the reaction and placing the porcelain boat on a hot plate to evaporate the solution. The reaction vessel of the oxidation apparatus is heated at a high temperature and oxygen is continuously supplied, where carbon dioxide is not detected in the exhaust gas and the treated sample is placed in the reaction vessel. The motor of the apparatus is started to rotated the stirring shaft under the action of the magnet and drived the stirring blade to stir the sample to be tested. The carbon dioxide generated in the reactor is discharged and stirring step is repeated until no carbon dioxide is discharged from the reactor and the total amount of carbon dioxide is detected to obtain the total organic carbon content in the shale.
   USE - Method for determining the shale organic carbon by using oxidation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables using oxidation device that the contact area and reaction area of the sample to be tested are increased, the reaction efficiency is improved, the reaction time is shortened and the organic carbon is more completely oxidized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A shale organic carbon determining method involves girnding the shale to be tested to a particle size of 80-200 mesh, placing the grounded shale in a porcelain boat that has been burning constant weight, adding an excess hydrochloric acid solution to the grounded shale until there are no more bubbles in the reaction and placing the porcelain boat on a hot plate of 100-120 degrees C to evaporate the solution. The reaction vessel of the oxidation apparatus is heated at a high temperature and oxygen is continuously supplied, where carbon dioxide is not detected in the exhaust gas and the treated sample is placed in the reaction vessel. The motor of the apparatus is started to rotated the stirring shaft under the action of the magnet and drived the stirring blade to stir the sample to be tested. The carbon dioxide generated in the reactor is discharged and stirring step is repeated until no carbon dioxide is discharged from the reactor and the total amount of carbon dioxide is detected to obtain the total organic carbon content in the shale. The oxidizing device comprises a closed reaction vessel (1) and a stirring mechanism (2) disposed in the reaction vessel and the reaction vessel (1) is sealed with an openable feed gate and is used for accessing oxygen aeration port, where the stirring mechanism includes a stirring shaft (3) rotatably disposed vertically in the reaction kettle, and an upper end of the stirring shaft (3) is mounted with a first magnet (4) along the stirring shaft radially extending, a strip-shaped magnetic strip portion (5) is formed on the stirring shaft with a stirring blade (6) for agitating the sample to be detected. The top of the reaction kettle is further provided with a bracket (7) on which a motor (8) disposed vertically downward is mounted and an output shaft of the motor is disposed coaxially with the stirring shaft, a second magnet (9) is fixedly mounted below the output shaft, the second magnet (9) is projected in the vertical direction with the first magnet (4) and the first magnet is within the magnetic field of the second magnet.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of an oxidizing device.
   Reaction vessel (1)
   Stirring mechanism (2)
   Stirring shaft (3)
   First magnet (4)
   Strip-shaped magnetic strip portion (5)
   Stirring blade (6)
   Bracket (7)
   Motor (8)
   Second magnet (9)

TI Performing cyclical reaction using calcium            carbonate decomposition to form biocement, involves            decomposing calcium carbonate forming calcium chloride            and ammonia, reacting ammonia with carbon dioxide to            form urea, followed by form biocement.
AU SMITH K J
   DOSIER J M
   DOSIER G K
AE BIOMASON INC (BIOM-Non-standard)
GA 2021E8214G
AB    NOVELTY - Performing a cyclical reaction using calcium                carbonate decomposition to form a biocement,                involves decomposing calcium carbonate forming                calcium chloride and ammonia. The ammonia is                reacted with carbon dioxide in a process to form                urea. The urea is reacted with calcium chloride to                form biocement.
   USE - Method for performing cyclical reaction using                calcium carbonate decomposition to form                biocement.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables increasing sustainability,                extending performance, work with local feedstock                components, reducing production costs, and is                rapidly deployable.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The decomposing forms                  calcium oxide and carbon dioxide, and the calcium                  oxide is treated with ammonium chloride.Preferred Method: The step of decomposing                  involves treating calcium carbonate with elevated                  temperatures, where the decomposing is performed                  with an acid, by corona discharge. The step of                  reacting urea with calcium chloride further forms                  ammonium chloride, further comprises treating                  with urease and/or urease-producing organisms.                  The step of performing a cyclical reaction                  further involves reacting urea with calcium                  chloride in the presence of urease-producing                  organisms and supplemental materials.Preferred Components: The acid comprises a                  mineral acid, an organic acid, hydrochloric acid,                  phosphoric acid, nitric acid and acetic acid. The                  supplemental materials comprise organic or                  inorganic material, rock, glass, wood, paper,                  metal, plastic, polymers, fibers, and                  minerals.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The urease-producing                  organisms comprise spores and vegetative cells.                  The urease-producing organisms comprise                  Sporosarcina spp., S. pasteurii, S. ureae,                  Proteus spp., P. vulgaris, P. mirabilis, Bacillus                  spp., B. sphaericus, B. megaterium, Myxococcus                  spp, M. xanthus, Helicobacter spp., H. pylori,                  and variants, serotypes, mutations, recombinant                  forms.

TI Methanol vapour and hydrogen mixed gas integrated in the hydrogen compression system comprises three-phase device, reforming device, hydrogen separating device, steam trap, refrigerator and carbon dioxide liquefaction device.
AU YUE X
   LI J
   LV S
   HAN D
   ZHAO J
   YUE Y
   CHEN F
AE ZHONGKE LIQUID SUNSHINE SUZHOU HYDROGEN (ZHON-Non-standard)
   CAS LIQUID SUNSHINE SUZHOU HYDROGEN TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (CASL-Non-standard)
GA 202018156G
AB    NOVELTY - Methanol vapor and hydrogen mixed gas integrated in the hydrogen compression system comprises a three-phase device, reforming device (3), a hydrogen separating device, a steam trap, a refrigerator (5) and a carbon dioxide liquefaction device. The reforming device comprises a reaction chamber, the reaction chamber is set outside the heating chamber (33). The reaction chamber comprises reaction chamber is adapted to lower the methanol steam reforming reaction and for the upper reaction cavity is suitable for reforming the hydrogen mixed gas, upper reaction chamber (31) and a lower reaction chamber (32). The upper reaction cavity is filled with copper filler or a zirconium-based filler, the lower reaction chamber is filled with a copper filler, or a zirconium-based filler, the upper reaction chamber provided with a first inlet and a first outlet, the lower reaction chamber is provided with a second inlet.
   USE - Methanol vapour and hydrogen mixed gas integrated in the hydrogen compression system.
   ADVANTAGE - The methanol vapor and hydrogen mixed gas integrated in the hydrogen compression system is easy to use and improves hydrogen production efficiency of hydrogen system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Methanol vapor and hydrogen mixed gas integrated in the hydrogen compression system comprises a three-phase device, reforming device (3), a hydrogen separating device, a steam trap, a refrigerator (5) and a carbon dioxide liquefaction device. The reforming device comprises a reaction chamber, the reaction chamber is set outside the heating chamber (33). The reaction chamber comprises reaction chamber is adapted to lower the methanol steam reforming reaction and for the upper reaction cavity is suitable for reforming the hydrogen mixed gas, upper reaction chamber (31) and a lower reaction chamber (32). The upper reaction cavity is filled with copper filler or a zirconium-based filler, the lower reaction chamber is filled with a copper filler, or a zirconium-based filler, the upper reaction chamber provided with a first inlet and a first outlet, the lower reaction chamber is provided with a second inlet. The first inlet is connected with a methanol steam inlet pipe, the first outlet is connected with the hydrogen separation apparatus (4) inlet, the hydrogen separation device is connected with pure hydrogen outlet pipe and carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet pipe. The carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet pipe is connected with a three-phase device, trap, a refrigerator and a carbon dioxide liquefaction device (6), the carbon dioxide liquefaction device is connected to the carbon dioxide outlet pipe and hydrogen mixed gas outlet pipe. The hydrogen mixed gas outlet pipe is connected with the second inlet, and the hydrogen mixed gas outlet pipe is provided with an air pump for lifting pipe in the hydrogen mixed gas delivery pressure. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for squeezing hydrogen, which involves:
   (A) transferring liquid pump and methanol water into the methanol water vapor pipe, where the pump pressure is 7-18 megaaPascal, heating methanol water to vaporize into methanol water vapor and enters the upper reaction chamber of the reformer;
   (B) re-evaluating methanol water vapor in the upper reaction chamber, generating whole reaction a mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and transferring mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide to a hydrogen separation chamber of a hydrogen separation device, where the gas phase composition of the mixed gas of 65-75% hydrogen, 20-26% carbon dioxide and 0.3-3% carbon monoxide;
   (C) separating hydrogen absorption tube in the hydrogen separation chamber the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, collecting the separated pure hydrogen from the output of pure hydrogen, outputting remaining carbon dioxide mixed residual gas from the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas, controlling pressure of the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas by a liquid pump, controlling the refrigerator the temperature of the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas, transferring carbon dioxide mixed residual gas to a carbon dioxide separation device for carbon dioxide liquefaction separation, where the gas phase components of the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas are 25-45% hydrogen, 55-75% carbon dioxide, 0-3% water, and 0.3-3% carbon monoxide;
   (D) controlling pressure by the liquid pump to 7-18 megaPascal, and controlling temperature by the refrigerator is -25-18 degrees C;
   (E) preparing carbon dioxide mixed residual gas into a liquid carbon dioxide and hydrogen mixed residual gas in a carbon dioxide separator, and collecting liquid carbon dioxide, where the components of the hydrogen mixed residual gas are 65-75% hydrogen, 20-26% carbon dioxide, and 3-9% carbon monoxide;
   (F) transferring residual hydrogen mixed gas into the lower reaction chamber of the reformer, using water to make a reformed mixed gas, transferring water according to the content of carbon monoxide, reforming residual hydrogen mixed gas with water in the lower reaction chamber to form a reformed mixed gas, where the gas phase components of the reformed mixed gas are 62-77% hydrogen, 22-27% carbon dioxide, and 0.5-1.5% carbon monoxide, and the ratio of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide in the reformed mixed gas is close to the ratio of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide in the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide; and
   (G) mixing reformed mixed gas enters the upper reaction chamber with a mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide, and mixing reformed mixed gas with the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and carbon monoxide in the upper reaction chamber and transferring to hydrogen separation together hydrogen separation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows as schematic view of system.
   Reforming device (3)
   Refrigerator (5)
   Upper reaction chamber (31)
   Lower reaction chamber (32)
   Heating chamber (33)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The output pure hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide mixed residual gas are output after heat exchange and cooling of the three-phase heat exchange device, and the methanol water is passed through the three-phase heat exchange device and heat exchange vaporizes into methanol water vapor. The methanol water is replaced by natural gas.

PN CN108193228-A; CN108193228-B
TI Preparing cathode material comprises heating, calcining the h62 brass piece in a flowing air bath, cooling and then carrying out electrochemical reduction of calcined brass flakes in the electrolyte.
AU YANG X
   HU H
   DU N
   WANG Y
   TANG Y
   WAN P
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 201851406T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cathode material comprises (i) heating and calcining the h62 brass piece in a flowing air bath, (ii) cooling and then carrying out electrochemical reduction of calcined brass flakes in the electrolyte to obtain cathode material.
   USE - The cathode material is useful in electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has simple preparation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) cutting the h62 brass piece into a size suitable for the shape of the electrolytic cell, (ii) de-oiling the cut brass pieces, (iii) heating tube furnace at 100-500 degrees C and continuously opening air in a flow rate of 100-300 ml/minute, (iv) placing the clean brass piece in a tube furnace, heating at constant temperature for 0.5-12 hours and naturally cooling in air atmosphere, and (v) carrying out electrochemical reduction of heat treated brass sheet, where electrolyte is 0.5 M potassium hydrogen carbonate solution; counter electrode is ruthenium(IV) oxide-coated titanium mesh electrode; reference electrode is saturated calomel electrode; reduction potential is -1.3 V; reduction to current curve remains steady, removing reduction electrode sheet and then drying under an inert gas to obtain final product. Preferred Components: The h62 brass material is zinc-copper alloy, where content of copper is 63 wt.%, content of zinc is 36 wt.%, and thickness is 0.5-2 mm.

PN CN109372604-A; CN209179832-U
TI Semi-closed energy conversion and sequestration system remote comprises e.g. using subsystem, magnesium reacts with carbon dioxide and renewable energy power light-emitting electronic system of electrolytic magnesium sequestration subsystem.
AU ZHENG K
   HUANG Z
AE SHANGHAI POWER EQUIP RES INST (SNPT-C)
GA 201920334Y
AB    NOVELTY - Semi-closed energy conversion and sequestration system remote comprises e.g. using subsystem, magnesium reacts with carbon dioxide and renewable energy power light-emitting electronic system of electrolytic magnesium sequestration subsystem, use of renewable energy power electrolytic magnesium subsystems comprises a renewable energy power plant and electrolytic magnesium factory, regenerating redundant power conveying system of the energy plant and electrolytic magnesium factory connecting, magnesium electrolytic plant by a link connecting magnesium feeding device, the magnesium reacts with carbon dioxide and generating sub-system comprises magnesium and carbon dioxide supercritical carbon dioxide circulation loop and a reactor, the supercritical carbon dioxide circulation loop comprises a compressor, the outlet of the compressor is divided into three paths, the first circuit connected to the side inlet of the low temperature of the low temperature regenerator.
   USE - Used as semi-closed energy conversion and sequestration system remote.
   ADVANTAGE - The system remote: consumes a large amount of renewable energy, can realizes large-scale long-distance transmission of energy, can generates electricity with high efficiency, and can fixes carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Semi-closed energy conversion and sequestration system remote comprises using subsystem, magnesium reacts with carbon dioxide and renewable energy power light-emitting electronic system of electrolytic magnesium sequestration subsystem, use of renewable energy power electrolytic magnesium subsystems comprises a renewable energy power plant (11) and electrolytic magnesium factory (12), regenerating redundant power conveying system of the energy plant and electrolytic magnesium factory connecting, magnesium electrolytic plant by a link connecting magnesium feeding device (14), the magnesium reacts with carbon dioxide and generating sub-system comprises magnesium and carbon dioxide supercritical carbon dioxide circulation loop and a reactor (6), the supercritical carbon dioxide circulation loop comprises a compressor (1), the outlet of the compressor is divided into three paths, the first circuit connected to the side inlet of the low temperature of the low temperature regenerator, the second path is connected with the magnesia carbon fixation device inlet connection, and the third way connected with carbon dioxide side inlet of the combustion product collector, a low-temperature regenerator, the low temperature side outlet and magnesia carbon fixation device are converged and then connected with the high temperature regenerator, the low temperature side inlet. after the high temperature regenerator, the low temperature side outlet with the combustion products collector carbon dioxide side outlet connected to an inlet of the carbon dioxide of the reactor, a carbon dioxide outlet of the reactor is connected to inlet of the dust remover, the dust remover connecting the turbine inlet, outlet connecting the high temperature regenerator of the turbine high side inlet, outlet of the high temperature side of the high temperature regenerator connecting the low temperature regenerator high temperature side inlet, a low temperature regenerator high-temperature side outlet connection pre-cooler inlet, the pre-cooler outlet is connected to the inlet of the compressor, a generator connected to the turbine, the reactor for burning the solid product outlet connected to a combustion products collector, magnesium supplying device connecting reactor magnesium, carbon dioxide supply device is connected to inlet of the compressor, the carbon fixation sub-system comprises magnesia carbon fixation device and a carbon dioxide supply device, a combustion products collector connecting magnesia carbon fixation device solids inlet, carbon dioxide supply device connecting a magnesia-carbon device gas inlet. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for processing semi-closed energy conversion remote transfer and carbon sequestration using magnesium as a carrier, comprising performing semi-closed energy conversion remote transfer and carbon fixation by using a magnesium as a carrier system including (i) supplying the redundant power of the renewable energy power plant to the electrolytic magnesium plant, and the magnesium produced by the electrolytic magnesium plant is transported to the magnesium supply at the place of use through the transportation link device; (ii) using magnesium supply device inputs magnesium to the reactor, and reacting magnesium and carbon dioxide in reactor to form magnesium oxide and carbon i.e. reacting two molecules of magnesium with carbon dioxide to form two molecules of magnesium oxide with release of carbon (2Mg+CO2 = 2MgO+C), and the reactor is released, heat transferring to the bypass carbon dioxide working fluid entering the reactor; (iii) dividing the carbon dioxide working fluid at the outlet of the compressor into three ways, the first way enters the low temperature regenerator, the second way enters the magnesium oxide carbon fixation device absorbs the heat of carbonation reaction, and the third road enters the combustion product collector to absorb the waste heat of the combustion products, and the two carbon dioxide working materials from the low temperature regenerator and the magnesium oxide carbon fixation device merge into the high temperature regenerator, combining the carbon dioxide working fluid from the high temperature regenerator with carbon dioxide working fluid from the combustion product collector, and then entering the reactor to absorb heat, and then entering the turbine to expand work and pushes the generator, generating electric energy, the turbine exhaust gas enters the high temperature regenerator and the low temperature regenerator transfers part of the heat to the carbon dioxide working medium, cooling cooled by the pre-cooler and returning to the compressor; and (iv) carbon dioxide supply device inputs carbon dioxide to the compressor and the magnesium oxide carbon fixation device, and the reactor, output solid product enters the combustion product collector, the separated magnesium oxide enters the magnesium oxide carbon fixation device, and the carbonation reaction occurs in the magnesium oxide carbon fixation device i.e. (MgO+CO2= MgC), performing carbonation reaction by transferring heat to carbon dioxide working fluid.

   Compressor (1)
   Reactor (6)
   Renewable energy power plant (11)
   Electrolytic magnesium factory (12)
   Magnesium feeding device (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material for electrolytic magnesium in the electrolytic magnesium plant is magnesium chloride. Preferred Method: The reaction temperature of magnesium and carbon dioxide in the reactor is: 800-1400 degrees C, pressure is 20-40 MPa. In the step (iii), the high temperature regenerator is extracted at a high pressure side, the carbon dioxide working fluid is used to cool the reactor and the turbine. In the step (iv), the carbonation reaction temperature in magnesium oxide carbon fixation device is 200-500 degrees C, and the pressure is more than 8 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The high temperature regenerator high pressure side carbon dioxide bypass outlet connecting the reactor bypassing the carbon dioxide inlet and turbine inlet.

PN WO2022067064-A1; US2022111308-A1
TI Extracting cannabinoids/cannabinoid acids from cannabis biomass by drying cannabis biomass to form dried biomass, purifying dried biomass by exposing dried biomass to carbon dioxide and extracting high purity cannabinoids/cannabinoid acids.
AU CARRAHER J M
   BAKER Z J
AE MEDPHARM IOWA LLC (MEDP-Non-standard)
   MEDPHARM IOWA LLC (MEDP-Non-standard)
GA 2022476850
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting cannabinoids/cannabinoid acids from cannabis biomass, comprises: drying cannabis biomass to form dried biomass; purifying the dried biomass by exposing the dried biomass to carbon dioxide to remove impurities and form a purified biomass; and extracting high purity cannabinoids/cannabinoid acids from the purified biomass with at least one liquified hydrocarbon.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting cannabinoids/cannabinoid acids from cannabis biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides 95% of high purity cannabinoids/cannabinoid acids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: decarboxylating the dried biomass; destemming the cannabis biomass before the purifying step; precipitating fats and waxes from the purified biomass; and grinding the dried biomass. The decarboxylating step takes place either before or after the purifying step, where the purifying step comprises contacting the dried biomass with subcritical carbon dioxide and/or supercritical carbon dioxide. The method does not comprise a distilling step, and decarboxylating step. The drying step comprises either air drying or use of a vacuum oven. The purification step is performed under subcritical conditions at a pressure of 1000-1100 psi and a temperature of 55-75 degrees F, where liquified hydrocarbon is in a condensed phase. Preferred Components: The liquified hydrocarbon comprises: a linear or branched 1-20C hydrocarbyl or water; and fluorinated hydrocarbon, pentane, butane, and propane. The extracted cannabinoids/cannabinoid acids are at least 90% pure.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of carbon dioxide pre-extraction conditions for dried (non-decarboxylated) material that do not support the claims.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)

AU WANG M
   ZHU J
   XU T
   XU C
AE ZHONGSHAN WEIJIE SHOES CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2021136960
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sustained-release foamed polyurethane involves adding cracking catalyst to the carbon dioxide-based polymer for pre-cracking to obtain the pre-cracking product A, adding pre-cracked product A to the foamed polyurethane prepolymer and stirring at the preset temperature to obtain the mixture B, and extruding mixture B and demoulding after cooling to obtain a finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing sustained-release foamed polyurethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good foaming effect and low finished product density.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The cracking temperature is 120-160 degrees C, and the cracking time is 10-60 minutes, and preset temperature is 40-60 degrees C. The molecular weight of the carbon dioxide-based polymer is 8000-50000. The addition amount of the cracking catalyst is 0.1-10% of the total mass of the carbon dioxide-based polymer. The addition amount of the pre-cracked product A is 0.01-2.0% of the total amount of the foamed polyurethane prepolymer, when the mixture B is extruded, the inside of the mold is in a low air pressure state where the mold is evacuated, where the air pressure in the mold is 0.01-0.8 atm. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-based polymer is a propylene oxide/carbon dioxide binary copolymer, preferably propylene oxide/ethylene oxide/carbon dioxide terpolymer, more preferably propylene oxide/cyclohexane oxide/carbon dioxide terpolymer. The cracking catalyst is selected from one or more of organic carboxylate zinc catalysts, diethyl zinc, organoaluminum, rare earth catalysts and multi-metal mixed catalysts. The organic aluminum includes aluminum triacetylacetonate or/and aluminum trialkoxy. The rare earth catalyst is selected from one or two of praseodymium oxide and cerium oxide. The multi-element mixed catalyst is zinc (Zn)-cobalt (Co)-based double metal cyanide.

The method involves concentrating water vapor produced by the distillation by evaporation to obtain concentrated condensed water, which is used for diluting the saccharide raw material.

PT P
PN CN111071989-A; CN211998803-U
TI Methanol steam and hydrogen mixer integrated low-pressure hydrogen production system comprises three-phase heat exchange device, reforming device, hydrogen separation device, steam trap, air compressor, and refrigerator.
AU YUE X
   LI J
   LV S
   HAN D
   ZHAO J
   YUE Y
   CHEN F
AE CAS LIQUID SUNSHINE SUZHOU HYDROGEN TECH (CASL-Non-standard)
GA 2020380475
AB    NOVELTY - Methanol steam and hydrogen mixer integrated low-pressure hydrogen production system comprises three-phase heat exchange device (2), reforming device (3), hydrogen separation device (4), steam trap, air compressor (5), refrigerator (6) and carbon dioxide liquefaction device (7). The reforming device includes a reaction chamber, and a heating chamber is provided outside the reaction chamber. The reaction chamber includes an upper reaction chamber suitable for reforming the methanol water vapor and a lower reaction chamber suitable for reforming the hydrogen mixed residual gas, and the upper reaction chamber is in communication with the lower reaction chamber. The upper reaction chamber is filled with copper-based filler or zirconium-based filler, and the lower reaction chamber is filled with copper-based filler or zirconium-based filler.
   USE - Methanol steam and hydrogen mixer integrated low-pressure hydrogen production system.
   ADVANTAGE - The methanol steam and hydrogen mixer integrated low-pressure hydrogen production system is helps to improves the hydrogen production efficiency of the hydrogen production system, and optimizes and simplify the structure of the entire hydrogen production system, so that the hydrogen production system can be used to make small hydrogen production equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing low-pressure hydrogen, which involves:
   (A) sending methanol water through liquid pump into the methanol steam pipe inlet pipe with pump pressure of 2-5 mega Pascal;
   (B) vaporizing methanol water by heating into methanol water vapor and entering the upper reaction chamber of the reformer;
   (C) reforming methanol steam in the upper reaction chamber to produce a mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide;
   (D) sending mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide to a hydrogen separation device for hydrogen separation, where gas phase components of the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide are 65-75 wt.% hydrogen, 20-26 wt.% carbon dioxide, and 0.3-3 wt.% carbon monoxide;
   (E) separating the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide through hydrogen absorption tube in the hydrogen separation chamber, and collecting separated pure hydrogen is from the output of the hydrogen absorption tube;
   (F) outputting remaining carbon dioxide mixed residual gas from the hydrogen separation chamber, and pressure-controlling carbon dioxide mixed residual gas by an air compressor;
   (G) controlling the temperature of the carbon dioxide mixture residual gas through refrigerator, and then sending the carbon dioxide mixture residual gas into the carbon dioxide separation device for carbon dioxide liquefaction separation;
   (H) making carbon dioxide mixed residual gas into liquid carbon dioxide and hydrogen mixed residual gas in the carbon dioxide separator, outputting liquid carbon dioxide and collection, where components of the residual hydrogen mixed gas are 65-75 wt.% hydrogen, 20-26 wt.% carbon dioxide, and 3-9 wt.% carbon monoxide;
   (I) sending the residual hydrogen gas mixture into the lower reaction chamber of the reformer, mixing water to make a reformed mixture gas, and distributing water according to the content of carbon monoxide, where water distribution ratio (carbon monoxide: water) is 1:1-20;
   (J) reforming mixed residual gas of hydrogen in the lower reaction chamber with water to form a reformed gas mixture, where gas phase components of the reformed gas mixture are 62-77 wt.% hydrogen, 22-27 wt.% carbon dioxide, and 0.5-1.5 wt.% carbon monoxide in order to make the ratio of hydrogen;
   (K) entering carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the reformed gas mixture close to the ratio of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide;
   (L) entering reformed mixed gas in the upper reaction chamber and mixing with the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide; and
   (M) entering reformed mixed gas into the hydrogen separation chamber together with the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide for hydrogen separation operation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of methanol steam and hydrogen mixer integrated low-pressure hydrogen production system. (Drawing includes non-English text).
   Three-phase heat exchange device (2)
   Reforming device (3)
   Hydrogen separation device (4)
   Air compressor (5)
   Refrigerator (6)
   Carbon dioxide liquefaction device (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The methanol water is replaced by natural gas.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J07 (Refrigeration; ice; gas liquefaction/ solidification - including machines, freezing of (semi)liquids, gas separation/ liquefaction by cooling or pressure, fractionation of air (F25).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))

GA 201929563P
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorbing carbon dioxide in a gas comprises adsorbing a low concentration of carbon dioxide in an electron gas using an alkali metal ion-modified ZSM-5 molecular sieve as an adsorbent, where the concentration of carbon dioxide in the electron gas is 100-1000 part per million by volume.
   USE - The method is useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide in a gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorption method is environmentally friendly; and has low processing cost. The adsorbent: has high thermal stability, strong hydrophobicity and strong water resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of carbon dioxide in the electron gas adsorbed by the adsorbent is less than 0.01 part per million by volume. The electron gas is hydrochlorofluorocarbon, hydrofluorocarbon, perfluorocarbon, hydrofluoroether, hydrochlorofluoroether, perfluoroether, nitrogen gas, hydrogen gas, argon gas, oxygen gas, nitrous oxide, nitrogen trifluoride, carbonyl fluoride, fluorine, dichlorine, tungsten hexafluoride, hydrogen bromide, ammonia, boron trichloride, phosphine, arsine, sulfur hexafluoride, trimethylaluminum, tetrafluoromethane , fluoroform, fluoromethane, difluoromethane, pentafluoroethane, hexafluoroethane, 1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoropropane, octafluoropropane, hexafluoro-2-butyne, octafluorocyclobutane, and/or octafluorocyclopentene. The alkali metal ion is lithium ion (preferred), sodium ion or potassium cation, and the alkali metal salt corresponding to the alkali metal ion is chloride. Preferred Method: The adsorption temperature is 5-75 degrees C (preferably 5-50 degrees C). The adsorption pressure is 1-5 atm (preferably 1 atm). The injection space velocity of the electron gas is 100-600 h-1(preferably 100-200 h-1).

PT P
PN CN111348681-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide synthesis of bismuth sulfide nanorod used e.g. for solar cells involves dissolving bismuth salt and sulfur precursor in deionized water, supercritical extracting with carbon dioxide, filtering, and washing.
AU WANG Y
AE SHANGHAI CUILI ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020630376
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide synthesis of bismuth sulfide nanorod comprises dissolving bismuth salt and sulfur precursor in deionized water, putting into supercritical device, pumping carbon dioxide, extracting for 20-30 minutes, stopping ventilating, heating to 50-80 degrees C, continuously pressurizing carbon dioxide int high-pressure kettle until pressure reaches to 100-150 bar, heat-preserving for 2-3 hours, releasing pressure, filtering, and washing.
   USE - The method is used for supercritical carbon dioxide synthesis of bismuth sulfide nanorod for solar cells, photodiodes, lithium batteries, thermoelectric devices, memories, and sensors.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bismuth salt is bismuth nitrate or bismuth chloride at concentration of 1-5 mmol/L. The sulfur precursor is sodium thiosulfate, potassium thiosulfate, or thioacetamide at molar weight of 1.5 times. Preferred Method: The nanorod is prepated by alternately washing with deionized water and ethanol, centrifuge precipitating or suction filtering, re-dispersing in deionized water or ethanol, repeatedly filtering until pH of the filtrate is 7, washing with ethanol, and vacuum drying at 50-80 degrees C and less than 133 Pa.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN CN110541690-A
TI Improving natural gas recovery by carbon-dioxide reinjection in gas field wellhead comprises using natural gas to produce hydrogen, processing natural gas and re-injecting carbon-dioxide to a gas field.
AU WANG X
   SONG P
   HOU J
   LI Y
   SHAN T
   ZHANG D
   ZHAO S
   YAO H
   MU X
   ZHANG Y
   HOU H
AE CNOOC GAS & POWER GROUP CO LTD (CNOO-C)
GA 2019A43555
AB    NOVELTY - Improving natural gas recovery by carbon-dioxide reinjection in gas field wellhead comprises using natural gas to produce hydrogen, processing natural gas from the wellhead of the gas field to obtain purified carbon-dioxide and hydrogen, re-injecting carbon-dioxide to a gas field for improving natural gas recovery in high pressure and high airspeed conditions.
   USE - The method is useful for improving natural gas recovery by carbon-dioxide reinjection in gas field wellhead.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces gas pressure and energy loss; and improves the recovery factor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for improving natural gas recovery further comprises addition of oxygen or air to the natural gas. The carbon-dioxide is injected back into the gas field after being pressurized by a compressor. The method for producing hydrogen from natural gas comprises methane carrying out reforming decarbonization reaction, carbon monoxide shift reaction, low temperature methanol washing and pressure swing adsorption, washing the low-temperature methanol to obtain the purified carbon-dioxide and using pressure swing adsorption to obtain the purified hydrogen. The purge gas generated by the pressure swing adsorption is mixed with the natural gas after being pressurized. The heat generated by a heat exchanger provided between the methane reforming decarburization reaction and carbon monoxide shift reaction is used to produce steam. The method for improving natural gas recovery comprises performing heat exchange treatment between the methane reforming decarbonization reaction and the carbon monoxide shift reaction and between the carbon monoxide shift reaction and the low temperature methanol washing before the methane reforming decarbonization reaction. The method for producing hydrogen from natural gas further comprises performing a desulfurization treatment on the natural gas.

PN CN113688565-A
TI Optimal weight balance based ultra-critical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor Bayesian optimizing method, involves constructing sample data, and determining design variable from one-dimensional pneumatic calculation model of ultra-critical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor.
AU LU Y
   SHAO W
   WANG X
   BIAN C
   LIU H
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2021E1336P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing sample data. Design variable is determined from a one-dimensional pneumatic calculation model of an ultra-critical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor according to real gas. A Gaussian process proxy model is constructed between design variable vector and a sample response value by utilizing the sample data set. Uncertainty vector of a sample point in a sample data set is calculated. Geometric parameter and a pneumatic index value of the ultra-critical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor are obtained according to the optimal sample point vector and the sample response value. Self-adaptive confidence upper boundary acquisition function is constructed according to weight coefficient value.
   USE - Optimal weight balance based ultra-critical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor Bayesian optimizing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing local development and global exploration dynamically weighing process in optimization process, improving ultra-critical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor Bayesian optimizing efficiency, ultra-critical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor Bayesian optimizing capability, obtaining a pneumatic optimization design result of the ultra-critical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor, increasing ultra-critical carbon dioxide centrifugal compressor Bayesian searching efficiency and avoiding local optimal solution.

PN EP3967386-A1; WO2022053676-A1
TI Separating gas mixture containing carbon dioxide and inert gaseous species into carbon dioxide product stream and inert product stream, comprises feeding gas mixture into adsorption column and feeding stripping gas having water into column.
AU BOON J
   VAN DIJK H A J
   COBDEN P D
   JAMES J D
AE NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
GA 2022372976
AB    NOVELTY - Separating a gas mixture containing carbon dioxide and at least one inert gaseous species into a carbon dioxide product stream and an inert product stream, comprises: (a) feeding the gas mixture into an adsorption column via a first inlet located at a first side of the column, where the adsorption column contains a solid carbon dioxide sorbent loaded with water molecules and thus desorbing water molecules and adsorbing carbon dioxide molecules, to obtain a sorbent loaded with carbon dioxide and the inert product stream; and then (b) feeding a stripping gas comprising water into the adsorption column via a second inlet located at a second side which is opposite to the first inlet, thus stripping the sorbent and desorbing carbon dioxide molecules and adsorbing water molecules, to obtain a sorbent loaded with water and the carbon dioxide product stream, where the adsorption column is reused in step (a) after is stripped in step (b).
   USE - The process is useful for separating a gas mixture containing carbon dioxide and at least one inert gaseous species into a carbon dioxide product stream and an inert product stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: separates gaseous mixtures, preferably in the context of a sorption-enhanced water gas shift process; can operate under isobaric and isothermic conditions; does not require a pressure swing adsorption or a temperature swing adsorption in order to provide excellent separation of dihydrogen and carbon dioxide; employs the roll-up effect which specifically occurs in step (b), and to a lesser extent in step (a) in which steam is desorbed in a more dispersive fashion; no distillation step is required; and does not require intermediate purification and avoiding the energy-intensive steps; provides an elegant and cost- and energy-efficient process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an apparatus for the separation of a carbon dioxide product stream and an inert product stream, comprising a first adsorption column and a second adsorption column, where (i) the first adsorption column comprises a first inlet and a second outlet located on one side of the column and a first outlet and second inlet located at the opposite side of the column, and a solid carbon dioxide sorbent, and (ii) the second adsorption column comprises a first inlet and a second outlet located on one side of the column and a first outlet and second inlet located at the opposite side of the column, and a solid carbon dioxide sorbent, both adsorption columns are configured in a first phase to receive a gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide and at least one inert gaseous species via the first inlet and to discharge an inert product stream via the first outlet, and in a second phase to receive a stripping gas comprising carbon dioxide via the second inlet and to discharge a carbon dioxide product stream via the second outlet, the apparatus is configured so that one adsorption column operates in the first phase while the other adsorption column operates in the second phase, and further comprises means for simultaneous switching both adsorption columns between the first and second phase.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The steps (a) and (b) are performed at a pressure of 1-50 bar, and at a temperature of 200-500 degrees C, where the pressure during the process varies for at most 1 bar, and the temperature during the process varies for at most 40 degrees C. The adsorption columns operate in series, where at least one is performing step (a) and at least one is simultaneously performing step (b). The downstream of step (a) inert gaseous molecules are separated from the inert product stream, preferably by condensation of water. The pressure during the process varies for at most 0.3 bar, more preferably at most 0.1 bar, and/or the temperature during the process varies for at most 10 degrees C, more preferably at most 5 degrees C. Preferred Components: The inert gaseous species comprises dihydrogen, dinitrogen, methane and other carbohydrates, preferably the inert species is dihydrogen and/or dinitrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The first inlet and a second outlet of both adsorption columns are the same structural element, and/or the first outlet and second inlet of both adsorption columns are the same structural element.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of packed-bed and intraparticle model, numerical solution strategy, isobaric system investigations, isobaric system and investigation of temperature effects that do not support the claims.

   TIAN J
   XU Y
   ZHANG X
   HUANG J
   WANG X
AE WUHAN GRENON BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2022B0273S
AB    NOVELTY - A bacteriophage for preventing and treating                infectious serositis of duck, and capable of lysing                Riemerella anatipestifer, is new. The bacteriophage                is R.anatipestiferphage GRAP2 (CCTCC M                20211478).
   USE - The bacteriophage is useful for preventing and                treating infectious serositis of duck, lysing                R.anatipestifer and preventing and treating                R.anatipestifer disease caused by R.anatipestifer                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bacteriophage has excellent lysis effect                and preventive effect on R.anatipestifer ducklings,                high environmental adaptability and stress                resistance, and lysis rate of 53.8%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                separating and purifying method of bacteriophage                for preventing and treating infectious serositis of                duck, which involves collecting 20 ml 30 pts. wt.                water sample near a meat duck farm, centrifuging at                5000 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 10 minutes,                filtering and sterilizing the supernatant,                uniformly mixing the filtrate with 2&#215;                tryptic soy broth (TSB) liquid culture medium                (comprising 10% newborn calf serum) and 1 ml                R.anatipestifer bacterial liquid (107 colony                forming unit (cfu)/ml) in logarithmic phase,                culturing overnight at 37&#176;C in 5% carbon                dioxide incubator, enriching bacteriophage,                centrifuging the sample enriched liquid at 5000 rpm                for 10 minutes, obtaining the micro-pore filter                film with supernatant liquid, passing through 0.22                microns to remove bacteria to obtain filtrate                comprising the bacteriophage, mixing 100                &#181;l the filtrate and 500 &#181;l host                duck R.anatipestifer bacterial liquid, maintaining                for 15 minutes to make fully combined with the                receptor on the surface of the bacteria, adding the                mixed solution into 7 ml TSB semi-solid agar                culture medium (comprising 5% newborn calf serum),                cooling to 50&#176;C, uniformly mixing,                immediately arranging on the solidified TSA flat                plate, after the agar is solidified in 5%carbon                dioxide incubator at 37&#176;C for 12-16 hours                for inverted culture, observing the growth                condition of the plaque, using sterile gun to                select large and transparent plaque on the                double-layer flat plate forming plaque, after                oscillating desorption in 1 ml SM liquid, removing                bacteria to obtain phage filtrate by microporous                filter membrane of 0.22 &#181;m, inoculating                into 5 ml TSB liquid culture medium (comprising 5%                newborn calf serum), adding 100 &#181;L host                duck R.anatipestifer bacterial liquid, uniformly                mixing the materials, placing in 5% carbon dioxide                incubator at 37&#176;C for overnight culture,                centrifuging at 5000 rpm for 10 minutes, obtaining                supernatant and filtering by bacterial filter                membrane, observing the plaque shape by dual-layer                flat plate method, and repeating the operation for                3-5 times to obtain the plaques with same shape and                size.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Biomolecule: The bacteriophage is                  obtained by separating the R.anatipestifer strain                  GRA116 as host belonging to long-tail phage,                  where the size of the plaque is 1 mm.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial.

   TAHIR M
AE UNIV MALAYSIA TEKNOLOGI UTM (UYMA-Non-standard)
GA 2022D6025N
AB    NOVELTY - Silver-lanthanum (Ag-La) loaded protonated                carbon nitrides nanotubes (pCNNT) comprises:                protonated carbon nitride nanotubes (CNNTs)                photocatalyst; and silver-lanthanum (Ag-La), where                the silver-lanthanum (Ag-La) is used for a                single-step conversion of many greenhouse gases                into at least one product under UV irradiation and                visible light irradiation.
   USE - The nanotubes are useful for fabricating                carbon nitride nanotubes photocatalyst and reducing                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The nanotubes are fabricated via a                template-free sonicated assisted one-pot                hydrothermal method; and can be extended to                establish a solar based carbon management                application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                fabrication of CNNTs photocatalyst and of it use                for carbon dioxide reduction, comprising heating at                least 5 g melamine placed in a crucible to                550&#176; C at 5&#176; C per minute for 2                hours to obtain graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4)                powder, utilizing the g-C3N4 powder for a surface                charge modification by protonating the g-C3N4                powder in an acetic solution, immersing at least 1                g the g-C3N4 powder in a nitric acid solution by                stirring for 24 hours to obtain a slurry, filtering                the slurry to obtain a first sludge and washing the                obtained first sludge with distilled water, drying                the washed sludge at 110&#176; C in under air                flow for 12 hours, where the g-C3N4 powder is                denoted as a CN and protonated g-C3N4 powder is                denoted as a pCN, dissolving a predefined amount of                lanthanum nitrate in water, adding the dissolved                lanthanum nitrate into pCN solution, and sonicating                for at least 30 minutes, stirring for at least 6                hours and sonicating for 30 minutes to obtain a                second sludge, centrifuging the second sludge and                dried at 110&#176; C for 12 hours to obtain                lanthanum solution, loading silver nanoparticles                over the pCN solution via sonication and                photo-deposition mechanism, dispersing a predefined                amount of the pCN solution in 20 ml water, adding a                predefined amount of silver nitrate and sonicated                for at least 30 minutes, stirred for at least 6                hours and then sonicated for at least 30 minutes at                room temperature, and UV-irradiating for at least                15 minutes to obtain silver nanoparticles loaded                pCN solution, mixing the lanthanum solution and                silver nanoparticles loaded pCN solution,                sonicating the mixed solution for at least 60                minutes and stirred the sonicated solution for 24                hours, drying the stirred solution for at                110&#176; C for 12 hours to obtain Ag-La loaded                protonated pCNNT.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pCNNT comprises a                  loaded Ag-La over a monolithic honeycomb                  structure. The CNNTs photocatalyst                  is fabricated through a template-free sonicated                  assisted one-pot hydrothermal mechanism.

   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                intelligent pressure sensing elastomer foamed                material generating an electrical signal when it is                subjected to an external force (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

TI Extracting icariin comprises e.g. mixing pretreated Epimedium powder, ethanol solution and aqueous ammonia, concentrating and drying extract to obtain ultrasonic extract, placing ultrasonic extract in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and extracting to obtain icariin.
AU LIU X
   WANG Z
   LI S
AE ANHUI JINYUAN PHARM CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202250400G
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting icariin comprises (1) cleaning the Epimedium, drying, and crushing to obtain Epimedium powder, (2) microwave processing the Epimedium powder once for 1-3 minutes, mixing it with the enzyme solution A in a mass ratio of 1:0.005-0.015, performing enzymatic hydrolysis for 10-25 minutes at a temperature of 47-50degrees Celsius, mixing with enzyme solution B in a mass ratio of 1:0.005-0.015, performing enzymolysis at temperature of 47-50degrees Celsius for 20-30 minutes, and performing secondary microwave treatment for 2-5 minutes obtain pretreated Epimedium powder, (3) mixing pretreated Epimedium powder, ethanol solution and aqueous ammonia, soaking at room temperature for 20-60 minutes, carrying out ultrasonic extraction at 60-75degrees Celsius, and concentrating and drying the extract to obtain ultrasonic extract, and (4) placing the ultrasonic extract obtained in the step (3) in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and extracting to obtain the icariin.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting icariin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting icariin comprises (1) cleaning the Epimedium, drying, and crushing to obtain Epimedium powder, (2) microwave processing the Epimedium powder once for 1-3 minutes, mixing it with the enzyme solution A in a mass ratio of 1:0.005-0.015, performing enzymatic hydrolysis for 10-25 minutes at a temperature of 47-50degrees Celsius, mixing with enzyme solution B in a mass ratio of 1:0.005-0.015, performing enzymolysis at temperature of 47-50degrees Celsius for 20-30 minutes, and performing secondary microwave treatment for 2-5 minutes obtain pretreated Epimedium powder, where the enzyme solution A is mixture of cellulase, rhamnosidase and water, and the enzyme solution B is mixture of pectinase, cellulase, xylosidase and water, (3) mixing pretreated Epimedium powder, ethanol solution and aqueous ammonia, soaking at room temperature for 20-60 minutes, carrying out ultrasonic extraction at 60-75degrees Celsius, and concentrating and drying the extract to obtain ultrasonic extract, and (4) placing the ultrasonic extract obtained in the step (3) in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and extracting to obtain the icariin, where the extraction comprises primary extraction and secondary extraction, and the temperature of the primary extraction is 45-55degrees Celsius, the pressure is 25-30 MPa, the time is T1 minutes, the temperature of the secondary extraction is 50-60degrees Celsius, the pressure is 32-38 MPa, the time is T2 minutes, and T1 and T2 is 80-130.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the drying comprises drying and microwave drying, the drying temperature is 70-80degrees Celsius, the time is 10-25 minutes, and the microwave drying time is 30-90 seconds.Preferred Method: In the step (1), in the Epimedium powder, the Epimedium powder with particle size larger than 20 mesh accounts for 6.5-7 wt.%, the Epimedium powder with particle size of 20-50 mesh accounts for 15-21 wt.%, the Epimedium powder with particle size of 50-70 mesh accounts for 55-60 wt.%, and the Epimedium powder with particle size of 70-100 mesh accounts for 10-15 wt.%. In the step (2), the power of one microwave treatment is 400-500 W. The power of the secondary microwave treatment is 550-650 W. The enzyme liquid A is mixture of cellulase, rhamnosidase and water in weight ratio of 1:1:5-10. The enzyme solution B is mixture of pectinase, cellulase, xylosidase and water in a weight ratio of 1:1:1:5-12. In the step (3), the solid-liquid ratio is 1 g:5-10 ml during mixing. The ethanol solution is ethanol solution with mass fraction of 40-45%, the aqueous ammonia is aqueous ammonia with a mass fraction of 20-25%, and the volume ratio of the ethanol solution to the aqueous ammonia is 95-99:1-5. The ultrasonic extraction comprises one ultrasonic extraction, two ultrasonic extractions and three ultrasonic extractions. The power of the first ultrasonic extraction is 300-500 W, and the time is t1 minute, the power of the second ultrasonic extraction is 600-700 W, and the time is t2 minute, and the power of three ultrasonic extractions is 300-500 W, the time is t3 minute, and t1, t3 and t2 is 30-60. In the step (4), the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 18-20 l/hour during the extraction process. In the first extraction process, ethanol with a mass fraction of 60-70% is used as cosolvent, and the flow rate of the cosolventis 2-3 ml/minute. The T1greater than or equal to2xT2 and 10.

PN CN113932502-A
TI Oil-gas separator for carbon dioxide air source heat pump water heater, has bearing frame whose left side is movably inserted with oil discharging pipe, and heat supply assembly that is fixed on outer wall of shell.
AU JIANG T
   TAO Y
   MEI S
   LI C
   WANG D
AE HOUPU NANJING LIFE SCI RES INST CO LTD (HOUP-Non-standard)
GA 2022169504
AB    NOVELTY - The separator has a heat-conducting inner cylinder (12) whose left side is movably inserted with an oil discharging pipe (110) that is connected with a left side of a bearing frame (18). A left end of the bearing frame is fixedly inserted with a heat conducting inner cylinder and a shell. A heat supply assembly is fixed on an outer wall of the shell. The shell is provided with a bottom frame. Left and right sides of the bottom frame are fixed with a spiral heating piece (116). A right top side of the heat conducting internal cylinder is fixed with an inlet pipe (15). A top left side of an exhaust pipe (16) connected with each other. An end of an introducing pipe passes through an outer side wall of a side portion of a shell. A bottom of an inner cavity of the heating inner cylinder is formed with an annular oil-gas separating layer.
   USE - Oil-gas separator for a carbon dioxide air source heat pump water heater. Can also be used for a solar product and a heat pump device.
   ADVANTAGE - The separator makes the mixed liquid enter into the heat conducting inner cylinder through the introducing pipe, under the action of the flow, the following impeller is matched to rotate the rotating shaft, at the same time, the spiral heating piece is opened to heat the heat-conducting inner cylinder, so that mixed liquid is heated to separate oil and gas. The pressing plate downwards synchronously drives the connecting ring so as to press mixed liquid and gas downwards to make it be further separated by the oil-gas separating layer, improving the oil gas separating quality. The heated air enters the spiral heat conducting pipe in the water tank for cooling and then discharged, and converted into kinetic energy by the fan blade group, generating by the generator, reducing the energy consumption of the separator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of an oil-gas separator for a carbon dioxide air source heat pump water heater.
   Separation assembly (1)
   Heat conducting inner cylinder (12)
   Rotating shaft (14)
   Introducing pipe (15)
   Exhaust pipe (16)
   Follow-up impeller (17)
   Bearing frame (18)
   Oil-gas separating layer (19)
   Oil discharging pipe (110)
   Bearing ring (111)
   Movable ring (112)

   Interconnecting pipe (115)
   Spiral heating element (116)
   Cleaning brush plate (117)
   Follow-up disk (118)
   T-shaped rod (119)
   Wedge-shaped block (120)

PN WO2021099811-A1; HU201900397-A1
TI Permanently high hydrogen-containing reducing carbonated bottled drinking water is produced by spraying purified/cooled raw water into low pressure carbon dioxide gas, spraying carbonated water with high pressure hydrogen gas, and bottling.
AU SIMON L P
   SIMON LASZLA PAL
AE SIMON L P (SIMO-Individual)
GA 202157322G
AB    NOVELTY - A permanently high hydrogen-containing reducing carbonated bottled drinking water is produced by spraying purified and cooled raw water at a pressure of e.g., 2-16 bar, into a low pressure (e.g., 1-15 bar) carbon dioxide (CO2) gas; spraying carbonated water with a concentration of 1-10 g/3 dl with a pump at a pressure of e.g., 4-16 bar, into a subsequent saturating vessel containing high pressure (e.g., 3-15 bar) hydrogen (H2) gas with a concentration resulting oxidation-reduction potential (ORP) value of - 340 mV or better; and immediately bottling hydrogen-rich carbonated water in a glass or in a bottle of other special anti-diffusion material so that it completely fills the space without a gas layer above, and the volume changes due to the thermal expansion of the liquid are compensated for e.g., by the presence of dissolved carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas.
   USE - A permanently high hydrogen-containing reducing carbonated bottled drinking water.
   ADVANTAGE - Hydrogen-rich water can be produced automatically and economically, retaining a hydrogen content of nearly 10.5 rH2, - 340 mV ORP for at least three months from bottling, and can be sold commercially without explosion risk, thanks to special bottling, and can be easily accessible to everyone. The purpose of adding carbonated water is twofold: on the one hand, it disinfects the water and protects it from contamination in the future, and on the other hand, it makes bottling safer by eliminating the risk of explosion. Production is much more economical, because water pumps for water level control make it easy to automate, there is no need for a difficult-to-produce homogeneous gas mixture, and with this method the ratio of the two gasses in the water can be easily controlled between stable values. Thanks to the water spray system, the condensed water mixture after spraying can be transferred and used almost immediately, which allows continuous operation and eliminates the need for large saturating tanks, thus making the developed technology significantly simpler and cheaper. The method can easily produce physiologically excellent water that is very high in purity, has excellent solubility (thus very effectively detoxifies the body) and contains only substances that are well known to the human body and are used daily.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for automated production of permanently high hydrogen-containing reducing carbonated bottled drinking water by gas pressure and water level control.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Mineral content of water is reduced to a value of 2-20 mg/L by water treatment system with a reverse osmosis system, and chemical impurities are also removed. Preferred Processes: Purified water is cooled to 5-10 degrees C to facilitate better dissolution of the gas. Saturation system is controlled by gas pressure and liquid level conditions in such a way that the discharge of hydrogen-rich soda water for bottling starts and stops the pumps involved in the saturation process, thus, the whole process is controlled by keeping the gas pressure and the liquid level values within stable intervals. Preferred Conditions: CO2 gas has a pressure of 1-1.5 bar, and the purified and cooled water is sprayed into it at a pressure of 8-10 bar. CO2 gas in the water has a concentration of 1-2 g/3 dl. H2 gas has a pressure of 8-10 bar, and the carbonated water is sprayed into it at a pressure of 11-15 bar. Hydrogen-rich water is bottled in glass against diffusion. Bottling is carried out at normal atmospheric pressure.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN CN112646229-A; CN112646229-B
TI Preparing organosilicone aerogel with            super-amphiphobic performance comprises e.g. preparing            organosilicone sol, adding fluorosilane, aging and            subjecting semi-transparent organosilicone gel to            carbon dioxide supercritical drying.
AU ZHONG Y
   LIU Y
   CUI S
   SHEN X
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
   JIANGSU RUIYING NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY DEV CO              LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU RUIYING NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202148673M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing organosilicone aerogel with                super-amphiphobic performance comprises (i) mixing                the organosilane and initiator uniformly to form                solution, transferring into hydrothermal reaction                kettle, placing it in the oven at 100-120 degrees C                to react for 12-24 hours, taking out, cooling to                obtain the transparent viscous liquid polyethylene                siloxane, mixing the obtained polyvinyl siloxane,                methyl trimethoxy silane, benzyl alcohol,                de-ionized water and fluorocarbon surfactant evenly                to prepare solution, stirring for 0.5-2 hours and                dripping alkaline catalyst continuously to obtain                the organosilicone sol; (ii) adding fluorosilane                drop wise into the prepared organosilicone sol,                stirring uniformly, sealing film, placing in the                vacuum drying box at 50-60 degrees C for 0.5-2                hours, adding aging liquid and aging the                organosilicon composite wet gel to obtain                semi-transparent organosilicone gel; and (iii)                subjecting the translucent silicone gel sample                obtained to carbon dioxide supercritical drying                treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                organosilicone aerogel with super-amphiphobic                performance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces areogel with double                phobic properties, high porosity, and high specific                surface area, anti-fog, icing and frost effects and                moisture-proof; and uses organic bridged                alkoxysilane as the silicon source to improve the                mechanical brittleness of aerogels, and uses                fluorosilane as the surface modifier to impart                double phobicity to the aerogels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing organosilicon aerogel with                super-amphiphobic performance comprises (i) mixing                the organosilane and initiator at volume ratio of                1: (0.1-1) uniformly to form solution, transferring                into hydrothermal reaction kettle, placing it in                the oven at 100-120 degrees C to react for 12-24                hours, taking out, cooling to obtain the                transparent viscous liquid polyethylene siloxane,                mixing the obtained polyvinyl siloxane, methyl                trimethoxy silane, benzyl alcohol, de-ionized water                and fluorocarbon surfactant at volume ratio of                1:(1-4):(2- 8):(2-10):(0.2-0.6) evenly to prepare                solution, stirring for 0.5-2 hours and dripping                alkaline catalyst continuously to obtain the                organosilicone sol; (ii) adding fluorosilane drop                wise into the prepared organosilicone sol, where                control the volume ratio of organosilane to                fluorosilane at 1:(0.2-0.6), stirring uniformly,                sealing film, placing in the vacuum drying box at                50-60 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours to obtain the                organosilicone composite wet gel, adding aging                liquid and aging the organosilicone composite wet                gel to obtain semi-transparent organosilicone gel;                and (iii) subjecting the translucent organosilicone                gel sample obtained to carbon dioxide supercritical                drying treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  alkaline catalyst comprises aqueous ammonia,                  ammonium fluoride, and tetramethylammonium                  hydroxide solution, where the mass concentration                  of the alkaline catalyst is 20-30% and the volume                  of alkaline catalyst added accounts for 1-3 wt.%                  of the total solution volume.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  organosilane comprises vinyl trimethoxy silane,                  vinyl triethoxy silane or vinyl methyl dimethoxy                  silane. In the step (i), the initiator is                  di-tert-butyl peroxide. In the step (i), the                  fluorocarbon surfactant comprises Capstone FS-61                  (RTM: volatile organic compound-free, alkylphenol                  ethoxylates-free, aqueous anionic                  fluorosurfactant) fluorocarbon surfactant, Zonyl                  9361 (RTM: Anionic fluorosurfactant,)                  fluorocarbon surfactant or Zonyl TM (RTM:                  2-(Perfluoroalkyl)ethyl methacrylate)                  fluorocarbon surfactant. In the step (ii), the                  fluorosilane is 1H, 1H, 2H,                  2H-perfluorodecyltriethoxysilane or                  1H,1H,2H,2H-perfluorodecyltriethoxysilane. In the                  step (ii), the aging liquid is a mixed solution                  of alcohol and water in a volume ratio of                  1:(1-4), where the alcohol is ethanol,                  isopropanol or methanol. Preferred Method: The                  reaction temperature of carbon dioxide                  supercritical drying is 45-50 degrees C,                  controlling the pressure of the reaction kettle                  at 10-12MPa, and drying for 12-24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the                  aging treatment comprises replacing the aging                  solution for every 8-12 hours, where the number                  of replacements is 3-5 times.
