TI Performing induction treatment for enhancing biological characteristics of human mesenchymal stem cells, by obtaining primary cells, adding medium, resuspending, culturing, passaging, selecting mesenchymal stem cells, and verifying purity.
AU LIU X
   ZHANG C
   LIU H
AE BEIJING SINO TIANYI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202104020Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing induction treatment for enhancing the biological characteristics of human mesenchymal stem cells, involves (a) performing different treatment methods and primary cell culture of mesenchymal stem cell tissues to obtain primary cells, removing the supernatant, adding a stem cell culture medium containing a serum substitute, resuspending the cell pellet evenly, combining into a centrifuge tube, and counting on a cell counter, (b) inoculating the resuspended cell suspension into a culture flask containing a stem cell culture medium containing a serum substitute, shaking, and placing in a carbon dioxide incubator for culturing, (c) inoculating (1-10)x 104 cells/cm2 into T150 culture bottles, adding 20 ml culture medium to each bottle, passaging, selecting mesenchymal stem cells of P3-P6 generation as the source of induced stem cells, and verifying the purity, and (d) performing induction and treatment of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells.
   USE - The method is useful for performing induction treatment for enhancing the biological characteristics of human mesenchymal stem cells, which is used for providing antioxidation, antiapoptosis, antiaging and angiogenesis effect, and repairing tissue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for performing induction treatment for enhancing the biological characteristics of human mesenchymal stem cells, involves (a) performing different treatment methods and primary cell culture of mesenchymal stem cell tissues to obtain primary cells, where the primary cell culture is the cells after centrifugation, removing the supernatant, adding a stem cell culture medium containing a serum substitute, resuspending the cell pellet evenly, combining into a centrifuge tube, and counting on a cell counter, (b) inoculating the resuspended cell suspension into a culture flask containing a stem cell culture medium containing a serum substitute, shaking, and placing in a carbon dioxide incubator for culturing at 37 plus minus 0.5 degrees C, and 5 plus minus 0.2% carbon dioxide concentration until the cell confluence reaches 80-90%, (c) inoculating (1-10)x 104 cells/cm2 into T150 culture bottles, adding 20 ml culture medium to each bottle, passaging successively according to the above passaging method, selecting mesenchymal stem cells of P3-P6 generation as the source of induced stem cells, verifying the purity of the obtained mesenchymal stem cells by flow cytometry, and (d) performing induction and treatment of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by selecting P3-P6 generations of mesenchymal stem cells, inoculating in a T150 culture flask at (1-10)x 104 cells/cm2, adding 20 ml Loropetalum chinense extract medium containing 1-100 ug/ml to each bottle, and continuing to culture for 48 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Cells: The sources of mesenchymal stem cells comprise bone marrow, umbilical cord, placenta, dental pulp, cord blood and all human tissues rich in mesenchymal stem cells. Preferred Method: The method involves obtaining umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells as an example. The method involves preparing mesenchymal stem cells by (i) taking the umbilical cord of a full-term healthy fetus and test the umbilical cord serum HbsAg, anti-hepatitis C virus (HCV), anti-hepatitis D virus (HDV), anti-hepatitis E virus (HEV), anti-HIV-1/2, HCV-RNA, HIV-1-RNA, and cytomegalovirus (CMV)-DNA, where the test results are all negative and available, (ii) wiping the saline bottle with alcohol-sprayed gauze, removing the sealing plastic at the opening of the bottle and adding in a clean bench in operation, (iii) peeling and washing of umbilical cord Wharton's glue by washing in normal saline to remove blood stains from the umbilical cord, soaking in alcohol for disinfection, washing again with normal saline, cutting the umbilical cord into 2-3 cm sections in a Petri dish filled with normal saline, washing with normal saline to remove congestion and clots in the small blood vessels of the umbilical cord, peeling off the Wharton's glue and washing with saline, (iv) preparing tissue blocks by cutting the umbilical cord Wharton's glue by cutting the washed umbilical cord Wharton's glue into a tissue homogenate of 1-4 mm, washing the tissue homogenate with saline and centrifuging, (v) performing vaccination by removing the supernatant from the tissue homogenate after centrifugation, inoculating into an inoculation culture flask such as T150, and spreading flat on the bottom surface of the entire inoculation culture flask, (vi) culturing umbilical cord Wharton's glue by inoculating the culture bottle in the horizontal position, placing in the carbon dioxide incubator, slowly standing up after 6-12 hours, adding stem cell culture medium containing 10 wt.% serum substitute to the bottom of the culture flask, slowly letting infiltrate the entire tissue mass, and culturing 37 plus minus 0.5 degrees C under 5 plus minus 0.2% carbon dioxide, (vii) changing liquid by (1) exchanging first fluid by culturing the umbilical cord Wharton's glue until the 6-7 days for full fluid exchange, adding the old medium and unadhered tissue pieces, adding an equal amount of medium containing 10% serum replacement, laying the culture bottle flat and placing in the carbon dioxide incubator to continue the culture, (2) secondly changing medium by after umbilical cord Wharton's glue is cultured for 9-10 days, repeating the first change of medium, and (3) thirdly changing fluid by observing the cell growth under the mirror image, when the cell confluence does not reach 70-80%, and after umbilical cord Wharton's glue is cultured for 12-13 days, adding a full volume change operation, (viii) harvesting primary cells by observing cell by observing all the culture flasks under a microscope, when it is observed that the cell confluence reaches 70%-80%, then digesting and harvesting, performing primary cell harvest by configuring cell digestion solution by mixing pancreatin and physiological saline in a volume ratio of 1:4, where the mixture is the cell digestion solution, removing the old culture fluid by shaking the culture bottle gently to make the non-adherent tissue pieces fall off, and then adding the tissue pieces and the old culture fluid into the waste fluid bottle together, washing by adding physiological saline into each bottle from which the old culture fluid is removed, shaking the culture bottle gently to make the physiological saline liquid soak the bottom surface of the culture bottle and the tissue mass, then pouring the physiological saline, and repeating the washing once, digesting by adding digestion solution to each culture flask after washing, so that the digestion solution of each culture flask infiltrates the entire bottom surface of the culture flask, standing for 1 minute, shaking the culture flask up and down to make the digestive juice fully contact the cells, and stopping digestion after 90% of the cells have fallen off and become rounded under the microscope, and stopping digestion by adding mesenchymal stem cell culture medium to each flask that can stop digestion to stop digestion, shaking the flask back and forth to make full contact dilution, and then transferring to a 50 ml centrifuge tube for centrifugation, and (ix) passaging primary cells (P0 pass P1) by centrifuging the cells, removing the supernatant and add the stem cell culture medium containing the serum substitute, resuspending the cell pellet evenly, combining into a centrifuge tube, and counting on a cytometer, inoculating the resuspended cell suspension into a culture flask, diluting to a certain volume with stem cell culture medium containing serum substitutes to make the cells reach a certain concentration, shaking well, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator, and culturing at 37 plus minus 0.5 degrees C under 5 plus minus 0.2% carbon dioxide, and culturing until the cell confluence reaches 80-90%, inoculating (1-10)x 104 cells/cm2 into T150 culture bottles, adding 20 ml culture medium to each bottle, passaging sequentially according to the above-mentioned passaging method, selecting mesenchymal stem cells of P3-P6 generation as the source of induced stem cells, identifying the purity of the obtained mesenchymal stem cells by flow cytometry, where the cell phenotypes are cluster of differentiation (CD)-34/CD45/CD11b/CD19/human leukocyte antigen-DR isotype (HLADR)(-), and CD90/CD105/CD73(+), performing induction and treatment of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by selecting P3-P6 generations of mesenchymal stem cells, inoculating in a T150 culture flask at (1-10)x 104 cells/cm2, adding 20 ml L.chinense extract medium containing 1-100 ug/ml to each bottle, and continuing to culture for 48 hours. The L.chinense extract medium is the stem cell culture medium containing the serum substitute added with L.chinense extract, and the content concentration of L.chinense extract is 1-100 ug/ml. The method involves using (1-10)x 104 cells/cm2, preferably 50000 cells/cm2.
   ACTIVITY - Antiapoptotic; Nootropic; Dermatological; Angiogenesis inhibitor.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN112058274-A; CN112058274-B
TI Preparation of catalyst used for carbon monoxide oxidation, involves dissolving organic salts of metals in ethanol, adding cationic polyacrylamide, hydrothermally reacting, mixing with aluminum sol, hot-pressing, and baking.
AU CHENG X
   KANG B
   LI X
   WANG Z
   WANG P
   MA C
   WANG L
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 2020C69514
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of catalyst involves dissolving an aluminum isopropoxide powder in deionized water, heating the water bath to 85 degrees C, mechanically stirring for 1 hour, adjusting pH value of the solution using an acid, stirring in the water bath to prepare an aluminum sol, dissolving a certain amount of organic salts of copper, manganese, iron, cerium, titanium, and zirconium in ethanol, adding a cationic polyacrylamide, adjusting pH, stirring with a magnetic stirrer at room temperature, hydrothermally reacting the mixture in a hydrothermal reactor to obtain a slurry, mixing the slurry with the aluminum sol, coating on a glass fiber paper, hot-pressing with a hot press, and baking the hot-pressed catalyst in a muffle furnace.
   USE - Preparation of catalyst used for carbon monoxide oxidation, preferably for removing carbon monoxide and eliminating whitening of flue gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces catalyst which has excellent applicability and low energy consumption property during operation. The catalyst can completely remove carbon dioxide with low operating cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) system for removing carbon dioxide and eliminating whitening of flue gas, which has a demister, a dehumidification runner, a gas-detection module, a diversion module, a heating module, a catalytic oxidation module is a stacked catalyst bed, and a flue gas return module; and
   (2) removal of carbon monoxide and eliminating whitening of flue gas, which involves installing the system at the end of flue gas treatment after wet desulfurization, dry denitration or semi-dry desulfurization, and before a chimney, passing the wet flue gas after desulfurization through a mist eliminator to remove droplets, impacting together the return flue gas and inlet flue gas to dehumidification runner blades, simultaneously removing water vapor in the flue gas and regenerating the dehumidification runner, reducing cranking output and improving the regeneration efficiency of the dehumidification runner, passing the flue gas through the catalytic oxidation module, evaluating the flue gas using carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide sensors installed at the flue gas outlet, directly discharging the flue gas through the chimney when the flue gas temperature is higher than 80 degrees C, and starting the flue gas heating module when the flue gas temperature is lower than 80 degrees C, increasing the flue gas return flow and the speed of dehumidification rotor, heating the flue gas to more than 80 degrees C to avoid generation of white feathers, and discharging the flue gas into atmosphere through the chimney.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of aluminum isopropoxide and deionized water is 1:1. The acid is acetic acid or nitric acid, preferably acetic acid having a concentration of 1-1.5 mol/L. The catalyst comprises 2-5 wt.% copper, 2-5 wt.% manganese, 35-40 wt.% iron, 35-40 wt.% cerium, 3-6 wt.% zirconium, 1-2 wt.% titanium, and 3-6 wt.% cationic polyacrylamide based on content of the aluminum sol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic salt of copper is copper glycinate, copper methanesulfonate, or copper citrate. The organic salt of manganese is L-manganese aspartate and/or manganese 2-aminoacetate. The organic salt of iron is ferrous creatine glycerate, iron stearate, or iron phthalocyanine. The organic salt of cerium is cerium isopropoxide or cerium 2-methoxyethoxide. The organic salt of titanium is propoxy titanium and/or oxytitanium phthalocyanine. The organic salt of zirconium is zirconium ethoxide or zirconium n-butoxide. The mass ratio of ethanol and total content of organic salts of copper, manganese, iron, cerium, titanium and zirconium is 1-1.5:1.

PN KR2112137-B1
TI Control valve cap for coffee bean packaging casing, has compartments having protruding surface protruding to lower surface protrudes outward and including pressing assembly that is integrally formed with hole on upper side.
AU AN J
AE COFFEELEC KOREA CO LTD (COFF-Non-standard)
GA 202050760F
AB    NOVELTY - The cap has a cap receiving groove (23) that is coupled to the discharge portion of the package. A discharge portion receiving groove has a second O-ring seating groove and a first O-ring seating groove (22). A control valve (20) in which a receiving groove to which a gas discharge unit (40) is coupled to the central portion. An O-ring (28) is fitted in the first and the second O-ring seating grooves. A flange portion is adhered to the inner surface of the receiving groove at the upper portion. Multiple through holes are provided on the lower bottom surface. The outer peripheral surfaces of the through holes are projected. A housing is formed integrally with the annular projection. An oil layer is formed on an upper surface of the annular protrusion. Multiple compartments have a protruding surface protruding to the lower surface protrudes outward and includes a pressing assembly that is integrally formed with a hole on the upper side.
   USE - Control valve cap for coffee bean packaging casing.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide gas generated from the roasted very fresh beans can be easily discharged to the outside of the packaging casing, so that the packaging condition such as swelling can be prevented from being deformed and can prevent the deterioration of purchasing bath. After a certain period of time has elapsed, the control valve is closed using a cap to physically block the inflow of air and the emission of carbon dioxide to delay the rate of rancidity, so that the freshness of the coffee can be maintained for a long time, thus enhancing the deep aroma of coffee. The consumer maintains the freshness according to the taste for a long time. The film valve formed inside the control valve cap for the coffee bean packaging casing is physically brought into close contact with the gas discharge unit to maintain a complete sealing force even for long-term use by being physically brought into contact with the gas discharge unit. The produced carbon dioxide gas is smoothly discharged, and the inflow of air and foreign substances from the outside is fundamentally blocked to prevent deterioration and contamination of the beans contained in the package.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded perspective view of the control valve cap for the coffee bean packaging casing.
   Control valve (20)
   First O-ring seating groove (22)
   Cap receiving groove (23)
   O-ring (28)
   Gas discharge unit (40)

TI Preparation of polycarbonate involves copolymerizing epoxy compound and carbon dioxide in presence of catalytic system comprising catalyst comprising transition metal complex, and co-catalyst comprising quaternary ammonium compound.
AU BIAGINI P
   PO R
   BOGGIONI L
   VERONESE L
AE ENI SPA (ENIE-C)
   CNR (CNRC-Non-standard)
   ENI SPA (ENIE-C)
   CONSIGLIO NAZ DELLE RICERCHE (CNDR-C)
   ENI SPA (ENIE-C)
GA 202033165R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polycarbonate involves copolymerizing an epoxy compound and carbon dioxide in the presence of a catalytic system comprising catalyst(s) comprising a transition metal complex (I), and co-catalyst(s) comprising an ionic compound, preferably quaternary ammonium compound (II).
   USE - Preparation of polycarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables easy, economical and environmentally-friendly preparation of polycarbonate with high yield, and without using hazardous reagents, such as phosgene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of polycarbonate involves copolymerizing an epoxy compound and carbon dioxide in the presence of a catalytic system comprising catalyst(s) comprising a transition metal complex of formula (I), and co-catalyst(s) comprising an ionic compound, preferably quaternary ammonium compound of formula (II).
   M = metal atom chosen from chromium, manganese, iron, cobalt, nickel and aluminum, preferably chromium or cobalt;
   R1-R6 = optionally saturated, linear or branched 1-20C alkyl, preferably optionally saturated, linear or branched 1-12C alkyl optionally containing heteroatoms, or aryl, heteroaryl, cycloalkyl, or heterocyclyl (all optionally substituted);
   or (R2+R3), (R5+R6), (R7+R8), (R7+R10), (R8+R9), (R9+R10), (R7+R11), (R7+R12), (R10+R11), (R11+R10), (R13+R14), (R15+R16), (R17+R16), (R19+R20) = optionally saturated 2-12C aryl optionally containing heteroatoms, such as oxygen, sulfur, nitrogen, silicon, phosphorus and/or selenium, preferably oxygen and/or nitrogen;
   Y' = e.g. halide anion, or inorganic anion, or organic anion;
   Z = group chosen from groups of formulae (i)-(iii);
   R7-R12 = H, optionally saturated 1-20C alkyl optionally containing heteroatoms, preferably optionally saturated 1-12C alkyl optionally containing heteroatoms, aryl, heteroaryl, cycloalkyl, or heterocyclyl (all optionally substituted);
   E = atom chosen from phosphorus, arsenic, antimony and bismuth, preferably phosphorus;
   R13-R20 = H, optionally saturated 1-20C alkyl optionally containing heteroatoms, preferably optionally saturated 1-12C alkyl optionally containing heteroatoms, or aryl, heteroaryl optionally in cationic form, cycloalkyl, heterocyclyl optionally in cationic form (all optionally substituted), or trialkyl- or triaryl-silyl;
   W' = halo, such as chloro, bromo, fluoro or iodo, preferably chloro or bromo, or optionally saturated, linear or branched 1-20C alkoxy, preferably optionally saturated, linear or branched 2-20C alkoxy, optionally substituted aryloxy, or oxyamino;
   X = e.g. halide anion or inorganic anion;
   a = 0-4, preferably 1-3;
   b = 0-4, preferably 1-4;
   c = 0 or 1;
   a+b+c = 4;and
   n = 1-4, preferably 1 or 2.
   Full definitions are given in the DEFINITIONS (Full Definitions) Field. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the catalytic system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The epoxy compound is chosen from 1-20C alkylene oxide, optionally substituted with halogen atoms and/or alkoxy groups, 4-20C cycloalkylene oxide, optionally substituted with halogen atoms and/or alkoxy groups, and 8-20C styrene oxide, optionally substituted with halogen atoms, alkoxy groups, alkyl groups and/or aryl groups, preferably ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, butene oxide, pentene oxide, hexene oxide, octene oxide, decene oxide, dodecene oxide, tetradecene oxide, hexadecene oxide, octadecene oxide, butadiene monoxide, 1,2-epoxide-7-octene, epifluorohydrin, epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, isopropyl glycidyl ether, butyl glycidyl ether, tert-butyl glycidyl ether, 2-ethylhexyl glycidyl ether, allyl glycidyl ether, cyclopentene oxide, cyclohexene oxide, cyclooctene oxide, cyclododecene oxide, alpha -pinene oxide, 2,3-epoxinorbornene, limonene oxide, dieldrin, 2,3-epoxypropylbenzene, styrene oxide, phenylpropylene oxide, stilbene oxide, chlorostilbene oxide, dichlorostilbene oxide, 1,2-epoxy-3-phenoxypropane, benzyloxymethyl oxirane, glycidyl methyl phenyl ether, chlorophenyl-2,3-epoxypropyl ether, epoxy propyl methoxyphenyl ether, biphenyl glycidyl ether and/or glycidyl naphthyl ether, more preferably cyclohexene oxide or propylene oxide. The volume ratio of organic solvent with respect to epoxy compound is 0:100-99:1, preferably 0:100-90:1. At least one epoxy compound acts as a solvent. The molar ratio of catalytic system with respect to epoxy compound is 1:100 to 1:100000, preferably 1:1000 to 1:10000. The molar ratio of catalyst with respect to co-catalyst is 100:1 to 1:100, preferably 1:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The process is performed in the presence of organic solvent(s) chosen from aliphatic hydrocarbons, such as pentane, octane, decane and cyclohexane, aromatic hydrocarbons, such as benzene, toluene and xylene, and/or halogenated hydrocarbons, such as dichloromethane, chloroform, carbon tetrachloride, 1,1-dichloroethane, 1,2-dichloroethane, ethyl chloride, trichloroethane, 1-chloropropane, 2-chloropropane, 1-chlorobutane, 2-chlorobutane, 1-chloro-2-methylpropane, chlorobenzene and/or bromobenzene, preferably 1,2-dichloromethane, at a temperature of 20-250 degrees C, preferably 40-160 degrees C and at a pressure of 1-100 atm, preferably 2-60 atm for 30 minutes to 36 hours, preferably 3-24 hours. Preferred Composition: The polycarbonate comprises a polycarbonate comprising more than 95%, preferably equal to 100% carbonate bonds in a chain, and a polycarbonate/polyether copolymer comprising 15-90%, preferably 35-85% ether bonds in a chain, in a weight ratio of 1000:1 to 1:1000, preferably 100:1 to 1:100.

PN CN109401920-A
TI Controllable slope type system useful for culturing microalgae comprises e.g. a microalgae pretreatment tank, ramp, algae liquid collection and treatment tank, and microalgae cultivation device is arranged on the ramp.
AU WANG D
   CHEN T
   ZU T
   LUO G
AE UNIV HEXI (UHXI-C)
GA 2019226482
AB    NOVELTY - Controllable slope type system comprises a microalgae pretreatment tank, ramp, and algae liquid collection and treatment tank, where the microalgae pretreatment tank is provided with a nutrient solution and an accessing algae inside. A microalgae cultivation device is arranged on the ramp, and comprises a straight tube and a U-shaped tube, where the straight tube and the outer wall of the U-shaped tube are sleeved with a vacuum tube. The straight tube and the U-shaped tube are coated with an endothermic coating on the inner tube wall on one side of the slope, where the U-shaped tube is provided with a carbon dioxide intake valve. The beginning of the straight tube is provided with an inlet valve, and an outlet valve and a flow controller are arranged at the end of the straight tube. The outer wall of straight pipe is connected with an oxygen discharge pipe, and an ultrasonic generator is disposed on the right side of the microalgae cultivation device.
   USE - The system is useful for culturing microalgae (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: promotes the increase of the oil content in the microalgae; and greatly improves the conversion rate of the oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for culturing the microalgae using the system comprising (i) installing the microalgae culture device and the ultrasonic generator on the slope, (ii) then opening the inlet valve to introduce the algae solution to be cultured in the microalgae pretreatment tank into the inside of the straight tube, where the algae liquid is under the action of gravity, continuously flowing down the U-tube and the straight tube, opening the carbon dioxide intake valve provided on the U-shaped tube, conducive to the thorough mixing of algae and carbon dioxide, connecting the outer wall of the straight tube with an oxygen venting tube, discharging excess oxygen, applying heat-absorbing coating to the inner tube wall of the straight tube and the U-shaped tube on one side of the slope, absorbing solar energy to increase the temperature of algae fluid, and maintaining the algae liquid warm using vacuum tube, and (iii) when the algae liquid fills the straight tube and the U-shaped tube, aligning the source of the ultrasonic generator with the straight tube and the U-tube, then turning on the ultrasonic generator for 5-10 minutes, performing sonication, and continuously culturing the algae liquid in a microalgae culture machine for 18-32 hours.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the controllable slope type system for culturing microalgae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The straight tube, the U-shaped tube and the vacuum tube are all made of transparent glass or polyethylene material.

PT P
PN WO2019022666-A1
TI Composite material comprises ellipsoidal nanoparticles which comprises multiple of porous layers of aluminum oxide, and nickel oxide, where each layer has internal surface facing towards core of nanoparticle and outer face.
AU VEKSHA A
   GIANNIS A
   OH W D
   CHANG W
AE UNIV NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL (UYTU-C)
GA 201912238P
AB    NOVELTY - Composite material comprises ellipsoidal nanoparticles which comprises multiple of porous layers of aluminum (III) oxide, and nickel (II) oxide, where each layer has internal surface facing towards core of nanoparticle and outer surface facing towards exterior of nanoparticle. The each layer of aluminum (III) oxide is spaced apart from each adjacent layer by a gap. The nickel (II) oxide is homogeneously dispersed within the gaps between adjacent layers of aluminum (III) oxide and/or homogeneously dispersed over the surfaces of each layer of aluminum (III) oxide. The nickel (II) oxide in the composite material is in substantially amorphous form.
   USE - Composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has high water-gas shift activity and stable performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for steam reforming hydrocarbons with subsequent water-gas shift reaction, which involves contacting composite material with activating gaseous fluid flow which comprises hydrogen for first period of time at 500-900 degrees C to provide activated composite material, and contacting activated composite material with a reactive gaseous fluid flow comprising a hydrocarbon material and water, in form of steam at 500-1000 degrees C, where hydrocarbon material is catalytically converted into carbon monoxide and hydrogen gas through contact with the activated composite material and steam, and carbon monoxide subsequently undergoes water-gas shift reaction to provide carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas through contact with the activated composite material and steam;
   (2) a method for converting synthesis gas into carbon dioxide and hydrogen through a water-gas shift reaction, which involves contacting composite material with activating gaseous fluid flow comprising hydrogen for first period of time at 500-900 degrees C to provide an activated composite material, and contacting the activated composite material with a synthesis gas fluid flow comprising carbon monoxide, hydrogen and water, in the form of steam at 500-1000 degrees C to convert the carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas via the water-gas shift reaction; and
   (3) a method for manufacturing composite material, which involves adding precursor aluminum (III) oxide material to solution of nickel (II) salt in solvent to form mixture or vice versa, removing the solvent and then drying the resulting solid material for a first period of time at 50-150 degrees C to form dried material, and calcinating the dried material at 400-900 degrees C for second period of time to form composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nanoparticles of aluminum (III) oxide has nanocage structure. The innermost layer of aluminum (III) oxide defines the internal surface of the nanocage which is coated with nickel (II) oxide. The composite material further comprises an additive material which is selected from halides, nitrates, oxides, hydroxides and carbonates of alkali metals, oxides, hydroxides and carbonates of alkaline earth metals, oxides of transition metals, oxides of gallium. The hydrocarbon comprises chlorinated hydrocarbon. The precursor aluminum (III) oxide material is aluminum hydroxide and solvates with diameter of 0.1-10 mm. The solvent is selected from water, alcohol, ketone, and ether. The nickel (II) salt is selected from nickel nitrate, nickel chloride, nickel sulfate, nickel perchlorate, nickel bromide, nickel acetate, nickel acetylacetonate, and solvates. Preferred Condition: The total thickness of aluminum (III) oxide layers that form the nanocage is 1-10 nanometer. The diameter of aluminum (III) oxide nanoparticles is 5-50 nanometer. The Brunauer Emmett Teller surface area of composite material is 80-250 m2/g. The total pore volume of composite material is 0.1-0.6 mL/g. The bulk density of composite material is 0.5-1.5 g/mL. The diameter of aluminum (III) oxide layers is less than or equal to 22 nanometer. The average pore diameter of aluminum (III) oxide layers is 7-15 nanometer. The diameter of nanoparticles of nickel (II) oxide is 0.1-5 nanomter. The material has nickel 2p3/2 core level x-ray photoelectron spectra that only has peaks at about 856 electronvolt and a shake-up satellite peak at about 861 electronvolt. The material has x-ray diffraction pattern. The reactive gaseous fluid flow comprises hydrochloric acid. The reactive gaseous fluid flow is 10-10000 parts per million by volume. The calcination temperature is 500-700 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The amount of nickel (II) oxide is 1-30 wt.%. The amount of aluminum (III) oxide is 65-99 wt.% of the total weight of the composite material. The amount of nickel (II) oxide is 20-30 wt.% nickel (II) oxide. The amount of additive material is 0.1-5 wt.% of the total weight of the composite material. Preferred Process: The material is resistant to poison by hydrogen chloride when used as catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108967961-A
TI Granules used for enhancing human immunity, comprise Dendrobium officinale, American ginseng, honeysuckle, Cordyceps flower, snow fungus, pollen, mint leaves, pearl powder, rock sugar, honey, lotus root starch, maltodextrin and water.
AU XIE Z
AE GUANGXI CHISHENG AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A18136
AB    NOVELTY - Granules comprise 50-55 pts. wt. Dendrobium officinale, 10-12 pts. wt. American ginseng, 8-10 pts. wt. honeysuckle, 6-10 pts. wt. Cordyceps flower, 10-12 pts. wt. snow fungus, 3-6 pts. wt. pollen, 5-6 pts. wt. mint leaves, 5-8 pts. wt. pearl powder, 3-6 pts. wt. rock sugar, 5-8 pts. wt. honey, 5-6 pts. wt. lotus root starch, 10-12 pts. wt. beta -cyclodextrin, 15-20 pts. wt. maltodextrin and an appropriate amount of water.
   USE - The granules are useful for enhancing human immunity.
   ADVANTAGE - The granules have excellent mouthfeel and unique fragrance of Chinese herbal medicine, do not easily absorb moisture for a long time, and are easy to consume.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the granules, involving (a) placing D.officinale in a freezing chamber, freezing at temperature of -40 degrees C until the central temperature is -18 degrees C, drying in a vacuum freeze-drying machine for 10-12 hours, taking out D.officinale and grinding into powder to obtain D.officinale freeze-dried powder, (b) cutting honeysuckle, Cordyceps flower and American ginseng into small sections of 1-2 mm, uniformly mixing, placing in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank, adding ethanol solution to the tank, extracting at 25-28 degrees C under 18-19 MPa for 60-80 minutes, where the carbon dioxide flow rate is 80-120 ml/minute, and separating carbon dioxide and ethanol in the obtained extract solution to obtain an extract solution, (c) adding D.officinale freeze-dried powder obtained in step (a), pearl powder, and pollen to a high-energy nano-impact tank, introducing argon for protection and grinding for 4-5 hours to obtain ultrafine powder, (d) placing lotus root starch in a cooking pot, steaming, closing the steam valve when the temperature is increased to 85-90 degrees C, cutting mint leaves into filaments, mixing with snow fungus and boiling for 40-60 minutes, and adding rock sugar, honey and steamed lotus root starch and fully dissolving to obtain a puree, and (e) uniformly mixing the extract solution obtained in step (b), the ultrafine powder obtained in step (c) and the puree obtained in step (d) with beta -cyclodextrin and maltodextrin, preparing into granules, sieving using 20-40-mesh sieve, drying until the moisture content is less than 4%, sterilizing and packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The granules comprise 53 pts. wt. D.officinale, 11 pts. wt. American ginseng, 9 pts. wt. honeysuckle, 7 pts. wt. Cordyceps flower, 11 pts. wt. snow fungus, 5 pts. wt. pollen, 5.5 pts. wt. mint leaves, 5.5 pts. wt. pearl powder, 5 pts. wt. rock sugar, 7 pts. wt. honey, 5.5 pts. wt. lotus root starch, 11 pts. wt. beta -cyclodextrin, 16 pts. wt. maltodextrin and an appropriate amount of water. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves sieving D.officinale freeze-dried powder using a 40-60-mesh sieve. The step (b) involves adding 6-8 times 50-55 vol.% ethanol solution.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN107970935-A; CN107970935-B
TI Mesoporous alumina catalysts comprises mesoporous alumina is used as a carrier to support the active component of ferric oxide.
AU YAN B
   JIANG T
   GAO Y
   LIU D
AE UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
GA 2018352073
AB    NOVELTY - Mesoporous alumina catalysts comprises mesoporous alumina is used as a carrier to support the active component of ferric oxide, where the weight of the iron element is 2-12%, the mesoporous alumina catalyst with anchored iron oxide is formed, the catalyst has a 2D hexagonal mesoporous structure with an average specific surface area of 360-380 m2/g, an average pore volume of 0.65-0.8 cm3/g, and an average pore diameter of 5-6 nm.
   USE - The mesoporous alumina catalyst is useful for the dehydrogenation of 1-butene to 1,3-butadiene in carbon-dioxide oxidation.
   ADVANTAGE - The mesoporous alumina catalyst has highly ordered structure, uniform pore having a high specific surface area, large aperture and excellent heat stability and avoids agglomeration and sintering of active components so as to improve the activity and stability of the catalyst; and utilizes self-assembling method to prepare one-step synthesis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIMs are also included for:
   (1) preparing the mesoporous alumina catalyst, comprising dropping a solution of aluminum isopropoxide in ethanol into an ethanol solution of iron-containing F127 and stirring to form a uniformly dispersed system, drying mixed system to volatilize the solvent to obtain a pale yellow sample, grinding pale yellow sample and calcining, heating to 20-25 degrees C at a heating rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute and warming up to 700-800 degrees C, insulation roasting for 4-8 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature at 20-25 degrees C to obtain iron oxide-containing regular mesoporous alumina catalyst, where the aluminum isopropoxide in ethanol solution consists of aluminum isopropoxide, absolute ethanol, and concentrated nitric acid, the ratio of the mole fraction of aluminum isopropoxide, the volume fraction of absolute ethanol, and the volume fraction of concentrated nitric acid is (10-15):(15-20):(1.5-2), the mass percentage of concentrated nitric acid is 60-65 wt.%, the ethanol solution of iron containing F127 is composed of ferric nitrate nonahydrate, F127 and absolute ethanol, the ratio of mass fraction of hydrated ferric nitrate, mass fraction of F127 and volume fraction of absolute ethanol is (0.05-1.2):(1-1.5):(15-20), the F127 is a triblock copolymer poly ethylene oxide-polyoxypropylene-polyoxyethylene; and
   (2) dehydrogenation of 1-butene to 1,3-butadiene in carbon dioxide oxidation using mesoporous alumina catalyst comprises the molar ratio of 1-butene to carbon dioxide of the starting material is 1:(2-10) and the reaction temperature is 500-700 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ethanol solution of aluminum isopropoxide consists of aluminum isopropoxide, anhydrous ethanol, and concentrated nitric acid. The ratio of the mole fraction of aluminum isopropoxide, the volume fraction of absolute ethanol, and the volume fraction of concentrated nitric acid is (10-12):(15-18):1.5, the ethanol solution of iron containing F127 is composed of ferric nitrate nonahydrate, F127 and anhydrous ethanol, the mass fraction of ferric nitrate nonahydrate, the mass fraction of F127 and anhydrous ethanol is (0.07-1.08):(1.2-1.5):(18-20). Preferred Method: The roasting conditions comprises heating from 20-25 degrees C at the rate of rate of 1-2 degrees C/minute to 700-750 degrees C, maintaining heat and roasting for 5-6 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature to 20-25 degrees C and during roasting air atmosphere is adopted. The mixed system is dried at 50-60 degrees C for 36-48 hours to volatilize the solvent. The method comprises uniformly dropping ethanol solution of aluminum isopropoxide into the ethanol solution of iron containing F127 within 30-60 minutes, stirring for 6-10 hours, preferably 8-10 hours to form dispersion and uniformly mixing the system. The reaction pressure of the dehydrogenation reaction is normal pressure (i.e. a standard atmospheric pressure) and the mass-space velocity based on 1-butene is 1.5-7.5/ hour and the amount of catalyst is 0.1-0.2 g. The molar ratio of 1-butene to carbon dioxide is 1:(5-8) and the reaction temperature is 550-650 degrees C.

TI Producing fermentation product e.g. ethanol or            isoprene from gaseous stream, by obtaining gaseous            stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide,            generating compressed bioreactor tail gas stream, and            recycling tail gas stream.
AU CONRADO R J
   SIMPSON S D
   MARTIN M E
   BROMLEY J C
   YOUNGS D E
   ROSIN R R
AE LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
GA 2022C8947X
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated process for producing at least one                fermentation product from a gaseous stream involves                (i) (a) obtaining a first gaseous stream comprising                hydrogen and a second gaseous stream comprising                carbon dioxide (CO2), (b) producing a CO enriched                exit stream, (c) passing the CO enriched exit                stream to a bioreactor having a culture of one or                more Cl-fixing bacterium and fermenting to produce                at least one fermentation product stream and a                bioreactor tail gas stream, (d) compressing the                bioreactor tail gas stream to generate a compressed                bioreactor tail gas stream, (e) generating a                compressed treated bioreactor tail gas stream, (f)                recycling the compressed treated bioreactor tail                gas stream, and (g) recycling a second portion of                the compressed bioreactor tail gas stream to                combine with the CO enriched exit stream or to the                bioreactor.
   USE - The integrated process is useful for producing                fermentation product from gaseous stream. The                fermentation product is chosen from ethanol,                acetate, butanol, butyrate, 2,3-butanediol,                lactate, butene, butadiene, methyl ethyl ketone,                ethylene, acetone, isopropanol, lipids,                3-hydroxypropionate, isoprene, fatty acids,                2-butanol, 1,2-propanediol, hexanol, octanol, or                1-propanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated process for producing at least one                fermentation product from a gaseous stream involves                (i) (a) obtaining a first gaseous stream comprising                hydrogen and a second gaseous stream comprising                carbon dioxide (CO2), (b) passing at least a                portion of the first gaseous stream and at least a                portion of the second gaseous stream to a CO2 to CO                conversion system operated under conditions to                produce a CO enriched exit stream, (c) passing the                CO enriched exit stream to a bioreactor having a                culture of one or more Cl-fixing bacterium and                fermenting to produce at least one fermentation                product stream and a bioreactor tail gas stream,                (d) compressing the bioreactor tail gas stream to                generate a compressed bioreactor tail gas stream,                (e) passing at least a first portion of the                compressed bioreactor tail gas stream to (i) a gas                desulfurization and or acid gas removal unit, (ii)                a gas component removal unit, or (iii) both the gas                desulfurization and or acid gas removal unit and                the gas component removal unit, to generate a                compressed treated bioreactor tail gas stream, (f)                recycling the compressed treated bioreactor tail                gas stream, (i) to combine with the first gaseous                stream, the second gaseous stream, or their                combination, (ii) to the CO2 to CO conversion                system, (iii) to combine with the CO enriched exit                stream, or (iv) their combination, and (g)                recycling a second portion of the compressed                bioreactor tail gas stream to combine with the CO                enriched exit stream or to the bioreactor, (ii) (a)                obtaining a first gaseous stream comprising                hydrogen and a second gaseous stream comprising                CO2, (b) compressing at least a portion of the                first gaseous stream, at least a portion of the                second gaseous stream or their combination in a                first compressor to generate a compressed first                gaseous stream, a compressed second gaseous stream,                and or a compressed combination first gaseous                stream and second gaseous stream, (c) treating (i)                at least a portion of the first gaseous stream or                the compressed first gaseous stream, or both, and                at least a portion of the second gaseous stream or                the compressed second gaseous stream, or both, or                (ii) the compressed combination first gaseous                stream and second gaseous stream, in a gas                treatment zone comprising a gas component removal                unit, a gas desulfurization/acid gas removal unit,                or both to generate a treated stream, (d)                converting CO2 in at least a first portion of the                treated stream to form CO in a CO2 to CO conversion                system operated under conditions to produce a CO                enriched exit stream, (e) passing the CO enriched                exit stream to a bioreactor having a culture of one                or more Cl-fixing bacterium and fermenting to                produce at least one fermentation product stream                and a bioreactor tail gas stream, and (f) recycling                the tail gas stream to the first compressor, the                gas treatment zone, the CO2 to CO conversion                system, the first gaseous stream, the second                gaseous stream, or the combination of the first                gaseous stream and the second gaseous stream, or                (iii) (a) obtaining a first gaseous stream                comprising hydrogen and a second gaseous stream                comprising CO2, (b) passing at least a portion of                the second gaseous stream and a portion of the                first gaseous stream to a CO2 to CO conversion                system operated under conditions to produce a CO                enriched exit stream, (c) passing at least a                portion of the first gaseous stream comprising                hydrogen and the CO enriched exit stream to a                bioreactor having a culture of one or more                Cl-fixing bacterium and fermenting to produce at                least one fermentation product stream and a                bioreactor tail gas stream, (d) compressing the                bioreactor tail gas stream to generate a compressed                bioreactor tail gas stream, (e) passing at least a                first portion of the compressed bioreactor tail gas                stream, to (i) a gas desulfurization and or acid                gas removal unit, (ii) a gas component removal                unit, or (iii) both the gas desulfurization and or                acid gas removal unit and the gas component removal                unit, to generate a compressed treated bioreactor                tail gas stream, (f) recycling the compressed                treated bioreactor tail gas stream, (i) to combine                with the first gaseous stream, the second gaseous                stream, or their combination, (ii) to the CO2 to CO                conversion system, (iii) to combine with the CO                enriched exit stream, or (iv) their combination,                and (g) recycling a second portion of the                compressed bioreactor tail gas stream to combine                with the CO enriched exit stream or to the                bioreactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (i) further                  involves combining at least a second portion of                  the first gaseous stream, at least a second                  portion of the second gaseous stream or their                  combination with the CO enriched exit stream,                  passing at least at least a second portion of the                  first gaseous stream, at least a second portion                  of the second gaseous stream or their combination                  to the bioreactor, compressing any portions of                  the first gaseous stream, the second gaseous                  stream or their combinations, controlling the                  relative amounts of the first portion of the                  compressed tail gas stream and the second portion                  of the compressed tail gas stream using a control                  valve, and passing at least a portion of the tail                  gas stream to a tail gas CO2 to CO conversion                  system chosen from a reverse water gas shift                  unit, a thermo-catalytic conversion unit, a                  partial combustion unit, a plasma conversion                  unit, a gasification unit, or a reforming unit,                  to generate a CO enriched effluent stream and                  recycling the second CO enriched effluent stream                  to the bioreactor. The CO enriched exit stream                  comprises a H2:CO:CO2 molar ratio of 5:1:1,                  4.5:1:1, 4.33:1:1, 3:1:1, 2:1:1, 1:1:1; or 1:3:1.                  The CO2 to CO conversion system comprises at                  least one of a reverse water gas shift unit, a                  thermo-catalytic conversion unit, a partial                  combustion unit, a reforming unit, or a plasma                  conversion unit. The first gaseous stream                  comprising hydrogen is produced by a hydrogen                  production source comprising at least one of a                  water electrolyzer, a hydrocarbon reforming                  source, a hydrogen purification source, a solid                  biomass gasification source, a solid waste                  gasification source, a coal gasification source,                  a hydrocarbon gasification source, a methane                  pyrolysis source, a refinery tail gas production                  source, a plasma reforming reactor, partial                  oxidation reactor, or their combination. The                  second gaseous stream comprising CO2 is produced                  by a gas production source comprising at least                  one of a sugar-based ethanol production source, a                  first generation corn-ethanol production source,                  a second generation corn-ethanol production                  source, a sugarcane ethanol production source, a                  cane sugar ethanol production source, a sugar                  beet ethanol production source, a molasses                  ethanol production source, a wheat ethanol                  production source, a grain based ethanol                  production source, a starch based ethanol                  production source, a cellulosic based ethanol                  production source, a cement production source, a                  methanol synthesis source, an olefin production                  source, a steel production source, a ferroalloy                  production source, a refinery tail gas production                  source, a post combustion gas production source,                  a biogas production source, a landfill production                  source, an ethylene oxide production source, a                  methanol production source, an ammonia production                  source, mined CO2 production source, natural gas                  processing production source, a gasification                  source, an organic waste gasification source,                  direct air capture, or their combination. The                  step (ii) further involves combining at least a                  portion of the treated stream, the first gaseous                  stream, the second gaseous stream, the                  combination of the first gaseous stream and the                  second gaseous stream, the compressed first                  gaseous stream, the compressed second gaseous                  stream, or the compressed combination first                  gaseous stream and second gaseous stream, with                  the CO enriched exit stream. The step (ii)                  further involves passing at least a portion of                  the tail gas stream to a tail gas CO2 to CO                  conversion system chosen from reverse water gas                  shift unit, a thermo-catalytic conversion unit, a                  partial combustion unit, a plasma conversion                  unit, a gasification unit, or a reforming unit,                  to generate a CO enriched effluent stream and                  recycling the second CO enriched effluent stream                  to the bioreactor. The gas treatment zone further                  comprises a deoxygenation unit, a catalytic                  hydrogenation unit, an adsorption unit, a thermal                  oxidizer, or their combination. The CO enriched                  exit stream comprises hydrogen, and the process                  further involves separating hydrogen from the CO                  enriched exit stream and recycling the separated                  hydrogen to combine with the tail gas stream or                  to the compressor. The step (ii) further involves                  compressing the remainder of the CO enriched exit                  stream after the separation of hydrogen. The tail                  gas stream comprises methane, and the process                  further involves passing a portion of the tail                  gas stream to a methane conversion unit to                  generate a methane conversion unit effluent and                  combining the methane conversation unit effluent                  with the tail gas stream. The step (ii) further                  involves generating a stream comprising oxygen                  from an oxygen source and passing the stream                  comprising oxygen to the methane conversion unit,                  and passing a second gaseous stream comprising                  hydrogen from the hydrogen source to the                  bioreactor or to combine with the CO enriched                  exit stream, passing a second gaseous stream                  comprising CO2 from the CO2 source to the                  bioreactor or to combine with the CO enriched                  exit stream. The combining of the second gaseous                  stream comprising hydrogen from the hydrogen                  source with the CO enriched exit stream, or the                  combining of the second gaseous stream comprising                  CO2 from the CO2 source with the CO enriched exit                  stream, or both utilize mixing in a mixer. The                  ratio of the second gaseous stream comprising                  hydrogen from the hydrogen source to the CO                  enriched exit stream entering the bioreactor is                  greater than 0:1-4:1. The CO2 to CO conversion                  system comprises a fired heater having burners,                  and the tail gas stream is recycled at least to                  the burners of the fired heater. The CO2 to CO                  conversion system comprises a steam generator                  producing steam, or a water knockout unit                  generating a water stream, or both. The step (ii)                  further involves passing a portion of CO enriched                  exit stream to an inoculator reactor, a buffer                  tank, or both. The step (iii) further involves                  passing at least another portion of first gaseous                  stream comprising hydrogen to the CO2 to CO                  conversion system, compressing any portions of                  the first gaseous stream or the second gaseous                  stream, controlling the relative amounts of the                  first portion of the compressed tail gas stream                  and the second portion of the compressed tail gas                  stream using a control valve, and passing at                  least a portion of the tail gas stream to a tail                  gas CO2 to CO conversion system chosen from a                  reverse water gas shift unit, a thermo-catalytic                  conversion unit, a partial combustion unit, a                  plasma conversion unit, a gasification unit, or a                  reforming unit, to generate a CO enriched                  effluent stream and recycling the second CO                  enriched effluent stream to the bioreactor.                  Preferred Microorganism: The Cl fixing bacterium                  is Clostridium autoethanogenum, C.ljungdahlii, or                  C.ragsdalei.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Determining efficacy of treatment for organic            acidemia in subject by administering to subject,            composition having isotope-labeled propionate,            collecting breath samples, and measuring carbon dioxide            ratio of breath samples.
AU VENDITTI C P
   MANOLI I
AE US DEPT HEALTH & HUMAN SERVICES (USSH-C)
   MANOLI I (MANO-Individual)
   VENDITTI C P (VEND-Individual)
   US DEPT HEALTH & HUMAN SERVICES (USSH-C)
   VENDITTI C P (VEND-Individual)
   MANOLI I (MANO-Individual)
   VENDITTI C P (VEND-Individual)
   MANOLI I (MANO-Individual)
   US DEPT HEALTH & HUMAN SERVICES (USSH-C)
GA 201940588F
AB    NOVELTY - Efficacy of treatment for organic acidemia in                subject, is determined by, prior to treatment:                administering to subject, composition having                isotope-labeled propionate; collecting breath                samples; measuring carbon dioxide (13CO2/12CO2)                ratio of breath samples; determining 1st                isotope-labeled propionate oxidation rate based on                measured 13CO2/12CO2ratio and measured carbon                dioxide (CO2) production rate; and following                treatment: administering to subject, composition                having isotope-labeled propionate; collecting                breath samples; (vii) measuring 13CO2/12CO2ratio of                breath samples; determining 2nd isotope-labeled                propionate oxidation rate based on measured                13CO2/12CO2ratio and measured CO2production rate;                and comparing 1st isotope-labeled propionate                oxidation rate with 2nd isotope-labeled propionate                oxidation rate. An increase in 2nd isotope-labeled                propionate oxidation rate compared to 1st                isotope-labeled propionate oxidation rate indicates                efficacy of treatment.
   USE - The methods are useful for determining                efficacy of treatment for organic acidemia in a                subject; for treating organic acidemia in subject;                for improving hepatic enzyme activity in subject                having organic acidemia; for diagnosing hepatic                mitochondrial dysfunction in subject suffering from                organic acidemia; and for determining the efficacy                of treatment for metabolic disorder in subject (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Better methods are provided for monitoring                and/or determining the efficacy of therapies, for                MMA, PA, cobalamin metabolic disorders and other                organic acidemias in a subject. The real time                methods for monitoring and/or determining the                efficacy of a treatment provide tremendous                practical advantages for care providers because the                efficacy of treatment can be tested non-invasively,                at the patient's bed-side, with results provided in                &#8804; 2 hours.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   treating an organic acidemia in a subject,                which involves: prior to a treatment: (i)                administering to the subject a composition having                isotope-labeled propionate; (ii) collecting breath                samples from the subject at time points after step                (i); (iii) measuring the 13CO2/12CO2ratio of the                breath samples from step (ii); (iv) determining a                1st isotope-labeled propionate oxidation rate based                on the measured 13CO2/12CO2ratio of step (iii) and                measured CO2production rate; administering a                treatment to the subject to improve compromised                hepatic enzyme activity associated with the organic                acidemia after step (ii); following the treatment:                (v) administering to the subject a composition                having isotope-labeled propionate; (vi) collecting                breath samples from the subject at time points                after step (v); (vii) measuring the                13CO2/12CO2ratio of the breath samples from step                (vi); (viii) determining a 2nd isotope-labeled                propionate oxidation rate based on the measured                13CO2/12CO2ratio of step (vii) and measured                CO2production rate; discontinuing, altering, or                continuing the treatment based on the 2nd                isotope-labeled propionate oxidation rate after                treatment compared to the 1st isotope-labeled                propionate oxidation rate before the                treatment;
   improving hepatic enzyme activity in a subject                having an organic acidemia, which involves prior to                a treatment: (i) administering to the subject a                composition having sodium isotope-labeled                propionate; (ii) collecting breath samples from the                subject at time points after step (i); (iii)                measuring 13CO2/12CO2ratio of the breath samples                from step (ii); (iv) determining a 1st                isotope-labeled propionate oxidation rate based on                the measured 13CO2/12CO2ratio of step (iii) and                measured CO2 production; administering a treatment                to the subject to improve compromised hepatic                enzyme activity associated with the organic                acidemia after step (ii); following the treatment:                (v) orally administering to the subject a                composition having isotope-labeled propionate; (vi)                collecting breath samples from the subject at time                points after step (v); (vii) measuring                13CO2/12CO2ratio of the breath samples from step                (vi); (viii) determining a 2nd isotope-labeled                propionate oxidation rate based on the measured                13CO2/12CO2ratio of step (vii) and measured                CO2production rate; discontinuing, altering, or                continuing the treatment based on the 2nd                isotope-labeled propionate oxidation rate after                treatment compared to the 1st isotope-labeled                propionate oxidation rate before the                treatment;
   diagnosing hepatic mitochondrial dysfunction                in a subject suffering from an organic acidemia,                which involves: (i) administering to the subject a                composition having isotope-labeled propionate; (ii)                collecting breath samples from the subject at time                points after step (i); (iii) measuring the                13CO2/12CO2ratio of the breath samples from step                (ii); (iv) determining a 1st isotope-labeled                propionate oxidation rate based on the measured                13CO2/12CO2ratio of step (iii) and measured                CO2production rate; where a decrease in                isotope-labeled propionate oxidation rate compared                to a predetermined standard level indicates that                the subject is suffering from hepatic mitochondrial                dysfunction;
   a kit comprising a predetermined amount of                sodium 1-13C propionate; breath collection bags;                guidance for testing before, during, and/or after                treatment; and guidance for interpreting the test                results; and
   determining the efficacy of a treatment for a                metabolic disorder in a subject, which involves:                prior to the treatment: (i) administering to the                subject a composition comprising a                1-13C-isotopomer; (ii) collecting breath samples                from the subject at time points after step (i);                (iii) measuring the 13CO2/12CO2ratio of the breath                samples from step (ii); (iv) determining a 1st                1-13C-isotopomer oxidation rate based on the                measured 13CO2/12CO2ratio of step (iii) and                measured CO2production rate; following the                treatment: (v) administering to the subject a                composition comprising a 1-13C-isotopomer; (vi)                collecting breath samples from the subject at time                points after step (v); (vii) measuring the                13CO2/12CO2ratio of the breath samples from step                (vi); (viii) determining a 2nd 1-13C-isotopomer                oxidation rate based on the measured                13CO2/12CO2ratio of step (vii) and measured                CO2production rate; and comparing the 1st                1-13C-isotopomer oxidation rate with the 2nd                1-13C-isotopomer oxidation rate, where an increase                in the 2nd 1-13C-isotopomer oxidation rate compared                to the 1st 1-13C-isotopomer oxidation rate                indicates efficacy of the treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: Alternatively,                  determining the efficacy of treatment for organic                  acidemia in subject, involves: following the                  treatment: (i) administering to the subject a                  composition having isotope-labeled propionate;                  (ii) collecting breath samples from the subject                  at time points after step (i); (iii) measuring                  the 13CO2/12CO2ratio of the breath samples from                  step (ii); (iv) determining a 1st isotope-labeled                  propionate oxidation rate based on the measured                  13CO2/12CO2ratio of step (iii) and measured                  CO2production rate, comparing the 1st                  isotope-labeled propionate oxidation rate with a                  predetermined rate, where an increase in the 1st                  isotope-labeled propionate oxidation rate                  compared to the predetermined rate indicates                  efficacy of the treatment. Determining efficacy                  of treatment for organic acidemia in subject,                  involves: prior to the treatment: (i)                  administering to the subject a composition having                  isotope- labeled propionate; (ii) collecting a                  1st breath sample from the subject with a                  disposable breath collection kit a 1st duration                  after step (i); (iii) measuring a 1st                  13CO2/12CO2ratio of the 1st breath sample;                  administering the treatment on the subject after                  step (ii); following the treatment: (iv)                  administering to the subject a composition having                  isotope-labeled propionate; (v) collecting a 2nd                  breath from the subject sample with a disposable                  breath collection kit the 1st duration after step                  (iv); (vi) measuring a 2nd 13CO2/12CO2ratio of                  the 2nd breath sample; comparing the 1st                  13CO2/12CO2ratio with the 2nd 13CO2/12CO2ratio,                  where an increase in the 2nd 13CO2/12CO2ratio                  compared to the 1st 13CO2/12CO2ratio indicates                  efficacy of the treatment. Determining the                  efficacy of treatment for organic acidemia in                  subject, involves: after the treatment: (i)                  administering an isotope-labeled metabolite to                  the subject where the isotope-labeled metabolite                  is 1-13C-propionate, 1-13C-glycine, or                  1-13C-methionine; (ii) measuring a level of an                  isotope-labeled product of the isotope-labeled                  metabolite in exhaled breath of the subject                  following administration of the isotope-labeled                  metabolite; and (iii) comparing the measured                  level of isotope-labeled product of the                  isotope-labeled metabolite in the subject to a                  predetermined level; where an increase in the                  measured level of isotope-labeled product                  compared to the predetermined level indicates                  efficacy of the treatment. Determining the                  efficacy of treatment for organic acidemia in                  subject, involves: prior to the treatment: (i)                  measuring CO2production rate of the subject; (ii)                  administering to the subject a composition having                  isotope-labeled propionate; (iii) collecting                  breath samples from the subject at time points                  after step (ii); (iv) measuring the                  13CO2/12CO2ratio of the breath samples from step                  (iii); (v) determining a 1st isotope-labeled                  propionate oxidation rate based on the measured                  13CO2/12CO2ratio of step (iv) and the measured                  CO2production rate of step (i); following the                  treatment: (vi) measuring CO2production rate of                  the subject; (vii) administering to the subject a                  composition having isotope-labeled propionate;                  (viii) collecting breath samples from the subject                  at time points after step (v); (ix) measuring the                  13CO2/12CO2ratio of the breath samples from step                  (viii); (x) determining a 2nd isotope-labeled                  propionate oxidation rate based on the measured                  13CO2/12CO2ratio of step (ix) and the measured                  CO2production rate measured of step (vi); and                  comparing the 1st isotope-labeled propionate                  oxidation rate with the 2nd isotope-labeled                  propionate oxidation rate, where an increase in                  the 2nd isotope- labeled propionate oxidation                  rate compared to the 1st isotope-labeled                  propionate oxidation rate indicates efficacy of                  the treatment. Determining the efficacy of                  treatment for metabolic disorder in subject,                  involves: following the treatment: (i)                  administering to the subject a composition                  comprising a 1-13C-isotopomer; (ii) collecting                  breath samples from the subject at time points                  after step (i); (iii) measuring the                  13CO2/12CO2ratio of the breath samples from step                  (ii); (iv) determining a 1st 1-13C-isotopomer                  oxidation rate based on the measured                  13CO2/12CO2ratio of step (iii) and measured                  CO2production rate, comparing the 1st                  1-13C-isotopomer oxidation rate with a                  predetermined rate, where an increase in the 1st                  l-13C-isotopomer oxidation rate compared to the                  predetermined rate indicates efficacy of the                  treatment. Treatment is a liver-directed                  treatment. Treatment involves administering to                  the subject a liver-directed gene transfer                  vector. It is liver transplantation or combined                  liver and kidney transplantation. The treatment                  is selected from gene therapy, cell therapy,                  small molecules, enzyme specific chaperonins,                  engineered microbes/microbiome, mRNA therapy,                  enzyme replacement therapy, genome editing,                  read-through agents, stem cell therapies,                  chaperones, enzyme replacement therapy (ERT), or                  any other processes that could improve                  methylmalonyl-coenzyme A (CoA) mutase (MUT) or                  propionyl- CoA (PCC) activity or propionate                  oxidation or associated mitochondrial                  dysfunction. The organic acidemia is selected                  from methylmalonic acidemia (MMA), propionic                  acidemia (PA), isovaleric acidemia, glutaric                  aciduria type 1 (GA1), &#946; -ketothiolase                  deficiency (BKT), 3-methylcrotonyl-CoA                  carboxylase deficiency (3-MCC),                  3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA lyase deficiency                  (HMG), 3-methylglutaconic acidemia or                  3-methylglutaconyl-CoA hydratase deficiency                  (MGA), D-2 hydroxyglutaric aciduria (D2-HGA),                  isobutyryl- CoA dehydrogenase deficiency                  3-hydroxyisobutyric aciduria (ICBD),                  L-2-hydroxy-glutaricaciduria (L2HGA), malonyl-CoA                  decarboxylase deficiency also known as malonic                  acidemia (MA), multiple carboxylase deficiency                  (MCD), holocarboxylase synthetase, and                  3-hydroxyisobutyryl-CoA hydrolase deficiency                  (HIBCH). It is methylmalonic acidemia or                  propionic acidemia. It is a disorder of                  propionate metabolism or a cobalamin metabolic                  and transport disorder causing MUT deficiency.                  The disorder of propionate metabolism is caused                  by isolated methylmalonyl-CoA mutase due to                  (MUT), MMAA, MMAB, MMADHC deficiency, or mut,                  cblA, cblB, cblD variant 2 classes of MMA. The                  cobalamin metabolic and transport disorders are                  selected from the patients with MMACHC, MMADHC,                  LMBRD1, ABCD4, TC2, CD320, AMN deficiency, cblC,                  cblD, cblF, cblJ, TCBLR and Imerslund-Graesbeck                  forms of combined MMAemia-hyperhomocysteinemia.                  The organic acidemia is a disorder of propionate                  metabolism causing PCC deficiency. The disorder                  of propionate metabolism is caused by PCC due to                  mutations in PCCA or PCCB. CO2production rate in                  step (iv) is measured by an indirect calorimetry                  cart on the same day prior to step (i), where the                  CO2production rate in step (viii) is measured by                  an indirect calorimetry cart on the same day                  prior to step. The metabolic disorder is selected                  from maple syrup urine disease (MSUD),                  phenylketonuria (PKU), biopterin recycling                  defects, fatty acid oxidation disorders                  (FAOD)-medium chain (MCAD), fatty acid oxidation                  disorders (FOAD)long chain (LCAD), fatty acid                  oxidation disorders (FOAD)-very long chain                  (VLCAD), fatty acid oxidation disorders-long                  chain hydroxylacyl-CoA dehydrogenase (TFP,                  LCHAD), glycogen storage disorders (GSD1,3),                  peroxisomal disorders, multiple acyl-CoA                  dehydrogenase deficiency (MADD), mitochondrial                  disorders, including complex 1, 2, 3 and 4,                  mitochondrial disorders, DNA depletion syndromes,                  pyruvate dehydrogenase deficiency, Krebs cycle                  enzyme defects, non-ketotic hyperglycinemia,                  non-alcoholic liver disease, galactosemia (GALT),                  tyrosinemia, glutaric acidemia type I (GCDH),                  isovaleric acidemia, fatty acid oxidation                  disorder-carnitine transport and metabolism                  (CPT1, CPT2, CTD), and 3-MCC deficiency.                  Preferred Components: The enzyme is selected from                  methylmalonyl-CoA mutase, propionyl CoA                  carboxylase, isovaleryl-CoA dehydrogenase,                  glutaryl CoA dehydrogenase, &#946;                  -ketothiolase, 3-methylcrotonyl-CoA carboxylase,                  3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA lyase,                  3-methylglutaconyl-CoA hydratase, isobutyryl-CoA                  dehydrogenase, malonyl-CoA decarboxylase,                  multiple carboxylase, and 3-hydroxyisobutyryl-CoA                  hydrolase. The guidance for testing comprises                  instructions for directing the subject to collect                  breath samples at predetermined time intervals.                  The kit further comprises a therapeutic agent for                  treating an organic acidemia. It also comprises                  guidance for discontinuing, altering, or                  continuing the therapeutic agent based on the                  test results.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The isotope-labeled                  propionate is sodium 1-carbon isotope                  (13C)-propionate. The 1-13C-isotopomer is                  selected from 1-13C-leucine, 1-13C-isoleucine,                  1-13C-valine, 1-13C-alphaketoisocaproic acid,                  1-13C-phenylalanine, 1-13C-octanoate,                  1-13C-palmitate, 1-13C-glucose,                  1-13C-docosahexaenoic acid, 1-13C-phytanic acid,                  1-13C-lysine, 1-13C-tryptophan, 1-13C-methionine,                  1-13C-pyruvate, 1-13C-acetate, 1-13C-glycine,                  1-13C-methionine, 1-13C-alphaketoisocaproic acid,                  1-13C-galactose, 1-13C-tyrosine, and                  1-13C-propionate.
   ACTIVITY - Metabolic; Hepatotropic. No biological data                given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The isotope-labeled propionate is administered                in the amount of at most 10 &#956; mol/kg, e.g.                1-8 &#956; mol/kg, 1-7 &#956; mol/kg, or                4-6 &#956; mol/kg. The composition having                isotope-labeled propionate is orally administered,                or via gastric route (all claimed).
