PT P
PN WO2022122817-A1
TI Producing fermentation product, involves reducing carbon dioxide to 1C compound, contacting portion of 1C compound with culture comprising methylotrophic microorganism, and fermenting 1C compound with methylotrophic microorganism to obtain fermentation product further produces carbon dioxide.
AU VANLERBERGHE B M
   DE BRUYN F B A
   HOFLACK L M C
   GHEERAERT J H C
AE CALIDRIS BIO (CALI-Non-standard)
GA 2022811319
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a fermentation product, involves (a) reducing carbon dioxide to a 1C compound, (b) contacting at least a portion of the 1C compound with a culture comprising a methylotrophic microorganism, and (c) fermenting 1C compound with the methylotrophic microorganism to obtain the fermentation product further produces carbon dioxide, which is at least partially recovered and recycled to the reducing step.
   USE - The method is used for producing a fermentation product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method develops more efficient, synergistic efficiency and sustainable methods for large-scale fermentative production of high value organic chemicals, and reduces emission of greenhouse gases, such as carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus comprising a reduction vessel for reducing carbon dioxide to a 1C compound, a fermentation vessel for culturing a methylotrophic microorganism and connected to the first vessel via a duct that allows to transport the 1C compound to the fermentation vessel, a carbon dioxide recovering unit connected to the reduction and fermentation vessels that allows to recover at least part of the carbon dioxide produced in the fermentation vessel and to recycle the recovered carbon dioxide to the reduction vessel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The fermentation product is chosen from a protein (preferred), an enzyme, an antibiotic, a chemical, an amino acid, a plant biostimulant, a growth enhancer, a probiotic, a prebiotic, a biofertilizer, a food, a feed, a vitamin, a lipid, a bioplastic, a polysaccharide, biomass, a bioceutical or a pharmaceutical. The fermentation product comprises a carbon backbone which is five carbons or longer. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide reduction process co-generates oxygen, where the oxygen is used to maintain aerobic conditions in the fermentation process. The carbon dioxide reduction process involves performing electrolysis of water to oxygen and hydrogen, where the hydrogen is used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide and the oxygen is used to maintain aerobic conditions in the fermentation process. The carbon dioxide reduction process involves performing electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide. The method involves performing electrolysis of water to hydrogen and oxygen, where the hydrogen is used for the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol, the oxygen is used to maintain aerobic conditions during the fermentation of the methanol with the methylotrophic microorganism and where the fermentation produces carbon dioxide which is partially recovered and recycled to the hydrogenation process. The method involves performing electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to methanol, where the oxygen is co-generated, the oxygen is used to maintain aerobic conditions during the fermentation of the methanol with the methylotrophic microorganism and the fermentation produces carbon dioxide which is partially recovered and recycled to the electrochemical reduction process. The method involves performing electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to methanol, where the oxygen is co-generated and the oxygen is used to maintain aerobic conditions during the fermentation of the methanol with the methylotrophic microorganism and the fermentation produces carbon dioxide which is partially recovered and recycled to the electrochemical reduction process. Preferred Microorganism: The methylotrophic microorganism is chosen from Methylomonas, Methylobacter, Methylococcus,Methylosinus, Methylocystis, Methylomicrobium, Methanomonas, Methylophilus, Methylobacillus, Methylobacterium, Hyphomicrobium, Xanthobacter, Bacillus, Paracoccus, Nocardia,Arthrobacter, Rhodopseudomonas, Pseudomonas, Candida,Hansenula, Pichia,Torulopsisand Rhodotorula.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The apparatus further comprises an electrolysis vessel for electrolysis of water, where the electrolysis vessel comprises a duct to transport hydrogen generated from water electrolysis to the reduction vessel, and a duct to transport oxygen generated from water electrolysis to the fermentation vessel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 1C compound is soluble in water. The 1C compound is chosen from methanol (preferred), formaldehyde, formic acid and/or formate.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN JP2021078463-A
TI Carbon dioxide supply apparatus for discharging the combustion gas containing carbon dioxide in a cultivation room, such as a greenhouse, comprises combustor that generates combustion gas, and control unit is used for stopping the combustor.
AU ZUSHI Y
   NAKAMOTO T
AE NORITSU KK (NOTS-C)
GA 202157483D
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) comprises combustor that generates combustion gas containing carbon dioxide by combustion. The combustor is operated when the carbon dioxide concentration in the cultivation room is equal to or less than the lower limit concentration. A control unit (151) is used for stopping the combustor when the carbon dioxide concentration in the cultivation chamber is equal to or higher than the upper limit concentration. Multiple concentration zones are provided between the upper limit concentration and the lower limit concentration. The control unit controls the combustor so that the output of the combustor becomes lower as the concentration band has a higher carbon dioxide concentration.
   USE - Carbon dioxide supply apparatus for discharging the combustion gas containing carbon dioxide in a cultivation room, such as a greenhouse and a vinyl greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon diode supply device is capable of suppressing a change range of carbon diode concentration while suppressing an increase in the frequency of ignition and extinguishing of a combustor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the carbon dioxide supply apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Apparatus (1)
   Heat-source Equipment (10)
   Thermal Radiation Machine (20)
   Operating Panel (50)
   Control unit (151)

PN KR2020000769-A; KR2067898-B1
TI Liquefied natural gas treatment system for use in offshore structure e.g. power generation facilities, has liquefaction unit liquefying gas, and cooling unit for utilizing carbon dioxide separated from distillation unit.
AU KIM Y R
   PARK S M
   YEOMSEUNGJONG
   HWANG S B
AE KOREA SHIPBUILDING & OFFSHORE ENG CO LTD (HHIH-C)
GA 2020048364
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a cooling unit (10) for cooling gas produced in a gas well. A distillation unit (20) performs cryogenic distillation to separate carbon dioxide from the cooled gas. A carbon dioxide treatment unit (30) re-injects the separated carbon dioxide into an underground injection well. A liquefaction unit liquefies the gas from which the carbon dioxide is separated. The cooling unit utilizes the carbon dioxide separated from the distillation unit as refrigerant. A pre-cooler (11) pre-cools the gas. A heat exchanger (12) exchanges the gas returned from the distillation unit. The pre-cooler is provided with a refrigerant circulating unit that circulates refrigerant to exchange heat with the gas.
   USE - Liquefied natural gas (LNG) treatment system for use in an offshore structure (claimed) e.g. onshore gas facilities or power generation facilities.
   ADVANTAGE - The system facilitates processes of gas treatment, cryogenic distillation, carbon dioxide separation and carbon dioxide re-injection in an efficient and safe manner during process of natural gas so as to maximize gas production efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a hydraulic circuit diagram of an LNG treatment system.
   Cooling unit (10)
   Pre-cooler (11)
   Heat exchanger (12)
   Distillation unit (20)
   Carbon dioxide treatment unit (30)

AB    NOVELTY - Carbonic anhydrase fixture comprises porous boehmite, binder supported carbonic anhydrase on porous boehmite, and magnetic nanoparticles.
   USE - The fixture is useful in carbon dioxide separation device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fixture has excellent durability and activity, and prevents loss during operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) producing carbonic anhydrase fixture, comprising (a) preparing the aqueous solution of boehmite by mixing aqueous aluminum nitrate solution and ammonia water, and (b) adding carbonic anhydrase, magnetic nanoparticles and binder into the boehmite aqueous solution to prepare mixed solution;
   (2) carbon dioxide separation device comprising a housing (1) for accommodating absorbent, a filling unit (200) formed at the end of the housing and containing the carbonic anhydrase fixture, a gas supply unit for introducing carbon dioxide containing gas into the lower part of the filling unit, and a gas discharge unit formed on the filling unit for discharging carbon dioxide removed gas; and
   (3) manufacturing carbon dioxide separation device, comprising hydrolyzing carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide-containing gas into the absorbent liquid (100) using the carbonic anhydrase fixture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the carbon dioxide separation device.
   Housing (1)
   Absorbent liquid (100)
   Filling unit (200)
   Carbonic anhydrase (210)
   Magnet (230)

The carbonic anhydrase fixture is impregnated with the absorbent. PN KR2022034980-A
TI Hydrogen production float for ships, contains hydrogen production unit comprising steam reformer for converting carbon monoxide and steam into hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and hydrogen separator for separating and purifying hydrogen.
AU PARK A
   RYU S
   JAE J
   CHOI B Y
AE SAMSUNG HEAVY IND CO LTD (SMSU-C)
GA 2022424875
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen production float comprises a desulfurizer for receiving natural gas through a natural gas supply line connected to a gas unit, a hydrogen production unit, a component measuring unit provided in the natural gas supply line, a hydrogen production line, a bypass line branched from the natural gas supply line, a control valve (A) provided in the front end of the desulfurizer of the natural gas supply line, a control valve (B) provided in the bypass line, and a control unit for controlling the flow path of the gas. The desulfurizer removes sulfur components from the natural gas. The hydrogen production unit comprises a steam reformer for converting carbon monoxide in the reformed gas with steam into hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and a hydrogen separator for separating and purifying hydrogen from mixed fluid passed through the steam reformer. The component measuring unit is provided to measure a component of the natural gas supplied from the gas unit.
   USE - Hydrogen production float for ships.
   ADVANTAGE - The float can be supplied with natural gas mined from a gas unit, effectively produces hydrogen through a floating facility, and improves efficiency and automation of hydrogen production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrogen production float comprises a desulfurizer for receiving natural gas through a natural gas supply line connected to a gas unit, a hydrogen production unit, a component measuring unit provided in the natural gas supply line, a hydrogen production line, a bypass line branched from the natural gas supply line, a control valve (A) provided in the front end of the desulfurizer of the natural gas supply line, a control valve (B) provided in the bypass line, and a control unit for controlling the flow path of the gas. The desulfurizer removes sulfur components from the natural gas. The hydrogen production unit comprises a steam reformer for converting carbon monoxide in the reformed gas with steam into hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and a hydrogen separator for separating and purifying hydrogen from mixed fluid passed through the steam reformer. The component measuring unit is provided to measure a component of the natural gas supplied from the gas unit. The bypass line is connected between the desulfurizer and a pre-reformer of the hydrogen production line. The hydrogen production line connects the desulfurizer, the pre-reformer, the steam reformer and the hydrogen separator. Based on the content of sulfur components in the natural gas measured by the component measuring unit, the valves (A) and (B) are opened and closed to open and close the natural gas supply line.

PN CN114159961-A
TI Absorbent for desulfurizing and decarbonizing flue            gas, is formed by mixing calcium hydroxide slurry and            absorbent comprising basic solvent, active amine,            water, corrosion inhibitor and antioxidant.
AU SONG F
   HAN Q
   XU Q
   SONG Z
   LIU H
AE KUNYUE INTERNET ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY (KUNY-Non-standard)
GA 202241783S
AB    NOVELTY - An absorbent is formed by mixing an absorbent                (A) and an absorbent (B) in a volume ratio of                1:10-1:20. The absorbent (A) is a calcium hydroxide                slurry with a concentration of 10-15 wt.%. The                absorbent (B) comprises basic solvent, active                amine, water, corrosion inhibitor and antioxidant                in a mass ratio of 3:1:6:0.015:0.005.
   USE - Absorbent is used for desulfurizing and                decarbonizing flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The flue gas desulfurization and                decarburization absorbent is combined with lime wet                desulfurization technology and alcohol amine                absorption method carbon capture technology. It is                convenient to operate, and reduces the investment                cost of the device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   1. preparation of the absorbent, which                involves preparing calcium hydroxide slurry using                lime and water, mixing basic solvent, active amine,                water, corrosion inhibitor and antioxidant, mixing                the absorbent (A) and absorbent (B), and adjusting                pH of the mixed solution to 11 using an acid-base                buffer solution. The solvent is                N-(2-hydroxyethyl)ethylenediamine (AEEA). The                active amine is piperazine (PZ). The antioxidant is                acetaldoxime (AAO). The corrosion inhibitor is a                mixed solution of sodium molybdate and zinc acetate                in a mass ratio of 4:1; and
   2. application of the absorbent in flue gas                desulfurization and decarbonization, which involves                loading the absorbent into a circulation tank,                conveying the absorbent to the top of a tower by a                pump, uniformly spraying, countercurrently                contacting with flue gas introduced into the bottom                of the tower, such that calcium hydroxide in the                absorbent is reacted with sulfur dioxide to                generate calcium sulfite, and AEEA and PZ in the                absorption liquid is reacted with carbon dioxide to                form carbonate, discharging purified flue gas from                the chimney after defogging and reheating,                introducing the absorption liquid to an oxidation                tank at the bottom of the tower to oxidize calcium                sulfite to calcium sulfate under blast conditions,                crystallizing into gypsum, separating gypsum slurry                from the absorption liquid, dehydrating to complete                separation of desulfurization product, conveying                the absorbing liquid separated from the gypsum                slurry to heat exchanger for heat exchange, then                introducing into a regeneration tower, decomposing                carbonate under the action of heating to desorb                carbon dioxide, subjecting desorbed carbon dioxide                to purification and liquefaction processes,                converting absorbent from rich liquid to poor                liquid to complete regeneration, returning                regenerated absorbent to the circulation tank with                fresh lime slurry, and continuing flue gas                desulfurization and decarbonization process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                composition and structure of the absorbent.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: When the pH value of the                  mixed solution is less than 11, saturated calcium                  hydroxide solution is selected as the acid-base                  buffer solution, and the obtained absorbent is                  used as sulfur dioxide absorbent. When the pH                  value of the mixed solution is more than 11,                  acetic acid solution is selected as the acid-base                  buffer solution, and reacted with calcium                  hydroxide to generate calcium acetate which is                  used as carbon dioxide absorbent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The acid-base buffer                  solution is calcium hydroxide solution. The flue                  gas contains 4000 ppm sulfur dioxide and 12%                  carbon dioxide, and has liquid/gas ratio of 4.7                  L/m3 and gas velocity of 3 m/second.

PT P
PN WO2021201776-A1
TI Catalyst useful for reforming and water-gas shift reactions, comprises porous support substrate, nickel oxide nanoparticles and nickel-aluminate nanoparticles, where nickel oxide and nickel-aluminate nanoparticles are attached to substrate.
AU MOHAMED D K B
   VEKSHA A
   LISAK G
AE UNIV NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL (UYTU-C)
GA 2021B5186P
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises a porous support substrate, nickel oxide nanoparticles and nickel-aluminate nanoparticles, where the nickel oxide and nickel-aluminate nanoparticles are attached to the substrate. The ratio of nickel ion species in the form of nickel oxide to nickel ion species in the form of nickel-aluminate is 1.2:1-4:1, as measured by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) analysis.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for reforming and water-gas shift reactions.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst high tolerance to poisoning, high resistance to hydrogen sulfide, hydrochloric acid and hydrogen bromide poisoning, high water-gas shift activity and high reforming activity, and reduces amount of halogenated hydrocarbon wastes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method of steam reforming hydrocarbons with a subsequent water-gas shift reaction, involving (i) contacting the catalyst with an activating gaseous fluid flow comprising hydrogen at 500-1000 degrees C to obtain an activated catalyst and (ii) contacting the activated catalyst with a reactive gaseous fluid flow comprising a hydrocarbon material and water, in the form of steam at 500-1000 degrees C, preferably 500-900 degrees C, where the hydrocarbon material is catalytically converted into carbon monoxide and hydrogen gas through contact with the activated catalyst and steam. The carbon monoxide subsequently undergoes a water-gas shift reaction to provide carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas through contact with the activated catalyst and steam;
   (2) a method for converting a synthesis gas into carbon dioxide and hydrogen through a water-gas shift reaction, involving (a) contacting the catalyst with an activating gaseous fluid flow comprising hydrogen at 500-1000 degrees C to obtain an activated catalyst and (b) contacting the activated catalyst with a synthesis gas fluid flow comprising carbon monoxide, hydrogen and water, in the form of steam at 500-1000 degrees C, preferably 500-900 degrees C to convert the carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas via the water-gas shift reaction; and
   (3) a method for producing the catalyst, involving (1a) providing a solution comprising a precursor aluminum (III) oxide material, a nickel (II) salt, a precipitant, and a solvent to obtain a precursor mixture, (1b) subjecting the precursor mixture to a hydrothermal reaction at 100-250 degrees C, preferably 180 degrees C to obtain a precipitate and (1c) collecting and calcinating the precipitate at 200-900 degrees C, preferably 400 degrees C to obtain the product, where the precipitant is an amine, an amide or a urea.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of nickel ion species in the form of nickel oxide to nickel ion species in the form of nickel-aluminate is 1.3:1-3:1, preferably 1.35:1-2.1:1, as measured by XPS analysis. The total amount of nickel in the nickel oxide and nickel aluminate nanoparticles together is 0.5-15 wt.%, preferably 5-7 wt.% of the total weight of the catalyst, with the porous support substrate. The oxygen in nickel oxide and the aluminate in the nickel aluminate together is 85-99.5 wt.%, preferably 93-95 wt.% of the total weight of the catalyst. The ratio between the total mole value of the precursor aluminum (III) oxide material and the nickel (II) salt to the mole value of the precipitant is 1:1-1:20, preferably 1:2-1:15. Preferred Method: The step (1a) involves adding a surfactant to the precursor mixture. Preferred Components: The porous support substrate is alumina, preferably gamma -alumina. The porous support substrate is provided in the form of nanostructures that support the nickel oxide and nickel-aluminate nanoparticles. The nanostructures are chosen from nanoflake and nano-spindle support structures. The catalyst further comprises 0.1-5 wt.% additive material chosen from one or more of oxides of alkali metals, oxides of alkaline earth metals, oxides of gallium, and oxides of a transition metal, where the oxides of a transition metal is not a nickel oxide. The catalyst is resistant to poisoning by one or more of halide- and sulfur-containing compounds comprising hydrogen chloride, chloride-containing hydrocarbons, hydrogen bromide, bromide-containing hydrocarbons, carbonyl sulfide, and hydrogen sulfide, when used in a reforming and/or water-gas-shift reaction. The hydrocarbon material is chosen from one or more of a tar, toluene, xylenes, ethylbenzene, styrene, phenol, cresol, naphthalene and anthracene. The hydrocarbon material is a mixture comprising one or more of xylenes, ethylbenzene, phenol, cresol, anthracene, naphthalene, toluene and styrene. The reactive gaseous fluid flow of step (ii) comprises a contaminant chosen from one or more of halide-containing compounds and a sulfur-containing compound. The contaminant is chosen from one or more of 10-10000 ppmv, preferably 500-2000 ppmv hydrogen chloride, chloride-containing hydrocarbons, 10-10000 ppmv, preferably 500-2000 ppmv hydrogen bromide, bromide-containing hydrocarbons, carbonyl sulfide and 1-200 ppmv, preferably 25-50 ppmv hydrogen sulfide. The precursor aluminum (III) oxide material is chosen from one or more of an aluminum nitrate (preferred), an aluminum chloride, an aluminum sulfate, an aluminum perchlorate, an aluminum bromide, an aluminum acetate, an aluminum acetylacetonate, and their solvates. The aluminum nitrate comprises aluminum nitrate nonahydrate. The solvent is chosen from a polar aprotic solvent, a polar protic solvent, water, an alcohol, a ketone, and an ether, preferably water, methanol, ethanol and isopropanol. The nickel (II) salt is chosen from a nickel nitrate, nickel chloride, a nickel sulfate, a nickel perchlorate, a nickel bromide, a nickel acetate, a nickel acetylacetonate, and their solvates. The nickel (II) salt is nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate. The precipitant is chosen from N-methylurea, 1,1-dimethylurea, N,N'-dimethylurea, N,N'-dimethylformamide (all preferred), N,N'-dimethylformamide, N-methylformamide, N-ethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, acetamide, urea, 1,1-dimethylurea, tetramethylurea, N-ethylurea 1,3-diethylurea. The additive material precursor is chosen from one or more of halides, nitrates, oxides, hydroxides and carbonates of alkali metals, hydroxides and carbonates of alkaline earth metals, nitrates and halides of gallium, and nitrates and halides of a transition metal, where the transition metal is not nickel, and is added to the precursor mixture. The surfactant is chosen from cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, cetyltrimethylammonium chloride, Triton TX-100 (RTM: t-Octylphenoxypolyethoxyethanol, or Polyethylene glycol tert-octylphenyl ether), Tween 80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80), sodium dodecyl sulfate and sodium laurate. Preferred Properties: The nanoflakes have a length of 10-250 nm, preferably 16-200 nm, and a width of 2-120 nm, preferably 10-40 nm. The nano-spindles have a length of 10-50 nm and a width of 1-10 nm, preferably 2-6 nm. The catalyst has a bulk density of 0.4-0.7 g/ml, preferably 0.48-0.62 g/ml. The catalyst has a nickel load per bed volume of 0.02-0.04 g/0.5 ml, preferably 0.023-0.03 g/0.5 ml. The catalyst has a BET surface area of 150-300 m2/g, preferably 183-243 m2/g. The catalyst has a total pore volume of 0.2-0.8 ml/g, preferably 0.24-0.7 ml/g based on nitrogen adsorption isotherms. The catalyst has catalyst displays having (ai) at least one reduction peak at 500-760 degrees C and/or (aii) at least one reduction peak at 800-1000 degrees C, as measured by temperature-programmed reduction.
   EXAMPLE - Example 1-13 describes about a preparation of a catalyst, so does not support the claim.

PT P
PN WO2020165074-A1; CN113439132-A; EP3924536-A1; US2022112613-A1
TI Preparing gas diffusion layer used in electrode of electrochemical cell, by preparing carrier-binder paste and adhesive composition, combining layers of supporting material, adhesive composition and carrier-binder paste and pressing layers.
AU PHILIPS M F
   ANSOVINI D
   FIGUEIREDO M C C
   KRASOVIC J
AE AVANTIUM KNOWLEDGE CENT BV (AVAN-Non-standard)
   AVANTIUM KNOWLEDGE CENT BV (AVAN-Non-standard)
   AVANTIUM KNOWLEDGE CENT BV (AVAN-Non-standard)
GA 202079777E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a gas diffusion layer involves (a) preparing a carrier-binder paste comprising a solvent, a fluorinated binder and conductive carrier particles, (b) preparing an adhesive composition comprising a solvent, a fluorinated binder and less than or equal to 15 wt.% conductive carrier particles, based on the total weight of fluorinated binder and any conductive carrier particles, and (c) combining a layer of supporting material, a layer of the adhesive composition and a layer of the carrier-binder paste, in which the layer of the adhesive composition is applied between the layer of supporting material and the layer of the carrier-binder paste, pressing the combination of supporting material, adhesive composition and carrier-binder paste at a pressure of at least 15 kilopascal and/or heating the combination of supporting material, adhesive composition and carrier-binder paste at a temperature of at least 300 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a gas diffusion layer, which is useful in a gas diffusion electrode for an electrochemical cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas diffusion layer has improved mechanical stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing a gas diffusion electrode involving (i) preparing the carrier-binder paste, (ii) preparing the adhesive composition, and (iii) combining a layer of supporting material, a layer of the adhesive composition and a layer of the carrier-binder paste, in which the layer of the adhesive composition is applied between the layer of supporting material and the layer of the carrier-binder paste, pressing the combination of supporting material, adhesive composition and carrier-binder paste at a pressure of at least 15 kilopascal and/or heating the combination of supporting material, adhesive composition and carrier-binder paste at a temperature of at least 300 degrees C, and (iv) applying a catalytic layer comprising a catalyst, onto the layer of the carrier-binder paste on the side opposite of the side where the layer of the adhesive composition is applied; and
   (2) a process for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide involving introducing an anolyte to a first cell compartment of an electrochemical cell comprising an anode, introducing a catholyte and carbon dioxide to a second cell compartment of the electrochemical cell comprising a cathode, and applying an electrical potential between the anode and the cathode sufficient to reduce the carbon dioxide to a reduced reaction product, where the cathode comprises the gas diffusion layer and/or the gas diffusion electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The conductive carrier particles comprise carbon particles. The method involves based on the total weight of fluorinated binder and conductive carrier particles together, using 20-60 wt.% carrier-binder paste based on the fluorinated binder, or 40-80 wt.% carrier-binder paste based on the conductive carrier particles. The carrier-binder paste and/or the adhesive composition comprises solvent comprising water and/or an alkanol (preferred), where a suspension of the fluorinated binder in such solvent is prepared by combining the fluorinated binder and the solvent and mixing the fluorinated binder and the solvent for 10 seconds to 15 minutes. The adhesive composition comprises a higher concentration of fluorinated binder, based on grams of fluorinated binder per milliliter of solvent, than the carrier-binder paste. The catalytic layer comprises a catalyst, a fluorinated binder and conductive carrier particles. The catalyst comprises indium and/or bismuth. Preferred Method: In step (a), the carrier-binder paste is prepared by preparing a suspension of the fluorinated binder in a solvent and subsequently mixing the conductive carrier particles with the suspension. In step (c), a layer of the adhesive composition is coated onto a side of the layer of the carrier-binder paste, where the supporting member is attached to the coated side of the layer of carrier-binder paste.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fluorinated binder comprises fluorinated polymers chosen from PTFE polymers, perfluoroalkoxy polymers, fluorinated ethylene propylene polymers and polyvinylidene difluoride polymers.

PN CN111537640-A
TI Multi-purpose volatile organic compound combined            detection method comprises sending gas to be tested            into detection device, leaving water and VOC, releasing            carbon dioxide, using photoionization detector to            quantitatively detect VOC gas.
AU ZHONG G
   ZHAO H
   CAO H
   LI X
AE BEIJING AUTOMOTIVE GROUP OFF-ROAD VEHICL (BAIC-C)
GA 202080622N
AB    NOVELTY - Multi-purpose volatile organic compound (VOC)                combined detection method comprises (a) sending the                gas to be tested into the detection device through                the intake pump, (b) entering the gas to be tested                into the primary cold trap placed in the primary                electric refrigeration chamber, leaving the water                and VOC in the gas to be tested, releasing carbon                dioxide, pushing the first-level cold trap into the                first-level electric heating greenhouse, removing                the VOC gas and water vapor, (c) entering the                obtained VOC gas and water vapor into the secondary                cold trap placed in the secondary electric                refrigeration chamber, condensing the VOC gas and                water vapor, pushing the secondary cold trap into                the secondary electric heating greenhouse, (d)                entering of the obtained VOC gas into the analysis                column, separating the VOC gas qualitatively and                (e) using photoionization detector (PID) to                quantitatively detect the VOC gas flowing from gas                chromatography (GC).
   USE - The method is useful for multi-purpose VOC                combined detection method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Multi-purpose volatile organic compound (VOC)                combined detection method comprises (a) sending the                gas to be tested into the detection device through                the intake pump, (b) entering the gas to be tested                into the primary cold trap placed in the primary                electric refrigeration chamber, leaving the water                and VOC in the gas to be tested, releasing carbon                dioxide, pushing the first-level cold trap into the                first-level electric heating greenhouse, when                entering the primary cold trap after the volume of                the measured gas reaches the required level,                adjusting the temperature rise program to raise the                temperature of the first-level cold trap to                200-240&#176; C, removing the VOC gas and water                vapor, (c) entering the obtained VOC gas and water                vapor into the secondary cold trap placed in the                secondary electric refrigeration chamber,                condensing the VOC gas and water vapor, pushing the                secondary cold trap into the secondary electric                heating greenhouse, when entering the secondary                cold trap after the gas volume reaches the                requirement, adjusting the temperature rise program                to raise the temperature of the secondary cold trap                to 10-60&#176; C to drive out the VOC gas, (d)                entering of the obtained VOC gas into the analysis                column, separating the VOC gas qualitatively and                (e) using photoionization detector (PID) to                quantitatively detect the VOC gas flowing from gas                chromatography (GC).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The internal temperature                  of the first-level cold trap placed in the                  first-level electric refrigeration chamber is                  minus 30-55&#176; C. The internal temperature                  of the secondary cold trap placed in the                  secondary electric refrigeration chamber is                  &#8804; -130&#176; C. The analytical                  column is formed by connecting a polar                  chromatographic column and a non-polar                  chromatographic column in series. The length of                  the polar chromatographic column and the                  non-polar chromatographic column are both                  &#8805; 30 m. The first-level electric                  refrigeration room and the first-level electric                  riser greenhouse share a room or a different                  room. Preferred Components: The inside of the                  first-stage cold trap is filled with adsorbent.                  The adsorbent is a mixture of silica gel,                  activated carbon and Tenax. The secondary cold                  trap is an empty trap.

PN CN111392943-A
TI Processing and recycling high concentration            ammonia nitrogen and chemical oxygen demand wastewater            comprises e.g. adding alkali to wastewater, adjusting            pH of wastewater to alkaline, adding sodium sulfite            reducing agent and reacting.
AU HUANG Y
   SHI J
   HU K
AE BEIJING YAOZHI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   BAYANNUR YINHAI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (BAYA-Non-standard)
GA 202067611N
AB    NOVELTY - Processing and recycling high concentration                ammonia nitrogen and chemical oxygen demand                wastewater comprises e.g. (i) adding alkali to the                wastewater, adjusting the pH of wastewater to                alkaline, and adding sodium sulfite reducing agent                to the wastewater, reacting with the metal ions in                the wastewater to obtain precipitate, and then                removing the produced precipitate and other solid                impurities, (ii) removing the ammonia nitrogen in                the wastewater, adding pre-treated wastewater to                the stripping device, injecting steam into the                stripping and stripping device, when the steam                releases the ammonia nitrogen in the sewage into                ammonia gas and discharging through the stripping                method, (iii) removing ammonia nitrogen and                chemical oxygen demand in wastewater, adding                powdered titanium dioxide catalyst to wastewater,                passing ozone, and decomposes ammonia nitrogen and                chemical oxygen demand wastewater in sewage into                nitrogen and carbon dioxide through catalytic ozone                oxidation method.
   USE - The method is useful for processing and                recycling high concentration ammonia nitrogen and                chemical oxygen demand wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has good removal effect, simple                process flow, and easy operation; and achieve the                purpose of resource recycling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing and recycling high concentration                ammonia nitrogen and chemical oxygen demand                wastewater comprises (i) adding alkali to the                wastewater, adjusting the pH of wastewater to                alkaline, and adding sodium sulfite reducing agent                to the wastewater, reacting with the metal ions in                the wastewater to obtain precipitate, and then                removing the produced precipitate and other solid                impurities, (ii) removing the ammonia nitrogen in                the wastewater, adding pre-treated wastewater to                the stripping device, injecting steam into the                stripping and stripping device, when the steam                releases the ammonia nitrogen in the sewage into                ammonia gas and discharging through the stripping                method, (iii) removing ammonia nitrogen and                chemical oxygen demand in wastewater, adding                powdered titanium dioxide catalyst to the sewage                treated by the stripping method obtained in the                step (ii), passing ozone, and decomposes ammonia                nitrogen and chemical oxygen demand wastewater in                sewage into nitrogen and carbon dioxide through                catalytic ozone oxidation method, oxidizing ozone                and decomposing ammonia nitrogen and chemical                oxygen demand wastewater in sewage into nitrogen                and carbon dioxide through catalytic ozone                oxidation method, and(iv) treating sewage by the                catalytic ozonation method of step (iii), filtering                through the two-stage microfiltration ceramic                membrane and the reverse osmosis system membrane,                discharging and reaching the dischargeable or                reusable quality, separating the catalyst by the                microfiltration ceramic membrane and the reverse                osmosis membrane, reusing the catalyst, repeating                steps three and four, and completing the sewage                treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises recovering recovery of ammonia                  nitrogen, discharging the ammonia gas from step                  (ii) to the ammonia nitrogen recovery device,                  adding water to the ammonia nitrogen recovery                  device, and dissolving the ammonia gas into the                  water and becomes ammonium hydroxide, or adding                  sulfuric acid or hydrochloric acid to the ammonia                  nitrogen recovery device, dissolving the ammonia                  gas in the acid to become ammonium sulfate or                  ammonium chloride, and recovering the ammonia gas                  in the wastewater. The pH of the wastewater is                  adjusted to 10-12. In the process of removing                  ammonia nitrogen in sewage by the stripping and                  stripping method, and temperature of the sewage                  in the stripping device is 70-80 degrees C. The                  volume ratio of total steam consumption to sewage                  is 55.5-74.1. The temperature of the sewage in                  the stripping and stripping device is 70-80                  degrees C. The ratio of the total amount of steam                  used to the volume of the sewage is 55.5-74.1.                  The stripping time is 60-110 minutes. In the step                  (iii), the process of removing ammonia nitrogen                  and COD in sewage by the catalytic ozonation                  method, the amount of titanium dioxide is 1-10                  g/l. The temperature of the sewage is 70-90                  degrees C. In the step (iv), the filtering                  process includes first-level microfiltration                  ceramic membrane retains particles with diameter                  above 0.1 mu m, average pore diameter of the                  secondary microfiltration ceramic membrane is                  0.02-10 mu m, retaining high molecular substances                  with diameter of 0.05-10 mu m or molecular weight                  greater than 1 million, operating pressure                  difference is 0.01-0.2 MPa, where the reverse                  osmosis membrane retains substances larger than                  0.0001 microns, retaining dissolved salts and                  organic substances with molecular weight greater                  than 100. The steam adopts the thermal energy                  or/and steam in the factory. In the pre-treatment                  process of the sewage, using sodium hydroxide as                  the alkali.

PT P
PN CN110079430-A
TI Preparing whiskey using beer fermentation broth            involves pulverizing malt to form wort, then added with            yeast after cooling, where yeast convert sugar in wort            into alcohol and carbon dioxide, fermentating and            concentrating alcohol.
AU TONG F
AE NANYANG JINGDE BEER TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (NANY-Non-standard)
GA 201969510V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing whiskey using beer fermentation                broth involves pulverizing malt to form wort, then                added with yeast after cooling, where yeast convert                the sugar in the wort into alcohol and carbon                dioxide, and after the fermentation is completed,                then alcohol is concentrated in the fermentation                broth to 5-7 wt.%, and the higher alcohol is                480-850 mg/L. The fermentation broth at this time                is called tender beer, and the fermentation process                is completed in the fermentation tank.
   USE - Method for preparing whiskey using beer                fermentation broth.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare whiskey using                beer fermentation broth, which allows to solve the                problem that the beer is wasteful in the                off-season, and at the same time beer is turned                into whiskey, and increases the profit is greatly                increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The fermenter is slowly unloaded in the tank                and placed for 48 hours to discharge the carbon                dioxide gas in the fermentation broth, and then the                fermentation broth is pumped into a vessel at the                bottom of the distiller for distillation, and after                first distillation of the fermentation broth, the                alcohol concentration is increased to 23-25 wt.%,                where higher alcohol is 900-1200 mg/L, and after                second distillation the alcohol concentration is                increased to 56-58 wt.%, and then diluted to 36-38                wt.%, at this time the higher alcohol is 1100-1700                mg/L. The container is heated by steam in the                jacket, or may be electrically heated, and the                upper portion of the container is a hoist-type                head, and the outlet of the head is connected to                the distillation column. The distillation column is                connected to the condenser at the rear end, and the                distilled gas fraction is cooled to a liquid, and                the liquid fraction is collected at the outlet of                the condenser to get raw wine. The main component                of the fraction is alcohol, and contains various                flavor substances for making whiskey. The raw wine                after distillation is placed in oak barrels for                aging and develops good color. The alcohols of                different alcohol content are mixed into the                required alcohol content, and the other is to blend                the wines of different aging times and after the                mixing is completed, it is placed for about one                week, so that the wines of different sources are                fully fused and reacted, and finally stabilized,                and then filtered once to remove impurities, and                then bottled, labeled, boxed, and filled.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The wort concentration                  is 6-8 degrees P, and fermentation liquid sugar                  content is 160 g/ L to ensure the growth of yeast                  and fermentation requirements for nutrients, and                  the fermentation temperature to 20 degrees C,                  yeast inoculum 1x 107 cfu/mL for 6 days. The                  shape of the gourd-shaped head and the number of                  stages of the distillation column affect the                  whiskey and flavor is selected according to the                  market needs of different hoist type and                  distillation column series. The wine residues are                  ramoved by 10%, or 20%, so the concentration of                  the collected wine is 40-70%, and the liquor at                  this time is called new wine. The oak barrel is                  made of special oak and baked in oak barrels. The                  aging is closely related to the taste of the                  whiskey, and the aging temperature is controlled                  at 5-20 degrees C, and the lower the temperature,                  and longer the aging time for not less than two                  years.

PT P
PN CN110048120-A; CN110048120-B
TI Preparing nano-lithium ferrite comprises e.g.            adding ammonium sulfate crystals to hot pure water,            dissolving, adjusting pH, adding pure water to battery            grade lithium carbonate slurry, and introducing carbon            dioxide.
AU WANG K
AE WANG K (WANG-Individual)
GA 201965882M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-lithium ferrite comprises e.g.                adding ammonium sulfate crystals to hot pure water,                dissolving to obtain solution, adjusting pH, adding                pure water to battery grade lithium carbonate                slurry, introducing carbon dioxide to obtain clear                lithium hydrogencarbonate solution, adding base                solution into the reaction kettle, raising the                temperature, adding ferrous solution, lithium                hydrogencarbonate solution, and acid-base                conditioner, stirring to obtain slurry,                transferring the slurry into the reaction kettle,                stir reacting, reducing the temperature after                pressure relief, taking out the material,                filtering, washing to obtain precipitate, adding                precipitate into the roller furnace, calcining,                introducing air during calcined process, cooling                the calcined material, crushing by the airflow,                grading by grading wheel, sieving and removing iron                to obtain nano-lithium ferrite.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                nano-lithium ferrite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and economical. The                nano-lithium ferrite has large specific surface                area and uniform particle size distribution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano-lithium ferrite comprises (1)                adding ammonium sulfate crystals to hot pure water,                stirring and dissolving to obtain solution, adding                acetic acid or sodium acetate to adjust pH of                solution to 2-2.5 to obtain ferrous solution,                adding pure water to battery grade lithium                carbonate slurry, then introducing carbon dioxide                under stirring to obtain clear lithium                hydrogencarbonate solution, (2) adding base                solution into the reaction kettle, where the base                solution is mixed solution of urea and polyethylene                glycol, raising the temperature until the                temperature is 95-98 degrees C, adding ferrous                solution, lithium hydrogencarbonate solution, and                acid-base conditioner to the reaction kettle,                maintaining stirring speed at 200-300                revolutions/minute, reaction temperature at 95-98                degrees C, the reaction process pH is 7-7.5, the                feeding time is 1-1.5 hours, finishing feeding                after stirring and reacting at this temperature for                1-2 hours to obtain slurry, (3) transferring the                slurry into the reaction kettle, stir reacting at                220-250 degrees C for 3-5 hours under 0.6-0.9 MPa                pressure, reducing the temperature after pressure                relief, taking out the material, filtering, and                washing to obtain precipitate, and (4) adding                precipitate into the roller furnace, calcining at                400-500 degrees C for 5-8 hours, introducing air                during calcined process, maintaining gas flow rate                inside the calcining furnace at 2-3 m/second,                cooling the calcined material, crushing by the                airflow, grading by grading wheel, sieving and                removing iron to obtain nano-lithium ferrite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  molar ratio of ammonium ferrous sulfate to                  lithium hydrogencarbonate is 1:5.01-5.05. In the                  step (2), the ratio of the molar amount of urea                  in the bottom liquid to the added iron is                  1.2-1.5:1, the concentrations of urea and                  polyethylene glycol in the bottom liquid are                  1.5-2 mol/l and 0.01-0.02% respectively, the                  acid-base regulator is ammonium carbonate,                  sulfuric acid, and hydrochloric acid. Preferred                  Method: In the step (3), the stirring speed of                  the high pressure hydrothermal reaction process                  is 100-150 revolutions/minute. The step (4)                  airflow pulverization uses 0.25-0.35 MPa of dry                  air, the diameter of the grading wheel is 1/3-1/2                  of the inner diameter of the grading chamber, and                  the rotation speed of the grading wheel is                  1300-1800 revolutions/minute. The step (4) is                  sieved uses 200-300 mesh ultrasonic vibrating                  sieve. In addition to iron, a two-stage                  electromagnetic iron remover is used to control                  the magnetic substance of nanometer lithium iron                  oxide less than 1 ppm.

PN CN109181920-A
TI Preparing soft wheat malt comprises e.g.            screening, grading soft wheat raw materials and            removing impurities, steeping processed soft wheat with            process of intermittent three-steeping and continuously            pumped dry-impregnated carbon dioxide.
AU GE J
AE GANSU XIANGYONG BEER MATERIAL CO LTD (GANS-Non-standard)
GA 2019102040
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing soft wheat malt comprises e.g. (i)                screening, grading soft wheat raw materials and                removing impurities, (ii) steeping processed soft                wheat as raw material with process of intermittent                three-steeping, total steeping time of 27-28 hours                of continuous ventilation steeping time, without                water continuously pumped dry-impregnated carbon                dioxide, (iii) after soaking soft wheat,                germinating soft wheat, wheat temperature is                controlled, germinating time is 80-96 hours,                supplying water in 12th hours of germinating and                adding gibberellin, subsequently supplying water                twice at 24th hour, 36 hour of germination,                controlling germination water and germinating                humidity, (iv) drying wheat malt after germinating,                large air quantity moisture removal is used in                earlier stage, drying, heating and maintaining it                for 4 hours, baking, and (v) finishing drying,                entering a deculming machine to remove roots,                leafbuds, removing malt dust by a dust blower, and                packing it by a wrapping machine.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing soft wheat                malt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has shortened germination cycle of                soft wheat malt; short production cycle; and                reduces production cost of enterprise.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing soft wheat malt comprises (i)                screening, grading soft wheat raw materials and                removing impurities, (ii) steeping processed soft                wheat as raw material with process of intermittent                three-steeping, I.e., 3 hours soaking for 2-3 hours                without water, further 3 hours without water                soaking for 8 hours, flooding again three hours                without water for 8 hours steeping process, total                steeping time of 27-28 hours of continuous                ventilation steeping time, without water                continuously pumped dry-impregnated carbon dioxide,                (iii) after soaking soft wheat, germinating soft                wheat, wheat temperature is controlled at 11-13                degrees C, germinating time is 80-96 hours,                supplying water in 12th hours of germinating and                adding 15-20 ppm gibberellin, subsequently                supplying water twice at 24th hour, 36 hour of                germination, germination water is controlled at                44-45 %, germinating humidity is controlled at not                greater than 80%, (iv) drying wheat malt after                germinating, large air quantity moisture removal is                used in earlier stage, wind temperature is 45-65                degrees C, drying for 12-14 hours, heating to 65-77                degrees C and maintaining it for 4 hours, baking it                for 1.5-2 hours at 80-82 degrees C, and (v)                finishing drying, entering a deculming machine to                remove roots, leaf-buds, removing malt dust by a                dust blower, and packing it by a wrapping                machine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  screening and grading soft wheat raw material,                  dust, impurity, wheat shell, stone and grass                  seeds in soft wheat raw material are cleaning by                  de-stonner. In the step (ii), the water overflows                  for 20 minutes before each water cut, and water                  is draining, ventilating, and drained during                  overflow process. In the step (ii), adding second                  wet steeping to edible grade sodium hydroxide,                  adding amount of edible sodium hydroxide is                  0.22-0.255 kg/t of raw material treating soft                  wheat. In the step (ii), the steeping temperature                  is 10-12 degrees C. In the step (iii), the                  germination process adopts continuous ventilation                  and turning once per 12 hours. In the step (iv),                  during drying the oven is blowing at 45 degrees                  C, and large amount of air is exhausted, heating                  to 65 degrees C for 12-14 hours, water is reduced                  to 12%, and heating to 65-77 degrees C for 50                  hours with 50% return air for 2 hours. Reducing                  water to 8% and water is lowering to 6-6.5% at a                  constant temperature of 77 degrees C for 2 hours,                  and heated to 80-82 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours,                  and all the return air is using for baking.

PN CN109020471-A
TI Preparation of three-dimensional network            halloysite-silica composite aerogel material by            preparing halloysite dispersion, adding            furosemide-containing solution, dispersing,            vacuumizing, washing product, dispersing, and            polymerizing.
AU LI H
   SONG L
   LIU H
   ZHOU J
   ZHANG B
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 2018A5193T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of three-dimensional network                halloysite-silica composite aerogel material                includes adding halloysite nanotubes to mixture of                deionized water and ethanol, ultrasonically                dispersing to obtain halloysite dispersion, adding                sodium styrene sulfonate, polydivinylsiloxane,                initiator and furosemide to mixture of deionized                water and ethanol, stirring, adding to halloysite                dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing, vacuumizing,                releasing pressure, repeatedly vacuumizing, washing                product, dispersing in water, and water bath                heating to initiate polymerization; washing,                dispersing in water, adding tetraethyl                orthosilicate, hydrochloric acid, initiator and                nifedipine, stirring, water bath heating to                initiate polymerization, adding sodium hydroxide,                and stirring; and adding                methacryloyloxydodecyltrimethylammonium chloride,                standing, adding to carbon dioxide supercritical                high pressure extraction device, and                supercritically drying.
   USE - Method for preparation of three-dimensional                network halloysite-silica composite aerogel                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite aerogel material releases drug                nifedipine loaded in mesopores as the soaking time                is gradually increased, releases furosemide drug                loaded in micropores as soaking time is further                increased, and achieves taking the drug once and                administering multiple times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of three-dimensional network                halloysite-silica composite aerogel material                comprises:
   (A) adding 0.5-20 pts. wt. halloysite                nanotubes to mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water                and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, and ultrasonically                dispersing to obtain halloysite dispersion; and                adding 0.5-20 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate,                0.05-5 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.5 pt.                wt. initiator and 0.05-15 pt. wt. furosemide to                mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts.                wt. ethanol, stirring uniformly, adding to                halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersiing,                vacuumizing, releasing pressure to normal pressure,                repeatedly vacuumizing for 3 times, washing                product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, and                water bath heating to 60-120 degrees C to initiate                polymerization for 48 hours;
   (B) washing, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water,                adding 70-160 pts. wt. tetraethyl orthosilicate,                0.05-2 pts. wt. 12 mol/L hydrochloric acid, 0.01-2                pts. wt. initiator and 0.05-20 pts. wt. nifedipine,                stirring, water bath heating to 30-75 degrees C to                initiate polymerization for 48 hours, adding                0.01-0.5 pt. wt. sodium hydroxide, and stirring                until completely dissolved; and
   (C) adding 0.05-2 pts. wt.                methacryloyloxydodecyltrimethylammonium chloride,                standing, adding to carbon dioxide supercritical                high pressure extraction device, and                supercritically drying to 30-60 degrees C at 5-15                MPa for 3 hours using carbon dioxide as                medium.
   The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide or                azobisisobutyronitrile. The composite aerogel                material has average specific surface area of                600-608 m2/g, nano-scale average of 10-12 nm and                micro-scale pore average of 2-4 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The                  polydivinylsiloxane has average molecular weight                  of 500-5000, preferably 1000-3000, and vinyl                  content of 0.1-5%.

PN CN108940254-A; CN108940254-B
TI Preparing catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol comprises mixing zinc nitrate solution and zirconium nitrate solution, adjusting the pH, filtering, washing with deionized water, drying and calcining.
AU DING H
   LIU Q
   WANG L
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2018A0704X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst comprises: (i) mixing the equal volume of zinc nitrate solution and zirconium nitrate solution, adjusting the pH to 8 using ammonium carbamate, mixing the solution at 50-90 degrees C for 2-6 hours, cooling, filtering, washing with deionized water, drying, calcining under static air for 2-6 hours to obtain powder; (ii) doping the calcined powder and zinc nitrate solution in methylcyclopentyltrimethane solution, stirring, cooling, filtering, washing with deionized water, drying at 90-130 degrees C for 3-6 hours to obtain methylcyclopentyltrimethylzinc zirconium; and (iii) placing the redox graphene carrier in an atomic layer deposition technology device, reaction chamber of atomic layer deposition technology device and reaction chamber door device, reacting at 220-260 degrees C, adjusting the injection line of the device to 100-140 degrees C, heating methylcyclopentyltrimethylzinc zirconium to 120-150 degrees C in the device, pulsing, carrying out nitrogen cleaning and oxygen pulse.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst improves the conversion of carbon dioxide and selectivity of methanol, and enhances the stability of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing catalyst comprises: (i) mixing the equal volume of 0.4 mol/l zinc nitrate solution and 0.4-1.6 mol/l zirconium nitrate solution, adjusting the pH to 8 using 1 mol/l ammonium carbamate, mixing the solution at 50-90 degrees C for 2-6 hours, cooling to 10-35 degrees C, filtering, washing 3-8 times with deionized water, drying at 110-150 degrees C for 4-8 hours, calcining at 300-500 degrees C under static air for 2-6 hours to obtain powder; (ii) doping the calcined powder and zinc nitrate solution in 0.6-0.9 mol/l methylcyclopentyltrimethane solution, stirring at 50-70 degrees C for 2-5 hours, cooling to 10-35 degrees C, filtering, washing with deionized water, drying at 90-130 degrees C for 3-6 hours to obtain methylcyclopentyltrimethylzinc zirconium; and (iii) placing the redox graphene carrier in an atomic layer deposition technology device, reaction chamber of atomic layer deposition technology device and reaction chamber door device, reacting at 220-260 degrees C, adjusting the injection line of the device to 100-140 degrees C, heating methylcyclopentyltrimethylzinc zirconium to 120-150 degrees C in the device, pulsing, carrying out nitrogen cleaning for 50-120 seconds, performing oxygen pulse for 60-120 seconds to obtain zinc zirconium/graphene oxide diatomic catalyst.

PN WO2018204243-A1; US2020063079-A1
TI System useful for brewing fermented alcoholic            beverage, preferably kombucha-based alcoholic beverage,            comprises brew vessel, primary fermentation vessel,            secondary fermentation vessel, aerobic fermenter, and            mixing tank.
AU BYSTROM-WILLIAMS A K
   HILL J W L
AE HONEYMOON BREWERY LLC (HONE-Non-standard)
   HONEYMOON BREWERY LLC (HONE-Non-standard)
GA 201887971W
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a brew vessel for steeping                tea in a quantity of hot water to form tea liquor,                a primary fermentation vessel for receiving the tea                liquor and one or more yeasts and one or more                fermentables, a secondary fermentation vessel for                receiving the alcoholic brew from the primary                fermentation vessel, where the secondary                fermentation vessel configured for further                anaerobic fermentation of the alcoholic brew to an                alcohol by volume (ABV) of at least 7% such that                carbon dioxide released during further anaerobic                fermentation to escapes from the secondary                fermentation vessel and air is prevented from                entering the secondary fermentation vessel, an                aerobic fermenter for fermenting the tea liquor                when mixed with sugar and inoculated with a                symbiotic culture of bacteria and yeast (SCOBY) to                form a fermented tea liquor, and a mixing tank                configured for mixing the alcoholic brew having an                ABV of at least 7% with a second batch of fermented                tea liquor.
   USE - The system is useful for brewing a fermented                alcoholic beverage (all claimed), preferably                kombucha-based alcoholic beverage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - System comprises a brew vessel for steeping                tea in a quantity of hot water to form tea liquor,                a primary fermentation vessel for receiving the tea                liquor and one or more yeasts and one or more                fermentables, where the primary fermentation vessel                configured for anaerobic fermentation of the tea                liquor into an alcoholic brew such that carbon                dioxide released during primary fermentation                escapes from the primary fermentation vessel and                air is prevented from entering the primary                fermentation vessel, a secondary fermentation                vessel for receiving the alcoholic brew from the                primary fermentation vessel, where the secondary                fermentation vessel configured for further                anaerobic fermentation of the alcoholic brew to an                alcohol by volume (ABV) of at least 7% such that                carbon dioxide released during further anaerobic                fermentation to escapes from the secondary                fermentation vessel and air is prevented from                entering the secondary fermentation vessel, an                aerobic fermenter for fermenting the tea liquor                when mixed with sugar and inoculated with a                symbiotic culture of bacteria and yeast (SCOBY) to                form a fermented tea liquor, and a mixing tank                configured for mixing the alcoholic brew having an                ABV of at least 7% with a second batch of fermented                tea liquor, where the mixing tank dimensioned and                configured to mix the alcoholic brew and the second                batch of fermented tea liquor in desired ratios to                produce a fermented alcoholic beverage having an                ABV of approximately 3-7%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a method for brewing a fermented                alcoholic beverage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred System: The brew vessel is a                  heated liquor tank. The aerobic fermenter                  includes a fermentation filter prevent outside                  contamination to the tea liquor/sugar mixture.                  The system further comprises a cooler for cooling                  the tea liquor. The primary fermentation vessel                  includes an oxygen supply for aerating the tea                  liquor. The primary fermentation vessel includes                  a first fermentation lock to allow carbon dioxide                  released during primary fermentation to escape                  from the primary fermentation vessel while                  preventing air from entering the primary                  fermentation vessel. The secondary fermentation                  vessel includes a second fermentation lock to                  allow carbon dioxide released during further                  anaerobic fermentation to escape from the                  secondary fermentation vessel while preventing                  air from entering the secondary fermentation                  vessel.

PT P
PN CN108751235-A
TI Preparing nano-grade high-purity light magnesium carbonate comprises taking industrial grade magnesium hydroxide coarse powder as raw material, firstly grinding the magnesium hydroxide suspension slurry of nanoscale by liquid phase.
AU MO Y
AE SUZHOU ZEMEI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018885888
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-grade high-purity light magnesium carbonate comprises taking industrial grade magnesium hydroxide coarse powder as raw material, firstly grinding the magnesium hydroxide suspension slurry of nanoscale by liquid phase, slowly adding the ammonium carbonate(Sic) solution to nano magnesium hydroxide sizing agent to react, introducing carbon dioxide as bicarbonatehydrolysis reaction of balance agent until all the magnesium hydroxide into magnesium carbonate precipitate, filtering and separating the solid precipitate, drying to obtain the nano-grade highpurity magnesium carbonate powder, specifically adding de-ionized water, industrial grade magnesium hydroxide crude powder and aqueous dispersant according to a certain proportion, weighing and mixing uniformly, adding uniformly mixed solution into the sand mill to wet liquid-phase ball milling, the ball milling medium is high purity yttria-stabilized zirconia ceramic ball.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano-grade high-purity light magnesium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, has mild reaction condition, and satisfy the requirement of batch industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano-grade high-purity light magnesium carbonate comprises taking industrial grade magnesium hydroxide coarse powder as raw material, firstly grinding the magnesium hydroxide suspension slurry of nanoscale by liquid phase, slowly adding the ammonium carbonate solution to nano magnesium hydroxide sizing agent to react, introducing carbon dioxide as bicarbonatehydrolysis reaction of balance agent until all the magnesium hydroxide into magnesium carbonate precipitate, filtering and separating the solid precipitate, drying to obtain the nano-grade highpurity magnesium carbonate powder, specifically adding de-ionized water, industrial grade magnesium hydroxide crude powder and aqueous dispersant according to a certain proportion, weighing and mixing uniformly, adding uniformly mixed solution into the sand mill to wet liquid-phase ball milling, the ball milling medium is high purity yttria-stabilized zirconia ceramic ball, wet grinding and mixing for 2-40 hours to obtain evenly-mixed nano magnesium hydroxide sizing agent, the ammonium bicarbonate solution with the concentration of 1 mol/l to obtain the nanograde magnesium hydroxide in the slurry for reaction, controlling temperature at 60-90 degrees C, and controlling the pH value between 8.5-9.0, reaction time is 1-6 hours, continuously introducing carbon dioxide to the reverse reaction of ammonium double hydrolysis, until all of the magnesium hydroxide is converted into light magnesium carbonate precipitate, filtering the solution, separating the solid precipitate, drying to obtain the nano-grade high-purity magnesium carbonate powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The de-ionized water is industrial grade magnesium hydroxide coarse powder and aqueous dispersant with ratio of 50-80: 10-40:1-10. The nano level magnesium hydroxide slurry average particle size is 50-100. The ball milling medium is 0.1-0.05 mm. The drying temperature is 100-180 degrees C and drying for 1-5 hours.

PT P
PN CN107720784-A; CN107720784-B
TI Preparing alkali using a mixed salt solution comprises adopting process flow, which is divided into two stages as start-up phase and a recycling phase, where the start-up phase includes concentrating to obtain saturated mother liquor.
AU CHEN J
   HAN M
   LI L
   LI Z
   LIU X
   SONG Y
   WEI Y
AE BEIJING SILVER LINING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201817941P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alkali using a mixed salt solution comprises adopting process flow, which is divided into two stages as a start-up phase and a recycling phase, where the start-up phase includes concentrating the mixed salt solution to obtain saturated mother liquor of fixed composition, reacting the saturated mother liquor with carbon dioxide and ammonia to obtain a base and a slurry I containing sodium bicarbonate, subjecting the slurry I to solid-liquid separation, washing the solid part, drying and calcining to obtain soda ash, evaporating the liquid part and concentrating to obtain a slurry II.
   USE - The method does not waste water and discharges waste residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not waste water and discharges waste residue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing alkali using a mixed salt solution comprises adopting process flow, which is divided into two stages as a start-up phase and a recycling phase, where the start-up phase includes (i) concentrating the mixed salt solution to obtain saturated mother liquor of fixed composition; (ii) reacting the saturated mother liquor with carbon dioxide and ammonia to obtain a base and a slurry I containing sodium bicarbonate; (iii) subjecting the slurry I to solid-liquid separation, washing the solid part, drying and calcining to obtain soda ash; (iv) evaporating the liquid part and concentrating to obtain a slurry II containing ammonia salt; (v) subjecting the slurry II to solid-liquid separation, washing the solid part and drying to obtain mixed ammonium salt; (vi) subjecting the liquid part to supplementary saturated mother liquor to obtain circulating reaction liquid, recycling the circulating reaction liquid for two cycles according to the recycling phase process and steady operating period; which directly adding the circulating reaction liquid during the stable operation period, where it is possible to skip the driving phase and directly perform the cycle phase; the recycling phase includes (a) concentrating the mixed salt solution to obtain fixed composition of the saturated mother liquor; (b) reacting the cyclic reaction liquid and carbon dioxide with ammonia to obtain slurry I; (c) subjecting the slurry I to solid-liquid separation, washing the solid part, drying and calcining to obtain soda; (d) evaporating the liquid part and concentrating to obtain a slurry II containing ammonia salt; (e) subjecting the slurry II to solid-liquid separation, washing the solid part and drying to obtain mixed ammonium salt and (f) subjecting the liquid part to supplementary saturated mother liquor to obtain circulating reaction liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) the saturated mother liquid is has sodium sulfate content of 84.2 g/l and sodium chloride content of 271.9 g/l; (b) the stable operation circulating reaction liquid has sodium ion concentration of 58.8-62.3 g/l, the ammonium ion concentration of 111.5-117.4 g/l, chlorine ion concentration of 175.5-179.8 g/l, the sulfate ion concentration range of 173.2-177.98 g/l; (c) the reaction temperature is 35 degrees C; carbon dioxide and ammonia is added in an amount ratio of 1.2-1.5 times of stoichiometric chemical reaction; (d) slurry evaporation concentration operation control parameters of the slurry II is 0.46-0.62 with the volume ratio of the original circulating reaction liquid; and (e) the saturated mother solution of the reaction liquid replenishing operation control parameters includes circulating reaction liquid volume obtained after the last circulating reaction liquid volume ratio of 0.95-1.05.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
