PN CN107723243-A; CN107723243-B
TI Cultivating algae comprises e.g. configuring algae concentrate, primary culturing, secondary culturing and tertiary culturing.
AU PAN X
   PAN Y
   ZHU Z
   ZHUANG X
AE HAINAN LIANGZHIYUAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 201818223F
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating algae comprises e.g. configuring algae concentrate, primary culturing by taking culture concentrate, adding water to obtain primary culture nutrient solution with concentration of 55-80 g/l, placing in culture bottle, inoculating algae into culture bottle, where inoculating density is 180-230x 103 cells/ml, injecting 8-12 wt.% carbon dioxide, shaking culturing at 3500 plus minus 500 Lux, slowly heating to 45 degrees C from room temperature within 1 hour, maintaining the temperature for 1-2 hours, slowly cooling to room temperature, secondary culturing and tertiary culturing.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating algae.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: satisfy the nutrient requirements of algae plants needed by aquaculture; realizes nutrient solution of culture concentrate can be continued to use after recycling, thus saves resource; reduces pollution in algae culture period; achieves rapid propagation of algae after culturing for 5 days, i.e. the algae concentration reaches 1.5x 1010 cells/ml, thus the culturing period is more than twice quicker than that of traditional method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating algae comprises (i) configuring algae concentrate, (ii) taking culture concentrate, adding water to obtain primary culture nutrient solution with concentration of 55-80 g/l, placing in culture bottle, inoculating algae into culture bottle, where inoculating density is 180-230x 103 cells/ml, injecting 8-12 wt.% carbon dioxide, shaking culturing at 3500 plus minus 500 Lux, slowly heating to 45 degrees C from room temperature within 1 hour, maintaining the temperature for 1-2 hours, slowly cooling to room temperature, adding ice cubes at room temperature conditions to cool the water in the algae culture flask slowly to 0 degrees C, maintaining for 5-6 hours, replacing the primary culture nutrient solution, recovering the water in the culture pond to room temperature, and adjusting pH by hydrochloric acid or sodium hydroxide to 7-10, (iii) taking the culture concentrate, adding water to configure into secondary culture nutrient solution with concentration of 130-150 g/l, inoculating the primary culture algae into secondary culture nutrient solution according to 1:6-7 volume ratio in 10 l culture tank, filling 10-13 vol.% carbon dioxide and ozone mixed gas, culturing under room temperature at 4000 plus minus 500 Lux for 4-6 days, concentrating algae liquid, transforming the concentrated algae liquid and secondary culture nutrient liquid into 20 l culture tank according to 1:6-7 volume ratio, (iii) taking the culture concentrate, adding water to configure concentration of 170-190 g/l tertiary culture nutrient solution, inoculating the secondary culture algae and tertiary culture nutrient solution according to 1:25-70 volume ratio in 1000 l culture tank and (v) collecting algae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The culture concentrate comprises 110-120 g, preferably 120 g sodium chloride, 35-40 g, preferably 36 g magnesium sulfate, 0.3-0.8 g, preferably 1 g ammonium molybdate, 1.2-1.7 g, preferably 1.5 g sodium hydrogen phosphate, 0.6-0.9 g, preferably 0.7 g ferric citrate, 0.4-0.8 g, preferably 0.5 g manganese chloride, 1-3.5 g, preferably 3 g L-threonate calcium, 2-3 g, preferably 2 g selenate ester polysaccharide, 5-8 g, preferably 6 g Pulicaria dysenterica fine powder, adding water to prepare into 1 l solution, and concentrating to obtain the culture concentrate. The algae include Nannochloropsis, Chaetoceros, Dunaliella salina, Tetraselmis, Platymonas, Phaeodactylum tricornutum and Nitzsehia closteztuma. Preferred Method: In the step (v) performing centrifugal concentration after adding inorganic flocculating agent, and then collecting.

TI Carbon dioxide electrolysis device for producing            methanol, comprises anode for forming oxygen, anode            solution flow path, cathode arranged to reduce carbon            dioxide and generate carbon compound, gas flow path,            diaphragm comprising porous film supported with            hydrophilic polymer, and protective member.

AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide electrolysis device,                comprises: 1) an anode (11) arranged to oxidize                water or a hydroxide ion, for generating oxygen, an                anode solution flow path (112) arranged to supply                an anode solution to the anode; 2) a cathode (12)                arranged to reduce carbon dioxide and generate a                carbon compound; 3) a gas flow path (122) provided                for suppling a gas containing carbon dioxide, to                the cathode; 4) a diaphragm (13) provided between                the anode and the cathode, comprising a porous film                supported with a hydrophilic polymer; and 5) a                protective member (144) provided between the anode                and the diaphragm, for protecting the                diaphragm.
   USE - Carbon dioxide electrolysis device is used for                producing chemical substances such as                methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide electrolysis device has                improved electrolysis efficiency and long-time                operating efficiency. The device enables to reduce                carbon dioxide, to form carbon monoxide and                hydrogen, at an arbitrary ratio 1:2, and produce                methanol in subsequent chemical reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic diagram of carbon                dioxide electrolysis device.
   11Anode
   12Cathode
   13Diaphragm
   112Anode solution flow path
   122Gas flow path
   144Protective member
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: 1) The cathode of the                  carbon dioxide electrolysis device, is provided                  on the diaphragm.2) The device further comprises a cathode                  solution flow path provided for supplying a                  cathode solution to the cathode.3) The cathode comprises a first surface                  facing the gas flow path and a second surface                  facing the cathode solution flow path.4) The cathode solution flow path is                  arranged between the diaphragm and the cathode,                  to provide the cathode solution to the diaphragm                  and the cathode.5) The protective member contains a third                  surface on the anode and a fourth surface on the                  diaphragm.6) The anode of the device, comprises an                  oxidation catalyst supported on a support and                  provided on the third surface.7) The device further comprises a power                  supply arranged to supply an electric current                  between the anode and the cathode.Preferred Compounds: The cathode solution                  comprises phosphate ion, borate ion, sodium ion,                  potassium ion, calcium ion, lithium ion, cesium                  ion, magnesium ion, chloride ion, hydrogen                  carbonate ion and/or carbonate ion. The anode                  solution comprises phosphate ion, borate ion,                  sodium ion, potassium ion, calcium ion, lithium                  ion, cesium ion, magnesium ion, chloride ion,                  hydrogen carbonate ion and/or carbonate                  ion.Preferred Properties: The protective member                  of the device, comprises an insulator having                  electric resistivity 10 3- 10 19ohmm. The member                  further comprises a metal oxide, glass and/or                  ceramics.Preferred Conditions: The permeation rate of                  water passing through the protective member is                  equal to or more than the permeation rate of                  water passing through the diaphragm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The porous film of the                  diaphragm contained in the carbon dioxide                  electrolysis device, comprises                  polytetrafluoroethylene, polyetheretherketone,                  polyethylene, polyphenylsulfone, polysulfone,                  polyethersulfone, polyacrylonitrile,                  polyetherimide, polyamideimide, polyvinylidene                  fluoride, polycarbonate, polyester, acrylic,                  nylon, polyolefin, polyurethane and/or                  polypropylene.The hydrophilic polymer supported on the                  porous film of diaphragm, comprises polyvinyl                  alcohol, polyvinylpyrrolidone, methoxy                  polyethylene oxide methacrylate, polyacrylic                  acid, polyethyleneimine, polyvinyl amine,                  cyclodextrin and/or methyl cellulose.

   ZHANG F

AE NANJING YANCHANG REACTION TECHNOLOGY RES (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202264687H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate by catalyzing                carbon dioxide cycloaddition, comprises taking                Schiff alkali metal compound (I) and imidazole                bicarbonate compound (II) as catalyst, carbon                dioxide and epoxy compound as raw material and                carrying out cycloaddition reaction to obtain final                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic                carbonate by catalyzing carbon dioxide                cycloaddition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can reduce the temperature and                pressure required for the reaction; reduces the                energy consumption of the reaction, and improves                the reaction efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cyclic carbonate by catalyzing                carbon dioxide cycloaddition, comprises taking                Schiff alkali metal compound of formula (I) and                imidazole bicarbonate compound of formula (II) as                catalyst, carbon dioxide and epoxy compound as raw                material and carrying out cycloaddition reaction to                obtain final product.
   R = ethyl, phenyl, cyclohexyl or                naphthyl;
   M = copper, manganese, zinc or aluminum;
   X = Cl, Br, CH3, CH3CO2, BF4or PF6;
   R1= methyl, ethyl, propyl, n-butyl, n-pentyl                or n-hexyl; and
   R2= substitution.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                system for preparing cyclic carbonate by catalyzing                carbon dioxide cycloaddition, where the cyclic                carbonate is prepared by above method.
                                                                                                                                                                                 
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic                diagram of the system for preparing cyclic                carbonate by catalyzing carbon dioxide                cycloaddition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The cycloaddition reaction                  includes breaking carbon dioxide into micro-level                  micro-bubbles through the micro-interface, then                  mixing with the epoxy compound to carry out the                  cycloaddition reaction under the catalysis of the                  catalyst. The cycloaddition reaction is carried                  out at 25-80&#8451;, preferably 40&#8451;                  The pressure of the cycloaddition reaction is                  0.1-0.3 MPa, preferably 0.2 MPa.Preferred Components: The epoxy compound is                  ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin,                  butyl ethylene oxide, styrene oxide, isopropyl                  glycidyl ether, allyl glycidyl ether, phenyl                  glycidyl ether or cyclohexene oxide. The system                  comprises a reactor and a micro-interface unit;                  the micro-interface unit is arranged inside the                  reactor; the side wall of the reactor is                  sequentially connected with a first feed pipeline                  for introducing epoxy compounds and a second feed                  pipeline for introducing carbon dioxide from top                  to bottom; and the second feeding pipeline is                  connected with the micro-interface unit to                  disperse and break the carbon dioxide into                  micro-bubbles of micron level. The                  micro-interface unit includes a first                  micro-interface generator and a second                  micro-interface generator; the first                  micro-interface generator is arranged above the                  second micro-interface generator, and the first                  micro-interface generator is opposite to the                  outlet of the second micro-interface generator. A                  bubble distributor is provided at the outlet of                  the first micro-interface generator and the                  second micro-interface generator and the air                  bubble distributor is in the shape of a cone, and                  a plurality of distribution holes are arranged on                  the air bubble distributor. The reactor is                  connected with a flash tank, and the flash tank                  is connected with a distillation column and the                  part flows into the rectification column after                  the reaction product in the reactor is flashed by                  the flash tank and the other part is refluxed                  into the reactor.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing melamine by the pyrolysis of urea, comprises e.g. (a) producing melamine by the pyrolysis of urea in a specific reactor (R) and separating biphasic liquid/gas effluent produced in pyrolysis reaction of urea into a liquid stream of raw melamine and to first stream of anhydrous off-gases having ammonia, carbon dioxide and melamine vapor, (b) putting liquid stream of raw melamine in contact with stream of anhydrous gaseous ammonia in specific post-reactor (PR) and forming liquid stream of melamine impoverished in carbon dioxide and partially purified and second stream of anhydrous gases having ammonia , carbon dioxide and melamine vapor, and (c) putting first stream of anhydrous gases coming from step (a) in contact with aqueous washing stream, forming aqueous stream having melamine, ammonia, carbon dioxide and gaseous stream of wet off-gases, comprising ammonia , carbon dioxide and water vapor.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing melamine by the pyrolysis of urea, comprises (a) producing melamine by the pyrolysis of urea in a specific reactor (R) and separating biphasic liquid/gas effluent produced in pyrolysis reaction of urea into a liquid stream of raw melamine and to first stream of anhydrous off-gases having ammonia, carbon dioxide and melamine vapor, (b) putting liquid stream of raw melamine in contact with stream of anhydrous gaseous ammonia in specific post-reactor (PR) and forming liquid stream of melamine impoverished in carbon dioxide and partially purified and second stream of anhydrous gases having ammonia, carbon dioxide and melamine vapor, (c) putting first stream of anhydrous gases coming from step (a) in contact with aqueous washing stream, forming aqueous stream having melamine, ammonia, carbon dioxide and gaseous stream of wet off-gases, comprising ammonia , carbon dioxide and water vapor, (d) mixing second stream of anhydrous gases coming from step (b) with stream of recycled water and forming aqueous ammonia solution in which melamine contained in stream of anhydrous gases is dissolved, recovering sensible heat and condensation heat of gases, (e) removing, from aqueous stream of step (c), comprising melamine, ammonia, carbon dioxide, a part of carbon dioxide contained in and forming gaseous stream having carbon dioxide removed and aqueous stream comprising melamine, ammonia and impoverished in carbon dioxide, (f) subjecting liquid stream of melamine impoverished in carbon dioxide and partially purified, coming from post-reactor, to quench-ammonolysis treatment by contact with aqueous ammonia solution obtained in step (d), and formation of aqueous ammonia stream comprising purified melamine, and (g) recovering melamine contained in aqueous ammonia stream comprising purified melamine coming from step (f) and melamine contained in aqueous stream comprising melamine and impoverished in carbon dioxide coming from step (e) by crystallization step by cooling and separation with formation of stream of crystallized melamine and stream of mother liquor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for plant for the implementation of process for the production of melamine by the pyrolysis of urea comprising e.g. : (i) separation section for separating a biphasic liquid/gas effluent produced in a pyrolysis reaction of urea into a liquid stream of raw melamine and a first stream of anhydrous off-gases comprising ammonia, carbon dioxide and melamine steam, the separation section being connected to a reaction section (R) for the pyrolysis of urea from which it receives the biphasic liquid/gas effluent, the separation section being inside or outside the (R); (ii) stripping section or post-reactor (PR) for putting the liquid stream of raw melamine coming from the (R) in contact with a stream of anhydrous gaseous ammonia and forming a liquid stream of melamine impoverished in carbon dioxide and partially purified and a second stream of anhydrous gases comprising ammonia, carbon dioxide and melamine steam, the stripping section (PR) being connected to the (R) from which it receives the liquid stream of raw melamine;(iii) a washing section (Q1) for putting the first stream of anhydrous off-gases in contact with an aqueous washing stream and forming an aqueous stream comprising melamine, ammonia, carbon dioxide and a stream of wet off-gases comprising ammonia, carbon dioxide and water vapor, the (Q1) being connected to the separation section from which it receives the first stream of anhydrous off-gases; and (iv) a removal section of the carbon dioxide (STR) for removing from the aqueous stream comprising melamine, ammonia, carbon dioxide, at least a part of the carbon dioxide contained therein and comprising the carbon dioxide removed and an aqueous stream, comprising melamine, ammonia and impoverished in carbon dioxide, (STR) being connected to the washing section (Q1) from which it receives the aqueous stream, comprising melamine, ammonia, carbon dioxide; (v) a mixing and heat recovery section (AA) for mixing the second stream of anhydrous gases with a stream of recycled water and forming an aqueous ammonia solution in which the melamine contained in the stream of anhydrous off-gases is dissolved, and for recovering both the sensible heat and the condensation heat of the gases, by absorption of the ammonia, the (AA) being connected to the (PR) from which it receives the second stream of anhydrous gases and the quench-ammonolysis section (QAL) to which the aqueous ammonia solution is fed;(vi) the (QAL) for subjecting the liquid stream of melamine impoverished in carbon dioxide and partially purified, coming from the (PR), to a quench-ammonolysis treatment by contact with the aqueous ammonia solution coming from the mixing and (AA), and forming an aqueous ammonia stream, comprising purified melamine, the QAL being connected to the (PR) from which it receives the liquid stream of melamine impoverished in carbon dioxide and partially purified; (vii) at least one recovery section of the melamine (CR, SLS) for recovering both the melamine contained in the aqueous ammonia stream comprising purified melamine and also the melamine contained in the aqueous stream comprising melamine, ammonia and impoverished in carbon dioxide through crystallization by cooling and separation, with the formation of a stream of crystallized melamine and a stream of mother liquor, the (CR, SLS) being connected to both the QAL from which it receives the aqueous ammonia stream comprising purified melamine and to the removal section of the carbon dioxide (STR) from which it receives the aqueous stream comprising melamine, ammonia and impoverished in carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further comprises feeding a make-up ammonia stream to a subsequent demonization treatment of the portion of mother liquor stream not directly recycled, stream. The step (d), the second stream of anhydrous gases coming from step (b) is mixed with the stream of recycled water and with a stream obtained by joining a make-up ammonia stream and a stream of recycled ammonia forming the aqueous ammonia solution. The process has: at least a part of the mother liquor stream obtained in step (g) is used as aqueous stream in the quench-ammonolysis treatment of the liquid melamine stream impoverished in carbon dioxide and partially purified; at least a part of the mother liquor stream obtained in step (g) is subjected to demonization treatment, with the formation of a gaseous stream of ammonia substantially free of carbon dioxide, a stream comprising carbon dioxide and an aqueous stream of demonization mother liquor; the stream comprising carbon dioxide, ammonia and water is joined with a further stream, containing water, carbon dioxide and ammonia and coming from a following step of decomposition of the stream to form a single stream and recycled to a production plant of urea directly or after treatment which reduces its water content or the stream containing carbon dioxide, ammonia and water, is sent to the washing step (c) of the anhydrous off-gases; the aqueous stream of ammonia ted mother liquor is subjected to a decomposition treatment with the recovery of ammonia and carbon dioxide, and the production of an aqueous stream which is partly recycled as aqueous washing stream to the step (c) and partly recycled as aqueous stream to contribute to forming the aqueous ammonia stream fed to step (f) for the quench-ammonolysis treatment and a stream containing water, carbon dioxide and ammonia that is joined with the stream comprising carbon dioxide, ammonia and water, coming from the demonization treatment; the gaseous stream of ammonia substantially free of carbon dioxide, coming from the demonization treatment, is mixed with a part of the aqueous stream, to recover its condensation heat in step (d); the aqueous stream comprising melamine, ammonia and impoverished in carbon dioxide coming from step (e), is joined with the aqueous ammonia stream comprising purified melamine, with the formation of a single stream to be subjected to the step (g);in step (e), the removal of carbon dioxide from the aqueous stream comprising melamine, ammonia and carbon dioxide is effected by depressurization or by means of stripping; the stream comprising removed carbon dioxide, obtained in step (e), is recycled in a step of the same process following the step (g) for the recovery of melamine through crystallization by cooling and separation; the stream of wet off-gases, coming from step (c), comprising ammonia, carbon dioxide and water vapor, is recycled to a plant for the production of urea, or after being subjected to a condensation treatment by absorption in aqueous solution or another type of treatment; and the aqueous stream of deammoniated mother liquor is separated into (i) an aqueous stream enriched in melamine which is partly recycled as aqueous washing stream to the step (c) and partly recycled as aqueous stream to contribute to forming the aqueous ammonia stream fed to step (f) for the quench-ammonolysis treatment and (ii) an aqueous stream enriched in oxyaminotriazine, suitable for being decomposed with the recovery of ammonia and carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - The concentration of ammonia constant in stream leaving the quench-ammonolysis section, stream of make-up ammonia was in fact reduced. The stream of make-up ammonia was reduced by an amount equal to the ammonia contained in stream, i.e. by a quantity equal to 1.1 tones/hour (t/h) ,which was equal to the ammonia fed to the section. The quantity of make-up ammonia thus proved to be lower than that. The total amount of ammonia which must be fed to the melamine plant thus proved to be equal to the sum of streams. The stream of ammonia fed to the melamine plant was equal to 2.9 t/h, correspond to 0.58 t/t melamine produced. The total quantity of ammonia also proved to be lower. The stream of anhydrous gases leaving the post-reaction section was no longer sent to the washing section, it no longer contributes to the quantity of wet off-gases produced, neither does it give rise to the other negative effects, and allowed the quantity of anhydrous ammonia fed to the section, to be increased, thus obtaining a higher efficiency of the section, with a higher conversion of polycondensates into melamine and with the almost complete transformation of oxyaminotriazine into melamine. The heat recovered in treating stream in the section allowed a reduction equal to 450 kg/hour in the quantity of saturated vapor used for heating the ammonia solution. The ammonia and vapor consumptions of the process proved to be equal to 0.58 t/t(ton/ton) melamine.

PN CN114763516-A
TI Promoting carbon fixation and fatty acid            production in microalgae by plant hormones under stress            of mercury in flue gas, comprises e.g. inoculating            oil-producing microalgae on medium, culturing to log            phase, inoculating, culturing to logarithmic phase,            inoculating and culturing.
AU KONG W
   SHEN B
AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 202297325P
AB    NOVELTY - Promoting carbon fixation and fatty acid                production in microalgae by plant hormones under                stress of mercury in flue gas, comprises e.g.                inoculating oil-producing microalgae on medium and                culturing to log phase under specific conditions,                inoculating obtained microalgae into sterilized                wastewater, and culturing to logarithmic phase                under specific conditions, inoculating domesticated                microalgae into wastewater, adding indole-3-acetic                acid, culturing to log phase under specific                conditions to obtain biomass, Z at the maximum                biomass is Y, where introduce 5-20% CO2 gas of                volume of culture environment atmosphere in time of                illumination, and inoculating obtained domesticated                microalgae into wastewater at density of 5-10                mgdw.L-1, adding indole-3-acetic acid to make                concentration of indole-3-acetic acid to Y mg/l,                continue to culturing under specific conditions,                where introduce exhaust gas containing carbon                dioxide during illumination.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting carbon                fixation and fatty acid production in microalgae by                plant hormones under stress of mercury in flue                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves biomass yield of                microalgae, carbon-fixing capacity of microalgae,                capacity of purifying wastewater and yield of fatty                acid under stress of flue gas pollutants i.e.                mercury.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Promoting carbon fixation and fatty acid                production in microalgae by plant hormones under                stress of mercury in flue gas comprises (i)                inoculating oil-producing microalgae on medium at                density of 5-10 mgdw.L-1, and culturing to log                phase under specific conditions to obtain                microalgae, where the specific conditions includes                pH is 7-8, temperature is 25-30&#8451;, light                intensity is 3000-6000 Lux, and light time is 8-14                hours, (ii) inoculating obtained microalgae into                sterilized wastewater at density of 5-10 mgdw.L-1,                and culturing to logarithmic phase under specific                conditions to obtain domesticated microalgae, where                introduce 5-20% carbon dioxide gas during                illumination of volume of culture environment                atmosphere, (iii) inoculating domesticated                microalgae into wastewater at density of 5-10                mgdw.L-1, adding indole-3-acetic acid with                concentration of indole-3-acetic acid of Z mg/l, Z                is 0.1-10, culturing to log phase under specific                conditions to obtain biomass, Z at the maximum                biomass is Y, where introduce 5-20% carbon dixoide                gas of volume of culture environment atmosphere, in                time of illumination, and (iv) inoculating obtained                domesticated microalgae into wastewater at density                of 5-10 mgdw.L-1, adding indole-3-acetic acid to                make concentration of indole-3-acetic acid to Y                mg/l, continue to culturing under specific                conditions, where introduce exhaust gas containing                carbon dioxide during illumination.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                domesticated microalgae in improving fatty acid                production, biomass density, carbon fixation rate,                expression of PsbA gene and/or removal rate of                nitrogen nutrients in wastewater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  culture medium is BG11 culture medium. In the                  step (i), the oil-producing microalgae is                  Chlorella, Scenedesmus or Spirulina. In the step                  (ii), the waste water is filtered municipal waste                  water, domestic sewage or brewery waste                  water.Preferred Method: In the step (i), perform                  transfer every 10-45 days and continue to culture                  under the specific conditions during the culture.                  In the step (i), the culture is carried out in an                  air environment. In the step (iii), the                  absorbance is obtained by testing, and the Z                  corresponding to maximum absorbance is Y after                  culturing to logarithmic phase, where the                  absorbance is measured at wavelength of 680 nm,                  and the flow rate of gas in culture environment                  atmosphere is 0.05-0.2 vvm.

PN CN114410380-A
TI Extracting essential oil from folium artemisiae argyi comprises cutting dried folium artemisiae argyi, adding high-activity biological catalyst, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide, separating reactant, condensing, reacting and separating oil and water.
AU HOU S
AE HUNAN DEYUE ECOLOGICAL AGRIC DEV CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202269462S
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting essential oil from folium artemisiae argyi comprises (a) taking fresh folium artemisiae argyi, stacking in the vacuum dry box, drying, using a cutting device to cut, obtaining qualified dry artemisiae argyi leaf, (b) placing the obtained dry folium artemisiae argyi into the reaction kettle, adding high-activity biological catalyst into the reaction kettle, fully mixing, (c) placing the mixture into the extracting kettle, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide, (d) placing the reactant in the extracting kettle into the reaction kettle, adjusting the temperature and pressure in the reaction kettle, analyzing and separating the reactant, (e) placing the folium artemisiae argyi extract into molecular distillation device, sending the folium artemisiae argyi extract for molecular distillation, condensing, and (f) placing the mixture into the reaction kettle, reacting and separating the oil and water to obtain the folium artemisiae argyi essential oil heavy oil.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting essential oil from folium artemisiae argyi.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the reaction efficiency; reduces the production time through pretreatment of argyi leaves; improves the quality of finished products through molecular distillation technology and improves the obtaining rate of essential oil by adjusting the process parameters like pressure and pressurization time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting essential oil from folium artemisiae argyi comprises (a) taking fresh folium artemisiae argyi, stacking in the vacuum dry box, adjusting the vacuum degree and the vacuum temperature, drying for a period of time, obtaining the dry artemisiae argyi, using a cutting device to cut, obtaining qualified dry artemisiae argyi leaf, (b) placing the obtained dry folium artemisiae argyi into the reaction kettle, adding high-activity biological catalyst into the reaction kettle, fully mixing, allowing to stand for 1 hour, making it fully react, (c) placing the mixture into the extracting kettle, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide into the extracting kettle, adjusting the temperature in the extracting kettle, adjusting the pressure in the extracting kettle, making it fully react, (d) placing the reactant in the extracting kettle into the reaction kettle, adjusting the temperature and pressure in the reaction kettle, analyzing and separating the reactant to obtain the folium artemisiae argyi hydrosol and folium artemisiae argyi extract, (e) placing the folium artemisiae argyi extract into molecular distillation device, heating to 70degrees Celsius, sending the folium artemisiae argyi extract for molecular distillation, condensing, obtaining the needed folium artemisiae argyi essential oil and folium artemisiae argyi wax mixture and (f) placing the mixture into the reaction kettle, adjusting the temperature in the reaction kettle at 40degrees Celsius, where the reaction pressure is 42 MPa, reacting for 30-40 minutes and separating the oil and water to obtain the folium artemisiae argyi essential oil heavy oil.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method of extracting essential oil from folium artemisiae argyi. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The high-activity biological catalyst is animal and plant, microorganism derived enzyme or microbial cells. The microorganism-derived enzyme is endoglucanase, exoglucanase and/or beta-glucan glycosidase.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN114163641-A; CN114163641-B
TI New aminated polybenzimidazole useful in preparing            aminated polybenzimidazole ionomer solution used as            ionic liquid or electrolyte solution in fuel cells,            water electrolysis hydrogen production, carbon dioxide            conversion and electrochemical device fields in            preparing alkaline ion-exchange membrane.
AU GUO M
   ZHU X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202241459P
AB    NOVELTY - Aminated polybenzimidazole (I) is new. (I) is                obtained by condensation polymerization reaction of                aromatic tetraamine monomer, dicarboxylic acid                monomer and norbornene diacid monomer.
   USE - (I) is useful: in preparing aminated                polybenzimidazole ionomer solution used as ionic                liquid or electrolyte solution in fuel cells, water                electrolysis hydrogen production, carbon dioxide                conversion and electrochemical device fields; and                in preparing alkaline ion-exchange membrane used in                fuel cells, water electrolysis hydrogen production,                carbon dioxide conversion, flow batteries,                chlor-alkali industry and electrochemical devices                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has intrinsic viscosity of 1.5-5 dl/g;                and viscosity average molecular weight of                10,000-500,000 g/mol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Aminated polybenzimidazole of formula (I) is                new. (I) is obtained by condensation polymerization                reaction of aromatic tetraamine monomer,                dicarboxylic acid monomer and norbornene diacid                monomer.
   n, k, l = 0-1;
   m = 0-6;
   Z = chlorine ion, bromine ion, iodine ion,                bicarbonate ion or hydroxide ion;
   A = chemical single bond, methylene, ether                bond or carbonyl group;
   X = cyclohexane-1,4-diyl or                cyclohexane-1,3-diyl;
   Y = benzene-1,3-diyl, pyridine-2,6-diyl,                benzene-1,4-diyl, naphthalene-1,4-diyl or                naphthalene-1,5-diyl;
   G = tetramethylammonium ion,                1,1,1,3,3-pentamethylguanidinium ion,                1,1-dimethylpyrrolidinium ion,                1,1-dimethylpiperidinium ion,                4,4-dimethylmorpholin-4-ium ion or                1-methylpyridinium ion; and
   q = 2-18
   Provided that: (I) can be cross-linked by                adding compounds with double bonds when l is does                not equal 0; and G is methyl and Z is iodine ion                when m is 0.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) Preparation of (I);
   (2) Preparing aminated polybenzimidazole                ionomer solution, comprising stirring and                dissolving dried (I) in solvent C to prepare 1-10                wt.% solution, filtering and defoaming to obtain                transparent ionomer solution, where the solvent C                is dimethyl sulfoxide, N,N-dimethylformamide,                N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylpyrrolidone,                methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, formic acid,                acetone, acetic acid and/or methanesulfonic acid;                and
   (3) Preparing alkaline ion-exchange membrane                by using aminated polybenzimidazole, comprising (a)                adding dry (I) into solvent D, heating and stirring                to dissolve to make 1-20 wt.% solution, filtering,                defoaming, pouring the solution on a clean plate,                scraping the film with a scraper, and then placing                in a dry oven, peeling off the membrane to obtain                bromine-type I alkaline ion exchange membrane,                immersing bromine-type (I) alkaline ion membrane in                1M or 2M sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide                solution for ion exchange for 24-48 hours, then                taking out and washing with deionized water until                neutral to obtain alkaline ion exchange membrane of                OH-type (I), where the solvent D is                N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylpyrrolidone,                N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide,                methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, formic acid,                dichloromethane, tetrahydrofuran and/or                methanesulfonic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) performing solution                  polycondensation reaction on aromatic tetraamine                  monomer of formula (II), dicarboxylic acid                  monomer (III) and norbornene dicarboxylic acid,                  adding polyphosphoric acid into the reactor,                  stirring, adding phosphorus pentoxide and                  stirring under the protection of nitrogen, adding                  (II), (III) and norbornene dicarboxylic acid,                  stirring and dissolving and thus the total                  concentration of the monomer is 1-30 wt.%,                  raising the temperature to 90-160degrees Celsius,                  stirring and reacting for 0.5-10 hours, raising                  the temperature to 160-210degrees Celsius and                  reacting for 8-30 hours, pouring the reaction                  solution into deionized water, precipitating,                  adding excess sodium bicarbonate, stirring,                  filtering, washing repeatedly with deionized                  water until neutral, drying to obtain                  polybenzimidazole, where the total moles of (III)                  and norbornene dicarboxylic acid are equal to the                  moles of (II), norbornene dicarboxylic acid                  accounts for 0-40% of the total molar content of                  (III) and norbornene dicarboxylic acid; the mass                  fraction of phosphorus pentoxide in                  polyphosphoric acid is 1-26 wt.%, the (III) is                  HO(O=)C-X-C(=O)OH a or HO(O=)C-Y-C(=O)OH, (ii)                  when m is does not equal 0 adding halogenated                  alkane of formula (P-(CH2CH2) m-P) (IV) into the                  solvent A in a reactor, fully stirring and                  dissolving, slowly adding tertiary amine solution                  into the reactor and thus the total concentration                  of the reactants haloalkane and tertiary amine is                  1-50 wt.%, then stirring and reacting at                  15-100degrees Celsius for 5-70 hours, pouring the                  reaction solution into precipitant A, filtering,                  repeatedly washing the product with detergent A,                  and vacuum drying to obtain long-chain ammonium                  salt of formula (V); PN US2021275277-A1
TI Medical device for intracorporeally cleaning a laparoscope, comprises service box including pump and gas valve, where each of pump and gas valve is in fluid communication with exit valve through fluid feed line.
AU VANERIAN C
   BONUCCI T
   KUUTTILA W
   CROFT M
   KAUR S
   TALEKAR A
   KELLY R
   OZBEKI H G
   KRAMER K
   RECANATI M A
AE VANERIAN C (VANE-Individual)
   BONUCCI T (BONU-Individual)
   KUUTTILA W (KUUT-Individual)
   CROFT M (CROF-Individual)
   KAUR S (KAUR-Individual)
   TALEKAR A (TALE-Individual)
   KELLY R (KELL-Individual)
   OZBEKI H G (OZBE-Individual)
   KRAMER K (KRAM-Individual)
   RECANATI M A (RECA-Individual)
GA 2021A3606F
AB    NOVELTY - The medical device (100) has a service box (102) with a pump and a gas valve each in fluid communication with an exit valve via a fluid feed line. The pump transfers a liquid into the service box, through the fluid feed line, and out of the exit valve. The gas valve selectively permits a transfer of gas into the service box, through the fluid feed line, and out of the exit valve. A trocar (104) is in fluid communication with the exit valve via a flexible tubing (106) and includes a hub (132) and a cannula (134) having an open end. The hub receives a laparoscope and directs the laparoscope through the open end of cannula. The cannula receives liquid and gas from the exit valve and directs the liquid and gas to the open end of cannula, thus cleaning the laparoscope when received by hub.
   USE - Medical device for intracorporeally cleaning laparoscope of patient during minimally invasive surgery to maintain visualization of surgical field.
   ADVANTAGE - The medical device can clean the laparoscope and restore full visualization for the operator without having to remove it from the surgical field, optimizing the surgical procedure and overall efficiency. The laparoscopic surgery is a minimally invasive technique for performing surgical procedures without resorting to more invasive surgical procedures. The device can be safer than conventional tools as it has no parts which can separate during surgery. Provides a device capable of cleaning the laparoscope without removing it from the abdomen. Provides a device that has no moving components and no small loose components that can separate inside a patient. Provides a device that uses physiological saline under pressure as a way of cleaning the laparoscope lens. Provides a device that uses warm saline as a way of rapidly bringing the laparoscopic lens to body temperature and prevent fogging and condensation. Provides a device that uses a puff of carbon dioxide to dry the lens assembly. Provides a device that can, at the push of a button, sequence a bolus of saline and the puff of carbon dioxide with minimal intervention by the operator. Provides a device that is easy to assemble prior to laparoscopic surgical cases. Provides a device that is safe to use and has no sharp angles. Provides a disposable device. Provides a device that can interface with existing carbon dioxide insufflators and regulators commonly used in the operating room as well as normal saline containers, such as intravenous (IV) bags, which are found in the medical setting.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of performing a laparoscopic procedure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a top perspective view of a medical device that permits a laparoscope to be cleaned intracorporeally.
   Medical device (100)
   Service box (102)
   Trocar (104)
   Flexible tubing (106)
   Hub (132)
   Cannula (134)

PN CN111978430-A
TI Preparing oat beta-glucan comprises e.g. dehydrating, drying oat grains, crushing, separating and microwave drying oat bran, subjecting oat bran to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and inserting carbon rod electrode for electrolysis.
AU YAO C
   LU Q
AE GUANGZHOU BETA HEALTH BIOLOGICAL TECHNOL (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B9402H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing oat beta -glucan comprises e.g. (i) dehydrating and drying the oat grains, crushing, separating and microwave drying oat bran; (ii) subjecting the oat bran of step (i) to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction; (iii) dispersing the oat bran treated in step (ii) into clean water, and inserting a carbon rod electrode for electrolysis for 10-15 minutes; (iv) processing electrolyzed mixed solution under ultrasonic processing for 60-150 minutes, adjusting the temperature and pH, and adding the mixed enzymatic hydrolysis to obtain the enzymatic hydrolysis solution; (v) adjusting the pH of the enzymolysis solution to 8-10, extracting and repeating the operation for 1-3 times; (vi) adjusting the pH of the extract in step (v) to 3-6, heating, allowing to stand for precipitation and separating the supernatant; (vii) adding 0.01-2 wt.% chitosan into the supernatant of step (vi), adding 0.1-5 wt.% sodium alginate, stirring, allowing it to settle and separating to get supernatant.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing oat beta -glucan.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can provide improved the extraction rate of beta -glucan by remove impurities to ensure product purity, utilizes different specifications of separation membranes to separate products with different molecular weights in series; does not destroy chemical structure of beta -glucan to the greatest extent thus maintains the superior chemical activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing oat beta -glucan comprises (i) dehydrating and drying the oat grains, crushing to 10-150 meshes, separating and microwave drying oat bran at 20-60 kW for 5-10 minutes; (ii) subjecting the oat bran of step (i) to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction for 3-5 hours; (iii) dispersing the oat bran treated in step (ii) into clean water, and inserting a carbon rod electrode for electrolysis for 10-15 minutes; (iv) processing electrolyzed mixed solution under ultrasonic processing for 60-150 minutes, adjusting the temperature and pH, and adding the mixed enzymatic hydrolysis to obtain the enzymatic hydrolysis solution; (v) adjusting the pH of the enzymolysis solution to 8-10 at 20-95 degrees C, extracting for 30-90 minutes and repeating the operation for 1-3 times; (vi) adjusting the pH of the extract in step (v) to 3-6, heating to 80-100 degrees C for 30-90 minutes, allowing to stand for precipitation for 1-12 hours, and separating the supernatant; (vii) adding 0.01-2 wt.% chitosan into the supernatant of step (vi), adding 0.1-5 wt.% sodium alginate, stirring evenly, allowing it to settle for 1-15 hours, and separating to get supernatant; and (viii) filtering the supernatant of step (vii) with ultra filtration membranes with different molecular weight cut-offs by means of serial membrane separation, collecting the retentate in sections, concentrating the retentate, cooling to room temperature, collecting the precipitate after alcohol precipitation, and drying the precipitate under vacuum to obtain high-purity oat beta -glucan with different molecular weights
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction pressure is 20-50 MPa, the temperature is 40-60 degrees C, and the extraction flow rate is 2200-4300 liter/hour. In the step (iv), the ultrasonic treatment is carried out at 40-60 degrees C for 60-150 minutes. In the step (iv), the method comprises adjusting the temperature to 30-40 degrees C and pH 6-8 before enzymolysis, adding the mixed enzymes alpha -amylase, glucoamylase and alkaline protease, where the added amount of the alpha -amylase is 0.1-0.3 wt.%, the saccharification enzyme is 0.5-3 wt.% and the alkaline protease is 1-8 wt.%. In the step (iii), the concentration adopts double-effect concentration, the concentration temperature is 40-70 degrees C, the vacuum degree is 40-80Kpa, the time is 1-10 hours, and the concentration of the concentrated solution is 20-40 degrees Be. In the step (viii), the method comprises cooling to 20-30 degrees C before alcohol precipitation, where the volume concentration of alcohol added is 60-90 wt.%, and the added volume is 4-6 times the volume of the concentrated solution. In the step (vii), the vacuum drying is carried out at 50-80 degrees C, degree of 60-100KPa for 2-8 hours. Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the material-liquid ratio of oat bran to water is 1:(10-25), adjust pH to 3-4 and electrolysis voltage is 15-20 V. In the step (viii), the molecular weight cut-off of the ultra filtration membrane is 30000-1500000 Da respectively.

TI Preparation of super-crosslinked polymer for cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide, involves refluxing dimethylformamide, n-bromobutane and super-crosslinked polymer precursor containing waste expanded polystyrene, washing and drying.
AU XIONG X
   LIAO X
AE UNIV HUAQIAO (UYHQ-C)
GA 2020520650
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of super-crosslinked polymer involves adding deionized water, styrene oxide, phenylacetylene, sodium azide, and cuprous bromide to the reaction vessel, microwave heating, washing with deionized water, filtering, dissolving solid in ethyl acetate, filtering off residual cuprous bromide, concentrating and purifying to obtain hydroxy-functionalized triazole copolymer, adding waste expanded polystyrene, anhydrous ferric trichloride, methylal and hydroxy-functionalized triazole copolymer to round-bottom flask containing 1,2-dichloroethane, refluxing, raising temperature to 80-85 degrees C, stirring, refluxing for 24-28 hours, washing reaction product with methanol, drying and Soxhlet extracting using methanol, drying to obtain super-crosslinked polymer precursor, adding dimethylformamide, n-bromobutane and super-crosslinked polymer precursor to round bottom flask, stirring, refluxing, washing reaction product with ethyl acetate and drying.
   USE - Preparation of super-crosslinked polymer used as catalyst for cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide with epoxy compound (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple, short and uses easily available raw material to produces product with high catalytic activity, high yield of 90-99% and selectivity of up to 99%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of super-crosslinked polymer involves adding deionized water, styrene oxide, phenylacetylene, sodium azide, and cuprous bromide to the reaction vessel, microwave heating at 75-80 degrees C under power of 350-400 W for 20-30 for 20-30 minutes while stirring, washing with deionized water, filtering, dissolving solid in ethyl acetate, filtering off residual cuprous bromide, concentrating by rotary evaporation and purifying by column chromatography to obtain hydroxy-functionalized triazole copolymer, adding waste expanded polystyrene, anhydrous ferric trichloride as Lewis acid catalyst, methylal as crosslinking agent and hydroxy-functionalized triazole copolymer to round-bottom flask containing 1,2-dichloroethane, stirring, refluxing at 40-45 degrees C for 4-5 hours, raising temperature to 80-85 degrees C, stirring, refluxing for 24-28 hours, washing reaction product with methanol, drying and Soxhlet extracting at 110-120 degrees C using methanol, drying at 75-80 degrees C for 10-12 hours to obtain super-crosslinked polymer precursor, adding dimethylformamide, n-bromobutane and super-crosslinked polymer precursor to round bottom flask, stirring, refluxing at 75-80 degrees C for 20-24 hours, washing reaction product with ethyl acetate and drying at 75-80 degrees C for 10-12 hours. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application method of the super-crosslinked polymer, which involves adding epoxy compound and the super-crosslinked polymer in ratio of 1 mmol:8-10 mg to autoclave, vacuum evacuating, passing carbon dioxide under pressure of 2.0-2.2 MPa into autoclave, reacting at 120-140 degrees C for 1-2 hours, monitoring completion of the reaction thin layer chromatography, adding 20-30 mL ethyl acetate to the autoclave to dilute it, separating, centrifuging at 3500-4000 rpm, rotary evaporating, and purifying by column chromatography to form cyclic carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of styrene oxide and deionized water is 1.0 g:15-20 mL. The ratio of styrene oxide, phenylacetylene, sodium azide and cuprous bromide is 1:1.02-1.10:1.08-1.20:100-110. Preferred Component: The epoxy compound is one of styrene oxide, 2-(phenoxymethyl)oxirane, 2-((o-tolyloxy)methyl)oxirane, 2-((allyloxy)methyl)oxirane, 2-(butoxymethyl)oxirane, 2-(((2-ethylhexyl)oxy)methyl)oxirane, 1,2-bis(ethylene oxide-2-methoxy)ethane, 2-(chloromethyl)ethylene oxide, oxirane-2-methanol and resorcinol diglycidyl ether. The cyclic carbonate is 4-phenyl-1,3-dioxolan-2-one, 4-(phenoxymethyl)-1,3-dioxolan-2-one, 4-((o-tolyloxy)methyl)-1,3-dioxolan-2-one, 4-((allyloxy)methyl)1,3-dioxolan-2-one, 4-(butoxymethyl)-1,3-dioxolan-2-one, 4-(((2-ethylhexyl)oxy)methyl)-1,3-dioxolan-2-one, 4((2-((4-oxo-1,3-dioxolane-2-yl)methoxy)ethoxy)methyl)-1,3-dioxolan-2-one, 4-(chloromethyl)-1,3-dioxolan-2-one, 4-(hydroxymethyl)-1,3-dioxolan-2-one or 4,4'-((1,3-phenylenebis(oxy))bis(methylene))bis-1,3-dioxolan-2-one.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The ratio of waste expanded polystyrene and 1,2-dichloroethane is 1.0 g:10-20 mL. The ratio of waste expanded polystyrene, anhydrous ferric trichloride, methylal and hydroxy-functionalized triazole copolymer is 1.0:4.50-5.00:2.10-2.40:2.44-2.60. The ratio of super-crosslinked polymer precursor and dimethylformamide is 1.0 g:20-30 mL. The ratio of super-crosslinked polymer precursor and n-bromobutane is 1.0 g:10-12 mL.

PT P
PN WO2020075791-A1; CN111315520-A; EP3865240-A1; JP2020516938-X; CN111315520-B; EP3865240-A4
TI Method for controlling arc welding of base material e.g. plate material, involves welding base material using gas containing carbon dioxide gas as shielding gas by generating arc between base material and welding wire.
AU TAKAHIRO N
   AKIRA N
   JUNJI F
   NOGUCHI T
   NAKAGAWA A
   FUJIWARA J
AE PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
   PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
   PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
   PANASONIC IP MANAGEMENT CO LTD (MATU-C)
GA 202030205Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for controlling arc welding of base material involves alternately repeating a short-circuit period and an arc period, and welding a base material using a gas containing carbon dioxide gas as a main component, as a shielding gas. The welding current is increased from the current value immediately before the arc re-occurrence which is lower than the current value when the arc generated as short-circuit is opened towards a current value (c1) in the arc period, during the short-circuit period. An arc is generated between the base material and a welding wire, such that the short-circuit period is switched to the arc period, when the welding current reaches the current value, when the arc is generated. The arc period has a period (a1), a period (a2), a period (a3), and a period (a4). The length of the arc becomes a length (l1) in the period (a1), when increasing the welding current to the current value (c1) with a slope (s1).
   USE - Method for controlling arc welding of base material e.g. plate material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables control of arc welding of base material with high welding quality and suppressed generation of micro short-circuit and sputter, while maintaining constant transfer period of droplets.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for controlling arc welding of base material involves alternately repeating a short-circuit period and an arc period, and welding a base material using a gas containing carbon dioxide gas as a main component, as a shielding gas. The welding current is increased from the current value immediately before the arc re-occurrence which is lower than the current value when the arc generated as short-circuit is opened towards a current value (c1) in the arc period, during the short-circuit period. An arc is generated between the base material and a welding wire, such that the short-circuit period is switched to the arc period, when the welding current reaches the current value, when the arc is generated. The arc period has a period (a1), a period (a2), a period (a3), and a period (a4). The length of the arc becomes a length (l1) in the period (a1), when increasing the welding current to the current value (c1) with a slope (s1). The welding current is reduced from the current value (c1) to a current value (c2), which is higher than the current value immediately before the arc re-occurrence and lower than the current value (c1) with a slope (s2) in the period (a2), when reducing the arc reaction force applied to the droplets formed at the tip of welding wire. The length of the arc becomes a length (l2) in the period (a3), when increasing the welding current from the current value (c2) to a current value (c3) which is lower than the current value (c1) and higher than the current value (c2) with a slope (s3).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The base material is made of mild steel, high-tensile steel, or galvanized steel. Preferred Property: The base material has a thickness of 2-5 mm.

PT P
PN CN109758988-A; CN109758988-B
TI Preparing carbon-aluminum oxide-boron carbide composite aerogel, by stirring aluminum chloride crystals, water and ethanol, adding formaldehyde, ultrasonically dispersing boron source, adding solutions and pouring sol solution into mold.
AU HUANG S
   SUO H
   LU Y
   CUI S
   SHEN X
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 201946506P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing carbon-aluminum oxide-boron carbide composite aerogel involves (i) stirring aluminum chloride crystals, water and ethanol to obtain a clarified aluminum chloride crystal hydrolysis solution, (ii) adding formaldehyde, resorcinol, propylene oxide and a reaction catalyst to the hydrolysis solution and stirring to obtain a solution (a), (iii) ultrasonically dispersing boron source, reaction catalyst, deionized water and absolute ethyl alcohol, heating and stirring to obtain a boron source hydrolysis solution, (iv) adding formaldehyde and resorcinol to the hydrolysis solution and stirring to obtain a solution (b), (v) adding the solution (b) to the solution (a) and stirring to obtain a carbon-aluminum oxide-boron carbide composite aerogel sol solution, (vi) pouring the sol solution into a mold, reacting to obtain a gel and placing the gel into a oven, (vii) adding absolute ethanol, (viii) carrying out carbon dioxide supercritical drying treatment, and (ix) heating.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon-aluminum oxide-boron carbide composite aerogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite aerogel is prepared by simple and easy method, adopts simple and easily available raw materials, has high specific area, low density, low thermal conductivity and has unlimited potential in high-temperature thermal insulation materials at more than 1200 degrees C and solves the shortcomings of low aerogel strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing carbon-aluminum oxide-boron carbide composite aerogel involves (i) uniformly stirring aluminum chloride crystals, water and ethanol to obtain a clarified aluminum chloride crystal hydrolysis solution, (ii) adding formaldehyde, resorcinol, propylene oxide and a reaction catalyst to the hydrolysis solution and uniformly stirring to obtain a solution (a), (iii) ultrasonically dispersing boron source, reaction catalyst, deionized water and absolute ethyl alcohol in a molar ratio of 1:(0.005-0.03):(20-60):(8-25), heating and uniformly stirring to obtain a boron source hydrolysis solution, (iv) adding formaldehyde, resorcinol and a network forming agent to the boron source hydrolysis solution and uniformly stirring to obtain a solution (b), (v) adding the solution (b) to the solution (a) and continuously stirring to obtain a carbon-aluminum oxide-boron carbide composite aerogel sol solution, (vi) pouring the aerogel sol solution into a mold, reacting to obtain a gel and placing the gel into a oven for aging treatment, (vii) adding absolute ethanol to the gel for solvent replacement, (viii) carrying out carbon dioxide supercritical drying treatment on the composite wet gel, where the supercritical drying method is carried out by adopting carbon dioxide gas protection, reacting at 45-50 degrees C, controlling the pressure of the high-pressure reaction kettle to 10-12 MPa and reacting for 1-2 days, and (ix) heating the treated sample at 1350-1600 degrees C for 5-10 hours at the rate of 2-4 degrees C/minute under inert atmosphere protection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred composition: The concentration of the aluminum chloride crystal hydrolysis solution is 15-20 %mass. The molar ratio of resorcinol, formaldehyde, aluminum chloride crystals, propylene oxide and reaction catalyst is 1:2:(1-2):(7-15):(0.002-0.02). The molar ratio of resorcinol, formaldehyde, boron source and network forming agent is 1:2:(0.5-2):(0.5-2). The molar ratio of the aluminum chloride of the solution (a) to the boron source of the solution (b) in step (v) is 1:(0.5-2). Preferred Components: The reaction catalysts in steps (ii) and (iii) are anhydrous sodium carbonate or sodium hydroxide. The network forming agent is anhydrous citric acid, citric acid monohydrate or sodium citrate. The inert atmosphere is helium or argon. Preferred Method: The heating and stirring in step (iii) is carried out at 40-50 degrees C. The step of aging treatment is carried out at 40-70 degrees C for 1-2 days in oven. The step (vii) involves carrying out solvent replacement for every 10-12 hours and carrying out total replacement for 3-6 times.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred composition: The molar ratio of water to ethanol in step (i) is 1:(0.2-5).

PT P
PN JP2019075253-A; JP6970885-B2
TI Manufacturing method of positive electrode active material for lithium secondary batteries, involves performing heat treatment while exhausting carbon dioxide gas generated from precursor by flow of oxidizing gas by second air supply system.
AU TOKORO H
   GUNJI A
   TAKANO S
   TAKAHASHI S
   OTANI T
AE HITACHI METALS LTD (HITK-C)
GA 2019435989
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing lithium carbonate and the compound containing metallic elements. The precursor obtained through the mixing step is fired to obtain a lithium composite compound. The heat treatment is performed while rolling the precursor in a furnace core tube of the firing furnace. The firing furnace includes a first air supply system that injects an oxidizing gas toward the inner peripheral surface side of the core tube and a second air supply system that causes the oxidizing gas to flow toward the axial direction of the core tube. The oxidizing gas is sprayed from the inner cylindrical pipe, which is the first air supply system, onto the precursor that flows down while rolling from the upstream side to the downstream side in the core tube. The heat treatment is performed while exhausting carbon dioxide gas generated from the precursor by a flow of the oxidizing gas by the second air supply system by using the specific formula.
   USE - Manufacturing method of positive electrode active material for lithium secondary batteries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the positive electrode active material for a lithium secondary battery, which efficiently carry out firing of a precursor containing nickel in a short time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The inner layer of the furnace core tube or the outer layer of the inner cylinder pipe consists of a metallic nickel material or a nickel-alloy material. The furnace core tube or the inner cylinder pipe is a double or more multilayered structure. The furnace core tube or the inner cylinder pipe has a layer which consists of iron-based hightemperature-proof heat material. The second air supply system is comprised considering the space between the internal peripheral surface of the furnace core tube, and the outer peripheral surface of the inner cylinder pipe as an air supply route. The first heat treatment process obtains first precursor by heatprocessing the mixture obtained by the mixing process over 0.5 hour or more and 5 hours or less by heat processing temperature. The second heat treatment process obtains a second precursor by heatprocessing the first precursor over 0.1 hour or more and 50 hours or less by heat processing temperature. The third heat treatment process obtains a lithium complex compound by heat-processing a second precursor over 0.1 hour or more and 50 hours or less in heat processing temperature. The carbon dioxide gas emitted from the first precursor or second 2nd precursor is heat processed by the airflow of an oxidizing gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structure of rotary kiln used for manufacture of the positive electrode active material for lithium secondary batteries. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Rotary kiln (1A)
   Furnace core tube (10A)
   Heater (20A)
   Inner cylinder pipe (30A)
   Air supply rout (40A)
DC E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PT P
PN CN108554439-A; CN108554439-B
TI Preparation of ultra-thin titanium-based composite            photocatalyst used in reduction of carbon dioxide,            involves adding carrier slurry containing anionic            surfactant to colloidal solution containing titanium            tetrachloride, aging, and drying.
AU FENG J
   WANG K
   LI D
   HE Y
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 201878064B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ultra-thin titanium-based                composite photocatalyst involves dissolving                titanium tetrachloride, soluble divalent metal ion                and trivalent metal nitrate in deionized water to                prepare mixed salt solution, adding the mixed                solution and alkali solution including sodium                hydroxide to a reactor, reacting in inert                atmosphere at 40-180 degrees C for 4-24 hours,                washing with deionized water, filtering, drying at                40-60 degrees C for 6-24 hours, obtaining a nitrate                intercalated titanium-based hydrotalcite, adding                resultant product to a solvent, stirring for 1-48                hours, obtaining colloidal solution with                concentration of 0.2-2 g/L, dispersing carrier                having visible light response in anionic surfactant                solution for 20-60 minutes, obtaining carrier                slurry with concentration of 0.1-1 g/L, adding                carrier slurry to the colloidal solution in a mass                ratio of 10-1, aging at room temperature for 0.5-2                hours, filtering, washing, and drying at 40-60                degrees C for 12 hours.
   USE - Preparation of ultra-thin titanium-based                composite photocatalyst used in reduction of carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and efficient                preparation of ultra-thin titanium-based composite                photocatalyst having excellent visible light                responsiveness, activity and stability in                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of ultra-thin titanium-based                composite photocatalyst involves dissolving                titanium tetrachloride, soluble divalent metal ion                and trivalent metal nitrate in deionized water to                prepare mixed salt solution, adding the mixed                solution and alkali solution including sodium                hydroxide to a reactor, reacting in inert                atmosphere at 40-180 degrees C for 4-24 hours,                washing with deionized water, filtering, drying at                40-60 degrees C for 6-24 hours, obtaining a nitrate                intercalated titanium-based hydrotalcite, adding                resultant product to a solvent, stirring for 1-48                hours, obtaining colloidal solution with                concentration of 0.2-2 g/L, dispersing carrier                having visible light response in anionic surfactant                solution for 20-60 minutes, obtaining carrier                slurry with concentration of 0.1-1 g/L, adding                carrier slurry to the colloidal solution in a mass                ratio of 10-1, aging at room temperature for 0.5-2                hours, filtering, washing, and drying at 40-60                degrees C for 12 hours. The total concentration of                tetravalent titanium ion, divalent metal ion and                trivalent metal ion is 0.1-1 mol/L. The molar ratio                of total metal ions with respect to tetravalent                titanium ion is 1-10. The divalent metal ion is                divalent magnesium ion, divalent zinc ion, divalent                nickel ion, divalent copper ion or divalent                manganese ion, preferably divalent magnesium ion or                divalent copper ion. The trivalent metal ion is                trivalent aluminum ion, trivalent indium ion,                trivalent manganese ion or trivalent chromium ion.                The solvent is dimethylformamide,                N,N-dimethylformamide or n-butanol. The carrier is                oxidized graphite or graphitic carbon nitride. The                anionic surfactant is sodium dodecyl sulfate or                dodecylbenzenesulfonic acid with concentration of                0.1-0.5 g/L.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The composite                  photocatalyst has an average thickness of 1-2                  nm.

PN CN108056099-A
TI Effervescent tablet useful in e.g. health field and medium biological pests e.g. mosquito, bedbug, millipede, centipede and white ants, comprises medicine component (chlorine fluorine ethofenprox and propoxur) and auxiliary component.
AU ZHOU Q
   QIN D
   LIU W
   ZHANG Y
   PAN X
   SU E
AE JIANGSU YANGNONG CHEM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201843856C
AB    NOVELTY - Effervescent tablet comprises 10-20 wt.% medicine component and auxiliary component (balance amount), where the medicine component composed of component A and component B in a weight ratio of 1:5-50, the component A is chlorine fluorine ethofenprox (I), the component B is propoxur compound (II).
   USE - The effervescent tablet is useful in health field and medium biological pests e.g. mosquito, fly, red imported fire ant, bedbug, millipede, centipede, Scutigera coleoptrata, tick, cockroach and white ants (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The effervescent tablet: has expanding insecticidal spectrum and increased efficiency; and easy to storage and use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Effervescent tablet comprises 10-20 wt.% medicine component and auxiliary component (balance amount), where the medicine component composed of component A and component B in a weight ratio of 1:5-50, the component A is chlorine fluorine ethofenprox of formula (I), the component B is propoxur compound of formula (II). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing effervescent tablet, comprising (i) adding nano-inclusion carrier into 10-30 wt.% water, and heating to 30-100 degrees C to obtain saturated aqueous solution, then adding slowly component A and component B into anhydrous ethanol under magnetic stirring condition, and dissolving, where the mass ratio between sum of component A and component B with anhydrous ethanol is 1:5-20, the mass ratio between sum of component A and component B with nano inclusion carrier is 1:1-20, then stirring at 20-80 degrees C for 1-5 hours, freezing and separating out white precipitate, then filtering, and blast drying at 50-80 degrees C to obtain nano-inclusion component A and component B, (ii) mixing uniformly the nano-inclusion component A and component B with dispersant, wetting agent, disintegrant, lubricant and filler, then pre-crushing to 200 mesh, and crushing to 500-800 meshes using jet mill to obtain a mixture, (iii) crushing acid source and carbon dioxide source respectively to 300-500 meshes, and packaging independently, (iv) uniformly dividing the mixture obtained in step (i) into two parts, adding one part into solid acid powder obtained in step (iii), adding other part into carbon dioxide source powder obtained in step (iii), mixing respectively, uniformly dividing the adhesive into two parts and adding respectively into solid acid powder mixture and carbon dioxide source powder mixture, mixing uniformly and placing separately into a granulator and granulating, drying at 40-50 degrees C to a moisture content of less than 2%, then filtering through 20 mesh screen to obtain acid soft material particles and alkali washed granular soft material separately, and (v) adding the acid soft material particles and alkali washed granular soft material obtained in the step (iv) into mixer and stirring for 20 minutes, pressing into tablet specification, drying at below 120 degrees C, detecting, then sealing and packaging to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The component A is a mixture of stereoisomers of 2,3,5,6-tetrafluoro-4-methoxymethylbenzyl-3-(2,2-dichlorovinyl)-2,2-dimethylcyclopropanecarboxylate. The stereoisomers of component A are: cyclopropane 1 position in R configuration is greater than or equal to 55% enriched dextrorotatory 2,3,5,6-tetrafluoro-4-methoxymethylbenzyl-3-(2,2-dichlorovinyl)-2,2-dimethylcyclopropanecarboxylate, absolute configuration of cyclopropane 1 position in R configuration, 1, 3 positions without distinguishing cis-trans isomers is dextrorotatory 2,3,5,6-tetrafluoro-4-methoxymethylbenzyl-3-(2,2-dichlorovinyl)-2,2-dimethylcyclopropanecarboxylate, absolute configuration of cyclopropane 1 position in the R configuration, and 1,3 positions is trans isomerized 2,3,5,6-tetrafluoro-4-methoxymethylbenzyl-3-(2,2-dichlorovinyl)-2,2-dimethylcyclopropanecarboxylate (ISO name is chlorine fluorine ethofenprox, meperfluthrin) or the absolute configuration of cyclopropane 1st position in R configuration, and 1,3 positions is 2,3,5,6-tetrafluoro-4-methoxymethylbenzyl-3-(2,2-dichlorovinyl)-2,2-dimethylcyclopropanecarboxylate. The weight ratio between component A and component B is 1:8-16. The auxiliary component comprises 6 wt.% dispersant, 4 wt.% wetting agent, 5 wt.% disintegrant, 5 wt.% binder, 1 wt.% lubricant, 15 wt.% carbon dioxide source, 12 wt.% acid source, 25 wt.% nano inclusion complex carrier and filler (balance amount). The dispersant is alkyl naphthalene sulfonate, bis(alkyl)naphthalenesulfonate formaldehyde condensate, sodium methyl sulfonate formaldehyde condensate, naphthalene sulfonic acid formaldehyde condensate, arylphenol polyoxyethylene succinate sulfonate, octylphenol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate, polycarboxylate, lignosulfonate, alkyl aryl polyoxyethylene ether, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, alkylphenol polyoxyethylene ether formaldehyde condensate sulfate, calcium alkylbenzene sulfonate, sodium methylene bis naphthalene sulfonate, fatty amine polyethenoxy ether and/or fatty acid polyethenoxy ether; the wetting agent is sodium dodecyl sulfate, sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, alkyl sulfate, nekal BX (sodium butylnaphthalenesulfonate), saponin powder, tea seed oil cake and/or silkworm dropping; the disintegrant is sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, ammonium sulfate, carbamide, succinic acid, glucose and/or aluminum chloride; the binder is methyl cellulose, polyvinyl alcohol, vinyl pyrrolidone, sucrose, gum arabic, starch and/or soluble starch; the lubricant is magnesium stearate, talc powder, sodium lauryl sulfate and/or wheat starch; the carbon dioxide source is carbonate, basic carbonate and/or bicarbonate; the acid source is tartaric acid, citric acid, maleic acid, succinic acid, oxalic acid, malonic acid, lactic acid and/or fumaric acid; the nano inclusion complex carrier is beta -cyclodextrin, hydroxypropyl- beta -cyclodextrin, dimethyl- beta -cyclodextrin and/or nano fiber graphene oxide; and the filler is white carbon black, superfine silica gel, diatomite, kaolin and/or corn starch.
   ACTIVITY - Insecticide.
