   LI W
   LI C
   ZHENG Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC EXPLORATION & PRODN RES INST (SNPC-C)
GA 202124380N
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a natural gas liquefying system provided with a liquefied natural gas storage tank for storing liquefied natural gas. A carbon dioxide turbine generating system is provided with a burner and a turbine generator. The burner receives flash steam of the liquefied natural gas storage tank and burns to heat carbon dioxide to form a high temperature mixed medium to generate electricity by the turbine generator. The turbine generator discharges carbon dioxide energy to supply the burner. The carbon dioxide turbine generating system is provided with a carbon dioxide turbine generator.
   USE - Natural gas liquefying device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device uses flash steam of liquefied natural gas as fuel and generated high temperature gas as heat source of an ultra-critical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle power generation system so as to reduce generated power supplies energy consumption and avoid need to construct a natural gas pipe network and a power grid. The device is inexpensive and simple in structure.

   YU Z
   DONG B
   YU X
   ZHANG X
   YANG J
   CAI L
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202045226F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining parameter                range of each influencing factor according to a                carbon dioxide corrosion experimental working                condition. Corrosion weightlessness test is                performed on material within an identified                parameter. Function relationship diagram and a                relationship curve diagram of a corrosion rate and                corrosion influencing factors are established.                Weight loss corrosion test is performed on the                material at a carbon dioxide partial pressure                chloride ion concentration and different                temperatures to obtain measured corrosion rate                values. Non-linear fitting process is performed to                draw a carbon dioxide corrosion prediction plate                for determining carbon dioxide partial pressure and                chloride ion concentration.
   USE - Method for determining carbon dioxide                corrosion prediction plate considering                factors.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing reference basis                for preventing corrosion failure of oilfield metal                facilities and realizing guarantee for safe                utilization of materials with high prediction                accuracy and practicality.

TI Preparing two-dimensional polymer based porous carbon material, comprises mixing two-dimensional aromatic polymer and pore forming agent and then carbonizing in an inert atmosphere.
AU WU D
   XU D
   QI H
   YAN F
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 201873511W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing two-dimensional polymer based porous carbon material, comprises mixing two-dimensional aromatic polymer and pore forming agent in a mass ratio of 1:3-6, and then carbonizing at 400-1000 degrees C in an inert atmosphere to obtain final product.
   USE - The two-dimensional polymer based porous carbon material is useful in carbon dioxide adsorbent for adsorbing carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The two-dimensional polymer based porous carbon material has hydrophobicity and simple recovery operation; physically adsorbs carbon dioxide at high temperature (60-90 degrees C), and recycles at room temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) two-dimensional polymer based porous carbon material, is obtained by above method;
   (2) use of two-dimensional polymer-based porous carbon material for adsorbing carbon dioxide, where the adsorption temperature is 0-90 degrees C and the pressure of the carbon dioxide is 0.015-0.1 MPa; and
   (3) carbon dioxide adsorbent comprising two-dimensional polymer-based porous carbon material, where the carbon dioxide adsorbent adsorbs carbon dioxide at 0-90?C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The two-dimensional aromatic polymer has a specific surface area of 400-4000 m2/g, and the two-dimensional aromatic polymer phenyl two-dimensional polymer, naphthyl two-dimensional polymer, acenaphthenyl two-dimensional polymer or pyrenyl two-dimensional polymer. The structural formula of the phenyl two-dimensional polymer is bis(2-benzylphenyl)methane compound of formula (I), or (1,1';2',3'';1'',1''')quaterphenyl compound of formula (II), the naphthyl two-dimensional polymer is di1,1'-binaphthyl-7-ylmethane compound of formula (III) or (2,2';4',1'';7'',2''')quaternaphthalene compound of formula (IV), the acenaphthenyl two-dimensional polymer is 6-((1,2-dihydroacenaphthylen-3-yl)methyl)-3-methyl-4-((8-methyl-6-((3-methyl-1,2-dihydroacenaphthylen-4-yl)methyl)-1,2-dihydroacenaphthylen-4-yl)methyl)-1,2-dihydroacenaphthylene compound of formula (V), or 5-(7-(1,2-dihydroacenaphthylen-3-yl)-1,2-dihydroacenaphthylen-5-yl)-3-(1,2-dihydroacenaphthylen-4-yl)-1,2-dihydroacenaphthylene compound of formula (VI), and the pyrenyl two-dimensional polymer is 7-methyl-1-((4-((2-(pyren-1-ylmethyl)pyren-4-yl)methyl)pyren-2-yl)methyl)pyrene compound of formula (VII) or 5-(pyren-1-yl)-2-(4-(pyren-1-yl)pyren-2-yl)pyrene compound of formula (VIII). The pore forming agent is potassium hydroxide, potassium chloride, sodium chloride, ammonium chloride and/or sodium hydroxide. Preferred Method: The carbonization time is 0.5-2 hours. The adsorption temperature is 60-90 degrees C.m and n = number of repeating units.

PN WO2020053793-A2; WO2020053793-A3; US2021202968-A1
TI Fuel cell system for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas, has controller that reduces carbon dioxide utilization of fuel cell assembly when determined estimated utilization is above predetermined threshold utilization.
AU DALY J M
   JOLLY S
   VENKATARAMAN R
   NUHFER E
AE FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
GA 202022289C
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a fuel cell assembly (50) comprising fuel cell. The fuel cell comprises a cathode portion that is configured to receive the flue gas generated by a flue gas generating device or a derivative, as cathode inlet gas (53), and to output cathode exhaust gas (54). An anode portion is configured to receive an anode inlet gas (35) and to output anode exhaust gas (55). A fuel cell assembly voltage monitor (90) is configured to measure a voltage across the fuel cell assembly. A controller is configured to receive the measured voltage across the fuel cell assembly from the fuel cell assembly voltage monitor. The controller determines an estimated carbon dioxide utilization of the fuel cell assembly based on the measured voltage across the fuel cell assembly. The controller reduces a carbon dioxide utilization of the fuel cell assembly when the determined estimated carbon dioxide utilization is above a predetermined threshold utilization.
   USE - Fuel cell system for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas generated by flue gas generating device.
   ADVANTAGE - The cathode flow rate and/or fuel cell current is modulated to yield a desired product mix of carbon sequestration, power output, and hydrogen export, by determining the carbon content of a flue gas provided to the fuel cell system. The carbon dioxide moves from an environment in which the concentration of carbon dioxide is low and diluted with species of oxygen and nitrogen from which the carbon dioxide is difficult to remove to an environment in which the concentration of carbon dioxide is relatively high and in the presence of species of hydrogen and water from which the carbon dioxide is more easily removed. When the determined estimated carbon dioxide utilization is above predetermined high threshold utilization, the controller is configured to reduce the carbon dioxide utilization of the fuel cell assembly by reducing the current across the fuel cell assembly. Using the hydrogen gas analyzer allows for more optimal operation of the fuel cell assembly by precision determination of fuel utilization allowing for fine adjustment of the fuel gas flow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for controlling carbon dioxide utilization in fuel cell assembly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a fuel cell system adapted for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas.
   Fuel cell system (1)
   Anode inlet gas (35)
   Fuel cell assembly (50)
   Cathode inlet gas (53)
   Output cathode exhaust gas (54)
   Output anode exhaust gas (55)
   Fuel cell assembly voltage monitor (90)

PN KR2020007724-A; KR2389113-B1
TI Producing carbon cathode from carbon dioxide            useful for lithium ion battery, comprises preparing            boron-doped carbon material by heating gas containing            carbon dioxide and boron precursor under inert gas            atmosphere.
AU LEE J
   CHOI W Y
   LEE H
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202009241V
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbon cathode from carbon dioxide,                comprises either: preparing boron-doped carbon                material by heating gas containing carbon dioxide                and boron precursor under inert gas atmosphere;                preparing carbon material doped with boron and                nitrogen by heating gas containing carbon dioxide,                boron precursor and nitrogen precursor under inert                gas atmosphere; preparing silicon-carbon composite                by heating gas containing carbon dioxide and carbon                precursor, boron precursor or boron precursor and                nitrogen precursor and silicon-containing material                under inert gas atmosphere; or performing                recrystallization or electrospinning of a mixture                containing carbon precursor, nitrogen precursor or                boron precursor to form carbon nanofiber precursor,                and preparing carbon nanofibers by oxidizing and                carbonizing the carbon nanofiber precursors while                supplying a gas containing carbon dioxide.
   USE - The carbon cathode is useful for lithium ion                battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon cathode is economical and                environmentally friendly, and improves performance                and versatility of battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing carbon cathode from carbon dioxide,                comprises either: preparing boron-doped carbon                material by heating gas containing carbon dioxide                and boron precursor under inert gas atmosphere;                preparing carbon material doped with boron and                nitrogen by heating gas containing carbon dioxide,                boron precursor and nitrogen precursor under inert                gas atmosphere; preparing silicon-carbon composite                by heating gas containing carbon dioxide and carbon                precursor, boron precursor or boron precursor and                nitrogen precursor and silicon-containing material                under inert gas atmosphere; performing                recrystallization or electrospinning of a mixture                containing carbon precursor, nitrogen precursor or                boron precursor to form carbon nanofiber precursor,                and preparing carbon nanofibers by oxidizing and                carbonizing the carbon nanofiber precursors while                supplying a gas containing carbon dioxide;                performing recrystallization or electrospinning of                carbon precursor, nitrogen precursor or mixture of                boron precursor and silicon-containing material to                form silicon-carbon nanofiber precursor, and                preparing a silicon-carbon nanofiber composite by                oxidizing and carbonizing the silicon-carbon                nanofiber precursor while supplying a gas                containing carbon dioxide; heating a carbon                precursor, a nitrogen precursor or a mixture of a                boron precursor and a metal oxide precursor while                supplying a gas containing carbon dioxide to form a                metal oxide-porous carbon composite; using                composite of carbon with at least one of boron,                nitrogen, silicon and metal oxides; performing                recrystallizing or electrospinning of mixture                containing carbon precursor, nitrogen precursor or                boron precursor to form a carbon nanofiber                precursor, and preparing carbon nanofibers by                oxidizing and carbonizing the carbon nanofiber                precursors while supplying a gas containing carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises:                  mixing a conductive material and a binder                  solution in the boron-doped carbon material,                  boron and nitrogen-doped carbon material,                  silicon-carbon composite or metal oxide-carbon                  composite, and evaporating the solvent in the                  obtained slurry; mixing conductive material and                  binder solution with the carbon nanofibers or the                  silicon-carbon nanofiber composites, and                  evaporating the solvent in the obtained slurry.                  The method further comprises evaporating the                  solvent in the carbon nanofibers or the                  silicon-carbon nanofiber composites. The method                  further comprises washing the obtained product                  with acid, water or alcohol. The step (a) is                  carried out by increasing the temperature to                  400-700 degrees C at rate of 1-10 degrees                  C/minute. The supply flow rate of the carbon                  dioxide is less than or equal to 200 ml/minute.                  The distance between the syringe needle and the                  collector is 10-15 cm. the Radiation angle to the                  collector is 30-50 degrees in the horizontal                  direction. The operating voltage is 15-20 kV. The                  spinning flow rate is 0.5-2 ml/hour. The inflow                  of inert gas at flow rate of 20-200 ml/minute.                  The temperature is raised at rate of 1-10 and                  maintained at 200-1200 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours                  by flowing at 300-600 degrees C and flow rate of                  50-200 ml/minute to carbonize or oxidize the                  carbon precursor. The method comprises increasing                  temperature of the inert gas at rate of 1-10 by                  flowing at flow rate of 20-200 ml/minute,                  maintaining at 200-1200 degrees C for 0.5-5 days,                  introducing carbon dioxide at 300-600 degrees C                  and flow rate of 50-200 ml/minute to carbonize                  the carbon precursor and oxidize the metal oxide                  precursor for synthesizing metal oxide-porous                  carbon composite. Preferred Components: The boron                  precursor includes boron oxide, boric acid,                  lithium borohydride, sodium borohydride,                  potassium borohydride, magnesium borohydride,                  calcium borohydride, strontium borohydride and/or                  ammonia borane. The nitrogen precursor includes                  polypyrrole, polyaniline, melamine,                  N,N'-bis(2,6-diisopropyphenyl)-3,4,9,10-perylenetetracarboxylicdiimide,                  polyacrylonitrile, urea, ammonia, ammonia                  hydroxide, hydrazine, ammonia borane and/or                  nitrogen gas. The silicon-containing material                  includes silicon nanoparticles, silicon dioxide,                  silicon carbide, silicon tetrachloride, silicon                  nitride, silicon acetate, silicon tetrafluoride,                  silicon tetrabromide, silicon monoxide and/or                  tetraethyl orthosilicate. The metal oxide                  precursor includes cobalt oxide precursor,                  manganese oxide precursor and/or iron oxide                  precursor. The cobalt oxide precursor includes                  cobalt chloride, cobalt bromide, cobalt iodide,                  carbonylcobalt, cobalt(II) acetate, cobalt                  naphthenate, cobalt nitrate, cobalt sulfate                  and/or cobalt acetylacetonate. The manganese                  oxide precursor includes potassium permanganate,                  Potassium manganate, barium manganate, manganese                  chloride, Manganese sulfate, manganese sulfide,                  manganese(II) acetate, manganese(II)                  acetylacetonate, manganese(II) bromide and/or                  manganese(II) nitrate. The iron oxide precursor                  includes iron(II) chloride, iron(III) chloride,                  iron(II) iodide, Iron sulfide, iron disulfide                  and/or iron(III) acetylacetonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon precursor                  includes gas, amorphous carbon material and                  crystalline carbon material and polymer material,                  preferably carbon dioxide, activated carbon,                  carbon black, charcoal, graphite, graphene,                  polyacrylonitrile and/or polyaniline.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon nanofibers                  are used in the form of a free-standing                  interlayer in an independent form, a                  separator-attached interlayer attached to a                  separator, or an anode-attached interlayer                  attached to a cathode.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparing method of nano high zeolite socony            mobil-5 (HZSM-5) molecular sieve, involves drying and            roasting reaction product to obtain nano HZSM-5            molecular sieve and using silicon source and aluminum            source respectively in silicon dioxide and aluminum            oxide meter.

   DING J
   ZHANG Y
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2022B0480A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves grinding and mixing a                silicon source, an aluminum source and a template                agent for 15-30min to obtain a solid-phase reaction                mixture. The solid-phase reaction mixture is placed                in a supercritical crystallization reaction kettle.                The carbon dioxide gas is introduced and heated for                crystallization reaction to obtain a reaction                product. The reaction product is cooled to room                temperature and washed with deionized water until a                pH value of a filtrate is 7.0. The reaction product                is dried and roasted to obtain a nano HZSM-5                molecular sieve. The silicon source and the                aluminum source are respectively used in silicon                dioxide and aluminum oxide meter. A molar ratio of                the template agents is 1 (0.004-0.05) to 0.05-0.15.                The template agent is ammonium octenyl succinate                gluconate. The silicon source is solid silica gel                or white carbon black. The aluminum source is                aluminum isopropoxide or sodium                metaaluminate.
   USE - Preparation method of nano-HZSM-5 molecular                sieve.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses octenyl succinic acid glucose                ester ammonium as template agent, and avoids the                adding of inorganic alkali. The method directly                obtains HZSM-5 molecular sieve, does not need to                perform ion exchange, simplifies the process and                uses supercritical carbon dioxide high solubility                and high diffusion performance. The solid phase                reaction mixed material in the supercritical                crystallization kettle fully contacts fast                dissolving, reaction and short reaction time. The                obtained molecular sieves crystal grain size is                small, achieves uniform distribution, rich channel,                large specific surface area, the total acid amount                is large and the yield is high.

   FU H

   LIAN H

GA 2022A7923A
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of sheet layered copper-based                nitrogen-doped graphene catalyst involves (i)                taking graphene oxide slurry as a raw material and                urea as a nitrogen source, adding graphene oxide                slurry into a high-pressure reaction kettle, adding                deionized water and urea, mixing, and                ultrasonically dispersing, transferring the                high-pressure reaction kettle into an oven for                heating at 120-180&#8451; for 6-12 hours,                naturally cooling, washing the obtained product to                be neutral using deionized water, filtering,                drying, and grinding to obtain sheet shaped                nitrogen-doped graphene carrier powder, and (ii)                dispersing the obtained powder into deionized water                to form a suspension, ultrasonically dispersing,                adding copper nitrate and sodium carbonate aqueous                solution, stirring and reacting for 3-6 hours at                room temperature, filtering, washing and drying the                obtained product, roasting in an argon atmosphere                at 180-240&#8451; for 2-4 hours, and cooling to                room temperature.
   USE - Manufacture of sheet layered copper-based                nitrogen-doped graphene catalyst used in                electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to                prepare ethanol (all claimed) used as energy source                material for medical treatment and chemical                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides sheet-layered copper-based                nitrogen-doped graphene catalyst having excellent                stability, activity, and applicability. The                catalyst in the electro-catalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide provides ethanol, at -0.8V                electrolytic voltage, liquid product total Faraday                efficiency of 59.1%, the ethanol Faraday efficiency                reaches 34.1%, and ethanol selectivity is more than                55%, after 24 hours of electrolysis. The total                Faraday efficiency of the liquid product reaches                more than 50%, and the current density is                constant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                application of lamellar copper-based nitrogen-doped                graphene catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves                  placing the prepared lamellar copper-based                  nitrogen-doped graphene catalyst into a conical                  centrifuge tube, adding deionized water, ethanol                  and 5 wt.% nafion solution for ultrasonically                  dispersing to form dispersed solution, and                  coating carbon paper as working electrode with                  0.5-2 mg/cm2 uniformly dispersed solution, drying                  at normal temperature for electrocatalytic                  reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare                  ethanol.Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  graphene oxide slurry in step (i) is 2-6 mg/mL.                  The mass ratio of graphene oxide slurry to urea                  in step (ii) is 1:100-300. The volume ratio of                  graphene oxide slurry to the deionized water is                  1:1-3. The adding amount ratio of lamellar                  nitrogen-doped graphene carrier powder to the                  deionized water is 1 mg:4-10 mL. The copper oxide                  is used in an amount of 5-35 wt.% and nitrogen is                  used in an amount of 5-8 atomic%. The addition                  amount ratio of catalyst powder to ethanol,                  deionized water and 5 wt.% nafion solution is 1-3                  mg:100-300 &#956;L:100-300 &#956;L:20-50                  &#956;L. The sodium carbonate is used in an                  amount of 1.32&#215;10-3to                  1.92&#215;10-3mol/L. The ratio of addition                  amount of laminated nitrogen-doped graphene                  carrier powder and sodium carbonate aqueous                  solution is 1 mg: 1-7 mL.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (T) comprises (1) adding ketone monomer I, ketone                  monomer II and aryl monomer and methylene                  dichloride into the reactor, adding acid                  catalyst, reacting to obtain viscous solution;                  pouring the solution into the organic solvent                  polymer to separate out, filtering and dissolving                  by tetrahydrofuran, then separating organic                  solvent, washing polymer with water or alkali                  solution, filtering, drying to obtain piperidine                  group-containing main chain polymer, where the                  ketone monomer I is N-methyl-4-piperidone, ketone                  monomer II is trifluoroacetophenone,                  trifluoroacetone, perfluoroacetophenone,                  perfluoroacetone and/or pentafluorobenzaldehyde;                  the aryl monomer is biphenyl, terphenyl,                  fluorene, phenyl ether and/or                  diphenoxybenzophenone; the material ratio of the                  ketone monomer I to the ketone monomer II is                  9:1-1:4, preferably 4:1-3:7; the ratio of the                  total amount of the ketone monomer I and the                  ketone monomer II to the amount of the aryl                  monomer is 0.8:1-2.0:1; the addition amount of                  ketone monomer II is 20-40 wt.% of the total                  amount of the required ketone monomer II for the                  first time, then adding 40-50 wt.% of the amount                  of the total substance 20-40 minutes after adding                  the acid catalyst, then continuously reacting for                  1-3 hours, adding the remaining ketone monomer                  II; the acid catalyst includes                  trifluoromethanesulfonic acid, trifluoroacetic                  acid, aluminum trichloride, ferric chloride, tin                  tetrachloride, zinc chloride and/or                  1,4-dichloromethoxybutane; the acid catalysts are                  added at a temperature below 0&#176;C to                  above the freezing point of the solvent; the acid                  addition rate is controlled at 0.1-5 ml/minute                  (preferably 0.1-1 ml/minute); the acid catalyst                  substance amount is &#8805; total amount of                  ketone monomer I and ketone monomer II (the ratio                  of the amount of acid catalyzed material to the                  total amount of ketone monomer I and ketone                  monomer II is 1:1-9:1); the reaction is carried                  out at 0-100&#8451;, preferably                  0-60&#8451; for 4-96 hours, preferably 6-72                  hours; (2) adding solvent and piperidine                  group-containing main chain polymer obtained in                  step (1) to obtain suspension, adding                  functionalized monomer, reacting to obtain                  reaction solution; (A) taking the reaction                  solution as casting solution, filtering and                  uniformly coating on the substrate material,                  carrying out solvent evaporation and dry until                  the solvent in the reaction liquid is completely                  evaporated to obtain the film; or (B) adding                  piperidine group-containing main chain polymer                  obtained in step (1) into solvent in the                  container, stirring to obtain suspension, adding                  functionalized monomer, reacting to obtain                  reaction solution; then precipitating in the                  organic solvent, filtering, washing the                  precipitate for 3-5 times by deionized water,                  drying, hot pressing to obtain target polymer                  film, where the hot pressing comprises placing                  the dried target polymer prepared above between                  two layers of base materials and hot pressing; or                  (C) placing the piperidine group-containing main                  chain polymer prepared in step (1) between the                  two-layer base materials, hot-pressing, placing                  the hot-pressed film in the functionalized                  monomer solution, then reacting, fully washing                  with deionized water; (3) placing the film                  obtained in the step (2) in the salt solution for                  processing, then placing the film in the alkali                  solution and soaking for 12-96 hours to obtain                  final product.Preferred Method: In step (1), the volume of                  methylene dichloride is to make the aryl monomer                  concentration be any value of 1-2 mol/l; the                  precipitating polymer solvent is ethanol, ethyl                  acetate, acetone, methanol, water, concentration                  is the aqueous solution of 0.1-1 g/ml sodium                  hydroxide, concentration is the aqueous solution                  of 0.05-1 g/ml potassium hydroxide, concentration                  is the ethanolic solution of 0.01-0.5 g/ml                  potassium hydroxide, the concentration is the                  ethanol solution of 0.01-0.5 g/ml sodium                  hydroxide; the alkali solution used for washing                  the polymer is potassium carbonate solution,                  potassium bicarbonate solution, sodium carbonate                  solution, sodium bicarbonate solution, potassium                  hydroxide solution and sodium hydroxide solution,                  and the concentration is 0.01-5 M; the washing is                  carried out at 30-100&#8451; for 2-48 hours,                  and the washing is repeated more than once; the                  drying of the polymer is carried out at normal                  pressure-vacuum, room temperature-120&#8451;                  for 4-48 hours.    ADVANTAGE - The method for preparing triethylamine salt as                auxiliary materials has less impurity of                triethylamine salt, less cation residue, avoids                need to pass through acidic resin column, adjusts                proper proportion, reduces complexity of operation,                greatly reduces production cost of enterprise, and                reduces discharge of chemical waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing triethylamine salt as auxiliary                materials involves adding calcium salt in solution                of auxiliary materials of formula (I), controlling                reaction temperature, stirring for reaction,                reacting calcium salt with solution to generate                calcium sulfate slightly soluble in water, and                filtering; adding filtrate in hydrophilic organic                solvent, controlling temperature, stirring for                crystallization, filtering, and drying to obtain                calcium salt of auxiliary materials; dissolving                calcium salt of auxiliary materials by using water,                adding triethylamine, controlling reaction                temperature, introducing carbon dioxide gas and                stirring to adjust pH value to less than or equal                to 7, stirring for reaction, and filtering; adding                obtained filtrate in hydrophilic organic solvent,                controlling temperature, stirring for                crystallization, filtering, and drying to obtain                triethylamine salt of auxiliary materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for preparing                  triethylamine salt as auxiliary materials                  involves adding sucrose, sulfur trioxide-pyridine                  compound, 2-methylpyridine and dimethylformamide                  into reaction vessel, stirring and mixing                  uniformly, heating to 60-65degrees Celsius,                  stirring and reacting, cooling system solution                  after reaction is finished, and adding purified                  water to quench and react to obtain solution of                  auxiliary materials.Preferred Components: The calcium salt is at                  least one of calcium hydroxide, calcium oxide and                  calcium carbonate.Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of                  calcium salt to auxiliary materials is 4 to 16.                  The reaction temperature is controlled in first                  step to 0-60degrees Celsius. The stirring                  reaction time is 0.5-12 hours. The reaction                  temperature is controlled in second step to -10                  to 50degrees Celsius and time for stirred                  crystallization is 0 to 24 hours. The amount of                  triethylamine is 0.5-1 times weight of calcium                  salt as auxiliary materials. The reaction                  temperature is controlled in third step to                  -10-30degrees Celsius. The pH is adjusted in                  range of 5 to 6. The stirring reaction time is 0                  to 24 hours. The reaction temperature in fourth                  step is controlled to -10-30degrees Celsius; time                  for stirring and crystallizing is 0-24                  hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrophilic                  organic solvent comprises lower alcohols,                  acetone, tetrahydrofuran, N-Dimethylformamide                  (DMF), dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO),                  Dimethylacetamide (DMA), dioxane.Preferred Conditions: The theoretical weight                  ratio of hydrophilic organic solvent to auxiliary                  materials is 5 to 30 times.

PN WO2022053537-A1; LU102057-B1; TW202225416-A
TI Operating blast furnace for producing pig iron by            heating first stream of steam before or after mixing            with oxygen source, heating second stream of blast            furnace gas from blast furnace and natural gas, and            feeding in oxidation reactor.
AU CASTAGNOLA C
   MICHELETTI L
   KINZEL K P
   VALERIUS M
AE WURTH SA PAUL (WURP-C)
GA 2022383354
AB    NOVELTY - A blast furnace for producing pig iron is                operated by heating first stream of steam in first                heater, before or after mixing with oxygen source                selected from oxygen and oxygen-enriched air, to                provide first heated stream of oxygen-enriched                steam. A first stream of blast furnace gas is                heated from a blast furnace, and a second stream of                natural gas is heated in a second heater before or                after being mixed together to provide heated carbon                feed stream. The first heated stream and the heated                carbon feed stream are fed either as a combined                stream or separately to a catalytic partial                oxidation reactor i.e. Short Contact Time Catalytic                Partial Oxidation reactor, to produce a stream of                syngas. The stream of gas is fed to a shaft of the                blast furnace.
   USE - The method is useful for operating a blast                furnace for producing pig iron in a power plant and                a steel production plant, and for producing a                synthesis gas that is utilized for industrial                purpose.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of operating the blast                furnace installation without requiring expensive                and technically complex equipment. The method                reduces the amount of coke or other carbon sources                used, and improves the reduction potential and                injects it back into the furnace to aid the                reduction process, thus reducing the carbon dioxide                content in the furnace gas by pressure swing                adsorption (PSA) or vacuum pressure swing adaption                (VPSA). The method allows considerable reduction of                carbon dioxide content in blast furnace gas from                about 35% to about 5% and is very inexpensive to                acquire, to maintain and to operate and does not                need a lot of space.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for blast                furnace installation for producing pig iron.

PN WO2022033715-A1; GB2597953-A
TI Method for localizing leakage of gaseous medium at internal combustion engine, involves emitting electromagnetic radiation beam through analysis section, and measuring radiation characteristic of radiation beam after passing through section.
AU LENHARDT H
AE CATERPILLAR ENERGY SOLUTIONS GMBH (CATE-C)
GA 202226601D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves emitting an electromagnetic radiation beam (S2) through an analysis section disposed at or in the vicinity of an outer surface of an internal combustion engine. A radiation characteristic of the radiation beam is measured (S3) after passing through the analysis section. Leakage of a gaseous medium e.g. carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, into the section is determined (S4) in dependence on the measured radiation characteristic. The radiation beam has a radiation wavelength which substantially corresponds to an absorption wavelength of the medium the leakage of which is to be determined within the section, where the beam is performed by a diode laser.
   USE - Method for localizing leakage of a gaseous medium at an internal combustion engine i.e. petrol engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining the leakage of the gaseous medium into the analysis section in dependence on the measured radiation characteristic of the radiation beam, so that the leakage detection unit of the internal combustion engine and the test bench can be installed in a cost-effective manner, thus ensuring a proper leakage inspection in a short period of time, and hence reducing the cost of installation of camera systems in an effective manner. The method allows a control unit to determine the leakage in an accurate manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a leakage detection unit for of an internal combustion engine; and
   (2) a Test bench for an internal combustion engine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method.
   Defining the analysis section (S1)
   Emitting an electromagnetic radiation (S2)
   Measuring the radiation characteristic of the radiation beam (S3)
   Determining the Leakage of a gaseous medium e.g. carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, into the section (S4)

PN CN113892616-A
TI Preparing honey comprises e.g. stirring and heating raw honey and sterile water, entering first-level honey to sealed sterilizing tank filled by carbon dioxide, sterilizing, adding stabilizing agent, homogenizing, vacuum concentrating honey, cooling concentrated honey sterile canning and storing.
AU HUANG P
   ZHANG X
   WEI J
   ZHOU G
AE GUANGXI HONEY BEE IND CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022134984
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing honey comprises (a) stiiring raw honey and sterile water at volume ratio of 1:(1.5-3) in stirring tank for 40-60 minutes, heating to 38-40degrees Celsius, filtering by filter screen to obtain first level honey; (b) entering first-level honey to sealed sterilizing tank filled by carbon dioxide, sterilizing synergistic agent at 35-40degrees Celsius in 20-30 MPa for 60-80 minutes to obtain second-grade honey; (c) subjecting second-stage honey to ultra-filtration device to ultra-filtration treatment at 35-40degrees Celsius in 0.2-0.3 MPa, separating honey clear liquid after ultra-filtration; (d) adding stabilizing agent i.e. 0.8-1.2% honey clarifying liquid in honey clarifying liquid and homogenizing to obtain third-grade honey; (e) entering third level honey to vacuum concentrating tank at 720 mm mercury column in 0.08-0.09 MPa at 40degrees Celsius, vacuum concentrating third level honey to obtain concentrated honey; and (f) cooling concentrated honey to room temperature, sterile canning and storing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing honey.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adds soluble soybean polysaccharide and nano-cellulose as stabilizing agent, which maintains the honey clear and bright color, increase dietary fiber, and taste is more sweet and pure, further improve uniformity and stability of honey product, performs vacuum concentration after ultrafiltration, sterile canning and storing, obtains the sterile honey product by the method, which further maintains the nutrient substance and natural flavor of raw honey, avoids the honey product to generate precipitate, improves the quality of honey and prolongs the shelf life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The specification of the multi-channel filter screen in the step (a) is 60-200 meshes, and the honey passes through the multi-channel filter screen according to the order of the filter screen mesh number from small to large. The acetic acid solution is added to adjust the pH to 3.-4, when the high-density carbon dioxide is introduced into the step (b).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The synergistic agent in the step (b) is 0.3-0.5 wt.% acetic acid solution. The ultra-filtration device in the step (c) is a tubular ultra-filtration membrane. The interception molecular weight of the tubular ultrafiltration membrane is 2x105-3x105 to the power of Daltons. The stabilizing agent in the step (d) is soluble soybean polysaccharide and nano cellulose. The nano cellulose is nano cellulose crystal. The mass ratio of soluble soybean polysaccharide to the nano cellulose in the stabilizing agent is (1-9):(1-9).

TI Laser-based atmospheric carbon emission detection method, involves converting optical signal into electrical signal by detector, and sending electrical signal to main control module, and control module calculates fluctuations of carbon dioxide and methane (CH4) concentrations in atmosphere.
AU LI M
   WANG C
   LIU J
   YUAN S
AE ANHUI ZHONGKE HUAYEE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202204830Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring carbon dioxide (CO2) by a laser in a 2003nm band (4). The laser in a 1654nm band (3) is used to measure Methane (CH4). A main control module (1) time-division generates two modulated signals and outputs them to a laser driver module (2). The laser driver module drives two lasers to emit laser light. The two laser beams are combined with a fiber combiner (5). The combined laser passes through a fixed-length multiple reflection absorption cell (6). A suction pump (8) is installed in the absorption cell. A built-in suction pump is used to replace the air in the absorption tank in real time. A detector (7) is installed at the tail of absorption cell. The laser is detected by detector, after laser passes through absorption cell. The detector converts optical signal into an electrical signal, and sends electrical signal to main control module. The main control module calculates fluctuations of CO2 and CH4 concentrations in atmosphere in real time.
   USE - Laser-based atmospheric carbon emission detection method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method strengthens the basic theoretical research, system construction and related technical equipment research and development of carbon emission monitoring and governance in my country, and narrows the gap with developed countries, and strengthens my country air pollution control and disposal capabilities, address the shortcomings of current regulatory methods and means, and enhances atmospheric carbon emissions monitoring capabilities.

The main seal                cavity is filled with liquid metal. The energy                discharging channel is connected with the main seal                chamber and the high pressure air inlet and is                connected to the outside of the valve. The liquid                carbon dioxide pushes the sealing plunger to move                up through the high-pressure air inlets after the                phase change gasification of the liquid CO2. The                high pressure inlet is opened
   USE - Ultra-high pressure liquid metal sealing                electromagnetic control valve for blasting cracking                rock used in high gas or coal and gas outburst coal                mining and foundation pit excavation.
   ADVANTAGE - The sealing plunger can bear super high                pressure through liquid metal. The pressure                maintaining and quick release of the high pressure                gas can be realized through the control of the                electromagnetic stop valve, so as to quickly                release pressure. The liquid carbon dioxide is                independent and fully phase change, and can realize                the pressure maintaining, to replace the blasting                sheet, and realizing efficient rock breaking.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                ultra-high pressure liquid metal sealing                electromagnetic control valve.
   Valve housing (1)
   Main sealing component (2)
   Pressure relief component (3)
   Electromagnetic stop valve (5)
   Sealing plunger (21)

TI System for monitoring air quality of closed space, has monitoring devices that transmit monitored quality of air inside closed space along with location of placement of monitoring device and identified number of occupants to cloud server.
AU HILL D A
AE HILL D A (HILL-Individual)
GA 2021B5020E
AB    NOVELTY - The system has monitoring devices and multiple ducts. The ducts are coupled with the closed space. The monitoring devices monitor a quality of air inside the ducts. The monitoring devices store a location of placement of each of a monitoring device present inside the closed space. The monitoring devices learn a level of carbon dioxide present inside the closed space over a period of time. A number of occupants present inside the closed space is estimated based on the level of carbon dioxide present inside the closed space over the period of time using a machine learning model. The monitored quality of air inside the closed space is transmitted along with the location of the placement of each of the monitoring device and the identified number of occupants present inside the closed space to a cloud server.
   USE - System for monitoring air quality of closed space.
   ADVANTAGE - The monitoring devices transmit the monitored quality of air inside the closed space along with the location of the placement of each of the monitoring device and the identified number of occupants to the cloud server, thus, the monitoring devices can be placed at a location which is easy to access without the intervention of a technician, and thus, making it easy in times of repair of the equipment of the air quality monitoring system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) A method for remotely monitoring air quality of a closed space and
   (2) A non-transitory computer-readable medium storing program for remotely monitoring air quality of a closed space

PN CN113135597-A
TI Solid oxide fuel cell cathode material resistant to carbon dioxide poisoning, comprises bismuth strontium iron oxide compound.
AU LI Q
   ZHAO H
   SUN L
AE UNIV HEILONGJIANG (HEIO-C)
GA 2021863958
AB    NOVELTY - Solid oxide fuel cell cathode material comprises bismuth strontium iron oxide compound.
   USE - Solid oxide fuel cell cathode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material is resistant to carbon dioxide poisoning.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Solid oxide fuel cell cathode material comprises bismuth strontium iron oxide compound of formula: Bi0.5Sr0.5Fe1-xM1xO3-~d.
   M1 = Ti, Mo, Nb or Ta;
   x = 0.1-0.4;and
   delta = 0-0.1.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the material involving (a) mixing bismuth(III) oxide, strontium carbonate, ferric oxide and the metal oxide containing element (M) uniformly, grinding and calcinating to obtain cathode precursor powder, in the cathode precursor powder, the molar ratio of bismuth element, strontium element, iron element to element (M) is 0.5:0.5:(1-x):x, where x is 0.1-0.4, (b) adding the cathode precursor powder into a mold, pressing the cathode precursor powder under pressure to form a cylinder, adding the cylinder into a muffle furnace, sintering to obtain a dense ceramic sheet and grinding the dense ceramic sheet to obtain cathode powder, (c) adding the electrolyte powder of cerium oxide compound of formula: Ce0.9Gd0.1O1.95 into a mold, pressing and shaping the electrolyte powder under pressure to obtain a substrate precursor, adding the substrate precursor into a high-temperature furnace, and sintering to obtain a solid electrolyte substrate, (d) adding the cathode powder to the mixture of terpineol and ethyl cellulose and stirring uniformly to obtain the cathode powder mixture, using a brush to symmetrically paint the cathode powder mixture on the solid electrolyte substrate to obtain a symmetrical electrode, and (e) sintering the symmetrical electrodes in a high-temperature furnace to obtain the bismuth strontium iron oxide compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves mixing bismuth(III) oxide, strontium carbonate, ferric oxide and the metal oxide containing element (M) uniformly, grinding in a star ball mill for 6-12 hours at a grinding speed of 800-1200 rpm. The step of calcining is carried out at 800-900 degrees C for 5-6 hours. The step (b) involves pressing the cathode precursor powder under pressure of 150-250 MPa to form a cylinder with a diameter of 1-1.5 cm and a thickness of 0.1-0.15 cm, sintering at 900-1100 degrees C for 8-12 hours, in which the compactness of the dense ceramic sheet is greater than 95%, and grinding for 2-4 hours at a grinding speed of 500-1000 rpm. The step (c) involves pressing at a pressure of 180-220 MPa to obtain a base precursor with a cylinder shape having a diameter of 1-1.5 cm and a thickness of 0.1-0.15 cm, and sintering at 1200-1450 degrees C for 12-24 hours. The step (e) involves sintering at 900-1100 degrees C for 2-6 hours. Preferred Components: The metal oxide containing element (M) comprises tantalum pentoxide, molybdenum trioxide, niobium pentoxide or tantalum pentoxide. The mold is a stainless mold. Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of the mass of the cathode powder to the mixture of terpineol and ethyl cellulose is (0.1-0.3 g):(0.1-0.3 ml). The mass fraction of terpineol in the mixture of terpineol and ethyl cellulose is 3-4%.

The first control is                performed in which the welding current is increased                by a first inclination (S1) to a first current                value (I1) of 200 A or more and 300 A or less and                maintained at the first current value for a first                period (T1). A second control is performed in which                the welding current is reduced from the first                current value to a second current value (I2) by a                second inclination (S2) and maintained at the                second current value for a second period (T2). A                third control is performed to increase welding                current from the second current value to a third                current value (I3) higher than the first current                value with a third inclination (S3). The fourth                control for lowering the welding current from the                third current value is started within 0.5 ms after                the welding current reaches the third current value                by the third control.
   USE - Arc welding control method.

   Third inclination (S3)
   First period (T1)
   Second period (T2)

PN KR2021087898-A
TI Oxygen generating patch for mask with detachable function has container containing solid oxygen mixture for oxygen generation, cover with micropores formed to prevent escape of solid oxygen mixture, adhesive band, and protective film.
AU DEOK M
   KIM S
AE HEUNGSAN BIO HEALTH CO LTD (HEUN-Non-standard)
   KIM S (KIMS-Individual)
GA 202182569X
AB    NOVELTY - An oxygen generating patch (100) that can be used by being attached to a conventional mask, comprises container (101) containing solid oxygen mixture (102) for oxygen generation; cover (103) in which micropores (105) are formed to prevent solid oxygen mixture contained in the container from escaping to the outside, and to allow carbon dioxide, moisture, and oxygen to pass through; adhesive band (104) attached to upper surface of cover corresponding to the rim of the container; and protective film (106) for maintaining adhesive strength on the upper surface of the adhesive band.
   USE - An oxygen generating patch for a mask having a detachable function.
   ADVANTAGE - By simply attaching the oxygen generating patch to the outer surface of the mask, it is possible to solve the suffocation of breathing due to the increase in carbon dioxide inside the mask and the lack of oxygen concentration, and bacterial propagation by the moisture accumulated inside the mask. Not only can it prevent odor, but it also has the effect of alleviating the fogging phenomenon that occurs when wearing glasses. It is easy to store and handle, so the effect of improving portability can be expected.

TI Unmanned aerial vehicle fire extinguishing device            for dense residential areas, has connecting frames            fixed with camera, nozzle installed under camera            through bracket and air box filled with compressed            carbon dioxide gas.
AU ZHENG H
AE YUEQING WANZHENG FIRE FIGHTING EQUIP CO LTD (YUEQ-Non-standard)

GA 2020A6077G
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a machine portion (1) and an                adjustment mechanism. The top outer wall of the                machine portion is welded with evenly distributed                mounting rods (4). The top inner wall of end of the                mounting rod is connected to a wing (3) through a                shaft rotation. The bottom outer wall end of the                mounting rod is fixed to a wing motor (2) by the                screws. The output end of the wing motor is                rotatably connected to the shaft of the wing                through a coupler. The bottom outer wall of the                portion is fixed with a chassis by screws. The                bottom outer wall of the chassis is fixed with two                symmetrical connecting frames by the screws. The                two connecting frames are fixed with a camera                through an adjustment mechanism. A nozzle is                installed under the camera through a bracket. The                direction of the camera and the nozzle are the                same. A compressed air box (9) is filled with                compressed carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Unmanned aerial vehicle fire extinguishing                device for dense residential areas.
   ADVANTAGE - The device avoids the additional pipeline                connections, and can realize fire fighting                operations only by manipulating the drone, and can                quickly and effectively control the fire situation.                The valuable time is gained for the next fire                fighting work, and directly extinguishing the fire.                The accurate judgments of the fire situation are                obtained, and to find the root of the fire source                to extinguish the fire, which improves the                practicability and the efficiency of fire                fighting.

TI System used to methanol synthesis from synthesis            gas rich in hydrogen and carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide            contains e.g. first adiabatic reactor, first heat            exchanger, condenser, separator, first cold gas stream,            first outlet stream.
AU ENCISO RAMOS L
   RODRIGUEZ ALONSO S
   LLABRES VEGUILLAS J
   ENCISO R L
   RODRIGUEZ A S
   LLABRES V J
AE SENER ING & SISTEMAS SA (SENE-Non-standard)
   SENER ING & SISTEMAS SA (SENE-Non-standard)
   SENER ING & SISTEMAS SA (SENE-Non-standard)
GA 202095801B
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises: a first adiabatic reactor                (11) arranged vertically in a cylindrical envelope,                where the first adiabatic reactor with a structure                comprises an inlet stream, one Venturi type mixing                element, a first divergent nozzle, a second                catalytic bed and one outlet stream; a first heat                exchanger (15); a condenser (16) connected to the                heat exchanger downstream of the heat exchanger; a                separator (18); a first cold gas stream; a first                outlet stream (21) joining the heat exchanger to a                second adiabatic reactor comprising a structure                receiving the first outlet stream, an additional                catalytic bed, an additional Venturi type mixing                element, an additional divergent nozzle, a second                additional catalytic bed and an additional outlet                stream; an additional heat exchanger; an additional                condenser; an additional separator connected to the                additional condenser downstream the additional                condenser; an additional cold gas stream; and a                second outlet stream.
   USE - The system is useful to methanol synthesis                from a synthesis gas rich in hydrogen and carbon                dioxide/carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: allows for a cheaper solution than                the conventional one; has more units connected in                series than the conventional systems, but of much                lower diameter, reducing drastically the material                cost; required less cost of equipment; doesn't                required compressor to recycle the reagents;                operates in less electricity; provides small and                medium scale methanol production due to the fact of                avoiding the recycle compressor; required only less                cost of catalyst; is more reliable (less                complexity); is simple; is safety compared to the                conventional process; allows for a wider                rangeability; and is economical.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a methanol synthesis                system.

   First heat exchanger (15)
   Condenser (16)

   First outlet stream (21)

PT P
PN CN111715257-A
TI Carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction catalyst comprises doped semiconductor as carrier subjected to copper/nitrogen doping, where semiconductor is N-type silicon carbide, P-type silicon carbide or intrinsic type silicon carbide.
AU HUANG S
   WANG J
   WANG Q
   ZHU Y
   HU Z
   CHEN Z
AE UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 202098309V
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide (CO2) cycloaddition reaction catalyst comprises M-doped semiconductor as carrier subjected to copper (Cu)/nitrogen (N) doping. The semiconductor is N-type silicon carbide (SiC), P-type SiC or intrinsic type SiC, forming M-doped silicon carbide loaded copper/nitrogen composite MSiC/N-Cu composite structure, where M is boron, aluminum, indium or phosphorus.
   USE - The catalyst is used for CO2 cycloaddition reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Compared with the prior art, the catalyst has low cost, simple preparation, good catalytic performance for CO2 cycloaddition reaction, and excellent selectivity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) application of the CO2 cycloaddition reaction catalyst comprising using copper in the CO2 cycloaddition reaction catalyst as electron-poor Lewis acidic center to catalyze the CO2 cycloaddition reaction, using boron-doped P-type silicon carbide as electron-rich Lewis alkaline center, and constructing the acid-base double-catalysis center by the charge transfer effect at the copper/nitrogen-doped P-type silicon carbide interface; and
   (2) preparation of the CO2 cycloaddition reaction catalyst comprising uniformly dispersing the boron-doped P-type silicon carbide powder mixture in deionized water to obtain dispersion of boron-doped P-type silicon carbide; using soluble copper salt powder as the copper source and dissolving the soluble copper salt powder in deionized water to form copper salt solution; under stirring condition, slowly dripping the copper salt solution into the dispersion liquid of the boron-doped P-type silicon carbide; centrifuging the obtained solution, adding nitrogen source in the collected product, under the atmosphere of inert gas, performing heat treatment, and naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain boron-doped P-type silicon carbide loaded copper/nitrogen compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The doping amount of boron is 0.1-2 wt.%, preferably 1-2 wt.%. The silicon carbide is porous silicon carbide, alpha -silicon carbide, beta -silicon carbide, and/or mixed P-type semiconductor materials. Preferred Method: The concentration of the boron-doped P-type silicon carbide powder mixture in the obtained dispersion is 1-5 mg/ml. The copper source is copper nitrate, copper chloride, copper sulfate, copper acetate and/or copper oleate. The copper salt solution is slowly dropped into the dispersion at 1-5 drops/s, and dispersed for greater than or equal to 12 hours under stirring condition of not less than 500 revolutions/minute. Centrifuging is conducted for 3-10 minutes at 8000-10000 rpm. The inert gas is nitrogen (N2), hydrogen (H2) and/or argon (Ar) gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The nitrogen source is urea, potassium nitrate, ammonium carbonate, ammonium nitrate and/or dicyandiamide. The mass ratio of P-type silicon carbide to nitrogen source is 1:5-1:20 or the nitrogen source is 0. The heat treatment is controlled at 400-900 degrees C for 1-5 hours.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)
MC J04-E01; J04-E04; J04-E11; L04-A01B; L04-C02; L04-C16
IP B01J-027/24; C07D-317/36; C07D-317/42

TI System for producing solid carbonate, has circulation device which recirculates and transports carbonate solid, unreacted liquid and unreacted gas to upper end of carbonization device and enters carbonization device for cyclic reaction.
AU HUANG D
   GU C
   HUA J
   LIU F
   REN G
   FANG J
AE SHENZHEN BATIAN ECOTYPIC ENG CO LTD (SZBT-C)
GA 2020772360
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has a gas input device (200) and a liquid input device (300) that are connected to a carbonization device (100). The gas input device is used to input gas into the carbonization device. The liquid input device is used to input liquid to the carbonization device. An upper end of the carbonization device includes a gas input port (110) and a liquid input port (120). A gas input device is hermetically connected to the gas input port to communicate with the carbonization device. The liquid is a liquid containing calcium ions or magnesium ions. The ammonia and carbon dioxide gas input into the gas input device. The gas-liquid mixture reacts in the carbonization device to produce carbonate solids, to unreact liquids and to unreact gas. A circulation device is used to recirculate and transport a carbonate solid, the unreacted liquid and the unreacted gas to the upper end of the carbonization device and enters the carbonization device for cyclic reaction.
   USE - System for producing solid carbonate such as magnesium-containing calcium carbonate, calcium carbonate or magnesium carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system forms a gas-liquid mixture of carbon dioxide gas, ammonia gas and liquid in the carbonization device and reacts in the top-down movement process of the carbonization device, which improves the reaction generation efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing solid carbonate using the system.

PN CN111364895-A; CN111364895-B
TI Safe self-rescuing door has heat-proof door body hinged to heat-proof door frame through a heat-proof hinge, reaction cavity and pressurization cavity are located on water storage cavity and heat conduction plate embedded on heat-proof door.
AU OUYANG N
AE OUYANG N (OUYA-Individual)
   HUIZHOU HONGZHI JINGGONG HARDWARE PROD (HUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202064047V
AB    NOVELTY - Safe self-rescuing door comprises a heat-proof door body (2) hinged to a heat-proof door frame (1) through a heat-proof hinge (3). The heat-proof door body is embedded with a water expansion sealing around it. The heat-proof door body is provided with a water storage cavity, a reaction cavity and a pressurizing cavity. The reaction cavity and the pressurization cavity are located on the left side of the water storage cavity and the reaction cavity is located directly below the pressurization cavity. The reaction cavity is filled with reaction liquid. The top end of the pressurization cavity is connected with a jet pipe and a pressure valve is installed in the jet pipe. The end of the jet pipe far away from the pressurizing cavity is connected with a diffusion nozzle (13) and a heat conduction plate (17) is embedded on one side of the heat-proof door and at the corresponding position of the reaction chamber and the reaction liquid is sodium carbonate solution.
   USE - Used as safe self-rescuing door.
   ADVANTAGE - The self-rescuing door ensures that the carbon dioxide generated by the heating condition of carbonic acid is sprayed out under accumulating pressure; increase the concentration of carbon dioxide in the heat; reduces the concentration of oxygen and restrain the spread of heat; ensure a safe living environment for indoor residents; provides sufficient time for heat fighting and rescue and improves safety.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the safe self-rescuing door.
   Heat-proof door frame (1)
   Heat-proof door body (2)
   Heat-proof hinge (3)
   Diffusion nozzle (13)

PN CN111099589-A; CN111099589-B
TI Making biochar by using coal-fired fly ash as raw material used as soil improver, involves enriching and recovering unburned carbon in coal-fired fly ash, and heating one or more of steam, carbon dioxide, and air to form gas.
AU LIN Z
   HUANG C
   DAI M
   WU J
AE UNIV ZHAOQING (UNZQ-C)
GA 2020403859
AB    NOVELTY - Making biochar by using coal-fired fly ash as raw material, involves enriching and recovering the unburned carbon in coal-fired fly ash, the grade of recovered unburned carbon is 70-95%. The one or more of steam, carbon dioxide, and air are heated to form a gas with a temperature of 90-110 degrees C as the activation atmosphere. The inert gas is used to introduce the activation atmosphere and serve as a flow rate control unit. The unburned carbon is heat-treated at a high temperature in an activation atmosphere, with a heat treatment temperature of 300-1000 degrees C and an activation time of 0.5-8 hours to prepare the biochar.
   USE - Method for making biochar using coal-fired fly ash as a raw material used as a soil improver or an adsorbent or carrier material, serves as a carrier for live bacteria (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to make biochar which is cost-effective, saves energy and environmental protection. The prepared biochar material has porous characteristics and high specific surface, and also has multiple active groups on the surface of the biochar material, which can provide a good carrier environment for live bacteria. It has recycling and reuse, and no secondary pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using biochar material as a carrier material, which involves:
   (A) loading the biochar material as a carrier of live bacteria; and
   (B) immersing biochar material in a bacterial solution at 30-70 degrees C for 2-8 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The inert gas is nitrogen and the flow rate is 300-1000 milliliter per minute. The enriching and recovering unburned carbon are performed in coal-fired fly ash by ultrasonic flotation. The ultrasonic flotation method includes agitating, granulating and ultrasonic breaking.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2020086930-A1; CA3109615-A1; AU2019364624-A1; US2021179909-A1; CN112955520-A; KR2021084521-A; BR112021007677-A2; EP3870667-A1; JP2022505785-W; US11359123-B2
TI Composition used in refrigeration, air conditioning or heat pump systems, comprises refrigerant mixture comprising difluoromethane, 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene and carbon dioxide.
AU HUGHES J
   MINOR B H
AE CHEMOURS CO FC LLC (CMFC-C)
   CHEMOURS CO FC LLC (CMFC-C)
   CHEMOURS CO FC LLC (CMFC-C)
   CHEMOURS CO FC LLC (CMFC-C)
   CHEMOURS CO FC LLC (CMFC-C)
GA 202035898K
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises a refrigerant mixture for replacing R-410A refrigerant mixture (azeotropic mixture of difluoromethane and pentafluoroethane) comprising 25-38 wt.% difluoromethane, 55-65 wt.% 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene, and 3-10 wt.% carbon dioxide.
   USE - The composition is used in refrigeration, air conditioning or heat pump systems (all claimed), where the refrigeration is low temperature refrigeration and medium temperature refrigeration, the air conditioning or heat pump systems include residential air conditioners, residential heat pumps, chillers, including flooded evaporator chillers, direct expansion chillers, and centrifugal or screw chillers, mobile air conditioning units, dehumidifiers, and their combinations. The composition is useful in heat pumps designed to cool and heat the passenger compartments of hybrid and/or electric vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is non-ozone depleting, has significantly less direct global warming potential and match the performance of R-410A, and is environmentally sustainable alternative.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a process for producing cooling, comprising condensing the composition and evaporating the composition in the vicinity of a body to be cooled;
   (2) a process for producing heating, comprising evaporating the composition and condensing the composition in the vicinity of the body to be heated;
   (3) a method of replacing R-410A in air conditioning or heat pump systems, comprising providing the composition as replacement for R-410A in the air conditioning or heat pump system;
   (4) an air conditioning or heat pump system, comprising an evaporator, a compressor, a condenser, and an expansion device, containing the composition;
   (5) a method of replacing R-410A in refrigeration systems, comprising providing the composition as replacement for R-410A in the refrigeration system; and
   (6) a refrigeration system, comprising an evaporator, a compressor, a condenser, and an expansion device, containing the composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The refrigerant mixture comprises 25-37 wt.%, preferably 36 wt.% difluoromethane, 56-64 wt.%, preferably 58 wt.% 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene, and 5-10 wt.%, preferably 6 wt.% carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The composition further comprises one or more components chosen from lubricants, dyes, solubilizing agents, compatibilizers, stabilizers, tracers, anti-wear agents, extreme pressure agents, corrosion and oxidation inhibitors, metal surface energy reducers, metal surface deactivators, free radical scavengers, foam control agents, viscosity index improvers, pour point depressants, detergents, viscosity adjusters, and their mixtures. The lubricant is chosen from mineral oil, alkylbenzene, polyol esters, polyalkylene glycols, polyvinyl ethers, polycarbonates, perfluoropolyethers, synthetic paraffins, synthetic naphthenes, polyalpha-olefins, and their combinations. The stabilizer is chosen from hindered phenols, thiophosphates, butylated triphenylphosphorothionates, organo phosphates, or phosphites, aryl alkyl ethers, terpenes, terpenoids, epoxides, fluorinated epoxides, oxetanes, ascorbic acid, thiols, lactones, thioethers, amines, nitromethane, alkylsilanes, benzophenone derivatives, aryl sulfides, divinyl terephthalic acid, diphenyl terephthalic acid, ionic liquids, and their mixtures. The global warming potential is less than 250. Preferred Method: The air conditioning or heat pump system comprises an evaporator and the evaporator that operates at a midpoint temperature of 0-20 degrees C. Preferred System: The system includes one or more heat exchangers that operate in counter-current mode, cross-current mode, or cross-current mode with counter-current tendency. In the refrigeration system, the evaporator operates at a midpoint temperature of -25 degrees C to 0 degrees C or -45 degrees C to -10 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Smart volatile organic compounds (VOC) waste gas environmental protection treatment device in which VOC exhaust gas is compressed and burned in cylinder to become non-polluting carbon dioxide and water vapor and exhaust through exhaust pipe.
AU WU J
   HU J
   YU P
   WANG C
AE WUHU INST TECHNOLOGY (WHTI-C)
GA 2020313617
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a cylinder (1), intake valves (11) and exhaust valves (12) that are symmetrically arranged on both sides of the top of the cylinder. A spark plug (2) is fixedly mounted on the cylinder and between the intake valve and the exhaust valve. A crankshaft (3) is fixedly installed inside the cylinder. A connecting rod (4) is fixedly connected to the crankshaft. A piston (5) is fixedly connected to the connecting rod. An output end of an induced draft fan (6) is connected to the intake valve. An intake pipe (7) is connected to the input end of the induced draft fan. A main fan is connected to the exhaust pipe. A control system (10) is electrically connected to the induced draft fan and the main fan. An induced draft fan directs VOC exhaust gas into the cylinder through the intake pipe. The VOC exhaust gas is compressed and burned in the cylinder to become non-polluting carbon dioxide and water vapor and exhaust through the exhaust pipe.
   USE - Smart VOC waste gas environmental protection treatment device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has small volume, simple structure, low manufacturing and operating costs, simple and safe operation, and no secondary pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for smart VOC waste gas environmental protection treatment device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of smart VOC waste gas environmental protection treatment device.
