TI Preparing cemented carbide comprises ball milling ultra-fine tungsten-carbide powder, tungsten-carbide powder and cobalt powder mixing with glue, pressing, sintering in a sintering furnace by passing carbon dioxide gas, and cooling.
AU LI Q
   JIANG A
   SHI W
   ZHU W
   LIU Q
AE CHONGQING ACAD SCI & TECHNOLOGY (CHON-Non-standard)
   CHONGQING ACAD SCI & TECHNOLOGY (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 202025878S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cemented carbide, comprises (i) adding ultra-fine tungsten-carbide powder, tungsten-carbide powder with a particle size of 1.5-25 m and cobalt powder with a particle size of 2 m into a ball mill, and ball milling for 24 hours, where the weight ratio of the ultrafine tungsten-carbide powder, the tungsten-carbide powder with a particle size of 1.5-25 m and the cobalt powder with a particle size of 2 m is 5-15:80-90:6-20, (ii) adjusting the mixed powder so that the percentage of total carbon in the mixed powder is 6.15%, and mixing the glue, and (iii) pressing the powder, placing into the sintering furnace, and sintering at a temperature of 1420 degrees C for 2 hours by passing 2 Mpa of carbon dioxide gas, and cooling with the furnace to obtain the formed hard alloy.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cemented carbide.
   ADVANTAGE - The hard alloy has few microstructure defects, high micro hardness and other advantages, improves hardness and wear resistance of hammer head, extends life of hammer head and reduces cost of use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) sand-making composite wear-resistant hammer head, comprising the hard alloy; and
   (2) preparing the hammer head, comprising (i) placing the cemented carbide on the bottom of the hammer die and fixing with the hammer die, and (ii) casting the smelted Mn13 steel into the mold at 1400 degrees C, and releasing the mold and cooling to obtain a composite wear-resistant hammer for sand making.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cemented carbide has a groove-shaped structure, a multiple protrusions are provided in the groove of the cemented carbide, and the side of the cemented carbide facing away from the protrusions is placed in the bottom of the hammerhead mold. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the ultra-fine tungsten-carbide powder is tungsten-carbide powder obtained by ball milling 0.4 hours of tungsten-carbide powder at a speed of 250 revolutions/minutes for 96 hours. In the step (ii), the incorporated rubber is SD rubber, and the volume-mass ratio of the SD rubber to the mixed powder is 90-150 ml: 1 kg. In the step (ii), graphite powder is used to adjust the total carbon of the mixed powder.

PT P
PN CN110745853-A
TI Calcium oxide product used as adsorbent and decontaminant in sewage treatment agent and high temperature carbon dioxide remover and filler, comprises ammonium chloride, modified calcium hydroxide and sodium carbonate.
AU ZHENG Q
AE TANGSHAN FENGNAN FENGGANG ASH IND CO LTD (TANG-Non-standard)
GA 2020117116
AB    NOVELTY - Calcium oxide product comprises 40-60 mol ammonium chloride, 10-20 mol modified calcium hydroxide and 10-20 mol sodium carbonate.
   USE - The calcium oxide product is used as adsorbent and decontaminant in sewage treatment agent, sulfur fixing agent in desulfurization technology, and high temperature carbon dioxide remover and filler.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium oxide product has excellent surface activity and adsorption ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the calcium oxide product involving (i) taking modified calcium hydroxide and ammonium chloride, adding deionized water to modified sodium hydroxide, adjusting the concentration of modified calcium hydroxide solution to 1-1.5 mol/l, slowly dripping ammonium chloride into modified calcium hydroxide solution, stirring at 50-60 degrees C, reacting at 300-500 rpm for 30-40 minutes and filtering the reaction product to obtain a calcium chloride solution, (ii) taking sodium carbonate, adding to calcium chloride solution, placing the solution in a microwave catalytic synthesis/extraction apparatus and performing a microwave catalytic synthesis reaction at a microwave power of 600-1000 W for 20-30 minutes to obtain a suspension, (iii) subjecting the suspension to ultrasonic dispersion for 15-30 minutes, washing and filtering with ionized water, drying under vacuum for 3.5-4.5 hours and grinding to obtain fine particles of calcium carbonate powder, (iv) adding calcium carbonate powder to a steel container, placing in a vacuum heating furnace and calcining at 800-1000 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 hours, (v) cooling the calcined steel container to 500-600 degrees C for 0.5-1 hour, and (vi) quenching the steel container using cooling oil for 2-3 hours to obtain the finished calcium oxide product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The modified calcium hydroxide is obtained by cleaning and drying calcium carbide, grinding by a grinder, passing through a 250-300 mesh sieve, calcining in a vacuum furnace at 350-400 degrees C for 1-2 hours, cooling to obtain calcium carbide treated residue, slowly dripping calcium carbide treated residue into deionized water, stirring the solution using a magnetic stirrer at 1000 rpm for 4-6 minutes, leaving still for 20-40 minutes, filtering the solution, filtering and drying at room temperature to obtain the product. Preferred Composition: The calcium oxide product comprises 50 mol ammonium chloride, 16 mol modified calcium hydroxide and 15 mol sodium carbonate. The weight ratio of calcium carbide treated residue and deionized water is 1:5. Preferred Conditions: In step (iii), the ultrasonic treatment frequency is 80-100 KHz.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC D04-A01; D04-B07D; E11-Q02C; E31-F01C; E31-N05C; E34-D01; N07-J

TI Fluidized bed cold slag system with biomass            gasification co-combustion function has cooling unit            which is connected to gasification unit, and that cools            mixed ash after gasification reaction of gasification            unit, while recovering heat.
AU YANG H

AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2019A7011H
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a feeding unit, a gasification                unit and a cooling unit. The feeding unit is used                for adding biomass raw materials (1), so that the                biomass raw materials are mixed with high                temperature ash in advance. The gasification unit                is connected with the feeding unit, and is used to                achieve a sufficient mixing and gasification                reaction of the biomass raw material, the                high-temperature ash and the boiler flue gas as a                gasification agent, to produce combustible gas. The                gas is used for mixed combustion of a fluidized bed                boiler and coal. The cooling unit is connected to                the gasification unit, and is used to cool the                mixed ash after the gasification reaction of the                gasification unit, while recovering heat.
   USE - Fluidized bed cold slag system with biomass                gasification co-combustion function.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluidized bed cold slag system uses the                oxygen, carbon dioxide and water vapor in the flue                gas of the tail of the fluidized bed boiler as                gasification agents, and the physical sensible heat                of the coal-fired high temperature ash as a heat                source, so that the conditions for biomass                gasification is achieved, and the generated gas is                directly introduced into the fluidization. The bed                boiler is mixed with coal and is provided with                compact structure, high gasification efficiency,                and reduced carbon dioxide emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                fluidized bed cold slag method with a biomass                gasification mixed combustion function.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                fluidized bed cold slag system with biomass                gasification co-combustion function.

   SUDA S
   KOJI S
   WATANABE S
   ITABASHI H

AE CANON KK (CANO-C)
GA 2019756346
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing wax component sprinkling liquid to which binder resin made of polyester or polymerizable monomer selected from group which consists of styrene, styrene derivative, acrylate, and methacrylate, wax that contains ester wax, and organic solvent are mixed, and the wax is dispersed. The binder resin, coloring agent, wax component sprinkling liquid, and resin solution containing organic solvent are prepared. The dispersion is prepared which mixed dispersion medium containing resin solution and carbon dioxide, and droplet of resin solution dispersed to dispersion medium. The dispersion medium is circulated to dispersion, organic solvent contained in droplet is extracted to dispersion medium, organic solvent is removed from dispersion medium, and toner particle is obtained. The wax contains ester of the alcohol more than bivalence, aliphatic monocarboxylic acid or ester of carboxylic acid more than bivalence, and aliphatic monoalcohol.
   USE - Manufacturing method of toner.
   ADVANTAGE - The particle size distribution is sharp and the wax is highly dispersed inside the toner particles in the dissolution suspension method using carbon dioxide. Since the coarsening by uneven distribution on the surface of toner particle and aggregation of wax is suppressed in the time of droplet formation, the dispersion stability of droplet is improved. The dispersibility of wax is easily maintained in the time of droplet formation. The affinity with binder resin is improved, and the dispersibility of wax by using wax as ester wax is improved. The mold release property of binder resin at the time of fixing is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the manufacturing apparatus of toner.

TI Power generation system for generating power by using supercritical carbon dioxide as working fluid, has power generation portion configured to generate electric power using power transmitted from power transmission portion.
AU WYGANT K
AE HANWHA POWER SYSTEMS CO LTD (HANW-C)
GA 2019483274
AB    NOVELTY - The power generation system (100) has first compression portion (110) and a second compression portion (120) configured to independently compress the working fluid. A first regeneration portion (130) configured to heat the working fluid compressed by the first compression portion. A second regeneration portion (140) configured to heat the working fluid heated by the first regeneration portion and the working fluid compressed by the second compression portion. An expansion portion (160) configured to generate power by expanding the working fluid that has received heat from the main heat exchange portion. A power transmission portion (170) configured to transmit the power generated by the expansion portion to first and second compression portions. A power generation portion (180) configured to generate electric power using the power transmitted from the power transmission portion.
   USE - Power generation system for generating power by using supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) as working fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The control portion control the join portion so that the supercritical CO2 power generation system achieves the optimal performance. The bull gear is connected to the driving shaft of the power generation portion, and the power is transmitted to the power generation portion as the bull gear rotates to perform power generation. The overall structure of the supercritical CO2 power generation system is simplified so that installation operations are performed easily, and an installation space is effectively utilized, and a size of the installation space of the supercritical CO 2 power generation system is reduced.

PN CN109529843-A; CN109529843-B
TI Titanium-cobalt catalyst useful for photothermal chemical cycle decomposition of carbon dioxide comprises cobalt oxide and titanium oxide as active components.
AU FANG Z
   ZHANG Y
   YU J
   WANG Z
   LV A
   ZHOU J
   CHEN Z
   NI M
   HU J
   CEN K
AE ZHEJIANG ZHENENG BEILUN POWER GENERATION (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201931163P
AB    NOVELTY - Titanium-cobalt catalyst comprises cobalt oxide and titanium oxide as active components, where the catalyst is (I).
   USE - (I) is useful for photothermal chemical cycle decomposition of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has good photo-responsibility, redox property and photo-hydrophilicity, good anti-sintering ability, high activity, excellent photoresponse characteristics, good dispersibility, small grain diameter, and good stability; (I) is economical, environmentally-friendly. The method uses aqueous solution system, reduces the use of organic solvent, and has the controllable preparation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Titanium-cobalt catalyst comprises cobalt oxide and titanium oxide as active components, where the catalyst is of formula (CoxTi1-xO2-x) (I).
   x = 0-0.3.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing (I) comprising (i) adding cobalt-titanium catalyst, cobalt(II) sulfate and titanium oxide sulfate, then adding citric acid according to the molar ratio of cobalt-titanium catalyst to citric acid 1:3, adding cobalt(II) sulfate, titanium oxide sulfate and citric acid to deionized water, and heating to 80 degrees C in water bath to completely dissolve to obtaining a solution; (ii) adding sodium hydroxide solution drop-wise to the solution of the step (i) adjusting the pH to 9-10, stirring for 30 minutes to obtain gel-type mixture; (iii) transferring the gelatinous mixture to a stainless steel hydrothermal kettle lined with polytetrafluoroethylene, after heating to 160 degrees C, keeping the reaction for 24 hours; (iv) leaving the insoluble matter of the lower layer reaction product obtained by centrifuging in step (iii) washing using anhydrous ethanol and deionized water to obtain solid precipitate; and (v) placing the solid precipitate in a muffle furnace, heating to 160 degrees C, holding for 30 minutes, continuing to raise the temperature to 500 degrees C, calcining for 2 hours, then cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i) the amount of deionized water is controlled so the concentration of titanium oxide sulfate in the final solution is 0.2-0.5 mol/l. In the step (ii) the concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is 5-10 mol/l. Preferred Method: In the step (iii) the temperature increase rate is controlled to be 2-3 degrees C/minute. In the step (iv) the rotation speed of the reaction product is not less than 5000 revolutions/minute, when washing, the mixture is washed alternately with anhydrous ethanol and deionized water for 10 times, and each time after washing, is centrifuged and filtered. In the step (v) the temperature rise rate is controlled to 2-3 degrees C/minute at the first temperature rise, and the temperature increase rate is 5-7 degrees C/ minute at the second temperature rise.

GA 2019217892
AB    NOVELTY - An apparatus comprises a renewable electrical input, a panel docking port, DC battery, battery docking port, inverter, outlet relay, WiFi receiver, aquacontroller, switchplate, lighting array, air pumps, tank evacuation pumps (12), air stones, bioreactor housing structure (14), permeable bioreactor lid, bioreactor tank (16), bioreactor sled (17), tank footing stabilizer (18), Greenstone Device housing panels, hermetic sealing panel (20), mass extraction separation system (21), nutrient feed reservoir inlet (22), biomass gravity filter, water volume chambers, mass extraction removable cap, mass volume chamber, OUT A: biomass collection (27), OUT B: water return.
   USE - The apparatus is used for sequestering carbon dioxide within algae biomass (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Greenstone Device biotechnology enables consumers, businesses, municipalities and nations to mitigate their carbon output by providing a platform for capturing emitted carbon directly from the atmosphere, and converting that carbon into organic biomass fertilizer. Greenstone Device biotechnology is the opposite of a one-off design prototype, this concept is meant for mass production using modular systems that enable high volume biomass production for the private consumer. The biotechnology includes remote access, enabling maintenance and monitoring to a network of global carbon capture systems from a central hub. As a byproduct of capturing carbon the Greenstone Device biotechnology produces organic biomass that can be used in the following ways; carbon neutral fertilizer, aquaculture, livestock feed and nutrient supplements.

   WU A
AE HANGZHOU SPECIAL PAPER IND CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2019203562
AB    NOVELTY - A cup pad paper comprises 20-30 pts. wt.                softwood pulp fiber, 20-30 pts. wt. bamboo fiber,                30-50 pts. wt. polyethylene particles, 0.05-0.5 pt.                wt. graphene, and 5-10 pts. wt. acrylic                latex.
   USE - Cup pad paper.
   ADVANTAGE - The lightweight cup pad paper has high thermal                insulation properties and saves the raw                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of cup pad paper, which involves                placing polyethylene particles in a vacuum oven and                vacuum drying, melt-mixing with graphene at 140-190                degrees C, spinning to obtain polyethylene/graphene                composite fiber, immersing polyethylene/graphene                composite fiber material in 5-15 wt.% ammonium                sulfate solution as an anode, using carbon rod as a                cathode, applying current of 1-2 A/g, treating at                50-75 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, washing with                deionized water, drying to obtain oxidized                polyethylene/graphene composite fiber material,                dispersing oxidized polyethylene/graphene composite                fiber in ethanol solution, adding                3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane and refluxing at 75-90                degrees C for 12-18 hours, washing                polyethylene/graphene composite fiber with                anhydrous ethanol to obtain                3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane-modified                polyethylene/graphene composite fiber, placing                softwood fiber, bamboo fiber and                3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane modified                polyethylene/graphene composite fiber into 80-95                degrees C water for 10-15 hours, placing in a                refiner for refining at pressure of 3-5 N/mm,                placing into the beater for beating, adding acrylic                latex to the obtained slurry, and uniformly mixing                to obtain mixed slurry, putting mixed slurry into a                paper machine, performing papermaking, pressing and                drying to obtain base paper, placing the base paper                in a carbon dioxide environment at 20-40 degrees C,                adsorbing at 10-15 MPa for 10-15 hours, foaming Raw                paper adsorbed by carbon dioxide, natural cooling                and cutting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The mass fraction of                  graphene in polyethylene/graphene composite fiber                  material is 1-2%. The                  3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane is 2-5 %mass based                  on the mass of the ethanol solution. The mass                  ratio of softwood pulp fiber, bamboo fiber and                  3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane-modified                  polyethylene/graphene composite fiber is                  1:(0.8-1.2):(1-1.4). The mass of acrylic latex is                  6-10% based on the solid content of the slurry.                  The solid content of acrylic latex is 35-45 wt.%.                  The solid content of the slurry is 2-4 wt.%.                  Preferred Conditions: The speed of beater is                  20-30 Hz. The hot press foaming temperature is                  70-90 degrees C, and foaming pressure is 1-3                  MPa.

   LOPEZ C

   LIRAS TORRENTE M
   DE LA PENA O'SHEA V A

GA 201887873E
AB    NOVELTY - Porous conjugated polymer comprising repeated units including a core including at least one aryl, and branches, where each branch comprises substituted cyclic units of (I) or (II) are new. The nucleus is linked to at least fresh ramifications through simple bonds -carbon-carbon- or triple bonds -carbonyl- between the aryl or asylums of the nucleus and (I) or (II) and each branch it is linked to two nuclei.
   USE - The polymer is useful for preparing the hybrid material as photocatalyst in a process of photoproducing hydrogen from water, and a photoreduction process of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer has a specific surface area of greater than 10 m2/g, preferably greater than 100 m2/g (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Porous conjugated polymer comprising repeated units including a core including at least one aryl, and branches, where each branch comprises substituted cyclic units of formula (I) or (II) are new. The nucleus is linked to at least fresh ramifications through simple bonds -carbon-carbon- or triple bonds -carbonyl- between the aryl or asylums of the nucleus and (I) or (II) and each branch it is linked to two nuclei.
   R1, R2 = H, 1-4C alkyl, -phenyl, -phenylOCH3, -SO3, -COOR7, -PO3, -NO2, -NH2, -CCH, -SH or -SCOCH3;
   R7 = H or 1-4C alkyl; and
   R1a-R6a = R1.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) hybrid material comprising the porous conjugated polymer and at least one inorganic semiconductor;
   (2) preparing (M1) porous conjugated polymer; and
   (3) preparing (M2) the hybrid material, comprising mixing the mixtures of porous conjugate polymer with at least one inorganic semiconductor in an organic solvent, mixtures of organic solvents or mixture of water and an organic solvent, and removing the solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The semiconductor includes e.g. titanium dioxide, zinc oxide, tin(IV) oxide, indium(III) oxide and iron(III) oxide. The inorganic semiconductor is doped or additivated with at least one metallic and/or non-metallic element.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent includes acetonitrile, methanol, acetonitrile and/or water.

Preferred Components: Each core is linked to three branches. The nuclei comprising at least one aryl are e.g. N,N,N-tris(phenyl-4-yl)amine and benzen-1,3,5-tri-yl. Each core is linked to four branches. The cores comprising at least one aryl are e.g. benzen-1,2,4,5-tetra-yl, pyren-1,3,6,8-tetra-yl and tetrakis(phenyl-4-yl)methane. The porous conjugated polymer is in a content of 0.1-99.9 (preferably 1-15) wt.% with respect to the hybrid material. The monomer comprises at least one of substituted aryl monomers of formula (b1)-(b3).Y1 = e.g. -CCH and -B(OH)2.The second monomer comprises: at least three terminal aiquino substituents and the reaction takes place in the presence of a copper cocatalyst and a base, where the palladium(0) catalyst is (triphenylphosphine) palladium, the copper cocatalyst is copper iodide the base is triethylamine; and at least three substituents derived from boronic acid or boronic ester and the reaction takes place in the presence of base, where the catalyst is tetrakis-(triphenylphosphine) palladium and palladium diacetate and the base is potassium carbonate, sodium tert-butoxide, caesium carbonate and tripotassium phosphate.

TI Apparatus for performing transcutaneous treatment by carbon dioxide, has control unit for receiving signals of gas concentration measurement unit and controlling operation of valves and ventilation unit based on signals of measurement unit.
AU BERTA Z Z

GA 201861426L
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (10) has a gas tank (50) connected to a gas supply unit (40). A ventilation unit (60) is connected to a gas outlet (31) of a rigid-wall cabinet (20) through interposed controllable valves (42, 63). The ventilation unit is coupled to a space (70) hermetically separated from a room containing the cabinet through a pipe (62). The ventilation unit is designed to discharge entire volume of treatment gas from an inner space of the cabinet within 5 seconds. A control unit (80) receives signals of a gas concentration measurement unit (29a) and controls operation of the valves and the ventilation unit based on the signals of the gas concentration measurement unit.
   USE - Apparatus for performing transcutaneous treatment by utilizing a gas i.e. carbon dioxide, for treating angiology diseases for a human patient. Can also be used for performing treatment by utilizing synthetic gas mixtures, medical gas or gas compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus ensures that a pump unit generating a power for about 350 liters/m 3 can discharge the entire amount of gas from a cabinet with a volume of about 350 liters in approximately 3-6 seconds so as to reduce time period for a patient or person around to stay in a treatment room without health damage even in relatively extreme situations.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side sectional view of an apparatus for performing transcutaneous treatment in which a cabinet is in section.

PN EP3358512-A1
TI Distribution method for distributing heat-sensitive products to final customers, involves filling cooling containers of cooling boxes with snow based on required cooling energy for each cooling box for transporting heat-sensitive products.
AU LUERKEN F
   BROUNS M
AE AIR LIQUIDE DEUT GMBH (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2018612978
AB    NOVELTY - The distribution method involves providing a coolant in liquid state at a distribution center (2). Each of the heat-sensitive products is then allocated to a respective cooling box (5) for transporting the heat-sensitive products to a final customer (3). The required cooling energy for each of the cooling boxes is then determined. The liquid coolant is then transformed into a snow at the distribution center. The snow is provided to fill a respective cooling container (6) for each cooling box. The amount of snow that fills the respective cooling container is chosen according to the determined required cooling energy for each cooling box.
   USE - Distribution method for distributing heat-sensitive products to final customers. Can be used for distributing, e.g., frozen food products such as frozen vegetables and frozen fish, and fresh food products such as milk, yogurt and meat.
   ADVANTAGE - Provides a distribution method configured to distribute heat-sensitive products to final customers in an energy and coolant saving way. Ensures that multiple heat-sensitive products can be transported in a shared cooling box from a grocery store to the final customers. Uses cooling boxes that do not require external cooling for the duration of the transport in a transport vehicle due to the use of coolant in the cooling boxes. Ensures that the required amount of coolant, e.g., carbon dioxide, can be determined accurately to save carbon dioxide and energy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the coolant manufacturer site, a distribution center, and the final customers.
   Distribution center (2)
   Final customer (3)
   Cooling box (5)
   Cooling container (6)
   Control unit (9)

GA 2018602309
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises sump containing circulating cooling water, antiscaling subsystem for antiscaling treatment, hydration subsystem for hydrating collecting basin, drainage subsystem for discharging dirt in collection tank, and controller for controlling coordinated operation of each device. The antiscaling subsystem includes induced draft fan (9), dust collector and flue gas absorbing device for increasing absorption rate of carbon dioxide in circulating cooling water in flue gas. The induced draft fan is disposed in flue gas conveying pipe connected to dust collector and chimney (10). The dust collector is connected to the intake end of flue gas suction device through flue gas output pipe. The controller is coupled to controlled ends of draft fan and flue gas absorbing device, respectively.
   USE - System used for preventing scale treatment of circulating cooling water using furnace smoke.
   ADVANTAGE - The system utilizes furnace smoke containing large amount of carbon dioxide to carry out anti-scaling treatment, reduces use amount of sulfuric acid, and reduces running cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for antiscaling treatment of circulating cooling water comprising calculating flow rate, temperature and pH value of circulating cooling water; removing dust of flue gas, and cooling flue gas to 35-45 degrees C; calculating energy loss during operation of circulating cooling water system and calculating supplementary water amount based on energy loss; and monitoring pH value of circulating cooling water in real time by pH detector, adjusting amount of flue gas in real time according to monitoring result, and maintaining pH of circulating cooling water at 7.5-8.6.

PN CN108085201-A
TI Fruit beer comprises barley malt, hops, parts water, roxburgh rose juice, medlar juice, Ginkgo juice, and yeast, where fruit beer is prepared by selecting high-quality barley, then preparing roxburgh rose, medlar, and Ginkgo juice.
AU AO Y
AE AO Y (AOYY-Individual)
GA 201843458D
AB    NOVELTY - Fruit beer comprises 200-300 pts. wt. barley malt, 8 pts. wt. hops, 7000-8500 pts. wt. water, 100-150 pts. wt. roxburgh rose juice, 130-150 pts. wt. medlar juice, 5 pts. wt. Ginkgo juice, and 5 pts. wt. yeast. The fruit beer is prepared by selecting high-quality barley, then preparing roxburgh rose juice, medlar juice, and Ginkgojuice, and concentrating them to obtain roxburgh rose juice, medlar juice, and Ginkgo concentrate. The hops are boiled then placed in a sedimentation place cooled to 50-60 degrees C, precipitated for 1-1.5 hours to obtain clarified hops extract. The obtained clarified hops extract is cooled to 10-12 degrees C, then inoculated with yeast and mixed with roxburgh rose juice, and medlar juice concentrate in the fermenter and fermented using sterile air for 10 minutes.
   USE - Fruit beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The fruit beer, which satisfies different people needs, provides delicious taste, nutrition nourishing and health care functions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The fermentation process for the first time involves allowing the yeast to reproduce and carrying out pre-fermentation at a temperature of 10-13 degrees C for 70-98 hours and at the end of the fermentation, the Ginkgo concentrate is mixed uniformly to obtain mixture. The mixture is filtered using single-type cotton cake filter anjd carrying out two filter presses, where the first filtration pressure is maintained to 1.0-1.20 kg/cm2 and clear and transparent liquor is obtained, which is then filtered, cooled to 2-4 degrees C, and using carbon dioxide mixer to pressure of 4.5-5.0 kg / cm2 pressure for mixing carbon dioxide, then barreled, sterilized and bottled to obtain the desired product.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-B02

TI Three dimensional (3D) co-culture model based on            tumor cell and tumor-associated fibroblast, produced            using methylcellulose-based 3D cell co-culture method,            digesting cells, adding into wells and performing            spontaneous aggregation.
AU DANG Y
   YANG C
   LI Z
   NI X
   CUI Z
   GAO Q
   WANG J
AE UNIV FUDAN (UYFU-C)
GA 201841763B
AB    NOVELTY - Three dimensional (3D) co-culture model based                on tumor cell and tumor-associated fibroblast, is                produced using methylcellulose-based 3D cell                co-culture method which uses 3D cell culture medium                comprising Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium/F12                medium (DMEM/F12 medium) containing 10% fetal                bovine serum and 0.24% methylcellulose, digesting                the cells, adding 100 mu l 3D cell culture medium                containing 2500 cells to untreated 96-well round                bottom plate and after 3 days, performing                spontaneous aggregation to form a dense spherical                solid structure.
   USE - The 3D co-culture model based on tumor cell                and tumor-associated fibroblast is useful for                screening drug having proliferation inhibition                effect on tumor cells, and tumor-associated                fibroblast, and in drug research (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The 3D co-culture model selectively inhibits                tumor cell and tumor-associated fibroblast, and                simulates hypoxic environment of tumors in                vivo.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for constructing a 3D co-culture model based on                tumor cells and tumor-associated fibroblasts, which                involves culturing human pancreatic cancer cell                line Capan-1 in a DMEM medium comprising 10% fetal                bovine serum, and incubating at 37 degrees C, under                5% carbon-dioxide condition, culturing human                primary pancreatic stellate cells in DMEM/F12                medium comprising 10% fetal bovine serum and                incubating at 37 degrees C, under 5% carbon-dioxide                condition, infecting the Capan-1 cell line with                green fluorescent protein (GFP) virus, and labeling                the pancreatic stellate cells with red fluorescent                protein (RFP) to detect infection efficiency of two                cells, taking logarithmic growth phase cells,                adding 100 mu l 3D cell culture medium containing                1250 Capan-1 cells and 1250 pancreatic stellate                cells to untreated 96-well round bottom plates, and                detecting the hypoxic microenvironment and two                cells co-culture of fluorescence status.
TF ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

PN CN108034848-A
TI Preparing a metal matrix composite material by recovering metal wastes using a semi-solid process comprises dispersing reinforced phase in alcohol, adding pure metal scrap, stirring mixing, drying, preheating and cooling.
AU XU H
   YANG Z
   HU M
   WANG Y
   JI Z
   ZHAO C
   CHANG H
AE UNIV HARBIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHS-C)
GA 2018398362
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a metal matrix composite material by recovering metal wastes using a semi-solid process comprises (i) dispersing reinforced phase in the alcohol, adding pure metal scrap, adopting mechanical stirring and ultrasonic dispersing operation simultaneously, mixing, drying reinforced phase-metal mixed solution in a carbon dioxide atmosphere in the tank drying and (ii) preheating scobicular metal base composite material obtained in the step (i), and heat preserving with directly hot pressing process, cooling the blank semi-solid isothermal treatment hot extrusion.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a metal matrix composite material by recovering metal wastes using a semi-solid process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and has balling effect, enhancement phase infiltration, composite and increases interface bonding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a metal matrix composite material by recovering metal wastes using a semi-solid process comprises (i) uniformly dispersing the reinforced phase in the alcohol, adding pure metal scrap, adopting mechanical stirring and ultrasonic dispersing operation simultaneously, mixing uniformly to obtain the reinforced phase-metal mixed solution, drying the reinforced phase-metal mixed solution in a carbon dioxide atmosphere in the tank drying to obtain scobicular metal matrix composite, more preferably is graphite or aluminum oxide and (ii) preheating the scobicular metal base composite material obtained in the step (i), and heat preserving with directly hot pressing process to obtain the compressed blank, cooling the blank semi-solid isothermal treatment hot extrusion to obtain the metal-based composite material, where the extrusion ratio the hot pressing process is 25: (1-2). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for material prepared above by the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the dispersion is operated using ultrasonic dispersion, preferably the ultrasonic dispersing time is 20-40 minutes; preferably, the continuously stirring speed is 400-500 revolutions/minute. In the step (i), the mechanical stirring and ultrasonic dispersion time is 40-80 minutes for operation. In the step (i), the alcohol is mass analyzing alcohol concentration of 99.99%. In the step (i), the drying temperature is 30-80 degrees C. In the step (ii), the pre-heating temperature is 250-400 degrees C and the time of heat preservation is 5-30 minutes. In the step (ii), the diameter of the compressed blank is 38-42 mm. In the step (ii), the semi-solid isothermal processing temperature of the selected metal base material of liquid-solid temperature range, and the processing time is 20-40 minutes; preferably, the semisolid isothermal processing temperature is 560-570 degrees C. In the step (ii), the diameter of the metal based composite material is 6-10 mm.

   PARK K B
   SHIN K S
AE KNU IND COOP FOUND (UKAN-C)
GA 2018381674
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing an environmental condition input window and combination element input window for inputting an environmental condition for concrete curing on a screen. Program of high volume fly ash carbonation of concrete evaluation provision is performed in a computer. A value of a combination element for mixing concrete is provided. Carbon dioxide spreading factor and carbonation depth are calculated by utilizing a carbonation reaction model for considering reactivity of the high volume fly ash (S240). The calculated result is provided on the screen.
   USE - Method for computing carbonation of high volume fly ash concrete utilized in a concrete material territory.

The method enables showing a result value of the real test result according to a simulated result value so as to improve test accuracy. The method enables utilizing a carbonation evaluating program of the high volume fly ash concrete at concrete material territory due to reliability improvement and usability easiness of a result value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a a computer-readable recording medium for storing a set of instructions to perform a method for computing carbonation of high volume fly ash concrete.

PN US2018111060-A1; WO2018078530-A1; WO2018081046-A1; EP3532837-A1; EP3532836-A1; US10792589-B2; EP3532837-B1; EP3532836-B1
TI Separator for separating carbon dioxide and sample            component from fluid mixture, has liquid outlet being            in fluid communication with chamber and arranged            proximate to chamber outlet end to provide flow of            liquid separated from mixture.
AU BERG E J
   SHREVE J A
   DENECKE E H
AE WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
GA 201832053B
AB    NOVELTY - The separator (30) has a chamber (32)                including a chamber wall, inlet and outlet ends. A                fluid mixture inlet (34) is in fluid communication                with the chamber and arranged proximate to the                inlet end of the chamber to receive a flow of a                fluid mixture. A gas outlet (36) is in fluid                communication with the chamber and arranged                proximate to the outlet end of the chamber to                provide the flow of gas separated from the mixture.                A liquid outlet (38) is in fluid communication with                the chamber and arranged proximate to the outlet                end of the chamber to provide the flow of liquid                separated from the mixture.
   USE - Separator for separating gas i.e. carbon                dioxide, and liquid i.e. sample component (all                claimed), from a fluid mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The separator increases chamber pressure by                reducing gas flow discharged from the gas outlet,                and causing an increase in the flow of liquid                discharged from the chamber and a reduction in                liquid height. The separator utilizes an elbow to                reduce velocity of the fluid mixture before                reaching the fluid mixture inlet. The separator                significantly reduces the velocity of the fluid                mixture after entry into the chamber and allows a                more efficient separation of the gas and liquid                from the fluid mixture than other separators.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for separating gas and liquid from a fluid                mixture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a gas liquid separator that can be used in a                supercritical fluid chromatography system.
   Gas liquid separator (30)
   Chamber (32)
   Fluid mixture inlet (34)
   Gas outlet (36)
   Liquid outlet (38)

PT P
PN CN107873393-A
TI Improving selenium content of large-bodied coriander fruit body by mixing cotton seed hulls and sawdust material, mixing bran, lime and calcium carbonate, adding selenium diluent, bagging, inoculating fungus, and culturing.
AU LU C
   LIN J
   CHEN Z
   LI B
   QIU C
   ZHENG Y
AE PUTIAN AGRIC SCI RES INST (PUTI-Non-standard)
GA 2018281595
AB    NOVELTY - Improving selenium content of large-bodied coriander fruit body comprises prewetting cotton husk, removing, mixing with sprayed wood chips and composting; mixing cotton seed hulls and sawdust material, mixing with bran, lime and calcium carbonate, adding selenium diluent, adjusting water content, bagging and packing; sterilizing; inoculating fungus; culturing at 23-25 degrees C for 3-5 days; opening bag, covering paddy field soil at thickness of 2-3 cm with water content of 20 wt.%; and harvesting at 23-25 degrees C with humidity of 85-90 wt.% and carbon dioxide of 500-1000 ppm.
   USE - Method for improving selenium content of large-bodied coriander fruit body.
   ADVANTAGE - The fruiting body has selenium content of 2.2-3.9 mg/kg.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving selenium content of large-bodied coriander fruit body comprises:
   (A) prewetting cotton husk for 1 day, removing, mixing with sprayed wood chips and composting for 2 days;
   (B) mixing cotton seed hulls and sawdust material by shoveling stirrer, mixing uniformly with bran, lime and calcium carbonate to obtain culture material, adding selenium diluent, adjusting water content to 65 wt.%, bagging and packing;
   (C) sterilizing for 2 hours at 126 degrees C and 0.15 MPa;
   (D) sterilizing and cooling fungus bag to 28 degrees C, and inoculating at 2 wt.%/bag;
   (E) culturing at 23-25 degrees C for 3-5 days;
   (F) opening bag, covering paddy field soil at thickness of 2-3 cm with water content of 20 wt.%; and
   (G) harvesting at 23-25 degrees C with humidity of 85-90 wt.% and carbon dioxide of 500-1000 ppm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - t The culture material comprises 40 wt.% wooden meal, 38 wt.% cotton seed hulls, 18 wt.% bran, 2 wt.% lime and 32 wt.% calcium carbonate. The selenium stock solution is selenium-enriched amino acid.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
MC C04-A08G1; C04-A09; C05-A01B; C05-B02C; C05-C04; C10-B02; C14-S18; C14-T01A; D05-H08B; P13-E01; P13-E06; P13-E07

PT P
PN US2018079660-A1
TI Capturing nitrosamines from waterwash in carbon            capture system used in e.g. chemical industries            involves circulating waterwash from waterwash section            to activated carbon-containing sorbent bed and back to            waterwash section of the system.
AU WIDGER L R
   COMBS M L
   THOMPSON J G
   LIPPERT C A
   LIU K
AE UNIV KENTUCKY RES FOUND (KENT-C)
GA 201822343E
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrosamines are captured from waterwash in                carbon capture system (CCS) by successively (a)                establishing exit portal and entrance portal to                waterwash chamber of CCS and providing circulation                connection line between exit and entrance portal                such that waterwash can flow, (b) placing sorbent                bed (20) within circulation connection line, in                which sorbent bed comprises activated carbon, and                (c) circulating waterwash from waterwash section                (10) through sorbent bed and back to waterwash                section to capture nitrosamines.
   USE - Capturing nitrosamines from waterwash in                carbon capture system used in electric power                stations, chemical industries, post-combustion                carbon dioxide capture with aqueous amine                processes, and municipal water treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The circulation of water over the sorbent bed                of activated carbon provides a cost-effective                approach to selectively remove nitrosamines from                waterwash effluent to reduce the environmental                impact associated with amine-based carbon capture.                The sorbent bed can be regenerated or replaced to                provide continued nitrosamine capture over a                prolonged period of time. Adsorption of nitrosamine                compounds onto solid sorbent makes handling,                transport, and decomposition of nitrosamines                immobilized on spent filtration material much                easier and safer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon                capture system (CCS) comprising absorber section                (12), stripper section (11), waterwash section, and                sorbent bed section, in which waterwash section is                connected to the sorbent bed section such that                water can circulate from the waterwash section to                the sorbent bed section and return back to the                waterwash section, and sorbent bed section                comprises activated carbon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a generalized schematic                diagram of carbon dioxide capture process using                aqueous amine solvent and thermal swing                regeneration.
   Recirculating waterwash section (10)
   Stripper (11)
   Absorber (12)
   Lean-rich exchanger (13)
   Sorbent bed (20)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: Activated carbon in                  sorbent bed has average surface area of 600-1200                  m2/g, average pore volume of 0.3-0.7 cm3/g,                  average pore size of 2-3 nm, average mesh size of                  8-10 to 8-30 mesh, and surface pKa of 6.5-11.                  Preferred Devices: Sorbent bed further contains                  greater than or equal to 1 screen to prevent                  activated carbon from flowing into the waterwash                  section. Pump is connected to circulation                  connection line to assist in circulating the                  waterwash. At least one valve is provided to                  control the flow of waterwash to the sorbent bed.                  Preferred Compositions: Activated carbon in                  sorbent bed further contains surface oxygen such                  that surface content of carbon to oxygen is                  95:5-75:25. Activated carbon further contains                  chlorine, potassium, iron, sodium, aluminum,                  magnesium, phosphorus, iron, silicon, sulfur,                  and/or calcium. Activated carbon in sorbent bed                  comprises oxidized activated carbon. Preferred                  Processes: Method includes performing periodic                  regeneration of sorbent bed by thermal treatment                  of activated carbon in sorbent bed at 700-1000                  degrees C. Method includes periodically replacing                  sorbent bed.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3)); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC D04-A01F1; D04-A01F2; E10-A18B; E11-Q02B; E11-W; E31-H05; E31-N04C; E31-N05C; Q42-D; X25-H03

PT P
PN CN112588083-A; CN112588083-B
TI Method for efficiently capturing carbon dioxide, involves using cyclic acyl urea anion functionalized ionic liquid as absorbent to absorb carbon dioxide, and carrying out desorption process at normal pressure.
AU XU Y
   CHEN T
AE UNIV SHAOXING (UNSX-C)
GA 202135971F
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide comprises using cyclic acyl urea anion functionalized ionic liquid as absorbent to absorb carbon dioxide at absorption pressure of 0.02-0.1 MPa, absorption temperature of 20-100 degrees C, and absorption time of 0.5-4 hours. The desorption process is carried out at normal pressure to 70-120 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours. The cyclic acylurea functionalized ionic liquid is composed of alkyl quaternary phosphonium cations and cyclic acylurea anions.
   USE - Method for efficiently capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes multi-site multi-cooperation of carbon dioxide, has high absorption efficiency, large absorption capacity, low absorption enthalpy, wide use environment of absorbing carbon dioxide and wide absorption temperature control range, and can reduce viscosity of the system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cyclic acylurea anion is a cyclic monoacylurea or a cyclic polyacylurea structure. The cyclic acylurea anion functionalized ionic liquid is a cyclic monoacylurea functionalized ionic liquid or a cyclic diacylurea functionalized ionic liquid Ionic liquid. The cyclic monoacylurea functionalized ionic liquid is tributyl quaternary ethyl phosphine uracil, tributyl quaternary ethyl phosphine methyl uracil, tributyl quaternary ethyl phosphine hydantoin, tributyl quaternary ethyl phosphine dihydrouracil, trihexyl tetradecyl phosphine uracil, trihexyl tetradecyl phosphine methyl uracil, trihexyl tetradecyl phosphine hydantoin, and trihexyl tetradecyl phosphine dihydrouracil. The cyclic bisacylurea functionalized ionic liquid is tributyl quaternary ethyl phosphine cyanuric acid, tributyl quaternary ethyl phosphine glyoxal, tributyl quaternary ethyl phosphine malonyl urea, tributyl quaternary ethyl phosphine tetraoxane, trihexyl tetradecyl phosphine cyanuric acid, trihexyl tetradecyl phosphine glyoxal, trihexyl tetradecyl phosphine malonyl urea, and trihexyl tetradecyl phosphine tetraoxapyrimidine. The brominated alkane is bromoethane or 1-bromotetradecane. The trialkylphosphine is tributylphosphine or trihexylphosphine. The cyclic acylurea is a monoacylurea or a diacylurea. The monoacylurea is uracil, 5-methyl uracil, hydantoin or dihydrouracil. The diacylurea is cyanuric acid, oxalyl urea, malonyl urea or alloxan. Preferred Conditions: The cyclic acylurea anion functionalized ionic liquid is prepared by taking bromoalkanes and trialkylphosphines as raw materials in a nitrogen atmosphere at a molar ratio of 1:1 at 60-80 degrees C for 24 hours to synthesize bromide alkyl quaternary phosphine salt, and then passing through a strong basic anion exchange resin to obtain an ethanol solution of alkyl quaternary phosphine hydroxide. The ethanol solution of alkyl hydroxide quaternary phosphine and the cyclic acylurea are mixed at a molar ratio of 2:1 under stirring at 30 degrees C for 20 minutes, and then spin evaporated at 60 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain cyclic acylurea anion functionalized ionic liquid.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN TW201801786-A; TW626982-B1
TI Treatment method for reducing carbon dioxide            emissions by using microalgae culture to reduce the            emission of carbon dioxide for waste water.
AU LIN Z
AE LIN Z (LINZ-Individual)
GA 201825718U
AB    NOVELTY - The present invention relates to a treatment                method of reducing carbon dioxide emissions with                high efficiency. The method comprises the following                steps of: absorbing exhaust gas containing carbon                dioxide by using an alkaline aqueous solution                having a concentration lower than 20 weight                percentage to obtain a weakly acidic aqueous                solution rich in carbonate ions and bicarbonate                ions; utilizing the characteristics of                photosynthesis carbon fixation of microalgae to add                the weakly acidic aqueous solution to the                microalgae culture tank to provide the carbon                source needed for microalgae photosynthesis. The                present invention effectively absorbs carbon                dioxide by an alkaline aqueous solution, and then                uses the microalgae culture for photosynthesis to                solve the problems of ineffectiveness in poor                absorption of carbon dioxide by fresh water or                seawater, and environmental pollution caused by the                direct discharge of waste water, thereby                effectively reducing the emission of carbon                dioxide.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E11-Q02C; E11-W; E31-N05C; J01-E02A1

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the molecules ratio of carbon dioxide to epoxides or non-epoxides is 1-100; the carbon dioxide polymer is of formula -(CR1R2-CR3R4O)mC(=O)O-)n (I). In step (ii), the anthraquinone compounds are alpha -hydroxyanthraquinone, beta -hydroxyanthraquinone, 3-hydroxyanthraquinone, 1,2-dihydroxyanthraquinone, 3,6-dihydroxyanthraquinone, 1-aminoanthraquinone, 1-methylanthraquinone, 2-aminoanthraquinone and/or 1-nitroanthraquinone; the molecular ratio of carbon dioxide polymer to anthraquinone is 1-100. In step (i), catalyst used in: (a) zinc acetate, cobalt acetate, sodium stannate, diethyl zinc, pyrogallol and other active hydrogen compounds, diethyl zinc/acrylic acid, aluminum porphyrin/triphenylphosphine, organic zinc dicarboxylate, iron zinc or cobalt zinc complexes and/or zinc-carboxylate; (b) aluminum acetylacetonate or trialkoxy aluminum oxide, (c) acetylacetone and/or tertiary amine, and (d) phosphorus-containing compound or pyridine. In step (ii), catalyst is ion coordination catalyst e.g. cobalt carbonyl, rhodium carbonyl, titanium tetrachloride-alkyl aluminum, and/or titanium(III) chloride-alkyl aluminum.m and n = greater than or equal to 1;R1-R4 = H, 1-6C alkyl, alkenyl or alkynyl groups; andA = not defined.Preferred Method: In step (i), carrying out polymerization reaction of supercritical carbon dioxide and epoxide, or binary or ternary non-epoxide to obtain corresponding polymer, where the epoxide is ethylene oxide and/or propylene oxide, non-epoxide is episulfides, nitrides, vinyl ether, diyne compound and/or polyamine; the preparation of (I) comprises either (a) polymerizing supercritical carbon dioxide and episulfides or nitrides to obtain (I); or (b) polymerizing supercritical carbon dioxide and vinyl ether to obtain carbon dioxide polymer of formula (II) (sic); or (c) polymerizing supercritical carbon dioxide and diyne compound to obtain carbon dioxide polymer of formula (III); or (d) polymerizing supercritical carbon dioxide with polyamine to obtain carbon dioxide polymer of formula (IV). In step (i) and (ii), the supercritical state of carbon dioxide is: the temperature is 31.26~-120 degrees C, pressure is 7.29-15 MPa, carbon dioxide near-critical state is: the temperature is 10-31.26 degrees C and the pressure is 4-7.29 MPa. In step (i), the polymerization time is 10-200 hours, and in step (ii), the polymerization time is 10-100 hours.

PT P
PN CN110652847-A; CN210964557-U
TI Device for treating carbon dioxide in industrial waste gas based on coal mine goaf, comprises upper and lower portions of goaf which are upper and lower fracture zones, and bacterial liquid injection and carbon dioxide injection system.
AU SU X
   ZHAO W
   XIA D
   GUO H
   SONG J
   SUN C
   WANG Q
   ZHOU Y
   FAN Y
   HUANG S
   ZHANG M
   YAN X
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
GA 202006512G
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises upper and lower portions of goaf (1) which are upper fracture zone (2) and lower fracture zone (3). The device comprises a bacterial liquid injection system, a carbon dioxide injection system, a gas-liquid delivery pipe (4), a liquid injection gas injection pipe (5), an industrial exhaust gas delivery pipe (6), and an energy extraction system. The inlet of carbon dioxide injection system is connected to industrial exhaust gas delivery pipe. The exhaust gas filters (7) are arranged on industrial exhaust gas delivery pipes. The outlet of bacterial liquid injection system and carbon dioxide injection system is connected to inlet of the gas-liquid delivery pipe.
   USE - Device for treating carbon dioxide in industrial waste gas based on coal mine goaf.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has simple operation, low cost, wide application range, supplies exhaust gas containing more carbon dioxide directly through an industrial exhaust gas conveying pipe so that industrial exhaust gas containing a large amount of carbon dioxide is prevented from being discharged into atmosphere, and reduces carbon dioxide emission in atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for treating carbon dioxide in industrial exhaust gas by using device, which involves:
   (A) drilling surface above goaf, installing and connecting bacteria liquid injection system, carbon dioxide injection system, gas-liquid delivery pipe, liquid injection gas injection pipe, industrial exhaust gas delivery pipe and energy extraction system;
   (B) injecting bacteria liquid in bacteria liquid injection system and carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide injection system into goaf;
   (C) performing anaerobic fermentation to produce gas and liquid, and exhausting it to ground through the energy extraction system; and
   (D) solidifying goaf, and pumping liquid energy chemical products in goaf to ground through energy extraction system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of device.
   Goaf (1)
   Upper fracture zone (2)
   Lower fracture zone (3)
   Gas-liquid delivery pipe (4)
   Liquid injection gas injection pipe (5)
   Industrial exhaust gas delivery pipe (6)
   Exhaust gas filters (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The method involves selecting surface drilling location in combination with mined-out area and place where there are non-minable coal seams, and following ground temperature gradient to ensure that temperature of the mined-out area is suitable for growth and reproduction of microorganisms. The goaf of abandoned mine is far, and contains principle of non-minable thin coal seams and residual coal pillars from perspective of economics and injection of carbon dioxide in well with complex geological conditions to adopt a triple-open structure. The drilling construction is divided into vertical wells and cluster wells, and single-well throughput or multi-well combined production is adopted according to function of well, where a single-well throughput is a well for both injection of bacteria liquid and production of gas and liquid production. The multi-well combined production includes well injection and multiple wells output, where central well is production well and surrounding wells are injection wells. The drilling is performed on top of upper fracture zone, which is driven into technical casing cementing, and drilled for three times at bottom of lower fracture zone below goaf, and then entered into cementing screen, and if loose layer is relatively thin then well is drilled in a two-open structure. The liquid injection gas injection pipe is installed in injection well, and downhole portion of energy extraction system is installed in the production well. The above-ground portion of energy extraction system, bacteria liquid injection system, carbon dioxide injection system and industrial exhaust gas pipeline are on the ground. The microorganism is enriched on ground through the liquid storage tank for a period of time, first valve and sixth valve are opened, and bacterial liquid is sequentially discharged along drainage pipe, gas-liquid delivery pipe, and injection pipe under the action of gravity. The liquid injection gas pipe and gas injection liquid sieve pipe flow into goaf for a period of time. The first valve is closed, second and third valves are opened and booster pump is started to extend bacterial solution to a deeper place, and bacterial solution is injected into goaf through the booster pump. The injection amount is monitored by a liquid flow meter, and is determined according to water accumulation space in goaf in well control range. The water accumulation space is space occupied by water in the goaf, and injection volume is not less than 10% of volume of water accumulation space. The bacteria liquid injection operation is completed, second valve and third valve are closed, and then carbon dioxide injection operation is performed, fourth valve and fifth valve are opened, gas injection pump is started to inject carbon dioxide stored in gas storage tank into the goaf. The injection volume is monitored by a gas flow meter, and carbon dioxide injection volume is based on sum of free space volume of goaf and 50% of volume of standing water. The free space is space in the goaf that is not filled with solids and liquids. The fourth valve, fifth valve and sixth valve are closed after completing gas injection, and when gas in gas storage tank is low then seventh valve is opened on industrial exhaust gas pipe. The industrial exhaust gas is filtered by exhaust gas filter first to filter out oxygen in exhaust gas and enter gas storage. The gas in gas storage tank is nitrogen and carbon dioxide which has strong stability, and is safe to be injected into goaf at same time. The well is closed after injection of fluid and gas, and entered into stage of anaerobic fermentation, where goaf is used as a relatively closed underground space, which provides a relatively suitable reducing environment for growth and metabolism of anaerobic microorganisms and ensures anaerobic smooth fermentation. The microorganisms fully contact the non-minable coal seams/remaining coal pillars for metabolism to achieve purpose of biologically mining residual coals, and provides suitable reducing conditions for anaerobic fermentation, generates methane gas, and portion of injected carbon dioxide is dissolved in goaf, where one portion of water is adsorbed on coal wall around the goaf, and another portion is involved in action of microorganisms to generate methane gas. The eighth valve is opened, and nitrogen gas and generated methane gas enters outer air pipe through the sieve hole of the suction liquid sieve pipe, and are discharged by the exhaust pipe, and when air pressure in outer air pipe is lower then eighth valve is closed, ninth valve is opened, suction pump is started, and suction pump is used for negative pressure extraction of mixed gas of methane and nitrogen. The fourth valve, fifth valve, sixth valve and quick connector are opened, powdery quicklime is injected into intake pipe through quick connector, and gas injection pump and carbon dioxide gas are started at the same time. The powdered quicklime is injected into the goaf, and injection amount is determined according to water quality analysis and amount of calcium carbonate to be generated. The carbon dioxide is converted into methane under the action of microorganisms, and it exists in liquid phase in the form of bicarbonate ion/carbonate ion. The quicklime is dissolved in water to form calcium (II) positive ion, and makes medium alkaline, promotes conversion of bicarbonate ion to carbonate ion, and combines with calcium (II) positive into form calcium carbonate precipitation. The large amount of liquid-phase organic matter is dissolved in water in goaf, and sucker rod and barrel pump are started. The product is sucked out of sucker pipe by sucker rod, and finally discharged through outlet of sucker pipe and collected, and then negative pressure suction is performed.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN109566965-A
TI Production of green rice by carbon dioxide supercritical fluid extraction by putting rice to be purified into extraction kettle, adding purified water as entrainer, using supercritical carbon dioxide as extraction solvent, and extracting.
AU LIU Y
AE SICHUAN BAZHONG LEADING FOOD CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2019336791
AB    NOVELTY - Production of green rice by carbon dioxide supercritical fluid extraction comprises putting the rice to be purified into extraction kettle, adding purified water as entrainer, where the weight ratio of purified water and rice is 1:100-1000, using supercritical carbon dioxide as extraction solvent at flow rate of 0.5-10 extraction kettle volumes/hour, extracting at 7.2-29.8 MPa and 25-49 degrees C or 51-68 degrees C for 35-300 minutes, and separating the extraction kettle solvent at 25-49 degrees C or 51-65 degrees C and 3-12 MPa.
   USE - The method is for production of green rice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses supercritical fluid of carbon dioxide as the extracting agent, which has very strong solubility for pesticide residue and guarantees the effectiveness of extraction and integrity, removes the pesticide residue together with the entrainer oil or water in the extraction process, while removing harmful substances, grease, and moisture and killing insect spawn and bacteria, improves the shelf life and taste of rice, has reduced cost, is suitable for industrial implementation, does not pollute the environment, and does not cause secondary pollution to the rice.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus used for production of green rice comprising carbon dioxide storage tank, carbon dioxide storage tank, extracting kettle, separating kettle and carbon dioxide recovery tank, where one end of the carbon dioxide recovery tank is connected with the separating kettle, another end of the carbon dioxide recovery tank is connected with the cooler and first pressure pump through a second pressure pump, heater is provided between the extraction kettle and separating kettle, the carbon dioxide storage tank and carbon dioxide circulating tank are provided with the first pressure pump and cooler, the carbon dioxide circulating tank and extraction kettle are provided with third pressure pump and heater, and the third pressure pump, one end of the heater, fourth pressure pump and entrainer storage tank are connected with the separating kettle having lower portion provided with a collecting port.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The purified water as entrainer can be replaced with edible fat or edible ethanol.

TI System used for continuously separating carbon dioxide from gas containing carbon dioxide, has regeneration assembly having zone for heating contained portion of array of monoliths to release adsorbed carbon dioxide, and regeneration assembly is in relative movement based on array of porous monolith.
AU EISENBERGER P
   PING E W
   SAKWA-NOVAK M
   PRUETT J
   KLEPPER R
   WYPER S
AE EISENBERGER P (EISE-Individual)
   PING E W (PING-Individual)
   SAKWA-NOVAK M (SAKW-Individual)
   PRUETT J (PRUE-Individual)
   KLEPPER R (KLEP-Individual)
   WYPER S (WYPE-Individual)
GA 2021E36488
AB    NOVELTY - System has an array of porous monoliths (1), where each monolith supports sorbent for carbon dioxide within its pores, and the array forms a continuous loop, an adsorption assembly in which array of porous monoliths exposed to flow of gas mixture containing carbon dioxide, where the sorbent adsorbs carbon dioxide from flow of gas mixture, and a regeneration assembly containing portion of array of porous monoliths, where the regeneration assembly comprises at least one zone for heating contained portion of array of monoliths to release adsorbed carbon dioxide, the regeneration assembly is in relative movement based on array of porous monoliths. The flow of gas mixture is induced by at least one fan. At least one fan is oriented perpendicularly to array of porous monoliths and fan induces flow of gas mixture through array of porous monoliths and exhausts flow of gas mixture upwards.
   USE - The system is used for continuously separating carbon dioxide from gases containing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces the relative complexity of starting and stopping movement system, eliminates the need for rapid-cycling and high-cycling valves, reduces the required utility capacity of plant, leads to great plant reliability, lower instantaneous utility demands, less costs and lower capital expense, optimizes the fan speeds and size different from one another at different positions along monolith loop to optimize performance and energy use of digital-to-analog conversion process, and reduces the cost per ton of carbon dioxide captured. The monolith loop relative speed, whether moving the regeneration box or the monoliths can be controlled to a slow, safe and efficient while total output of plant can increase, and the process fluids can be shared between regeneration areas to reduce the total cost of plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for continuously separating carbon dioxide from mixture of gases containing carbon dioxide.

PN AU2021105046-A4
TI Rapid heating and gasification and combustion of coal under carbon dioxide enriched high-pressure environment involves selecting coal sample of target coal seam for collection, and putting coal sample into vacuum chamber.
AU LI Z
   SASAKI K
   ZHANG X
   SUGAI Y
   WANG Q
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2021B47051
AB    NOVELTY - Rapid heating and gasification and combustion of coal under carbon dioxide enriched high-pressure environment involves selecting coal sample of the target coal seam for collection, and putting the coal sample into the vacuum chamber for drying and crushing to make the coal samples for different requirements and number them. A carbon dioxide laser generator is used to quickly heat the gasification reactor to reach the target temperature in a short time. The carbon dioxide or oxygen enrichment environment is generated in the reactor under controlled oxygen concentration by adjusting the carbon dioxide flow rate.
   USE - Method for rapid heating and gasification and combustion of coal under carbon dioxide enriched high-pressure environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides rapid heating and gasification and combustion of coal under carbon dioxide enriched high-pressure environment in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with rapid target temperature increase in a short time to avoid slow oxidation of coal, strengthen weight loss rate of coal and production of syngas, and improve efficiency of gasification conversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Rapid heating and gasification and combustion of coal under carbon dioxide enriched high-pressure environment involves selecting coal sample of the target coal seam for collection, and putting the coal sample into the vacuum chamber for drying and crushing to make the coal samples for different requirements and number them. A carbon dioxide laser generator is used to quickly heat the gasification reactor to reach the target temperature in a short time. The carbon dioxide or oxygen enrichment environment is generated in the reactor under controlled oxygen concentration by adjusting the carbon dioxide flow rate. The sample is put into the ceramic cup at the bottom of the reactor, where the carbon dioxide laser beam is used to heat the coal sample in the completely enclosed reactor. The rapid heating experiment is used in carbon dioxide enrichment environment with different oxygen concentrations, where the generated mixed gas is filtered by a filter and then transmitted to a gas monitor by an air pump.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
