PN US2020392053-A1; WO2020251826-A1; US11091408-B2; EP3983576-A1; IN202127060983-A
TI Processing emissions from hydrocarbon combustion            appliance involves e.g. receiving hydrocarbon            combustion exhaust stream (HCES) comprising water or            carbon dioxide, then removing at least portion of water            to at least partially dry HCES.
AU WINKLER N H
   SASSOON M V
   GOLAN G
   ZERBIB S A
AE LIVOLT LTD (LIVO-Non-standard)
   LIVOLT LTD (LIVO-Non-standard)
   WINKLER N H (WINK-Individual)
   ARIEL SCI INNOVATIONS LTD (ARIE-Non-standard)
   SHAHAR TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS LTD (SHAH-Non-standard)
   STANDARD CARBON LTD (STCA-Non-standard)
   ARIEL SCI INNOVATIONS LTD (ARIE-Non-standard)
   STANDARD CARBON LTD (STCA-Non-standard)
   ARIEL SCI INNOVATIONS LTD (ARIE-Non-standard)
GA 2020C71164
AB    NOVELTY - Processing emissions from a hydrocarbon                combustion appliance comprises: receiving a                hydrocarbon combustion exhaust stream (232)                comprising water or carbon dioxide; removing at                least a portion of water to at least partially dry                the hydrocarbon combustion exhaust stream;                separating the carbon dioxide from the at least                partially dried hydrocarbon combustion exhaust                stream, where the separating comprises capturing                the carbon dioxide in a solvent, heating the                solvent and the captured carbon dioxide, or                removing the carbon dioxide from the heated                solvent; subjecting the removed water to                electrolysis to generate hydrogen; and reacting the                generated hydrogen and the separated carbon dioxide                to generate at least one hydrocarbons, including at                least methane.
   USE - The method is useful for processing emissions                from a hydrocarbon combustion appliance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides recover water and carbon                dioxide from combustion emissions; and can be                omitted, rearranged, combined, supplemented with                additional steps, and/or adapted in various                ways.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system (200) comprising a condensate tank (234)                configured to receive a hydrocarbon combustion                exhaust stream comprising water or carbon dioxide                and condense steam in the hydrocarbon combustion                exhaust stream, a desiccant dehumidifier configured                to at least partially dry the hydrocarbon                combustion exhaust stream, at least one separation                apparatus configured to separate the carbon dioxide                from the at least partially dried hydrocarbon                combustion exhaust stream, the separation apparatus                are configured to capture the carbon dioxide in a                solvent, heat the solvent, and separate the carbon                dioxide from the heated solvent, an electrolysis                apparatus configured to receive water removed from                the at least partially dried hydrocarbon combustion                exhaust stream and to carry out electrolysis on the                water to generate hydrogen, and a reactor                configured for conducting a reaction between the                generated hydrogen and the separated carbon dioxide                to generate at least one hydrocarbons, including at                least methane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of a system                used for carrying out the method.
   System (200)
   Exhaust stream (232)
   Condensate tank (234)
   Heat exchanger (258)
   Methanation reactor (266)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The removing comprises                  condensing the steam in the hydrocarbon                  combustion exhaust stream. The condensing                  comprises: injecting the hydrocarbon combustion                  exhaust stream into liquid water; and passing the                  hydrocarbon combustion exhaust stream through a                  heat exchanger (258). The removing at least a                  portion of water further comprises introducing                  the hydrocarbon combustion exhaust stream to a                  desiccant dehumidifier. The method further                  comprises: boiling and distilling the removed                  water prior to the electrolysis; transferring                  heat from the boiling to the desiccant                  dehumidifier to dry a desiccant; discharging the                  solvent with the carbon dioxide captured therein                  from the first chamber and passing the discharged                  solvent and carbon dioxide from the first chamber                  through a heat pump to heat the solvent;                  discharging the heated solvent from the second                  chamber and passing the discharged heated solvent                  from the second chamber through the heat pump to                  heat the solvent and carbon dioxide discharged                  from the first chamber; boiling and distilling                  the removed water prior to the electrolysis;                  transferring heat generated by the reaction to                  the removed water for the boiling; and generating                  oxygen by the electrolysis, and transferring the                  oxygen generated by the electrolysis to a                  combustion appliance. The capturing of the carbon                  dioxide in the solvent comprises: spraying the                  solvent on the at least partially dried                  hydrocarbon combustion exhaust stream to capture                  the carbon dioxide in the solvent in a first                  chamber; and the removing the captured carbon                  dioxide from the solvent comprising heating the                  solvent with the carbon dioxide captured therein                  to release the carbon dioxide from the heated                  solvent in a second chamber. The reacting                  comprises feeding the generated hydrogen and the                  separated carbon dioxide to a methanation reactor                  (266) for catalytic reaction to generate the                  methane. The condenser: is configured to inject                  the hydrocarbon combustion exhaust stream into                  liquid water; and comprises a heat exchanger. The                  separation apparatus comprises: a first chamber                  configured to spray the solvent on the at least                  partially dried hydrocarbon combustion exhaust                  stream to capture the carbon dioxide in the                  solvent; and a second chamber configured to                  separate the carbon dioxide from the solvent. The                  system further comprises: a heat pump configured                  to receive the solvent with the carbon dioxide                  captured therein from the first chamber, and                  receive the heated solvent from the second                  chamber and heat the solvent discharged from the                  first chamber while cooling the solvent received                  from the second chamber; a water purifier                  configured to boil and distill the water prior to                  the electrolysis; conduits to transfer heat                  generated by the reaction to the water purifier;                  conduits to transfer heat from the water purifier                  to the desiccant dehumidifier; a compressor for                  pressurizing the methane and a tank for storing                  the pressurized methane; and conduits to transfer                  oxygen generated by the electrolysis apparatus to                  a combustion appliance. The reactor comprises a                  methanation reactor configured to carry out a                  catalytic reaction between the generated hydrogen                  and the separated carbon dioxide to generate the                  methane. Preferred Components: The solvent                  comprises a sulfinol solvent.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); ; X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

TI Reactor used in online monitoring device for carbon dioxide absorption, has multi-layer support structure that is set inside upper tower frame and flow meter is provided at connection pipe between electric pump and spray device.

AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide absorption reactor (1) has a casing (4), an electric pump (9), a flow meter (10), a spray device (11), a multi-layer support structure (12). The casing is divided into a lower box (41) and an upper tower (42). The upper tower is located above the lower box. The upper opening of the lower box is connected to the lower opening of the upper tower. A air inlet (5) and a liquid inlet (6) are provided above the lower box. A sampling port (7) and an air outlet (8) are provided on the side wall of the lower box. The electric pump is provided outside the lower box and is connected with the spray device at the top of the upper tower through a pipeline. The flow meter is provided at the connection pipe between the electric pump and the spray device. The multi-layer support structure is set inside the upper tower frame for placing the catalyst. A gas outlet is provided at the lower end of the upper tower frame to discharge the treated gas.

The reactor is provided with a multi-layer support structure and the liquid and carbon dioxide are fully mixed and reacted. The catalyst catalytically converts carbon dioxide to effectively control the concentration of carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide online data monitor is connected to the computer terminal, which achieves the effect of monitoring carbon dioxide concentration in real time.

PN CN110092900-A; CN110092900-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide-based block copolymer comprises e.g. utilizing a cyclic acid anhydride monomer, a lactone monomer, an epoxy compound, and carbon dioxide as a monomer and initiating ring opening reaction of epoxy compound.
AU ZHOU X
   ZHANG G
   WANG Y
   XIE X
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 201971361Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide-based block copolymer comprises utilizing a cyclic acid anhydride monomer, a lactone monomer, an epoxy compound, and carbon dioxide as a monomer, a mixture of an organic Lewis acid and an organic Lewis base is used as a catalyst, and a terminal hydroxy compound is used as an initiator, the initiator initiates ring-opening copolymerization of a cyclic acid anhydride monomer, a lactone monomer, an epoxy compound and carbon dioxide, and preparing a carbon dioxide-based block copolymer by a one-step method, where the polymerization process comprises initiating a ring opening reaction of the epoxy compound under the synergistic catalysis of an organic Lewis acid and an organic Lewis base, the initiator, and coordinating with an organic Lewis acid to form an active species, the active species initiates copolymerization of the cyclic anhydride monomer and the epoxy compound to form a first polyester.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide-based block copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The copolymer has high controllability in molecular weight and block length.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide-based block copolymer comprises utilizing a cyclic acid anhydride monomer, a lactone monomer, an epoxy compound, and carbon dioxide as a monomer, a mixture of an organic Lewis acid and an organic Lewis base is used as a catalyst, and a terminal hydroxy compound is used as an initiator, the initiator initiates ring-opening copolymerization of a cyclic acid anhydride monomer, a lactone monomer, an epoxy compound and carbon dioxide, and preparing a carbon dioxide-based block copolymer by a one-step method, where the polymerization process comprises initiating a ring opening reaction of the epoxy compound under the synergistic catalysis of an organic Lewis acid and an organic Lewis base, the initiator, and coordinating with an organic Lewis acid to form an active species, the active species initiates copolymerization of the cyclic anhydride monomer and the epoxy compound to form a first polyester, after the reaction of the cyclic acid anhydride monomer, the initiator initiates carbon dioxide polymerization to form a polycarbonate, after the reaction reaches a certain degree or the carbon dioxide reaction is completed, polymerizing the lactone monomer continuously.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) introducing a cyclic acid anhydride monomer, a lactone monomer and an epoxy compound in an anhydrous oxygen-free environment to obtain a monomer mixture, (ii) mixing the monomer mixture, the catalyst, and the initiator to obtain a raw material mixture, (iii) charging the raw material mixture with carbon dioxide to cause a polymerization reaction, and after completion of the reaction, quenching the reaction to obtain a crude product, and (iv) adding the crude product to an organic solvent for precipitation, and separating the resulting precipitate and drying. The pressure of carbon dioxide in the reaction is 1-4 MPa. The reaction temperature is 40-80 degrees C and the reaction time is 4-24 hours. Preferred Components: The cyclic acid anhydride monomer is maleic anhydride, phthalic anhydride, 1-cyclopentene-1,2-dicarboxylic anhydride, cyclobutane dicarboxylic anhydride and bicyclo (2.2.1) or hept-5-ene-2,3-dicarboxylic anhydride. The lactone monomer is omega -caprolactone, omega -decalactone, racemic lactide, beta -butyrolactone, delta -valerolactone and gamma -caprolactone. The epoxy compound is ethylene oxide, cyclohexene oxide, styrene oxide, butylene oxide, and propylene oxide. The molar ratio of the acid anhydride monomer, lactone monomer and epoxy compound is 1:(1-2): (5-10). The molar ratio of the organic Lewis acid to the epoxy compound is (1-1.5): 500. The molar ratio of the organic Lewis base to the epoxy compound is (1-2): 1000. The organic Lewis acid is triethylamine, triethylborane, triphenylborane and tris(pentafluorophenyl)borane. The organic Lewis base is 1,8-diazabicycloundec-7-ene, 4-dimethylaminopyridine, 1,5,7-triazidebicyclo(4.4.0)non-5-ene and phosphazene ligand P4-tert-butyl. The initiator is water, benzyl alcohol or benzyl alcohol. In the step (iv), the organic solvent as precipitator, the precipitator is normal hexane, methanol and petroleum ether.

PN WO2018011567-A1; EP3484817-A1; US2019232216-A1; JP2019527178-W
TI Mineralization of carbon dioxide for forming            products useful in e.g. paper-making involves forming            dissolving carbon dioxide and alkali in water, mixing            with water source containing magnesium and calcium            cations, and precipitating products.
AU IMBABI M S
   GLASSER F
AE UNIV ABERDEEN (UYAB-C)
   UNIV ABERDEEN (UYAB-C)
   UNIV ABERDEEN (UYAB-C)
GA 2018056445
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide is mineralized by forming                alkaline aqueous solution containing carbonate                anions by dissolving carbon dioxide and alkali in                water; mixing alkaline aqueous solution with water                source containing magnesium and calcium cations;                (8a) selectively precipitating 1st product                containing calcium cations and carbonate anions in                1st precipitation step at a 1st pH; and (8b)                selectively precipitating 2nd product containing                magnesium cations and carbonate anions in 2nd                precipitation step at a 2nd pH, where the 2nd pH is                higher than the 1st pH.
   USE - Mineralization of carbon dioxide for forming                products useful in paper-making, polymer, paints,                adhesives, healthcare, food, agriculture, and                construction industries, and products for use as                cementitious construction materials that can form                dimensionally stable building products, such as                wallboards, insulation panels, and lightweight                construction blocks.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces practically and                commercially useful, non-hazardous solid materials.                Production of the 1st products does not release any                significant amounts of carbon dioxide, but instead,                enables carbon dioxide to be captured e.g. from                combustion wastes and converted to useful products                at a low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) system for mineral sequestration of carbon                dioxide comprising carbon dioxide inlet (1),                absorbing stage (7) connected to carbon dioxide                inlet for absorbing carbon dioxide in water                containing an alkali to form alkaline aqueous                solution containing carbonate anions, water source                inlet (4) for providing water source containing                magnesium and calcium ions, and precipitation stage                connected to water source inlet for mixing alkaline                aqueous solution containing carbonate anions with                water source, selectively precipitating 1st product                containing calcium cations and carbonate anions in                a 1st precipitation step (8a) at a 1st pH, and                selectively precipitating a 2nd product containing                magnesium cations and carbonate anions in a 2nd                precipitation step (8b) at a 2nd, higher pH;                and
   (2) oil/gas field comprising the system, where                water source is provided from produced water                separation and treatment plant connected to an                oil/gas well.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                mineralization method of carbon dioxide.
   Carbon dioxide inlet (1)
   Alkali feed (2)
   Water source inlet (4)
   Absorbing stage (7)
   1st precipitation stage (8a)
   2nd precipitation stage (8b)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Water source is a                  formation/connate brine, produced water brine, or                  another industrial waste brine. Water                  source/brine contains higher concentration of                  calcium ions than magnesium ions. Carbon dioxide                  is derived from an effluent gas from an                  industrial plant. Alkali for forming alkaline                  aqueous solution is selected from sodium                  hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium                  hydroxide, Clinker Kiln Dust (CKD), Lime Sludge                  (LS), or ammonia. The 1st product comprises                  precipitated calcium carbonate (PCC). The 1st                  product is selected from calcite, aragonite, or                  calcium carbonate monohydrate. The 1st product is                  aragonite, and the 1st precipitation step is                  carried out at greater than or equal to 40                  degrees C and a pH of 7-7.5. The 1st product is                  calcite, and the 1st precipitation step is                  carried out at ambient temperature and a pH of                  7-7.5. The 1st product is monohydrocalcite, and                  the 1st precipitation step is carried out at                  ambient temperature and at a pH of 7.5-8. The 2nd                  product is nesquehonite. The 1st product is                  aragonite or calcite, the 2nd product is                  nesquehonite, and the intermediate product is                  monohydrocalcite. Alkali is ammonia, where the                  method further includes ammonia recovery step for                  recovering ammonia from 2nd supernatant liquid                  remaining after the 2nd precipitation step.                  Alkali feed is connected to a supply of gaseous                  or aqueous ammonia. Preferred Processes: The 1st                  precipitation step is carried out at a 1st pH of                  less than or equal to 8.5. The method further                  includes intermediate precipitation step for                  precipitating greater than or equal to 1                  intermediate product containing calcium and/or                  magnesium cations and carbonate anions at a pH                  between the 1st pH and the 2nd pH. The method                  further includes activation of the 2nd product by                  heating. Absorbing stage is adapted to bubble the                  carbon dioxide through the alkaline aqueous                  solution or to spray the alkaline aqueous                  solution into/across a flow path of the carbon                  dioxide. Precipitation stage includes 1st                  precipitation stage for selectively precipitating                  1st product in a 1st precipitation step at a 1st                  pH of less than or equal to 8.5. The 1st                  precipitation stage is adapted to selectively                  precipitate the 1st product at a temperature                  between ambient temperature and 85 degrees C.                  Precipitation stage includes 2nd precipitation                  stage for selectively precipitating 2nd                  (magnesium) product in a 2nd precipitation step                  at a 2nd pH of greater than or equal to 9. The                  2nd precipitation stage is adapted to selectively                  precipitate the 2nd product at less than or equal                  to 40 degrees C. The system further includes                  alkali recycling stage for recycling calcium and                  magnesium ion-depleted 2nd supernatant liquid to                  the precipitation stage or to the absorbing                  stage. Alkali recycling stage is adapted to                  recover ammonia from the 2nd supernatant liquid.                  The system further includes an activation stage                  for activating 2nd product by heating. Preferred                  Conditions: The 2nd precipitation step is carried                  out at a 2nd pH of greater than or equal to 8.5.                  The 2nd precipitation step is carried out at a                  temperature of less than or equal to 25 degrees                  C. Preferred Devices: Carbon dioxide inlet is                  connected to a waste gas feed providing an                  effluent gas from an industrial plant. The system                  further comprises gas treatment stage which has                  carbon dioxide scrubber and/or particulate matter                  (PM) filter and/or thermal heat exchanger. Water                  source inlet is connected to a water source                  comprising formation/connate brine or produced                  water brine. The system further comprises                  produced water treatment stage including greater                  than or equal to 1 de-oiling stage, soluble                  organics removal stage, solids removal stage,                  and/or dissolved gas removal stage. Waste gas                  feed is connected to a power generating plant, a                  cement factory, a steel factory, or an oil                  refinery.

AB    NOVELTY - Dense oil reservoir carbon monoxide drive multi-scale control channeling system, comprises nano carbon dioxide responsive worm-shaped micelle system and microngrade carbon dioxide responsive dispersion glue, where preparation of micron-grade carbon dioxide responsive dispersion glue comprises dispersing silane coupling agent, and modified hydroxylated multi-wall carbon nano-tube in water under the condition of stirring speed of 300-500 revolutions/minutes, 15-25 wt.% acrylamide, 3-8wt.% response monomer, 0.5-0.1 wt.% initiator and 0.010-0.1 wt.% crosslinking agent, introducing nitrogen to the solution until the solution is thick, monitoring solution temperature change using a thermometer, carrying out heat preservation for 2-4 hours to obtain the black gel, granulating black gel, drying, crushing.
   USE - Used as dense oil reservoir carbon monoxide drive multi-scale control channeling system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has strong fluidity control capability, and the micronsized carbon dioxide responsive dispersion glue is introduced with high strength carbon nano-tube, which effectively improves the strength and long-term stability of the dispersion glue, the plugging effect of the crack is obviously enhanced, and the carbon dioxide in the system is replaced, the worm-shaped micelle is restored to spherical micelle, with better response reversibility
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing tight oil reservoir carbon dioxide flooding multiscale controlled channeling system comprises introducing micron-scale carbon dioxide responsive disperse gel to nanoscale carbon dioxide responsive worm-typpe micelle system to form In the initial mixing system, where concentration range of the micron-scale carbon dioxide-responsive dispersant in the initial mixing system is 0.3-0.5%, and stirring initial mixing system until the micron-scale carbon dioixide-responsive dispersant is in the nano-scale carbon dioxide-responsive worm-like micelle, uniformly suspending system in the system.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nanoscale carbon monoxide responsive worm-type micelle system is prepared by cross-linking 30-90 mmol/l carbon monoxide, 30-90mmol/l responsive monomer organic counter ion monomer and in the presence of solvent i.e water. The preparation of nanoscale carbon monoxide responsive worm-type micelle system is: The carbon dioxide responsive monomer is completely dissolved in water to obtain a carbon dioxide responsive monomer solution under the condition of stirring speed of 300-500 revolutions/minutes to the resulting solution was added an organic counter ion monomer in the same amount as the carbon dioxide responsive monomer, stirring at room temperature until uniform to obtain a nano-scale carbon dioxide -responsive worm-like micelle system. The carbon dioixde responsive monomer is N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine, N,N-dimethylbenzylamine, N,N-dimethylbutylamine, tetramethylethylenediamine, trimethylamine, N , N-dimethyloleic acid amidopropyl tertiary amine, diethylenetriamine, cocoyl dimethyl tertiary amine, and/or erucic acid amidopropyl dimethyl tertiary amine. The organic anti-ion monomer is sodium pstyrenesulfonate, sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, sodium p-toluenesulfonate, and/or sodium oleate. The silane coupling agent used for the modification of the hydroxylated multi-walled carbon nanotubes by the silane coupling agent is gamma-methacryloyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane, and/or vinyl tri(beta-methoxyethoxy) vinyl) silane and vinyl triethoxy silane. The responsive monomer is N-methyl-N-vinylformamide, ethylenediaminoethyl methacrylate, and dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate. The initiator is composed of ammonium persulfate, ammonium bisulfite and azobisisobutyramidine hydrochloride in a weight ratio of 1:1:2. The crosslinking agent is N,N-methylenebisacrylamide, phenolic crosslinking agent and/or polyethyleneimine.

PT P
PN CN113634087-A; CN113634087-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide from exit of oil production well in carbon dioxide oil recovery process involves collecting associated gas from exit of oil production well, pressurizing associated gas to performing preliminary processing.
AU ZENG G
   ZHANG R
   ZHANG F
   WANG L
   SONG X
   HOU Y
   CUI S
AE SHANDONG DESHI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   DESHI ENERGY TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG DESHI PETROLEUM EQUIP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D3151C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide from exit of oil production well in carbon dioxide oil recovery process involves collecting associated gas from exit of oil production well, pressurizing associated gas to performing preliminary processing of the associated gas to obtain the first processed gas, contacting the first processing gas with additive to removing hydrogen sulfide in the first processing gas to obtain a second processing gas, subjecting the second processing gas to pressure cooling, and separating to obtain liquid carbon dioxide, where the raw material for the preparation of the additive is prepared by polymerizing N-hydroxyethyl-N-methylbenzylamine, p-carboxystyrene acid, 2-acetamidopropene and N,N-methylenebisacrylamide.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide from exit of oil production well in carbon dioxide oil recovery process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbon dioxide by using additive to selectively adsorb the hydrogen sulfide gas in the first treatment gas can reduce the hydrogen sulfide content in the liquid carbon dioxide and improve the purity of the recovered carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device used for obtaining carbon dioxide from the outlet of an oil production well in the carbon dioxide oil recovery method, which comprises: a first recovery tower, which is provided with a first air inlet and a first air outlet, a first compressor is connected to the first recovery tower, a first refrigeration mechanism is connected to the outside of the first recovery tower, a second recovery tower, which is provided with a second air inlet and a second air outlet, an impurity removal channel is provided in the second recovery tower, the impurity removal channel is used to place the additive, the third recovery tower, which is provided with a third air inlet and a third air outlet, and the third recovery tower is connected with a second compressor and a second refrigeration mechanism.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of additive involves dissolving the N-hydroxyethyl-N-methylbenzylamine, p-carboxystyrene acid and 2-acetamido acrylic acid in the solvent, stirring uniformly, adding concentrated sulfuric acid, heating up to 80-150degrees Celsius, reacting for 2-5 hours to obtain the intermediate solution, cooling the obtained intermediate solution to 40-120degrees Celsius, where the additive also include silica sol and nano-montmorillonite, adding nano-montmorillonite to the intermediate solution, stirring at 80-150degrees Celsius for more than 1 hour, adding N,N-methylenebisacrylamide, catalyst and silica sol, stirring, curing, purifying and drying to obtain additive. Preferred Conditions: The pore diameter of the additive is 1-50 nanometer, the specific surface area is 700-1500 m2/g, and the pore volume is 0.5-4 cm3/g. The weight ratio of the N-hydroxyethyl-N-methylbenzylamine, p-carboxystyrene acid and 2-acetamido acrylic acid is 15:4:1. The weight ratio of the N,N-methylenebisacrylamide and N-hydroxyethyl-N-methylbenzylamine is 3:8. The weight of the nano-montmorillonite is 5-10 wt.%. The weight ratio of the intermediate solution to the silica sol is (20-50):(5-15). The associated gas is collecting from the exit of the oil production well, and pressurize the associated gas to perform preliminary processing of the associated gas to obtain the first processed gas, where the temperature is 10-20degrees Celsius and the pressure is 2.5-4 .0 megapascal. The first processing gas is contacting with additive to remove hydrogen sulfide in the first processing gas to obtain a second processing gas, and subjecting the second processing gas to pressure cooling and separation to obtain liquid carbon dioxide, where the temperature is 32-40degrees Celsius and the pressure is 7.3-7.5 megapascal.

PN DE202021105323-U1
TI Vehicle for transport of people and/or goods, has system for collecting and storing carbon dioxide that is provided with which carbon dioxide produced as by-product from process of converting original fuel, preferably methanol-water mixture or pure methanol, to pure hydrogen is collected and stored.
AU 
AE GUMPERT AUTOMOBILE GMBH (GUMP-Non-standard)
GA 2021D07581
AB    NOVELTY - The Vehicle (1) has an electric traction drive and a fuel cell (7), preferably fed with methanol, for directly or indirectly supplying the drive with electricity. A system (3) for collecting and storing carbon dioxide is provided with which the carbon dioxide produced as a by-product from the process of converting the original fuel, preferably a methanol-water mixture or pure methanol, to pure hydrogen is collected and stored. The system for collecting and storing carbon dioxide is comprised with a storage unit (6) in which carbon dioxide is stored permanently or until the carbon dioxide is actively released again. The system is comprised with a filter (5) upstream of each storage unit.
   USE - Vehicle such as car, van, and truck for transport of people and/or goods. Can also be used as truck, van, construction machinery, watercraft and aircraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The vehicle is operated in closed rooms or in the environments, in which little or no carbon dioxide is released. The vehicle emphasizes environmental considerations and leads to less direct local air pollution during operation, which results in simple, low-maintenance and safe system. The methanol and thus the original fuel are reprocessed, which results in a virtually closed fuel cycle, which leads to a saving in costs and effort, since no additional carbon dioxide has to be procured. The storage unit is removed or replaced without switching off the vehicle or the corresponding fuel cell, since the carbon dioxide is then collected by the other storage unit or units. Several storage units are installed on the vehicle, which contributes to the simplicity and adaptability of the overall system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the vehicle which is equipped with system for collecting and storing carbon dioxide.
   Vehicle (1)
   System for collecting and storing carbon dioxide (3)
   Filter (5)
   Storage unit (6)
   Fuel cell (7)

A unit is for                desorbing carbon dioxide from the sorbent and a                unit for venting the desorbed carbon dioxide                outside of the enclosed environment. The sorbent                comprises glycerol, and optionally further                comprises of water, an amine, an amine-containing                polymer or an ionic liquid.
   USE - Method for operating heating, ventilation, and                air-conditioning (HVAC) system for enclosed                environment used in building i.e. residential                living place and commercial building working space.    ADVANTAGE - The system provides a more uniform, safe level                of carbon dioxide within the building environment                rather than a potentially unhealthy, cyclical and                variable level of the carbon dioxide. The system                reduces both volatile organic compounds (VOC)                levels and pathogen levels within residential and                commercial living spaces, and provides an overall                feeling of air freshness. The carbon dioxide                scrubber is capable of continuously removing carbon                dioxide, the air filter can remove particulates,                and the air purifier, such as an air ionizer, can                remove VOCs, pathogens and other contaminants from                the recirculating air.    101,104Carbon sensors
   102Battery
   103Sprayer
   105Microcontroller
   106Liquid crystal display
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Process: Atmospheric air is                  captured through carbon filter as an inlet, where                  aqueous calcium hydroxide solution is sprayed                  through a sprayer (103). The sprayer is driven                  with the help of a battery (102), where solar                  power is used as a source of energy through solar                  panel. Silica gel is inured to absorb dust                  particles in air. Carbon dioxide in gaseous form                  present in the air reacts with the sprayed                  calcium hydroxide solution to form a byproduct of                  solid calcium carbonate and water. By-product                  water is taken for electrolysis which gives a                  considerably rich amount of oxygen which                  discharges through an outlet. Liquid crystal                  display (106) shows the incoming amount of carbon                  before reaction, the amount of carbon reduced,                  and amount of produced oxygen (all measured in                  ppm) through sensors (101,104) which is                  integrated with PIC 16F877A microcontroller                  (105).

   AOYAGI Y

AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 2022508714
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of alkynylsilane (I) involves decarboxylating a silyl alkynoate (II) in the presence of a metal complex.
   USE - Manufacture of alkynylsilane used as functional material, raw material and building block in synthetic chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and easily produces alkynylsilane which can be easily purified without salt byproduct due to production of harmless carbon dioxide, which is easily removable as a byproduct than the existing synthetic method using halosilane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of alkynylsilane of formula (I) involves decarboxylating a silyl alkynoate of formula (II) in the presence of a metal complex.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal complex contains at least one metal atom selected from copper, silver, zinc, nickel and palladium, and at least one ligand chosen from 1,10-phenanthroline, 4,7-diphenyl-1,10-phenanthroline, 1,2-bis(dicyclohexylphosphino)ethane, tricyclohexylphosphine, tricyclopentylphosphine, tri-isopropylphosphine, tri-n-butylphosphine, 1,3-bis(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)imidazole-2-ylidene, bis(diphenylphosphino)methane, 1,2-bis(diphenylphosphino)ethane, 1,3-bis(diphenylphosphino)propane and tris(dimethylamino)phosphine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silyl alkynoate (II) is preferably a silyl alkynoate of formula (III)-(V).R1,R2 = H or 1-20C hydrocarbyl optionally substituted by N, O, Si and halo;n = 1-4;andm = 1-5.
   EXAMPLE - In a glove box with a nitrogen atmosphere, a solution of 43.7 mg (0.2 mmol) trimethylsilyl 3-phenylpropiolic acid in 0.5 mL dimethylformamide (DMF) and 40 mu L DMF solution of 25 mM bis(tricyclohexylphosphine)copper chloride containing 0.5 mol% copper chloride and 1 mol% tricyclohexylphosphine were added to the mixture. The obtained mixture was stirred at 80 degrees C. After 6 hours, the reaction product was diluted with 0.5 mL dichloromethane and purified on a short silica gel column using n-hexane as a eluent. After concentrating, 34.5 mg trimethyl(2-phenylethynyl)silane was obtained as a colorless oil in a high yield of more than 95%.

   ADVANTAGE - The modified polypropylene has good mechanical properties and absorption properties, and does not release volatile organic compound itself, and it is very suitable for automotive interiors.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing modified polypropylene, which involves:
   (A) mixing polypropylene, polytetrafluoroethylene, polyethylene-polypropylene multi-block copolymer and nucleating agent using a twin-screw extruder; and
   (B) using supercritical carbon dioxide foaming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polytetrafluoroethylene is nano polyfluortetraethylene. The olefin polymerization catalyst is selected from the group consisting of a chromium-based olefin polymerization catalyst, a nickel-based olefin polymerization catalyst, a lanthanide-based olefin polymerization catalyst, and an iron-based olefin polymerization catalyst. The nucleating agent is selected from the group consisting of calcium carbonate, calcium citrate, calcium oxalate, calcium pimelate, calcium suberate, mesoporous silica, magnesium oxide, titanium dioxide, dibenzylidene sorbitol, -quinacridone, 2,2'-methylene-bis(4,6-di-n-butylphenol) sodium phosphate, methylene bis(2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl)aluminum phosphate, sodium benzoate, carboxyaluminum p-tert-butylbenzoate, polyvinylcyclohexane, polyvinylcyclopentane, dehydroabietic acid, sodium dehydroabietate, N,N'-dicyclohexyl-2,6-naphthalene diamide. Preferred Process: The sodium-naphthalene modified nano polytetrafluoroethylene comprises is prepared by adding nano polytetrafluoroethylene to a sodium-naphthalene complex tetrahydrofuran solution at 30-50 degrees C under the protection of an inert gas, stirring for 1-5 minutes, filter for solids, washing with 80-90 degrees C hot water and drying at 100-130 degrees C. The polyethylene-polypropylene multi-block copolymer is prepared by using 0.005-0.007 pts. wt. DIBPA, 0.004-0.005 pts. wt. olefin polymerization catalyst, 0.004-0.005 pts. wt. triphenyl by mass spectrometer under inert gas protection. The boron is dissolved in 15-80 pts. wt. toluene in a molar meter, the system is transferred to a closed container, 10-20 pts. wt. ethylene is added to mass spectrometer, and stirred at 22-35 degrees C for 1-5 hours to seal. The inside of the container is filled with propylene gas to a pressure of 2.0-3.0 atm in a closed container, stirred for 10-30 minutes, then reduced to normal pressure, added with 3-10 wt.% methanol solution of hydrogen chloride, stirred for 1-5 hours, filtered, washed with methanol, dried at 60-100 degrees C. The volume of the 3-10 wt.% methanol solution of hydrogen chloride is 0.8-1.2 times the volume of toluene. Preferred Compositions: The nucleating agent is a composite nucleating agent having a mass ratio of calcium pimelate to sodium dehydroabietate of (1-3):1.

PN CN109439623-A
TI Preparing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, by collecting umbilical cord sample, exposing internal gum, performing tissue adherent treatment, stucking adhesive to cell culture dish, and culturing medium.
AU YANG Z
   CHENG Y
   MA J
   HU X
   YU G
   PAN D
AE WEIFANG KANGHUA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WEIF-Non-standard)
GA 2019248588
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, involves collecting umbilical cord sample, removing the umbilical cord epithelial tissue, exposing the internal gum, removing the umbilical cord arteriovenous, preparing gum, performing tissue adherent treatment, stucking the adhesive into the cell culture dish, and culturing the medium in carbon dioxide incubator to obtain umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to perform.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves placing the collected umbilical cord samples in transport liquid, where the transport liquid comprises 5-20 mu g/ml antibacterial peptide, 50-100 mu g/ml gymnema vine, 0.5-10 mg/ml trehalose and phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) as solvent (pH 6.8-7.6). The method involves placing the collected umbilical cord sample in the umbilical cord before the transport liquid, removing the umbilical cord and performing sterile ligation at the end. The method involves removing umbilical cord epithelium by removing the umbilical cord from the clean bench, washing with a washing solution, placing 0.05-2 wt.% type I collagenase and 0.01-0.25% trypsin mixed digestive solution, digesting the mixture at 37 degrees C for 5-30 minutes, and removing umbilical cord. The method involves soaking umbilical cord after digestion in sterile PBS during umbilical cord epithelial tissue, and scraping with sterile cells. The umbilical cord tissue is edematous, congested, damaged, and ligated before the product is made into 1-2 cm fragment. The method involves culturing the cells in carbon dioxide incubator at saturated humidity, 5% carbon dioxide and 37 degrees C for 3-16 hours, rehydrating the mixture on 5-8 ml or 5-10 ml per plate rehydration on the 3-5th day, changing the liquid on the 6th to 10th day, observing the cell cloning under the microscope on the 6th day, if the cell morphology is uniform and the quantity meets the predetermined value, the tissue is discarded, if the number of cells does not meet the predetermined value, it is necessary to continue to change the amount of liquid until the number of cells meets the predetermined value, and then the tissue block is completely replaced, and then the liquid is changed every 2-3 days to reach a certain density to harvest the cells.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-F02B; B04-L05C; B04-N04; D05-A02C; D05-H08B

PN CN109160894-A
TI DL-2-amino-4-methylselenylbutyric acid environment-friendly production process involves taking methyl selenopropionaldehyde, water and hydrocyanic acid, adding catalyst, and performing cyanidation addition reaction.
AU ZHOU R
   PENG Q
   SU Y
   LIAO C
AE LUFENG TIANBAO PHOSPHORUS CHEM CO LTD (LUFE-Non-standard)
GA 2019062671
AB    NOVELTY - A DL-2-amino-4-methylselenylbutyric acid environment-friendly production process comprises taking methyl selenopropionaldehyde, water and hydrocyanic acid, adding catalyst, performing cyanidation addition reaction to obtain DL-2-hydroxy-4-methylselenobutyronitrile, reacting with ammonia, carbon dioxide and water, performing steam stripping, obtaining 5-(2-methylselenoethyl)-hydantoin aqueous solution, adding alkali, hydrolyzing, steam stripping, obtaining DL-2-amino-4-methylselenylbutyrate aqueous solution, acidifying, crystallizing, and purifying.
   USE - DL-2-amino-4-methylselenylbutyric acid environment-friendly production process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has has high product purity and yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The DL-2-amino-4-methylselenylbutyric acid environment-friendly production process comprises taking 1:2.0-3.0:1.01-1.1 molar ratio of methyl selenopropionaldehyde, water and hydrocyanic acid, adding catalyst including organic comprising triethylamine, pyridine or citric acid-sodium citrate, or inorganic alkali, performing cyanidation addition reaction at pH value of 4.5-6.0 at 40-78 degrees C to obtain DL-2-hydroxy-4-methylselenobutyronitrile, reacting with 6-9 wt.% ammonia, 11.6-17.5 wt.% carbon dioxide and water according to mass ratio of 1:2.0-3.0:1.5-2.25 at 80-120 degrees C for 10-30 minutes under 0.2-1.0 MPa, performing steam stripping, obtaining 10-50 wt.% 5-(2-methylselenoethyl)-hydantoin aqueous solution, adding alkali comprising sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide and 200 ppm ammonia water, hydrolyzing at 150-200 degrees C for 10-60 minutes, steam stripping, obtaining 12-18 wt.% DL-2-amino-4-methylselenylbutyrate aqueous solution, acidifying using inorganic acid sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, phosphoric acid, carbonic acid and carbon dioxide, crystallizing, and purifying. The hydrocyanic acid is prepared by taking hydrocyanic acid dehydrochlorinated by sulfuric acid, and naturalizing or oxidizing natural gas or methanol via Angstrom method.

AE UNIV DONGGUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDU-C)
GA 202033969D

   USE - The method is for preparation of hollow nano-calcium carbonate used as carrier particles to coat other components.

Preferred Components: The dryer is filled with desiccant comprising silica gel desiccant and/or molecular sieve desiccant.    YAN D

   UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of carbamate using carbon dioxide,                amine and aryl diazoacetate comprises dissolving                aryl diazoacetate and amine in solvent in                autoclave, adding silver catalyst, introducing                carbon dioxide, reacting mixture by heating and                stirring, cooling reaction mixture, releasing                unreacted carbon dioxide slowly, diluting reaction                solution with ethyl acetate, filtering mixture,                distilling solvent off under vacuum to obtain crude                product, and purifying by column                chromatography.
   USE - Method for synthesizing carbamate using carbon                dioxide, amine and aryl diazoacetate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, low cost raw                materials, high atomic economical efficiency, good                functional suitability, and wide substrate                adaptation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The silver catalyst is                  silver carbonate, silver acetate, silver                  tungstate, silver oxide, silver nitrate, silver                  phosphate, silver chloride, silver fluoride, or                  silver tetrafluoroborate. The pressure of carbon                  dioxide in reaction kettle is 1-6 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The arylazodiacetate is                  of formula (R1-C(=N2)-CO2R2) (I). The amine is of                  formula (R3-NH-R4) (II). TI System for capturing carbon dioxide and            distributing carbon dioxide to facility comprising            photosynthetic organisms, comprises outlet discharging            gas stream containing carbon dioxide, nitrogen and            oxygen from flue gas source.
AU KOLODJI B
AE KOLODJI B (KOLO-Individual)
   BLACK SWAN LLC (BLAC-Non-standard)
GA 2022892635
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an outlet (104) discharging a                gas stream containing carbon dioxide, nitrogen and                oxygen from a flue gas source (100). A cooling                apparatus receives and cools a portion of the gas                stream flow from the gas capturing structure                outlet. A carbon dioxide enrichment membrane                separates the portion of cooled gas stream into a                first permeate stream and a residue stream. A                manometric trap is arranged between the cooling                apparatus and a blower. A distribution array is                connected to a first facility comprising                photosynthetic organisms, where the first stream is                selectively discharged amidst the organisms.
   USE - System for capturing carbon dioxide from a                stationary flue gas generator from the atmosphere,                where the carbon dioxide is used for                bio-sequestration of the gas or for consumption                through photosynthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The application of carbon dioxide to the                plants promotes growth in the plant, such that the                plant has more robust root and shoot systems. In                addition, application of the gas to cultivated                plants increases crop yield per harvest, improves                the size and quantity of fruit, and/or improves the                productivity by allowing earlier harvests or                multiple harvest. The system utilizes the captured                carbon dioxide, as well as the elevated (with                respect to ambient conditions) of humidity and                temperature, for a beneficial purpose. It utilizes                carbon dioxide containment mechanisms such as                sheeting and draping to provide a barrier which                contains the carbon dioxide enriched air adjacent                to the plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                flue gas source and options for diverting a portion                of the flue gas froma flue gas source through the                gas extraction structure.
   Flue gas source (100)
   Venting stack (102)
   Outlet (104)
   Pipe (116)
   Valve (118)

TI Carbon dioxide separation system for separating            carbon dioxide from mixed gas, has negative pressure            generator placed on gas path of permeating gas that            connects separation-membrane downstream chamber and            carbon dioxide collector.
AU SUGIYAMA K
   SUGIYAMAGOUSEKI
   SUGIYAMA H
AE TOYOTA JIDOSHA KK (TOYT-C)
   TOYOTA JIDOSHA KK (TOYT-C)
   TOYOTA JIDOSHA KK (TOYT-C)
   TOYOTA MOTOR CORP (TOYT-C)
   TOYOTA JIDOSHA KK (TOYT-C)
GA 2021B01160
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide separation system comprises a                carbon dioxide separator, which includes a                separation membrane (21) configured to separate                carbon dioxide from mixed gas. The                separation-membrane upstream and downstream                chambers are demarcated by the separation membrane.                The carbon dioxide collector (50) is provided, that                permeating gas contains carbon dioxide, which is                introduced into the collector. The pressure                difference generator (40) is provided to generate a                pressure difference between the chambers such that                a pressure in the upstream chamber (22) is higher                than in the downstream chamber (23). The generator                includes a negative pressure generator arranged on                a gas path of the permeating.
   USE - System for separating carbon dioxide from                mixed gas, such as exhaust gas discharged from                internal combustion engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The CO 2 separation system can efficiently                collect CO 2 separated from mixed gas containing CO                2. The dehumidifier and the negative pressure                generator can be arranged on the gas path of the                permeating gas that connects the                separation-membrane downstream chamber and the CO 2                collector, in order of the heat exchanger and the                pressure difference generator from an upstream side                in a flow direction of the gas. The exhaust gas                switching valve can be configured to switch whether                to introduce the exhaust gas flowing out of the                catalyst device to outside air or to discharge it                to the outside air without introducing it into the                separation membrane upstream chamber, so that CO 2                can be efficiently collected from the mixed gas                discharged from the internal combustion engine                mounted on a moving object or from a stationary                internal combustion vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of system                for separating carbon dioxide from mixed gas from                internal combustion engine.
   Separation membrane (21)
   Upstream chamber (22)
   Downstream chamber (23)
   Generator (40)
   Carbon dioxide collector (50)

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a liquid storage tube that is used to store carbon dioxide liquid. Filling valve is connected to the liquid storage pipe and used to fill the liquid storage tube with liquid carbon dioxide. Vent headshots includes an explosion venting pipeline. The explosion relief pipeline is a Y-shaped structure and includes a first pipeline, a second pipeline, and a third pipeline communicating with each other. One end of the first pipeline is connected with a liquid storage tube. The other end of the first pipeline obliquely connects the first ends of the second pipeline and the third pipeline. The ends of the second pipeline and the third pipeline face a free surface (10) of a roadway to be excavated (6). The ends of the second pipeline and the third pipeline are explosion vents. The explosion vent is used to vent the pressure of carbon dioxide gas. The included angle between the explosion vent and the borehole axis is less than 90 degrees .

TI Cryogenic treatment system for dispensing carbon            dioxide from separate carbon dioxide source for            treatment of patient, has computer controller that            closes valve of carbon dioxide dispensing conduit to            halt flow of carbon dioxide.
AU OREILLY L
AE OREILLY L (OREI-Individual)
GA 201961545U
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has a connector fitting that                is mounted on the housing (20) and adapted for                operably connecting a carbon dioxide dispensing                conduit with a separate carbon dioxide source. A                temperature sensor (52) is operably mounted on the                housing adjacent a dispensing nozzle (36). An                accelerometer is operably mounted within the                housing. A computer controller is operably                connected with the temperature sensor and the                accelerometer for controlling the valve of the                carbon dioxide dispensing conduit responsive to the                temperature sensor and the accelerometer. A range                finder (50) is operably mounted on the housing                adjacent the dispensing nozzle for determining a                distance from the dispensing nozzle to a patient                (12). The computer controller is operably connected                to the range finder, and closes the valve of the                carbon dioxide dispensing conduit to halt flow of                carbon dioxide in an event that the distance from                the dispensing nozzle to the patient is too                short.
   USE - Cryogenic treatment system for dispensing                carbon dioxide from separate carbon dioxide source                for treatment of patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The light, alert, and audible instructions are                obviously used to guide the operator of the system,                and prevent treatment parameters from being                violated. The proper movement of the system by the                operator is helpful in correctly treating the                patient, and the potential injury to the patient is                avoided. The connection enable the hose to be                connected and disconnected quickly and easily, and                preferably without requiring the use of tools and                permanent connections is used.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a                cryogenic treatment system.
   Cryogenic treatment system (10)
   Patient (12)
   Housing (20)

PT P
PN WO2018083076-A1; CA3041192-A1; CN110121386-A; BR112019008894-A2; US2020047117-A1; ID201905886-A; RU2019116687-A; US10888816-B2; RU2748547-C2
TI Removal of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide            from feed gas stream e.g. sour gas stream, involves            converting hydrogen sulfide in feed gas stream to            elemental sulfur, and contacting gas stream portion(s)            with carbon dioxide absorbing medium.
AU ABDOLLAHI F
   JUST P
   SARLIS J N
   JUST P J J
   JUST P E
   SARLIS Y N
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   CANSOLV TECHNOLOGIES INC (CANS-Non-standard)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 201836378A
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide                from a feed gas stream involves converting hydrogen                sulfide in a feed gas stream to elemental sulfur in                a Claus unit to obtain elemental sulfur and a gas                stream comprising reduced amount of hydrogen                sulfide and carbon dioxide, and contacting gas                stream portion(s) with an aqueous lean absorbing                medium comprising amines chosen from e.g.                diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine,                tetraethylenepentamine and                N-(2-hydroxyethyl)piperazine, in an absorption zone                at a pressure of 0.9-2 bara, preferably 0.9-1.5                bara to absorb carbon dioxide.
   USE - Removal of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide                from feed gas stream e.g. sour gas stream from                natural gas wells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, efficiently removes                hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide from feed gas                stream, can be performed with less number of                equipment, and requires less amount of energy. The                method does not require the Claus off-gas to be                pressurized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removal of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide                from a feed gas stream involves (i) converting                hydrogen sulfide in a feed gas stream to elemental                sulfur in a Claus unit to obtain elemental sulfur                and a gas stream comprising a reduced amount of                hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide, and (ii)                contacting portion(s) of the gas stream with an                aqueous lean absorbing medium comprising one or                more amines chosen from diethylenetriamine,                triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine and                N-(2-hydroxyethyl)piperazine, in an absorption zone                at a pressure of 0.9-2 bara, preferably 0.9-1.5                bara to absorb carbon dioxide and to obtain a                carbon dioxide lean treated gas stream and spent                absorbing medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The step (iii) is                  performed in a reboiler, forced circulation                  reboiler, fired reboiler, falling film reboiler,                  direct steam reboiler, or thermosiphon,                  preferably thermosiphon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: At least 70%,                  preferably at least 95% gas stream obtained in                  the step (i) is treated in the single absorption                  unit in the step (ii). The feed gas stream                  comprises up to 25 vol.% carbon dioxide.                  Preferred Components: The gas stream comprises                  hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide and optionally                  carbonyl sulfide. Preferred Process: The gas                  stream is not incinerated between the step (i)                  and step (ii). The step (i) further involves                  removing even more hydrogen sulfide from the gas                  stream by using a solvent comprising amine to                  obtain a gas stream comprising a further reduced                  amount of hydrogen sulfide. The method further                  involves (iii) regenerating the spent absorbing                  medium in a regeneration zone to produce a                  regenerated aqueous absorbing medium and carbon                  dioxide, (iv) recycling portion(s) of the                  regenerated aqueous absorbing medium to the step                  (ii), (v) moving heat stable salts from another                  portion of the regenerated aqueous absorbing                  medium by ion exchange resin, electrodialysis,                  crystallization or thermal reclamation, and (vi)                  recycling portion(s) of the regenerated aqueous                  absorbing medium having a reduced heat stable                  salt content as obtained in the step (v) to step                  (ii).
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN WO2018033217-A1; US2019237835-A1; US11165271-B2
TI Control device for controlling charging of non-aqueous metal air battery, has control device unit for determine concentration and increase rate of concentration in battery, and stopping charging when increase rate exceeds threshold value.
AU BARDE F
   KOMIYAMA K
AE TOYOTA MOTOR EURO (TOYT-C)
   TOYOTA MOTOR EURO (TOYT-C)
GA 201813785H
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a control device unit for determining a carbon dioxide concentration and an increase rate of carbon dioxide concentration in a battery. The control device unit charges the battery in a case both the carbon dioxide concentration before starting charging exceeds predetermined carbon dioxide threshold and the increase rate of the carbon dioxide concentration during the charging is below a predetermined threshold value, and stops the charging when the increase rate exceeds the predetermined threshold value.
   USE - Control device for controlling charging of a non-aqueous metal air battery of a battery pack in a vehicle (all claimed) i.e. hybrid vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The device provides a charging control function allowing an extended and anyway safe use of a metal air battery during lifetime of the battery. The device prevents expected battery degradation as a result the lifetime of the battery can be extended. The device provides a safer charging procedure by monitoring during the charging both the carbon dioxide concentration and the increase rate of carbon dioxide concentration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a battery pack
   (2) a system for charging a battery
   (3) a method for controlling charging of a non-aqueous metal air battery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for controlling charging of a non-aqueous metal air battery.
   Step for measuring concentration of carbon dioxide (S2)
   Step for determining whether measured carbon dioxide concentration exceeds predetermined carbon dioxide concentration threshold (S3)
   Step for starting charging of battery (S4)
   Step for measuring carbon dioxide concentration and capacity of battery at time point (S10)
   Step for stopping charging of battery (S15)

TI Greenhouse carbon dioxide gas discharging device for reducing carbon dioxide recycling system, has cooling medium heat exchanger whose top part is connected with air inlet joint, where top part of air joint is connected to connecting pipe.
AU QIU G
AE NANJING YITAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202241232G
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a regeneration tower (1) whose inner side is provided with a cooling medium heat exchanger (9). A top part of the cooling medium heating exchanger is connected with an air inlet joint (10) and connected with a top part of a connecting pipe (7). An inner side of an electromagnetic valve (8) is provided inside the connecting pipe. A side of a top cover is connected to a top surface of the top cover. A top surface is bolted with a ventilation barrel between the ventilation pipe and the ventilation barrel. The ventilation barrel is filled with filtering particles and a top plate is bolted on the top surface.
   USE - Greenhouse carbon dioxide gas discharging device for reducing carbon dioxide gas recycling system and used for producing food grade, high-purity carbon dioxide gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can realize production of industrial grade carbon dioxide gas, zero emission of regeneration gas consumption, produce food grade, high-purity carbon dioxide gas, renewable gas consumption of 70 percent can be recycled and reused. The energy-saving and environment-friendly device has simple structure and obvious economic benefit. The device has compact structure and convenient installation, and has no need to increase energy consumption (electricity, water, steam and so on) cost using the existing circulating water or cooling medium cooling, reducing the carbon dioxide gas corresponding greenhouse gas emission, improving the carbon dioxide gas recovery rate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of greenhouse carbon dioxide gas discharging device for reducing carbon dioxide gas recycling system.
   Regeneration tower (1)
   Connecting pipe (7)
   Electromagnetic valve (8)
   Cooling medium heat exchanger (9)
   Air inlet joint (10)

PN CN113833542-A
TI Superheated carbon dioxide driven double-turbine power generating device, has main body pressurized by compressor after evaporating carbon dioxide gas by heater for forming compressed gas under high temperature state.
AU WANG X
   GU S
   XUE D
   SUN Z
AE SINO-INDO ALLWIN BEIJING TRADING CO LTD (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 2022103187
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a device main body comprises condensing state carbon dioxide/gasification carbon dioxide, which needs to be subjected to a working medium evaporation by a heater to obtain dry carbon dioxide gas. The main body is pressurized by a compressor (9) after evaporating carbon dioxide gas by a heater for forming compressed gas under a high temperature state. A flywheel device is entered through a gas conveying passage (8). A gas collecting pump, a control valve, a pressure monitoring device and an emergency braking system are arranged between the flywheel generating turbine main body and the gas conveying passage.
   USE - Superheated carbon dioxide driven double-turbine power generating device. Uses include but are not limited to beverage, metallurgy, food, tobacco, petroleum, agriculture, chemical industry and electronic fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The device fully utilizes carbon dioxide energy, and develops a way for industrial carbon dioxide, realizes gasification, pressurizes work by the compressor, finally entering a turbine air pump through a gas pipeline, arranges a turbine with a magnetic pole in a centre of the turbine for cutting a coil, generates current effect, provides an inner turbine disk for driving a wheel disk to rotate, achieves that rotating direction of an outer turbine is opposite to direction of an inner turbine, and utilizes the inner turbine for driving the magnetic pole to rotate under continuous action of a carbon dioxide gas nozzle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a superheated carbon dioxide driven double-turbine power generating device.
   Outer turbine (1)
   Inner turbine (2)
   Magnetic pole (3)
   Outer turbine blade (4)

PT P
PN CN111927429-A; CN111927429-B
TI Detection and early warning device for storing carbon dioxide in oil and gas field exploitation layer, has exhaust pipe which extracts carbon dioxide at required monitoring height, and detects concentration by carbon dioxide sensor.
AU WEI Y
   WANG P
   YU B
   LIU L
   SHEN M
   WANG W
   LIANG Q
   WANG Z
AE BEIJING INST TECHNOLOGY (BEIT-C)
GA 2020B53653
AB    NOVELTY - The device has retracting device and tube length measuring device which are installed at the head of the monitoring well (4). The retracting device is connected with the suction pipe (8), and the end of the suction pipe is connected with the air inlet (40). The monitoring cabinet is installed on the ground (1) next to the monitoring well. The carbon dioxide sensor and temperature sensor are installed on the monitoring cabinet. The retracting device and the tube length measuring device are electrically connected to the monitoring cabinet. The exhaust pipe extracts carbon dioxide at the required monitoring height, and detects the concentration by carbon dioxide sensor. The temperature is detected by the temperature sensor, the concentration and temperature are displayed by the monitoring cabinet, and the monitoring cabinet warns when the threshold is exceeded. When the air inlet reaches the bottom of the monitoring well or the groundwater surface (2), the air inlet is closed.
   USE - Detection and early warning device for storing carbon dioxide in oil and gas field exploitation layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The device monitors the concentration and temperature of carbon dioxide in different depths underground in real time, and give early warning when the carbon dioxide exceeds the concentration standard.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a detection and early warning method for storing carbon dioxide in oil and gas field production layer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the installation position of the detection and early warning device for storing carbon dioxide in oil and gas field exploitation layer.
   Ground (1)
   Groundwater surface (2)
   Monitoring well (4)
   Suction pipe (8)
   Air inlet (40)

PN KR2020037623-A
TI Controlling carbon dioxide integrated air volume system, comprises selecting cooling mode or heating mode based on indoor temperature, and measuring indoor carbon dioxide concentration when indoor temperature reaches set indoor temperature.
AU JOYOUNGHUM
   KIN H J
AE UNIV YEUNGNAM RES COOP FOUND (UYYE-C)
GA 2020297620
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling carbon dioxide integrated air volume system, comprises: selecting cooling mode or a heating mode according to indoor temperature; measuring indoor carbon dioxide concentration when indoor temperature reaches indoor set temperature; and setting ventilation mode when concentration measured by indoor carbon dioxide sensor is greater than set concentration. The air volume system comprises a wind flow amount terminal unit control unit (510) for adjusting the operation of an air flow sensor (410), a supply temperature sensor (420), an air supply damper actuator (453) and a reheat coil valve (460) based on the information received through an indoor temperature sensor (430) and an indoor carbon dioxide sensor (441), and an air conditioner control unit (520) for adjusting operation of exhaust damper actuator, external damper actuator, and supply fan variable frequency control device based on received information.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling carbon dioxide integrated air volume system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: controls the minimum air volume control and indoor carbon dioxide concentration of the air volume terminal unit system in conjunction with the air conditioner; controls the indoor carbon dioxide concentration by increasing only the air volume of the terminal unit of the air volume of the zone where the indoor carbon dioxide concentration is higher than the standard; can reduce cooling and heating energy and air conditioning energy by increasing the indoor ventilation air volume before increasing the outside air intake; reduces unnecessary heating and cooling energy and air conditioning energy by avoiding unconditionally introducing fresh outside air; improves indoor air quality and improving comfort; and provides a comfortable environment for the occupants by controlling the indoor carbon dioxide concentration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of an air conditioning system (Drawing includes non-English language text).
DC Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24)); X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)
MC Q74-A02; Q74-T01; Q74-T20; X27-E01B
IP F24F-011/00; F24F-011/65; F24F-110/10; F24F-110/70; F24F-013/10
