PT P
PN EP4032871-A1
TI Chemical-physical treatment and transformation of            solid organic waste and solid biomass by denaturizing            and basic hydrolyzing in aqueous solution of solid            organic waste and/or solid biomass, filtering products            and reducing material pH value.
AU FERULLI M
AE FERULLI M (FERU-Individual)
GA 2022951839
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical-physical treatment and transformation                of solid organic waste and/or solid biomass,                comprises: (a) denaturizing and basic hydrolyzing                in aqueous solution of solid organic waste and/or                solid biomass; (b) pressing the products of (a)                having a high degree of polymerization, and                subsequent drying with the production of an                alkaline biodegradable self-supporting material;                (c) filtering the products of (a) having a low                degree of polymerization, with recovery of an                alkaline organic filtrate and optional drying; (d)                reducing the pH value of the self-supporting                biodegradable alkaline material, by immersion in an                aqueous acid solution, and subsequent drying; (e)                reducing the pH value of the alkaline organic                filtrate in aqueous solution with the carbon                dioxide produced in (d); (f) filtering the reaction                products of (d), with recovery of an acid organic                filtrate; and (g) reacting in aqueous solution of                the alkaline organic filtrate with the acidic                organic filtrate, filtering and drying.
   USE - The process is useful for chemical-physical                treatment and transformation of solid organic waste                and/or solid biomass, with production of organic                fertilizer and biodegradable self-supporting                material in agronomic fields which is association                with or not with the organic fertilizer by laying                it on the surface of agricultural soil, and as a                cultivation substrate for soilless crops (all                claimed). No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: utilizes very short treatment                times and does not need heat sources with impacting                carbon dioxide emissions; itself does not emit                carbon dioxide, it totally recycles the water and                leads to a structurally simple and economical                treatment and transformation plant, this process                has the advantage of not emitting odors and of                using natural, renewable reagents, allowed in                organic farming, with a neutral carbon footprint                and which are easily manageable in terms of safety                for health and the environment; obtains two end                products free of pathogenic microorganisms, free of                phytotoxicity and free from vectors and/or viable                seeds derived from solid organic waste and/or                treated solid biomass, and these products are an                organic chelate of potassium fertilizer and a                biodegradable self-supporting material, both                innovative for their composition, structure and                use; provides organic fertilizer and biodegradable                self-supporting material which protects the soil                and restores its fertility at each crop succession,                this use allows to vary or not, according to the                different agronomic needs, the pH value of the                circulating solution, the carbon to nitrogen ratio                of the soil, to maximize the nitrogen fixing                process of the bacteria naturally present in the                soil with dedicated crops in succession and in the                at the same time increase the strength of the soil                (soil depth); is chemical-physical process with                zero carbon dioxide emissions for the treatment and                transformation of solid organic waste and/or solid                biomass, and the agronomic use of the fertilizing                organic potassium chelate and the biodegradable                self-supporting material, are functionally                connected in a system of capture and storage of                carbon dioxide in the soil with significant                technical, economic and environmental efficiencies;                provides biodegradable self-supporting material in                the pre-sown version in the agronomic field which                allows to maximize agricultural productions also                with organic farming methods, bringing them on a                par with the most recent soilless cultivation                techniques, but with the advantage of is                significantly cheaper, manageable and even more                sustainable from an environmental point of view;                utilizes low operating temperatures equal to room                temperature (25 degrees C); provides biodegradable                self-supporting material which has absence of                synthetic chemical compounds, glues, binders or                structural aids and allows high density of                cultivation plant and maximum yields of                agricultural production even with the organic                farming method; and is easy realization of plant                consociations and crop changes, minimum irrigated                and plant protection agronomic interventions,                absence of mechanical processing agricultural land                and crop-making mechanical care with considerable                economic savings, regeneration of agricultural soil                fertility by promoting the creation of habitats for                microorganisms, useful insects and animals,                significant organic substance input in agricultural                soils with carbon accumulation in the form of                humus, increased power of agricultural soils,                maximized natural nitrogen-fixing process in                agricultural soil, and creation of high natural                value agricultural areas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) organic fertilizer obtainable by the                process, comprising hydrolysis and denaturation                products having a low degree of polymerization,                where it is an organic potassium chelate with                different known pH values and different known                carbon to nitrogen ratio values, composed of an                organic component, an organic component with                potassium chelate, organic carbonates and traces of                potassium carbonate and/or potassium bicarbonate                and/or citric acid; and
   (2) biodegradable self-supporting material                obtainable by the process, comprising denatured and                hydrolyzed organic structures having a high degree                of polymerization, different known pH values and                different known carbon to nitrogen ratio                values.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The steps (a), (b), and                  (c) may alternatively not be followed by steps                  (d), (e), (f), and (g). The step (a) provides for                  denaturation and basic hydrolysis the use of                  potassium carbonate in aqueous solution having a                  pH value of greater than 11 and in the absence of                  oxygen for a period of time of 10-20 days at room                  temperature of 15-35 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (d) provides for                  the reduction of the pH value the use of citric                  acid in aqueous solution with a pH value of lower                  than 1.8.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Application of biodegradable self-supporting                material is sowing, mulching and fertilization,                preferably for mulching and fertilization                (claimed). No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of physical                characterization of the biomass that do not support                the claims.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. TI Treating off-gas and water effluent streams in a            naphtha complex comprises e.g. NHT stripper off-gas            stream from a NHT stripper column, 5-6C isomerization            stabilizer off-gas stream from a 5-6C isomerization            stabilizer column.

AB    NOVELTY - Treating off-gas and water effluent streams in                a naphtha complex comprising: thermally oxidizing                at least one of a naphtha hydrotreater (NHT)                stripper off-gas stream (125) from a NHT stripper                column (120), a 5-6C isomerization stabilizer                off-gas stream (205) from a 5-6C isomerization                stabilizer column (200), a regenerator off-gas                stream (270) from a continuous catalyst                regeneration zone of a reforming zone, a 4C                isomerization stabilizer off gas stream from a 4C                isomerization stabilizer column, and a NHT sour                water stream from a NHT product separator in a                thermal oxidation system, comprises e.g. thermally                oxidizing the e.g. at least one of the NHT stripper                off-gas stream; optionally recovering waste heat                from the flue gas in a waste heat recovery section;                removing at least one of sulfur oxides, hydrogen                chloride, and chlorine from the flue gas in a                sulfur oxides removal section to form a de-sulfur                oxides outlet flue gas; and contacting a caustic                solution or an ammonia based solution.
   USE - The process is useful for treating off-gas and                water effluent streams in a naphtha complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The process can significantly lower capital                and operating costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating off-gas and water effluent streams in                a naphtha complex comprising: thermally oxidizing                at least one of a naphtha hydrotreater (NHT)                stripper off-gas stream (125) from a NHT stripper                column (120), a 5-6C isomerization stabilizer                off-gas stream (205) from a 5-6C isomerization                stabilizer column (200), a regenerator off-gas                stream (270) from a continuous catalyst                regeneration zone of a reforming zone, a 4C                isomerization stabilizer off gas stream from a 4C                isomerization stabilizer column, and a NHT sour                water stream from a NHT product separator in a                thermal oxidation system, comprises thermally                oxidizing the at least one of the NHT stripper                off-gas stream, the 5-6C isomerization stabilizer                off-gas stream, the regenerator off-gas stream, the                4C isomerization stabilizer off-gas stream, and the                NHT sour water stream in a thermal oxidizing                section forming flue gas consisting essentially of                at least one of water, carbon dioxide, nitrogen,                oxygen, sulfur oxides, Nitric oxides, hydrogen                chloride, chlorine, dioxins, and furans; optionally                recovering waste heat from the flue gas in a waste                heat recovery section; removing at least one of                sulfur oxides, hydrogen chloride, and chlorine from                the flue gas in a sulfur oxides removal section to                form a de-sulfur oxides outlet flue gas consisting                essentially of at least one of water, carbon                dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen, Nitric oxides, dioxins,                and furans, where removing the at least one of                sulfur oxides, hydrogen chloride, and chlorine from                the flue gas comprises quenching the flue gas in a                quench section to form quenched flue gas after                recovering the waste heat; and contacting a caustic                solution or an ammonia based solution with the                quenched flue gas in scrubbing section to form the                de-sulfur oxides outlet flue gas and a liquid                stream comprising at least one of water, sodium                sulfite, sodium sulfate, sodium bisulfite, sodium                carbonate, sodium chloride, ammonium sulfate, and                ammonium chloride; or reacting the flue gas with a                reactant in an sulfur oxides reaction section to                form a reaction section flue gas consisting                essentially of at least one of water, carbon                dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen, sodium chloride, sodium                carbonate, sodium sulfate, sodium nitrate, calcium                chloride, calcium sulfate, calcium carbonate,                calcium nitrate, magnesium chloride, magnesium                carbonate, magnesium sulfate, magnesium nitrate,                chlorine, nitric oxides, dioxins, and furans, where                the reactant comprises at least one of sodium                bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate-sodium                carbonate-dihydrate, calcium carbonate, calcium                hydroxide, and magnesium hydroxide; and filtering                the reaction section flue gas in a filtration                section to remove sodium chloride, sodium                carbonate, sodium sulfate, sodium nitrate, calcium                chloride, calcium sulfate, calcium carbonate,                calcium nitrate, magnesium chloride, magnesium                carbonate, magnesium sulfate, and magnesium nitrate                to form the de-sulfur oxides outlet flue gas; and                optionally removing nitric oxides from the                de-sulfur oxides outlet flue gas in an nitric                oxides removal comprises a de-nitric oxides outlet                flue gas consisting essentially of at least one of                water, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen, dioxins,                and furans; and optionally removing dioxin, furan,                or both from the de-sulfur oxides outlet flue gas                or the de-nitric oxides outlet flue gas in a                dioxin-furan removal section to form a treated                outlet flue gas consisting essentially of at least                one of water, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen,                hydrogen chloride, and chlorine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows the schematic representation                of naphtha complex with thermal oxidation                system.

PT P
PN CN113351674-A; CN113351674-B
TI Recycling aluminum profile mold waste liquid involves using stirring multi-layer filter device, using first liquid pump to driving waste liquid generated in mold alkali washing tank into reaction layer by pipeline.
AU LI Q
AE GUANGDONG XINHE ALUMINUM IND XINXING CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A7794N
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling aluminum profile mold waste liquid involves using stirring multi-layer filter device, using first liquid pump (8) to driving the waste liquid generated in the mold alkali washing tank into reaction layer (1) by pipeline, passing in carbon dioxide gas, contacting fully, reacting the liquid inside the reaction layer with carbon dioxide gas by stirring mechanism (7) to producing sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, aluminum hydroxide solid and sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate mixture after fully reacting, opening the solenoid valve (31) to collecting the sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate mixture inside the reaction layer into the collection layer (3), leaving sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, and aluminum hydroxide solid in the reaction layer, sending out, collecting by the collection mechanism (5), transporting the mixture of sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate inside the collection layer into the reaction tank (10) by the second liquid pump (9).
   USE - Method for recycling aluminum profile mold waste liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has improve the filtering effect, and improves the recycling of the sodium ion, accelerates the process of the reaction by the stirring mechanism, and makes the reaction more sufficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling aluminum profile mold waste liquid involves using stirring multi-layer filter device, using the first liquid pump (8) to driving the waste liquid generated in the mold alkali washing tank into the reaction layer (1) by pipeline, passing in carbon dioxide gas, contacting fully, reacting the liquid inside the reaction layer with carbon dioxide gas by stirring mechanism (7) to producing sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, aluminum hydroxide solid and sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate mixture after fully reacting, opening the solenoid valve (31) to collecting the sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate mixture inside the reaction layer into the collection layer (3), leaving sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, and aluminum hydroxide solid in the reaction layer, sending out, collecting by the collection mechanism (5), transporting the mixture of sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate inside the collection layer into the reaction tank (10) by the second liquid pump (9), putting calcium hydroxide inside, reacting fully to obtain sodium hydroxide solution and calcium carbonate solid, separating solid and liquid inside the reaction box and using the sodium hydroxide solution used for the next alkaline washing of aluminum profile mold, The top of the reaction layer is provided with a top plate (2), and the bottom of the reaction layer is fixed with a collecting layer, one side of the collecting layer is provided with a reaction box and the bottom of the collecting layer and the reaction box are fixed with four supporting legs (4), one side of the reaction layer is close to the collecting layer is provided with feeding material collecting mechanism. The bottom of the top plate and the reaction layer is provided with a stirring mechanism for accelerating the mixing reaction of the mixed liquid and carbon dioxide. The outer wall of the reaction layer is provided with a lifting mechanism (6) capable of driving the top plate to vertically lift. The top portion of the reaction box is provided with a spreading box (15). The outer wall of the reaction box is provided with a translation mechanism (11) capable of driving the spreading box to move along the horizontal direction. The stirring mechanism (7) comprises a first motor (71), a gear set (72), first round of blessing frame (73), a fixing sleeve (74), stirring roller (75) and the second round of blessing frame (76). The top plate is fixedly mounted with a first motor, and the first motor output end is rotatably inserted into the top plate and is fixed with a driving shaft. The top of the collecting layer corresponding to the bottom of the driving shaft is fixed with an inserting cylinder (32),and the bottom of the driving shaft is glidingly inserted with the inserting cylinder. The surface of one side of the driving shaft close to the top plate is in transmission connection with a gear set (72). The surface of the driving shaft located at the bottom of the gear set is rotatably sleeved with first round first round and the surface of the driving shaft located at the bottom of the first round of blessing frame is fixed with a second round of blessing frame. The bottom of the gear group is connected with four stirring roller. The stirring roller (75) is fixed with a fixing sleeve. The stirring rod is rotatably connected with the fixing sleeve.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a recycling aluminum profile mold waste liquid by stirring multi-layer filter device.
   Reaction layer (1)
   Collection layer (3)
   Collection mechanism (5)
   Stirring mechanism (7)
   First liquid pump (8)
   Second liquid pump (9)
   Reaction tank (10)

PN CN113759704-A
TI Automatic control system useful for collecting purity of carbon dioxide by pressure swing adsorption in heat-engine plant, has upper and lower limiting modules whose output ends are set with adsorption time control end of heat-engine plant carbon dioxide pressure swing adsorption trapping system.
AU LIU J
   HU F
   ZHAO H
AE BEIJING BEZENITH ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202203552U
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide purity detection module whose output end is connected with an input end of a proportional-integral-derivative (PID) module (4). An output end of the PID module is connected to an input terminal of an adder (5). An input terminal and an output terminal of a smoke flow input terminal are connected to the carbon dioxide content input terminal. The output terminals of the adder and the upper- and lower limit modules (6) are connected with the adsorption time control end of carbon dioxide pressure swing adsorption system of the heat-engine plant. An output end of the upper and lower limiting module is connected with the adsorption time control end of the heat-engine plant carbon dioxide pressure swing adsorption trapping system.
   USE - The system is useful for collecting purity of carbon dioxide by pressure swing adsorption in a heat-engine plant for thermal power generation process in a thermal power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: improves the stability of product carbon dioxide purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an automatic control method of pressure swing adsorption capturing carbon dioxide purity in heat-engine plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a principle diagram of the automatic control system for collecting purity of carbon dioxide by pressure swing adsorption in heat-engine plant (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN DE102019003798-A1
TI Introducing growth-promoting carbon dioxide for producing biomass, e.g. algae cultures in aqueous solution in photobioreactors, comprises introducing pressure-controlled carbon dioxide gas into silicone rubber hose and dimensioning.
AU AXMANN J
   MODDEMANN H
AE AXMANN J (AXMA-Individual)
GA 2020C0400U
AB    NOVELTY - Introducing growth-promoting carbon dioxide for producing biomass, such as algae cultures in aqueous solution in photobioreactors, comprises introducing pressure-controlled carbon dioxide gas from a separate carbon dioxide gas source into a silicone rubber hose immersed in the liquid in the reaction chamber at a delivery pressure of 0.5-1.5 bar, preferably 2.0 bar such that the gas flow is conveyed in pressure intervals. The carbon dioxide gas diffuses through the hose wall of the silicone rubber hose and emerges on the outside of the hose in the form of nano gas bubbles in the order of 1-100 nm. The absorbing liquid loaded with bioculture flows around the rubber hose in countercurrent to the carbon dioxide gas flow and picks up the nano gas bubbles and that the liquid loaded with nano gas bubbles is acted upon with ultrasound to suppress the formation of microbubbles.
   USE - The method is useful for introducing growth-promoting carbon dioxide for producing biomass, such as algae cultures in a water solution in photobioreactors.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: minimizes carbon dioxide losses when using the nano carbon dioxide module; does not require high pressure technology, thus it is costs and energy efficient; and enables optimal biologically compliant photosynthesis with maximum values for algae growth and a maximum increase in biomass with a growth efficiency of 20-50%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for carrying out the above method.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111659432-A; CN111659432-B
TI Iron-based catalyst useful for carbon dioxide            hydrogenation to ethanol, comprises active sites            comprising iron carbides, and cementite.
AU ZHANG Y
   LU F
   CHEN X
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 202092240F
AB    NOVELTY - Iron-based catalyst comprises active sites                comprising iron carbide (I), iron carbide (II) and                cementite in a mass ratio of                (20-36):(7-12):(4-6).
   USE - The catalyst is useful for carbon dioxide                hydrogenation to ethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has ethanol selectivity of                greater than or equal to 20%, carbon monoxide                selectivity of less than or equal to 10%,                high-efficiency synergistic effect of additive and                active metal iron oxide and improved catalytic                reactivity, and regulates type and proportion of                active metal iron-carbon compound.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Iron-based catalyst comprises active sites                comprising iron carbide of formula: Fe5C2 (I), iron                carbide of formula: Fe2C (II) and cementite in a                mass ratio of (20-36):(7-12):(4-6). INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the iron-based                catalyst involving (a) dissolving additive salt                precursor comprising additive materials in a                solvent to form an additive salt solution, (b)                immersing the additive salt solution on an iron                (II,III) oxide catalyst, stirring and drying to                obtain iron (II,III) oxide solid containing the                additive, and (c) compressing the iron (II,III)                oxide solid containing the additive into tablets,                granulating, sieving, introducing activated gas                under heating to reduce and activate the ratio of                iron-based active sites to obtain the product;                and
   (2) use of the iron-based catalyst for carbon                dioxide hydrogenation to ethanol involving                introducing carbon dioxide/hydrogen atmosphere into                the iron-based catalyst, reacting carbon dioxide                hydrogenation to produce ethanol, where the carbon                dioxide/hydrogen atmosphere comprises                hydrogen/carbon dioxide of 3, hydrogen/carbon                dioxide of 4 (both preferred), hydrogen/carbon                dioxide of 1 and hydrogen/carbon dioxide of                2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  iron carbide (I), iron carbide (II) and cementite                  is 21:12:5. Preferred Components: The additive                  salt comprises (a1) lithium citrate, trisodium                  citrate, tripotassium citrate, potassium nitrate,                  manganese nitrate, magnesium sulfate, and                  manganese acetate, (a2) sodium permanganate and                  copper sulfate, or sodium sulfate and manganese                  sulfate, or (a3) lithium citrate, manganese                  acetate and zirconium nitrate, lithium citrate,                  manganese acetate and zinc nitrate, sodium                  citrate, manganese acetate and magnesium nitrate,                  sodium citrate, manganese acetate and zirconium                  nitrate, sodium citrate, manganese acetate and                  zinc nitrate. The solvent comprises water,                  ethanol, methanol, ethylene glycol, and acetone                  (all preferred), ethylenediamine,                  dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, glycerol,                  1-ethylene glycol and/or acetonitrile. The                  activated gas comprises synthesis gas, hydrogen                  or carbon monoxide. Preferred Properties: The                  granulated and sieved particles have a size of                  20-80 mesh, preferably 20-40 mesh. Preferred                  Method: In the step (c), (c1) when the additive                  salt is chosen from lithium citrate, trisodium                  citrate, tripotassium citrate or manganese                  acetate, the activation is carried out using                  synthesis gas at 280-400 degrees C, preferably                  300-350 degrees C for 6-20 hours, preferably                  10-15 hours, (c2) when the additive salt is                  chosen from potassium nitrate, manganese nitrate                  or magnesium sulfate, the activation is carried                  out using hydrogen at 300-450 degrees C,                  preferably 350-400 degrees C for 6-20 hours,                  preferably 10-15 hours, (c3) when the additive                  salt is selected from sodium permanganate and                  copper sulfate, or sodium sulfate and manganese                  sulfate, the step of activation is carried out                  using carbon monoxide at 300-450 degrees C,                  preferably 320-380 degrees C for 8-20 hours,                  preferably 10-12 hours, and (c4) when the                  additive salt is chosen from the components (a3),                  the step of activation is carried out using                  synthesis gas for 6-20 hours, preferably 10-15                  hours at 280-400 degrees C, preferably 300-350                  degrees C. The use of the catalyst involves                  reacting at 280-400 degrees C, preferably 300-350                  degrees C and a reaction pressure of 1-6 MPa,                  preferably 2-5 MPa.

PN CN111594289-A; CN212614918-U
TI Carbon dioxide Brayton cycle and turbocharged internal combustion engine waste heat utilization system for use in vehicle construction field, has waste heat exchanger for transferring heat to ultra-critical carbon dioxide.
AU LIAO G
   ZHANG F
   E J
   CHEN J
   LENG E
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 202086702S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a turbine provided with an impeller. A waste heat exchanger is connected with an internal combustion engine high temperature exhaust for transferring heat to a ultra-critical carbon dioxide. A main compressor, a recompressor, an air compressor and a motor are driven during the rotation of the turbine, where the supercritical carbon dioxide after work passes through a high temperature re-generator and a low temperature re-generator. The ultra-critical carbon dioxide is divided into a main path circulation and a secondary path circulation in the low-temperature regenerator. The ultra-critical carbon dioxide in the main path passes through a pre-cooler, the main compressor and the high-temperature regenerator in the low-temperature regenerator to flow back to the waste heat exchanger to complete the main path cycle.
   USE - Carbon dioxide Brayton cycle and turbocharged internal combustion engine waste heat utilization system for use in a vehicle construction field.
   ADVANTAGE - The system adopts carbon dioxide as the single working medium of turbocharging and Brayton cycle, and utilizes the stable chemical properties of carbon dioxide, low critical temperature and low compressibility near the critical point, and can recover high temperature waste heat, and improves the comprehensive utilization rate of internal combustion engine fuel, thermal efficiency, and the Brayton cycle and turbocharging can share a turbine for power output.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a carbon dioxide Brayton cycle and turbocharged internal combustion engine waste heat utilization system for use in a vehicle construction field.

TI Manufacturing carbonated concrete composite by            placing concrete precursor in carbonation reactor for            carbonation curing, receiving gas stream comprising            carbon dioxide, and maintaining conditions to react            concrete precursor with gas stream.
AU SIMONETTI D
   SANT G N
   FALZONE G D
   GUPTA R
   MEHDIPOUR I
AE SIMONETTI D (SIMO-Individual)
   SANT G N (SANT-Individual)
   FALZONE G D (FALZ-Individual)
   GUPTA R (GUPT-Individual)
   MEHDIPOUR I (MEHD-Individual)
GA 202293523Y
AB    NOVELTY - A carbonated concrete composite is                manufactured by placing concrete precursor into a                carbonation reactor for carbonation curing, where                concrete precursor comprises binder, one or more                aggregates, and water, and carbonation reactor                having at least one gas stream inlet and outlet;                receiving a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide at                the at least one inlet of the carbonation reactor;                and maintaining a suitable temperature, relative                humidity, carbon dioxide amount, and/or gas stream                flow rate in the carbonation reactor so a                carbonation rate constant of the concrete precursor                is at or above 0.005 to react the concrete                precursor with the gas stream and form the                carbonated concrete composite.
   USE - Manufacture of carbonated concrete composite                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses carbon dioxide gas (e.g.,                carbon dioxide from flue gases, carbon dioxide as                high pressure cylinder gas, etc.) during curing                processes to sequester carbon dioxide via carbonate                mineral formation in concrete composites. The                resulting carbonated concrete composite has                substantially similar engineering properties to a                traditional concrete composite made with cement                that hardens via hydration. Carbon dioxide                mineralization and utilization may advantageously                decarbonize cement production by creating a                waste-to-value or carbon-to-value economy (e.g., by                valorizing waste carbon dioxide borne in flue gases                and alkaline solid wastes, such as fly ashes),                reducing the costs and liabilities associated with                waste management, and promoting the principles of                circular economy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) carbon dioxide mineralization system;                and
   (2) manufacture of first carbonated concrete                composite and second carbonated concrete                composite.

PN CN113457381-A
TI Energy-saving chimney exhaust gas collecting and recycling carbon dioxide process, involves pre-treating flue gas, carbon dioxide absorption, barren rich liquor exchange and carbon dioxide recycling, and reducing regeneration gas content.
AU WANG Q
AE WANG Q (WANG-Individual)
GA 2021B7609B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pre-treating flue gas, introducing high-temperature flue air from the lower portion of the absorption tower into the absorbent tower, and praying the cooling water temperature carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide absorption liquid to form a rich liquid. The rich liquid is pumped from the rich solution pump, and the gas exchange heat is entered from the regeneration gas condenser and the top of the regeneration tower after pressurizing. The first-stage heat recovery is performed. The rich liquor is heated and entered into the heat recovery heater. The top spray head of the top nozzle of the regenerative tower is sprayed to the tower, and the carbon dioxide is absorbed after separating carbon dioxide on the carbon separator through special de-foaming and metering. The condensed liquid is entered the underground tank and returned to the barren liquid pump inlet.
   USE - Energy-saving chimney exhaust gas collecting and recycling carbon dioxide process for use in waste gas processing and recycling. Can also be used in domestic applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables recycling by multi-stage heat, thus reducing energy consumption, and recycling the regenerated gas, effectively reducing the regeneration gas content, so as to reduce the discharge, improving the recycling carbon dioxide efficiency, and realizing large-scale emission reduction of carbon dioxide in chimney exhaust gas, which is good for the protection of environment.

PN WO2019239009-A1; FI201805531-A; FI128568-B1; EP3802738-A1; US2021261479-A1
TI Formation of hydrocarbons used for e.g. producing fuel, involves supplying carbon dioxide and hydrogen as feed to reactor, forming carbon monoxide from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and forming hydrocarbons from carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
AU REINIKAINEN M
   SIMELL P
   KIHLMAN J
   KAISALO N
AE VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
   VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
   VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
GA 2019A6151H
AB    NOVELTY - Formation of hydrocarbons (3) involves supplying a feed (1) comprising at least carbon dioxide to the reactor (2), comprising two catalysts, which are iron-based catalyst (6) and cobalt-based catalyst (7), are arranged inside the same reactor, feeding hydrogen (4) into the reactor, arranging the feed to flow through the reactor, and to contact with hydrogen and catalysts in the reactor, treating the feed by carrying out two reactions, forming carbon monoxide from the carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and forming hydrocarbons from carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the reactor.
   USE - Formation of hydrocarbons e.g. 5-30C hydrocarbons used in Fischer-Tropsch process, treatment of carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide capture process, reduction of carbon dioxide emission, manufacturing of fuel, methanation process, production of methanol (all claimed), production of olefins and oxygenates.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables easy, simple, economical, and effective manufacture of hydrocarbons, without requiring a separate heating device or cooling device. The temperature inside the reactor can be kept low. Fischer-Tropsch reaction step provides heat for the reaction in which carbon dioxide is converted to carbon monoxide, and can be utilized for RWGS reaction. High exothermicity of the Fischer-Tropsch reaction subjects to mass-transfer limitations and unideal temperature profile during the reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus for forming hydrocarbons, which consists of at least one reactor for supplying the feed, two catalysts, and a feeding device for feeding hydrogen into the reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of formation of hydrocarbons.
   Feed (1)
   Reactor (2)
   Hydrocarbons (3)
   Hydrogen (4)
   Catalyst (6,7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The reactor is a heat-exchanger-type reactor in which heat is transferred from an exothermic reaction to the endothermic reaction by using the hydrogen, and consists of at least one catalyst zone (6) comprising iron-based catalyst and at least one catalyst zone (7) comprising cobalt-based catalyst. The heat exchanger-type reactor in which a portion of the plates are catalytically coated with iron-based catalyst layer and a portion of the plates are catalytically coated with cobalt-based catalyst layer, in which the plates are partly coated with catalysts. The apparatus further consists of a tube reactor consisting two tubes so that the tube (6) is inside the tube (7) and the catalyst layer (6), is arranged on the outer surface of the tube (6) and the catalyst (7), is on the inner surface of the tube (7). The reactor comprises one tube, catalyst (6), is arranged as inserts inside the tube and the catalyst (7), are on an inner surface of the tube. Preferred Process: The two reaction steps are reverse-water gas shift reaction (RWGS) and Fischer-Tropsch reaction in reactor, in which the reaction (6) is reverse-water gas shift reaction is carried out on the iron-based catalyst surface and reaction (7) is Fischer-Tropsch reaction is carried out on the both iron-based catalyst and cobalt-based catalyst. Preferred Condition: The reaction is carried out at 100-500 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalysts are arranged as a coating on a desired substrate to form a catalyst surface.

PN WO2018003323-A1; TW201808445-A; CA3024294-A1; CN109414677-A; JP2018524948-X; EP3479897-A1; US2019201832-A1; EP3479897-A4
TI Manufacture of adsorption agent used in device e.g. air conditioner for removing carbon dioxide from process target gas, involves reacting mixture containing tetravalent cerium, and component e.g. urea or its derivative and amide compound.
AU AOSHIMA M
   SHIMAZAKI T
   NAKAMURA H
   YOSHIKAWA K
   KANEEDA M
   MASAHIRO A
   TOSHIKATSU S
   HIDEHIRO N
   KOUHEI Y
   MASATO K
AE HITACHI CHEM CO LTD (HITB-C)
   HITACHI KASEI POLYMER CO LTD (HITB-C)
   HITACHI CHEM CO LTD (HITB-C)
   HITACHI CHEM CO LTD (HITB-C)
GA 2018011913
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of adsorption agent (80) involves reacting a mixture containing tetravalent cerium, and at least one component chosen from urea or its derivative and amide compound.
   USE - Manufacture of adsorption agent used in device e.g. air conditioner for removing carbon dioxide from process target gas containing carbon dioxide (all claimed) emitted from thermal power station, iron mill, vehicle, motor vehicle, space station, midget submarine, and manufacturing plant of foodstuff and chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides adsorption agent having excellent carbon dioxide adsorptivity, and trapping property of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) adsorption agent;
   (2) method for removing carbon dioxide, which involves contacting the adsorption agent with process target gas containing carbon dioxide, to adsorb carbon dioxide on the adsorbent;
   (3) carbon dioxide-removal device, which comprises the adsorption agent; and
   (4) air conditioner (100), which comprises a flow path (10) connected to air-conditioning target space (R) containing the process target gas, a removal portion (10c) for removing carbon dioxide in the process target gas, arranged in the flow path, and the adsorption agent arranged in the removal portion. The air conditioner is used for air-conditioning target space containing the process target gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of air conditioner.
   Flow path (10)
   Removal portion (10c)
   Adsorption agent (80)
   Air conditioner (100)
   Air-conditioning target space (R)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The tetravalent cerium is at least one chosen from cerium(IV) hydroxide, cerium(IV) ammonium nitrate, and cerium(IV) ammonium sulfate. The mixture further comprises a rare earth element other than cerium. The adsorption agent comprises a structure derived from the reaction mixture. Preferred Composition: The concentration of carbon dioxide in the process target gas, is 5000 ppm or less, preferably 1000 ppm or less. Preferred Process: The method further involves oxidizing trivalent cerium to obtain tetravalent cerium, and mixing the mixture with a base. The reacting step is carried out by maintaining the mixture at 25 degrees C or more.

PT P
PN CN110579079-A; CN110579079-B
TI Method for liquefying natural gas, involves            preparing raw material gas, and subjecting mixed gas to            sulfur-resistant water vapor transformation, followed            by subjecting separated hydrogen to heavy oil            hydrogenation.
AU DI F
   XU H
   LIU J
AE DI F (DIFF-Individual)
   GUANGDONG GUANGWU ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202000065D
AB    NOVELTY - A natural gas liquefying method involves                preparing raw material gas by passing coke oven gas                and coal-made gas into a gas cabinet to be fully                mixed to obtain a mixed gas. The 5-50 vol.% mixed                gas is subjected to sulfur-resistant water vapor                transformation or carbon dioxide reforming and                mixed with the remaining mixed gas to obtain crude                feed gas that meets the needs of methanation. The                crude feed gas is compressed and purified, hydrogen                sulfide and carbon dioxide are removed, and the                separated carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide are                returned to purification. The separated hydrogen is                subjected to heavy oil hydrogenation to produce                hydrogenated petrol blending oil or hydrogenated                diesel blending oil, and methane is obtained to                obtain qualified natural gas products after                dehydration compression. The natural gas product is                subjected to low-pressure treatment to form                low-pressure natural gas, and the low-pressure                natural gas is passed to a liquefaction device for                liquefaction treatment.
   USE - Method for liquefying natural gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A natural gas liquefying method involves                preparing raw material gas by passing coke oven gas                and coal-made gas into a gas cabinet to be fully                mixed to obtain a mixed gas. The 5-50 vol.% mixed                gas is subjected to sulfur-resistant water vapor                transformation or carbon dioxide reforming and                mixed with the remaining mixed gas to obtain crude                feed gas that meets the needs of methanation. The                crude feed gas is compressed and purified, hydrogen                sulfide and carbon dioxide are removed, the removed                hydrogen sulfide is desulfurized and recovered, the                removed carbon dioxide gas is vented, and the                purified raw material gas is methanized, and the                purge gas is separated by pressure swing                adsorption, and the separated carbon monoxide and                carbon dioxide are returned to purification. The                separated hydrogen is subjected to heavy oil                hydrogenation to produce hydrogenated petrol                blending oil or hydrogenated diesel blending oil,                and methane is obtained after methanation to obtain                qualified natural gas products after dehydration                compression. The natural gas product is subjected                to low-pressure treatment to form low-pressure                natural gas, and the low-pressure natural gas is                passed to a liquefaction device for liquefaction                treatment, where the liquefaction device includes a                thermal insulation structure, a cold medium                transport structure, a natural gas transport                structure, a liquid-gas separation structure, a                refrigerant fixedly installed inside the thermal                insulation structure, the cold medium transport                structure and natural gas conveying structure are                fixedly installed on the inner wall of the cold                medium conveying structure, the liquid-gas                separation structure is fixedly installed between                the natural gas conveying structure and the cold                medium conveying structure and liquid-gas                separation structure, the thermal insulation                structure includes a protective shell, a cover, and                a bottom plate, an upper cover of the protective                shell is fixed with a cover, an inspection opening                is provided in the middle of the casing cover, a                sealing cover is fixed on the flange of the                inspection opening and a screw on the lower side of                the casing is fixed at a bottom plate, a gas                transmission pipe and an evacuation pipe are                embedded in the lower outer wall of the protective                shell, and a check valve is installed on the                evacuation pipe, a regulating valve is installed on                the gas pipeline, multiple connection plates are                welded on the inner wall of the casing.

PT P
PN EP3564283-A1; WO2019211200-A1
TI Producing fine and closed-cell polyurethane or polyisocyanurate hard foam comprises e.g. mixing isocyanate-reactive polyol formulation with supercritical carbon dioxide, mixing formulation with polyisocyanate, reducing pressure inside mold.
AU VOR D E L
   HEINZ P
   HAHN C
   STREY R
   FRIEDRICH W
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 201993403N
AB    NOVELTY - Producing fine and closed-cell polyurethane or polyisocyanurate hard foam with an open cell content of less than 10% according to DIN EN ISO 4590-2003 and a mean cell size of less than 30 mu m, determined by cell size evaluation using a light microscope, comprises: (i) mixing an isocyanate-reactive polyol formulation with supercritical carbon dioxide, (ii) mixing the carbon dioxide blended polyol formulation with an organic polyisocyanate in a high pressure mixing head, (iii) introducing the mixture into a closed mold, where the closed mold is arranged such that internal volume of closed mold and/or the pressure prevailing in interior of closed mold are changed after introducing the mixture by external influence, (iv) holding the mixture in the closed mold for a predetermined period of greater than or equal to 0 seconds, (v) increasing internal volume of the closed mold and/or reducing the pressure prevailing inside the closed mold by external influence, and (vi) removing PUR or PIR hard foam.
   USE - The fine and closed-cell polyurethane or polyisocyanurate hard foam is useful as an insulating material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fine and closed-cell polyurethane or polyisocyanurate hard foam: has reduced amount of microemulsion-forming surfactants; exhibits increased compressive strength; and is cost efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing fine and closed-cell polyurethane or polyisocyanurate hard foam (PUR or PIR hard foam) with an open cell content of less than 10% according to DIN EN ISO 4590-2003 and a mean cell size of less than 30 mu m, determined by cell size evaluation using a light microscope, comprises: (i) mixing an isocyanate-reactive polyol formulation with supercritical carbon dioxide, (ii) mixing the carbon dioxide blended polyol formulation with an organic polyisocyanate in a high pressure mixing head, (iii) introducing the mixture into a closed mold, where the closed mold is arranged such that internal volume of closed mold and/or the pressure prevailing in interior of closed mold are changed after introducing the mixture by external influence, (iv) holding the mixture in the closed mold for a predetermined period of greater than or equal to 0 seconds, (v) increasing the internal volume of the closed mold and/or reducing the pressure prevailing inside the closed mold by external influence, and (vi) removing the PUR or PIR hard foam. The polyurethane reactive mixture comprises (a) at least one organic polyisocyanate, (b) a polyol component comprising (b1) at least one polyol, where at least one polyol component has a functionality of more than 2.5 which comprises polyether polyol, polyester polyol, polycarbonate polyol, polyether polycarbonate polyol and polyetherester polyol, (b2) a catalyst component, (b3) optionally auxiliaries and additives, (b4) optionally blowing agents except carbon dioxide comprising linear, branched or cyclic 1-6 alkanes, linear, branched or cyclic 1-6 fluoroalkanes, nitrogen, oxygen and/or argon and (c) supercritical carbon dioxide used as blowing agent, which contains no microemulsion stabilizing surfactant. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for fine and closed-cell polyurethane or polyisocyanurate hard foam with an open cell content of less than 10% according to DIN EN ISO 4590-2003 and a mean cell size of less than 30 mu m, determined by cell size evaluation on a light microscope, which is available from a polyurethane reaction mixture comprising (a) at least one organic polyisocyanate, and (b) a polyol component comprising (b1) at least one polyols.
TF EXAMPLE - 16 pts. wt. Supercritical carbon dioxide was added to 13.21 pts. wt. propylene oxide based polyol via an injection valve and a subsequent static mixer in front of the mixing head. Then polymeric methylene diphenyl diisocyanate with isocyanate content of 31.4 wt.% was added into the mixing head. The mixture was injected into a reaction space biased with nitrogen and a floating seal. After the viscosity had been built up, the polyurethane reaction mixture was expanded by depressurizing the nitrogen, and after 20 minutes the foam was removed from the mold. Then fine and closed-cell polyurethane or polyisocyanurate hard foam was obtained.
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.)

PN CN108998376-A; CN108998376-B
TI Dynamic cell culture device based on dielectric elastomer driver comprises e.g. container includes container body, cover body, high voltage power supply positive pole interface, and temperature control mechanism.
AU SONG J
   LI Z
   GU G
   GAO C
   ZOU J
   CHEN F
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 2018A3554X
AB    NOVELTY - Dynamic cell culture device based on dielectric elastomer driver comprises e.g. a container, a support module and a temperature control mechanism. The container comprises a container body, a cover body, a high voltage power supply positive pole interface, a high voltage power supply cathode interface and a carbon dioxide input interface. The cover body is openly fixed to the top of the container body. The high voltage power supply positive pole interface, the high voltage power supply cathode interface fixed to on the outer surface of the container. The carbon dioxide input interface is electrically connected to an inner cavity of the container main body, and a bottom of the inner cavity forms a groove. The support module is suspended and fixed in the inner cavity. The support module comprises a support main body and a dielectric elastomer thin film driver mounting structure.
   USE - Used as dynamic cell culture device based on dielectric elastomer driver.
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines standard cell culture environments with dielectric elastomer driver technology, allows stretching of cells during culture, and promotes mechanical stimulation e.g. compression.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dynamic cell culture device based on dielectric elastomer driver comprises a container, a support module and a temperature control mechanism. The container comprises a container body, a cover body ,a high voltage power supply positive pole interface, a high voltage power supply cathode interface and a carbon dioxide input interface. The cover body is openly fixed to the top of the container body. The high voltage power supply positive pole interface, the high voltage power supply cathode interface fixed to on the outer surface of the container. The carbon dioxide input interface is electrically connected to an inner cavity of the container main body, and a bottom of the inner cavity forms a groove. The support module is suspended and fixed in the inner cavity. The support module comprises a support main body and a dielectric elastomer thin film driver mounting structure. The dielectric elastomer film driver mounting structure comprises a first electrode element and a second electrode element. The first electrode element is electrically connected to the high voltage power supply positive pole interface. The second electrode element electrically connected to the high voltage power supply cathode interface. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for cell culture method of device, comprising (i) placing the device on a horizontal plane; (ii) adding water to the water tank and making the water level lower than the bottom surface of the heating sheet placing layer; (iii) passing carbon dioxide gas with a concentration of 5 vol.% into the inner cavity through the carbon dioxide input interface; (iv) pre-running the temperature control mechanism and the carbon dioxide concentration detecting mechanism for a fixed period of time; (v) placing a dielectric elastomer film driver attached to the cells to be cultured in the driver containing groove and sandwiching between the first electrode member and the second electrode member, where the dielectric elastomer film driver is connected to the first electrode member away from an electrode at one end of the cell to be cultured, and dielectric elastomer film driver is in contact with the electrode of one end of the cell to be cultured to contact the second electrode member; and (vi) connecting high voltage power supply to the positive voltage interface of the high voltage power supply and the negative voltage interface of the high voltage power supply, and then applying corresponding voltage stimulation to the cells to be cultured according to actual requirements.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing show a schematic representation of dynamic cell culture device based on dielectric elastomer driver.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device further comprises carbon-dioxide concentration detecting mechanism including at least one carbon-dioxide concentration sensor disposed in the inner cavity.

TI Gas treatment device used in carbon dioxide            recovery apparatus for collecting carbon dioxide, has            gas scrubber which contacts particles-containing gas            and cleaning fluid, gas cooling tower and            cleaning-liquid circulation line.

   INUI M
   KISHIMOTO S
AE MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)

GA 201940513E
AB    NOVELTY - A gas treatment device (100A) has a gas                scrubber (103) having scrubbing component which                carries out parallel current contact of                particles-containing gas (101) and cleaning fluid                (102), gas cooling tower which is provided in gas                flow downstream side of gas scrubber, and carries                out counterflow contact of particles-containing gas                and cooling fluid (105) after washing, gas                communicating path for introducing post-cleaning                gas (101a) cleaned in gas cleaning column (104) to                inside of gas cooling column (107), cleaning-liquid                storage component stores the falling cleaning                liquid, cleaning-liquid circulation line, condensed                water circulation line which makes gas cooling                component (106) circulate through the condensed                water from condensed water storage component,                cooler, demister provided in the gas flow                downstream side of the said gas cooling portion,                and a condensed water transfer line which transfers                condensed water of gas cooling tower to the gas                scrubber.
   USE - Gas treatment device is used in carbon dioxide                recovery apparatus for collecting carbon dioxide                (all claimed) from combustion exhaust gas of boiler                and gas turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The cleaning blasting fumes improves the                impingement efficiency to the liquid bath of the                gas which flows into communicating path by                providing inclination board which suppresses gas                flow to connection opening component at the side of                the gas scrubber of gas communicating path which                connects gas scrubber and gas cooling tower.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A gas treatment device has a gas scrubber                having scrubbing component which carries out                parallel current contact of particles-containing                gas and cleaning fluid, gas cooling tower which is                provided in the gas flow downstream side of the gas                scrubber, and carries out counterflow contact of                particles-containing gas and cooling fluid after                washing, gas communicating path which connects the                gas scrubber and gas cooling tower on the lower                side for introducing post-cleaning gas cleaned in                the gas cleaning column to the inside of the gas                cooling column, cleaning-liquid storage component                stores the falling cleaning liquid, cleaning-liquid                circulation line which makes the top portion side                of gas scrubber circulate through the cleaning                liquid from cleaning-liquid storage component,                condensed water storage component in which the gas                cooling tower stores the condensed water condensed                from the cleaning blasting fumes, condensed water                circulation line which makes gas cooling portion                circulate through the condensed water from                condensed water storage component, cooler which is                interposed by the condensed water circulation line                and cools the condensed water, demister provided in                the gas flow downstream side of the said gas                cooling portion, and a condensed water transfer                line which transfers condensed water of gas cooling                tower to the gas scrubber. The cleaning-liquid                circulation line is provided in the connection                opening portion at the side of the gas scrubber of                the gas communicating path, and has an inclination                board which suppresses a gas flow. The gas                treatment device is capable of contacting                particles-containing gas and cleaning liquid, and                collecting the particles in the                particles-containing gas. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are                included for the following:
   (1) carbon dioxide recovery apparatus, which                has gas treatment device, gas discharge line which                discharges process blasting fumes discharged from                the gas cooling tower, carbon dioxide absorption                tower which contacts carbon dioxide and carbon                dioxide absorption liquid in cooling blasting                fumes, and removes carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide                absorption liquid regeneration tower which                regenerates the rich solution which absorbed carbon                dioxide with the vapor of a reboiler, a rich                solution supply line which is introduced into                carbon dioxide absorption liquid regeneration-tower                side while extracting the rich solution from the                carbon dioxide absorption tower, while extracting                the lean solution by which carbon dioxide                regenerated in the carbon dioxide absorption liquid                regeneration tower is diffused from carbon dioxide                absorption liquid regeneration tower, and lean                solution supply line which recycles carbon dioxide                absorption liquid;
   (2) gas treatment method, which involves                carrying out parallel current contact of                particles-containing gas and cleaning liquid in gas                scrubber, colliding with liquid level of                cleaning-liquid storage component, condensing the                moisture content in cleaning blasting fumes with                cooling water, obtaining condensed water when                cooling the cleaning blasting fumes after dust                removal in gas cooling tower, supplying the                obtained condensed water to gas-scrubber side, and                replenishing cleaning liquid; and
   (3) carbon dioxide collection method, which                involves de-sulfurizing while introducing                particles-containing gas containing sulfur oxide                and carbon dioxide in gas scrubber, supplying basic                compound in the cleaning liquid through which it                circulates, condensing moisture content in cleaning                blasting fumes with cooling water, obtaining                condensed water when cooling the cleaning blasting                fumes after dust removal desulfurization in gas                cooling tower, supplying the obtained condensed                water to gas-scrubber side, replenishing cleaning                liquid, introducing process blasting fumes after                gas cooling into carbon dioxide absorption tower,                contacting carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide                absorption liquid in a cooling blasting fumes, and                removing carbon dioxide, introducing carbon                dioxide-absorbed rich solution to carbon dioxide                absorption liquid regeneration tower, regenerating                with reboiler vapor, and carrying out reuse loop of                the carbon dioxide absorption tower and carbon                dioxide absorption liquid regeneration tower for                the carbon dioxide absorption liquid by circulation                line.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of gas                treatment device.
   Gas treatment device (100A)
   Particles-containing gas (101)
   Post-cleaning gas (101a)
   Cleaning fluid (102)
   Gas scrubber (103)
   Gas cleaning column (104)
   Inclined plate (104c)
   Cooling fluid (105)
   Gas cooling component (106)
   Gas cooling column (107)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The gas treatment device                  has a liquid level indicator which measures the                  amount of liquid storage of cleaning liquid in                  the cleaning-liquid storage component, and a                  basic compound supply portion which supplies a                  basic compound in the cleaning liquid. Preferred                  Composition: The particles-containing gas                  contains sulfur oxide. Preferred Process: The                  pressure loss of gas scrubber is adjusted with                  adjustment of the liquid level of cleaning-liquid                  storage component.

TI Manufacture of urea involves heating urea            synthesis solution using medium-pressure steam as            heating source, separating gas containing ammonia and            carbon dioxide from synthesis solution, and obtaining            medium-pressure steam condensate.
AU YOSHIMOTO K
   SASAKI K
   KENJI Y
   KEIGO S
AE TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
   TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
   TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
   TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
   TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
GA 202009277U
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of urea involves carrying out a                synthesis process by synthesizing urea from ammonia                and carbon dioxide and producing a urea synthesis                solution, performing a high-pressure decomposition                process by heating the urea synthesis solution                using a medium-pressure steam as a heating source,                decomposing ammonium carbamate, separating a mixed                gas containing ammonia and carbon dioxide from the                urea synthesis solution, and obtaining a                medium-pressure steam condensate, carrying out                condensation process by absorbing a portion(s) of                the mixed gas, condensing, and generating                low-pressure steam using heat generated during the                condensation process, and performing primary                medium-to-low-pressure steam-generating process by                depressurizing the medium-pressure steam condensate                to a medium-to-low-pressure which is lower than the                pressure of the medium-pressure steam and higher                than the pressure of low-pressure steam, and                generating medium/low-pressure steam or                medium/low-pressure steam condensate.
   USE - Manufacture of urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of urea by                performing preheating process or medium-pressure                decomposition process, at low pressure, while                preventing the reduction of overall coefficient of                heat transfer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of urea involves carrying out a                synthesis process by synthesizing urea from ammonia                and carbon dioxide and producing a urea synthesis                solution, performing a high-pressure decomposition                process by heating the urea synthesis solution                using a medium-pressure steam as a heating source,                decomposing ammonium carbamate, separating a mixed                gas containing ammonia and carbon dioxide from the                urea synthesis solution, and obtaining a                medium-pressure steam condensate, carrying out                condensation process by absorbing a portion(s) of                the mixed gas in an absorbing medium, condensing,                and generating low-pressure steam using heat                generated during the condensation process,                performing primary medium-to-low-pressure                steam-generating process by depressurizing the                medium-pressure steam condensate to a                medium-to-low-pressure which is lower than the                pressure of the medium-pressure steam and higher                than the pressure of the low-pressure steam, and                generating medium/low-pressure steam or                medium/low-pressure steam condensate, and                performing a medium-pressure decomposition process                using a portion(s) of the medium/low-pressure steam                as a heating source at a pressure lower than the                high-pressure decomposition process, heating the                urea synthesis solution after the treatment in the                high-pressure decomposition process, decomposing                ammonium carbamate, and separating the mixed gas                containing ammonia and carbon dioxide from the urea                synthesis solution, or an ammonia preheating                process by heating the ammonia supplied to the                synthesis process using a portion(s) of the                medium/low-pressure steam generated in the primary                medium-to-low pressure steam-generating process as                a heating source. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included                for urea manufacturing apparatus, which consists of                a synthesizer, a high-pressure decomposer, a                condenser, a medium-to-low-pressure steam                generator, medium-pressure decomposer, and ammonia                preheater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Manufacture of urea                  further involves heating using a portion(s) of                  the medium/low-pressure steam as a heating source                  in the high-pressure decomposition process,                  performing secondary medium-to-low-pressure                  steam-generating process by depressurizing the                  medium-pressure steam condensate to a                  medium-to-low-pressure which is lower than the                  pressure of the medium-pressure steam and higher                  than the pressure of the low-pressure steam using                  a medium-pressure steam or a high-pressure steam                  as a drive fluid, and pressurizing the                  low-pressure steam using an ejector, and                  performing the synthesis process and the                  condensation process in a single pressure                  vessel.

PN WO2019096844-A1; DK201770862-A; CN111356371-A; US2020275671-A1; EP3709813-A1; RU2020119362-A
TI Ripening perishable products during transport in            reefer container or refrigerated lorry or trailer, each            comprising controlled atmosphere system, involves e.g.            establishing controlled atmosphere, changing            temperature, dosing ripening agent.
AU HOLM A G
   KLOSE M
   MADSEN P K
   POULSEN N N
   CLOSE M
AE MAERSK CONTAINER IND AS (MAER-Non-standard)
   MAERSK CONTAINER IND AS (MAER-Non-standard)
   MAERSK CONTAINER IND AS (MAER-Non-standard)
   MAERSK CONTAINER IND AS (MAER-Non-standard)
   MAERSK CONTAINER IND SA (MAER-Non-standard)
GA 201945762L
AB    NOVELTY - Ripening perishable products during transport                in a reefer container or a refrigerated lorry or                trailer (1), each comprising a controlled                atmosphere system (3), comprises: establishing a                controlled atmosphere within the container, lorry                or trailer with ripenable produce loaded; changing                temperature in the container, lorry or trailer from                a temperature; dosing ripening agent to the                controlled atmosphere in order to initiate ripening                while maintaining controlled atmosphere; check if                ripening rate is increasing or decreasing and                determine ripening status of products by measuring                carbon dioxide respiration rate; when carbon                dioxide respiration rate is rising a ripening model                is calibrated using input of timing for ripening                start based on scheduled delivery; when a                predetermined ripening stage is achieved, the                temperature is changed to transport temperature for                the ripenable produce; and products are maintained                at predetermined ripening state under controlled                atmosphere conditions.
   USE - The method is useful for ripening perishable                products e.g. fruit and vegetables during transport                in reefer container or refrigerated lorry or                trailer (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is cost effective; and adjusts or                slows down the rate of ripening in relation to                remaining time to delivery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ripening perishable products during transport                in a reefer container or a refrigerated lorry or                trailer (1), each comprising a controlled                atmosphere system (3), comprises: establishing a                controlled atmosphere within the container, lorry                or trailer with ripenable produce loaded; changing                temperature in the container, lorry or trailer from                a temperature, where ripenable produce are kept in                an unripening controlled atmosphere to a                temperature, and the ripenable products ripens in                controlled atmosphere; dosing ripening agent to the                controlled atmosphere in order to initiate ripening                while maintaining controlled atmosphere; check if                ripening rate is increasing or decreasing and                determine ripening status of products by measuring                carbon dioxide respiration rate; when carbon                dioxide respiration rate is rising a ripening model                is calibrated using input of timing for ripening                start based on scheduled delivery; when a                predetermined ripening stage is achieved, the                temperature is changed to transport temperature for                the ripenable produce; and products are maintained                at predetermined ripening state under controlled                atmosphere conditions. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for reefer container or refrigerated lorry                or trailer for shipping perishable products e.g.                fruit and vegetables, comprising a controlled                atmosphere system including sensors (7) capable of                measuring at least one of temperature, oxygen                molecule or carbon dioxide, a dosing mechanism (8)                for dosing ripening agent and a ripening process                controller (9), which controller controls the                ripening process based on sensed values of                temperature, oxygen molecule and/or carbon                dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a reefer container for                shipping perishable products such as fruit and                vegetables comprising equipment for establishing                and maintaining a controlled atmosphere within the                container.
   Reefer container or refrigerated lorry or                trailer (1)
   Controlled atmosphere system (3)
   Sensors (7)
   Dosing mechanism (8)
   Ripening process controller (9)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The oxygen molecule                  content in the container is increased from                  controlled atmosphere level to 5-21%, preferably                  7-10% before injection of ripening agent. The                  oxygen molecule content in the container is                  increased for a period of 12-48 hours after                  injection of ripening agent. The controlled                  atmosphere has an oxygen molecule content of                  1-10%, preferably 2-5% and a carbon dioxide                  content of 0-10%, preferably 3-7%. The specific                  heat production from the ripenable produce is                  kept below 300 Watt/ton by controlling the                  atmosphere within the container, lorry or trailer                  in relation to oxygen molecule, carbon dioxide,                  ripening agent concentration and/or temperature.                  The ripening process is controlled on basis of                  correlations of ripening time, temperature,                  oxygen molecule, carbon dioxide, and ripening                  agent dosing. The ripening process speed is                  controlled by regulating the oxygen molecule                  content inside the container atmosphere to                  achieve a specific carbon dioxide production or                  oxygen molecule consumption per mas unit of                  ripenable produce. The ripening stage is defined                  from accumulated carbon dioxide production (or                  oxygen molecule consumption) per mass unit of                  ripenable produce during the ripening process.                  The method is measuring an amount of chlorophyll                  and/or amount of beta carotene using an                  electronic color sensor. The ripening agent is                  ethylene and/or propylene and/or acetylene.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
