PN CN109233605-A
TI Preparing aqueous UV furniture paint comprises adding aqueous acrylate prepolymer and reactive diluent into high pressure reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide gas, adjusting pressure and making carbon dioxide as supercritical fluid.
AU LI E
AE ANHUI COSBONA HOUSEHOLD DECORATION MATER (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201913285G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aqueous UV furniture paint comprises (i) adding aqueous acrylate prepolymer and reactive diluent into high pressure reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide gas, adjusting pressure in the high pressure reaction kettle, making carbon dioxide as supercritical fluid; (ii) maintaining the pressure and temperature in the autoclave, stirring until completely dissolved, then adding film-forming auxiliary agent, nano-additive, adjusting the rotation speed to 800-1000 rotations/minute, stirring until completely dissolve, maintaining the temperature, reducing the pressure to 1-5 MPa, continue to stir for 1-1.5 hours, then deflating and cooling; (iii) mixing the cooled mixture with photoinitiator and water, stirring to obtain product at a rotation speed of 600-800 rotations/minute and an ultrasonic power of 400-600 W for 0.5-1 hour, adding the product into a vacuum drying oven, defoaming for 0.5-1 hour under conditions of a vacuum of -0.2 plus minus 0.1 MPa and a temperature of 20-30 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aqueous UV furniture paint.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high viscosity, good storage stability, simple construction, high production rate and high curing rate, is easy to control. The paint has high hardness, good toughness, good water resistance, good wear resistance, good stain resistance and indicators meet the national standards.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing aqueous UV furniture paint comprises (i) adding aqueous acrylate prepolymer and reactive diluent into high pressure reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide gas, adjusting the pressure in the high pressure reaction kettle to 60-80 MPa, temperature to 80-100 degrees C, making carbon dioxide as a supercritical fluid and the mass ratio of aqueous acrylate prepolymer to reactive diluent is 50-60:20-30; (ii) maintaining the pressure and temperature in the autoclave, stirring at a rate of 400-600 rotations/minute until completely dissolved, then adding film-forming auxiliary agent, nano-additive, adjusting the rotation speed to 800-1000 rotations/minute, stirring until completely dissolve, maintaining the temperature, reducing the pressure to 1-5 MPa, continue to stir for 1-1.5 hours, then deflating and cooling and mass ratio of the film-forming auxiliary agent, nano-auxiliary and water-based acrylate prepolymer is 2-4:1-2:50-60; (iii) mixing the cooled mixture with photoinitiator and water, stirring to obtain product at a rotation speed of 600-800 rotations/minute and an ultrasonic power of 400-600 W for 0.5-1 hour, adding the product into a vacuum drying oven, defoaming for 0.5-1 hour under conditions of a vacuum of -0.2 plus minus 0.1 MPa and a temperature of 20-30 degrees C and the mass ratio of photoinitiator, water and aqueous acrylate prepolymer is 2-3:5-20:50-60. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for aqueous UV furniture paint prepared by the method as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The step (i) further comprises adding pigment into autoclave, and the finished product is an aqueous UV primer and the finished product was prepared without addition of pigment and the mass ratio of pigment to aqueous acrylate prepolymer was 1-20:50-60. The aqueous acrylate prepolymer comprises aqueous urethane acrylate, aqueous epoxy acrylate, water-soluble amino acrylate and/or water-soluble acrylate copolymers. The reactive diluent comprises ethoxylated trimethylolpropane triacrylate, propoxylated trishydroxyl propane triacrylate, hydroxypropyl methacrylate and/or hydroxyethyl methacrylate. The photoinitiator is photoinitiator 1173, photoinitiator 184, photoinitiator 369, photoinitiator 819, photoinitiator 1700 and/or photoinitiator TPO-L. The film-forming auxiliary agent comprises leveling agent, wetting agent, photosensitive sensitizer and adhesion promoter. The nano-additive comprises nano-magnesium, nano-silica, nano-titanium dioxide and/or nano-modified bamboo charcoal. The pigment refers to various inorganic pigments available on the market.

PN US2022242729-A1
TI Recovering sulfur involves heating regenerating            first-stage adsorption vessel in temperature ramp to            selectively desorb components from first adsorbent in            regenerating first-stage adsorption vessel, where            components desorbed during temperature ramp comprise            primarily hydrogen sulfide.
AU DUVAL S A
   ALAMI I A
   OTHMAN R M
   LITHOXOOS G
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202297264Q
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering sulfur involves hydrogenating Claus                exhaust gas to produce hydrogenated gas comprising                hydrogen sulfide, water, and at least of one of                carbon dioxide or nitrogen; feeding quenched gas to                adsorbing first-stage adsorption vessel; adsorbing                water from quenched gas onto first adsorbent in                adsorbing first-stage adsorption vessel to produce                first outlet gas; adsorbing hydrogen sulfide from                first outlet gas onto second adsorbent in adsorbing                second-stage adsorption vessel to produce second                byproduct stream; feeding first portion of second                byproduct stream as regeneration gas to                regenerating first-stage adsorption vessel; and                heating regenerating first-stage adsorption vessel                in temperature ramp to selectively desorb                components from first adsorbent in regenerating                first-stage adsorption vessel to produce first                byproduct stream comprising desorbed components,                where components desorbed during first portion of                temperature ramp comprise primarily hydrogen                sulfide.
   USE - Method for recovering sulfur.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases sulfur recovery to or                above 99.9% with inexpensive equipments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering sulfur involves hydrogenating Claus                exhaust gas to produce hydrogenated gas comprising                hydrogen sulfide, water, and at least of one of                carbon dioxide or nitrogen; processing the                hydrogenated gas in a quench tower to produce                quenched gas comprising hydrogen sulfide, water,                and at least one of carbon dioxide or nitrogen;                feeding quenched gas to an adsorbing first-stage                adsorption vessel; adsorbing water from the                quenched gas onto first adsorbent in the adsorbing                first-stage adsorption vessel to produce a first                outlet gas, the first outlet gas comprising                hydrogen sulfide and at least one of carbon dioxide                or nitrogen; feeding the first outlet gas to an                adsorbing second-stage adsorption vessel; adsorbing                hydrogen sulfide from the first outlet gas onto                second adsorbent in the adsorbing second-stage                adsorption vessel to produce a second byproduct                stream comprising at least one of carbon dioxide or                nitrogen; feeding a first portion of the second                byproduct stream as regeneration gas to a                regenerating first-stage adsorption vessel; and                heating the regenerating first-stage adsorption                vessel in a temperature ramp to selectively desorb                components from first adsorbent in the regenerating                first-stage adsorption vessel to produce a first                byproduct stream comprising desorbed components,                where components desorbed during a first portion of                the temperature ramp comprise primarily hydrogen                sulfide.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for treating Claus exhaust gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The first byproduct                  stream further comprises at least one of carbon                  dioxide or nitrogen, and where the first                  adsorbent and the second adsorbent each comprise                  a molecular sieve. The method involves feeding                  the first byproduct stream to a reaction furnace                  of a Claus unit during the first part of the                  temperature ramp, where the first portion of the                  temperature ramp comprises an initial portion of                  the temperature ramp. The second portion of the                  temperature ramp, the components desorbed from                  the first adsorbent in the regenerating                  first-stage adsorption vessel comprise water or                  concentration of hydrogen sulfide in the first                  byproduct stream is less than a threshold value,                  or both, and the second portion of the                  temperature ramp is later in time and at greater                  temperatures than the first portion of the                  temperature ramp. The method involves feeding the                  first byproduct stream to the quench tower during                  a second portion of the temperature ramp. The                  first-stage adsorption unit comprises the                  adsorbing first-stage adsorption vessel and the                  regenerating first-stage adsorption vessel. The                  adsorbing first-stage adsorption vessel is a                  first-stage adsorption vessel operationally in an                  adsorption cycle. The regenerating first-stage                  adsorption vessel is a first-stage adsorption                  vessel operationally in a regeneration cycle, and                  the first adsorbent comprises molecular sieve 3A.                  The first-stage adsorption unit comprises a                  first-stage adsorption vessel operationally in                  standby, and the second adsorbent comprises                  molecular sieve 4A. The method involves feeding a                  second portion of the second byproduct stream to                  a regenerating second-stage adsorption vessel and                  desorbing hydrogen sulfide from second adsorbent                  in the regenerating second-stage adsorption                  vessel into the second portion of the second                  byproduct stream to produce a second outlet gas                  comprising hydrogen sulfide. The second outlet                  gas is provided as feed gas for a reaction                  furnace of a Claus unit that generated the Claus                  exhaust gas. The significant portion of the water                  in the quenched gas is adsorbed onto the first                  adsorbent in the adsorbing first-stage adsorption                  vessel, the first adsorbent in the adsorbing                  first-stage adsorption vessel adsorbs water while                  passes hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, and                  nitrogen to form the first outlet gas during an                  adsorption cycle. The second adsorbent adsorbs                  hydrogen sulfide while passes carbon dioxide and                  nitrogen to form the second byproduct stream                  during an adsorption cycle. The third portion of                  the second byproduct stream is fed to a thermal                  oxidizer, the hydrogenated gas does not comprise                  sulfur-containing compounds other than hydrogen                  sulfide. The first outlet gas does not comprise                  water, and the second byproduct stream does not                  comprise hydrogen sulfide. The Claus exhaust gas                  hydrogenation involves converting                  sulfur-containing compounds in the Claus exhaust                  gas into hydrogen sulfide in a hydrogenation                  reactor to produce the hydrogenated gas                  comprising hydrogen sulfide, water, and at least                  one of carbon dioxide or nitrogen. The Claus                  exhaust gas is hydrogenated by feeding the Claus                  exhaust gas comprising sulfur-containing                  compounds to a hydrogenation reactor and                  converting the sulfur-containing compounds in the                  hydrogenation reactor to hydrogen sulfide to                  produce the hydrogenated gas comprising hydrogen                  sulfide, water, and at least one of carbon                  dioxide or nitrogen. The processing the                  hydrogenated gas in a quench tower comprises                  feeding the hydrogenated gas to the quench tower,                  condensing water in the hydrogenated gas in the                  quench tower, and removing water condensate from                  the quench tower to produce the quenched gas, and                  the quenched gas comprises the hydrogenated gas                  without the water condensate removed.

PN CN113975931-A
TI High-purity oxygen purifying device, has supporting plates whose opposite sides are provided with carbon dioxide absorbing net distributed with equal distance, where inner wall of bottom of purifying box is provided with guide hole.
AU LIU Z
   YU T
   FENG X
   ZHANG X
AE SHANGHAI WEICHUANG STANDARD GAS ANALYTIC (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202223336L
AB    NOVELTY - High-purity oxygen purifying device comprises purifying box (5), four corners of the outer wall of the bottom of the purification box are provided with support legs (1), the bottom of the central outer wall of the purifying box is provided with an air guide hopper (3), and a collection pipe (2) is provided on the bottom outer wall of an air hopper. A box door (6) is hingedly installed on the front side of the top outer wall of the purifying box, and a handle (4) is installed on one side of a front side of the box door. An air inlet pipe (7) is arranged on the top outer wall of the purifying box. The bottom end of the air intake pipe is equipped with an air guide cylinder. The top of the purifying box is fixed with a motor through the mounting block. The opposite sides of the two supporting plates are provided with carbon dioxide absorbing net distributed with equal distance, the inner wall of the bottom of the purifying box is provided with a guide hole.
   USE - High-purity oxygen purifying device for use in high pure oxygen purifying technology field.
   ADVANTAGE - The device improves the efficiency of air conduction, ensures the unit time of air passing through, and improves efficiency of subsequent purification of air. The air of the air cylinder can rotate and guide air, which effectively promotes the rate of air flow, expands the flow of air purification, and ensures the efficiency and quality of oxygen purification. The carbon dioxide adsorption net effectively removes the carbon dioxide in the air, and introduces the air after removing carbon dioxide into the air guide hopper through the guide hole, and the high-purity oxygen is conducted and collected by the collecting pipe at the bottom of air guide bucket.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a high pure oxygen purifying technique.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a main view structure schematic diagram of a high purity oxygen purifying device.
   Supporting leg (1)
   Collecting pipe (2)
   Air guide hopper (3)
   Handle (4)
   Purifying box (5)
   Box door (6)
   Air inlet pipe (7)

AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises first reactor for reacting alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide in the presence of phenol-based organic catalyst to synthesize first reaction product containing alkylene carbonate.    USE - Apparatus for producing and purifying alkylene carbonate.

   (C) supplying the secondary reaction product to gas-liquid separation drum to separate carbon dioxide and a final reaction product, supplying the final reaction product to distillation column connected to a gas-liquid separation drum to purify alkylene carbonate, first reactor is heat exchanger-integrated reactor which is locating at upper end of the first reactor and has reaction retention portion having carbon dioxide, alkylene oxide and catalyst injection portions respectively, shell and tube type heat exchanger having steam condensate injection portion and first steam discharge portion connecting to the open bottom surface of the reaction holding portion and open top surface through flange coupling; and

PN CN110817871-A
TI Preparation of nitrogen-doped graphene-based carbon aerogel microspheres for lithium-ion capacitor assembly, by using e.g. resorcinol, formaldehyde, catalyst anhydrous sodium carbonate, graphene oxide, melamine, surfactant and acetone.
AU CHEN A
   SHI B
   YANG C
   CHEN X
   WANG Q
   MU Q
   CHEN T
AE GUIZHOU MEILING POWER SUPPLY CO LTD (CAES-C)
GA 202018154K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-doped graphene-based carbon aerogel microspheres involves: (S1) mixing resorcinol, formaldehyde, catalyst anhydrous sodium carbonate, graphene oxide, melamine and deionized water to obtain a reaction solution; (S2) adding the reaction solution to a surfactant and stirring to obtain a uniformly dispersed red-brown emulsion; (S3) subjecting the red-brown emulsion to sol-gel reaction, aging by using an acetone solution containing trifluoroacetic acid, soaking in acetone and frequently pressing and drying to obtain an organic aerogel; (S4) carbonizing the organic aerogel to obtain graphene-based carbon aerogel microspheres; (S5) activating the microspheres with potassium hydroxide to obtain a spherical carbon aerogel; and (S6) activating the spherical carbon aerogel by carbon dioxide by heating the spherical carbon aerogel and heat treating, introducing carbon dioxide under the condition of heat preservation for 3-4 hours, cooling and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nitrogen-doped graphene-based carbon aerogel microspheres, which are useful for lithium-ion capacitor assembly (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the nitrogen-doped graphene-based carbon aerogel microspheres with moderate particle size, high bulk density and porosity, highly effective specific surface area, and excellent conductivity and wettability with the electrolyte.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nitrogen-doped graphene-based carbon aerogel microspheres involves: (S1) mixing resorcinol, formaldehyde, catalyst anhydrous sodium carbonate, graphene oxide, melamine and deionized water to obtain a reaction solution; (S2) adding the reaction solution to a surfactant and stirring to obtain a uniformly dispersed red-brown emulsion; (S3) subjecting the red-brown emulsion to sol-gel reaction, aging for 44-48 hours by using an acetone solution containing trifluoroacetic acid, soaking in acetone for 48-120 hours and frequently pressing and drying to obtain an organic aerogel; (S4) carbonizing the organic aerogel to obtain graphene-based carbon aerogel microspheres; (S5) activating the microspheres with potassium hydroxide to obtain a spherical carbon aerogel; and (S6) activating the spherical carbon aerogel by carbon dioxide by heating the spherical carbon aerogel at a heating rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute to 800-1000 degrees C under a nitrogen atmosphere and heat treating for 1 hours, introducing carbon dioxide under the condition of heat preservation for 3-4 hours, cooling to room temperature under the protection of nitrogen after activation, and drying, where the ventilation rates of carbon dioxide and nitrogen are both 100-120 ml/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The specific preparation method of the reaction solution involves stirring resorcinol, formaldehyde, catalyst anhydrous sodium carbonate and graphene oxide dispersion until the resorcinol and the catalyst are dissolved, stirring in a constant temperature oil bath at 40-60 degrees C for 30-50 minutes, then adding melamine and formaldehyde in a molar ratio of 1:4 to the solution and stirring until the solution is clear, stirring in a constant temperature oil bath at 55-65 degrees C for 30-50 minutes, adding deionized water, and changing the solid content of the entire graphene-based carbon aerogel to 40% to obtain a reaction solution. The concentration of the surfactant in step (S2) is 1-50%, and the stirring rate is 600-800 rpm. The specific method of the sol-gel reaction involves sealing the red-brown emulsion, then placing in a constant temperature oil bath at 40-60 degrees C for 20-24 hours, and adjusting the temperature of the oil bath to 70-90 degrees C for 3-4 days. The carbonization method involves putting the organic aerogel into a tubular furnace, heating at a heating rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute to 800-1000 degrees C under the protection of inert atmosphere or vacuum, then carrying out heat preservation and carbonization for 3-5 hours, cooling to room temperature, grinding and sieving through a 325-mesh sieve to obtain graphene carbon aerogel composite material. The potassium hydroxide activation method involves placing the graphene-based carbon aerogel microspheres in a planetary ball mill, ball milling with a rotation speed of 400 rpm for 3-4 hours to obtain a powdered graphene-based carbon aerogel, stirring a potassium hydroxide solution, slowly adding the powdered graphene-based carbon aerogel during stirring, continuously stirring for 10-12 hours, uniformly mixing the mixture, drying the mixed mixture for 8-10 hours under the condition of 100-110 degrees C, then placing in a tube furnace under a nitrogen atmosphere, heating to 800-1000 degrees C at a rate heating of 3-5 degrees C/minute, activating at constant temperature for 3-5 hours, cooling to room temperature, taking out and crushing, then immersing in 0.8-1.2 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution for 20-24 hours, filtering, washing with deionized water until neutral, filtering and then placing in a constant temperature water bath at 115-125 degrees C for 1-2 days to obtain spherical carbon aerogel sample, where the ventilation rate of the nitrogen atmosphere is 80-100 ml/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of resorcinol to formaldehyde is 2:1. The surfactant is composed of Span 80 (RTM: Sorbitan monooleate) and cyclohexane in a volume ratio of 1:9.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); V01 (Resistors and Capacitors)

PN CN110668945-A; CN110668945-B
TI Method for extracting docosahexaenoic acid and            eicosapentaenoic acid, involves adding phosphoric acid            to fish oil, heating, filtering, performing            supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of obtained            oil, adding alkali, and glycerinating.
AU ZHANG Y
   ZHAO N
   ZHANG L
   WANG B
   SUN X
AE QINGDAO LIHE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2020061879
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting docosahexaenoic acid                (DHA) and eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) from fish                oil, involves (i) adding phosphoric acid to fish                oil, heating and stirring, filtering and removing                the gums to obtain degummed fish oil, (ii)                performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                of degummed fish oil to obtain decolored and                deodorized fish oil, (iii) adding alkali to the                decolorized and deodorized fish oil and stirring,                heating, filtering, washing and separating the                aqueous phase to obtain deacidified fish oil, (iv)                using a catalyst to methylate deacidified fish oil                with methanol, heating and stirring to obtain upper                layer of methylated fish oil, (v) using acetone as                a catalytic solvent, freezing methylated fish oil,                filtering off saturated fatty acids and distilling                off acetone, (vi) performing supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction of methylated fish oil from, and                (vii) glycerinating the obtained methylated DHA and                methylated EPA.
   USE - Method for extracting docosahexaenoic acid and                eicosapentaenoic acid from fish oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical extraction of                docosahexaenoic acid and eicosapentaenoic acid from                fish oil with improved purity using small amount of                solvent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting docosahexaenoic acid                (DHA) and eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) from fish oil                by combining a supercritical fluid, involves (i)                adding phosphoric acid to fish oil, heating and                stirring to flocculate the gums, filtering and                removing the gums to obtain degummed fish oil, (ii)                performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                of degummed fish oil to obtain decolored and                deodorized fish oil, (iii) adding alkali to the                decolorized and deodorized fish oil and stirring,                heating to separate the saponified product from the                oil, filtering off the saponified product, washing                the filtrate, and separating the aqueous phase to                obtain deacidified fish oil, (iv) using sodium                hydroxide, potassium hydroxide or sodium methoxide                as a catalyst to methylate deacidified fish oil                with methanol, heating and stirring until the two                phases are uniform, and leaving still the layers to                separate to remove the lower glycerol phase to                obtain upper layer of methylated fish oil, (v)                using acetone as a catalytic solvent, freezing                methylated fish oil at -15 degrees C to -30 degrees                C for 6-12 hours, filtering off saturated fatty                acids and distilling off acetone, (vi) performing                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of                methylated fish oil from which saturated fatty                acids is filtered, and separating methylated DHA                and methylated EPA using different saturated vapor                pressures of different components, and (vii)                glycerinating the methylated DHA and methylated                EPA.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The usage amount of                  phosphoric acid is 1-3 wt.% with respect to fish                  oil.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The stirring process in                  step (i) is carried out at 50-95 degrees C for                  25-45 minutes. The extraction process in step                  (ii) is carried out at a pressure of 10-20 MPa                  and a temperature of 35-50 degrees C for 3-4                  hours. The process in step (ii) is carried out at                  38-55 degrees C and a pressure of 6-10 MPa. The                  step (iii) involves calculating the amount of                  alkali based on the acid value of the decolorized                  and deodorized fish oil, preparing an alkali                  solution having a concentration of 10-25%, adding                  decolorized and deodorized fish oil and stirring                  for 10-20 minutes, filtering saponified product,                  washing the filtrate 3-6 times with saturated                  brine at 80-95 degrees C, separating the aqueous                  phase to obtain deacidified fish oil, dissolving                  saponified product in boiling water, and adding                  excess calcium chloride solution to obtain a                  fatty acid calcium salt. The step (iv) involves                  dissolving 0.3-0.7 wt.% sodium hydroxide,                  potassium hydroxide or sodium methoxide in                  methanol, adding to deacidified fish oil,                  stirring at 50-80 degrees C until the two phases                  are homogeneous, leaving still the obtained layer                  to separate, and removing the lower glycerol                  phase to obtain the upper layer of methylated                  fish oil. The step (v) involves adding 0.4-0.6                  %mass acetone to methylated fish oil, freezing at                  -15 degrees C to -30 degrees C for 6-12 hours,                  removing the saturated fatty acid by vacuum                  filtration, and recovering acetone at a heating                  temperature of 35-50 degrees C and a speed is                  85-100 rpm. The step (vi) involves separates                  methylated DHA and methylated EPA from other                  components in a separation kettle at an                  extraction pressure of 10-20 MPa and a                  temperature of 40-55 degrees C, and separating                  methylated DHA and methylated EPA in a                  distillation column at 30-45 degrees C, a                  pressure of 6-10 MPa and a flow rate of carbon                  dioxide of 30-90 L/hours for 4-5 hours. The step                  (vii) involves using 0.4-0.8 %mass sodium                  methoxide or toluenesulfonic acid as a catalyst,                  glycerinating the methylated DHA or methylated                  EPA at 180-220 degrees C using glycerol at molar                  ratio of 2:1-4:1, and reacting until the glycerol                  phase disappears. Preferred Composition: The                  molar ratio of methanol with respect to                  deacidified fish oil is 4.5:1-6:1.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)

PN CA3002749-A1
TI Production of petrochemical e.g. methanol, involves operating fuel cell to produce exhaust stream containing carbon-containing gas and reacting portion(s) of exhaust stream with reactant stream of natural gas to produce petrochemical stream.
AU MILLAR M
   LOURENCO J
AE 1304342 ALBERTA LTD (ONET-Non-standard)
   1304338 ALBERTA LTD (ONET-Non-standard)
GA 2019779395
AB    NOVELTY - Production of petrochemical using a hydrocarbon fuel cell involves operating a fuel cell to produce electricity, thermal energy, and exhaust streams comprising carbon-containing gas(s) and water, reacting at least one portion of the exhaust stream with a reactant stream of natural gas to produce petrochemical stream in a reactor, and heating the reactants using at least one portion of electricity and/or thermal energy.
   USE - Production of petrochemical e.g. methanol (claimed), syngas, ammonia, urea, polymers, prepolymers, hydrocarbon fuels, acetic acid, and/or glycol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces petrochemical with no or reduced greenhouse gas emission released into the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of methanol through the operation of fuel cell, which involves providing a pressurized natural gas stream, diverting at least one portion of the pressurized natural gas stream as a natural gas fuel stream to the fuel cell and at least one portion as a reactant natural gas stream to a methanol-production unit, expanding at least one portion of the pressurized natural gas stream to decrease the pressure of the pressurized natural gas stream to produce a refrigerant natural gas supply stream, operating the fuel cell to produce energy, an anode exhaust stream comprising carbon dioxide and water, and a cathode exhaust stream, passing the heated anode exhaust stream through a series of heat exchangers and separators to produce a stream of condensed water, a stream of condensed carbon dioxide, and a stream of gaseous carbon dioxide, heating the natural gas fuel stream for mixing with steam and reforming at a fuel cell anode, mixing an air stream with a portion of the carbon dioxide from the anode exhaust stream to form a fuel cell air supply, pre-heating the fuel cell air supply to meet an operating temperature of the fuel cell cathode, pressurizing and heating the stream of condensed carbon dioxide to a reactor operating pressure and temperature, pressurizing and heating one portion of the stream of condensed water to produce steam for mixing with the heated natural gas fuel stream for the fuel cell anode reformer, and pressurizing and heating another portion of the stream of condensed water to the reactor operating pressure and temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon-containing gas and water are produced from an anode of the hydrocarbon fuel cell. At least one portion of the thermal energy is carried by the exhaust streams. Production of petrochemical further involves separating the carbon-containing gas and water into separate streams using heat exchangers and phase separators, and powering a material handling equipment and/or a heater of the reactor. The reactant stream of natural gas and at least one portion of the exhaust stream are preconditioned in the heat exchangers using the thermal energy of the energy stream. Production of petrochemical alternately involves producing petrochemical from exhaust streams of a fuel cell by condensing, recovering, pumping and heating exhaust streams of carbon dioxide, water and nitrogen while producing electrical and thermal energy by providing the fuel cell having an anode and a cathode, a series of fluid streams connected to the fuel cell, and heat exchangers which heats and cools selected fluid streams, supplying the natural gas to the fuel cell in a fuel stream and to a petrochemical unit in a reactant stream connected to a petrochemical-production unit, heating the fuel stream of natural gas in another heat exchangers and mixing the heated fuel stream with steam at the anode, pre-heating an air stream to meet a temperature requirement of the cathode, cooling and separating the anode exhaust stream to produce a stream of condensed steam, a stream of condensed carbon dioxide, and a remaining anode exhaust stream comprising unreacted residue and carbon dioxide, and is being mixed with the air stream prior to being communicated to the cathode, pressurizing and heating the stream of condensed carbon dioxide to achieve an operating pressure and temperature of the petrochemical production units, pressurizing and heating one of the stream of condensed steam to produce the steam which is mixed with the heated fuel stream at the anode, and pressurizing and heating another portion of the stream of condensed steam to petrochemical units operating pressure and temperature of the petrochemical-production units, and cooling the pressurized natural gas to produce a refrigerant stream of natural gas which is used to cool the anode exhaust stream by passing the pressurized natural gas through a gas expander/generator to produce electricity or through a Joules-Thompson valve, and adding an external source of carbon dioxide, water and/or nitrogen to the petrochemical-production units. The remaining anode exhaust stream is compressed to meet desired operations properties for the fuel cell. The natural gas is diverted from an existing gas processing plant or straddle gas plant. The electricity generated in the fuel cell supplies motive power and thermal power to the petrochemical-production units. Preferred Components: The exhaust streams further comprise a nitrogen-containing gas produced from a cathode of the hydrocarbon fuel cell. The thermal energy is used to preheat the reactant stream of natural gas. The reactant stream of natural gas comprises methane, ethane, and/or propane. The fuel cell is powered by a fuel stream of natural gas, and the reactant stream of natural gas comprises a slipstream of the fuel stream of natural gas. The natural gas supply comprises liquid natural gas.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. PN WO2018093880-A1; AR110129-A1; CA3043358-A1; CN110072831-A; BR112019009520-A2; EP3541770-A1; US2019352239-A1; US10513471-B2; ZA201903436-A; EP3541770-B1; ES2889473-T3
TI Preparing 2-5C hydrocarbons comprises e.g. introducing feed stream comprising hydrogen gas and carbon-containing gas i.e. carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide into first reaction zone and contacting feed stream and hybrid catalyst.
AU NIESKENS D L S
   SANDIKCI A C
   GROENENDIJK P E
   FISH B B
   MALEK A
   MAREK A
   MALEK A M
   NIESKENS D L
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 2018411627
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2-5C hydrocarbons comprises: introducing a feed stream comprising hydrogen gas and a carbon-containing gas comprising carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide into a first reaction zone (101); contacting the feed stream and a hybrid catalyst in the first reaction zone; introducing a reaction zone product stream into a water removal zone that is downstream from the first reaction zone; and introducing a product stream from the water removal zone into a second reaction zone (103), resulting in a final stream comprising 2-5C hydrocarbons.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing 2-5C hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: converts feed carbon to desired products while minimizing the conversion of the feed carbon to carbon dioxide; and utilizes the hybrid catalyst to achieve a high conversion of carbon contained in a synthesis gas feed stream to desired products while minimizing the conversion of the feed carbon to carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 2-5C hydrocarbons comprises: introducing a feed stream comprising hydrogen gas and a carbon-containing gas comprising carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide into a first reaction zone (101); contacting the feed stream and a hybrid catalyst in the first reaction zone, where the hybrid catalyst comprises a methanol synthesis component and a microporous solid acid component, and the microporous solid acid component is a molecular sieve having 8-membered ring access; introducing a reaction zone product stream into a water removal zone that is downstream from the first reaction zone, where the water removal zone removes at least a portion of water from the reaction zone product stream; and introducing a product stream from the water removal zone into a second reaction zone (103), resulting in a final stream comprising 2-5C hydrocarbons. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for preparing 2-5C hydrocarbons comprising a first reaction zone including the hybrid catalyst, a water removal zone downstream from the first reaction zone, and a second reaction zone that is downstream from the first reaction zone.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a block diagram flow chart for preparing 2-5C hydrocarbons.
   First reaction zone (101)
   First water removal zone (102)
   Second reaction zone (103)
   Second water removal zone (104)
   Third reaction zone (105)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: A product stream is introduced into a fifth reaction zone that is located downstream of the second reaction zone, where the fifth reaction zone includes the hybrid catalyst. A final product stream comprising carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and hydrocarbons is introduced into a separation zone that is located downstream from the second reaction zone, where the separation zone separates the final product stream into a first product stream comprising hydrocarbons and a second product stream comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The second product stream is recycled by combining the second product stream and the feed stream. The first reaction zone is operated at reaction conditions to form a first reaction zone product stream, where the reaction conditions comprise: a reaction temperature of 300-440 degrees C; a reaction pressure of at least one bar; and a gas hourly space velocity of at least 500 reciprocal hours. Preferred Components: The second reaction zone comprises a second hybrid catalyst, where the second hybrid catalyst comprises the methanol synthesis component and the microporous solid acid component. The second reaction zone is a reverse water gas shift reaction zone that reacts carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the reaction zone product stream and forms carbon monoxide and water, where a separator is positioned downstream from the water removal zone. The second reaction zone is positioned downstream from the first reaction zone, and a second water removal zone is positioned downstream from the second reaction zone. A third reaction zone (105) is positioned downstream from the first reaction zone, upstream from the water removal zone, and upstream from the second reaction zone, and the third reaction zone is a reverse water gas shift reaction zone that reacts carbon dioxide and hydrogen and forms carbon monoxide and water. A fourth reaction zone is positioned downstream from the water removal zone and downstream from the second reaction zone, where the fourth reaction zone is a reverse water gas shift reaction zone that reacts carbon dioxide and hydrogen and forms carbon monoxide and water. The first reaction zone, the water removal zone, and the second reaction zone are stages within a single unit. The first reaction zone, the water removal zone, and the second reaction zone are separate units.
   EXAMPLE - 1 g HiFUEL R120 (RTM: Copper-zinc-aluminum methanol synthesis component) was mixed with 0.33 g silicoaluminophosphate catalyst by shaking them together in a bottle. Each of the catalysts had a particle size before mixing within 40-80 mesh. The catalyst was loaded in a single reactor. The silicoaluminophosphate catalyst was prepared in the following way. A reaction mixture was prepared by combining 8.2 g of aluminum isopropoxide with a solution of 3.9 g of 85 wt.% orthophosphoric acid in 8.4 g of water, while stirring. To this mixture, 1.2 g of an aqueous sol of 30 wt.% silica and 0.5 g of water were added, and the mixture was stirred until homogeneous. To this mixture, 16.8 g of an aqueous solution of 35 wt.% tetraethylammonium hydroxide was added. Synthesis conditions for the silicoaluminophosphate catalyst were placing the reaction mixture in a stainless steel stirred Parr reactor and heated to 200 degrees C at 0.5 degrees C/minute. The temperature was maintained for 120 hours under autogenous pressure while stirring at 60 rpm. The product was recovered by centrifugation, washed with water and dried at 90 degrees C overnight.

   USE - Electrocatalytic process of converting carbon                dioxide into formic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables selective conversion of                carbon dioxide into formic acid using copper                complex as electrocatalyst in the presence of                hydrogen under simple experimental conditions. The                ligand architecture around copper enables                selectivity and stability towards electrochemical                conversion of the carbon dioxide.

TI Improved process for converting carbon dioxide into fuel molecules used to reduce global warming, comprises metal organic frameworks supported bimetallic nanoparticles with multifunctional approach.
AU WAGHMODE S A
   GADALE S R
   MEROLIYA H
   KALYANKAR V K
   PATIL V N
AE WAGHMODE S A (WAGH-Individual)
   GADALE S R (GADA-Individual)
   MEROLIYA H (MERO-Individual)
   KALYANKAR V K (KALY-Individual)
   PATIL V N (PATI-Individual)
GA 2021D46319
AB    NOVELTY - Improved process for converting carbon dioxide into fuel molecules comprises metal organic frameworks (MOF) supported bimetallic nanoparticles with multifunctional approach.
   USE - The process is useful for converting carbon dioxide into fuel molecules ethanol and methanol which will help to reduce global warming and to convert carbon dioxide feedstock as a primary greenhouse gas from the environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The process converts carbon dioxide to ethanol by 98% and methanol by 74%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method utilizes renewable energy source for reaction i.e. visible light photo catalysis. The conversion is carried out at room temperature. Preferred Components: In method the solvent used for the reaction is water. The conversion is up to 40% but proper impregnation of bimetallic catalyst copper-zinc, titanium-silver and iron-copper ends to ethanol as a product with chromiun-NBDC, iron-NBDC and titanium-NBDC MOFs for 99% conversion. Only bimetallic iron-cerium gives selective results for methanol as product with 74% conversion. Pentaethylenehexamine results into ethanol product with MOF supported bimetallic nanoparticles. Ethanolamine results into 74% conversion to methanol with only iron-cerium bimetallic nano catalyst. Synthesis of iron-cerium by using natural extract counts for methanol product.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of chromium-NBDC, titanium-NBDC and iron-NBDC gel metal organic frameworks that do not support the claims.

TI Preparation of nickel-supported magnetic iron-rich            component catalyst for hydrogenation reduction of            carbon dioxide to produce diesel, involves dissolving            nickel salt in deionized water, configuring to form            nickel salt aqueous solution, adding magnetic iron-rich            component, pressing, and calcining.
AU HUA Z
   XIONG B
   YAN X
   LIU J
   YANG Y
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2021D9635A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-efficiency                nickel-supported magnetic iron-rich component                catalyst, involves (1) dissolving nickel salt in                deionized water under normal temperature and                stirring conditions to form a nickel salt aqueous                solution, (2) magnetically separating the magnetic                iron-rich component from the fly ash, and then                adding the magnetic iron-rich component to the                nickel salt aqueous solution obtained in step (1)                for stirring and immersing to obtain a mixed                reaction liquid, (3) placing the mixed reaction                liquid obtained in step (2) for 2-5 hours at                50-80degrees Celsius, reacting, evaporating the                excess water in the product obtained to form a                sample with a moisture content of 5-40%, then                pressing the sample into a shape to obtain a molded                sample, (4) calcining the shaped sample obtained in                step (3) under air atmosphere conditions to obtain                a calcined sample, and reducing the calcined sample                with a reducing gas.
   USE - Preparation of high-efficiency                nickel-supported magnetic iron-rich component                catalyst used in catalytic hydrogenation reduction                of carbon dioxide to produce hydrocarbon fuel,                carbon monoxide, natural gas, petrol, diesel, and                aviation fuel (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical, and                provides catalyst having excellent anti-sintering                performance, high carbon dioxide conversion                efficiency, and good target product                selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) the high-efficiency nickel-supported                magnetic iron-rich component catalyst comprising                10-80% iron and 2-30% nickel; and
   (2) application of the catalyst in catalytic                hydrogenation reduction of carbon dioxide to                produce hydrocarbon fuel, carbon monoxide, natural                gas, gasoline, diesel, and aviation fuel, at                200-500degrees Celsius.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel salt in                  step (1) is any one of nickel nitrate, nickel                  chloride, nickel bromide, nickel sulfate or                  nickel-containing minerals. The fly ash mentioned                  in step (2) is mainly any one or more of cheap                  coal-burning fly ash, garbage incineration fly                  ash, fly ash produced by comprehensive                  utilization of solid waste and fly ash produced                  after biomass utilization.Preferred Property: The shape of the molded                  sample in step (3) can be any one of honeycomb,                  spherical, and cylindrical.Preferred Conditions: The calcination                  temperature of the shaped sample in step (4) is                  300-600degrees Celsius, and the calcination time                  is 2-6 hours. The reduction temperature of the                  calcined sample in step (4) is 400-700degrees                  Celsius, and the reduction time is 1-5                  hours.

PN EP3895547-A1; US2021321644-A1; DE102020110502-A1; EP3895547-A4; CN214693241-U
TI Producing carbonated drink, comprises mixing deoxygenated water with mix component to obtain beverage mix, adding gas that contains carbon dioxide to beverage mix to get mixed drink mix, and reducing oxygen content of mixed beverage mix.
AU WEIGL M
   ENGEL E
   SCHERER C H
   FEILNER R
   JUSTL J
   OEHMICHEN T
   RICHTER V
   HANS S C
   RIHITE V
   FERNA R
   WEIGEL M
AE KRONES AG (KROE-C)
   KRONES AG (KROE-C)
   KRONES AG (KROE-C)
GA 2021C09915
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbonated drink, comprises providing oxygen-reduced water; mixing the deoxygenated water with a mix component to obtain a beverage mix; adding a gas that contains carbon dioxide (CO2) to the beverage mix to a mixed drink mix; reducing the oxygen content of those with CO2 mixed beverage mix in a first container (110, 210) in order to obtain an oxygen -reduced CO2 ; discharging the oxygen reduced CO2 displaced beverage mix from the first container; determining the CO2 -content; and adding more CO 2 to the discharged beverage mix based on the specific CO2 content.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbonated beverage e.g. beer and soda, in a container including glass bottle, plastic bottle and can.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a more reliable and accurate determination of the amount of carbon dioxide added to the carbonated beverage mix. The method reduces the oxygen content or, in general, the proportion of foreign gas, e.g. nitrogen or air, in order to significantly increase the shelf life of the bottled beverages, to avoid chemical reactions with the container (especially can) and problems during the filling process. This also enables higher filling temperatures, which results in lower cooling costs. Some beverages, such as beer and sodas, have carbon dioxide addition to them. The regulation/control of the degassing for oxygen reduction and carbonization are problematic in terms of the complexity of the process operations and the accuracy and reliability of the values.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) device for producing carbonated beverages; and
   (2) filling system (100, 200) for filling carbonated beverage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the filling system.
   Filling system (100)
   Over line (101)
   Line (104)
   Discharge line (105)
   First container (110)

PT P
PN WO2021158890-A1; CA3165817-A1; KR2022139941-A
TI Manufacture of furan-2,5-dicarboxylate used for            forming e.g. polymer, involves providing carbon dioxide            gas to mixture containing furan-2-carboxylate and            inorganic base salt, heating to obtain salt of            furan-2,5-dicarboxylate solid containing monovalent or            divalent metal cation and agitating.
AU KANAN M W
   BANERJEE A
   FRANKHOUSER A D
   MATTHEW W K
   BANNERGIANINDITA
   DELANO F H A
AE UNIV LELAND STANFORD JUNIOR (STRD-C)
GA 202193477J
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of furan-2,5-dicarboxylate (FDCA)                involves providing (204) furan-2-carboxylate,                providing (208) an inorganic base in a form of an                inorganic base salt to the furan-2-carboxylate to                form a mixture, providing carbon dioxide gas (212)                to the mixture, heating (216) the mixture to a                temperature to at least partially melt the mixture,                to synthesis salts of furan-2,5-dicarboxylate solid                and breaking up (220) the solid by mechanically                agitating the mixture containing                furan-2-carboxylate. The salt comprises furan-2,5                dicarboxylate and metal cation which is monovalent                metal cation (M+) or divalent metal cation                (M2+).
   USE - Manufacture of furan-2,5-dicarboxylate used as                bio-based molecule for forming polymers such as                polyesters and chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of                furan-2,5-dicarboxylate with high yield and without                needing catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of furan-2,5-dicarboxylate (FDCA)                involves providing furan-2-carboxylate, providing                an inorganic base in a form of an inorganic base                salt to the furan-2-carboxylate to form a mixture,                providing carbon dioxide gas to the mixture,                heating the mixture to a temperature to at least                partially melt the mixture, to synthesis solid of                furan-2,5-dicarboxylate salt (MxFDCA, where x is                1-2) and mechanically agitating the mixture                containing furan-2-carboxylate, to break up the                solid. The salt comprises furan-2,5 dicarboxylate                and metal cation which is monovalent metal cation                (M+) or divalent metal cation (M2+).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the                manufacture of furan-2,5-dicarboxylate.
   Providing furan-2-carboxylate (204)
   Providing an inorganic base (208)
   Providing carbon dioxide gas (212)
   Heating mixture (216)
   Breaking up solid (220)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic base                  salt is carbonate salt. The cation (M+) is alkali                  cation, preferably cesium cation. The cation                  (M2+) is alkaline earth cation. The mixture is a                  solution with a solvent and does not contain                  catalyst and zinc compounds, cadmium compounds,                  mercury compounds, and iron compounds.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The mechanically                  agitating is performed using an impeller, in                  which speed of rotation of the impeller and                  geometry of the impeller allow the impeller to                  break up the solid or extruder using one or more                  screws to break up the solid or in a ball mill.                  The carbon dioxide is flowed through a reactor                  for agitating the mixture. The heating of the                  mixture is performed without presence of metal                  containing catalyst. The inorganic base salt and                  carbon dioxide gas form carbonate salt. The                  furan-2-carboxylate is provided in a solution and                  the inorganic base is provided in a solution. The                  method involves cooling the mixture to solidify                  the mixture to form a solid mixture, reducing                  particle sizes of the solid mixture and reheating                  the mixture while providing carbon dioxide gas to                  partially melt the mixture and further involves                  removing the solvent of the solution. The cooling                  of the mixture is performed by solidifying the                  mixture to reduce sizes of the solid                  mixture.
   EXAMPLE - A salt mixture of cesium furan-2-carboxylate                (CsFA) and 0.55 equivalents cesium carbonate was                prepared by combining furan-2-carboxylic acid and                1.05 equivalents cesium carbonate in water and                evaporating to dryness on a hotplate at 150 degrees                C. The resulting salt mixture was dried overnight                at 150 degrees C in a vacuum oven and stored in a                sealed container until use. 100 g Salt mixture was                placed in the reactor and the reactor was purged                with carbon dioxide. The reactor was heated until                the thermocouple reached a temperature of 252                degrees C, while carbon dioxide was simultaneously                flowed at 5.5 l/minute at 275 psig and the stirrer                was rotated at 150 rpm. The line providing carbon                dioxide to the reactor was heated to 350 degrees C                to ensure that the gas flow did not cool the                reaction mixture. After 60 minutes with the                thermocouple at 252 degrees C, the heater was                turned off. After cooling, the reactor was opened                and solid contents were removed by dissolving in                water. An aliquot of the solution was dried and                analyzed to obtain 82.4 g cesium                furan-2,5-dicarboxylate with yield of 83% and 5.1 g                remaining cesium furan-2-carboxylate. The polymers                such as polyesters and chemicals were prepared by                using the obtained compound.

PN CN113153475-A
TI Power-heat complementary supercritical carbon dioxide power cycle power generation system, has power cycle subsystem and heat pump sub-system that are each closed independent cycle system, where heat exchange is realized through coupling of partition type heat exchanger.
AU LI C
   HE F
   WANG Y
   YANG B
AE UNIV SHANDONG TECHNOLOGY (SDUT-C)
GA 202191091B
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a power cycle subsystem (1) and a heat pump subsystem (2). The power cycle subsystem and the heat pump sub-system are each closed independent cycle system. A heat exchange is realized through a coupling of partition type heat exchanger. An outlet of a heat pump compressor (21) is connected to inlet of a heat pump condenser (22). An outlet of the heat pump condenser is connected to the inlet of a throttle valve (23). The outlet of the throttle valve is connected with inlet of a heat pump evaporator (24). The outlet of the heat pump evaporator is connected to the inlet of the heat pump compressor. A preheater (12) and the heat pump condenser are provided with same portion and a precooler (18) and the heat pump evaporator are provided in same portion.
   USE - Power-heat complementary supercritical carbon dioxide power cycle power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The low-temperature exhaust steam of the automatic force cycle takes heat and the condensation process releases heat to the supercritical carbon dioxide on the high-pressure side in the evaporation process of the heat pump cycle. The heat pump uses non-azeotropic mixture of working fluids which has temperature slip that matches the power cycle. The heat pump is used to improve the heat transfer matching characteristics of the low-temperature regenerative zone so as to effectively improving the thermal efficiency of the cycle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of the power-heat complementary supercritical carbon dioxide power cycle power generation system.
   Power cycle subsystem (1)
   Heat pump subsystem (2)
   Preheater (12)
   Precooler (18)
   Heat pump compressor (21)
   Heat pump condenser (22)
   Throttle valve (23)
   Heat pump evaporator (24)

PN CN112762459-A
TI Device for eliminating dioxin in fly ash, has secondary combustion chamber that oxidizes carbon monoxide to generate carbon dioxide, and heat exchanger performs heat exchange to gas in secondary combustion chamber.
AU HAN J
AE WUXI YANGTAI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202154151C
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an oxygen-enriched melting furnace (110), a secondary combustion chamber (120) and a heat exchanger (130). The oxygen-enriched melting furnace is set with a first inlet (111) and a first outlet (112). The secondary combustion chamber is set with second inlet (121) and second outlet (122). The oxygen-enriched melting furnace is used for heating the fly ash input by the first inlet. The dioxin in fly ash is cracked under the heating condition to generate carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. The secondary combustion chamber is used for oxidizing the carbon monoxide to generate the carbon dioxide. The heat exchanger is used for performing heat exchange to the gas in the secondary combustion chamber. The height of secondary combustion chamber is obtained according to gas flow of first outlet, discharge area and preset reaction time. The preset reaction time is gas reaction time in two-combustion chamber. The discharge area is cross-section area of the second inlet.
   USE - Device for eliminating dioxin in fly ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxidizing atmosphere is created for the carbon in the fly ash after pyrolysis of dioxins and the carbon substances in the flue gas through oxygen-rich environment, s as to oxidize to form carbon dioxide, realize harmlessness without quenching, save investment and operating costs, and use waste heat to discharge exhaust gas to meet standards.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for eliminating dioxin in fly ash.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the device for eliminating dioxin in fly ash. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Oxygen-enriched melting furnace (110)
   First inlet (111)
   First outlet (112)
   Secondary combustion chamber (120)
   Second inlet (121)
   Second outlet (122)
   Heat exchanger (130)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The chlorine substance reacts with the remaining calcium oxide in the fly ash to form calcium chloride.

TI Device for capturing and destroying insects,            comprises box and hot carbon dioxide generator, which            is composed of fan filter, where main axial bore is            passed through upstream face and downstream face, and            shouldered screws are formed.
AU LE MARCHAND A
AE LE MARCHAND A (LMAR-Individual)
   LE MARCHAND A (LMAR-Individual)

AU ZHAO F
AE SHANDONG SAIGAO NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202131637C
AB    NOVELTY - Micro-foaming type single-component polyurethane adhesive comprises 30 pts. wt. oligomerpolyether triol, 30 pts. wt. oligomer polyether glycol, 1-2 pts. wt. catalyst, 2-5 pts. wt. foam stabilizer, 30-50 pts. wt. flame retardant, 30-50 pts. wt. polymethylene polyphenylpolyisocyanate, 20 pts. wt. supercritical carbon dioxide, 0.1-1.5 pts. wt. foaming inhibitor, where the micro-foaming type single-component polyurethane adhesive, the oligomer polyether triol hydroxy value is 240 mgKOH/g, the molecular weight is 700, viscosity(25 degrees C) 325 mPs, the oligomer polyether glycol hydroxy value is 56 mgKOH/g, and the molecular weight is 2000, viscosity (25 degrees C) 350mPs.s.
   USE - Used as micro-foaming type single-component polyurethane adhesive.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyurethane adhesive: can be used normally at a temperature of 45 degrees C, 35 degrees C, 25 degrees C, and 10 degrees C, thus the product produces microporous foam, reduces the foaming volume, and avoids the decrease of product viscosity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the polyurethane adhesive, comprising (i) adding 30 pts. wt. oligomer polyether triol, 30 pts. wt. oligomer polyether diol, 1-2 pts. wt. catalyst, 2-5 pts. wt. foam stabilizer, 30-50 pts. wt. flame retardant, and 0.1-1.5 pts. wt. foaming inhibitor into the high pressure-resistant seamless steel bottle for mixing and stirring for 0.5-1 hour; (ii) adding 30-50 pts. wt. polymethylene polyphenyl polyisocyanate, stirring for 3-5 minutes; (iii) adding carbon dioxide to high-pressure filling machine with 20 pts. wt. supercritical carbon dioxide; and (iv) mixing for 0.5-1.5 hours by shaking, installing valve handle and nozzle, sealing and storing, allowing to stand for 24-48 hours at normal temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is tertiary amine catalyst. The foam stabilizing agent is silicon foam stabilizing agent. The flame retardant is organic phosphorus flame retardant, phosphorus is 10.5%, bromine is 7%, hydroxy value is 10 mg KOH/g, viscosity of 25 degrees C is18 mPas, and boiling point is 210 degrees C. The micro-foaming single-component polyurethane foam can be used normally at the temperature of -45 to -10 degrees C. The foaming inhibitor is calcium oxide. The capacity of the high pressure resistant seamless steel bottle can reach 7 kg.

PN WO2019228900-A1
TI Providing source of protein for e.g. food production, by providing microalgae and/or cyanobacteria, providing with light, contacting with zooplankton, and collecting zooplankton, where waste from zooplankton is contacted with bacteria.
AU PRADO-CABRERO A
   HERENA GARCIA R
   NOLAN J
AE WATERFORD INST TECHNOLOGY (WATE-Non-standard)
GA 2019A2318N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for providing a source of protein, involves (a) providing one or more species of microalgae and/or cyanobacteria, (b) providing the microalgae and/or cyanobacteria with light and carbon dioxide, (c) contacting the microalgae and/or cyanobacteria directly as obtained in step (b) with one or more species of zooplankton which feed on it, and (d) collecting a portion of the zooplankton, where waste from the zooplankton is contacted with bacteria and fed back to the microalgae and/or cyanobacteria.
   USE - The method is useful for providing a source of protein for food production and capturing carbon dioxide (claimed). The method is useful for providing a protein source for human and animal consumption, preferably aquaculture, and for providing food for humans, livestock and pets.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures maximum constant zooplankton production, provides a highly efficient environmentally-friendly method, does not require an external source of organic carbon and is carried out on any land surface, where the microalgae uses photosynthesis to feed itself using sunlight and carbon dioxide from the atmosphere, and the feed product is more palatable to fish than feed from land based sources, which increases the food intake and growth of the fish.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for providing a source of protein, comprising (i) a first chamber comprising microalgae and/or cyanobacteria, (ii) a second chamber comprising zooplankton, (iii) means for carrying the microalgae and/or cyanobacteria directly from the first chamber to the second chamber, (iv) means for carrying waste from the second chamber to the first chamber, (v) means for collecting a portion of the zooplankton, and optionally (vi) means for providing light and carbon dioxide to the first chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (a) is carried out in a first chamber, and step (c) is carried out in a second chamber. The method further involves (e) processing the zooplankton to provide a material suitable for use as a foodstuff. The method or apparatus involves a closed loop. The nitrifying bacteria are provided in the second chamber, preferably a solid support. The nitrifying bacteria are provided in a third chamber.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a supercritical carbon dioxide cycle coal-fired power generation method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of supercritical carbon dioxide circulating coal-fired power generation system.

PN CN109054322-A
TI Fully-degradable magnetic self-rotating bionic bait material comprises polylactic acid, carbon dioxide-based thermoplastic polyurethane, food attractant, modified magnetic starch, Parthenium argentatum emulsion, pigment, and pearl powder.
AU BAN Y
AE CHAOHU YULANG FISHING GEAR CO LTD (CHAO-Non-standard)
GA 201900890Q
AB    NOVELTY - Fully-degradable magnetic self-rotating bionic bait material comprises 65-137 pts. wt. polylactic acid, 21-75 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based thermoplastic polyurethane, 7-21 pts. wt. food attractant, 27-83 pts. wt. modified magnetic starch, 21-38 pts. wt. Parthenium argentatum emulsion, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. pigment, and 0.5-3 pts. wt. pearl powder.
   USE - Used as fully-degradable magnetic self-rotating bionic bait material.
   ADVANTAGE - The bait material: is environmentally-friendly and easy to use; has bionic bait effect; does not requires adjustment; and can saves time, and greatly improves fishing efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the fully-degradable magnetic self-rotating bionic bait material comprising (1) taking polylactic acid, carbon dioxide-based thermoplastic polyurethane, food attractant, modified magnetic starch, Parthenium argentatum emulsion, pigment, and pearl powder; (2) mixing the food attractant, the modified magnetic starch, the Parthenium argentatum emulsion, cross-linking agent, pigment and pearl powder, uniformly drying at 50 degrees C, and grinding to obtain magnetic filling powder; (3) uniformly stirring the magnetic filling powder and the polylactic acid and carbon dioxide-based thermoplastic polyurethane, and extruding and granulating by twin-screw extruder to obtain mixed granules; (4) adding the mixed granules to hopper of injection molding machine, according to the shape of fish and shrimp, injection molding to obtain bionic bait, where the barrel temperature is 150-170 degrees C, 160-180 degrees C, 185-190 degrees C, the mold temperature is 55-75 degrees C, the injection pressure is 40-60 MPa, and the holding pressure is 20-55 Mpa; and (5) magnetizing the bionic bait, where magnetizing current is 480-500 A, magnetization temperature is 80-110 degrees C, obtaining fully degradable magnetic self-rotating bionic bait.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pearl powder is a mixture of silicon dioxide coated mica and titanium dioxide. The food attractant is lees, surimi powder, fruit fly powder, wire drawing powder, shrimp shell powder, garlic powder, earthworm powder, ginger powder, locust powder, gadfly powder, cockroach powder, amino acid, and/or meat maggot powder. Preferred Method: The preparation of the modified magnetic starch comprises dissolving 70-93 pts. wt. starch in water, adjusting the pH to 8-11 with sodium hydroxide, adding 0.5-7 pts. wt. sodium sulfate to gelatinize, cooling to room temperature, soaking 13-67 pts. wt. nano-magnetite with 15 wt.% hydrogen peroxide solution for 10-15 minutes, washing with water, adding gelatinized starch, processing for 10-15 minutes, adding 0.2-1 pts. wt. N,N-methylenebisacrylamide, ultrasonically stirring to obtain starch/magnetite mixture, statically placing the starch/magnetite mixture in strong magnetic field, mechanically stirring for uniformly dispersing the magnetite without covering the inner wall of container, adding 0.1-2 pts. wt. sodium hexametaphosphate and 0.1-2 pts. wt. phosphorus oxychloride as crosslinking agent, stirring at at 55 degrees C under 1000 revolutions/minute for 2-4 hours, stirring at room temperature for 1-3 hours, adjusting pH to 7, carrying magnetic separation, vacuum freezing and drying to obtain the modified magnetic starch.

PT P
PN WO2018148524-A1; US2019389778-A1; US11142486-B2
TI Product for dual phase, molten salt membrane for            carbon dioxide capture has open cell structure with            multiple pores that connect through ceramic structure            from one side of ceramic structure to opposite side of            ceramic structure.
AU CAMPBELL P
   HERNANDEZ M C
   WORSLEY M A
AE LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT SECURITY LLC (USGO-C)
   LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT SECURITY LLC (USGO-C)
GA 201863738G
AB    NOVELTY - The product (200) has a three dimensional                ceramic structure (202) with an open cell structure                having multiple pores. The pores connect through                the ceramic structure from one side of ceramic                structure to opposite side of ceramic structure.                The ceramic structure contains 3 mole percent                yttria-stabilized zirconia (Y203-doped Zr02). The                molten hydroxide (204) is retained in the pores by                capillary action. The molten hydroxide is potassium                hydroxide or a mixture of of lithium hydroxide,                potassium hydroxide, and sodium hydroxide.
   USE - Product for dual phase, molten salt membrane                for carbon dioxide capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of oxide-conducting ceramic support                eliminates the need of electrodes. Provides systems                and techniques for separating carbon dioxide gas                from emissions sources such as flue gas of fossil                fuel power plants that are capable of operating at                the incipient temperature of the flue gas as                emitted from the combustion process with a                sufficient energy e.g. temperature to effectively                separate the carbon dioxide without consuming                substantial power from the source and without                associated infrastructure costs incurred by the                existing techniques and systems. Eliminates need to                provide external sources of steam and further                improves energy efficiency of separation since                steam is already present and available in various                suitable applications, such as power plants. The                faster movement of acid gases across the membrane                desirably minimizes infrastructure quantity and                cost of the overall separation                process/solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of forming a porous ceramic structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a                porous ceramic structure.
   Product (200)
   Ceramic structure (202)
   Molten hydroxide (204)

PN CN108373562-A
TI Preparing micro-foamed sheet comprises e.g. mixing whisker to polymer material, granulating, then extruding resultant polymer/whisker composite and placing resultant polymer/whisker composite sheet in high-pressure reaction vessel.
AU QIN L
   MA W
   SHI Z
   ZHANG C
AE NINGBO GMF NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201864425B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing micro-foamed sheet comprises (i) adding whisker to polymer material, mixing and granulating the mixture to obtain polymer/whisker composite material, (ii) extruding the polymer/whisker composite obtained in step (i) to obtain polymer/whisker composite sheet, and (iii) placing polymer/whisker composite sheet obtained in step (ii) in a high-pressure reaction vessel, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the high-pressure reactor, adjusting the pressure and temperature of high-pressure reactor to Keeping carbon dioxide in a supercritical state, pressure infiltration, rapid pressure reliefing, placing infiltrated polymer/whisker composite sheet in a water bath foaming device for heating and foaming to obtain micro-foamed sheet.
   USE - The micro-foamed sheet is useful for preparing polymer/whisker composite micro-foamed film (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The micro-foamed sheet has characteristics of high reflectivity, ultraviolet resistance, strong mechanical property, heat insulation and high radio wave penetration rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing polymer/whisker composite micro-foamed film, comprising (i) adding whisker to polymer material, mixing and granulating the mixture to obtain polymer/whisker composite material, (ii) extruding the polymer/whisker composite obtained in step (i) to obtain polymer/whisker composite sheet, (iii) placing polymer/whisker composite sheet obtained in step (ii) in a high-pressure reaction vessel, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the high-pressure reactor, adjusting the pressure and temperature of high-pressure reactor to Keeping carbon dioxide in a supercritical state, pressure infiltration, rapid pressure reliefing, placing infiltrated polymer/whisker composite sheet in a water bath foaming device for heating and foaming to obtain micro-foamed sheet, and (iv) rapidly flattening the micro-foamed sheet of step (iii) and stretching to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the polymer material is polyethylene, polypropylene, polyethylene terephthalate or polyamide; and the whisker are potassium titanate whiskers, where the addition amount of potassium titanate whisker is 10 wt.% based on total weight of polymer/whisker composite. In step (ii), the polymer/whisker composite sheet has a thickness of 0.5-1 mm. Preferred Method: In step (i), the mixed synthetic granulation is carried out by using a single screw extruder of phi 150 and L/D of 15:1, and the temperature of single screw extruder body is setting to 140-160 degrees C, 150-170 degrees C, 160-180 degrees C and 170-185 degrees C, the head temperature is 180-190 degrees C. In step (ii), the extrusion pressure delay preheating roller temperature is 75-95 degrees C, and the rolling temperature is 165-185 degrees C. In step (iii), the pressure is 7.38-30 MPa, the temperature is 31-80 degrees C, and the permeation time is 1.5-6 hours; the foaming temperature is 90-100 degrees C, and the foaming time is 30-360 seconds.
