PN CN107897838-A
TI Preparing seasoning product comprises e.g. taking Cinnamomum tamala primary long bud, crushing, sieving, extracting in carbon dioxide supercritical extraction apparatus and adding cosolvent anhydrous ethanol to form semi-solid paste.
AU QI H
AE QI H (QIHH-Individual)
GA 201830206G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing seasoning product comprises e.g. taking Cinnamomum tamala, choosing fresh primary long bud, crushing and sieving, extracting obtained powder in a carbon dioxide supercritical extraction apparatus, extracting, adding cosolvent anhydrous ethanol and extraction mixture is a semi-solid paste with a pale green viscous semi-solid paste of Cinnamomum tamala, concentrating mixture after extraction in vacuum, freeze-drying to obtain Cinnamomum tamala cream, adding ethanol to Cinnamomum tamala sticky paste to obtain Cinnamomum tamala extract.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing seasoning product.
   ADVANTAGE - The seasoning product has good flavor, is mellow in taste and adds faint flavor in cuisine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing seasoning product comprises (i) taking Cinnamomum tamala, choosing fresh primary long bud, crushing and sieving with 40 mesh sieve; (ii) extracting obtained powder in a carbon dioxide supercritical extraction apparatus, extracting with 25 MPa pressure at 35 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide at a flow rate was 20 l/hours and adding 0.5 Ml/g cosolvent anhydrous ethanol, extraction mixture is a semi-solid paste with a pale green viscous semi-solid paste of Cinnamomum tamala; (iii) concentrating mixture after extraction in vacuum at 40 degrees C and freeze-drying to obtain Cinnamomum tamala cream; (iv) adding ethanol to Cinnamomum tamala sticky paste to obtain Cinnamomum tamala extract and bottling for further use; (v) taking 200 g refined salt, adding 20-125 ml Cinnamomum tamala extract, 20 g sodium glutamate, 5-25 g maltodextrin, 1 g citric acid, mixing and freeze-drying for 8 hours; and (vi) grinding and sieving by 60 mesh sieve to obtain uniform particle size, bottling and storing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (v) adding 20 ml Cinnamomum tamala extract and 5 g maltodextrin.

The                  deposition temperature of the atomic layer                  deposition is 260-300&#8451;, or, the                  temperature of the copper source is                  140-160&#8451;, alternatively, the                  temperature of the titanium source is                  80-100&#8451;. The preparation of the                  titanium dioxide nanosheet comprises adding                  hydrofluoric acid into tetrabutyl titanate, and                  performing hydrothermal reaction at                  190-210&#8451;, where the volume ratio of                  hydrofluoric acid and tetrabutyl titanate is                  1:4.5-5.5.

GA 202154238G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing methanol based on                mixed ion conductor membrane reactor involves                passing carbon dioxide and water vapor into the                cathode and anode of the mixed ion conductor                membrane reactor, and applying direct current                voltage to the cathode and anode of the mixed ion                conductor membrane reactor, where the working                temperature is 400-600 degrees C, the electrolyte                of the mixed ion conductor membrane reactor is a                perovskite-type mixed ion conductor, the cathode                material is a nickel-containing perovskite-type                mixed ion conductor, and the anode material                comprises perovskite-type mixed ion conductor                containing cobalt and/or iron, zirconate-based ion                conductor-cerium oxide-based ion conductor mixture                and/or ceria-based ion conductor-cerium oxide-based                ion conductor mixture.
   USE - Method for synthesizing methanol based on                mixed ion conductor membrane reactor                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is high in ion conduction                efficiency, conversion efficiency and selectivity                of methanol, utilizes greenhouse gases, is                economical and simple, adopts wide range of raw                materials, alleviates the problem of carbon dioxide                pollution to a certain extent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrolyte of the                  mixed ion conductor membrane reactor comprises                  barium zirconate-based ion conductor, strontium                  zirconate-based ion conductor, calcium                  zirconate-based ion conductor, barium                  cerate-based ion conductor, strontium                  cerate-based ion conductor, calcium cerate-based                  ion conductor, zirconate-based ion                  conductor-cerium oxide-based ion conductor                  mixture and/or ceria-based ion conductor-cerium                  oxide-based ion conductor mixture. The cathode                  material comprises nickel-barium zirconate-based                  ion conductor mixture, nickel-strontium                  zirconate-based ion conductor mixture,                  nickel-calcium zirconate-based ion conductor                  mixture, nickel-barium cerate-based ion conductor                  mixture, nickel-strontium cerate-based ion                  conductor mixture, nickel-calcium cerate-based                  ion conductor mixture, nickel-zirconate-based ion                  conductor-cerium oxide-based ion conductor                  mixture and/or nickel cerate-based ion                  conductor-cerium oxide-based ion conductor                  hybride. The anode material comprises mixture of                  cobalt and strontium doped lanthanum ferrite                  based ion conductors, cobalt and iron doped                  zirconate-based ion conductor mixture, cobalt and                  yttrium doped zirconate-based ion conductor                  mixture, zirconate-based ion conductor-cerium                  oxide-based ion conductor mixture and/or                  cerium-based ion conductor-cerium oxide-based ion                  conductor mixture. The mixed ion conductor                  membrane reactor comprises electrode supporting                  type and/or electrolyte layer supporting type.                  The surface of the cathode comprises methanol                  catalyst comprising zinc-chromium catalyst,                  copper-based catalyst, palladium-based catalyst                  and/or molybdenum-based catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The working                  temperature of the mixed ion conductor membrane                  reactor is 450-500 degrees C. The direct current                  (DC) voltage is 1-3 V.

PN TW202111990-A
TI Human-simulating breathing device for simulating oxygen consumption of human body in target space, has control unit coupled to human-like breathing device and gas monitoring element to simulate breathing movement in target space.
AU LO H
   SUNG T
   LIN Y
   LEE J
AE UNIV LUNGHWA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYLU-Non-standard)
GA 202181331X
AB    NOVELTY - Disclosed is a human breathing simulation equipment, which comprises a human breathing simulation device, a gas monitoring device, and a control unit. The human breathing simulation device has a proton exchange membrane fuel cell structure, an air input end, and a carbon dioxide output end. The human breathing simulation device is activated to operate in a manner of performing an electrochemical reaction of the proton exchange membrane fuel cell structure such that the human breathing simulation device inputs air from the air input end, and simultaneously emits carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide output end to simulate human breathing process. The gas monitoring device is configured to monitor the oxygen consumption in a target space. The control unit is coupled to the human breathing simulation device and the gas monitoring device and controls the operation of the human breathing simulation device and the gas monitoring device. As a result, the human breathing simulation equipment is used to simulate the oxygen consumption of the human body in the target space.

PT P
PN CN112454891-A
TI Manufacturing workpiece used as antistatic material, by uniformly blending polymer powder and metal powder to prepare polymer composite powder material, and sintering in selective laser sintering equipment using carbon dioxide laser.
AU LI J
   WEN J
   BAO G
   CHEN L
AE HUNAN FARSOON HIGH-TECH CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2021252875
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing high tenacity workpiece involves: (1) uniformly blending a polymer powder and a metal powder in a weight ratio of 95-99.5:0.5-5 to prepare a polymer composite powder material; and (2) adding the polymer composite powder material to a selective laser sintering equipment using a carbon dioxide laser and a fiber laser as a mixed light source for sintering. The sintering process involves paving the polymer composite powder material with a thickness of 0.05-0.2 mm, preheating the material to a preset temperature, which is 5-20 degrees C lower than the melting point of the polymer powder, sintering the powder to be sintered with carbon dioxide laser, then performing fiber sintering on the powder to be sintered with fiber laser, and repeating the sintering process until the workpiece is sintered.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing high tenacity workpiece, which is useful as antistatic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides workpiece with excellent performance characteristics and low surface resistivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for manufacturing the high tenacity workpiece.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal powder is iron powder, copper powder, nickel powder, aluminum powder, cobalt powder, titanium powder and/or silver powder. Preferred Property: The average particle size of the metal powder is 1-50 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer powder is polyamide powder, polyethylene powder, polyurethane powder, polypropylene powder, polystyrene powder, polybutylene terephthalate powder, polyphenylene sulfide powder or polyetheretherketone powder. Preferred Properties: The average particle size of the polymer powder is 40-80 mu m. The average particle size of the polymer composite powder material is 40-75 mu m.

The sensor is                controlled to detect according to the current                detection frequency. A detection value is displayed                through the display unit.
   USE - Method for controlling a sensor                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables prolonging service life of                a light emitting source in the carbon dioxide                sensor, and improving measuring accuracy of carbon                dioxide concentration of the carbon dioxide sensor                in the air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a device for controlling a sensor;
   a storage medium for storing a set of                instructions to execute a method for controlling a                sensor; and
   a processor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for controlling a sensor. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN CN112430744-A
TI Recycling waste lead paste by reacting waste lead            paste with sulfuric acid, adding ammonia oxide solution            to calcium oxide reaction, performing solid-liquid            separation and carrying out high-temperature smelting            of calcium sulfate.
AU DAI S
   XIANG C
AE CHILWEE POWER GROUP CO LTD (CHIL-Non-standard)
GA 202123488G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recycling waste lead paste,                involves (a) reacting the waste lead paste with                sulfuric acid to obtain lead sulfate lead paste,                (b) reacting the lead sulfate and ammonium                carbonate, and a solution of lead carbonate and                ammonium sulfate is obtained after solid-liquid                separation, (c) adding an ammonia oxide solution to                a calcium oxide reaction, and performing                solid-liquid separation to obtain calcium sulfate                and aqueous ammonia solution, smelting lead                carbonate at high temperature to obtain crude lead                and carbon dioxide gas, and (d) carrying out                high-temperature smelting of calcium sulfate to                obtain calcium oxide, sulfur dioxide and carbon                dioxide gas, using sulfur dioxide for preparing                sulfuric acid to return to the waste lead paste,                using the calcium oxide for returning ammonia                sulfate solution to prepare aqueous ammonia, and                absorbing the carbon dioxide with the aqueous                ammonia to prepare ammonia carbonate solution for                preparing lead carbonate.
   USE - The method is used for recycling waste lead                paste.
   ADVANTAGE - The waste lead paste recovery method forms a                recycling system with high efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the waste                  lead paste is transported into the reaction                  kettle and mixed and sheared with 10-30% waste                  sulfuric acid for 1-3 hours, and the reaction                  temperature is 70-100 degrees C to obtain lead                  sulfate lead paste. In step (b), the lead sulfate                  and ammonium carbonate are mixed and sheared at                  room temperature for 1-2 hours, and a solution of                  lead carbonate and ammonium sulfate is obtained                  after solid-liquid separation. In step (c), the                  ammonium sulfate solution is added to calcium                  oxide for mixing, grinding and shearing at room                  temperature for 1-3 hours, and the solid-liquid                  separation is carried out to obtain calcium                  sulfate and ammonia solution. The lead carbonate                  and coal powder are mixed and smelted at 700-900                  degrees C to obtain crude lead and carbon dioxide                  gas, after the carbon dioxide is surface-cooled,                  is absorbed with aqueous ammonia to prepare an                  ammonia carbonate solution. In step (d), the                  calcium sulfate and coal powder are mixed and                  calcined at 600-800 degrees C to obtain calcium                  oxide, sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide gas. The                  sulfur dioxide is absorbed by hydrogen peroxide                  to prepare sulfuric acid. The carbon dioxide that                  reacts with the aqueous ammonia to prepare the                  ammonia carbonate solution includes carbon                  dioxide obtained by smelting calcium sulfate and                  carbon dioxide obtained by smelting lead                  carbonate.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

TI Gas storage tank type folding carbon dioxide laser, has discharge tube whose two ends are respectively connected with output mirror and tail mirror to form laser resonant cavity, where discharge tube is connected with air storage tank.
AU LI X
   LIU X
AE ANHUI MICAI INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020B11347
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an air storage tank type folding carbon dioxide laser, comprising a laser tube assembly and an external air storage tank; the laser tube assembly comprises a discharge tube and a cooling device; the discharge tube is connected with a positive potential electrode and a negative potential electrode; two ends of the discharge tube are respectively connected with the output mirror and the tail mirror to form a laser resonant cavity; the discharge tube is connected with the air storage tank. The utility model divides the original three-layer structure of the laser tube, divided into two layers of sleeve structure of the laser tube and an external air storage tank, simplifies the manufacturing process of the laser tube, reduces the cost and prolongs the service life. The two-layer sleeve structure of the laser tube, reduces the size limit requirement of the glass tube, can adopt large-calibre discharge tube, so as to improve the output power of the carbon dioxide laser.

PN DE102019111072-A1
TI Method for catalytic production of formamide e.g. methanamide, involves reacting ammonia, carbon dioxide and hydrogen in presence of ruthenium-phosphine complex, and performing thermal decomposition of obtained ammonium formate.
AU MAKHYNYA Y
   EL-HAWARY T
   GLOTZBACH C
   TENHUMBERG N
   LEITNER W
   KLANKERMAYER J
   SCHUMACHER H
AE RHEINISCH WESTFAELISCHE TECH HOCHSCHULE (RWTH-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
GA 2020A61538
AB    NOVELTY - Method for catalytic production of formamide involves reacting ammonia, carbon dioxide and hydrogen in an aqueous medium and at least one organic solvent in the presence of a ruthenium-phosphine complex as a catalyst and an acid at 60-180 degrees C, preferably 90-110 degrees C and a pressure of 35-210 bar, preferably 80-190 bar for 1 minute to 24 hours, preferably 30 minutes to 15 hours to form ammonium formate or to form formamide and ammonium formate, and performing thermal decomposition of ammonium formate at 100-185 degrees C, preferably 150-180 degrees C with formation of water and formamide.
   USE - Method for catalytic production of formamide e.g. methanamide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables time-saving catalytic production of formamide with improved yield under mild pressure and temperature conditions. The catalyst used in the method has improved catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the ruthenium-phosphine complex as a catalyst for catalytic production of ammonium formate and/or formamide from ammonia, carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ruthenium-phosphine complex comprises at least one chosen from monophosphine compound of formula: PR1R2R3, a diphosphine compound, triphosphine compound of formula (I) and compound comprising more than three phosphine groups. The triphosphine compound (I) is preferably 1,1,1-tris(diphenylphosphinomethyl)ethane. The ruthenium-phosphine complex is of formula: (A)Ru(L)3. The usage amount of ruthenium-phosphine complex is 0.01 mmol to 1 mol-%, preferably 1 mmol% to 0.1 mol% with respect to molar content of ammonia. The acid is a Lewis acid or a protic acid, preferably at least one chosen from organoaluminum compounds, organoboron compounds, sulfonic acids, scandium compounds and perfluorinated copolymers comprising at least one sulfo group. The molar ratio of ruthenium-phosphine complex with respect to acid is 1:800-1:1, preferably 1:50-1:10. The aqueous medium comprises mixture of water and at least one organic solvent.R1-R3 = alkyl, aryl or heteroaryl (all optionally substituted), preferably optionally substituted phenyl;R1'-R6 = aryl or heteroaryl (both optionally substituted), preferably optionally substituted phenyl;R7' = H, alkyl, cycloalkyl or aryl;A = triphosphine compound (I);andL = monodentate ligand, bidentate ligand or tridentate ligand.Preferred Process: The catalytic conversion of ammonia, carbon dioxide and hydrogen is a homogeneous or heterogeneous catalytic reaction, preferably homogeneous catalytic reaction.

PN CN111808593-A; CN111808593-B
TI Agent useful for inhibiting strains of sulfate reducing bacteria in carbon dioxide flooding, contains arecoline quaternary ammonium salt, mixed oleic acid imidazoline, 1,3,5-tris(2-hydroxyethyl)-hexahydros-triazine, thiourea and water.
AU SHI W
   LIU H
   LV H
   YU X
   WANG J
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC JIANGSU OILFIELD CO (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP JIANGSU OILF (SNPC-C)
GA 2020A6745C
AB    NOVELTY - Agent comprises 1-35 wt.% arecoline quaternary ammonium salt, 1-20 wt.% mixed oleic acid imidazoline, 5-15 wt.% 1,3,5-tris(2-hydroxyethyl)-hexahydros-triazine, 5-10 wt.% thiourea, 1-5 wt.% monoethanolamine, 10-20 wt.% 2-phosphate-1,2,4-tricarboxylic acid butane and 0-70 wt.% water.
   USE - The agent is useful for inhibiting strains of sulfate reducing bacteria for carbon dioxide flooding combined with bacterial acidification (claimed). The agent is used in oil wells with high temperature and high carbon dioxide partial pressure and rich in harmful microorganisms.
   ADVANTAGE - The agent exhibits low usage and good effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the agent, which involves (a) adding the arecoline quaternary ammonium salt and the mixed oleic acid imidazoline to the water, and mixing evenly, and (b) adding 1,3,5-tris(2-hydroxyethyl)-hexahydros-triazine, thiourea, monoethanolamine and 2-phosphate-1,2,4-tricarboxylic acid butane, and mixing uniformly; and
   (2) use of the agent for inhibiting strains of sulfate reducing bacteria.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The mixed oleic acid imidazoline is synthesized by mixing the mixed oleic acid with polyvinyl polyamine and hydroxyethylene diamine, adding a dehydrating agent to react, performing acylation at 160-185 degrees C and vacuum degree of 3.55-5.93 kPa for 2-3 hours, and performing cyclization at 230-240 degrees C for 4-6 hours by adding 0.5-3 wt.% diethylenetriamine. The arecoline quaternary ammonium salt is synthesized by adding arecoline and solvent to a reactor equipped with a reflux condenser, adding sodium chloroacetate dropwise at 60-100 degrees C, reacting for 6-10 hours, cooling, adding solvent, stirring product, and cooling arecoline quaternary ammonium salt. The temperature of the water is controlled at 60 degrees C. Preferred Agent: The agent comprises arecoline, solvent and sodium chloroacetate in a weight ratio of 3:2:1. The agent is configured as 100 mg/l solution. Preferred Components: The solvent is chosen from methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, dimethyl fumarate (DMF), water or their mixture. The dehydrating agent is toluene.

TI Method of obtaining gasified natural grape juice by forced aerated carbonation, involves using carbon dioxide with pressure of two and half volumes, by forced carbonation of natural grape juice, without adding water or any food additive.
AU ANDRADE PEREIRA E
   RIBAS M F

   CITADIN I
AE UNIV PARANA TECNOLOGICA FEDERAL (UYPA-Non-standard)
GA 2020A48464

PN CN111249325-A
TI Collecting honeysuckle cell fluid comprises e.g. collecting honeysuckle, pretreating honeysuckle, collecting honeysuckle cell fluid, separating cell fluid, and purifying cell fluid.
AU ZHANG Q
AE SHANDONG HUAXIA GAOKE AGRIC TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
   LINYI ACAD AGRIC SCI (LINY-Non-standard)
GA 202054693W
AB    NOVELTY - Collecting honeysuckle cell fluid comprises (i) choosing to collect honeysuckle in morning on sunny day, and stacking collected honeysuckles one by one to avoid large amount of honeysuckle accumulation, (ii) subjecting collected honeysuckle to impurity removal treatment to remove impurities on surface, and air-drying honeysuckle to increase surface temperature, (iii) placing pre-treated honeysuckle into heating kettle, adjusting temperature, passing carbon dioxide that has reached supercritical state into heating kettle, passing entraining agent into heating kettle, which is maintained at constant temperature and constant pressure for 2-3 hours, (iv) passing fluid in heating kettle to separator for separation treatment to obtain crude product and gaseous carbon dioxide, and discharging gaseous carbon dioxide to obtain cell fluid, and (v) purifying obtained crude product cell fluid to obtain final cell fluid, and storing collected honeysuckle cell fluid under anaerobic low temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for collecting honeysuckle cell fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has improved collection efficacy of honeysuckle cell fluid; and increases collection rate of honeysuckle cell fluid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of process of collecting honeysuckle cell fluid. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the entraining agent is methanol, ethanol, vegetable oil or n-hexane, preferably ethanol, and the purity of entraining agent is more than 96%. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the air-drying temperature is 20-25 degrees C, and the air-drying time is 15-30 minutes, during air-drying process, the honeysuckle should be turned over. In the step (iii), the temperature in heating kettle is 45-55 degrees C, and the pressure in the heating kettle is 35-45 MPa, the temperature of the heating kettle rises uniformly in the beginning, and the rising rate is 5 degrees C/minutes. In the step (iii), the flow rate of supercritical carbon dioxide is 22-28 l/hours, and the purity of the supercritical carbon dioxide is more than 99%. In the step (iv), the temperature of the separation equipment is controlled at 15-20 degrees C during the separation, and the separation pressure is controlled at 8-12 MPa. In the step (v), the purification technique is membrane filtration, gelatin method, adsorption or charge neutralization method, preferably membrane filtration method.

PT P
PN CN110540904-A
TI Extracting wormwood leaf essential oil by washing            and soaking wormwood stems and leaves, soaking in            liquid nitrogen, drying, crushing, sieving, mixing            solvent, introducing super-critical carbon dioxide to            extraction kettle and separating.
AU YANG C
AE JIANGSU ZHINENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A43727
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting wormwood leaf essential                oil, involves (i) washing and soaking fresh                wormwood stems and leaves, placing in a dehydrator,                completely soaking the dehydrator in liquid                nitrogen for 30-60 minutes, lifting up, placing at                room temperature for 5-10 minutes, and completely                soaking the dehydrator in liquid nitrogen for 30-60                minutes, (ii) drying, crushing, and sieving the                wormwood leaves obtained in step (i) through a 5-30                mesh sieve to obtain wormwood powder, (iii)                uniformly mixing the wormwood powder and solvent,                placing in a extraction kettle, introducing                super-critical carbon dioxide to the extraction                kettle, and maintaining the extraction kettle at                40-50 degrees C and 30-45 MPa, and (iv) taking out                the carbon dioxide-containing extract from the                extraction kettle, transferring the extract to a                separation kettle, and separating under reduced                pressure.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting wormwood                leaf essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the loss of effective                components and improves the quality and yield of                wormwood essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The biological enzyme                  is beta -glucosidase, pectinase or                  cellulase.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (ii) involves                  vacuum-drying the wormwood leaves at low                  temperature by staggering wormwood leaves in a                  vacuum-drying box, controlling the vacuum degree                  at 0.009-0.36 MPa, and vacuum-drying at 40-52                  degrees C, where the wormwood is dried until the                  moisture content is below 5%. The step (iii)                  involves completely mixing the wormwood powder                  with 0.01-0.08 times biological enzyme before                  placing the wormwood powder in the extraction                  kettle, and leaving still for 30-60 minutes. In                  step (iv), the separation kettle is maintained at                  7-9 MPa and at 25-30 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The solvent is a low boiling point                  solvent comprising acetone, ethanol, ether,                  methanol and/or dichloromethane.

TI Gel particle useful for preventing gas channeling            in continuous carbon dioxide injection flooding            process, comprises reactive monomer, cross-linking            agent, initiator and water.
AU BI W
   WANG S
   LIU X
   HE Z
   TAN J
   MA L
   YANG T
   LI L
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2019A04021
AB    NOVELTY - Gel particle comprises reactive monomer,                cross-linking agent, initiator and water, where                cross-linking agents are stable cross-linking agent                and unstable cross-linking agent (I).
   USE - The gel particles are useful for preventing                gas channeling in continuous carbon dioxide                injection flooding process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gel particle has significant second-stage                swelling during subsequent continuous carbon                dioxide injection, and achieves plugging of                hypertonic channels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gel particle comprises reactive monomer,                cross-linking agent, initiator and water, where                cross-linking agents are stable cross-linking agent                and unstable cross-linking agent of formula                (I).
   n = less than or equal to 1-500.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing gel particles comprising (i) adding                reactive monomer and cross-linking agent into                water, and stirring to dissolve completely to                obtain liquid 1, (ii) adding alkaline pH adjuster                into liquid 1, and adjusting the pH of the liquid 1                to 9-11 to obtain liquid 2, and (iii) adding                initiator into liquid 2 under inert gas atmosphere,                then carrying out polymerization by heating to                obtain gel and then crushing to obtain gel                particle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The reactive monomers                  are acrylamide and                  2-acrylamido-2-methylpropanesulfonic acid. The                  stable cross-linking agent is                  N,N'-methylene-bis(acrylamide). The initiator is                  persulfate, where the persulfate is ammonium                  persulfate or potassium persulfate. The gel                  particles comprises 6.5-9 wt.% reaction monomer,                  0.02-0.05 wt.%, stable cross-linking agent,                  0.3-1.0 wt.% unstable cross-linking agent,                  0.05-0.10 wt.% initiator and water (balance                  amount). The gel particles further comprises                  white oil and emulsifier. Preferred Method: The                  method comprises (i) adding reactive monomer and                  cross-linking agent into water, and stirring to                  dissolve completely to obtain liquid 1, (ii)                  adding alkaline pH adjuster into liquid 1, and                  adjusting the pH of the liquid 1 to 9-11 to                  obtain liquid 2, and (iii) mixing white oil and                  emulsifier and stirring to obtain oil phase, then                  adding liquid 2 into the oil phase, and adding                  initiator under inert gas atmosphere, carrying                  out polymerization by heating, and crushing to                  obtain gel particles.

TI Manufacture of acetic acid involves setting            operating conditions including hydrogen partial            pressure, carbon dioxide partial pressure and operating            temperature, and controlling gas- and/or liquid-phase            oxygen concentrations to preset value.
AU SHIMIZU M
AE DAICEL CORP (DAIL-C)
   DAICEL CORP (DAIL-C)
   DAICEL CORP (DAIL-C)
   DAICEL CORP (DAIL-C)
   DAICEL CORP (DAIL-C)
GA 201998264Y
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of acetic acid involves setting                (i) operating conditions (a) including a hydrogen                partial pressure of less than 500 kPa (absolute                pressure), a carbon dioxide partial pressure of                less than 70 kPa (absolute pressure) and an                operating temperature of more than 150 degrees C,                or operating conditions (b) including a hydrogen                partial pressure of 5 kPa (absolute pressure) or                less, a carbon dioxide partial pressure of less                than 20 kPa (absolute pressure) and an operating                temperature of more than 100 degrees C, and                controlling (ii) a gas-phase oxygen concentration                to less than 7 vol.% and/or a liquid-phase oxygen                concentration to less than 7x 10-5 g/g.
   USE - Manufacture of acetic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and efficient                manufacture of acetic acid with improved purity,                reduced concentration of formic acid, suppressed                product discoloration, and suppressed localized                corrosion of an acetic acid manufacturing                apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the                manufacture of acetic acid.
   Reaction tank (1)
   Evaporation tank (2)
   Distilling columns (3,5,6)
   Ion-exchange resin column (7)
   Scrubber system (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The manufacture of acetic                  acid involves using a reaction mixture comprising                  50-90 %mass acetic acid, 200-10000 mass ppm metal                  catalyst in terms of metal, 1-20 %mass methyl                  iodide, 1-25 %mass ionic iodide, 0.1-15 %mass                  water, 0.1-30 %mass methyl acetate, and 102 mass                  ppm or less formic acid, reacting under the                  operating conditions (a), and performing                  evaporation or distillation satisfying the                  operation conditions (b). In the evaporation                  process, the preparation liquid charged into an                  evaporation tank (2) comprises 50-90 %mass acetic                  acid, 200-10000 mass ppm metal catalyst in terms                  of metal, 1-20 %mass methyl iodide, 1-25 %mass                  ionic iodide, 0.1-15 %mass water, 0.1-30 %mass                  methyl acetate, and 10000 mass ppm or less formic                  acid. In the distillation process, the                  preparation liquid charged into a distillation                  column (3,5,6) comprises 40-85 %mass acetic acid,                  2-50 %mass methyl iodide, 0.2-20 %mass water,                  0.2-50 %mass methyl acetate, and 5-10000 mass ppm                  formic acid, or the preparation liquid charged                  into the distillation column comprises 80-99.9                  %mass acetic acid, 0.01-16 %mass methyl iodide,                  0.05-18 %mass water, 0.01-16 %mass methyl                  acetate, and 5-10000 mass ppm formic acid, or the                  preparation liquid charged into the distillation                  column comprises 99.1-99.999 %mass acetic acid                  and 5-9000 mass ppm formic acid. The process (ii)                  involves introducing component(s) chosen from                  oxygen-containing gas, oxygen-containing                  compound, and oxygen generator, and controlling                  the oxygen concentration in the gas phase to 1                  volume ppt or less and/or controlling the oxygen                  concentration in the liquid phase to 0.1x 10-9                  g/g or more. In the process (ii), the oxygen                  concentration is controlled to a concentration of                  0.25 mol or less with respect to 1 mol of the                  total amount of hydrogen iodide and methyl                  iodide. The manufacture of acetic acid involves                  performing carbonylation reaction by reacting                  methanol with carbon monoxide under the operating                  conditions (a) to produce a reaction mixture                  comprising acetic acid, evaporating the reaction                  mixture and separating into a vapor stream and a                  residual liquid stream, subjecting the vapor                  stream to distillation under operating conditions                  (b) to separate into an overhead stream rich in                  low-boiling components and an acetic acid stream                  (s1) rich in acetic acid, distilling the stream                  (s1) to separate into a water-rich overhead                  stream and an acetic acid stream (s2) enriched in                  acetic acid than the stream (s1), degassing the                  stream (s1) or (s2) to separate into a stream                  rich in high-boiling components and an acetic                  acid stream (s3) enriched in acetic acid than the                  stream (s2), processing the stream (s1), (s2) or                  (s3) with an ion-exchange resin to obtain an                  acetic acid stream (s4), and distilling the                  stream (s1), (s2), (s3) or (s4) to obtain an                  acetic acid stream (s5) enriched in acetic acid                  over the acetic acid stream before subjecting to                  distillation, and recycling a process liquid                  comprising 10 mass ppm or more formic acid                  concentration to a process that satisfies the                  operating conditions including a hydrogen partial                  pressure of less than 500 kPa (absolute                  pressure), a carbon dioxide partial pressure of                  less than 70 kPa (absolute pressure), and an                  operating temperature of more than 100 degrees C.                  The residence time in the process that satisfies                  the operation conditions (a) or the process that                  satisfies the operation conditions (b) is 1                  minute or more. Preferred Composition: The ratio                  of oxygen with respect to carbon monoxide in the                  gas phase in the process (ii) is 2 vol.% or less.                  The ratio of oxygen with respect to carbon                  monoxide in the liquid phase in the process (ii)                  is 2 vol.% or less.

TI Recovering extracted component by introducing            supercritical carbon dioxide and modifier to container            containing sample, introducing extracted components to            trap column, collecting components, introducing eluate            to trap column and eluting.
AU FUJITO Y
   ISHII R
   BUTSUGAN M
   SAKURAI K
   RISA I
   MICHIO B
   KEITA S
AE SHIMADZU CORP (SHMA-C)
   SHIMADZU CORP (SHMA-C)
   HITACHI CHEM TECHNO SERVICE CO LTD (HITB-C)
   SHIMADZU CORP (SHMA-C)
   SHIMADZU CORP (SHMA-C)
   SHOWA DENKO MATERIALS TECHNO SERVICE CO (SHOW-C)
GA 201986315P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recovering extracted component,                involves (a) introducing supercritical carbon                dioxide and modifier to a container which is placed                on an upstream of a back pressure control valve and                containing the sample, and extracting a component                contained in the sample, (b) introducing the                extracted components into a trap column together                with carbon dioxide and modifier, collecting the                components with a trap column, and introducing the                eluate into the trap column, (c) eluting the                components, collecting the eluted components, and                filling the trap column with polymer beads as a                filler.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering extracted                components contained in biological samples such as                blood, agricultural products, food, and soil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method extracts target component from wide                variety of analytical samples with high efficiency,                and has strong diffusion power into sample,                exhibits excellent solubility in many substances,                improves the extraction efficiency, and                simultaneously extracts wide range of substances                from low to high polar substances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                analysis method, involving (i) performing the                above-mentioned method, and (ii) analyzing the                eluted components by a chromatography and/or mass                spectrometry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The components extracted                  from the sample are collected in the trap column,                  where the trap column is placed on the upstream                  of the back pressure control valve.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The polymer beads                  contain a crosslinked polymer, where the degree                  of swelling when absorbing tetrahydrofuran, and                  the degree of swelling when absorbing methanol                  are 1.4 or less. PT P
PN CN110180592-A; CN110180592-B
TI Preparing catalyst useful for carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction comprises reacting tri(2-chloroisopropyl) phosphate and its metal ion with sodium hydroxide solid, centrifuging, washing, carrying out calcination and hydrogen reduction.
AU ZHAN G
   ZHAO F
   HUA D
   ZHOU S
AE UNIV HUAQIAO (UYHQ-C)
GA 2019760958
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst comprises (a) adding Tri(2-Chloroisopropyl) Phosphate (TCPP) or Tri(2-Chloroisopropyl) phosphate transition metal ion (TCPP (M)) in a container, adding deionizing water and sodium hydroxide solid, stirring at 20-50 degrees C under an inert protective atmosphere and reacting for 24 hours, centrifuging the obtained solid product, washing and drying to obtain an anionic ligand sodium-tri(2-chloroisopropyl) phosphate or sodium-tri(2-chloroisopropyl) phosphate metal ion, (b) taking copper aluminum-lactate dehydrogenase and anionic ligand sodium-tri(2-chloroisopropyl) phosphate metal ion or sodium-tri(2-chloroisopropyl) phosphate of step (a) in a container, adding deionized water in an inert protective atmosphere and 60-10 degrees C, stirring and reacting for 6-12 hours, completing reaction, subjecting the obtained solid product to centrifugation, washing, drying, carrying out high-temperature calcination and hydrogen reduction treatment to obtain the product.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, has good repeatability; Catalyst: has uniform composition, high active component dispersion, high stability and high carbon dioxide conversion rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) further comprises (i) placing the TCPP (M) and acetate solid in container and introducing protective gas to create an inert protective atmosphere, (ii) adding organic solvent to vessel in step (i), stirring until dissolved to obtain mixed solution, reacting mixed solution in an inert protective atmosphere at 80-200 degrees C for 3-10 hours, completing the reaction, centrifuging the obtained solid product, washing, drying to obtain TCPP containing transition metal ion and designating as TCPP (M). The calcination temperature in high-temperature calcination of step (b) is at 200-500 degrees C for 1-10 hours. The reduction temperature in the hydrogen reduction process of step (b) is 250-350 degrees C for 1-5 hours. The copper aluminum-lactate dehydrogenase in step (b) is obtained by a co-deposition method, deposition precipitation method, sol-gel method or a method. Preferred Components: The acetate solid in step (i) is transition metal ion acetate, zinc acetate, cobalt acetate, nickel acetate, iron acetate and/or ferrous acetate. The organic solvent in step (b) is N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-diethylformamide, N,N-dibutylformamide and/or N,N-dimethylpropionamide are mixed. The inert protective atmosphere in step (i) and (ii) is a nitrogen atmosphere. Preferred Composition: The ratio of copper aluminum-lactate dehydrogenase and anionic ligand sodium- tri(2-chloroisopropyl) phosphate metal ion in step (b) is 1:0.25-2, and the ratio of copper aluminum-lactate dehydrogenase and anionic ligand sodium-tri(2-chloroisopropyl) phosphate is 1:0.25-2.

PT P
PN PL420166-A1
TI Method for sorption of carbon dioxide from air, involves utilizing potassium modified titanium dioxide as adsorbent, modifying and mixing with potassium hydroxide solution, followed by washing with distilled water and drying.
AU MORAWSKI W A
   KAPICA-KOZAR J
   PIROG E
   NARKIEWICZ U
   WROBEL R
   MICHALKIEWICZ B
AE UNIV ZACHODNIOPOMORSKI TECHNOLOGICZNY (UZAC-C)
GA 201862336Y
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide sorption method involves utilizing potassium modified titanium dioxide as adsorbent, modifying, mixing with 10 M potassium hydroxide solution in a weight ratio of 1:20 until a homogeneous suspension is obtained, reheating the material at 140 degrees C and spontaneous cooling to room temperature to obtain suspension. The suspension is washed several times with 0.1 M hydrochloric acid solution under constant stirring until the pH is strongly acidic, washed with distilled water until neutral pH is obtained to get nanomaterial. The nanomaterial is dried in an oven in an air atmosphere at 350 degrees C for 2 hours and milled to obtain a milled material, followed by reacting titanium dioxide with an anatase of 100% and 1.06% carbon for the hydrothermal reaction, heating the milled material at 140 degrees C for 12, 24 or 48 hours to obtain adsorbent, modifying the adsorbent by adding to a solution of tetraethylenepentamine and water and stirring for 6 hours and drying at 80 degrees C for 2 hours.
   USE - Method for sorption of carbon dioxide from air.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Preparing lithium difluorophosphate involves performing purification of raw materials by introducing industrial grade lithium carbonate in carbonization tower, adding carbon dioxide for carbonization treatment, removing impurities.
AU GU F
AE CHANGZHOU HONGZHENG NEW ENERGY CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201846453H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lithium difluorophosphate involves performing purification of raw materials by introducing industrial grade lithium carbonate in a carbonization tower, adding carbon dioxide for carbonization treatment. The impurities of carbon dioxide whihc are insoluble are removed, then dried and pulverized to obtain high-purity lithium carbonate having particle size of less than 1000 mesh. The lithium hexafluorophosphate is slowly added to the purified ester solvent to control the concentration to 0.2 to 1.5 moles/L, at 0 to 30 degrees C for 1 to 2 hours, ultrapure water is added to the completely dissolved solution. 0.01 to 0.5% ultrapure water is added at 40-100 degrees C. The high-purity lithium carbonate is added to lithium hexafluorophosphate in mass ratio of 0.98-1.07:1, and reaction is performed for 1 to 2 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing lithium difluorophosphate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables prepare lithium difluorophosphate in simple manner, which has high purity and high yield, and is easy to obtain and suitable for industrial production, which is worthy of promotion.

AB    NOVELTY - Oral liquid comprises 6-15 pts. wt. ginger extract, 615 pts. wt. fennel extract, 5-15 pts. wt. liquorice juice, 8-20 pts. wt. jujube juice and 5-20 pts. wt. honey.
   USE - The oral liquid is useful for treating infantile cold and abdominal pain (claimed), and conditioning spleen.
   ADVANTAGE - The oral liquid is convenient to take.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the oral liquid, comprising (i) crushing ginger and fennel, respectively and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain ginger extract and fennel extract, where the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process conditions are as follows: extraction pressure is 25-30 MPa, extraction temperature is 40-60 degrees C, extraction time is 150-200min and carbon dioxide flow is 380-390 kg/hour; (ii) soaking the licorice in water for 30-60 minutes, boiling for 3 times, combining decoction, filtering, and concentrating to obtain licorice juice, and soaking jujube in water for 30-60 minutes, boiling for 3 times, combining decoction, filtering, concentrating to obtain jujube juice; and (iii) combining the ginger extract, fennel extract, licorice juice and jujube juice, mixing with honey, adding auxiliary material, stirring uniformly and filtering.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The oral liquid comprises 10 pts. wt. ginger extract, 9 pts. wt. fennel extract, 8 pts. wt. liquorice juice, 9 pts. wt. jujube juice and 10 pts. wt. honey. The composition further comprises auxiliary material, where the auxiliary material is pure water, glycerin, propylene glycol, sodium citrate, sorbic acid, potassium sorbate, citric acid, benzoic acid, sodium benzoate, Nipagin (RTM: Antimicrobial agent), xanthan gum, EDTA, sodium bicarbonate and/or flavoring agent. The oral liquid comprises 0.6-1.5 mg/ml ginger extract, 0.6-1.5 mg/ml fennel extract, 0.5-1.5 mg/ml licorice juice, 0.8-2.0 mg/ml jujube juice and 0.5-2.0 mg/ml honey. Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises: (i) cleaning ginger and fennel, slicing ginger, drying in drying oven at 50 degrees C, and crushing ginger and fennel and sieving through 60-mesh sieve, respectively, and carrying out supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction separately, where the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process conditions as follows: extraction pressure is 29 MPa, extraction temperature is 50 degrees C, extraction time is 180 minutes, and carbon dioxide flow rate is 380 kg/hour; (ii) soaking licorice in water for 30-60 minutes, boiling for 3 times, combining decoction, filtering, and concentrating to 50% of the original volume of the filtrate to obtain licorice juice, and soaking jujube in water for 30-60 minutes, boiling for 3 times, combining decoction, filtering, concentrating to 50% of the original volume of the filtrate to obtain jujube juice; and (iii) combining the ginger extract, fennel extract, licorice juice and jujube juice, mixing with honey, adding auxiliary material with pure water, stirring uniformly, filtering, dividing the filtrate into brown bottles and sterilizing.
   ACTIVITY - Virucide; Analgesic.

AB    NOVELTY - The mosquito killer has a housing (1) and a control unit. The housing is provided on the side wall with a first passage opening (101) through which mosquitoes are flown. The carbon dioxide energy box, a fan and an electrically charged grid unit are located inside the housing. The control unit is provided for controlling the operation of the carbon dioxide energy box, the fan and the grid unit.
   USE - Mosquito killer.
   ADVANTAGE - The mosquito repellent effect can be improved, and the mosquitoes escape from the mosquito killer can be effectively prevented. The user against mosquito bites can be protected. The upper housing portion and the bottom portion can be assembled more conveniently, so that the simple repair and easy replacement of internal components can be guaranteed. The energy saving and emission reduction of the mosquito killer can be achieved, and easy replacement of consumable material can be allowed. The attractive effect on the mosquitoes can be increased.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the mosquito killer.
