TI Preparing polycarbonate ether polyol, comprises            e.g. mixing catalyst of metal complexes, double metal            cyanide catalyst and optionally carbon dioxide and/or            solvent with epoxide and starter compound and/or carbon            dioxide to form mixture.
AU KEMBER M
   LEELAND J
   KABIR R
   HOLLIS E
AE ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
   ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
   ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
   ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
GA 201937538W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (M1) a polycarbonate ether polyol,                comprises: (Ia) mixing catalyst of metal complexes                (I), double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst and                optionally carbon dioxide and/or solvent with                epoxide and optionally starter compound and/or                carbon dioxide to form mixture ( alpha ); or (b)                mixing DMC catalyst and optionally starter                compound, carbon dioxide and/or solvent with                epoxide and optionally carbon dioxide and/or                solvent to form mixture ( alpha ); or (c) mixing                epoxide, catalyst of (I), starter compound and                carbon dioxide and optionally solvent to form                mixture ( alpha ); or (d) mixing catalyst of (I),                DMC catalyst and optionally starter compound,                epoxide, carbon dioxide and/or solvent to form                mixture ( alpha ); and (Ib) adding at least one of                starter compound, epoxide, carbon dioxide, catalyst                of (I), DMC catalyst and/or solvent to mixture (a)                to form mixture ( beta ) comprising starter                compound, epoxide, carbon dioxide, catalyst of (I),                DMC catalyst and optionally solvent.
   USE - The method (M1) is useful for preparing                polycarbonate ether polyol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method (M1): increases activity of the                catalyst, thus leading to a more efficient process                as compared with conventional methods in which all                of the materials provided at the start of the                reaction; utilizes the complexing agent that                increases or decreases the crystallinity of the DMC                catalyst; and increases process safety,                productivity and product design.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing (M1) a polycarbonate ether polyol,                comprises: (a) mixing catalyst of metal complexes                of formula (I), double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst                and optionally carbon dioxide and/or solvent with                epoxide and optionally starter compound and/or                carbon dioxide to form mixture ( alpha ); or (b)                mixing DMC catalyst and optionally starter                compound, carbon dioxide and/or solvent with                epoxide and optionally carbon dioxide and/or                solvent to form mixture ( alpha ); or (c) mixing                epoxide, catalyst of (I), starter compound and                carbon dioxide and optionally solvent to form                mixture ( alpha ); or (d) mixing catalyst of (I),                DMC catalyst and optionally starter compound,                epoxide, carbon dioxide and/or solvent to form                mixture ( alpha ); and adding at least one of                starter compound, epoxide, carbon dioxide, catalyst                of (I), DMC catalyst and/or solvent to mixture (a)                to form mixture ( beta ) comprising starter                compound, epoxide, carbon dioxide, catalyst of (I),                DMC catalyst and optionally solvent.
   M1, M2=Zn(ll), Cr(ll), Co(ll), Cu(ll), Mn(ll),                Mg(ll), Ni(ll), Fe(ll), Ti(ll), V(ll), Cr(lll)-X,                Co(lll)-X, Mn(lll)-X, Ni(lll)-X, Fe(lll)-X, Ca(ll),                Ge(ll), Al(lll)-X, Ti(lll)-X, V(lll)-X, Ge(IV)-(X)2                or Ti(IV)-(X)2;
   R1, R2=alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, haloalkyl,                aryl, heteroaryl, alkoxy, aryloxy, alkylthio,                arylthio, alicyclic or heteroalicyclic group (all                optionally substituted), H, halide, a NO2, a                nitrile group, an imine, NH2, an ether group, a                silyl group, a silyl ether group, a sulfoxide                group, a sulfonyl group, a sulfinate group or an                acetylide group;
   R3=alkylene, alkenylene, alkynylene,                heteroalkylene, heteroalkenylene, heteroalkynylene,                arylene, heteroarylene or cycloalkylene (all                optionally substituted by aryl, heteroaryl,                alicyclic or heteroalicyclic);
   R5=aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic,                heteroalicyclic, aryl, heteroaryl, alkylheteroaryl                or alkylaryl (all optionally substituted) or                H;
   E1=C or N;
   E2=O, S or NH;
   E3-E6=N, NR4, O or S, when E3-E6 are N and                when E3-E6 are NR4, O or S;
   R4=aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic,                heteroalicyclic, aryl, heteroaryl, alkylheteroaryl                (all optionally substituted), -alkyl-C(O)OR19 or                -alkyl-CN, alkylaryl or H;
   X=aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic,                heteroalicyclic, aryl or heteroaryl (all optionally                substituted), OC(O)R1x, OSO2R1x, OSOR1x, OSO(R1x)2,                S(O)R1x, OR1x, phosphinate, halide, nitrate, OH,                carbonate, amino or amido, where each X may be the                same or different and X may form a bridge between                M1 and M2;
   R1x=aliphatic, haloaliphatic, heteroaliphatic,                alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl, alkylaryl or                heteroaryl (all optionally substituted) or H;                and
   G=absent or a neutral or anionic donor ligand                which is a Lewis base.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing a high molecular weight                polyether carbonate, comprising (IAA) (a1) mixing                catalyst of (I), DMC catalyst and optionally carbon                dioxide and/or solvent with epoxide and optionally                carbon dioxide to form mixture ( alpha ), or (b1)                mixing DMC catalyst and optionally carbon dioxide                and/or solvent with epoxide and optionally carbon                dioxide and/or solvent to form mixture ( alpha ),                or (c1) mixing epoxide, catalyst of (I) and carbon                dioxide and optionally solvent to form mixture (                alpha ), or (d1) mixing catalyst of (I), DMC                catalyst and optionally epoxide, carbon dioxide                and/or solvent to form mixture ( alpha ), and                (IIAA) adding at least one of epoxide, carbon                dioxide, catalyst of (I), DMC catalyst and/or                solvent to mixture ( alpha ) to form mixture ( beta                ) comprising epoxide, carbon dioxide, catalyst of                (I), DMC catalyst and optionally solvent;
   (2) a product formed by the method (M1) or                (M2);
   (3) a polycarbonate ether polyol or polyether                carbonate produced by the method (M1) or (M2);                and
   (4) a higher polymer produced from the                polycarbonate ether polyol or polyether                carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst of (I)                  has a symmetric or asymmetric macrocyclic ligand.                  The catalyst of (I) comprises substituted metal                  complexes of formula (IV)-(VIII). The DMC                  catalyst, in addition to at least two metal                  centres and cyanide ligands, also comprises at                  least one of complexing agents, water, a metal                  salt and/or an acid, optionally in                  non-stoichiometric quantities. The DMC catalyst                  is prepared by treating a solution of a metal                  salt with a solution of a metal cyanide salt in                  the presence of at least one of complexing agent,                  water and/or an acid, optionally the metal salt                  comprises metal complexes (M1a(X1)p) (IX) and the                  metal cyanide salt comprises substituted metal                  cyanide salts ((Y1)qM2a(CN)(A)c) (X), the                  complexing agent comprises a (poly)ether, a                  polyether carbonate, a polycarbonate, a                  poly(tetramethylene ether diol), a ketone, an                  ester, an amide, an alcohol and/or a urea,                  optionally comprising propylene glycol,                  polypropylene glycol, (m)ethoxy ethylene glycol,                  dimethoxyethane, tert-butyl alcohol, ethylene                  glycol monomethyl ether, diglyme, triglyme,                  methanol, ethanol, isopropyl alcohol, n- butyl                  alcohol, isobutyl alcohol and sec-butyl alcohol,                  3-buten-1-ol, 2-methyl-3-buten-2-ol,                  2-methyl-3-butyn-2-ol and/or                  3-methyl-1-pentyn-3-ol, and the acid, if present,                  has substituted acids (HrX3) (XI). The DMC                  catalyst comprises substituted metal complexes                  (M1ad(M2ae(CN)f)g) (XII).M1a=Zn(ll) (preferred), Ru(ll), Ru(lll),                  Fe(Il), Ni(ll), Mn(ll), Co(ll), Sn(ll), Pb(ll),                  Fe(lll), Mo(IV), Mo(VI), Al(lll), V(V), V(VI),                  Sr(ll), W(IV), W(VI), Cu(ll) or Cr(lll);X1=anion comprising halide, oxide,                  hydroxide, sulfate, carbonate, cyanide, oxalate,                  thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate,                  carboxylate or nitrate;p=1 or more and the charge on the anion                  multiplied by p satisfies the valency of                  M1;M2a=Co(ll), Co(lll) (both preferred),                  Fe(ll), Fe(lll), Cr(ll), Cr(III), Mn(ll),                  Mn(lll), Ir(lll), Ni(ll), Rh(lll), Ru(ll), V(IV)                  or V(V);Y1=proton or alkali metal ion or alkaline                  earth metal ion (e.g. K+);A=anion comprising halide, oxide, hydroxide,                  sulfate, cyanide oxalate, thiocyanate,                  isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate or                  nitrate;q, b=1 or more;c=0 or 1 or more, where the sum of the                  charges on the anions Y1, CN and A multiplied by                  q, b and c respectively satisfies the valency of                  M2a;X3=anion comprising halide, sulfate,                  phosphate, borate, chlorate, carbonate, cyanide,                  oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate,                  carboxylate or nitrate;r=integer corresponding to the charge on the                  counterion X3;d=3;e=1;f=6; andg=2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the method (M1): the                  mixture (a) is held at 50-150 degrees C prior to                  step (Ia), optionally 80-130 degrees C; the                  mixture (a) is held at 0-120 degrees C prior to                  step (Ia), optionally 50-90 degrees C; the                  mixture (a) is held for at least 1 minute prior                  to step (Ia), optionally at least 5 or at least                  16 hours; the mixture (a) comprises less than 1                  wt.% water, optionally 0 wt.% water; the step                  (Ia) comprises firstly mixing catalyst of (I),                  DMC catalyst and optionally carbon dioxide to                  form mixture ( alpha 1), and subsequently adding                  epoxide and optionally starter compound and/or                  carbon dioxide to form mixture ( alpha ); the                  mixture ( alpha 1) is held at 0-250 degrees C                  prior to the subsequently adding, optionally                  80-130 degrees C; the step (Ib) comprises mixing                  DMC catalyst, epoxide, and optionally starter                  compound, carbon dioxide and/or solvent to form a                  pre-activated mixture and adding the                  pre-activated mixture to mixture ( alpha ) to                  form mixture ( beta ); the pre-activated mixture                  is held at 50-110 degrees C prior to adding,                  optionally 60-90 degrees C; the step (III) is                  conducted so that the molar ratio or weight ratio                  of epoxide, starter compound, carbon dioxide                  and/or solvent to catalyst of (I) in mixture (                  gamma ) does not fall below 75% of the                  predetermined molar or weight ratio; the step                  (III) is conducted so that the quantity of                  catalyst of (I) in the mixture ( gamma ) does not                  fall below 50% of the predetermined quantity; the                  step (III) is conducted so that the quantity of                  DMC catalyst in mixture ( gamma ) does not fall                  below 50% of the predetermined quantity; there                  are two starter compounds in mixture ( beta ),                  where the starter compound in step (Ia) is a                  first starter compound and step (Ib) comprises                  (A) adding at least one of first starter                  compound, epoxide, carbon dioxide, catalyst of                  (I), DMC catalyst and/or solvent to mixture (                  alpha ), and (B1) adding a second starter                  compound and optionally epoxide, carbon dioxide,                  catalyst of (I), DMC catalyst and/or solvent to                  form mixture ( beta ) comprising first starter                  compound, second starter compound, epoxide,                  carbon dioxide, catalyst of (I), DMC catalyst and                  optionally solvent; the step (B1) is conducted at                  least 1 minute after step (A), optionally at                  least 5 hours; in mixture ( alpha ), the quantity                  of the catalyst of (I) and the quantity of the                  DMC catalyst are at a predetermined weight ratio                  of 300:1-1: 100 to one another, preferably                  5:1-1:5. In step (Ia), the DMC catalyst is                  dry-mixed with the other components; in step                  (Ia), the DMC catalyst is mixed as a slurry,                  where the slurry comprises DMC catalyst and the                  starter compound and/or solvent; in step (Ia),                  the catalyst of (I) is dry-mixed with the other                  components; in step (Ia), the catalyst of (I) is                  mixed as a solution, where the solution comprises                  the catalyst of (I) and at least one of the                  starter compound, epoxide and/or a solvent; the                  epoxide is added in step (Ib); the catalyst of                  (I) is added in step (Ib); the DMC catalyst is                  added in step (Ib) ; the starter compound is                  added in step (Ib); both epoxide and starter                  compound are added in step (Ib); epoxide,                  catalyst of (I), DMC catalyst and/or starter                  compound is continuously or discontinuously added                  in step (Ib); and the carbon dioxide is provided                  continuously. The method (M1): employs a total                  quantity of epoxide and 1-95% of the total                  quantity of epoxide is mixed in step (Ia), with                  the remainder added in step (Ib), optionally                  1-75%, preferably 5-20% being mixed in step (Ia);                  employs a total quantity of starter compound, and                  1-95% of the total quantity of starter compound                  is mixed in step (Ia), with the remainder added                  in step (Ib), optionally 1-75%, preferably 5-20%                  being mixed in step (Ia); employs a total                  quantity of catalyst of (I), and 1-100% of the                  total quantity of catalyst of (I) is mixed in                  step (Ia), with the remainder added in step (Ib),                  optionally 1-75%, preferably 5-20% being mixed in                  step (Ia); employs a total quantity of DMC                  catalyst and 1-100% of the total quantity of DMC                  catalyst mixed in step (Ia), with the remainder                  added in step (Ib), optionally 1-75%, preferably                  5-20% being mixed in step (Ia); employs a total                  quantity of carbon dioxide, and 1-100% of the                  total quantity of carbon dioxide is mixed in step                  (Ia), with the remainder added in step (Ib),                  optionally 1-75%, preferably 5-20% being mixed in                  step (Ia); employs a total quantity of solvent,                  and 1-100% of the total quantity of solvent is                  mixed in step (Ia), with the remainder added in                  step (Ib), optionally 1-75%, preferably 5-20%                  being mixed in step (Ia); is continuous, there is                  a predetermined molar ratio or weight ratio of                  epoxide to catalyst of formula (I) in mixture (                  beta ), where the method further comprises (III)                  adding epoxide to mixture ( beta ) to form                  mixture ( gamma ), where the epoxide is added at                  a quantity sufficient to bring the molar ratio or                  weight ratio of epoxide to catalyst of (I) in                  mixture ( gamma ) to at least 75% of the                  predetermined molar ratio or weight ratio,                  optionally step (III) is repeated; is continuous,                  there is a predetermined molar ratio or weight                  ratio of starter compound to catalyst of (I) in                  mixture ( beta ), and where the method further                  comprises (III) adding starter compound to                  mixture ( beta ) to form mixture ( gamma ), where                  the starter compound is added at a quantity                  sufficient to bring the molar ratio or weight                  ratio of starter compound to catalyst of (I) in                  mixture ( gamma ) to at least 75% of the                  predetermined molar ratio or weight ratio,                  optionally step (III) is repeated; is continuous,                  there is a predetermined molar ratio or weight                  ratio of carbon dioxide to catalyst of formula                  (I) in mixture ( beta ), where the method further                  comprises (III) adding carbon dioxide to mixture                  ( beta ) to form mixture ( gamma ), where the                  carbon dioxide is added at a quantity sufficient                  to bring the molar ratio or weight ratio of                  carbon dioxide to catalyst of (I) in mixture (                  gamma ) to at least 75% of the predetermined                  molar ratio or weight ratio, optionally step                  (III) is repeated; is continuous, there is a                  predetermined quantity of catalyst of (I) in                  mixture ( beta ), where the method further                  comprises (III) adding catalyst of (I) to mixture                  ( beta ) to form mixture ( gamma ), where the                  catalyst of (I) is added at a quantity sufficient                  to bring the molar ratio or weight ratio of                  catalyst of (I) in mixture ( gamma ) to 50-550%                  of the predetermined quantity, optionally step                  (III) is repeated; is continuous, there is a                  predetermined quantity of DMC catalyst in mixture                  ( beta ), where the method further comprises                  (III) adding DMC catalyst to mixture ( beta ) to                  form mixture ( gamma ), DMC catalyst is added in                  a quantity sufficient to bring the quantity of                  DMC catalyst in mixture ( gamma ) to 50-550% of                  the predetermined quantity, optionally step (III)                  is repeated; and is carried out at a pressure of                  1-60 bar carbon dioxide, optionally 1-5 bar. In                  the method (M2): the mixture ( alpha ) is held at                  50-110 degrees C prior to step (IIAA), optionally                  60-90 degrees C; the mixture ( alpha ) is held at                  0-120 degrees C prior to step (IIAA), optionally                  50-90 degrees C; the mixture ( alpha ) is held                  for at least 1 or at least 5 minutes prior to                  step (IIAA), optionally at least 5 or at least 16                  hours; the mixture ( alpha ) comprises less than                  1 wt.% water, optionally 0 wt.% water; the step                  (IAA) comprises firstly mixing catalyst of (I),                  DMC catalyst and optionally carbon dioxide to                  form mixture ( alpha 1), and subsequently adding                  epoxide and optionally carbon dioxide to form                  mixture ( alpha ); the mixture ( alpha 1) is held                  at 0-250 degrees C prior to the subsequently                  adding, optionally 80-130 degrees C; the step                  (IIAA) comprises mixing DMC catalyst, epoxide,                  and optionally carbon dioxide and/or solvent to                  form a pre-activated mixture and adding the                  pre-activated mixture to mixture ( alpha ) to                  form mixture ( beta ); the pre-activated mixture                  is held at 50-110 degrees C prior to adding,                  optionally 60-90 degrees C; the step (IIIa) is                  conducted so that the molar ratio or weight ratio                  of epoxide, carbon dioxide and/or solvent to                  catalyst of (I) in mixture ( gamma ) does not                  fall below 75% of the predetermined molar or                  weight ratio; the step (IIIa) is conducted so                  that the quantity of catalyst of (I) in the                  mixture ( gamma ) does not fall below 50% of the                  predetermined quantity; the step (IIIa) is                  conducted so that the quantity of DMC catalyst in                  mixture ( gamma ) does not fall below 50% of the                  predetermined quantity; in mixture ( alpha ), the                  quantity of the catalyst of (I) and the quantity                  of the DMC catalyst are at a predetermined weight                  ratio of 300:1-1:100 to one another, preferably                  5:1-1:5; in step (IAA), the DMC catalyst is                  dry-mixed with the other components; in step                  (IAA), the DMC catalyst is mixed as a slurry,                  where the slurry comprises DMC catalyst and                  solvent; in step (IAA), the catalyst of (I) is                  dry-mixed with the other components; in step                  (IAA), the catalyst of (I) is mixed as a                  solution, where the solution comprises the                  catalyst of (I) and at least one of the epoxide                  and/or a solvent; the epoxide is added in step                  (IIAA); the catalyst of (IAA) is added in step                  (IIAA); the DMC catalyst is added in step (IIAA);                  epoxide, catalyst of (I), DMC catalyst is                  continuously or discontinuously added in step                  (Ib); and the carbon dioxide is provided                  continuously. The method (M2): employs a total                  quantity of epoxide and 1-95% of the total                  quantity of epoxide is mixed in step (IAA), with                  the remainder added in step (IIAA), optionally                  1-75%, preferably 5-20% being mixed in step                  (IAA); employs a total quantity of catalyst of                  (I), and 1-100% of the total quantity of catalyst                  of (I) is mixed in step (IAA), with the remainder                  added in step (IIAA), optionally 1-75%,                  preferably 5-20% being mixed in step (IAA);                  employs a total quantity of DMC catalyst and                  1-100% of the total quantity of DMC catalyst                  mixed in step (IAA), with the remainder added in                  step (IIAA), optionally 1-75%, preferably 5-20%                  being mixed in step (IAA); employs a total                  quantity of carbon dioxide, and 1-100% of the                  total quantity of carbon dioxide is mixed in step                  (IAA), with the remainder added in step (IIAA),                  optionally 1-75%, preferably 5-20% being mixed in                  step (IAA); employs a total quantity of solvent,                  and 1-100% of the total quantity of solvent is                  mixed in step (IAA), with the remainder added in                  step (IIAA), optionally 1-75%, preferably 5-20%                  being mixed in step (IAA a); is continuous, there                  is a predetermined molar ratio or weight ratio of                  epoxide to catalyst of formula (I) in mixture (                  beta ), where the method further comprises (IIIa)                  adding epoxide to mixture ( beta ) to form                  mixture ( gamma ), where the epoxide is added at                  a quantity sufficient to bring the molar ratio or                  weight ratio of epoxide to catalyst of (I) in                  mixture ( gamma ) to at least 75% of the                  predetermined molar ratio or weight ratio,                  optionally step (IIIa) is repeated; is                  continuous, there is a predetermined molar ratio                  or weight ratio of carbon dioxide to catalyst of                  formula (I) in mixture ( beta ), where the method                  further comprises (IIIa) adding carbon dioxide to                  mixture ( beta ) to form mixture ( gamma ), where                  the carbon dioxide is added at a quantity                  sufficient to bring the molar ratio or weight                  ratio of carbon dioxide to catalyst of (I) in                  mixture ( gamma ) to at least 75% of the                  predetermined molar ratio or weight ratio,                  optionally step (IIIa) is repeated; is                  continuous, there is a predetermined molar ratio                  or weight ratio of solvent to catalyst of (I) in                  mixture ( beta ), where the method further                  comprises (IIIa) adding solvent to mixture ( beta                  ) to form mixture ( gamma ), the solvent is added                  in a quantity to bring the molar ratio or weight                  ratio of solvent to catalyst of (I) in mixture (                  gamma ) to at least 75% of the predetermined                  molar ratio, optionally step (IIIa) is repeated;                  is continuous, there is a predetermined quantity                  of catalyst of (I) in mixture ( beta ), where the                  method further comprises (IIIa) adding catalyst                  of (I) to mixture ( beta ) to form mixture (                  gamma ), where the catalyst of (I) is added at a                  quantity to bring the molar ratio or weight ratio                  of catalyst of (I) in mixture ( gamma ) to                  50-550% of the predetermined quantity, optionally                  step (IIIa) is repeated; is continuous, there is                  a predetermined quantity of DMC catalyst in                  mixture ( beta ), where the method further                  comprises (IIIa) adding DMC catalyst to mixture (                  beta ) to form mixture ( gamma ), DMC catalyst is                  added in a quantity to bring the quantity of DMC                  catalyst in mixture ( gamma ) to 50-550% of the                  predetermined quantity, optionally step (IIIa) is                  repeated; and is carried out at a pressure of                  1-60 bar carbon dioxide, optionally 1-5 bar. In                  the methods (M1-M2), the temperature of the                  reaction increases during the course of the                  method. The methods (M1-M2) are carried out on an                  industrial scale. Preferred Components: The first                  starter compound has a molecular weight of at                  least 200 Da and the second starter compound has                  a molecular weight of at most 200 Da. The second                  starter compound comprises polypropylene glycol                  having a molecular weight of 200-1000 Da,                  optionally 400 Da. Each starter compound has at                  least two hydroxy groups, optionally at least                  eight hydroxy groups. Each starter compound has                  substituted alkyl compounds (Z-(R1z)a) (IA),                  preferably 1,2-ethanediol (ethylene glycol),                  1-2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol (propylene                  glycol), 1,2-butanediol, 1,3-butanediol,                  1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol, 1,6-hexanediol,                  1,8-octanediol, 1,10-decanediol,                  1,4-cyclohexanediol, 1,2-diphenol, 1,3-diphenol,                  1,4-diphenol, neopentyl glycol, catechol,                  cyclohexenediol, 1,4-cyclohexanedimethanol,                  dipropylene glycol, diethylene glycol,                  tripropylene glycol, triethylene glycol,                  tetraethylene glycol, polypropylene glycols                  (PPGs) or polyethylene glycols (PEGs) having an                  number average molecular weight of up to 1500                  g/mol e.g. PPG 425, PPG 725, PPG 1000, triols                  e.g. glycerol, benzenetriol, 1,2,4-butanetriol,                  1,2,6-hexanetriol, tris(methylalcohol)propane,                  tris(methylalcohol)ethane,                  tris(methylalcohol)nitropropane, trimethylol                  propane, polypropylene oxide triols, polyester                  triols, calix(4)arene,                  2,2-bis(methylalcohol)-1,3-propanediol,                  erythritol, pentaerythritol, sorbitol, oxalic                  acid, malonic acid, succinic acid, glutaric acid,                  adipic acid, pimelic acid, suberic acid, azelaic                  acid, sebacic acid, undecanedioic acid,                  dodecanedioic acid, lactic acid, glycolic acid,                  3-hydroxypropanoic acid, 4-hydroxybutanoic acid,                  5-hydroxypentanoic acid, ethanolamine,                  diethanolamine, methyl diethanolamine or phenyl                  diethanolamine.Z=any group which can have 2 or more, where                  R1z groups attached to it;R1z=-OH, -NHR1a, -SH, -C(O)OH,                  -P(O)(OR1a)(OH), -PR1a(0)(OH)2 or                  -PR1a(O)OH;R1a=alkyl, heteroalkyl, aryl, heteroaryl,                  cycloalkyl or heterocycloalkyl (all optionally                  substituted) or H; anda=at least 2.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M1, M2=Mg(ll), Zn(ll) or Ni(ll);
   X=OC(O)R1x, OSO2R1x, OS(O)R1x, OSO(R1x)2,                S(O)R1x, OR1x, halide, nitrate, carbonate, amino,                nitro, amido, alkyl, heteroalkyl, aryl or                heteroaryl;
   R1x=alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, heteroalkyl,                aryl, heteroaryl, cycloalkyl or alkylaryl (all                optionally substituted), where each X may be the                same or different and where X may form a bridge                between M1 and M2;
   E3-E6=NR4;
   R4=H, Me, Et, Bn, iPr, tBu, Ph or                -CH2-(pyridine);
   E1, E2=O; and
   R5, R2=H.
   EXAMPLE - 10.3 mg Double metal cyanide catalyst was                taken into a 100 ml oven dried reactor along with                2.615 g of dodecane diol (2.615 g). Mixture was                dried at 120 degrees C under vacuum for 1 hour. The                reactor was cooled down to room temperature and 15                ml of ethyl acetate was injected into the vessel                via a syringe under continuous flow of carbon                dioxide gas. The vessel was heated to the desired                temperature (130 degrees C) and filled to the                desired carbon dioxide pressure (5 bar). 6% 1.49 g                Total propylene oxide was added in 3 bursts (2 wt.%                each, 0.5 g) with 30 minutes between each. Reactor                was cooled to 70 degrees C, then 103 mg of catalyst                dissolved in 5 ml of ethyl acetate and 20 wt.% 4.98                g of propylene oxide was added via a HPLC pump. The                remaining 74 wt.% 18.43 g of propylene oxide was                added over 3 hours. The reactor was cooled to below                10 degrees C and the pressure was released to                obtain polycarbonate ether polyol.

PN US2022325950-A1; KR2022139046-A
TI System for producing hydrogen by reforming liquid            natural gas, has circulation pump that is provided to            pump liquid carbon dioxide from cooler into storage            tank and circulate portion of liquid carbon oxide into            cooler.
AU KO D S
AE KIA CORP (KIAK-C)
   HYUNDAI MOTOR CO LTD (HYMR-C)
   HYUNDAI MOTOR CO LTD (HYMR-C)
   KIA CORP (KIAK-C)
GA 2022C92273
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a reformer (3) provided to                receive liquid natural gas (LNG) from an LNG tank                (1). A carbon dioxide pressure swing adsorption                (PSA) unit (5) extracts carbon dioxide from off-gas                generated from the reformer. A cooler (7) is                utilized to cool and liquefy the extracted carbon                dioxide using the liquid natural gas. A storage                tank (9) stores liquid carbon dioxide of the                cooler. A circulation pump (11) is utilized to pump                the carbon dioxide into the storage tank and                circulate a portion of the liquid carbon oxide into                the cooler. The cooler is provided with a cooling                coil (21).
   USE - System for producing hydrogen by reforming                LNG.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can effectively separate carbon                dioxide generated in the reforming process and                store the separated dioxide in a liquid state and                efficiently and stably maintain the pressure in the                space storing the liquid carbon dioxide, so that                the carbon dioxide can be easily stored for long                time and a space occupied by the system can be                reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for controlling a LNG reforming system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a system for producing hydrogen by reforming                LNG.
   1LNG tank
   2Cooling coil
   3Reformer
   5PSA unit
   7Cooler
   9Storage tank
   11Circulation pump

A desulfurizing device is connected with a filtering device of a filtering tar. A fluidized bed reactor outlet is connected with a carbon dioxide conversion unit. The carbon source gas discharged from air outlet of the fluidized bed reactor is sent back to carbon dioxide conversion unit for circulation.

PN CN112176320-A; CN112176320-B
TI Controllable growing of two-dimensional semiconductor film by dissolving excess metal precursor in supercritical carbon dioxide to reach saturation state, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide into nuclear reaction area, and growing film.
AU XU Q
   WANG Q
   YIN J
   LIU B
   WANG Z
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202106412Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for controllable growing of 2-dimensional semiconductor film involves (a) dissolving excess metal precursor in supercritical carbon dioxide to reach saturation state, (b) introducing supercritical carbon dioxide containing metal precursor, (c) introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide into a nuclear reaction zone at 40-80 degrees C and a pressure of 20-80 MPa, (d) growing a seed crystal on a carrier, (e) heating the carrier in a pressure-releasing nucleation reaction area, introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide and growing film, (f) introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide into the pressure releasing nucleation reaction area for recoiling, (g) adding non-metal precursors, (h) introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide into the pressure relief nucleation reaction zone for recoil, (i) relieving the pressure to generate a periodic film, and (j) repeating the steps (a)-(h) and forming film to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for controllable growing of 2-dimensional semiconductor film by supercritical carbon dioxide pulse.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases concentration of precursor, reduces growth temperature, environmental pollution and dosage of the precursor, improves growth efficiency, solves the problem that the chemical vapor deposition method has high reaction temperature, uncontrollable film quality and low thin film forming efficiency, and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method involves using a substrate comprising silicon, sapphire, silicon dioxide/silicon, graphene, mica or polyimide. The metal precursor comprises a compound containing molybdenum, tin, tantalum or tungsten. The non-metal precursor comprises an organic compound containing sulfur, selenium, and nitrogen.

TI Monitoring carbon dioxide storage based on            time-shifted full waveform inversion using electronic            device involves obtaining multiple time-lapse seismic            data, and determining diffusion situation of carbon            dioxide after full waveform inversion.
AU LI D
   PENG S
   GUO Y
   LU Y
   CUI X
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 202061054Q
AB    NOVELTY - Monitoring carbon dioxide storage based on                time-shifted full waveform inversion comprises (i)                obtaining multiple time-lapse seismic data from                different periods of the formation to be monitored                during storage of carbon dioxide; (ii) performing                curvelet transform operation on each time-shift                seismic data separately to obtain multiple curvelet                coefficients; (iii) performing a full waveform                inversion operation on the multiple time-shifted                seismic data based on the multiple curved wave                coefficients to obtain multiple target time-shifted                inversion results, where the time-shifted inversion                results of the multiple targets comprises formation                parameters of the formation to be monitored at                different periods in the process of storing carbon                dioxide; and (iv) determining the diffusion                situation of the carbon dioxide stored in the                formation to be monitored in different periods                based on the time-shifted inversion results of the                multiple targets.
   USE - The method is useful for monitoring carbon                dioxide storage based on time-shifted full waveform                inversion using electronic device (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                carbon dioxide storage monitoring system.

A cloud platform server stores data through a Wi-Fi or a GSM.

PN FI127925-B1; WO2019175476-A1; CA3093759-A1; EP3765404-A1; US2021246034-A1; EP3765404-A4
TI Method for producing carbon monoxide.
AU KAISALO N
   SIMELL P
   FRILUND C
   VIDAL VAZQUEZ F
   HANNULA L
   HANNULA I
   VIDAL V F
AE VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
   VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
   VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
GA 201950944A
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon monoxide is produced by supplying                oxygen to carbon dioxide stream for forming carbon                dioxide based mixture, supplying carbon dioxide                based mixture to hydrogen based stream to form                gaseous feed, supplying hydrocarbon containing                stream to hydrogen based stream before supply of                carbon dioxide based mixture, feeding gaseous feed                into a reactor which comprises greater than or                equal to 1 catalyst, treating gaseous feed by                partial oxidation in the reactor so that carbon                dioxide reacts with hydrogen in the reactor in the                presence of oxygen, and heat is formed during the                reaction, and recovering product composition                comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen from the                reactor.
   USE - Production of carbon monoxide for use in                production of hydrocarbons, Fischer-Tropsch                process, treatment of carbon dioxide, carbon                dioxide capture process, catalytic partial                oxidation process, methanation process, and/or                production of methanol (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide based feed can be treated and                converted easily and effectively. The partial                oxidation also brings the necessary heat for the                reaction in which carbon dioxide is converted to                carbon monoxide. When the oxygen is fed to carbon                dioxide stream, the reaction of the oxygen and the                burning can be prevented such that the reactions do                not take place too quickly. Then, it can be ensured                that the reactions take place with the catalyst in                the reactor, not before the catalyst.                Non-condensable products, such as off-gases, of the                Fischer-Tropsch (FT) process can be utilized and                recirculated to the partial oxidation process. By                recirculation, the yield of oils and waxes can be                improved in the FT process. Furthermore, the method                helps in controlling the carbon or coke formation                by using oxygen to partially oxidate the                hydrocarbons. The method and apparatus offer a                possibility to treat carbon dioxide and carbon                monoxide easily, and energy- and cost-effectively.                The method provides an industrially applicable,                simple, and affordable way to produce carbon                monoxide, and further to produce desired                hydrocarbons by FT reaction. The method and                apparatus are easy and simple to realize in                connection with production processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus                for producing carbon monoxide comprising feeding                devices for supplying oxygen to carbon dioxide                stream for forming carbon dioxide based mixture,                for supplying carbon dioxide based mixture to a                hydrogen based stream to form the gaseous feed, for                supplying hydrocarbon containing stream to the                hydrogen based stream before the supply of the                carbon dioxide based mixture, and for feeding the                gaseous feed into a reactor, greater than or equal                to 1 reactor which comprises greater than or equal                to 1 catalyst and to which the gaseous feed is                supplied and in which the gaseous feed is treated                by partial oxidation so that carbon dioxide reacts                with hydrogen in the reactor in the presence of                oxygen, and heat is formed during the reaction, and                greater than or equal to 1 recovering device for                recovering a product composition comprising carbon                monoxide and hydrogen from the reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: Catalyst is selected                  from rhodium/alumina (Rh/Al2O3) catalyst, nickel                  Rh/Al2O3 (NiRh/Al2O3) catalyst, and Ni/Al2O3                  catalyst. Preferred Processes: Product                  composition is cooled after the reactor. Product                  composition is supplied as the feed to a                  Fischer-Tropsch step. Off-gas stream comprising                  hydrocarbons is recycled from the Fischer-Tropsch                  step to be used as the hydrocarbon containing                  stream. Preferred Conditions: Gaseous feed is fed                  with high velocity to the reactor such that the                  velocity of the gas stream is greater than or                  equal to 0.5 m/s. Treatment temperature is                  800-1500 degrees C, preferably 800-950 degrees C,                  in the reactor. Pressure is 15-30 bar in the                  reactor. Fischer-Tropsch reaction is carried out                  at 150-350 degrees C and pressure of 15-25 bar.                  Preferred Devices: Reactor is a tube reactor or a                  tubular reactor. Reactor is a catalytic partial                  oxidation (CPOX) reactor. The apparatus comprises                  greater than or equal to 1 Fischer-Tropsch                  reactor into which the product composition is                  supplied. The apparatus comprises greater than or                  equal to 1 recirculation device for recycling an                  off gas stream comprising hydrocarbons from                  Fischer-Tropsch step to be used as the                  hydrocarbon containing stream.

PN CN212688117-U; CN113512622-A
TI Gas analysis based converter smelting full process            end point carbon dynamic control system for converter            steelmaking system, has converter smelting process            state and information detecting device connected with            converter base automatic programmable logic controller            device.
AU GAO Y
   GAO J
AE BEIJING KAIDE HENGYUAN TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202130326S
AB    (CN212688117-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a converter smelting                full process end carbon dynamic control system                based on gas analysis, belonging to the converter                smelting control technology; the system is provided                with a converter field gas detection probe,                collecting before each smelting; analyzing the gas                mol fraction for gas analysis in the air; ensuring                the accuracy of the gas analysis basic data;                constructing a converter smelting full process gas                analysis decarburization dynamic model;                constructing a dynamic end point carbon time                prediction model; The constructed system and                dynamic mathematical model overcomes the defect of                the existing technology, which can realize                converter smelting full process end carbon dynamic                control, widely suitable for new, various converter                steelmaking system for expansion or                reconstruction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Controlling of dynamic end-point carbon of the                converter smelting process based on gas analysis                involves installing converter site gas detection                probe in the system structure, collecting and                analyzing the gas mole fraction used for gas                analysis in the air before each smelting to ensure                the accuracy of the basic data of gas analysis,                constructing a dynamic model of gas analysis and                decarbonization in the whole process of converter                smelting, and determining the mole fraction of                carbon dioxide in the air entering the furnace                mouth by using the helium mole fraction obtained                through the analysis of flue gas detection gas. The                calculation of the dynamic model of gas analysis                and decarburization in the whole process of                converter smelting involves obtaining mole fraction                of carbon dioxide in the flue gas by the analysis                of flue gas detection gas minus the mole fraction                of carbon dioxide contained in the air entering the                furnace mouth, obtaining the total amount of the                mole fraction of carbon dioxide produced by the                smelting process and the mole fraction of carbon                dioxide produced by the combustion of the air                entering the furnace mouth is obtained, then adding                the total mole fraction of carbon dioxide and the                mole fraction of carbon monoxide in the flue gas                obtained from the flue gas detection gas analysis,                then multiplying the total amount of flue gas                measured to get the total amount of carbon monoxide                and carbon dioxide produced during the smelting                process, then calculating the amount of carbon                released in this oxygen blowing smelting process                based on this total amount, and then subtracting                the amount of carbon released this time from the                previous residual carbon in the molten steel, which                is equal to the amount of residual carbon in the                molten steel. The construction of a dynamic end                point carbon time prediction model involves                obtaining three sets of data after three iterations                of the converter smelting process gas analysis and                decarbonization dynamic model, then using the three                sets of data to construct a one-element quadratic                expression of the dynamic decarbonization process,                collecting new set of data after replacing the set                (S1) of data in the previous three sets of data,                and constructing a new one-dimensional quadratic                equation model to express the dynamic                decarbonization process, and then with the smelting                process continuously updating dynamic equation                model to form a dynamic curve of the dynamic                decarbonization process and be used to predict the                end point of carbon. The characteristic of the gas                analysis and decarburization dynamic model of the                converter smelting process is that the carbon                remaining in the molten steel can be dynamically                calculated during the entire smelting process. The                dynamic end-point carbon dynamic control of                converters of various scales and different smelting                processes is calculated using the carbon content in                the molten steel of the i-th calculation result                (Cpi), mole fraction of carbon dioxide in the flue                gas of the i-th gas analysis (Pgc2i), the mole                fraction of carbon dioxide in the air stored in the                gas analysis computer database (Pac2), mole                fraction of helium in the flue gas of the i-th gas                analysis (Pghi), the total flow of flue gas in the                i-th gas analysis (Qgi), helium mole fraction in                the air, constantly, stored in the gas analysis                computer database (Pah), the mole fraction of                carbon monoxide in the flue gas of the i-th gas                analysis (Pgc1i), weight of molten steel (G), and                the amount of carbon in the molten steel calculated                last time (Ci-1), satisfies the relation: Cpi =                (Ci-1-(Pgc2i-Pac2xPghi/Pah+Pgc1i)xQgix(12/22.4))/G                (1), and the value in the initial calculation is                calculated using carbon content in molten iron                stored in the gas analysis computer database (Ce),                carbon content in scrap steel stored in the gas                analysis computer database (Cs), the amount of                carbon in the pig iron block stored in the gas                analysis computer database (Cm), carbon content                correction value, input at the computer operation                station of the main control room of the converter                (Ca), satisfies the relation: Ci-1 = Ce+Cs+Cm+Ca                (2), and the dynamic end point carbon time                prediction model calculated using end-point carbon                content in molten steel (Cf), end point carbon                prediction time (t), constant (A, B, C), and                engineering coefficient (k), which is 0.5-1.5,                satisfies the relation: Cf = k(At2+Bt+C) (3).
EA    NOVELTY - Controlling of dynamic end-point carbon of the                converter smelting process based on gas analysis                involves determining the mole fraction of carbon                dioxide in the air entering the furnace mouth by                using the helium mole fraction. The dynamic                end-point carbon dynamic control of converters is                calculated using the carbon content in the molten                steel of the i-th calculation result (Cpi), mole                fraction of carbon dioxide in the flue gas of the                i-th gas analysis (Pgc2i), the mole fraction of                carbon dioxide in the air stored in the gas                analysis computer database (Pac2), mole fraction of                helium in the flue gas of the i-th gas analysis                (Pghi), the total flow of flue gas in the i-th gas                analysis (Qgi), helium mole fraction in the air                stored in the gas analysis computer database (Pah),                the mole fraction of carbon monoxide in the flue                gas of the i-th gas analysis (Pgc1i), weight of                molten steel (G), and the amount of carbon in the                molten steel calculated last time (Ci-1).
   USE - Controlling of dynamic end-point carbon of the                converter smelting process based on gas analysis                used in converter steelmaking system of new                construction, expansion or reconstruction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables analysis of the gas mol                fraction for gas in the air, thus ensuring the                accuracy of the gas analysis basic data. The method                allows the calculation of carbon quantity released                in the oxygen blowing smelting process, to reduce                the carbon amount in the steel liquid before the                residual carbon amount, which is equal to the                residual amount in a steel liquid, thus to                realizing automatic control of converter                steelmaking of various scales and different                operating conditions.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); T01 (Digital Computers); T06 (Process and Machine Control)
MC M24-B02C; M24-C06; M24-E; S03-E14P; T01-F06; T01-J04E; T06-A04B1; T06-A05; T06-A06; T06-A07B; T06-D10; M24-A05; T01-L02; T01-J05B4P

PN CN114810003-A
TI Numerical simulation efficient development thick            oil reservoir method involves selecting reservoir to            obtain specific parameters of reservoir, using            numerical simulation software to perform PVT fitting on            high-temperature and high-pressure physical property            experimental results.
AU CHEN Q
   PAN Y
   DU Q
   LIU Y
   HOU J
   LI X
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 2022A2034E
AB    NOVELTY - Numerical simulation efficient development                thick oil reservoir method involves selecting the                reservoir to obtain the specific parameters of the                reservoir. The reservoir depth is 1200m, the                thickness is 21m, the average permeability is                5-2000md, the viscosity of the formation crude oil                is greater than or equal to5000mPascal, the crude                oil density is 0.93g/cm3, using the numerical                simulation software to perform PVT fitting on the                high-temperature and high-pressure physical                property experimental results obtained from the                previous laboratory experiments, completing PVT                data, including critical temperature, critical                pressure, component density, specific gravity, mole                fraction, composition, thermal expansion                coefficient, compressibility coefficient and                corresponding viscosity at different temperatures,                firstly fitting the basic experimental data of                crude oil and the results of the swelling test, the                constant composition swelling experiment                (CCE).
   USE - Numerical simulation efficient development                thick oil reservoir method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method inhibits carbon dioxide extracting                thick oil extraction and light hydrocarbon                component volatilization, and nitrogen has the                effect of keeping pressure and heat insulation, to                improve the recovery rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Numerical simulation efficient development                thick oil reservoir method involves selecting the                reservoir to obtain the specific parameters of the                reservoir. The reservoir depth is 1200m, the                thickness is 21m, the average permeability is                5-2000md, the viscosity of the formation crude oil                is greater than or equal to5000mPascal, the crude                oil density is 0.93g/cm3, using the numerical                simulation software to perform PVT fitting on the                high-temperature and high-pressure physical                property experimental results obtained from the                previous laboratory experiments, completing PVT                data, including critical temperature, critical                pressure, component density, specific gravity, mole                fraction, composition, thermal expansion                coefficient, compressibility coefficient and                corresponding viscosity at different temperatures,                firstly fitting the basic experimental data of                crude oil and the results of the swelling test, the                constant composition swelling experiment (CCE) and                the saturation pressure experiment results after                carbon dioxide and nitrogen injection to obtain                basic physical parameters of crude oil, adding                n-hexane solvent component to the fluid                composition, dissolving n-hexane in heavy oil as an                auxiliary to dilute viscosity and reduce viscosity,                assist carbon dioxide dissolution, deriving the                basic parameters of each component and the                liquid/liquid under different temperature and                pressure conditions, and liquid K value and                gas-liquid K value provide basic parameters for                numerical simulation of thermochemical composite                flooding, adjusted the parameters of the EOS                equation of state to ensure that the calculated                results and laboratory measurement results can                match each other, then output the fitted EOS                equation of state to the component model as the                equation of state for component simulation,                adjusted parameters such as critical pressure,                critical temperature and eccentricity factor of                crude oil, while parameters of other components                (carbon dioxide, nitrogen and water) do not need to                be adjusted, and different properties of crude oil                are selected for adjustment during the fitting                process, fitted the viscosity and density of the                crude oil through the phase state fitting module,                and established a fluid property model with the                experimental error within the requirement of                fitting accuracy, established a reservoir                geological model, divided the grid into multiple                cells, and coarsened the grid, imported the fitted                PVT data into the reservoir geological model, and                inputted other reservoir parameters such as phase                permeability data, drilled a production well and an                injection well in the model, set parameters of the                injection well and the production well, and the                well spacing is 200 meters and designed the                injection scheme of n-hexane, steam, carbon                dioxide, nitrogen, single steam flooding, 70% mole                steam + 20% mole carbon dioxide + 10% mole nitrogen                flooding, 80% mole steam + 20% mole n-hexane                solvent flooding and 50% mole four injection                schemes of steam + 20% mole carbon dioxide + 10%                mole nitrogen + 20% mole n-hexane flooding. The                different injection schemes are used, injected four                1PV steam, carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas slugs                and n-hexane slugs into the oil reservoir                respectively through the steam flooding injection                method, injected 0.5PV n-hexane slugs, diluted                crude oil to reduce viscosity, then injected 1PV                steam slug to further reduce viscosity of heavy oil                1PV is injected into carbon dioxide slug to expand                crude oil to reduce viscosity and increased                mobility ratio. The 1PV nitrogen slug is injected                to maintain pressure and heat insulation and                controls the minimum bottom hole flow pressure of                20000KPa and the injection speed of 800m3/d, when                injecting gas into the injection well, opened the                production well for production, controlled the                bottom hole flow pressure to be 20000 KiloPascal,                the daily oil production is 200m3/d, and the well                is opened for 20 years of production to obtain the                cumulative oil production and the statistical chart                of the viscosity reduction law of different                schemes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reservoir depth of                  choosing oil reservoir is 1200m. The thickness is                  21m, the average permeability is 5-2000md, the                  viscosity of the crude oil in the formation is                  greater than or equal to5000mPascal, and the                  density of the crude oil is 0.93g/cm3. The method                  includes using numerical simulation software to                  obtain in the early stage laboratory experiment,                  fitting the experimental results of high                  temperature and high pressure physical                  properties, mainly by fitting the results of                  crude oil viscosity, steam injection expansion                  experiments to obtain complete PVT data,                  including critical temperature, critical                  pressure, component density, thermal expansion                  coefficient, compressibility coefficient and                  corresponding viscosity and other parameters at                  different temperatures. The basic experimental                  data of the crude oil are fitted with the                  experimental results of the swelling test after                  injecting carbon dioxide and nitrogen to obtain                  the basic physical parameters of the crude oil.                  The method includes adding n-hexane solvent                  component, using n-hexane as an additive to                  dissolve in heavy oil to dilute viscosity, assist                  carbon dioxide dissolution, and deriving the                  basic parameters of each component and the                  liquid/liquid K value and gas-liquid K value                  under different temperature and pressure                  conditions, which are the values of                  thermochemical composite flooding simulation                  provides basic parameters. The EOS equation of                  state parameter is adjusted to ensure that the                  calculation results obtained, and the laboratory                  measurements can be matched with each other. The                  critical temperature and eccentricity of crude                  oil parameters such as factors can be adjusted.                  The density is fitted, and the experimental error                  is within the requirements of the fitting                  accuracy. The conceptual model of oil reservoir                  is established, fitted the PVT phase state data                  is imported into the reservoir geological model.                  The method includes designing n-hexane, steam,                  carbon dioxide, nitrogen injection scheme, single                  steam flooding, 70% steam + 20% carbon dioxide +                  10% nitrogen flooding, 80% steam + 20% n-hexane                  solvent flooding and 50% steam + 20% carbon                  dioxide + 10% nitrogen + 20% n-hexane flooding                  four injection schemes, n-hexane as a solvent                  dissolved in crude oil can dilute the viscosity                  of heavy oil, and n-hexane can inhibit the                  extraction and extraction of heavy oil by carbon                  dioxide, inhibit the volatilization of light                  hydrocarbon components, and nitrogen has the                  effect of maintaining pressure and heat                  insulation. The different injection schemes are                  adopted through the injection mode of steam                  flooding, injected 4 1PV steam+ carbon dioxide +                  nitrogen gas slugs and n-hexane slugs into the                  oil reservoir respectively in the injection well,                  first injected 0.5PV n-hexane slugs, first                  diluted the crude oil to reduce viscosity, then                  injected 1PV steam slugs, reduced the viscosity                  of heavy oil, injected 1PV into carbon dioxide                  slug, made crude oil expand and reduced                  viscosity, increased the mobility ratio, and                  finally injected 1PV nitrogen slug, which plays                  the role of maintaining pressure and heat                  insulation, controlled the minimum bottom hole                  flow pressure of 20000 KiloPascal, and the                  injection speed of 800 m3/d. The bottom hole flow                  pressure of control production well is 20000                  KiloPascal. The daily oil production is 200m3/d,                  and the well opening time is 20 years.
