PT P
PN IN202221024650-A
TI Synthesis of zinc titanate for converting carbon            dioxide to fuel, comprises mixing titanium (IV)            butoxide with surfactant, ading concentrated            hydrochloric acid, mixing with zinc acetate dihydrate            ultrasonicating, centrifuging suspension, drying            product and calcining dried product.
AU JOSHI S
   BOPPELLA R
   BORNARE D T
AE JOSHI S (JOSH-Individual)
GA 202279307M
AB    NOVELTY - A process for synthesis of zinc titanate                interfaced with titanium dioxide particles having                nano-rhombohedron structure, (a) mixing titanium                (IV) butoxide with a surfactant to obtain a first                admixture, (b) admixing concentrated hydrochloric                acid to the first mixture, (c) admixing zinc                acetate dihydrate to the second admixture with                vigorous stirring for a second predetermined time                period to obtain a third admixture, (d)                ultrasonicating the third admixture, (e) heating                the ultrasonicated admixture, (f) cooling the                heated admixture, (g) adding deionized water to the                cooled precipitate and suspending the precipitate                in the deionized water to obtain a suspension, (h)                centrifuging the suspension at a predetermined                centrifugation speed to obtain a creamy yellow                product, (j) washing the washed product with an                alcohol, (k) drying the alcohol washed product to                obtain a dried product, and (l) calcining the dried                product.
   USE - The process is used for synthesis of zinc                titanate for conversion of carbon dioxide to                fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is carried out in simple, time                saving and cost economical manner, provides                photocatalyst with band gap in the visible                spectrum, and enables titanium dioxide interfacing                in a single process rather than two separate                processes as in conventional processes. The process                provides a solution to reducing the amount of                carbon dioxide and like gases in atmosphere. The                process provides a nanomaterial which is capable of                reducing the amount of carbon dioxide and further                converting it into useful fuel. The process                provides an ecofriendly and single shot process,                where the need for a separate step of decoration is                not required. The process enables the achievement                of well-shaped and structured zinc titanate,                achievement of rhombohedral shaped zinc titanate                with control morphology in the form of capsule,                evading synthesis of pure zinc titanate which is                n-type semiconductor and is highly resistive with                bandgap in UV range, avoids post synthesis                decoration methods to form heterostructures of zinc                titanate, ensures one-pot hydrothermal technique                which itself generates in-situ n-type ternary                heterostructures, that is (n-n-n) with probable                bandgap in ultraviolet-visible range enabling us to                harvest natural sunlight, enables photoconversion                of carbon dioxide gas to hydrocarbon fuels                utilizing natural sunlight as well as                nano-photocatalyst, and the process comprises                generates high quality nanoparticles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A process for synthesis of zinc titanate of                formula: ZnTiO3, Zn2Ti3O8 and Zn2TiO4 interfaced                with titanium dioxide particles having                nano-rhombohedron structure, comprises (a) mixing                titanium (IV) butoxide with a surfactant to obtain                a first admixture, (b) admixing concentrated                hydrochloric acid with the first mixture, where the                concentrated hydrochloric acid is added in a                dropwise manner while stirring for a first                predetermined time period to obtain a second                admixture, (c) admixing zinc acetate dihydrate with                the second admixture with vigorous stirring for a                second predetermined time period to obtain a third                admixture, (d) ultrasonicating the third admixture                at a predetermined ultrasonicating power and for a                third predetermined time period to obtain an                ultrasonicated admixture, (e) heating the                ultrasonicated admixture to a first predetermined                temperature for a fourth predetermined time period                to obtain a heated admixture comprising                precipitate, (f) cooling the heated admixture                comprising the precipitate to a temperature of                20-35 degrees C to obtain a cooled precipitate, (g)                adding deionized water to the cooled precipitate                and suspending the precipitate in the deionized                water to obtain a suspension, (h) centrifuging the                suspension at a predetermined centrifugation speed                and for fifth time period to obtain a creamy yellow                product, (i) repeating the steps (g) to (h) more                than one time to remove residual impurities in the                creamy yellow product to obtain a washed product,                (j) washing the washed product with an alcohol to                further remove the residual impurities to obtain an                alcohol washed product, (k) drying the alcohol                washed product for a sixth predetermined time                period at a second predetermined temperature to                obtain a dried product, and (l) calcining the dried                product at a third predetermined temperature and                for a seventh predetermined time period to obtain                the zinc titanate interfaced with titanium dioxide                particles having nano-rhombohedrons structure. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process for                conversion of carbon dioxide to fuel, comprising                providing a powdered form of zinc titanate                interfaced with titanium dioxide particles having                nano-rhombohedron structure, admixing the zinc                titanate interfaced with titanium dioxide particles                having nano-rhombohedron structure with water in a                sealed reactor, purging the reactor continuously                with an inert gas to remove all gases in water                along with other impurities, purging the reactor                with carbon dioxide gas and saturating the reactor                with carbon dioxide gas, and irradiating the carbon                dioxide gas saturated reactor with a light source                for a time period of 5-600 minutes to obtain the                fuel gases comprising methane and carbon                monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The surfactant is oleic                  acid. The amount of titanium (IV) butoxide is                  5-10 mL. The amount of the surfactant is 25-10                  mL, the concentration of hydrochloric acid is                  5-12 M and the amount of hydrochloric acid is 2-5                  mL. The rate of addition of hydrochloric acid is                  1-10 drops per minute. The stirring speed in step                  (b) is 50-100 rpm, the first predetermined time                  period is 5-30 minutes, the amount of zinc                  acetate dehydrate is 2-10 g, the stirring speed                  in the step (c) is 100-500 rpm, the second                  predetermined time period is 10-60 minutes, the                  ultrasonicating power is 10-50 W in a frequency                  range of 15-20 KHz, the third predetermined time                  period is 5-30 minutes, the first predetermined                  temperature is 180-250 degrees C, and the fourth                  predetermined time period is 600-1600 minutes.                  The step of cooling is carried out for 600-1600                  minutes, the amount of deionized water used is                  100-500 mL, the predetermined centrifugation                  speed is 5000-15000 rpm, the fifth time period is                  1-60 minutes, the steps (g) and (h) are repeated                  two to five times, the alcohol is ethanol, the                  amount of alcohol used is 100-500 mL, the sixth                  predetermined time period is 600-1600 minutes,                  and the second predetermined temperature is                  100-250 degrees C. The third predetermined                  temperature is 500-1000 degrees C. The seventh                  predetermined time period is 60-180 minutes.                  Preferred Composition: The amount of zinc                  titanate interfaced with titanium dioxide                  particles having nano-rhombohedrons structure is                  10-100 mg. The amount of water is 10-500 mL, and                  the water is double distilled water, the inert                  gas is nitrogen and the rate of flow of the inert                  gas is 50-250 cm3/minute at a temperature of                  30-50 degrees C. Preferred Condition: The rate of                  the flow of carbon dioxide gas is 5-100                  cm3/minute at a temperature of 30-70 degrees C                  for a time period of 30-120 minutes. Preferred                  Components: The light source is one selected from                  medium pressure mercury vapor lamp, xenon arc                  lamp, white cold floodlight light emitting diode,                  natural sunlight and their combinations.
   EXAMPLE - 6.84 mL (0.02 moles) of titanium (IV) butoxide                was added to a beaker of 100 mL capacity filled                with 50 mL oleic acid. Then, 3.42 mL concentrated                hydrochloric acid was added to the mixture drop                wise and kept for stirring for 10 minutes. To this                solution, 4.411 g (0.02 moles) of zinc acetate                dihydrate was added and stirred vigorously for 20                minutes. The suspension was ultrasonicated for 15                minutes, and the contents were transferred to a 100                mL Teflon (RTM: PTFE)-lined autoclave and placed in                a programmable oven. The temperature was raised to                200 degrees C at a heating rate of 5 degrees                C/minute and held constant at this temperature for                24 hours. The resultant precipitate was suspended                in deionized water and centrifuged at a speed of                10000 rpm for 5 minutes to obtain a creamy yellow                product. Repeated centrifuging, sonication and                washing with deionized water followed by an ethanol                wash were carried out to ensure complete removal of                all residual impurities. The product was dried                overnight in an oven at 75 degrees C. The resultant                dried precipitate obtained post hydrothermal method                at 200 degrees C for 24 hours was subjected to                calcination at 600-900 degrees C for 2 hours at                each temperature to achieve nano rhombohedron                structures.
DC E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. PN US2019241482-A1; US10407357-B2
TI Processing amorphous poly alpha olefin (APAO) made with substantially zero carbon dioxide emission involves converting alkanes to ethylene, propylene, and 1-butene, using renewable electric power in oxidative-coupling of methane (OCM) plant.
AU FOWLER J N
   LAWRENCE D
   MCHANEY S C
AE FOWLER J N (FOWL-Individual)
   LAWRENCE D (LAWR-Individual)
   MCHANEY S C (MCHA-Individual)
   LAWRENCE D (LAWR-Individual)
GA 2019685386
AB    NOVELTY - Processing amorphous poly alpha olefin (APAO) made with substantially zero carbon dioxide emission involves converting alkanes to ethylene, propylene, and 1-butene, using renewable electric power in an oxidative-coupling of methane (OCM) plant which comprises an ethylene plant, a 2-butene plant, a propylene plant, and a 1-butene plant. The ethylene plant comprises an oxidation reactor and a first fractionation train. The natural gas and oxygen are converted in the oxidation reactor to produce oxidation reactor effluent which comprises ethylene. The oxidation reactor effluent is passed to the first fractionation train to recover a first ethylene stream, a second ethylene stream, and a third ethylene stream. The 2-butene plant comprises a dimerization unit and a second fractionation train. The portion of the first ethylene stream is converted in the dimerization unit to produce a dimerization unit effluent which comprises 2-butene.
   USE - Method for processing amorphous poly alpha olefin (APAO) made with substantially zero carbon dioxide emission.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to process amorphous poly alpha olefin (APAO) made with substantially zero carbon dioxide emission that is eco-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing amorphous poly alpha olefin (APAO) made with substantially zero carbon dioxide emission involves converting alkanes to ethylene, propylene, and 1-butene, using renewable electric power in an oxidative-coupling of methane (OCM) plant which comprises an ethylene plant, a 2-butene plant, a propylene plant, and a 1-butene plant. The ethylene plant comprises an oxidation reactor and a first fractionation train. The natural gas and oxygen are converted in the oxidation reactor to produce oxidation reactor effluent which comprises ethylene. The oxidation reactor effluent is passed to the first fractionation train to recover a first ethylene stream, a second ethylene stream, and a third ethylene stream. The 2-butene plant comprises a dimerization unit and a second fractionation train. The portion of the first ethylene stream is converted in the dimerization unit to produce a dimerization unit effluent which comprises 2-butene. The dimerization unit effluent is passed to the second fractionation train to recover a first 2-butene stream and a second 2-butene stream. The propylene plant comprises a metathesis reactor and a third fractionation train. The portion of the first 2-butene stream and a portion of the second ethylene stream are converted in the metathesis reactor to produce a metathesis reactor effluent which comprises propylene and passing the metathesis reactor effluent to the third fractionation train to recover the propylene. The 1-butene plant comprises a butene isomerization unit and a fourth fractionation train. The portion of the second 2-butene stream is converted in the butene isomerization unit to produce a butene isomerization unit effluent which comprises 1-butene. The butene isomerization unit effluent is passed to the fourth fractionation train to recover the 1-butene. The 1-butene, propylene, or third ethylene stream are polymerized in an APAO plant which comprises boilers in which boiler stack gasses are produced. The polymerization temperature is 130-175 degrees F. The renewable electric power is used to power the boilers while scrubbing one of the boiler stack gasses with a solvent to sequester carbon dioxide from the one of the boiler stack gases. The zero carbon dioxide emissions results into the atmosphere and the formation of an amorphous poly alpha olefin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The natural gas and oxygen are converted in the oxidation reactor to produce the oxidation reactor effluent. The catalyst is contacted in the oxidation reactor with oxygen and the natural gas at a pressure of 6-16 bar and a temperature of 600-800?OC to produce the oxidation reactor effluent. The catalyst is selected from lanthanide series metal supported catalyst and an actinide series metal supported catalyst. The natural gas comprises a mixture of alkanes. The oxidation reactor effluent comprises ethylene and carbon dioxide. The dimerization unit effluent comprises 2-butene and by-products.

PN CN111115649-A; CN111115649-B
TI Preparation of boron carbonitride nanosheet used            as photocatalyst for decomposing water and reducing            carbon dioxide by using boron source, nitrogen source,            carbon source and water-soluble metal compound as raw            materials and heat treating.
AU TANG Z
   GAO Y
   LI M
   QIU X
AE NAT CENT NANOSCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY CHINA (NANI-C)
GA 202041417L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of boron carbonitride (BCN)                nanosheet comprises using boron source, nitrogen                source, carbon source and water-soluble metal                compound as raw materials and heat treating at                750-1050 degrees C.
   USE - The method is for preparation of BCN nanosheet                used as photocatalyst for decomposing water and                reduction of carbon dioxide under visible light                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for BCN                nanosheet having band gap energy of 1.9-3.6 eV,                preferably 2-3 eV.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The water-soluble metal                  compound comprises alkali metal compound and/or                  alkaline earth metal compound. The alkali metal                  compound comprises alkali metal nitrate and/or                  alkali metal hydroxide. The alkaline earth metal                  compound comprises alkaline earth metal nitrate.                  The alkali metal nitrate comprises ammonium                  nitrate, sodium nitrate, potassium nitrate,                  rubidium nitrate and/or cesium nitrate. The                  alkali metal hydroxide includes lithium                  hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide,                  rubidium hydroxide and/or cesium hydroxide. The                  alkaline earth metal nitrate comprises calcium                  nitrate, magnesium nitrate, strontium nitrate                  and/or barium nitrate. The degassing treatment                  comprises vacuum degassing. The heat treatment is                  conducted under protective gas i.e. nitrogen                  (preferred) and/or ammonia gas for 2-6 hours,                  preferably 4 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  preparing a mixed solution of boron source,                  nitrogen source, carbon source and water-soluble                  metal compound; stirring, drying, heat treating,                  and grinding to obtain precursor; degassing and                  heat treating at 750-1050 degrees C. The mass mol                  ratio of boron source, nitrogen source, carbon                  source and water-soluble metal compound is 1                  g:2-5 g:0.05-0.4 g:0.05-0.2 mmol. The boron                  source comprises boric acid, methyl borate,                  phenyl boric acid and/or boron oxide. The                  nitrogen source comprises urea, carbamate,                  guanidine and/or tripolycyanamide. The carbon                  source comprises glucose and/or glycine. The                  third step further comprises purification                  comprising acid treatment at 50-100 degrees C,                  preferably 80 degrees C using acid including                  hydrochloric acid and/or preferably less than or                  equal to 10 mass% excluding 0 mass% nitric acid,                  washing using solvent i.e. ethanol and/or water,                  stripping, which is ultrasonic stripping, and                  drying at 60-120 degrees C, preferably 100                  degrees C.

PN CN110305010-A; CN110305010-B
TI Synthesizing 2,5-dimethylphenylacetic acid involves reacting 2,5-dimethylbenzyl halide with magnesium to form Grignard reagent 2,5-dimethylbenzyl magnesium halide, reacting 2,5-dimethylbenzyl magnesium halide with carbon dioxide.
AU BU L
   ZHANG P
   WU Y
   HOU Y
AE JIANGSU FLAG CHEM IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU FLAG CHEM IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201996174X
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing 2,5-dimethylphenylacetic acid involves reacting 2,5-dimethylbenzyl halide with magnesium to form a Grignard reagent 2,5-dimethylbenzyl Magnesium halide, reacting 2,5-dimethylbenzyl magnesium halide with carbon dioxide to form 2,5-dimethylphenylacetic acid.
   USE - Method for synthesizing 2,5-dimethylphenylacetic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to synthesize 2,5-dimethylphenylacetic acid is eco-friendly and cost-effective, simplifies the process and shortens the reaction steps, snd has high yield, overcomes the shortcomings and suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: molar ratio of 2,5-dimethylbenzyl halide and magnesium is 1:0.8-2.0, preferably 1:1.0-2.0. The initiator iodine, a 1,2-dibromoethane or an isopropyl Grignard reagent, and the initiator and 2,5-dimethylbenzyl halide are further added in a molar ratio of 1:0.0001-0.05. The molar ratio of the 2,5-dimethylbenzyl magnesium halide and carbon dioxide is 1:0.9-20.0, preferably 1:1.1-10.0. Preferred Components: The solvent includes an ether solvent such as tetrahydrofuran, 2-methyltetrahydrofuran or diethyl ether or a mixed solvent of an ether solvent and toluene. The carbon dioxide comprises carbon dioxide gas or dry ice. Preferred Conditions: The 2,5-dimethylbenzyl halide is added to the remaining reaction reagents in batches, and the first batch is added in an amount of 10-20 wt.% total amount of 2,5-dimethylbenzyl halide. The balance is added after the reaction solution turned grayish white.

PT P
PN CN110105249-A; CN110105249-B
TI Preparing 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate comprises carrying out coupling reaction using 4,4'-diaminodiphenylmethane and gaseous carbon dioxide as raw material and polyoxometalate as a catalyst.
AU YU H
   DAN D
   WU Z
   WEI Y
   HAN S
AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)
GA 201970683K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate comprises carrying out coupling reaction using 4,4'-diaminodiphenylmethane and gaseous carbon dioxide as raw material and polyoxometalate as a catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst (M-Anderson type heteropoly acid): has mild reaction condition, high selectivity and specificity, can be recycled, is environmentally friendly, improves the cleanness of the industrial reaction, improves the economy of the process, reduces the producing cost and wastes, reduces environment pressure, and favors the industrialized production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The preparation further comprises adding 4,4'-diaminodiphenylmethane, polyoxometalate and solvent into a reaction container, adding phenylsilane, acid-binding agent and dehydrating agent, uniformly mixing and finally introducing gaseous carbon dioxide, maintaining the reaction temperature at 0-50 degrees C, stirring and reacting for 5-25 hours and separating and purifying. The reaction temperature is set at 30 degrees C for 5-15 hours. The polyoxometalate catalyst is recycled after the reaction is recovered using an organic solvent. Preferred Components: The catalyst is an Anderson-type polyoxometalate having iron, aluminum, manganese and copper as center metal or an Anderson-type polyoxometalate having iron, aluminum, manganese and copper as center metal modified by a trialkoxy derivative. The acid-binding agent is pyridine or triethylamine. The dehydrating agent is dimethyl sulfoxide or phosphorus oxychloride. The solvent is anhydrous acetonitrile or N,N-dimethylformamide. The molar ratio of polyoxometalate, 4,4'-diaminodiphenylmethane, phenylsilane, acid-binding agent and dehydrating agent is 0.02:2:4:1:2. The ratio of the solvent to 4,4'-diaminodiphenylmethane is 3 ml/mmol.

PN CN108341428-A; CN108341428-B
TI Preparing metal oxide composite material involves mixing metal salt or organometallic compound and tetraethyl orthosilicate, surfactant in organic solvent under action of catalyst to react, adding carbon dioxide to promote reaction.
AU LI J
   LIN Z
   SONG L
   SU Y
   WANG H
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 201861891J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal oxide composite material involves mixing metal salt or organometallic compound and tetraethyl orthosilicate, surfactant in organic solvent under action of catalyst to react, adding carbon dioxide to promote reaction, drying and calcining reaction product to obtain metal oxide composite material.
   USE - Method for preparing metal oxide composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare metal oxide composite material that has high specific surface area and high aperture/oxide composite of the pore volume, and exhibits good catalytic performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal salt or the organometallic compound is a salt or an organic compound containing a target metal. The target-containing metal salt or organic compound is selected from nickel chloride hexahydrate, tetrabutyl titanate. The solvent is ethanol. The surfactant is hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide. The catalyst is acidic aqueous solution, preferably oxalic acid aqueous solution. Preferred Condition: The reaction temperature is 25 degrees C. The flow speed is 15 megapascal. The drying temperature is 60 degrees C. The calcining temperature is 550 degrees C for 10 hours. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide is added to promote reaction in the reaction system with stirring until the pressure is 7.6 megapascal for 1 day.

PT P
PN CN107630365-A; CN107630365-B
TI Preparation of ultra-wetting fabric by subjecting fabric made from thermoplastic polymer fiber to supercritical carbon dioxide foaming.
AU SHOUJI Q
   XIE P
   MINZHI R
   ZHANG M
   QIU S
   RONG M
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 201810132D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ultra-wetting fabric comprises subjecting fabric made from thermoplastic polymer fiber to supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) foaming at 7-25 MPa and 30-180 degrees C for 30-180 minutes, where pressure relief rate is 0.5-8 MPa/s.
   USE - Preparation of ultra-wetting fabric.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides ultra-wetting fabric, which has superhydrophilic or superhydrophobic and ultra-wetting performance, and uses supercritical CO2 foaming, which has environmental protection and simple operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of supercritical CO2 in preparation of superhydrophilic or superhydrophobic fabric.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The thermoplastic polymer fiber has diameter of 0.5-80 mu m and aperture of 2-200 mu m. It comprises hydrophilic thermoplastic polymeric fiber and hydrophobic thermoplastic polymer fiber. The hydrophilic thermoplastic polymer fiber is polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyvinyl alcohol and/or polyacrylamide. The hydrophobic thermoplastic polymer fiber is polyethylene, polypropylene, PVC, polyacrylonitrile, polymethyl methacrylate, polylactic acid, polyethylene terephthalate and/or nylon.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); F04 (Braiding, knitting - including trimmings and non-woven fabrics (D04).); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).)

PT P
PN DE102016212850-A1; WO2018010741-A1
TI Producing methane by generating hydrogen and oxygen from water by isolating peptides having hydrogenase activity, separating oxygen and hydrogen, producing methane from hydrogen and carbon dioxide and liquefying methane.
AU SALVETZKI R
AE SALVETZKI R (SALV-Individual)
GA 201805099P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing methane involves (a) generating hydrogen and oxygen from water by isolating synthetic and/or recombinant peptides having hydrogenase activity in an aqueous reaction mixture (hydrogenation), separating generated oxygen from generated hydrogen, producing methane from generated hydrogen and carbon dioxide using methanogenesis bacteria (methanogenesis), and separating and optionally liquefying obtained methane.
   USE - The method is useful for producing methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly and inexpensive, eliminates the use of fossil resources, and enables the efficient use of alternative energy sources.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves isolating a peptide with hydrogenase activity from green algae and/or cyanobacteria, or recombinantly or synthetically producing an amino acid sequence which is identical or similar to the amino acid sequence of peptides having hydrogenase activity from green algae and/or cyanobacteria. The peptides with hydrogenase activity are used to generate hydrogen in an aqueous solution. The peptides with hydrogenase activity are bound to a substrate by adsorption, crosslinking, ionic bonding or covalent bonding. The peptides with hydrogenase activity are bound to the substrate by bi-reactive linker peptide, bi-reactive alkane, alkene, or alkyne. The method involves separating the substrate with bound peptides from the reaction mixture. The peptides with hydrogenase activity are activated by the uptake of electrons, where the electrons are provided to the peptides by an electron donor by (a) applying an electrical voltage to electrodes, which are introduced into the aqueous reaction mixture, and the electrode is platinum, copper, titanium, silicon, palladium, iron, nickel, lead, gallium, indium, cadmium, ruthenium, cobalt, silver, gold, iridium, osmium, mercury, zinc, hafnium, zirconium, vanadium, niobium, molybdenum, tungsten, chromium or manganese electrode, and/or (b) transfer of electrons via an electron transport chain in the aqueous reaction mixture, preferably an electron transport chain in which at least one electron donor such as NADH participates, or at least one photosensitizer from the group of porphyrins e.g. zinc tetraphenylporphyrin tetrasulfonic acid (ZnTPPS) or chlorophyll, or at least one electron carrier such as methyl viologen participates. The peptides with hydrogenase activity are covalently linked to an electrode. In the method, 1 mg peptide with hydrogenase activity produces more than 0.5 l, preferably 4.67 l hydrogen for 1 hour. In the method, 1 mg peptide with hydrogenase activity produces more than 500 mu mol, preferably 3500 mu mol hydrogen/minute. Preferred Biomolecules: The isolated peptide with hydrogenase activity is chosen from hydrogenases, hydrogenase-like peptides, hydrogenase variant, and their fragments or combinations. The synthetic or recombinant peptides with hydrogenase activity is chosen from hydrogenases, hydrogenase-like peptides, hydrogenase variant, hydrogenase mimics, and their fragments or combinations. The peptides with hydrogenase activity have iron (Fe), (Fe Fe), (Fe, nickel) or (nickel, iron selenide) complexes in their active center. The hydrogenase activity of the peptides is oxygen-resistant. Preferred Microorganisms: The green algae and cyanobacteria are chosen from Chlamydomonas reinhardtii, Ralstonia eutropha, Paracoccus spp., Pyrococcus spp., Alcaligenes spp., Bradyrhizobium spp., Oligotropha spp., Acidovorax spp., Hydrogenophaga spp., Carboxydothermus spp., Desulfovibrio spp., Megasphaera elsdenii, Scenedesmus obliquus, Desulfomicrobium baculatum, Ralstonia metallidurans, Clostridium acetobutylicum, C.pasteurianum, Aquifex aeolicus, Synechococcus strain BG 04351, S.elongatus strain PCC 7942, Spirulina spp., Chroococcidiopsis spp., Synechocystis strain PCC 6803, Anabaena strain PCC 7120, Thiocapsa roseopersicina, Allochromatium vinosum, Desulfovibrio fructosovorans, D.gigas, Nostoc punctiforme, Fischerella muscicola, Anabaena siamensis, Cyanothece spp., Gloeocapsa alpicola, Aphanothece halophytica, Arthrospira maxima, Thiocapsa roseopersicina, Alteromonas macleodii, Aquifex aeolicus, Rubrivivax gelatinosus, and Shewanella oneidensis.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The photosensitizer is excited by light from at least one light source suspended in the aqueous hydrogenation reaction mixture. The photosensitizer is excited by light from multiple light sources that are powered wirelessly, and which are suspended freely in the aqueous reaction mixture during the hydrogenation. The oxygen is bound in the aqueous reaction mixture during or after the hydrogenation, preferably by adding at least one binder. The binders are regenerated after the uptake of oxygen. The oxygen is bound biochemically or chemically. The biochemical binding of the oxygen involves adding hemoglobin, myoglobin or porphyrins as binder. The chemical bonding of the oxygen involves adding hydrazine, preferably alpha -terpene, isoprene, and/or their derivative as binder. The myoglobin and/or porphyrins are regenerated electrochemically, biochemically or physically after the absorption of oxygen. The method involves (a) separating the gaseous intermediates of hydrogen and oxygen from the aqueous reaction mixture, and (b) separating methane from the bacterial medium via a membrane, preferably porous membrane made of crosslinked polyethylene (CLPE) with an inner layer of poly (acrylamide-tert-butyl-sulfonic acid) (PATBS). The electrical voltage is given by alternative power generation systems, preferably wind power or photovoltaic systems. The method produces 0.124 l or 1.16 l pure carbon dioxide-neutral methane. Preferred Components: The substrate is chosen from silica gel, aluminum hydroxide, aluminum oxide hydroxide, alumina, and the materials used for separation by ion exchange or affinity chromatography is silica gel particles adapted to bind a peptide having hydrogenase activity, preferably by a coating with antibodies specific for the peptides, or by coating with the respective affinity partner for histidine, Myc, hemagglutinin (HA), FLAG (RTM: peptide sequence Asp-Tyr-Lys-Asp-Asp-Asp-Lys), Avi (RTM: Biotinylation tag), protein A, protein G, calmodulin, transporter associated with antigen processing (TAP), Strep (RTM: Affinity chromatography systems), glutathione S-transferase (GST) tags, titanium oxide, and silicides. The silicides is chosen from silicides with copper, titanium, platinum, palladium, iron, nickel, lead, gallium, indium, cadmium, ruthenium, cobalt, silver, gold, iridium, osmium, mercury, Zinc, hafnium, zirconium, vanadium, niobium, molybdenum, tungsten, chromium or manganese. The carbon dioxide is atmospheric carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide from industrial or combustion processes. The aqueous reaction mixture comprises 0.8 vol.%, preferably 0.05 vol.% oxygen or is oxygen-free.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC D05-A02A; D05-C14; E10-J02D1; E11-M; E11-W; H06-A05; H06-P; A04-G02E; A11-C01C; A12-T03A; A12-W11A; A12-W11L

PN CN114470846-A
TI Purifying gas involves cooling initial gas to obtain first carbon dioxide liquid and first gas, purging first gas with lean methanol liquid to obtain purified gas and first methanol-rich liquid, flashing first carbon dioxide liquid under reduced pressure to obtain first effective gas.
AU WEI S
   ZHANG J
   DONG Z
AE YUCHUANG TIANJIN CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUCH-Non-standard)
GA 202270810V
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying gas involves cooling initial gas to obtain first carbon dioxide (CO2) liquid and first gas, where the first CO2 liquid temperature is -50-58degrees Celsius, and pressure is 1.6-6 megapascal, the temperature of the first gas is -50--58degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 1.6-6 megapascal, purging first gas with lean methanol liquid to obtain purified gas and first methanol-rich liquid, where the temperature of the purified gas is -45--55degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 1.6-6 megapascal, the temperature of the first methanol-rich liquid is -20--30degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 1.6-6 megapascal.
   USE - Method for purifying gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has reduced energy consumption of recovering lean methanol liquid to 30-45%, reduced diameter of each treatment tower, and realizes miniaturization of the equipment, and has high popularization and application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying gas involves cooling initial gas to obtain first carbon dioxide (CO2) liquid and first gas, where the first CO2 liquid temperature is -50-58degrees Celsius, and pressure is 1.6-6 megapascal, the temperature of the first gas is -50--58degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 1.6-6 megapascal, purging first gas with lean methanol liquid to obtain purified gas and first methanol-rich liquid, where the temperature of the purified gas is -45--55degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 1.6-6 megapascal, the temperature of the first methanol-rich liquid is -20--30 degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 1.6-6 megapascal, flashing first carbon dioxide liquid under reduced pressure to obtain first effective gas and second carbon dioxide liquid, the temperature of the first effective gas is -30--40degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 0.8-1.6 megapascal, the temperature of the second CO2 liquid is -50-58degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 0.8-1.6 megapascal, evaporating second CO2 liquid to obtain second gas, where the temperature of second gas is -50--58degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 0.1-0.8 megapascal, flashing the first methanol-rich liquid under reduced pressure to obtain second semi-lean methanol liquid and third gas, where the temperature of the second semi-lean methanol liquid is -40--55degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 0.1-0.8 megapascal, the temperature of the third gas is -60--65degrees Celsius, and the pressure is not less than0.1 megapascal, mixing second gas and third gas to obtain fourth gas, where the temperature of the fourth gas is -50--60degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 0.1-0.8 megapascal, desulfurizing the fourth gas to obtain CO2 product gas, where the temperature of CO2 product gas is -50--60degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 0.1-0.8 megapascal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first methanol-rich liquid includes third sulfur-containing methanol-rich liquid and second sulfur-free methanol-rich liquid.Preferred Process: The treatment process of the second semi-lean methanol liquid involves cooling the third sulfur-containing methanol-rich liquid with second sulfur-containing semi-lean methanol liquid, heating the second sulfur-containing semi-lean methanol liquid to obtain methanol-lean liquid and gas, where the temperature of the lean methanol liquid is 70-80degrees Celsius and the pressure is 0.1-0.3 megapascal, the temperature of the gas is 50-70degrees Celsius, and the pressure 0.1-0.3 megapascal, cooling, separating the gas step by step to obtain a gas containing hydrogen sulfide (H2S), where the temperature of the gas is 20-30degrees Celsius and the pressure is 0.1-0.3 megapascal, cooling the initial gas with the gas, and recovering gas.

PN CN113943595-A; CN113943595-B
TI Preparing synthetic sulfonate additive comprises            heating e.g. diluent oil and alkaline component calcium            hydroxide, stirring, adding e.g. alcohol accelerator,            neutralizing, cooling, adding e.g. alcohol accelerant            alkyl phenol, high-alkali degree reaction, heating,            centrifuging and vacuum distilling.
AU TANG Z
   MA T
   LIU Y
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2022192400
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing synthetic sulfonate additive                comprises (a) adding diluent oil, alkaline                component calcium hydroxide, long-chain alkyl                benzene sulfonic acid to a reaction kettle,                heating, stirring, (b) adding alcohol accelerator                and auxiliary agent in the reaction system,                neutralizing in solvent-free reaction system, (c)                removing alcohol accelerator and the water                generated by the reaction, cooling, (d) adding e.g.                alcohol accelerant alkyl phenol, vulcanized alkyl                phenolic calcium and alkaline component calcium                hydroxide, introducing carbon dioxide gas for                high-alkali degree reaction, adding alkaline                component calcium hydroxide for the second high                alkalinization, stopping introducing carbon                dioxide, finishing high-alkali degree reaction and                (e) heating reaction system to remove alcohol                accelerant and the water generated by the reaction                in the system to obtain the reaction crude product,                centrifuging to remove slag, subjecting to vacuum                distillation to obtain a synthetic sulfonate                additive.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing synthetic                sulfonate additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The synthetic sulfonate additive has excellent                colloidal stability, high-temperature detergency,                acid neutralization performance, excellent                oil-water separation performance; meets the use                requirements of high-grade internal combustion                engine oil; is suitable for use in marine oil and                other oil products that require high oil-water                separation performance and has excellent                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing synthetic sulfonate additive                comprises (a) adding diluent oil, alkaline                component calcium hydroxide, long-chain alkyl                benzene sulfonic acid to a reaction flask or the                reaction kettle, heating, stirring evenly, (b)                adding alcohol accelerator and auxiliary agent in                the reaction system of step (a), neutralizing in                solvent-free reaction system, (c) removing the                alcohol accelerator and the water generated by the                reaction in the system, cooling, (d) continuously                adding alcohol accelerant, alkyl phenol, vulcanized                alkyl phenolic calcium and alkaline component                calcium hydroxide in the reaction system of the                step (c), continuously introducing carbon dioxide                gas for high-alkali degree reaction, where the                alkaline component calcium hydroxide is uniformly                added for 2 times, the mol equivalent ratio of the                carbon dioxide to the first adding alkaline                component calcium hydroxide reaches 0.7-0.9: 1,                adding the alkaline component calcium hydroxide for                the second high alkalinization when the total                introduction amount of carbon dioxide and the high                alkalinity reaction add the basic component calcium                hydroxide molar equivalent ratio to reach 0.8-0.95,                stopping introducing carbon dioxide, finishing                high-alkali degree reaction and (e) finishing the                high alkalinization reaction of step (d), heating                the reaction system to remove the alcohol                accelerant and the water generated by the reaction                in the system to obtain the reaction crude product,                centrifuging the crude product to remove slag,                subjecting to vacuum distillation to obtain a                synthetic sulfonate additive with a base value of                more than 400 mgKOH/g.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                synthetic sulfonate additive prepared by the above                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of                  long-chain alkylbenzene sulfonic acid, diluent                  oil, alkaline component calcium hydroxide,                  alcohol accelerator, auxiliary agent, alkyl                  phenol and vulcanized alkyl phenolic calcium is                  60-100:100:80-140:10-60:1-10:0-20. The long-chain                  alkyl benzene sulfonic acid is a long-chain alkyl                  benzene sulfonic acid with an alkyl carbon chain                  of 18C, 20C, 22C, 24C, and 20-24C. The alcohol                  accelerator comprises methanol, ethanol,                  propanol, isopropanol, n-butanol, isobutanol                  and/or tert-butanol, preferably methanol and                  n-butanol. The auxiliary agent is an ionic                  solution formed by alkali metal chloride,                  alkaline earth metal chloride and lower fatty                  acid. The alkali metal chloride comprises sodium                  chloride and/or potassium chloride. The alkaline                  earth metal chloride comprises calcium chloride,                  magnesium chloride and/or zinc chloride. The                  lower fatty acid comprises formic acid, acetic                  acid, propionic acid and/or butyric acid. The                  auxiliary agent is an ionic solution formed by                  sodium chloride, potassium chloride, calcium                  chloride, formic acid and/or acetic acid. The                  auxiliary agent is an ionic solution formed by                  calcium chloride and acetic acid. The alkyl                  phenol comprises dodecyl phenol, pentadecyl                  phenol and/or diesel alkyl phenol. The vulcanized                  alkyl phenolic calcium comprises vulcanized                  dodecyl phenolic calcium, vulcanized pentadecyl                  phenolic calcium and/or vulcanized diesel alkyl                  phenolic calcium. The diluent oil comprises                  MVI150, MVI200, HVI150, HVI200, HVIS150, HVIW150                  class I and class II base oils.Preferred Method: In the step (a) the                  heating temperature is 35-45degrees Celsius. In                  the step (b) the neutralization reaction process                  is carried out at 60-90degrees Celsius for                  0.5-1.5 hours. In the step (c) the heating                  temperature is 120-160degrees Celsius and the                  cooling temperature is 35-65degrees Celsius. In                  the step (d) the high alkalinity reaction                  temperature is 35-65degrees Celsius. In the step                  (e) the heating temperature is 120-170degrees                  Celsius.

PT P
PN CN113521967-A; CN216654007-U
TI Coupling device for carbon capture and peak shaving, has carbon dioxide (CO2) compression energy storage system that has liquid CO2 in CO2 liquid storage device after absorbing thermal energy stored in second heat exchange device and vaporizing under reduced pressure.
AU GAO S
   LIU L
   NIU H
   WANG S
   FAN J
   LIU R
   GUO D
   WANG H
AE CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
   HUANENG POWER INT INC (CHHN-C)
   HUANENG POWER INT INC (CHHN-C)
   CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 2021C68324
AB    NOVELTY - The coupling device has a carbon dioxide (CO2) compression energy storage system (3) that has a state of releasing heat energy in which the CO2 gas output by a CO2 gas storage device (16) is compressed, cooled and liquefied into liquid CO2 through a second CO2 compression device (17). The heat energy is stored by a second heat exchange device (19), in the state of releasing heat energy, and stores the cooled liquid CO2 in a CO2 liquid storage device (20) through the second heat exchange device. The CO2 compression energy storage system has the liquid CO2 in the CO2 liquid storage device after absorbing the thermal energy stored in the second heat exchange device and vaporizing under reduced pressure, and enters an expander (21) to do work, to drive a generator set (22) to generate electricity through the expander. The CO2 compression energy storage system is switched between the heat release energy state and the power generation state.
   USE - Coupling device for carbon capture and peak shaving.
   ADVANTAGE - The captured CO2 uses phase change energy storage in the CO2 compression energy storage system to achieve the purpose of peak shaving of renewable energy power generation, to realize the dual benefits of the allocation and utilization of surplus power resources and CO2 emission reduction. The contradiction of excess power generation under favorable power generation conditions and insufficient power generation under unfavorable conditions in renewable energy power plants are solved, and realize the dual benefits of the allocation and utilization of excess power resources and CO2 emission reduction. The CO2 concentration in the air is reduced, while realizing the peak shaving of renewable energy power plants, which improve the power generation efficiency and alleviate the greenhouse effect, and the installation of the device is less restricted by the location, which is suitable for a variety of places with renewable energy power generation. The operating state of the renewable energy power generation system is adjusted, so that the net power generation and power generation cost of the unit is adjusted within a certain range, and economic benefits are realized. The device has high energy storage density, flexible application, economy and environmental protection, and high conversion efficiency. The independent control of the carbon capture absorption link, the carbon capture analysis link, and the CO2 compression energy storage link is conducive to improve the ability of the integrated system to accept volatile new energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a coupling method for carbon capture and peak shaving.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of coupling device for carbon capture and peak shaving. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide compression energy storage system (3)
   Carbon dioxide gas storage device (16)
   Second carbon dioxide compression device (17)
   Second heat exchange device (19)
   Carbon dioxide liquid storage device (20)
   Expander (21)
   Generator set (22)
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN112138131-A
TI Preparing total gingerol external preparation used for providing e.g. analgesic effect, by extracting dried ginger using supercritical carbon dioxide, performing macroporous resin adsorption treatment and purifying by HPLC.
AU XIAO F
AE CHENGDU CHROMA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202103047F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing total gingerol external preparation involves (i) crushing the dried ginger into fine powder, and using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent for extraction, (ii) performing macroporous resin adsorption treatment by macroporous resin column chromatography on crude extract of total gingerol obtained after supercritical extraction, where the macroporous resin adsorption treatment involves (a) soaking the macroporous resin for 5-10 minutes and again in ethyl acetate for 5-10 minutes, (b) adding the pretreated macroporous resin on column by wet method, and (c) dissolving the crude extract of total gingerol in methanol, performing centrifugal precipitation and filtering, and (iii) purifying the eluate by HPLC, and obtaining the total gingerol gel preparation.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing total gingerol external preparation for providing anti-mutagenic, anti-inflammatory, anti-rheumatic and antibacterial effects (claimed), antipyretic and analgesic effects.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared total gingerol external preparation avoids serious side effects caused by long term oral administration on digestive tract.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing total gingerol external preparation involves (i) crushing the dried ginger into fine powder, adding the fine powder into a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle after sieving, using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent for extraction, adding an entrainer comprising 60-70 %mass ethanol, 5-8 %mass methylparaben, 2-3 %mass triethanolamine, and water during extraction process, extracting at 25-32 MPa, 52-60 degrees C and carbon dioxide flow rate of 5-15 l/hour for 2-4 hours, the flow rate of entrainer and carbon dioxide solvent is 2-5%, and centrifuging to remove impurities to obtain a crude extract of total gingerol, (ii) performing macroporous resin adsorption treatment by macroporous resin column chromatography on crude total gingerol obtained after supercritical extraction, where the macroporous resin adsorption treatment involves (a) soaking the macroporous resin for 5-10 minutes in dilute alkali with a pH of 7.5-8.5, washing with deionized water to neutral, again soaking in ethyl acetate for 5-10 minutes, and washing with ionized water to neutrality to obtain pretreated macroporous resin, (b) putting the pretreated macroporous resin on the column by wet method, stirring the macroporous resin and deionized water, continuously adding to glass chromatography column, and releasing excess water from below, so that macroporous resin gradually settle to middle of glass chromatography column, and (c) dissolving the crude extract of total gingerol in methanol, performing centrifugal precipitation and filtering, adding to top of glass chromatography column, slowly reducing the mixture of crude extract of total gingerol and methanol to level with stationary phase, and adding mobile phase for elution, and (iii) purifying the eluate by HPLC, placing under reduced pressure, evaporating to dryness after purification to obtain refined gingerol, and adding the refined gingerol to the gel matrix after mixing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The macroporous resin is AB-8 type macroporous resin or D4020 macroporous resin. The volume ratio of the crude extract of total gingerol to methanol in the step (iii) is 1:1-2, the mobile phase is 60-70% ethanol solution, and the elution flow rate is 0.3-1 ml/minute. The step (c) involves obtaining a tube of eluate every 5 ml, and detecting total gingerol content in eluate until the eluate that does not contain total gingerol. The method involves in supercritical extraction process, adopting continuous pumping method to add entrainer, and after supercritical extraction, separating and analyzing at 5-8 MPa, 35-45 degrees C, and flow rate of 10-15 l/hour. The total gingerol gel preparation comprises 100 pts. wt. refined gingerol and 5-15 g Carbomer (RTM: Poly(acrylic acid)), 20-30 g glycerin, 0.1-0.2 g polysorbate, and distilled water.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Antirheumatic; Antibacterial; Antipyretic; Analgesic; Cytostatic.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN110907333-A; CN110907333-B
TI Evaluation of micro-sealing properties of barrier involves obtaining e.g. relationship between change of interlayer rock porosity, permeability and breakthrough pressure after carbon dioxide injection and evaluating micro-sealing level.
AU HAN X
   FENG F
   YAN M
   CONG Z
   LIU S
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV NORTHEAST PETROLEUM (UYPT-C)
GA 202024464U
AB    NOVELTY - Change in porosity of an interlayer rock caused by a chemical reaction is attributed to the volume change of minerals participating in the chemical reaction. The porosity of the interlayer rock caused by the chemical reaction is calculated from the change in mineral composition. The relationship between the pore radius and the permeability after the reaction of the interlayer rock changes is obtained. The relationship between the breakthrough pressure of the barrier and the median radius of the pore is obtained based on the Washburn theory. The relationship between the dynamic change of the interlayer rock porosity, the interlayer rock permeability and the breakthrough pressure the interlayer rock after carbon dioxide injection due to dissolution is obtained. The breakthrough pressure is converted into the equivalent gas column sealing height. The micro-sealing level of the barrier is evaluated based on the standard for dividing the height of the sealing gas column.
   USE - Dynamic evaluation of micro-sealing properties of barrier under action of carbon dioxide-water-rock reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method accurately reflects the actual blocking capacity of the interlayer to carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Change in porosity of the interlayer rock caused by a chemical reaction is attributed to the volume change of minerals participating in the chemical reaction. The volume fraction of each mineral is changed due to a small reaction. The porosity of the interlayer rock is constantly changed every time during calculation. The volume change of the mineral is obtained by multiplying the molar volume of a single mineral by the reaction rate and the reaction time. The porosity of the interlayer rock caused by the chemical reaction is calculated from the change in mineral composition. The change of matrix permeability of a single rock reference unit is calculated by the change of the porosity of the interlayer rock. The change of permeability with porosity is calculated based on the Carman-Kozeny equation. The Kozeny model equates porous media to a large number of tortuous capillary bundles with the same pore radius, and combined with Darcy's law to solve the Poisson equation, to obtain relationship between the pore radius and the permeability after the reaction of the interlayer rock changes. The relationship between the breakthrough pressure of the barrier and the median radius of the pore is obtained based on the Washburn theory. The relationship between the dynamic change of the interlayer rock porosity, the interlayer rock permeability and the breakthrough pressure the interlayer rock after carbon dioxide injection due to dissolution is obtained. The breakthrough pressure result of the interlayer after the carbon dioxide injection is converted into the equivalent gas column sealing height. The maximum allowable breakthrough pressure of the rock at the bottom of the interlayer is obtained by Smith1966 formula. The micro-sealing level of the barrier is evaluated based on the standard for dividing the height of the sealing gas column.
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC S03-F06B

PT P
PN CN109363969-A; CN109363969-B
TI Method for utilizing active ingredients of            wormwood, involves dividing wormwood into Artemisia            argyi folium and wormwood root, extracting total            flavonoids and crude polysaccharides of Artemisia            argyi, concentrating, separating and purifying.
AU HUANG J
   LIN Z
   LI J
   YU P
   SUN Z
AE GUANGDONG GEECHING BIOLOGICAL MEDICINE (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019207050
AB    NOVELTY - Method for utilizing active ingredients of                wormwood, involves dividing the wormwood into                Artemisia argyi folium and wormwood root, drying                the leaves of A.argyi folium, crushing into a                powder, extracting the A.argyi coarse powder using                a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device,                separating using a supercritical carbon dioxide                rectification column to obtain A.argyi essential                oil light oil, A.argyi essential oil heavy oil and                extract to obtain A.argyi cake, (S4) purifying the                A.argyi essential oil extract by molecular                distillation to obtain A.argyi essential oil heavy                oil and A.argyi wax, adding the A.argyi cake to a                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device,                adding entrainer, extracting the total flavonoids                and crude polysaccharides of the A.argyi to obtain                a total flavonoids and crude polysaccharide extract                of the A.argyi, concentrating, separating and                purifying.
   USE - Method for utilizing active ingredients of                wormwood.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively provides main product                e.g. essential oil, total flavonoids,                polysaccharides, and A.argyi hydrosol, and                increases the added value of wormwood                resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for utilizing active ingredients of                wormwood, which involves (S1) dividing the wormwood                into A.argyi folium and wormwood root, (S2) drying                the leaves of A.argyi folium to a moisture content                of 8% or more, crushing into a powder of 20-100                mesh, (S3) extracting the A.argyi coarse powder                using a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                device, separating using a supercritical carbon                dioxide rectification column to obtain A.argyi                essential oil light oil, A.argyi essential oil                heavy oil and extract to obtain A.argyi cake, (S4)                purifying the A.argyi essential oil extract by                molecular distillation to obtain A.argyi essential                oil heavy oil and A.argyi wax, (S5) adding the                A.argyi cake to a supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction device having gas flow rate of 10-30                kg/hour, adding 1-3 mL/g entrainer which is 20-95%                ethanol, extracting the total flavonoids and crude                polysaccharides of the A.argyi at 35-80 degrees C                and 20-40 MPa for 30-180 minutes, analyzing the                analytical kettle temperature of 20-35 degrees C                and pressure of 3-6 MPa to obtain a total                flavonoids and crude polysaccharide extract of the                A.argyi, (S6) concentrating, separating and                purifying the total flavonoids and crude                polysaccharide extracts of the A.argyi. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for                utilizing active ingredients of wormwood, which                involves dividing the wormwood into A.argyi folium                and wormwood root, washing the wormwood root,                crushing to 20-100 mesh, distilling the wormwood                root raw material in a steam distillation device at                80-95 degrees C for 2-4.5 hours, cooling the                collected distillate at 20-30 degrees C, collecting                the distillate to obtain A.argyi hydrosol, and                concentrating by reverse osmosis at 0.85-2.5 MPa                using a reverse osmosis membrane having salt                permeability of 98.0-99.6% and membrane pore size                of 0.15-1.6 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The concentration,                  separation and purification of the total                  flavonoids and crude polysaccharide extracts of                  the A.argyi involves concentrating the total                  flavonoids and crude polysaccharide extract of                  the A.argyi in a concentration tank at 30-60                  degrees C to a concentration of (1/5)-(1/2) of                  the original volume to obtain a concentrated                  liquid, precipitating the concentrated liquid                  with 80-100% ethanol at 4 degrees C overnight,                  filtering to obtain an alcohol precipitation                  filtrate and an alcohol precipitation residue,                  concentrating the alcohol precipitation filtrate                  under reduced pressure at 30-60 degrees C to                  obtain a concentrated extract, drying the                  concentrated extract to obtain a crude total                  flavonoids, diluting the concentrated extract                  with distilled water or 20-95% ethanol to obtain                  0.2-0.5 mg/mL sample solution having pH of 4-6,                  adsorbing using a macroporous adsorption resin at                  (0.5 g crude)/(100 g macroporous adsorption                  resin), eluting the adsorbed macroporous resin                  with 3-4 times distilled water and 60-80% ethanol                  at 1-3 BV/hour, collecting the ethanol eluate,                  concentrating the eluate, drying under vacuum at                  60 degrees C to obtain a total flavonoids,                  centrifuging the alcohol precipitation residue to                  obtain a precipitate, drying the precipitate to                  obtain a crude polysaccharide, performing                  fractional alcohol precipitation of the                  precipitate using 2-4 vol.% ethanol having a                  concentration of 80%, 90%, 95%, and 100%,                  precipitating the alcohol at 4 degrees C for 4                  hours each time, removing the supernatant by                  centrifugation, and drying the precipitate to                  obtain a primary polysaccharide. The molecular                  distillation involves heating and liquidizing the                  A.argyi essential oil extract in a water bath,                  collecting the A.argyi essential oil heavy oil in                  a light component bottle, and collecting the                  A.argyi wax in a heavy component bottle. The                  A.argyi cake is extracted from the essential oil                  of A.argyi, and dried. The extraction of                  essential oil from the total flavonoids and crude                  polysaccharides of A.argyi is continuous process                  or intermittent process. Preferred Conditions:                  The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is                  carried out at 35-60 degrees C, inlet pressure of                  20-28 MPa and outlet pressure of 19-26 MPa. The                  supercritical carbon dioxide distillation is                  carried out at 15-60 degrees C, inlet pressure of                  18-26 MPa, and outlet pressure of 8-25 MPa. The                  separation is carried out at 15-75 degrees C and                  5-25 MPa. The molecular distillation is carried                  out at evaporation surface temperature of 65-85                  degrees C, feed rate of 100-150 mL/hour,                  condensation layer temperature of 35-50 degrees C                  and pressure of 0.1-0.5 Pa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The macroporous                  adsorption resin is S-8 macroporous resin, D-101                  (ion-exchange resin), XDA-1, AB-8 (ion-exchange                  resin), LSA-20 macroporous resin, NKA-9                  macroporous resin, and/or NKA-II macroporous                  resin.

PN CN114568514-A
TI Preservative-free aerated beverage useful for            hydration of macromolecular substances, comprises cow            milk components, concentrated lactic acid bacteria            fermentation liquid, alpha-cyclodextrin, soluble            soybean polysaccharide, carbon dioxide, fructose syrup,            erythritol and sucralose.
AU MENG D
   YAN P
   HOU T
   FAN Q
   BAGENNA
   LI J
   WEI Z
AE INNER MONGOLIA YILI IND GROUP CO LTD (IMYL-C)
GA 202286308C
AB    NOVELTY - Preservative-free aerated beverage comprises                3-18.5 wt.% cow milk component, 1-5 wt.%                concentrated lactic acid bacteria fermentation                liquid, 0.2-0.6 wt.% alpha-cyclodextrin, 1.5-4 wt.%                soluble soybean polysaccharide, and 2-4 wt.% carbon                dioxide. The milk protein content in the aerated                beverage is 0.3-1.2%.
   USE - The preservative-free aerated beverage is                useful for hydration of macromolecular substances,                e.g. protein, so that the space for dissolving                carbon dioxide gas and forming carbonic acid which                greatly reduces the material liquid gas-soluble and                final product gas content.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage has high protein content,                nutritional value, good taste, bubble and flavor,                and no preservative thus jointly maintains the                quality and taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing preservative-free aerated beverage,                comprising (i) heating the batching water to                60-65degrees Celsius, adding soluble soybean                polysaccharide in a fully stirred state, using the                chemical equipment to mix the material for 15-20                minutes to make the material liquid into a uniform                mixed liquid, and cooling the material to below                20degrees Celsius after mixing; (ii) mixing skim                milk or reduced milk and gum liquid evenly in the                state of turning on stirring, adding concentrated                lactic acid bacteria fermentation liquid and                alpha-cyclodextrin, diluting the volume of the                mixture to 70% of the batching amount with the                batching water, and mixing and stirring the                material and liquid evenly; (iii) diluting the                above-mentioned mixed materials to the batching                amount with the batching water, and stirring                evenly; (iv) subjecting the material to                homogenization, ultra-high temperature                sterilization and degassing treatment, where the                homogenization is carried out at 70-75degrees                Celsius, adjusting the homogenizing pressure to the                secondary pressure 40 bar, and the primary pressure                to 200 bar, and sterilizing at 110-112degrees                Celsius for 4 seconds; (v) cooling the feed liquid                of to 2-7degrees Celsius, mixing online with                aseptic carbon dioxide according to the gas content                requirements of the final product and storing                temporarily in a sterile carbonization buffer tank                with a carbon dioxide back pressure of 0.4-0.5 MPa;                and (vi) sterilizing the packaging material,                performing isobaric filling and capping in a                sterile airtight environment, where the filling                temperature is 2-10degrees Celsius.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The cow milk component                  comprises at least one of skimmed milk powder,                  defatted milk and/or whey protein powder. The                  preservative-free aerated beverage further                  comprises 0-80 wt.% fructose syrup, 0-50 wt.%                  erythritol, 0-0.3 wt.% sucralose, 0-90 wt.% white                  granulated sugar and at least one of more acidity                  regulators comprising 0.5-3 wt.% citric acid,                  0.1-0.3 wt.% sodium citrate, 0-3 wt.% lactic acid                  and 0.5-2 wt.% phosphoric acid and 0-2 wt.%                  edible flavor.Preferred Method: The method before step (i)                  comprises heat the ingredient water that accounts                  for 9% of the ingredient amount to 50-55degrees                  Celsius, adding at least one of skimmed milk                  powder and/or whey protein powder slowly under                  full stirring, stirring the ingredients for 15-20                  minutes thus makes the feed liquid fully                  dissolved and evenly, adding the dissolved                  reduced milk at 45-55degrees Celsius for 25-30                  minutes, hydrating, filtering the reconstituted                  milk through a 60-80 meshes single or double                  filter, and cooling to 1-12degrees Celsius. The                  step (i) comprises heating 20-25% of the                  ingredient water to 60-65degrees Celsius, adding                  fructose syrup, erythritol and soluble soybean                  polysaccharide slowly under full stirring,                  adopting chemical equipment with mixing,                  dispersing, shearing and emulsifying effects,                  chemical material for 15-20 minutes to make the                  material liquid into a uniform mixed liquid                  without visible particles, and cooling the                  material to below 20degrees Celsius after mixing.                  The step (ii) comprises adding sodium citrate and                  sucralose are added while the concentrated lactic                  acid bacteria fermentation broth and                  alpha-cyclodextrin are added. The preparation                  method between step (ii) and step (iii) comprises                  preparing of acid solution comprising dissolving                  the citric acid with the mixing water equivalent                  to 10 times the volume of the acid for 10-15                  minutes and regulating acid online by mixing the                  acid liquid and the milk gum mixture online                  according to the ratio of 1:2.5-1:3.5 and cooling                  to 1-12degrees Celsius and the step (iii)                  comprises using the batching water to adjust the                  volume of the mixed material obtained by acid                  preparation and online acid adjustment to the                  batching amount, adding the edible flavor and                  stirring evenly.
