PN CN114560486-A
TI Carbon dioxide indirect mineralization method with            recyclable leaching aid, comprises e.g. preparing            calcium-rich leaching solution, performing            mineralization to obtain calcium carbonate primary            product and mineralization mother liquor, purifying,            recovering ammonia, regenerating amino acid.
AU OU Y
   WANG Y
   LI C
   ZHU X
   GONG Y
AE UNIV LANZHOU TECHNOLOGY (UYLZ-C)
GA 202277401C
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide indirect mineralization method                with recyclable leaching aid, comprises (i)                dissolving aspartic acid in ammonia water to obtain                alkaline leaching aid, (ii) adding industrial                by-product gypsum into leaching aid, mixing, and                filtering to obtain calcium-rich leaching solution,                (iii) performing mineralization reaction, and                filtering to obtain initial calcium carbonate                primary product and mineralization mother liquor,                (iv) washing the calcium carbonate primary product                with ammonia water, and filtering to obtain                high-purity calcium carbonate and washing                filtrate,(v) mixing the mineralized mother liquor                and washing filtrate, evaporating and concentrating                to obtain concentrated solution rich in aspartic                acid, and recycling the steam-condensed ammonia                water for secondary use, and (vi) adding sulfuric                acid to adjust pH, and precipitating aspartic acid                crystals in the liquid phase, recycling after aging                and filtration.
   USE - The method is useful for indirect                mineralization of carbon dioxide with recyclable                leaching aid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes alkaline leaching aid has                ideal leaching efficiency and high purity of                calcium carbonate which solves the key problems of                large consumption of ammonia and difficulty in                separation of ammonium sulfate and leaching aid                that exist when acid or neutral leaching aid is                used in the prior art.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide indirect mineralization method                with recyclable leaching aid, comprises (i)                dissolving aspartic acid in ammonia water to obtain                alkaline leaching aid, (ii) adding industrial                by-product gypsum into leaching aid, stirring and                mixing, and filtering after the leaching reaches                equilibrium to obtain calcium-rich leaching                solution, (iii) introducing carbon dioxide into the                leaching solution to carry out mineralization                reaction, and filtering to obtain initial calcium                carbonate product and mineralization mother liquor,                (iv) washing the calcium carbonate primary product                with ammonia water, and filtering to obtain                high-purity calcium carbonate and washing filtrate,                (v) mixing the mineralized mother liquor and                washing filtrate, evaporating and concentrating to                obtain concentrated solution rich in aspartic acid,                and recycling the steam-condensed ammonia water for                secondary use, and (vi) adding sulfuric acid to                adjust the pH of the concentrated solution to                acidity, and precipitating aspartic acid crystals                in the liquid phase, recycling after aging and                filtration, and the filtrate can be used to recover                ammonium sulfate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aspartic acid is                  preferably L-aspartic acid. The industrial                  by-product gypsum is desulfurized gypsum,                  phosphogypsum, and/or titanium gypsum dry powder,                  and the average particle size is less than 150                  &#956;m. The leaching aid is composed of the                  concentration of ammonia water is 3-12 wt.%, in                  terms of ammonia, the addition amount of aspartic                  acid is not higher than the saturated mass of                  aspartic acid in the ammonia water concentration                  range, and the pH value of leaching aid is                  ensured to be 9.0-11. In step (iv), the                  concentration of ammonia water for washing is                  3-12 wt.%, and in terms of NH3, the calcium                  carbonate purity is greater than or equal to 97                  %. In step (v) the pH value of the concentrated                  solution is 6.5-8.5. In step (vi), the sulfuric                  acid is sulfuric acid aqueous solution of                  concentration 5-10 mol/l, and the pH value of                  concentrated solution is 2-3 when separating out                  aspartic acid.Preferred Method: In step (ii), the                  solid-liquid ratio of by-product gypsum to                  leaching aid is 10:1-50:1 g/L, the leaching                  temperature is 30-60&#8451;, and the time for                  leaching to reach equilibrium is 1-2 hours. In                  step (iii), Carbon dioxide (CO2) is pure CO2gas,                  or flue gas discharged in industrial production,                  CO2content is 10-100 %, mineralization reaction                  is carried out at 20-60&#8451; for 30-120                  minutes.

PN CN114233649-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide compressor, has compressor impeller set in compressor shell, and compressor volute matched with impeller, where volute has two one-stage blades of impeller and volute space matched with secondary blade.
AU ZHANG N
   WANG G
   WANG Q
   WANG B
   TIAN Y
   JIANG P
AE SHANGHAI PUDONG ADVANCED ENERGY & POWER (SHAN-Non-standard)
   INST ENG THERMOPHYSICS CHINESE (CAET-C)
GA 2022490862
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide compressor comprises a compressor shell and at least one compressor impeller set in the compressor shell. The compressor volute is fixedly set in a compressor housing, and is rotatably arranged in the compressed volute, where the compressor impellers includes an impeller hub, a first-stage blade, and a second-level blade. The two rows of comb teeth structures are designed on the radial wall surfaces of the clapboard balance step structure, and are used to realize the inter-stage sealing between the two-stage blades of the compressor. The hub balance steps are matched with the partition balance steps, so as to achieve the self-balance of the axial force of the compression volute. The compression volut is processed with one balance air hole channel that is not connected to the volute space, so as to further reduce the inter-stage leakage between the two stages of the compressor, at the same time, balancing a portion of the axial thrust.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide compressor for use in a power system for compression of super critical carbon dioxide. Can also be used for a nuclear reactor, solar energy, geothermal energy, industrial waste heat and chemical dye combustion.
   ADVANTAGE - The axial length of the compressor is effectively reduced, the critical rotating speed of the rotor is improved, the compressor structure is more compact, the rotor operation safety is reliable, and the processing cost is reduced. The compressor level leakage is reduced, at the same time, balancing a portion of the axial thrust. The sealing effect is good, the leakage rate is low, and also the number of the comb teeth is changed, adjusting the level leakage amount. The impeller itself realizes self-balance of a part of axial force, and reduces the design difficulty and processing cost of the thrust bearing. The volute is designed with a balance air hole, which is induced from the outside and the other end is connected with the impeller balance step position, the air entraining pressure is higher than the outlet pressure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the supercritical carbon dioxide compressor.

   Inlet channel (3)
   Volute (4)

   Dry gas sealing (8)
   Flange (9)

PT P
PN WO2021225641-A1; US2021340077-A1
TI Integrated process for conversion of feed stream comprising carbon dioxide to product stream comprising hydrocarbons, by performing electrolysis step, reverse water gas shift step, hydrocarbon synthesis step, and auto-thermal reforming step.
AU SCHUETZLE R
   SCHUETZLE D
   WRIGHT H
   HANBURY O
   CALDWELL M
   RODRIGUEZ R
AE INFINIUM TECHNOLOGY LLC (INFI-Non-standard)
   INFINIUM TECHNOLOGY LLC (INFI-Non-standard)
GA 2021C59026
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated process for the conversion of a feed stream comprising carbon dioxide to a product stream comprising hydrocarbons, involves (a) performing an electrolysis step by converting an electrolyzer feed stream comprising water to an electrolyzer product stream comprising hydrogen and oxygen, (b) performing a reverse water gas shift step by reacting at least a portion of the hydrogen from the electrolyzer product stream with a stream comprising carbon dioxide to produce a reverse water gas shift product stream comprising carbon monoxide, (c) performing a hydrocarbon synthesis step by reacting at least a portion of the hydrogen from the electrolyzer product stream with a stream comprising at least a portion of the reverse water gas shift product stream to produce a hydrocarbon synthesis product stream comprising hydrocarbons, and (d) performing an auto-thermal reforming step.
   USE - The integrated process is used for producing fuels in addition to chemicals (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The integrated process is economical, enhances catalyst performance, and has highly versatile and excellent resistance to contaminants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated process for the conversion of a feed stream comprising carbon dioxide to a product stream comprising hydrocarbons, involves (a) performing an electrolysis step by converting an electrolyzer feed stream comprising water to an electrolyzer product stream comprising hydrogen and oxygen where at least a portion of the electricity used in the electrolysis step is from renewable or low carbon sources, (b) performing a reverse water gas shift step by reacting at least a portion of the hydrogen from the electrolyzer product stream with a stream comprising carbon dioxide to produce a reverse water gas shift product stream comprising carbon monoxide, (c) performing a hydrocarbon synthesis step by reacting at least a portion of the hydrogen from the electrolyzer product stream with a stream comprising at least a portion of the reverse water gas shift product stream to produce a hydrocarbon synthesis product stream comprising hydrocarbons, and (d) performing an auto-thermal reforming step by reacting at least a portion of the oxygen produced by electrolysis with a stream or streams comprising (a) unreacted reactants from the hydrocarbon synthesis step and (b) products from the hydrocarbon synthesis step that are not hydrocarbons 5-24C in length.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pressure of reverse water gas shift step and the hydrocarbon synthesis step are operated at pressures within 50 psi of each other. The reverse water gas shift reactor feedstock is heated with an electric radiant furnace to at least 1500 degrees F and the reactor vessel is an adiabatic reactor where the exit temperature is at least 100 degrees F less than the reactor inlet temperature. The electricity used in the radiant furnace is less than 0.5 MWh (megawatt-hour) electricity/metric ton (MT) of carbon dioxide in the feed gas. Preferred Composition: The reverse water gas shift reactor feed has a composition such that the molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide is 2.5-3.5. The hydrocarbon synthesis feedstock has a molar hydrogen to carbon monoxide ratio of 1.90-2.20 and the hydrocarbon synthesis catalyst comprises cobalt and the 4-24C selectivity is greater than 70% and where the amount of carbon monoxide converted to products heavier than 24C is less than 10%. Preferred Method: The auto-thermal reforming step involves passing steam as a feed where the steam to carbon ratio is 0.40-1. One of the feeds to the auto-thermal reforming step comprises natural gas. The radiant elements may be divided into zones to give a controlled pattern of heating of the reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reactor. Preferred Components: The autothermal reforming (ATR) catalyst is a solid solution catalyst. The chemicals produced as portion of the process comprise methanol, solvents, olefins and n-paraffins.
   EXAMPLE - Examples given describes about general preparation of raw material of integrated process which do not support the above claims.

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide generator set molten salt heat storage peak regulation system has group B exothermic molten salt heat exchanger whose hot side outlet is connected with inlet of group B molten salt cold tank.

   WU S
   ZHANG T
   YAO M
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 202181159S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a group B molten salt cold tank (11) whose outlet is connected to the cold side inlet of a group B heat storage molten salt heat exchanger (14). The cold side outlet of the group B heat storage molten salt heat exchanger is connected with the inlet of the group B molten salt heat tank. The outlet of the group B molten salt tank is connected to the hot side inlet of a group B exothermic molten salt heat exchanger (18) through a group B exothermic molten salt regulating valve (16), a group B exothermic molten salt pump (17), and the hot side inlet of the group B exothermic molten salt heat exchanger. The hot side outlet of the group B exothermic molten salt heat exchanger is connected with the inlet of the group B molten salt cold tank. The working medium of the two sets of molten salt heat storage peak shaving system is molten salt.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide generator set molten salt heat storage peak regulation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system heats the molten salt by extracting the high-temperature working fluid from the boiler outlet and stores it in a hot tank when the power grid dispatch load decreases. The stored high-temperature molten salt is set to quickly heat portion of the circulating working fluid to enter the turbine for work when the grid dispatching load increases. The molten salt heat release is completed to realize the function of heat storage and peak regulation in the supercritical carbon dioxide cycle power generation system, which greatly promotes the consumption of renewable energy power generation by the power grid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an operating method of the molten salt heat storage peak regulation system of a supercritical carbon dioxide generator set.

   Group B exothermic molten salt heat exchanger (18)

TI System for capturing carbon dioxide and            distributing for agricultural purpose, has distribution            array that comprises blower, valve and gas emitters            through which dewatered cooled stream of flue gas is            selectively discharged amidst plants.

GA 202109939P
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has a gas extraction device                that receives a portion of the flue gas stream                flowing through the venting stack and directs a                portion of the flue gas into a first conduit                connected to the gas extraction device. A gas                distribution manifold is connected to a first flue                gas quench column. The gas distribution manifold                receives the cooled stream of flue gas. The flue                gas distribution manifold comprises manometric trap                for water condensate drainage from the cooled                stream of flue gas resulting in a dewatered cooled                stream of flue gas. A distribution array is                connected to the gas distribution manifold. The                distribution array receives the dewatered cooled                stream of flue gas and is placed in a field                comprising the plants. The distribution array                comprises a blower, a valve (36) and multiple gas                emitters through which the dewatered cooled stream                of flue gas is selectively discharged amidst the                plants upon activation of the valve.
   USE - System for capturing carbon dioxide and                distributing for agricultural purpose.
   ADVANTAGE - The application of carbon dioxide can be                supplemented by providing additional components                directly to the plants which maintain a level of                fertilization and irrigation suitable for the                increased biomass and water utilization efficiency                resulting from the increased intake of carbon                dioxide by the plants. The multiple smaller sized                diameter ducting in the duct bank provide the                advantage of higher external surface area than a                single large duct to facilitate increased cooling                and condensation by contact of the outside of the                ducting with ambient air. The valves can be                manipulated according to changes in environmental                conditions such as wind speed and direction, or                lack of wind altogether to accomplish optimal                cooling/dilution/distribution/use of the flue gas                exiting the array in the midst of the local field                by virtue of release to ambient atmosphere in the                vicinity of the flora desired to be enhanced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for capturing carbon dioxide from a flue gas stream                emitted from a venting stack of a stationary flue                gas generator.

PN CN111153877-A
TI Continuously synthesizing furandicarboxylic acid using furancarboxylic acid comprises e.g. dissolving furancarboxylic acid in solvent to obtain raw material solution and using raw material solution and carbon dioxide as mixed raw materials.
AU QIAN C
   JIN H
   ZHOU S
   RUAN J
   WANG S
   CHEN X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG QUZHOU RES INST (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202044229L
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously synthesizing green furandicarboxylic acid using furancarboxylic acid, comprises e.g. dissolving furancarboxylic acid as a raw material in a solvent to obtain a raw material solution according to the furancarboxylic acid, carbon dioxide, using the raw material solution and carbon dioxide as mixed raw materials to enter the fixed bed reactor from the top of the fixed bed reactor for reaction, and fixing transition metal supported catalyst in the fixed bed reactor, where the mass ratio of the transition metal-supported catalyst to the total amount of furancarboxylic acid, the reaction pressure is normal pressure, the reaction product is discharged from the bottom of the fixed bed reactor, then entering the vacuum distillation tower for heating, discharging furandicarboxylic acid from the bottom of the vacuum distillation column, and discharging gas discharged from the top of the vacuum distillation column to enters the recovery tank and condensing.
   USE - The method is useful for continuously synthesizing furandicarboxylic acid using furancarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: prepares furandicarboxylic acid, which has simple process, environmentally-friendly and high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Continuously synthesizing furandicarboxylic acid using furancarboxylic acid, comprises dissolving furancarboxylic acid as a raw material in a solvent to obtain a raw material solution according to the furancarboxylic acid: carbon dioxide at a molar ratio of 1:1.5 2.5, using the raw material solution and carbon dioxide as mixed raw materials to enter the fixed bed reactor from the top of the fixed bed reactor for reaction, and fixing transition metal supported catalyst in the fixed bed reactor, where the mass ratio of the transition metal-supported catalyst to the total amount of furancarboxylic acid undergoing the reaction is 5-10%, the reaction temperature set in the fixed bed reactor is 70-100 degrees C, the reaction pressure is normal pressure, the residence time of the raw materials in the fixed bed reactor is 100-150 minutes, the reaction product is discharged from the bottom of the fixed bed reactor, then entering the vacuum distillation tower for heating, discharging furandicarboxylic acid from the bottom of the vacuum distillation column, discharging gas discharged from the top of the vacuum distillation column to enters the recovery tank and condensing, and then circulating back to the top of the fixed bed reactor by the pump and entering the fixed-bed reactor together with mixed raw materials for secondary reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is ethylene glycol dimethyl ether, dioxane, and acetonitrile. The feed liquid ratio of furan formic acid to solvent is 1 kg/(10 plus minus 1) l. The transition metal includes nickel, copper, rhodium, and palladium. The molecular sieve is in 4A type, X type, Y type, ZSM-5 type, aluminum trioxide, silicon dioxide and zirconium dioxide. Preferred Method: The preparation method of the transition metal supported catalyst comprises (i) dissolving the soluble salt of the transition metal in water to obtain the transition metal salt solution, dispersing the molecular sieve in water to obtain the dispersion liquid, mixing the transition metal salt solution and the dispersion liquid under stirring conditions for 3-6 hours, allowing to stand for 1-2 hours, the weight ratio of transition metal in the soluble salt to the molecular sieve is 1-1.6:100; (ii) roasting the precipitate obtained in step (i) at 400-600 degrees C for 3-5 hours to obtain transition metal supported catalyst.

PN CN110093230-A
TI Production of sparkling yellow wine involves taking raw materials including sticky rice, cooking, steaming, cooling, adding yellow wine yeast, saccharifying, fermenting, pressing, filtering, adding carbon dioxide gas, testing, and filling.
AU LI M
AE LI M (LIMM-Individual)
GA 201970199M
AB    NOVELTY - Production of sparkling yellow wine comprises taking drinking water, sticky rice, yellow wine yeast, raw wheat koji, ripe wheat koji, and food-grade liquid carbon dioxide; soaking selected stick rice in water, kneading, and measuring water absorption rate of higher than 125%; cooking for 20 minutes, mixing, leveling, pouring warm water, covering, steaming for 30 minutes, and taking out; cooling, adding yellow wine yeast, and stirring; digging hole, culturing bacteria, saccharifying, heat preserving, cooling, filling with brewing water, adding raw wheat koji, stirring, adding drinking water, controlling temperature to 25 degrees C, and fermenting; adding ripe wheat koji, keeping temperature at 10-12 degrees C, and fermenting; plate-and-frame pressing and filtering; diatomite filtering and fine filtering; refrigerating and de-fogging; ultra-filtration membrane filtering to obtain sterile clear wine; and adding food-grade carbon dioxide gas, sampling for testing, and filling.
   USE - The method is used for producing sparkling yellow wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The wine is refreshing, delicate, and nutritious and has improved quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of sparkling yellow wine comprises:
   (A) taking 3000 pts. wt. drinking water, 1000 pts. wt. sticky rice, 5 pts. wt. yellow wine yeast, 40 pts. wt. raw wheat koji, 80 pts. wt. ripe wheat koji, and 3 pts. wt. food-grade liquid carbon dioxide;
   (B) soaking selected stick rice in water, kneading, and measuring water absorption rate of higher than 125%;
   (C) cooking for 20 minutes, mixing, leveling, pouring warm water, covering, steaming for 30 minutes, and taking out;
   (D) cooling using 35-38 degrees C cold tap water, adding 0.5 wt.% yellow wine yeast, and stirring; digging hole, culturing bacteria, saccharifying at 40 degrees C, heat preserving, cooling, filling with brewing water for 48 hours, adding raw wheat koji, stirring, adding drinking water, controlling temperature to 25 degrees C, and fermenting for 48 hours;
   (E) adding ripe wheat koji, keeping temperature at 10-12 degrees C, and fermenting for 55 degrees C days;
   (F) plate-and-frame pressing and filtering;
   (G) diatomite filtering and fine filtering;
   (H) refrigerating at - 2 degrees C to - 4 degrees C for more than 72 hours and de-fogging;
   (I) ultra-filtration membrane filtering to obtain sterile clear wine; and
   (J) adding food-grade carbon dioxide gas to the saturated state through a gas-water mixer to form gas-containing sake, sampling for testing, and filling.

PN CN109604776-A
TI Robot automatic welding seam tracking detection method involves taking visual image of molten pool during short circuit of carbon dioxide dripping and extracting geometric parameters of weld pool, after image is processed by computer.
AU ZHANG L
AE WUHU CHANGRUI AUTO PARTS CO LTD (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 201935903K
AB    NOVELTY - The robot automatic welding seam tracking detection method involves (S1) using artificial neural network algorithm to simulate the dynamic process of pulse welding molten pool, (S2) training the artificial neural network model with the dynamic characteristic parameters of the weld pool obtained by real-time acquisition and processing, and performing self-learning, (S3) taking a visual image of the molten pool during the short circuit of the carbon dioxide dripping, so that acquiring the welding scene feature and (S4) extracting the geometric parameters of the weld pool, after the image is processed by the computer, where the geometric parameters of the weld pool are the weld pool area, the weld pool length, the weld pool width and the weld pool profile curve and adjusting the welding current in real time according to the weld pool geometry parameters to control welding penetration.
   USE - Robot automatic welding seam tracking detection method.
   ADVANTAGE - The interference of arc flashing, splashing and soot are avoided on the imaging process by taking the molten pool in the process of excessive carbon dioxide dripping short circuit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The robot automatic welding seam tracking detection method involves (S1) using artificial neural network algorithm to simulate the dynamic process of pulse welding molten pool, (S2) training the artificial neural network model with the dynamic characteristic parameters of the weld pool obtained by real-time acquisition and processing, and performing self-learning, (S3) taking a visual image of the molten pool during the short circuit of the carbon dioxide dripping, so that acquiring the welding scene feature, (S4) extracting the geometric parameters of the weld pool, after the image is processed by the computer, where the geometric parameters of the weld pool are the weld pool area, the weld pool length, the weld pool width and the weld pool profile curve, and adjusting the welding current in real time according to the weld pool geometry parameters to control welding penetration, recording the start and end time of the image taken by the camera with a logic control circuit, the circuit starts to expose when a short circuit occurs when the carbon dioxide drops, the exposure time is 2 ms, and the short circuit time is greater than 2 ms and automatically shielding two short circuits in the same exposure.

AB    NOVELTY - A hydroxy-substituted amine-based metal complex catalyst, is new. The catalyst is in solid or dissolved state.
   USE - New hydroxy-substituted amine-based metal complex catalyst used in copolymerization of carbon dioxide and epoxide e.g. cyclohexene oxide, oxirane, propylene oxide, limonene oxide, styrene oxide, and cyclopentene oxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydroxy-substituted amine-based metal complex catalyst having high chemoselectivity even at low pressures of carbon dioxide, is manufactured efficiently and economically by simple method without using toxic phosgene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydroxy-substituted amine-based metal complex catalyst of formula: LxMx (I), is new. The catalyst is in solid or dissolved state.
   M = Zn(II), Cr(II), Co(II), Mn(II), Fe(II), Ni(II), Cu(II), Cr(III), Co(III), Mn(III), Fe(III), Al(III) or Mg(II);
   L = group of formula (a1) or (a2);
   R1,R1a,R10,R10a,R3-R7,R40,R3a-R7a,R40a = H, halo (preferably, F, CI, Br, or I), CF3, CN, SR'k, S(O)R'k, S(O)2R'k, OR'k, SiR'kR'lR'm, C(O)R'k, C(O)OR'k, C(O)N(R'l)R'k, OC(O)R'k, OC(O)OR'k, OC(O)N(R'k)R'l, NHR, NH2, NO2, or 4-12C aryl, 1-10C alkyl, 1-10C heteroalkyl, 3-7C cycloalkyl, 3-7C heterocycloalkyl, or 4-12C heteroaryl (all optionally substituted);
   R2 = H, CF3, CN, or alkyl or aryl (both optionally substituted);
   or two or more R3-R7,R40,R3a-R7a,R40a = double bond;
   R20,R20a,R30 = R1;
   or R20a+R30 = bond in order to provide cis-conformation at the imine-moiety;
   a,d = 0-2, preferably 0;
   a/d = 1 or 2;
   b = 0 or 1, preferably 0;
   c,e = 0-2, preferably 2;
   c/e = 1 or 2;
   x = 2-12;
   R,R'k,R'l,R'm = H, optionally substituted 1-4C alkyl (preferably, CH3), 1-4C heteroalkyl, 3-7C cycloalkyl, 3-7C heterocycloalkyl, 4-12C aryl, or 4-12C heteroaryl;and
   or 2 or more of R'k,R'l,R'm = optionally substituted (hetero)alicyclyl or (hetero)aryl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of compound (I); and
   (2) copolymerization of carbon dioxide and epoxide, which involves contacting the catalyst with carbon dioxide and epoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The compound (I) is obtained by reacting a hydroxy-substituted amine of formula (I) as a ligand with metal compound.
   EXAMPLE - 178.2 mg (S)-3-(2-(hydroxydiphenylmethyl)pyrrolidin-1-yl)propan-1-ol was dissolved in 7 mL dry dichloromethane, 231 mu l zinc bis(bis(trimethylsilyl)amide) was added, the solution was stirred overnight, volatile substance was removed under vacuum, and crystallized with pentane or diethyl ether, to obtain 214.5 mg (S)-3-(2-(hydroxydiphenylmethyl)pyrrolidin-1-yl)propan-1-ol-zinc complex.

PT P
PN CN108080012-A
TI Preparing dioctyl phthalate solid acid catalyst comprises e.g. calcining phosphotungstic acid solution, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, adding chitosan, bentonite, activated carbon and iron powder and heating.
AU WU M
AE WU M (WUMM-Individual)
GA 2018435133
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dioctyl phthalate solid acid catalyst comprises (i) preparing phosphotungstic acid solution, (ii) dipping the carrier in the phosphotungstic acid solution, drying, and calcining to obtain catalyst carrier, (iii) crushing the catalyst carrier, loading the crushed catalyst carrier with supercritical carbon dioxide extraction agent, and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, (iv) dropping the acid solution to the supercritical-extraction catalyst carrier, allowing to stand, adding chitosan with a degree of deacetylation greater than 90%, cooling, and allowing to stand to obtain catalyst blank, and (v) adding bentonite, activated carbon and iron powder to the catalyst blank, stirring, adding adhesive, stirring, allowing to stand, placing in an oxygen-deficient or reducing atmosphere, heating, stopping heating, reducing the room temperature to obtain porous solid acid catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing dioctyl phthalate solid acid catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing dioctyl phthalate solid acid catalyst comprises (i) preparing 0.6-0.7 mol/l phosphotungstic acid solution, (ii) dipping the carrier in the phosphotungstic acid solution for 12-13 hours, drying, and calcining to obtain catalyst carrier, (iii) crushing the catalyst carrier through a 100 meshes sieve, loading the crushed catalyst carrier with supercritical carbon dioxide extraction agent for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, (iv) uniformly dropping the acid solution to the supercritical-extraction catalyst carrier, allowing to stand for 2-3 hours at room temperature, adding chitosan with a degree of deacetylation greater than 90% under stirring at a rotating speed of 35-50 revolutions/minute at 55-56 degrees C for 2-3 hours, cooling at room temperature, and allowing to stand for 1-2 hours to obtain catalyst blank, and (v) adding bentonite, activated carbon and iron powder to the catalyst blank, uniformly stirring, continuously adding adhesive, stirring uniformly, allowing to stand for 2-3 hours, placing in an oxygen-deficient or reducing atmosphere, heating at 60 degrees C/hour, heating for 2.5 hours, when the furnace temperature reaches to 550-600 degrees C, continuously heating at 90 degrees C/hour, heating for 3.5-4 hours till 750-800 degrees C, stopping heating, reducing the room temperature at 120-125 degrees C/hour to obtain porous solid acid catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of the carrier to the phosphotungstic acid solution is 1 g/2 ml. The carrier is a mixture of aluminum oxide, zirconium dioxide and molecular sieve in a ratio of 5:1:4. The ratio of acid solution to catalyst carrier is 3 ml/g. The acid solution is a hydrochloric acid solution or a sulfuric acid solution. The amount of free hydrogen ions in the acid solution is 5-6 mol/l. The mass ratio of the catalyst blank, bentonite, activated carbon and iron powder is 30:25:15:7. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the extraction pressure is 31-32 MPa, the extraction temperature 50-55 degrees C, the carbon dioxide flow rate is 29-30 kg/hours, and the extraction time is 3-3.5 hours. The oxygen-deficient or reducing atmosphere is a vacuum, a nitrogen atmosphere, an argon atmosphere or a hydrogen atmosphere.

PT P
PN EP4006006-A1; WO2022111916-A1
TI Extracting cannabidiol from hemp comprises milling dried hemp biomass, decarboxylating, subcritical liquid carbon dioxide extraction, loading extract onto support e.g. celite, and subjecting to subcritical liquid carbon dioxide.
AU MARRIOTT R
AE BRIDGE FARM NURSERIES LTD (BRID-Non-standard)
   BRIDGE FARM NURSERIES LTD (BRID-Non-standard)
   MARRIOTT R (MARR-Individual)
GA 2022741220
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting cannabidiol from hemp comprises milling dried hemp biomass, decarboxylating, filling the first vessel with the milled and decarboxylated biomass, extracting a product comprising cannabidiol, tetrahydro-cannabinol (THC), Delta 9-THC and Delta 8-THC and other cannabinoids by injecting subcritical liquid carbon dioxide, retrieving the extraction product from the first vessel and loading onto a support e.g. celite, acidic, neutral or basic alumina or silica, loading the support into a second vessel, subjecting to a flow of subcritical liquid carbon dioxide to separate cannabidiol, separating and retrieving cannabidiol, and optionally continuing the separation process by submitting the second vessel to a flow of supercritical carbon dioxide at a mass ratio carbon dioxide to biomass of at least 10 kg carbon dioxide/kg biomass and for 30 minutes to 8 hours in order to separate the remaining products from the support.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting cannabidiol from hemp (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures simple and safe extraction and separation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting cannabidiol from hemp comprises (a) providing an apparatus comprising 2 separate vessels, (b) milling dried hemp biomass in order to obtain a product having a bulk density of at least 0.2 g/cm3, (c) decarboxylating the dried biomass of the step (b), (d) filling the first vessel with the milled and decarboxylated biomass of the step (c), (e) extracting a product comprising cannabidiol, tetrahydro-cannabinol (THC), Delta 9-THC and Delta 8-THC and other cannabinoids from the milled and decarboxylated biomass injecting subcritical liquid carbon dioxide at a pressure of at least 65 bars and at a temperature of less than 31 degrees C, using a mass ratio carbon dioxide to biomass of at least 10 kg carbon dioxide/kg biomass and for a period of 30 minutes to 8 hours, (f) retrieving the extraction product from the step (e) from the first vessel and loading onto a support selected from celite, acidic, neutral or basic alumina or silica with a mass ratio extraction product to support ranging 1-20 wt.%, preferably 5-10 wt.%, (g) loading the support of the step (f) into the second vessel, (h) submitting the second vessel of the step (g), containing a support loaded with the extraction products, to a flow of subcritical liquid carbon dioxide at a mass ratio carbon dioxide to biomass of at least 10 kg carbon dioxide/kg biomass and for 30 minutes to 8 hours to separate cannabidiol, (i) separating and retrieving cannabidiol, and (j) optionally continuing the separation process by submitting the second vessel to a flow of supercritical carbon dioxide at a mass ratio carbon dioxide to biomass of at least 10 kg carbon dioxide/kg biomass and for 30 minutes to 8 hours in order to separate the remaining products from the support, where the products comprises traces of cannabidiol, Delta 9-THC and Delta 8-THC, and other cannabinoids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The extraction and separation steps are carried out sequentially in a continuous process where the extraction vessel and the separation vessel are connected to one another, which comprises the steps of (i) providing 2 vessels connected to each other, (ii) milling dried hemp biomass in order to obtain a product having a bulk density of at least 0.2 g/cm3, (iii) decarboxylating the dried biomass of the step (ii), (iv) filling the first vessel with the milled and decarboxylated biomass of the step (iii), (v) filling the second vessel with a support selected from celite, acidic, neutral or basic alumina or silica, (vi) extracting a product comprising cannabidiol, Delta 9-THC and Delta 8-THC and other cannabinoids from the milled and decarboxylated biomass using subcritical liquid carbon dioxide at a pressure of at least 65 bars at a temperature of less than 31 degrees C in the first vessel, with a mass ratio carbon dioxide to biomass of at least 10 kg carbon dixoide/kg biomass and for 30 minutes to 8 hours, (vii) flowing the extraction product of the step (vi) into the second vessel filled with support and submitting the second vessel to the same flow of subcritical liquid carbon dioxide using the same mass ratio carbon dioxide to biomass, the same temperature and the same pressure as in the first vessel and for a period 30 minutes to 8 hours, (viii) separating and retrieving cannabidiol, and (ix) optionally continuing the separation process by submitting the second vessel to a flow of supercritical carbon dioxide at a mass ratio carbon dioxide to biomass of at least 10 kg carbon dioxide/kg biomass for 30 minutes to 8 hours in order to separate the remaining products from the support, where the products comprises traces of cannabidiol, Delta 9-THC and Delta 8-THC, and other cannabinoids. The decarboxylation is carried out at 100 degrees C for at least 1 hour. The pressure for the extraction step (e) or in extraction step (vi) is of at least 90 bars, preferably at least 100 bars. The temperature in the extraction of the step (e) or in extraction step (vi) 2 is 25 degrees C, preferably 20 degrees C. The extraction step is carried out using subcritical carbon dioxide at a mass ratio carbon dioxide to biomass of at least 25 kg carbon dioxide/kg biomass. Preferred Components: The milled biomass has a density of at least 0.3 g/ml. The mass ratio carbon dioxide to biomass of at least 30 kg carbon dioxide/kg biomass. The inert support of the step (f) or step (v) is basic alumina in order to separate CBD with undetectable levels of THC. In the step (f), the extraction product from the first vessel is loaded onto the support with a mass ratio extraction product to support ranging 5-10 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - 473 g Biomass was extracted with 32 kg carbon dioxide/kg biomass over a period of 5 hours at a pressure of 110 bar and a temperature of 15 degrees C with the extractor exit connected to the 2nd vessel containing alumina. After a period of 2.5 hours the back pressure increased and the separator was found to contain a white powder. Unlike the silica, the alumina did not absorb the CBD but did bind other cannabinoids including THC resulting in a powder that had undetectable levels of THC. After 5 hours of extraction, samples of the extracted biomass were collected from the top and bottom of the 2000 ml extractor for analysis. The carbon dioxide flow of 40 g/minute was then connected to the extractor containing the alumina and run at a pressure of 300 bar and a temperature of 50 degrees C for a further 2 hours to collect any absorbed cannabinoids.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)

PT P
PN US2018036674-A1; WO2018026518-A1; US10350538-B2
TI Producing hydrogen and carbon dioxide from a            gaseous stream, comprises e.g. passing a gaseous stream            comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide into a swing            adsorption reactor, adsorbing carbon dioxide,            recovering hydrogen stream and purging.
AU SUNDARAM N
   THOMANN H
   CALABRO D C
   MITTRICKER F
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2018136151
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen and carbon dioxide from a                gaseous stream, comprises: passing a gaseous stream                comprising at least about 60 vol.% hydrogen and at                least 10 vol.% carbon dioxide into a swing                adsorption reactor comprising many adsorbent beds                containing an adsorbent material; adsorbing carbon                dioxide on the adsorbent material in a first                adsorbent bed; recovering hydrogen stream; reducing                the pressure in the first adsorbent bed to a                pressure from 5-15 bara; further reducing the                pressure in the first adsorbent bed to a pressure                from 1.0-4 bara; and purging.
   USE - The method is useful for producing hydrogen                and carbon dioxide from a gaseous stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides separates syngas gas to                generate high purity carbon dioxide and hydrogen                streams, while reducing and/or minimizing the                energy required for the separation, and without                needing to reduce the temperature of the flue gas;                recovers high purity of carbon dioxide and high                purity hydrogen while reducing/minimizing the                amount of energy lost to capture of the exhaust                gases; utilizes reactor that increase the duration                of feed step time, while simultaneously reducing                the overall number of blow down/purge steps as well                as the number of repressurization steps per cycle                as compared to a conventional three or four bed                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing hydrogen and carbon dioxide from a                gaseous stream, comprises: passing a gaseous stream                comprising at least about 60 vol.% hydrogen and at                least 10 vol.% carbon dioxide into a swing                adsorption reactor comprising many adsorbent beds                containing an adsorbent material, where the gaseous                stream has pressure of 10-40 bara; adsorbing carbon                dioxide on the adsorbent material in a first                adsorbent bed at an adsorption temperature of at                least 400 degrees C where the adsorbent material                comprises an one of an (i) alkali metal carbonate                and an oxide of an alkaline earth metal or a                transition metal and (ii) alkaline earth metal                carbonate and an oxide of a transition metal;                recovering hydrogen stream with many at least 99                vol.% from a forward end of the first adsorbent                bed, where the recovered hydrogen stream has                pressure that differs from the pressure of the                gaseous stream by 0.5 bar or less; reducing the                pressure in the first adsorbent bed to a pressure                from 5-15 bara by equalizing the first adsorbent                bed with a another of the adsorbent beds via                equalization piping; further reducing the pressure                in the first adsorbent bed to a pressure from 1.0-4                bara by outputting a blow down stream from at least                one end of the first adsorbent bed; and purging the                first adsorbent bed with a steam purge at a                pressure from 1-4 bara to generate carbon dioxide                recovery stream, where the carbon dioxide recovery                stream comprises at least 90% of the carbon dioxide                present in the gaseous stream, and the steam purge                contains less than 1 mole of water per mole of                carbon dioxide recovered.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The swing adsorption                  reactor comprises four adsorbent beds, where                  during any one quarter of a cycle, one of the                  adsorbent beds is used only as storage vessel.                  The swing adsorption reactor comprises four                  adsorbent beds, where prior to the                  repressurizing, the first adsorbent bed increases                  its pressure via equalization with a second                  adsorbent bed and a third adsorbent bed. The                  swing adsorption reactor comprises four adsorbent                  beds, further comprises holding the first                  adsorbent bed for a period of time during which a                  second adsorbent bed, a third adsorbent bed, and                  a fourth adsorbent bed are engaged in a blowdown,                  feeding, and repressurizing step,                  respectively.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of passing, the                  adsorbing, the recovering, the reducing, further                  reducing, and the purging comprise a pressure                  swing adsorption cycle, where the gaseous stream                  and the steam purge comprises at least 95 vol.%                  of the gases introduced into the swing adsorption                  reactor during the pressure swing adsorption                  cycle. The method further comprises                  repressurizing the first adsorbent bed to a                  pressure of at least 10 bara with the gaseous                  stream. The step of reducing the pressure in the                  first adsorbent bed: comprises outputting a first                  co-current blow down stream; and further                  comprises outputting a second counter-current                  blowdown stream. The steam purge is initiated by                  contacting the adsorbent material with liquid                  water, the adsorbent material thus producing                  steam for the steam purge. The adsorbent in the                  reactor is organized as a structure possessing a                  void fraction less than 0.3. The swing adsorption                  reactor comprises three adsorbent beds, where                  during any one third of a cycle, one of the                  adsorbent beds is used only as storage vessel.                  Preferred Components: The steam purge contains                  less than 0.8 moles (preferably 0.6) water per                  mole of carbon dioxide recovered. The alkali                  metal carbonate is potassium carbonate, lithium                  carbonate, or sodium carbonate. The adsorbent                  comprises a transition metal oxide of a                  transition metal that forms an oxide with the                  metal in a +2 or +3 oxidation state. The                  adsorbent comprises at least one of lithium                  carbonate and potassium carbonate and at least                  one of lanthanum oxide, yttrium oxide, and                  magnesium oxide. The alkaline earth metal                  carbonate is magnesium carbonate or calcium                  carbonate. The adsorbent comprises at least one                  of magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate and                  at least one of lanthanum oxide, yttrium oxide,                  and magnesium oxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - Microfluidic cell culture with carbon dioxide concentration control device comprises a cell culturing chamber, the pure carbon dioxide storage bottle (1) through the pressure reducing valve (2), first valve (4) is connected with the gas mixing chamber, the first piston (3) is connected to the first valve and controlling the gas in pure carbon dioxide storage bottle, which is connected with the gas mixing chamber through the second valve (6) into the gas mixing chamber. The second piston (5) is connecting the second valve and control the external gas mixing chamber is between the gas-flow through the second valve.
   USE - Microfluidic cell culture with carbon dioxide concentration control device.
   ADVANTAGE - The microfluidic cell culture with carbon dioxide concentration control device increases the success rate of cell culture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for controlling a carbon dioxide concentration, which involves
   (A) detecting the gas pressure and the carbon dioxide concentration in the gas mixing chamber using gas pressure sensor and the carbon dioxide sensor and input the MCU control system, setting the current carbon dioxide concentration C1, the current air pressure value P1, the desired carbon dioxide concentration C0 and the desired gas pressure P0 is compared;
   (B) calculating pure carbon dioxide need to supplement the volume of MCU control system V if C1 is less than C0x 0.95 and is a gas mixing chamber inside the volume, and then the Vco2 value decomposition, determining four unitcharge amount V0, and calculating the dosing times n0, n1, n2, n3 corresponding to each unit chargeamount, orderly filling the n0 volume V0, n1 primary volume V1, n2 volume V2, pure carbon dioxide n3 times volume of V3;
   (C) controlling the MCU control system of the piston excitation coil in the second piston to be energized if P1 is less than P0x 0.95, moving the permanent magnet upward with the gas piston head and the piston excitation coil, entering the air in the second valve, changing the direction of the current of the piston excitation coil, moving the permanent magnet downward, and charging the air in the second valve into the gas mixing chamber;
   (D) controlling the MCU control system ofthe second piston in the piston magnet exciting coil which is electrified if C1 is greater than P0C0x 1.05 or P1x 1.05, moving the permanent magnet with the air piston head upward with the piston magnet exciting coil, putting gas mixing gas chamber into the second valve, the current direction changing piston excitation magnetic coil, moving the permanent magnet downwards; and
   (E) discharging the second mixed gas valve.

PN CN113698834-A; CN113698834-B
TI Preparing hydrophilic zwitterion-terminated calcium carbonate oligomer reinforced siloxane sandstone cultural relic protection material comprises e.g. adding calcium carbonate oligomer gel and polymer solution to ethanol, and stirring and adding tetramethoxysilane.
AU PAN A
   SHI C
   LU X
   HE L
   CHEN Z
   LIANG J
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2021E7888V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrophilic zwitterion-terminated calcium carbonate oligomer reinforced siloxane sandstone cultural relic protection material comprises (1) using methacryloxypropyl trimethoxysilane and methacryloxyethyl phosphorylcholine as reaction monomer, ethanol as solvent, and azobisisobutyronitrile as initiator, mixing, heating and stirring to perform radical polymerization to obtain first polymer solution; (2) precipitating the first polymer solution with normal-hexane, purifying and drying the precipitate, and dissolving in ethanol to form a second polymer solution; (3) dissolving calcium chloride in ethanol, adding triethylamine and stiring, adding carbon dioxide gas while stirring, stopping introduction of carbon dioxide gas, stirring to form calcium carbonate oligomer dispersion; (4) centrifuging and separating the calcium carbonate oligomer dispersion to obtain calcium carbonate oligomer gel; and (5) adding calcium carbonate oligomer gel and second polymer solution to ethanol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hydrophilic zwitterion-terminated calcium carbonate oligomer reinforced siloxane sandstone cultural relic protection material.
   ADVANTAGE - The protection material: can plays a protective effect for long time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hydrophilic zwitterion-terminated calcium carbonate oligomer reinforced siloxane sandstone cultural relic protection material comprises (1) using methacryloxypropyl trimethoxysilane and methacryloxyethyl phosphorylcholine as reaction monomer, ethanol as solvent, and azobisisobutyronitrile as initiator, mixing, heating and stirring to perform radical polymerization to obtain first polymer solution; (2) precipitating the first polymer solution with normal-hexane, purifying and drying the precipitate, and dissolving in ethanol to form a second polymer solution; (3) dissolving calcium chloride in ethanol, adding triethylamine and stiring, adding carbon dioxide gas while stirring, stopping the introduction of carbon dioxide gas, stirring to form calcium carbonate oligomer dispersion; (4) centrifuging and separating the calcium carbonate oligomer dispersion to obtain calcium carbonate oligomer gel; and (5) adding the calcium carbonate oligomer gel and second polymer solution to ethanol, and adding tetramethoxysilane under stirring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), total molar concentration of methacryloxypropyl trimethoxysilane and methacryloxyethyl phosphorylcholine is 0.5-1.5 mmol/l; the amount of the methacryloxypropyl trimethoxysilane accounts for 30-50 wt.% of total amount of the methacryloxypropyl trimethoxysilane and methacryloxyethyl phosphorylcholine. In the step (1), the addition amount of the azobisisobutyronitrile is 0.009-0.011 mmol/L. In the step (3), the concentration of the calcium chloride is 2.9-3 g/L, and the concentration of the triethylamine is 50-60 g/L. In the step (5), ratio of the calcium carbonate oligomer gel to the second polymer solution is (0.2-0.4g): 0.3ml, added amount of the tetramethoxysilane is 40-80 ml/L. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the heating temperature is 60-65degreesC, and the stirring time is 16-20 hours. In the step (3),, time for introducing carbon dioxide gas is 25-35 minutes, after stopping the introduction of carbon dioxide gas, the stirring time is 20-40 minutes. In the step (4), centrifugal separation speed is 5500-6000 revolutions/minute, and the centrifugal time is 5-10 minutes.

AE COHERENT INC (COHE-C)
GA 202069114E
AB    NOVELTY - The slab laser (40) has a laser-housing (42) that comprises an elongated unitary metal hollow extrusion (44A) with an interior surface (52A) forming a first elongated electrode. A second elongated electrode is located within the hollow portion of the elongated extrusion, and spaced-apart and parallel to the first elongated electrode. The spaced-apart first and second elongated electrodes define a discharge-gap of the slab laser. Neither the elongated extrusion nor the second elongated electrode comprises any fluid coolant channels. A ceramic element is spaced apart the first and second elongated electrodes and a first fluid-cooled plate is attached to a first exterior surface of the elongated extrusion, adjacent the first elongated electrode. The elongated extrusion is made from aluminum. The ceramic element is made from one of a group of ceramics consisting of aluminum nitride, aluminum oxide, and beryllium oxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide gas-discharge slab-laser for dental applications such as hard-tissue and soft-tissue operations.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide slab-laser delivers radiation at a high peak power, at a relatively low average power, thus the need for the complex cooling arrangements that increase cost is eliminated. The expensive and difficult operations to bore holes within the grounded extrusion and the live electrode are avoided. The arrangement prevents contact between the ceramic spacers and the gas-discharge, thus possible erosion of the ceramic spacers by the gas-discharge is avoided.

   Step for performing comparison between the calculated transmittance and threshold value set in advance (S03)
   Step for determining that the fire is caused by alcohol combustion and issuing warning when gas concentration exceeds threshold value (S11)
   Step for determining absence of abnormality when gas concentration is below of threshold value (S12)

PN KR1996994-B1
TI Sleeping child prevention system in vehicle, has control unit which is configured to output open control signal for opening window, when concentration of carbon dioxide measured by air measurement unit exceeds allowable reference value.
AU CHO J R
AE CHO J R (CHOJ-Individual)
GA 201963653E
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a vehicle sensing unit (120) that comprises an air measurement unit for measuring concentration of carbon dioxide in vehicle. A delay timer (180) is provided for setting the first time and the second time. The second time is set as an operation delay time of vehicle sensing unit so that a driver who gets off after confirming the presence of a residual child in vehicle is not sensed through the vehicle sensing unit. A control unit (140) outputs an open control signal for opening the window, when the concentration of carbon dioxide measured by air measurement unit exceeds allowable reference value. A closing control signal for closing the window is outputted, after the lapse of a predetermined delay time in the state in which the window is opened, when the concentration of carbon dioxide measured by the air measuring unit is lower than allowable reference value. An open control signal for opening window is outputted, when the allowable reference value is exceeded.
   USE - Sleeping child prevention system in vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The sleeping child restraint system alerts that a child is boarding in the vehicle, after the driver of the vehicle has finished his or her final operation and provides a warning, when the driver or the care taker is not nearby. The child in the vehicle can be prevented from being left in the vehicle by making possible to report the child to the vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram schematically illustrating sleeping child restraint system.
   Sleeping child prevention system (100)
   Vehicle sensing unit (120)
   Alarm delay unit (130)

GA 2019290726

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing sink fragrant volatile liquid, which involves

   (B) retaining the grease constituents in the separating column to be taken out;
   (C) receiving the grease constituents in the separation column, performing filtering, using anhydrous sodium sulfate to dry after refining to obtain effective extract of each raw material;

PN CN109225239-A
TI Carbon monoxide oxidation catalyst under water vapor and carbon dioxide atmosphere, comprises carrier including mixture of ferric oxide-cerium dioxide (copper oxide) and active ingredient including mixture of platinum-palladium-copper oxide.
AU ZHANG W
   SONG Y
   JIN Z
   LUO X
   XIA S
   ZHOU Y
AE JINHUA POLYTECHNIC (JIVO-C)
GA 201913442J
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon monoxide oxidation catalyst under water vapor and carbon dioxide atmosphere, comprises a carrier including mixture of ferric oxide-cerium dioxide (copper oxide), and an active ingredient including mixture of platinum-palladium-copper oxide. The carrier is loaded with 0.2 wt.% cerium dioxide (copper oxide) composite material by ferric oxide. The mass fraction of the total mass of platinum and palladium on the ferric oxide-cerium dioxide (copper oxide) carrier is 1%. The mass ratio of platinum to palladium is 1:4-4:1. The loading of copper oxide is 0.1 wt.% of ferric oxide-cerium dioxide (copper oxide) carrier in terms of copper oxide.
   USE - Used as carbon monoxide oxidation catalyst under water vapor and carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high activity in water vapor and carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing catalyst, comprising (i) preparing copper oxide-cerium dioxide composite oxide by citrate sol-gel method using cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate as cerium source, copper(II) nitrate trihydrate as a copper source, then immersing the composite oxide in 10 wt.% nitric acid solution to dissolve a part of copper oxide, washing with water and drying at 120 degrees C to obtain a cerium dioxide (copper oxide) composite material, adding iron(III) nitrate nonahydrate solution to the composite material, and loading 0.2 wt.% composite material by ferric oxide, drying and calcining to obtain ferric oxide-cerium dioxide (copper oxide) mixture as catalyst carrier, and (ii) loading a mixed solution of platinum(II) acetylacetonate, palladium acetylacetonate and copper(II) acetylacetonate in benzene on carrier, drying and calcining in an air atmosphere to obtain a finished product of the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) dissolving weighed copper(II) nitrate trihydrate and cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate at molar ratio of 1:9 in deionized water, adding weighed to the above mixed solution, stirring the mixed solution to dissolve completely, then heating in water bath at 90 degrees C and continuously stirring until the sol is produced, quickly transferring the sol to large container, heating at 120 degrees C and drying to form a fluffy porous gel precursor, and calcining the fluffy porous gel precursor at 600 degrees C for 4 hours in a muffle furnace to obtain a copper oxide-cerium dioxide composite oxide, where molar ratio of citric acid to metal ion is 1.2:1, (b) immersing the composite oxide in 10 wt.% nitric acid solution to dissolving part of copper oxide, washing with water and drying at 120 degrees C to obtain a cerium dioxide (copper oxide) composite material, (c) adding iron(III) nitrate nonahydrate solution to the composite material and immersing for 12 hours at room temperature, fried drying the mixture in water bath at 90 degrees C, drying in an oven at 100 degrees C for 8 hours, and calcining in an air atmosphere at 400 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain ferric oxide-cerium dioxide (copper oxide) mixture as carrier, and (d) adding a mixed solution of platinum(II) acetylacetonate, palladium acetylacetonate and copper(II) acetylacetonate in benzene to the carrier, immersing for 12 hours at room temperature, fried drying in 90 degrees C water bath, drying in oven at 100 degrees C for 8 hours and calcining in an air atmosphere at 400 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain a finished catalyst.

PN CN107812509-A; CN107812509-B
TI Preparing filter membrane material for removing mercury ions in liquid, involves dissolving transition metal salt in solvent to form solution, adding carbon film in obtained solution, heating and then cooling to room temperature.
AU XIA Y
   FANG R
   LU C
   ZHANG W
   LIANG C
   HUANG H
   GAN Y
   ZHANG J
   TAO X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201823944Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing filter membrane material involves dissolving transition metal salt in the solvent to form a solution, adding carbon film in 10-60 ml solution, uniformly mixing, adding into 100 ml reaction kettle to react under vacuum pressure of 0.01-0.1 megaPascals, filling in carbon dioxide to raise internal pressure to 8-15 megaPascals, heating to 35-60 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours, releasing carbon dioxide to attain the normal pressure inside the reaction kettle, cooling to room temperature to obtain reaction liquid, evaporating reaction liquid and drying to obtain the precursor carbon film.
   USE - Method for preparing filter membrane material for removing mercury ions in a liquid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare filter membrane material which has uniform distribution of transition metal, good consistency, has high absorption efficiency and high adsorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing filter membrane material involves dissolving transition metal salt in the solvent to form a solution, adding carbon film in 10-60 ml solution, uniformly mixing, adding into 100 ml reaction kettle to react under vacuum pressure of 0.01-0.1 megaPascals, filling in carbon dioxide to raise internal pressure to 8-15 megaPascals, heating to 35-60 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours, releasing carbon dioxide to attain the normal pressure inside the reaction kettle, cooling to room temperature to obtain reaction liquid, evaporating reaction liquid, drying the remaining solid matter to obtain the precursor carbon film, heating precursor carbon film at 700-1000 degrees C to react for 2-8 hours to obtain transition metal/carbon composite film, soaking the transition metal/carbon nanotube composite film in 10 ml carbon disulfide solution for 1-12 hours, filtering and drying to obtain the filter membrane material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metal salt is sulfate, nitrate or chloride of iron, cobalt, nickel, manganese, copper and zinc. The solvent is water, ethanol and isopropanol. Preferred Conditions: The concentration of the transition metal salt in the solution is 0.01-1 moles/L. The protective gas is argon, hydrogen and nitrogen.

PN US2018071672-A1
TI Sorbent for reduction of carbon dioxide from            indoor air of enclosed space comprises a set of solid            particles in which a portion of particles comprises            support material, and amine-based compound for            capturing a portion of carbon dioxide.
AU MEIRAV U
   BIRAN I
AE ENVERID SYSTEMS INC (ENVE-Non-standard)
GA 201821163X
AB    NOVELTY - A sorbent (120) for reduction of carbon                dioxide (CO2) from indoor air of an enclosed space                (140) comprises a set of solid particles (130)                having average diameter of greater than or equal to                0.1 mm, where a portion of the particles comprises                a support material; and an amine-based compound.                Amine-based compound further comprises water, where                support is combined with amine-based compound; and                amine-based compound is configured to capture a                portion of the CO2 within the indoor air (114) of                the enclosed space and release a portion of the                captured CO2 by streaming purge air (124) through                the sorbent.
   USE - A sorbent for reduction of carbon dioxide                (CO2) from indoor air of an enclosed space                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sorbent is structured to allow indoor air                to flow with relatively low flow resistance and                relatively rapid reaction kinetics. Regeneration                may be performed at relatively low outdoor air                temperatures, minimizing the thermal energy                required for regenerating the sorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for reduction                of CO2 contained in air from an enclosed                environment which involves providing sorbent                comprising a support including a set of solid                particles having average diameter of greater than                or equal to 0.1 mm and amine-based compound                comprising polyamine and water; streaming 1st gas                comprising CO2 from inside an enclosed environment                through the sorbent such that sorbent captures a                portion of the CO2 from the 1st gas; and streaming                2nd gas through sorbent such that sorbent releases                a portion of the captured CO2 to the 2nd gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a                sorbent construct.
   Indoor air (114)
   Sorbent (120)
   Purge air (124)
   Solid particles (130)
   Enclosed space (140)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The support is                  selected from electro-magnetically charged                  objects, fly ash, activated carbon, carbon                  nanotubes, alumina nanoparticles, synthetic                  zeolite, porous alumina, porous minerals, porous                  silica, silica nanoparticle, flamed silica,                  activated charcoal, aluminum phyllosilicates,                  bentonite, montmorillonite, ball clay, fuller's                  earth, kaolinite, attapulgite, hectorite,                  palygorskite, saponite, sepiolitemetal, and/or                  organic frameworks. The purge air comprises                  indoor air or outdoor air. The support comprises                  particles, fine particles, or a powder-based                  solid, where fine particles are agglomerated into                  larger particles to facilitate air flow through                  the sorbent. The 2nd gas comprises less CO2 than                  the 1st gas. The 2nd gas has a temperature higher                  than the 1st gas. The 2nd gas comprises outdoor                  air from outside the enclosed environment. The                  2nd gas comprises indoor air from inside the                  enclosed environment. Preferred Process: The 2nd                  gas is heated before streaming the 2nd gas                  through the sorbent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: At least 25%,                  preferably greater than or equal to 50% of amine                  functional groups of the amine-based compound are                  secondary amines. Amine-based compound comprises                  monoethanolamine (MEA), ethanolamine,                  methylamine, branched polyethyleneimine (PEI),                  linear polyethyleneimine (PEI), diethanolamine                  (DEA), dimethylamine, diethylamine,                  diisopropanolamine (DIPA) tetraethylenepentamine                  (TEPA), methyldiethanolamine (MDEA), and/or                  methylethanolamine. The support is impregnated by                  amine-based compound with additional mechanical                  stimulation, catalysts, or external energy                  sources, such as heat.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The support is                  selected from gels, molecular sieves,                  nanotube-containing materials, porous materials,                  sponge and sponge-like materials, porous organic                  polymers, ion exchange resins, polymeric                  absorbent resins, acrylic ester polymers,                  polystyrene divinyl benzene, polymethyl                  methacrylate (PMMA), polystyrene, and/or styrene                  divinylbenzene (SDB). The support comprises a set                  of particles with an average diameter dimension                  of 0.1-10 mm. The support is selected from                  granules, beads, pellets, and/or                  extrudates.

GA 201817924L
AB    NOVELTY - Laser cladding strengthening method of water-cooling wall pipe of power station furnace involves sequentially placing light-absorbing paint and nickel-based multi-component alloy powder in high-speed circulating large-flow ultrafine nano-level wet-grinding machine in the weight ratio of 1:1-1:1.25, completely dispersing, forming paint, covering the surface of water-cooling water pipe with the paint with the coating thickness of 0.3-0.5 mm, and laser cladding with carbon dioxide laser machine or semiconductor laser machine under argon protection.
   USE - Laser cladding strengthening of water-cooling wall pipe of power station furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The laser cladding strengthening of water-cooling wall pipe of power station furnace, is efficiently carried out with excellent high-temperature performance and reduced generation of cracks.

The laser scanning speed in carbon dioxide laser machine is 400-450 mm/minute. The laser power density in carbon dioxide laser machine is 11200-12800 w/cm2. The incident laser power density of semiconductor laser machine is 18000-20000 W/cm2. The scanning speed in semiconductor laser machine is 350-400 mm/minute.

PT P
PN IN201831001913-A; IN394346-B
TI Isolating adipose-derived stem cell by washing and mincing adipose tissue, incubating with sodium taurocholic acid solution, adding Dulbeccos modified eagle medium (DMEM) media, filtrating, resuspending in DMEM media and incubating.
AU CHAKRABORTY E
   KUMAR H
   SINGH D K
AE ELIZA C (ELIZ-Individual)
   MEERUT ENG & TECHNOLOGY INST (MEER-Non-standard)
   MEERUT ENG & TECHNOLOGY INST (MEER-Non-standard)
   CHAKRABORTY E (CHAK-Individual)
GA 201813312E
AB    NOVELTY - Method of isolating adipose-derived stem cell involves (a) taking adipose tissue piece and washing into phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), (b) mincing the tissue pieces into slurry, (c) incubating the slurry with a solution of sodium taurocholic acid, (d) incubating under 5% carbon dioxide, (e) adding Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) high glucose media solution and filtering, (f) centrifuging filtrate, (g) resuspending the pellet in DMEM high glucose media, (h) transferring the solution to a flask and adding complete DMEM high glucose media, and (l) incubating the flask.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating adipose-derived stem cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective, and ensures reliability and high safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of isolating adipose-derived stem cell involves (a) taking adipose tissue piece and washing into phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), which contains antibiotic and antimycotic solution followed by ethanol and DPBS, (b) mincing the tissue pieces into slurry, (c) incubating the slurry with a solution of sodium taurocholic acid, (d) incubating with 5% carbon dioxide, (e) adding Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) high glucose media solution, and filtering using cell strainer (100 mu m) to remove undigested tissue, (f) centrifuging the filtrate at 16500 revolution per minute (rpm) for 11 minutes, (g) resuspending the pellet in 1 ml volume of DMEM high glucose media, (h) transferring the solution to a flask and adding 1.5 ml complete DMEM high glucose media, and (l) incubating the flask in carbon dioxide incubator at 5% carbon dioxide and at 37 degrees C for 24-48 hours, where lipoaspirate is prepared in vitro by bile salt. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the isolated adipose-derived stem cell produced by the above-mentioned process, where the incubation was performed for 24-48 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The change of medium was performed at a particular period of time. The incubation period is 1 hour and 20 minutes (every 20 minutes repeated with gentle pipetting 3 times). Preferred Composition: The concentration of sodium taurocholic acid solution is 20 mg/ml. The DMEM high glucose contains basal media, newborn calf serum (NBCS), and 1% antibiotics. The DMEM high glucose comprises 10% NBCS. Preferred Components: The bile salt is chosen from sodium salt of glycocholic acid, cholic acid, chenodeoxycholic acid, deoxycholic acid, and lithocholic acid and other bile salts. Preferred Cell: The isolated adipose-derived stem cell is taken from rat.
   EXAMPLE - Adipose tissue was minced in Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline using sterile surgical blades and forceps. It was digested using collagenase enzyme (500 mu l 0.1% collagenase enzyme type II and 1500 mu l phosphate-buffered saline) and was incubated for 1.5 hours at 5% carbon dioxide and 37 degrees C. After adding 1 ml Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) high glucose media, the solution was filtered using cell strainer (100 mu m) to remove undigested tissue. The filtrate was centrifuged at 1800 rpm for 10 minutes. The pellet was resuspended in 1 ml volume of DMEM high glucose media (basal media, 10% newborn calf serum, 1% penicillin/streptomycin and 1% L-glutamine). Cells were seeded in culture flasks with 1.5 ml of complete medium. The flasks were incubated in carbon dioxide incubator at 5% carbon dioxide and 37 degrees C for 24-48 hours. After 48 hours, l ml of complete media was added to each flask.

The potassium carbonate/potassium hydrogen carbonate mixture is transported from the source apparatus. The carbon dioxide is desorbed from potassium carbonate at a stationary facility decoupled from the absorber.

   ADVANTAGE - Performing carbon dioxide capture on the source apparatus reduces the complexity and weight of the mobile apparatus. The desorption is performed at a stationary site, where cheap low carbon dioxide energy increases the efficiency of the process. The system provides carbon dioxide capture and storage, compact low-weight absorption and synergy with renewable energy sources.

PN CN113967461-A
TI Amine-rich porous carbon material having specific surface area and nitrogen element content useful as carbon dioxide trapping agent, prepared by heat-treating mixture of fused-ring aromatic hydrocarbon compound and nitrogen-containing compound, subjecting to crosslinking treatment, and carbonizing.
AU HAN L

   WANG A
   ZHU G
   JI W
   ZHANG T
   XUE R
   CHEN J
AE SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
