GA 2022226607
AB    NOVELTY - Amine-rich porous carbon material having specific surface area of greater than 600 m2/g, preferably 600-2000 m2/g, and nitrogen element content of 10-40%, preferably 15-35%, is claimed.
   USE - The amine-rich porous carbon material is useful as carbon dioxide trapping agent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method: produces high efficient carbon dioxide trapping agent; has low cost, and simple process; does not require activation and cleaning processes when compared to the usual activated carbon preparation process; does not require metal catalyst and harsh processes necessary for hypercrosslinked polymers; and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of the amine-rich porous carbon material, comprising (1) heat-treating a mixture comprising fused-ring aromatic hydrocarbon compound and nitrogen-containing compound to obtain intermediate amine-based pitch, (2) subjecting the intermediate amine-based pitch to crosslinking treatment to obtain crosslinked amine-based pitch, and (4) carbonizing the crosslinked amine-based pitch to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The heat treatment process is carried out in a solvent at 150-300 degreesC, preferably 200-240 degreesC for 1-10 hours, preferably 3-5 hours under inert gas atmosphere, and the reaction system is cooled to room temperature, preferably to 20-35 degreesC after the heat treatment reaction, where the solvent includes tetrahydrofuran, toluene, N-methylpyrrolidone, ethylene glycol, cyclohexane and/or diacetone alcohol, the inert gas includes nitrogen and/or argon, preferably mixture of nitrogen and argon. In step (2): crosslinking agent is added to perform crosslinking treatment on the intermediate amine-based pitch; the crosslinking agent includes azobisisobutyronitrile, azobisisoheptanenitrile, glyoxal, succinaldehyde and/or hydroxy silicone oil; the added amount of the crosslinking agent accounts for 5-30%, preferably 10-15% of the total mass of the fused-ring aromatic hydrocarbon compound and the nitrogen-containing compound; the reaction temperature of the crosslinking treatment is 50-200 degreesC, preferably 80-150 degreesC; the crosslinking treatment time is 0.5-6 hours, preferably 1-2 hours; and the reaction system is cooled to room temperature, preferably to 20-35 degreesC after the crosslinking treatment reaction. The preparation method further comprises (3) carrying out extraction treatment on the crosslinked amine-based pitch using an extractant. The carbonization treatment is carried out at 400-600 degreesC, preferably 500-550 degreesC under inert gas atmosphere.Preferred Components: Preferred Components: The porous carbon material has carbon dioxide adsorption amount (25 degreesC, 1 bar) of greater than or equal to 50 mg/g, preferably 50-300 mg/g. The fused-ring aromatic hydrocarbon compound is 8-18C fused-ring aromatic hydrocarbon (optionally substituted) including naphthalene, phenanthrene, anthracene, pyrene, benzanthracene, benzopyrene, perylene, triphenylene and/or methylnaphthalene, preferably naphthalene, methylnaphthalene, anthracene, phenanthrene and/or pyrene. The nitrogen-containing compound includes ethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine, urea, hexamethylenetetramine and/or melamine. The molar ratio of the fused-ring aromatic hydrocarbon compound to the nitrogen-containing compound is 1:0.3-5, preferably 1:0.8-1.2. The extractant includes at least one ketone extractant, preferably acetone, butanedione and/or butanone, and mass ratio of the extractant to the crosslinked amine-based pitch is 1-6:1, preferably 1.5-2:1.

PN CN113842876-A
TI Preparation method of hydrophobic molecular sieve rotor used for separating carbon dioxide, involves passing the first inorganic fiber paper and the second inorganic fiber paper through the molecular sieve slurry, second inorganic fiber paper and corrugated paper bonded and drying, calcining.
AU YUAN S
   SHI L
AE UNIV SHANGHAI ZHEJIANG JIAXING EMERGING (UYSH-Non-standard)
GA 202209820B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of hydrophobic molecular sieve rotor involves passing the first inorganic fiber paper and the second inorganic fiber paper through the molecular sieve slurry at a uniform speed, drying, and then immersing the first inorganic fiber paper and the second inorganic fiber paper in inorganic glue, and dipping the first inorganic fiber paper, hot-pressing the paper to obtain corrugated paper, and the second inorganic fiber paper and the corrugated paper bonded to form a honeycomb body, and drying. Calcining the preliminary rotor body in a high-temperature furnace for 1-8 hours, and the calcination temperature is 200-800 degreesC. Performing hydrophobic treatment on the preliminary rotor body to assemble the hydrophobic molecular sieve rotor.
   USE - Preparation method of hydrophobic molecular sieve rotor used for separating carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - Hydrophobic molecular sieve rotor keeps high-efficiency carbon dioxide absorption capacity in a high-humidity environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow chart of the preparation method of the hydrophobic molecular sieve rotor. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Inorganic glue is aluminum sol, silica sol, sodium silicate, and/or aluminum silicate. Preferred Method: Uniform speed of the first inorganic fiber paper and the second inorganic fiber paper passing through the molecular sieve slurry is 1-3 meter per second. The temperature of the hot pressing operation is 35-60 degreesC. The flute height of the corrugated paper is 1.0-2.0 millimeter, and the flute spacing of the corrugated paper is 0.5-2 times the flute height. The honeycomb body is dried at a temperature of 45-100 degreesC. In the activation process, the calcination time is 3-5 hours, and the calcination temperature is 300-500 degreesC. The hydrophobic treatment is to carry out the dipping treatment of silicone resin on the crude rotor, and the ratio of the coating amount of the silicone resin to the molecular sieve slurry is 0.1-4:25. The mass fraction of the molecular sieve slurry is 10-15 mass%, and the viscosity is 10-15 mpa.s (millipascal-second).

   x,y = 1-500;and

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is any one or a mixture of two or more chosen from dicumyl peroxide, azobisisobutyronitrile, and benzoyl peroxide. The step (1) is performed in the presence of solvent which is any one or two chosen from water and dimethyl sulfoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): The compound (I) is prepared by (1) polymerizing the carbon dioxide affinity monomer and the zwitterionic monomer in the presence of an initiator, (2) introducing an additive containing ferric ions comprising any one or a mixture of two or more chosen from iron(III) chloride and iron(III) fluoride, and (3) adding the additive containing ferric ions, in which the copolymer comprises 1.5-3 wt.% ferric ion. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide affinity monomer is poly(ethylene glycol) methyl ether methacrylate. The zwitterionic monomer is sulfobetaine methacrylate. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of the carbon dioxide affinity monomer with respect to the zwitterionic monomer is 2-5:1. The copolymer comprises 0.5-4 wt.% ferric ion. The ideal carbon dioxide selectivity is obtained by dividing the carbon dioxide single gas permeability value of the gas separation membrane by the methane single gas permeability value measured using a mixed gas obtained by mixing carbon dioxide and methane in a volume ratio of 1:1.

PT P
PN CN111378507-A
TI Processing hydrogen production by coal gasification comprises e.g. carrying out coal gasification obtains gasification gas, reacting gas and oxygen carrier, reducing the oxygen carrier, separating and reacting.
AU LIANG H
   WANG T
   YIN Z
   LIU Q
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
GA 202067174K
AB    NOVELTY - Processing hydrogen production by coal gasification comprises (i) carrying out coal gasification obtains gasification gas, where the composition of which includes carbon monoxide, water, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, methane and hydrogen sulfide, (ii) reacting the gas and the oxygen carrier at 450-600 degrees C and a pressure of 3-5 MPa to obtain carbon dioxide and water, including a small amount of ethane and hydrogen sulfide and hydrogen, (iii) reducing the oxygen carrier and separating and reacting with water vapor at 400-600 degrees C under pressure is 3-5 MPa to produce hydrogen, (iv) in the step (ii), partially oxidizing the oxygen carrier, and completely oxidizing by reaction with air after separation at 500-600 degrees C under pressure 3-5 MPa, and completing reaction with gasification gas and recycle.
   USE - The method is useful for processing hydrogen production by coal gasification.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces equipment investment; improves economy, and energy efficiency of hydrogen production from coal, increases hydrogen recovery rate, and produces high-purity hydrogen, avoids the problem of large amounts of carbon dioxide emissions in the coal chemical process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the reaction temperature is preferably 450-550 degrees C. The oxygen carrier is ferric oxide-magnesium oxide or manganese(III) oxide- cerium-zirconium trioxide. The oxygen carrier ferric oxide-magnesium oxide uses ferric oxide as an active component and has a mass fraction of 20-50%, and magnesium oxide as a carrier, with a mass fraction of 50-80%. The oxygen carrier manganese(III) oxide-cerium-zirconium trioxide takes manganese(III) oxide as the active component, The mass fraction is 20-50%, cerium-zirconium trioxide is the carrier, and the mass fraction is 50-80%. The oxygen carrier is prepared by an impregnation method or a coprecipitation method. In the coal gasification, gasification gas is obtained by using coal water slurry gasification, pulverized coal chilling gasification and pulverized coal waste boiler gasification methods.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

TI Stabilization of power grid involves balancing generation and consumption of electrical energy by synthesizing hydrocarbon using e.g. hydrogen obtained by electrolysis of water using to-be-stored excess electrical energy from power grid.
AU VOLKMER P
AE VOLKMER P (VOLK-Individual)
GA 202057280T
AB    NOVELTY - Stabilization of power grid involves balancing generation and consumption of electrical energy by removing excess electrical energy from the power grid, converting excess electrical energy into storable energy source and reconverting the stored energy source and feeding into the power grid. The removal and storage of excess electrical energy is carried out by removing increased generation of electrical energy and converting and storing in chemical form as hydrocarbon(s) as a storable chemical energy source, using carbon dioxide formed in preceding cycle by reconversion. The hydrocarbon is synthesized using carbon dioxide and hydrogen which is obtained by electrolysis of water using to-be-stored electrical energy.
   USE - Stabilization of power grid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical, environmentally-friendly and efficient stabilization of power grid by effectively storing excess electrical energy in storable chemical energy form, with reduced generation of waste heat and load on power transmission lines, and suppressed carbon dioxide emission. The performance of the power grid is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) power grid stabilization installation; and
   (2) power network stabilization system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The hydrocarbon is gaseous hydrocarbon. Preferred Process: The reconversion of the stored energy source is carried out by combustion of hydrocarbon in a gas and steam power plant or a fuel cell device.

PT P
PN CN110368411-A; CN110368411-B
TI Extracting white-backed sunflower polyphenols involves taking stem of white-backed sunflower leaves and drying, sifting to obtain white-backed sunflower powder, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and drying extract.
AU MA J
   LIU X
   LIU Y
   CHEN X
   LIN Z
AE UNIV LONGYAN (ULGY-C)
   UNIV LONGYAN (ULGY-C)
GA 201990368K
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting white-backed sunflower polyphenols involves taking stem of white-backed sunflower leaves and drying it, sifting to obtain white-backed sunflower powder, charging the powder into the extraction kettle under 30-40 MPa extraction pressure, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction for 1.5-2.5 hours at 35-45 degrees C temperature, and 15-25 L/hour carbon dioxide flow rate under the condition of 90% ethanol, and drying the extract by vacuum freeze dryer for 24 hours to obtain crude extract of polyphenols.
   USE - Method for extracting white-backed sunflower polyphenols (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that the content of polyphenols is increased from 14.73% in the crude extract to 45.21% in the purified extract. The in vitro antioxidant test shows that the scavenging rate of 2,2-diphenyl-1-picrylhydrazyl and 2,2-azino-bis(3-ethylbenzothiazoline-6-sulphonic acid) free radicals increases with the increase of its concentration. In vivo antioxidant tests shows that polyphenol extract significantly increases superoxide dismutases, glutathione peroxidase, chloramphenicol acetyltransferase activity and decreases malondialdehyde content in serum and liver tissues of aging model group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: Extracting white-backed sunflower polyphenols involves drying at 60-65 degrees C temperature, using sieve mesh number of 60 mesh. The extraction pressure is 35 MPa, extracting time is 2 hours, extracting temperature is 40 degrees C, and carbon dioxide flow rate is 20 L/hour. The pretreatment is done by soaking the D101 resin in 95% ethanol for 24 hours, washing with distilled water until alcohol-free, then immersing in 5% hydrochloric acid solution for 12 hours, washing with distilled water until neutral, and finally soaking with 5% sodium chloride solution, washing with distilled water until neutral, and filtering the resin for use. The sample solution has a mass concentration of 1 mg/mL, the sample solution has a pH of 3.1, and the sample solution has a flow rate of 2 mL/minute. The elution ethanol solution has a volume fraction of 70% and a flow rate of 2 mL/minute. Preferred Processes: The separation and purification of polyphenols involve selecting D101 resin as a resin for purifying the crude extract of white backed polyphenol, and pretreating, weighing 12 g pretreated D101 resin, and selecting a 16 mm /asterisk 20 cm glass column for wet packing, using the polyphenol extract as 0.5-1.5 mg/mL sample solution for adsorption having 3-3.5 pH and 150 mL sample load, using a constant current pump to adjust the flow rate of the sample solution to 1.5-2.5 mL/minute, after the adsorption complete, eluting with a flow rate of 1.8-2.2 mL/minute with 60 mL of 65-75% ethanol solution, concentrating and lyophilizing the eluate in vacumm to obtain a purified polyphenol.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

PT P
PN CN108840359-A
TI Producing porous spherical superfine calcium carbonate comprises e.g. crushing calcite, adding water, heating, adding hydrochloric acid, ultrasonically vibrating, blowing carbon dioxide, washing and grinding with zirconium oxide beads.
AU CAI M
AE LIDA MICRON IND CO LTD (LIDA-Non-standard)
GA 201894797S
AB    NOVELTY - Producing porous spherical superfine calcium carbonate comprises (i) crushing and sieving calcite to obtain calcite powder, adding calcite into water, stirring and dropping hydrochloric acid solution, heating to 40-50 degrees C, reacting and stirring for 20-40 minutes, cooling to room temperature, filtering and removing residue; (ii) adjusting pH of filtrate to 8-9 by sodium hydroxide solution, adding sodium stearate to filtrate while stirring, adding zinc sulfate and EDTA, continuously stirring for 40-60 minutes, ultrasonically vibrating and blowing carbon dioxide gas into the filtrate while adding sodium carbonate solution, where the temperature of carbon dioxide gas is 20-40 degrees C with flow rate of 0.8-1.5 m/seconds; and (iii) filtering, washing the obtained calcium carbonate with water, drying, adding calcium carbonate into grinder to grind with zirconium oxide beads as grinding medium and adjusting the controlling temperature of grinder at 70-80 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for producing spherical superfine calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate has specific surface area of greater than or equal to 800 m2/kg.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of calcite powder and water is 1:100-200. In the step (i), the molar concentration of the hydrochloric acid solution is 0.1-0.2 mol/l. In the step (ii), the molar ratio of zinc sulfate and EDTA is 1:1. In the step (ii), the molar concentration of the sodium carbonate solution is 0.08-0.14 mol/l. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the calcite is crushed and sieved via 200-400 mesh sieve. In the step (ii), the frequency of ultrasonic vibration is 40-60 KHz. In the step (iii), the solid calcium carbonate washed is more than 3 times at 110-130 degrees C. In the step (iii), the grinding time is 80-100 minutes.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E12-A10; E31-V; E34-D03A

PN CN108727661-A
TI Preparation of polyethylene composition for bottle            cap, involves mixing polyethylene, antioxidant, halogen            absorbent, deodorant and processing aid, extruding,            adding supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, vacuum            degassing, and granulating.
AU ZUO S
   YUAN X
   WANG J
   QIU D
   XU Z
   FU Y
AE SINOPEC YANGZI PETROCHEMICAL CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2018872427
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polyethylene composition                involves mixing 100 pts. wt. polyethylene,                0.05-0.15 pt. wt. primary antioxidant comprising                hindered phenolic compound, 0.1-0.3 pt. wt.                secondary antioxidant, 0.05-0.2 pt. wt. halogen                absorbent, 0-5.0 pts. wt. deodorant and 0-0.01 pt.                wt. processing aid in a high speed mixer, adding                mixture from the heating zone (A) of a twin-screw                extruder, adding 2-30 %mass supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid from the heating zone (B) or (C) to                the twin-screw extruder, extruding at 150-230                degrees C at 50-500 rpm, vacuum degassing before                the extruder die, cooling using water, granulating                the extruded material strip, and drying. The                polyethylene is a high density polyethylene having                a melt flow rate of 0.5-20 g/10 minute.
   USE - Preparation of polyethylene composition for                bottle cap.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical, and uses                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid which                effectively prevents the thermal and oxygen                degradation of polyethylene during extrusion, while                greatly accelerating the dissolution of small                molecule volatile.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The deodorant                  comprises medical stone, porous silica gel and/or                  molecular sieve, preferably porous silica                  gel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The primary                  antioxidant comprises n-octadecyl-( beta                  )-(4-hydroxy-3,5-di-tert-butylphenyl)-propionate,                  tetrakis(( beta                  )-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propanoic                  acid)pentaerythritol ester and/or                  1,3,5-tris(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxybenzyl)isocyanuric                  acid, preferably tetrakis(( beta                  )-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propanoic                  acid)pentaerythritol ester. The secondary                  antioxidant comprises                  (2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl)phosphite triester                  and/or pentaerythritol bisphosphite                  bis(2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl)ester, preferably                  (2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl)phosphite triester. The                  halogen absorbent comprises calcium stearate,                  zinc stearate and/or hydrated magnesium silicate,                  preferably hydrated magnesium silicate. The                  processing aid is fluoroelastomer.

PT P
PN CN108557880-A; CN108557880-B
TI Preparing zirconium tetrachloride used for preparing zirconium dioxide, involves adding zircon sand, carbon reducing agent and chlorine gas to boiling chlorination furnace and heating to form e.g. carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.
AU YIN B
   HUANG B
   FAN X
   SUN Y
   LIU X
   HE L
   WU Z
AE XINJIANG JINGSHUO ADVANCED MATERIALS CO (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 201878007D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing zirconium tetrachloride involves: (1) adding zircon sand, carbon reducing agent and chlorine gas to a first-stage boiling chlorination furnace, heating to generate a first-stage chlorination reaction to form zirconium tetrachloride, silicon tetrachloride, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to obtain a gas mixture (A); and (2) separating carbon monoxide from the gas mixture (A), introducing carbon monoxide into a second-stage boiling chlorination furnace, adding zircon sand and chlorine gas to the second-stage boiling chlorination furnace, heating to generate a second-stage chlorination reaction to generate zirconium tetrachloride, silicon tetrachloride and carbon dioxide to obtain a gas mixture (B).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing zirconium tetrachloride, which is useful for preparing zirconium dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of zirconium tetrachloride with reduced greenhouse gas emissions during the whole process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process for preparing zirconium dioxide, which involves: (A) preparing zirconium tetrachloride by the above preparation method; and (B) mixing the zirconium tetrachloride with an oxidizing gas and heating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method further involves: (3) introducing the gas mixture (A) and/or the gas mixture (B) into a cooler group, cooling to 200-300 degrees C, solidifying the zirconium tetrachloride and separating solid-phase zirconium tetrachloride and a gas-phase mixture (A) respectively, where the cooler group in step (3) includes a cooler (A) and a cooler (B), and the cooler (A) is connected to the cooler (B); (4) passing the gas phase mixture (A) into a cooler (C) after the step (3) is completed, cooling to 140-170 degrees C, solidifying aluminum chloride and separating solid phase aluminum chloride and a gas phase mixture (B) respectively; (5) passing the gas phase mixture (B) into cooler (D) after the step (4) is completed, cooling to 60-80 degrees C, solidifying the titanium tetrachloride and separating solid phase titanium tetrachloride and a gas phase mixture (C) respectively; (6) passing the gas phase mixture (C) into a deep cooler after the step (5) is completed, cooling to -20 degrees C to 0 degrees C, liquefying silicon tetrachloride and separating solid phase silicon tetrachloride and gas phase mixture (D), respectively; and (7) passing the gas phase mixture (B) into an alkali washing tank to remove carbon dioxide, and then passing through a dryer to remove water to obtain carbon monoxide which is introduced into the second-stage boiling chlorination furnace. The step (3) further involves passing the gas mixture (A) and/or the gas mixture (B) into the cooler (A) and cooling to 350-450 degrees C, then passing to the cooler (B) and cooling to 200-300 degrees C. The step (1) further involves adding an additive to the first-stage boiling chlorination furnace, and/or the step (2) further involves adding an additive to the second-stage boiling chlorination furnace, where the additive is silicon carbide, ferrosilicon and/or waste silicon powder formed during the production of polycrystalline silicon. The step (2) further involves removing the zirconium tetrachloride, aluminum trichloride, titanium tetrachloride and silicon tetrachloride in the gas mixture (A) and/or gas mixture (B) by cooling, carrying out alkali washing to remove carbon dioxide, then drying to remove water, and introducing the gas obtained by drying into the second-stage boiling chlorination furnace to serve as a raw material gas. Preferred Condition: In steps (1) and (2), the heating temperature is 900-1300 degrees C. In step (1), the feed rate of solid material added to the first-stage boiling chlorination furnace is 10-100 kg/hour, and the chlorine gas feed rate is 20-25 kg/hour. In step (2), the feed rate of solid material added to the second-stage boiling chlorination furnace is 10-50 kg/hour, the chlorine gas feed rate is 20-25 kg/hour, and the carbon monoxide feed rate is 6-20 kg/hour. Preferred Components: In step (1): the mass ratio of zircon sand and additive is (6-10):1; the mass ratio of zircon sand and carbon reducing agent is (4-8):1; and the carbon reducing agent is chosen from petroleum coke, activated carbon and/or clean coal.

TI Solar supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle system, comprises solar heat absorber, turbine unit and Brayton cycle unit, and storage tank, where solar heat absorber and Brayton cycle unit are connected to turbine unit through pipeline.
AU XIAO G
   PANG H
   NI M
   LUO Z
   CEN K
   CHENG L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201971554H
AB    NOVELTY - A solar supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle system comprises a solar heat absorber, a turbine unit and a Brayton cycle unit, and storage tank, where the solar heat absorber and the Brayton cycle unit are connected to the turbine unit through a pipeline, and the storage tank is connected in parallel or in series with a conduit between the solar thermal absorber and the turbine unit for storing metal carbonates and metal oxide, the air tank is further disposed in the Brayton cycle unit for storing supercritical carbon dioxide, the lighting conditions are sufficient, the circulating supercritical carbon dioxide absorbs heat in the solar heat absorber, the heat transfer medium absorbs heat in the solar heat absorber and is transferred to the metal carbonate in the storage tank.
   USE - Solar supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle system.
   ADVANTAGE - The solar supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle system has high-efficiency energy storage and heat transfer, and can improve the device efficiency, and reduce the costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A solar supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle system comprises a solar heat absorber, a turbine unit and a Brayton cycle unit, and storage tank, where the solar heat absorber and the Brayton cycle unit are connected to the turbine unit through a pipeline, and the storage tank is connected in parallel or in series with a conduit between the solar thermal absorber and the turbine unit for storing metal carbonates and metal oxide, the air tank is further disposed in the Brayton cycle unit for storing supercritical carbon dioxide, the lighting conditions are sufficient, the circulating supercritical carbon dioxide absorbs heat in the solar heat absorber, the heat transfer medium absorbs heat in the solar heat absorber and is transferred to the metal carbonate in the storage tank, and the metal carbonate undergoes decomposition reaction, stores heat, generates supercritical carbon dioxide, and enters the turbine unit to perform work together with the circulating supercritical carbon dioxide, the supercritical carbon dioxide after work is passed through the Brayton cycle unit, a part of which is stored in the gas storage tank, and another part is returned to the solar heat absorber to absorb heat and recycle work, the lighting conditions are poor or no light, the solar heat absorber is isolated, and the supercritical carbon dioxide stored in the gas storage tank enters the storage tank through the Brayton cycle unit together with the circulating supercritical carbon dioxide, enters the storage tank, reacts with the metal oxide, releases heat, heats the supercritical carbon dioxide, and enters the turbine unit is working externally.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The storage tank is any one of a fixed bed, a bubbling bed, a fluidized bed, a porous medium, or a honeycomb ceramic. The storage tank is a porous medium or a honeycomb ceramic, the metal oxide or silicon carbide is used as a matrix, and the metal carbonate is supported on the surface of the porous medium or the honeycomb structure.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heat absorbing medium in the solar heat absorber is supercritical carbon dioxide, and the supercritical carbon dioxide is both a heat transfer medium and a work medium. The metal carbonate comprises carbonates of lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, cesium, francium, beryllium, magnesium, calcium, strontium, barium or radium.

PN US10928113-B1
TI System for generating hydrogen-rich ice has            eductor, gas inlet lines, heat exchanger,            thermal-insulated container, vertical tubes, coolant            container, insulated blanket, ice cutter, isolating            lock chamber, and guillotine dampers.
AU ALTSHULER B
   ALTSHULER A
AE ALTSHULER B (ALTS-Individual)
   ALTSHULER A (ALTS-Individual)
GA 202122897F
AB    NOVELTY - A system for generating hydrogen-rich ice                (HRI) comprises eductor (204); hydrogen gas inlet                line; carbon dioxide gas inlet line; water inlet                line; heat exchanger; thermal-insulated 1st                container; a set of vertical tubes (301); coolant                container; insulated blanket (302) surrounding the                set of vertical tubes; recycling pump; 1st                recycling line; ice cutter (304); isolating lock                chamber (319) with carbon dioxide overpressure; 1st                guillotine damper (308) and 2nd guillotine damper                (309); and inlet port (306) to isolating lock                chamber with carbon dioxide overpressure, where                water is introduced into interior chamber of the                eductor. Water comprises a motive fluid causing                hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas to be drawn and                mixed into the interior chamber of the eductor,                where water, hydrogen gas, and carbon dioxide gas                are mixed in the interior chamber of the eductor                and are introduced into an interior chamber of the                thermal-insulated 1st container.
   USE - A system for generating hydrogen-rich ice                (HRI).
   ADVANTAGE - The insulated lock chamber prevents the                release of hydrogen to the atmosphere and is                enclosed on either end with two guillotine dampers.                While the design of chamber is not included in the                process of making HRI, it is a safety system                support feature to minimize explosive hydrogen                concentration. Once cut ice has entered the                chamber, the guillotine dampers are closed, and                carbon dioxide enters through the inlet port. Then,                HRI in the form of cut tube-ice is discharged from                chamber through exit the upon opening of the 2nd                guillotine damper.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system for generating hydrogen-rich ice                (HRI) comprises eductor (204); hydrogen gas inlet                line; carbon dioxide gas inlet line; water inlet                line; heat exchanger; thermal-insulated 1st                container; a set of vertical tubes (301); coolant                container; insulated blanket (302) surrounding the                set of vertical tubes; recycling pump; 1st                recycling line; ice cutter (304); isolating lock                chamber (319) with carbon dioxide overpressure; 1st                guillotine damper (308) and 2nd guillotine damper                (309); and inlet port to isolating lock chamber                with carbon dioxide overpressure, where water is                introduced into interior chamber of the eductor.                Water comprises a motive fluid causing hydrogen gas                and carbon dioxide gas to be drawn and mixed into                the interior chamber of the eductor, where water,                hydrogen gas, and carbon dioxide gas are mixed in                the interior chamber of the eductor and are                introduced into an interior chamber of the                thermal-insulated 1st container. The                thermal-insulated 1st container is heated by heat                provided by heat exchanger, where mixture of water,                hydrogen gas, and carbon dioxide gas is introduced                into each of an interior of the set of vertical                tubes, and where the set of vertical tubes is                disposed within an interior of the coolant                container comprising coolant agent. Mixture of                water, hydrogen gas, and carbon dioxide gas is                circulated by recycling pump multiple times through                the interior of the vertical tubes through the 1st                recycling line, where temperature in an interior                chamber in each of the set of vertical tubes is                maintained by the coolant agent at                232-240&#176;K. When the mixture of water,                hydrogen gas, and carbon dioxide freezes to form                tube-ice (303) in the interior of the set of                vertical tubes, the tube-ice is released from the                interior of the set of vertical tubes, where the                tube-ice is cut into distinct pieces of tube-ice by                the cutter upon release from the interior of the                set of vertical tubes, and the cut tube-ice is                introduced into an interior of the isolating lock                chamber with carbon dioxide overpressure, in which                excess hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas that                exits the set of vertical tubes is recirculated to                the eductor. Isolating lock chamber with carbon                dioxide overpressure is isolated from external                atmosphere by closure of the 1st guillotine damper                at one end of the isolating lock chamber with                carbon dioxide overpressure and the 2nd guillotine                damper at the opposing end of the isolating lock                chamber with carbon dioxide overpressure, where                isolating lock chamber with carbon dioxide                overpressure is filled with carbon dioxide gas                through the inlet port, and the 2nd guillotine                damper is opened and the HRI is removed from the                interior chamber of the isolating lock chamber with                carbon dioxide overpressure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                tube-ice machine for use in generating                hydrogen-rich ice.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred                  Compositions: Temperature within the interior of                  each of the set of cooled vertical tubes forms                  carbon dioxide clathrate hydrate clusters from                  the mixture. Coolant comprises anhydrous ammonia                  refrigerant or R22 refrigerant. Water comprises a                  coloring agent. Mixture comprises a relative                  ratio of around 1 gallon water, 8 scf carbon                  dioxide, and 32 scf hydrogen. Preferred                  Condition: Pressure within the interior of the                  cooled vertical tubes is around                  atmospheric.

PT P
PN CN108456547-A
TI System useful for producing fuel chemicals, comprises e.g. first high temperature thermochemical reactor, first temperature lowering device, carbon dioxide removing device, and second high temperature thermal chemical reactor.
AU CHEN Q
   SUN Y
   TANG Z
   GU Y
   WANG D
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
   UNIV CHINESE ACAD SCI (UCAS-C)
GA 2018691500
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises first high temperature thermochemical reactor, first temperature lowering device, carbon dioxide removing device, second high temperature thermal chemical reactor, second cooling device, dehydrating device, methane partial oxidation reactor, and fuel chemical synthesis tower; the first high temperature thermochemical reactor is configured to receive the first solar thermal energy after the aggregation, and subject carbon dioxide-rich gas passing therein to thermochemical decomposition reaction to generate carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed gas and first oxygen; the inlet of the first cooling device is connected to the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet of first high temperature thermochemical reactor for cooling the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixture; the inlet of the carbon dioxide removal device connected to outlet of first cooling device for removing carbon dioxide in carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixture to obtain a carbon monoxide-rich gas.
   USE - The system is useful for producing fuel chemicals using solar high temperature heat coupled methane (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - System comprises first high temperature thermochemical reactor, first temperature lowering device, carbon dioxide removing device, second high temperature thermal chemical reactor, second cooling device, dehydrating device, methane partial oxidation reactor, and fuel chemical synthesis tower; the first high temperature thermochemical reactor is configured to receive the first solar thermal energy after the aggregation, and subject the carbon dioxide-rich gas passing therein to a thermochemical decomposition reaction to generate a carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed gas and a first oxygen; the inlet of the first cooling device is connected to the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet of the first high temperature thermochemical reactor for cooling the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixture; the inlet of the carbon dioxide removal device is connected to an outlet of the first cooling device for removing carbon dioxide in the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixture to obtain a carbon monoxide-rich gas; the second high temperature thermochemical reactor is configured to receive the second solar thermal energy after being collected, and subjecting the water vapor passing therein to a thermochemical decomposition reaction to generate a water vapor/hydrogen mixture gas and a second oxygen; the inlet of the second cooling device is connected to the water vapor/hydrogen mixed gas outlet of the second high temperature thermochemical reactor for cooling the water vapor/hydrogen mixture; the inlet of the hydrophobic device is connected to an outlet of the second cooling device for removing water vapor in the water vapor/hydrogen mixture to obtain a hydrogen-rich gas; the methane partial oxidation reactor is connected to an oxygen outlet of the first high temperature thermochemical reactor and an oxygen outlet of the second high temperature thermochemical reactor to receive the first oxygen and the second oxygen, and reacting the methane-rich gas introduced therein with the first oxygen and the second oxygen to form a syngas; the fuel chemical synthesis tower and the carbon monoxide-rich gas outlet of the carbon dioxide removal device, the hydrogen-rich gas outlet of the dehydration unit and the syngas outlet of the methane partial oxidation reactor are connected. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing fuel chemical using the system comprising (1) introducing carbon dioxide-rich gas into first high-temperature thermochemical reactor, undergoing thermochemical decomposition reaction to generate carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixture gas and first oxygen gas under the first sunlight, passing the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixture gas through the first cooling device and carbon dioxide removing device in sequence to become carbon monoxide-rich gas; (2) introducing water vapor into the second high temperature thermochemical reactor, under the irradiation of the second sunlight, carrying thermochemical decomposition reaction occurs to generate water vapor/hydrogen mixture gas and second oxygen gas, introducing the water vapor/hydrogen mixed gas through the second cooling device and hydrophobic device becomes hydrogen-rich gas; (3) providing methane-rich gas, mixing the methane-rich gas, first oxygen, and second oxygen, and reacting in the methane partial oxidation reactor to form synthesis gas; and (4) mixing the synthesis gas, the carbon monoxide-rich gas, and the hydrogen-rich gas, feeding to fuel chemical synthesis column to obtain fuel chemical.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The first high temperature thermochemical reactor and the second high temperature thermochemical reactor have the same structure including the rotating ring structure having fins on the surface and a reactor barrel surrounding the periphery of the rotating ring structure; the reactor cylinder comprises an oxygen outlet, a carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet or a water vapor/hydrogen mixed gas outlet, a carbon dioxide-rich gas inlet or a water vapor inlet; the sunlight illuminates one side of the rotating ring structure on the side of the sunlight, the fin undergoes a reduction reaction to generate oxygen and a fin reducer, and the oxygen is output through the oxygen outlet, the fin reducer is rotated to the other side by the rotating ring structure; the fin reducing material is oxidized with a carbon dioxide-rich gas input from the carbon dioxide-rich gas inlet to form a carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed gas, the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixture is output from the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet; or the fin reducer is oxidized with water vapor input from the water vapor inlet to form a water vapor/hydrogen mixture, the water vapor/hydrogen mixture is output from the water vapor/hydrogen mixed gas outlet. The first temperature lowering device includes a first waste heat boiler connected to a carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet of the first high temperature thermochemical reactor and a first heat exchanger connected to the first waste heat boiler, the first compressor is further disposed between the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet of the first heat exchanger and the inlet of the carbon dioxide removal unit. The carbon dioxide-rich gas outlet of the carbon dioxide removal device is connected to the carbon dioxide-rich gas inlet of the first heat exchanger, the first mixer and the first preheater are sequentially connected between the carbon dioxide-rich gas outlet of the first heat exchanger and the carbon dioxide-rich gas inlet of the first high temperature thermochemical reactor. The second cooling device includes a second waste heat boiler connected to the water vapor/hydrogen mixed gas outlet of the second high temperature thermochemical reactor and a first cooler connected to the second waste heat boiler, the second compressor is further disposed between the first cooler and the hydrophobic device. The system also includes a second mixer, the second mixer inlet is connected to an oxygen outlet of the first high temperature thermochemical reactor and an oxygen outlet of the second high temperature thermochemical reactor, the second mixer outlet is connected to the oxygen inlet of the methane partial oxidation reactor. The second mixer outlet and the oxygen inlet of the methane partial oxidation reactor are connected in turn. a third waste heat boiler, a second heat exchanger, a second cooler, a vacuum pump, a third compressor, and a third heat exchanger. The system further includes a fourth compressor, the fourth compressor being connected to the methane-rich gas inlet of the methane partial oxidation reactor after passing through the second heat exchanger, the second heat exchanger is used for heat exchange between the first oxygen and the second oxygen and the methane-rich gas. The system further includes a third mixer, the inlet of the third mixer and the carbon monoxide-rich gas outlet of the carbon dioxide removal device respectively, the hydrogen-rich gas outlet of the dehydration unit and the syngas outlet of the methane partial oxidation reactor are connected, the outlet of the third mixer is connected to the fuel chemical synthesis column via a fifth compressor and a second preheater. The fourth waste heat boiler, the third heat exchanger is sequentially connected between the synthesis gas outlet of the methane partial oxidation reactor and the inlet of the third mixer, and the third heat exchanger is used for the first oxygen And heat exchange between the second oxygen and the syngas. The fuel chemical synthesis column includes a combination of methanol synthesis column, Fischer-Tropsch synthesis column, olefin synthesis column, and/or ethylene glycol synthesis column. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the first cooling device comprises first waste heat boiler connected to the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet of the first high temperature thermochemical reactor and a first heat connected to the first waste heat boiler exchanger, passing the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed gas from the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed gas outlet of the first high temperature thermochemical reactor first through the first waste heat boiler to recover heat, passing through the first heat exchanger to heat and cool, pressurizing by the first compressor to a normal pressure of ~2 MPa, introducing the carbon dioxide removal device, where the carbon dioxide-rich recycle gas from the carbon dioxide-rich gas outlet of the carbon dioxide removal unit exchanges heat with the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixture through the first heat exchanger, mixing the carbon dioxide-rich recycle gas with the newly supplied carbon dioxide in the first mixer, entering the first high temperature thermochemical reactor after being preheated by the first preheater to become the carbon dioxide-rich gas described in step (1); where the operating pressure of the first high temperature thermochemical reactor is between 0.15-0.2 atm, the temperature of the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixture from the carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixture outlet of the first high temperature thermochemical reactor is between 800-1100 degrees C, the first oxygen temperature from the oxygen outlet of the first high temperature thermochemical reactor is between 1300-1600 degrees C. In the step (2), the second cooling device includes a second waste heat boiler connected to the water vapor/hydrogen mixed gas outlet of the second high temperature thermochemical reactor and a first cooler connected to the second waste heat boiler; first recovering the steam/hydrogen mixed gas from the water vapor/hydrogen mixed gas outlet of the second high temperature thermochemical reactor by the second waste heat boiler and then cooled by the first cooler, pressurizing by the second compressor to normal pressure, entering into dehydration device; where the operating pressure of the second high temperature thermochemical reactor is between 0.15-0.2 atm, the temperature of the water vapor/hydrogen mixture from the water vapor/hydrogen mixture outlet of the second high temperature thermochemical reactor is between 800-1100 degrees C, the second oxygen temperature from the oxygen outlet of the second high temperature thermochemical reactor is 1300-1600 degrees C. In the step (3), first mixing the first oxygen and the second oxygen by second mixer, recovering the heat by the third waste heat boiler in turn, performing heat exchange of the second heat exchanger, cooling to 120-200 degrees C, cooling to below 40 degrees C via the second cooler. pumping by the vacuum pump, compressing to 3.5-5 MPa by the third compressor, raising heat exchange in third heat exchanger to 180-200 degrees C, entering the methane partial oxidation reactor, controlling the total amount of the first oxygen and the second oxygen entering the methane partial oxidation reactor at 0.6-0.7 Nm3/Nm3 of methane-rich gas; first pressurizing the methane-rich gas to 3.5-5 MPa through the fourth compressor, entering the second heat exchanger and the first oxygen and the second oxygen exchange heat, heating to 300-350 degrees C, re-entering the methane partial oxidation reactor reacts with the first oxygen and second oxygen to form a syngas. In the step (3), the reaction temperature of the methane partial oxidation reactor is 1260-1400 degrees C, the produced synthesis gas has a hydrogen to carbon ratio of 1-3, and the synthesis gas has molar content of methane at 0.5-1.5%. In the step (4), entering the synthesis gas from the outlet of the methane partial oxidation reactor into the fourth waste heat boiler to recover heat, entering the third heat exchanger to exchange heat with the first oxygen and the second oxygen, after the heat exchange, cooling the syngas to 230-250 degrees C, entering third mixer, mixing with the carbon monoxide-rich gas and the hydrogen-rich gas. In the step (4), mixing synthesis gas, the carbon monoxide-rich gas, and the hydrogen-rich gas in the third mixer, sequentially compressing by the fifth compressor, preheating by the second preheater, fuel chemical synthesis column reacting to obtain fuel chemical.

TI Waterproofing of surface of structure e.g.            concrete structure, involves treating surface of            structure with carbonated nano-bubble water, surfacing,            applying waterproof mortar to surface and applying            ceramic paint containing water-soluble modified latex            and fluorine-modified resin to surface.
AU HAM B
AE ECOENC CO LTD (ECOE-Non-standard)
GA 2022B38366
AB    NOVELTY - Waterproofing of surface of structure involves                treating the surface of the structure with                carbonated nano-bubble water, performing surface                surfacing and applying a waterproof mortar to the                surface according to the condition of the structure                and applying a ceramic paint having ozone                resistance to the surface. The nano-bubble                carbonate water is carbon-dioxide nano-bubble water                in which carbon dioxide is dissolved in a mixture                of water and alcohol and treated as nano-sized                bubbles, and 0.01-1.0 pts. wt. calcium oxide which                is dissolved based on 100 pts. wt. of the total                weight. The ceramic paint having ozone resistance                comprises 5-10 pts. wt. water-soluble modified                latex to 100 pts. wt. of the fluorine-modified                resin, 1-10 pts. wt. alkoxysilane hydrolyzate,                0.1-5 pts. wt. siliconate-based liquid component,                0.05-5 pts. wt. initiator, and 0.05-5 pts. wt.                emulsifier and a ceramic component comprising one                or two or more chosen from talc, clay, silica, and                acid clay.
   USE - Waterproofing of surface of structure e.g.                concrete structure, offshore wind power generation                structure for wind power and water treatment                facility structures.
   ADVANTAGE - The method stably protects the structure by                improving the acid resistance and provides the                structure with excellent ozone resistance,                waterproofing and corrosion resistance,                adhesiveness and physical performance and acid                resistance. By using ozone-resistant paint with                excellent physical performance such as adhesiveness                and durability, the life of the facility can be                maintained for a long time. The structure has                excellent self-healing performance, lifespan and                waterproofing effect by increasing the density of                the voids and salt damage prevention properties in                the case of coastal or offshore structures. The                application of the ozone-resistant paint is smooth                by arranging the surface using the mortar with                waterproof performance, and it improves the                properties of the surface and exhibits the                ozone-resistance performance more                effectively.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Waterproofing of surface of structure involves                treating the surface of the structure with                carbonated nano-bubble water, performing surface                surfacing and applying a waterproof mortar to the                surface according to the condition of the structure                and applying a ceramic paint having ozone                resistance to the surface. The nano-bubble                carbonate water is carbon-dioxide nano-bubble water                in which carbon dioxide is dissolved in a mixture                of water and alcohol and treated as nano-sized                bubbles, and 0.01-1.0 pts. wt. calcium oxide which                is dissolved based on 100 pts. wt. of the total                weight. The ceramic paint having ozone resistance                comprises 5-10 pts. wt. water-soluble modified                latex to 100 pts. wt. of the fluorine-modified                resin, 1-10 pts. wt. alkoxysilane hydrolyzate,                0.1-5 pts. wt. siliconate-based liquid component,                0.05-5 pts. wt. initiator, and 0.05-5 pts. wt.                emulsifier and a ceramic component comprising one                or two or more chosen from talc, clay, silica, and                acid clay. The weight ratio of the liquid resin                component and the ceramic component is 100:100-500.                The fluoro-modified resin comprises a mixture of a                fluororesin having weight average molecular weight                of 1000-50000 and an acrylic urethane resin having                weight average molecular weight of 5000-100000. The                weight ratio of the fluororesin and the acrylic                urethane resin is 100:100-150. The fluororesin uses                a vinylidene fluoride resin. The acrylic urethane                resin is prepared by mixing and stirring 130 pts.                wt. ethanol and 12-13 pts. wt. polyisocyanate                curing agent based on 100 pts. wt. acrylic urethane                based on acrylic polyol. The water-soluble modified                latex comprises 10-20 pts. wt. acrylic resin, 10-20                pts. wt. styrene-butadiene rubber, 0.1-5 pts. wt.                hydroxyl acrylate monomer, 15-30 pts. wt.                unsaturated polyester resin, 0.1-5 pts. wt. gallic                acid, 1-10 pts. wt. metal cations, 0.1-1.0 pt. wt.                aluminum chloride, 0.5-5 pts. wt. dispersant, and                40-70 pts. wt. water.The acrylic urethane resin has                weight average molecular weight of 5000-100000. The                aluminum chloride is formed by the reaction of an                acid and aluminum hydroxide. The ceramic paint                having the ozone resistance property further                comprises a thickener, a plasticizer, a diluent, a                flame retardant, a UV blocker and a latent curing                agent. The sunscreen is prepared by mixing 23-32                pts. wt. zinc oxide with respect to 12-29 pts. wt.                titanium dioxide having average particle size of 25                to 38 nm powder, mixing 12-15 pts. wt. silane-based                compound of (&#947;                )-aminoethyleneaminopropyltrimethoxysilane with the                mixture to impart adhesion, and adding 2-5 pts. wt.                silicone oil having pH of 7 to 8 in the mixed                solution, mixing 3-5 pts. wt. surfactant and                modifying the surface by stirring at                12-18&#176; C for 30-45 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Process: The waterproof mortar is                  prepared by preparing 20-50 wt.% fast-hardening                  cement, 30-60 wt.% Portland cement, 5-30 wt.%                  (&#945; )-type hemihydrate gypsum and                  0.1-5.0% wt.% nitrite powder to prepare binder,                  and mixing a filler and aggregate to the binder                  by mixing with water. In the process of treating                  the carbonate nanobubble water, water and alcohol                  are mixed in a weight ratio of 100:0.1-100,                  respectively. In the process of treating the                  carbonic acid nanobubble water, the pH of the                  carbonic acid nanobubble water adjusted to 4-6.5                  is used. The application of the ceramic paint                  having ozone resistance is applied to the surface                  to be constructed by using a painting, rolling or                  spraying method and then hardening. An acrylic                  urethane-based or siloxane-based water repellent                  is additionally coated. Preferred Components: The                  ceramic paint having ozone resistance further                  comprises 0.1-5 pts. wt. antifoaming agent,                  dispersing agent, cationic wetting agent,                  leveling agent, surface tension reducing agent,                  shrinkage reducing agent, surface fluidity                  adjusting agent, retarders, antibacterial agents                  and inorganic pigments and 5-20 pts. wt.                  non-reactive diluent chosen from dodecylphenol,                  benzyl alcohol and propylene glycol monomethyl                  ether.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The nitrite powder has                  specific surface area of 1000-8000 cm2/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The siliconate-based                  liquid component comprises 0.1- 5 wt.% potassium                  methylsiliconate, 0.1-5 wt.%                  3-iodo-2-propynyl-N-butyl carbamate, 0.1-10 wt.%                  epoxy-based binder resin and 0.1-5 wt.% inorganic                  polymer containing fluorine group.

PN CN114275938-A
TI High-hardness waste water processing device            comprises intermediate pool, first-level pH adjustment            tank connected between first-level softener and pool,            FBL filter A, second-level pH adjustment tank,            second-level softener, FBL filter B and cleaning            tank.
AU ZHAO H
AE WUHAN FEIBOLE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 202251836U
AB    NOVELTY - High-hardness wastewater treatment device                includes an intermediate pool, a first-level pH                adjustment tank, a first-level softener, FBL filter                A, a second-level pH adjustment tank, a                second-level softener, an FBL filter B and a                cleaning tank. The first-level PH adjustment tank                is connected between the intermediate pool and the                first-level softener, the FBL filter A is connected                between the first-level softener and the                second-level PH adjustment tank, and the                second-level softener is connected between the                secondary PH adjustment tank and the FBL filter B.                The FBL filter B is set in communication with the                cleaning tank. The supernatant in the FBL filter B                is introduced into the cleaning tank to be                reused.
   USE - High-hardness wastewater treatment                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device improves the pH value in the waste                water by adding calcium (OH) and sodium hydroxide                (NaOH). The pH value is adjusted to neutral and                carbon dioxide (CO2) gas reacts with water to                generate carbonic acid, so that the water hardness                is reduced to less than 100 mg/l, convenient for                recycling and using, by using gas CO2 as production                waste gas, collecting it for reducing hardness,                greatly reducing the processing cost, and not                introducing the rest impurity ions. The calcium                carbonate residue generated at the same time also                can be used as raw material for neutralizing the                lime. The waste gas and waste slag are effectively                used to realize the effective treatment of waste                acid waste water, compared with the existing                conventional method, reducing the cost of                processing cost and Na + introduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for high-hardness waste water treatment, which                involves:
   (a) introducing the high-hardness water in the                intermediate pool into the first-level pH                adjustment tank, where water volume is 30-50 m3/h,                and adding the prepared calcium hydroxide solution                into the first-level pH adjustment tank, adjusting                the pH value to 12, reacting in the reactor for                20-40 minutes, stirring and fully reacting and                fully reacting the calcium ion and magnesium ion to                generate calcium carbonate and magnesium hydroxide                precipitation, where removal rate is 50%, while pH                value is adjusted to 12-12.5, and the reaction time                is 40-60 minutes;
   (b) flowing reacted liquid in the first-stage                pH adjusting tank into the first-stage softener,                and introducing carbon dioxide waste gas into the                first-stage softener by the induced draft fan and                the carbon dioxide buffer tank, where air pressure                is 60-70Kpa, and the gas volume is 10- 30 Nm3/h,                the reaction time is 40-60 minutes, after stirring                and fully reacting, removing calcium ion and                magnesium ion in the wastewater, at the removal                rate of 80%, and adjusting pH value to 9.5-10                during the reaction;
   (c) pumping reacted liquid in the primary                softener into the FBL filter A through the pump                body A and filtered, and flowing filtered                supernatant in the FBL filter to the secondary pH                adjustment tank, and introducing bottom mud into                the mud inside the pool;
   (d) adding the configured sodium hydroxide                solution (concentration is 8-10%) into the                secondary pH adjustment tank, adjusting the pH                value to 11.5-13.5, reacting in the reactor for                20-40 minutes, stirring and fully reacting and                putting calcium ion and magnesium ion, fully                reacting to form calcium carbonate and magnesium                hydroxide precipitation, at removal rate of                85-89%;
   (e) flowing reacted liquid in the secondary pH                adjustment tank to the secondary softener, and                passing cabon dioxide waste gas into the secondary                softener by the induced draft fan and the carbon                dioxide buffer tank after stirring and fully                reacting, removing calcium ion and magnesium ion in                the wastewater, at the removal rate of 90-95%, and                the pH value adjusted to 8-9 during the reaction                process;
   (f) pumping reacted liquid in the secondary                softener into the FBL filter B through the pump                body B and filtering, and flowing filtered                supernatant in the FBL filter B to the cleaning                tank for reuse, and depositing bottom mud into the                mud inside the pool; sending mud comprising a                mixture containing calcium carbonate, to the sewage                acid neutralization device by the pump body C as a                chemical for recycling; and
   (g) utilizing clear liquid in the clear liquid                pool and utilizing as circulating water to                supplement water or reverse osmosis raw                water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a high                hardness waste water processing device and                processing method. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).

TI Production of multi-level quality control material            used in tracing calibration of blood gas analyzer,            involves adding hydroxyethyl-piperazineethanesulfonic            acid to fluorocarbon emulsion, and replacing by fixed            ratio steel air cylinder.
AU LOU T
   LI A
   LI F
AE NANJING PERLONG BIOMEDICAL CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202198390H
AB    NOVELTY - Production of multi-level quality control                material involves preparing fluorocarbon emulsion                matrix using one of perfluorotributylamine,                perfluorotripropylamine and perfluorodecalin,                emulsifying, filtering filter film to obtain                substrate, adding potassium chloride, sodium                chloride, calcium chloride, sodium acetate,                (4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic                acid), sodium salt of                (4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic                acid), glucose and sodium lactate, to fluorocarbon                emulsion to make initial quality control liquid,                pouring initial quality control liquid into liquid                storage device and sealing, connecting one end of                liquid storage device to air pipe of fixed ratio                steel gas cylinder, using syringe needle to take                out the quality control liquid from liquid storage                device, encapsulating in ambolite bottle, inserting                fixed ratio steel gas cylinder air pipe into liquid                surface of ambolite bottle, quickly sealing with                flame gun, and storing after cooling.
   USE - Production of multi-level quality control                material used in tracing calibration method of                blood gas analyzer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing fluorocarbon                emulsion as substrate so as to effectively avoid                oxygen partial pressure and interference of carbon                dioxide partial pressure detected liquid path. The                method enables completely balancing initial quality                control gas-liquid phase so as to avoid forming                measuring error. The method enables effectively                simulating reference calibration data of red cell                pressure accumulation specific volume so as to                realize accuracy and validity of tracing                calibration process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of multi-level quality control                material involves preparing fluorocarbon emulsion                matrix using one of 20-40 %w/v                perfluorotributylamine, 20-40 %w/v                perfluorotripropylamine and 15-30 %w/v                perfluorodecalin, emulsifying, filtering the filter                film to obtain substrate, preparing initial quality                control liquid by configuring dosage and chemical                reagents of 0.1-0.5 g/L potassium chloride, 4-8 g/L                sodium chloride, 0.1-0.4 g/L calcium chloride, 1-5                g/L sodium acetate, 2-15 g/L                (4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic                acid) (Hepes), 1-7 g/L sodium salt of                (4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic                acid) (Hepes-Na), 0.5-3.5 g/L glucose and 0.1-1.5                g/L sodium lactate, adding the chemical reagents to                an appropriate amount of fluorocarbon emulsion to                make initial quality control liquid which does not                contain oxygen partial pressure and carbon dioxide                partial pressure, pouring the initial quality                control liquid into liquid storage device and                sealing, placing the device in 30-39 degrees C                water bath for 10-30 minutes, connecting one end of                liquid storage device to air pipe of fixed ratio                steel gas cylinder with air outlet pressure of                10-100 Pa.second, where the ventilation time is                10-60 minutes, after the ventilation replacement is                completed, sealing again, using syringe needle to                take out the quality control liquid from liquid                storage device, encapsulating in ambolite bottle,                inserting fixed ratio steel gas cylinder air pipe                into liquid surface of ambolite bottle at outlet                pressure of 10-100 Pa.second for 3-10 seconds,                after air in the bottle is emptied, quickly sealing                with flame gun, and storing after cooling. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for tracing                calibration method of blood gas analyzer, which                involves:
   (A) setting multiple initial quality control                liquid;
   (B) setting potassium, sodium, calcium,                chloride, lactic acid, glucose concentration and                oxygen-containing partial pressure, carbon dioxide                partial pressure, hydrogen ion, red cell specific                volume concentration value and configuring by                reagent, where in the configuration process, each                set of initial quality control liquid are                correspondingly configured with different gas                components of the steel gas bottle;
   (C) using qualified blood gas analyzer to                respectively each data of multiple different                initial quality control liquid;
   (D) comparing detection data of each initial                quality control liquid with corresponding set                numerical value, if not consistent, the quality                control liquid configuration error, need to be                reconfigured, if they are consistent, performing                traceability calibration verification to the item                in each initial quality control liquid, using                direct weighing method for measuring and tracing                sources of potassium, sodium, calcium, chloride,                lactic acid, glucose concentration,                oxygen-containing partial pressure, carbon dioxide                partial pressure by qualified blood gas analyzer                detection value into the calculation formula to                calculate gas content;
   (E) configuring corresponding to used steel                gas component content comparison for tracing                source, where the red cell specific volume is                traced to source through measurement of qualified                blood-gas electrolyte analyzer or the comparison                with the qualified quality control material-off                calibration and hydrogen ion concentration is                measured to be traced by metering qualified pH                meter; and
   (F) comparing the traceability data with the                qualified blood gas analyzer to judge data.

The stabilizing agent                  added in the emulsification process is 1-5%                  propanetriol.
