PN CN110759360-A; CN211255278-U
TI Producing sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, and sodium chloride by evaporating natural alkali comprises e.g. subjecting the natural alkali to first mechanical recompression vapor evaporation, and performing first solid-liquid separation.
AU LI M
   ZHANG K
   YIN S
   ZHANG Y
   YUAN W
   FAN W
AE UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
   HENAN YUANHENG FINE CHEM CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
   YIKELI TIANJIN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (YIKE-Non-standard)
GA 202013779H
AB    NOVELTY - Producing sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, and sodium chloride by evaporating natural alkali comprises (a) subjecting the natural alkali to a first mechanical recompression vapor evaporation to obtain a first solid-liquid mixture and distilled water, (b) performing first solid-liquid separation on the first solid-liquid mixture, (c) thermally decomposing the mixed alkali to obtain solid sodium carbonate and carbon dioxide tail gas, (d) carbonizing the first mother liquor using the carbon dioxide tail gas, and subjecting the obtained second solid-liquid mixture to second solid-liquid separation, (e) subjecting second mother liquor to second mechanical recompression vapor evaporation to obtain a third solid-liquid mixture and distilled water, and (f) performing third solid-liquid separation on the third solid-liquid mixture to obtain sodium chloride solid and third mother liquor, returning the third mother liquor to the step (d), and carbonizing together with first mother liquor.
   USE - The method is useful for producing sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, and sodium chloride by evaporating natural alkali.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes steam mechanical recompression technology to replace the multi-effect evaporation technology in the traditional method; designs a recycling process of exhaust gas and waste water; solves the problem of a large amount of high salt water efflux; is reliable; realizes the full-component resource utilization in the production of natural alkali at low cost, and the multiple effects of energy saving, emission reduction, efficiency improvement and clean production; has demonstration significance for the alkali nitrate industry; is economical and environmentally friendly; and provides low system operation cost, low energy consumption, small equipment investment, and easy industrialization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, and sodium chloride by evaporating natural alkali comprises (a) subjecting the natural alkali to a first mechanical recompression vapor evaporation to obtain a first solid-liquid mixture and distilled water, (b) performing first solid-liquid separation on the first solid-liquid mixture to obtain a mixed alkali and a first mother liquor, (c) thermally decomposing the mixed alkali to obtain solid sodium carbonate and carbon dioxide tail gas, (d) carbonizing the first mother liquor using the carbon dioxide tail gas, and subjecting the obtained second solid-liquid mixture to a second solid-liquid separation to obtain a sodium bicarbonate solid and a second mother liquor, (e) subjecting the second mother liquor to a second mechanical recompression vapor evaporation to obtain a third solid-liquid mixture and distilled water, and (f) performing third solid-liquid separation on the third solid-liquid mixture to obtain a sodium chloride solid and a third mother liquor, returning the third mother liquor to the step (d), and carbonizing together with the first mother liquor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system for producing sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate and sodium chloride by evaporating natural alkali comprises a first steam mechanical recompression evaporation device. The first steam mechanical recompression evaporation device is provided with a natural alkali liquid inlet, a distilled water outlet and a solid-liquid mixture outlet. The first solid-liquid separation device is provided with a solid-liquid mixture inlet, a solid outlet, and a liquid outlet. The solid-liquid mixture inlet is connected to the solid-liquid mixture outlet of the first steam mechanical recompression evaporation device. The thermal decomposition device is provided with a solid inlet and a gas outlet, and the solid inlet is connected to the solid outlet of the first solid-liquid separation device. The mother liquid carbonization device is provided with a liquid inlet, a gas inlet and a solid-liquid mixture outlet. The liquid inlet is connected to the liquid outlet of the first solid-liquid separation device, and the gas inlet is connected to the gas outlet of the thermal decomposition device. A second solid-liquid separation device is provided with a solid-liquid mixture inlet, a solid outlet, and a liquid outlet. The solid-liquid mixture inlet is connected to the solid-liquid mixture outlet of the mother liquid carbonization device. A second steam mechanical recompression evaporation device, the second steam mechanical recompression evaporation device is provided with a liquid inlet, a distilled water outlet and a solid-liquid mixture outlet. The liquid inlet is connected with the liquid outlet of the second solid-liquid separation device. A third solid-liquid separation device is provided with a solid-liquid mixture inlet, a solid outlet, and a liquid outlet. The solid-liquid mixture inlet is connected to the solid-liquid mixture outlet of the second steam mechanical recompression evaporation device. The liquid outlet is connected with the liquid inlet of the mother liquid carbonization device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The content of sodium carbonate in the natural alkali is 70-200 g/l, the content of sodium bicarbonate is 60-160 g/l, and the content of sodium chloride is 5-60 g/l. The content of sodium chloride in the first mother liquor is 80-230 g/l, based on the content of sodium carbonate, the total content of sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate in the first mother liquor is 200-450 g/l. The content of sodium chloride in the second mother liquor is 240-300 g/l, based on the content of sodium carbonate, the total content of sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate in the second mother liquor is 80-250 g/l. The content of sodium chloride in the third mother liquor is 160-280 g/l, based on the content of sodium carbonate, the total content of sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate in the third mother liquor is 200-350 g/l. Preferred Method: The temperature of evaporation of the first mechanical recompressed steam is 85-98 degrees C. The temperature of the carbonization is 20-60 degrees C, and the pH of the carbonization end point is 6-11. The evaporation temperature of the second mechanical recompressed steam is 85-96 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The steam compressors in the first steam mechanical recompression evaporation device and the second steam mechanical recompression evaporation device are independently a centrifugal steam compressor, a roots type steam compressor, or a centrifugal steam compressor.

TI Autonomous vehicle energy replenishment system,            has autonomous vehicle which is powered by hydrogen            fuel cell adapted to have its energy source replenished            through hydrogen fuel dispensed from hydrogen            dispenser.
AU COOPER B
AE RENAM PROPERTIES PTY LTD (RENA-Non-standard)
   RENAM PROPERTIES PTY LTD (RENA-Non-standard)
GA 201950471T
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has a source of renewable                methane. An electricity generating module (16) is                operatively coupled to and fuelled by renewable                methane from the renewable methane source for                generating electricity. A methane reformer (18) is                arranged to receive renewable methane from the                renewable methane source. A hydrogen dispenser (20)                is operatively coupled to the methane reformer to                receive the hydrogen fuel for dispensing. An                autonomous vehicle (12a) is powered by a hydrogen                fuel cell adapted to have its energy source                replenished through the hydrogen fuel dispensed                from the hydrogen dispenser. The synthetic methane                reactor (50) produces the renewable methane in the                form of renewable synthetic methane. The synthetic                methane reactor is operatively coupled to a carbon                dioxide source (52) and a hydrogen source (54)                provide carbon dioxide and hydrogen respectively to                the synthetic methane reactor to combine in a                catalytic reaction for the production of the                renewable methane.
   USE - Energy replenishment system for autonomous                vehicle e.g. passenger vehicles, buses, heavy duty,                hydrogen and electric powered driverless                trucks.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides an environmentally                acceptable solution to replenishing or refuelling                autonomous vehicles. The system lends itself to                replenishing or refuelling both hydrogen fuel cell                and battery powered autonomous vehicles. The system                is configured to provide communication with the                autonomous vehicles to control staging of                replenishment. The system in recovering waste heat                associated with the generation of electricity                efficiently operates to promote reforming of                renewable methane, cracking of ammonia, or                processing of other hydrogen carrier fuel sources                thus the external energy/heating requirement for                the processing is reduced. The system provides the                capability to generate hydrogen at the required                level of purity for fuelling hydrogen fuel cells of                autonomous vehicles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The renewable methane recovery module is                operatively coupled to the methane reformer to                recover unreformed methane from reformer and direct                it to the electricity generating module to provide                a supplementary fuel source in generating                electricity at generating module. The autonomous                vehicle control module is configured to communicate                with either the hydrogen fuel cell or battery                powered autonomous vehicle to control its movement                relative to either the hydrogen dispenser or the                electricity dispenser, respectively. The control                module is configured to operatively communicate                with hydrogen dispenser to sequentially stage the                arrival and departure of the autonomous vehicles in                connection with their energy replenishment. The                hydrogen processing module is located between the                methane reformer and the hydrogen dispenser for                processing the hydrogen fuel produced in the                methane reformer into a purified form suitable for                dispensing to the autonomous vehicle having the                hydrogen fuel cell. The hydrogen processing module                includes a hydrogen compressor which is operatively                coupled to the methane reformer for compressing the                hydrogen fuel prior to dispensing it to the                hydrogen fuel cell of the associated autonomous                vehicle. The maintenance modules are powered by the                electricity generating module. The maintenance                modules include autonomous vehicle servicing                modules, autonomous vehicle cleaning modules and                autonomous vehicle restocking modules. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of                replenishing an energy source of an autonomous                vehicle involves producing renewable methane. The                electricity utilizing some of the produced                renewable methane is generated as a fuel source.                The hydrogen fuel from produced renewable methane                is produced by reforming it in a methane reformer                harnessing waste heat produced in the generation of                electricity to promote reforming of the methane.                The hydrogen fuel is dispensed to an autonomous                vehicle powered by a hydrogen fuel cell and the                energy source of the hydrogen fuel cell is                replenished. The communication is made with either                the hydrogen fuel cell or battery powered                autonomous vehicle to control its movement relative                to either the hydrogen or electricity dispenser,                respectively. The communication is effected through                the hydrogen or electricity dispenser to                sequentially control the arrival and departure of                the autonomous vehicles in connection with energy                replenishment or refuelling. The renewable methane                is produced in the form of renewable synthetic                methane in a synthetic methane reactor. The                biomethane produced by the biogas reactor is                combined with the renewable synthetic methane                produced in the catalytic reaction within the                synthetic methane reactor in the event the carbon                dioxide is obtained from anaerobic digestion of                biomass within the biogas reactor. The hydrogen is                provided to the synthetic methane reactor from                either electrolysis of water to produce the                hydrogen. The electrolysis is powered by a                renewable energy source, or anaerobic digestion of                biomass within an anaerobic digester to produce the                hydrogen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                autonomous vehicle energy replenishment                system.

   Electricity generating module (16)
   Methane reformer (18)
   Hydrogen dispenser (20)
   Synthetic methane reactor (50)
   Carbon dioxide source (52)

PN CN109401878-A; CN109401878-B
TI Brewing fruity and soft dry red wine involves            selecting rape raw materials with mature and healthy,            disease-free mildew and sugar content, selecting one of            Matheran, Merlot and Cabernet Franc, pre-filling with            carbon dioxide or nitrogen.
AU ZHOU P
   LI J
   CHENG J
   QIU H
   YUAN X
   YAN H
   WU F
   LIU Y
AE COFCO GREATWALL WINERY PENGLAI CO LTD (COFC-Non-standard)
GA 2019226490
AB    NOVELTY - Brewing fruity and soft dry red wine involves                selecting rape raw materials with mature and                healthy, disease-free mildew and not greater than                190g/L sugar content, selceting one of Matheran,                Merlot and Cabernet Franc, pre-filling with a                purity of not greater than 99.9% carbon dioxide or                nitrogen in the tank when the detection                concentration is not greater than 98%, placing the                artificially sorted grape raw material into the                tank, reducing the temperature inside the tank to                0-4 degrees C through the tank's own temperature                control system, immersing for 2-5 days at low                temperature, and detecting the gas concentration                and maintaining not greater than 98% every day. The                grape raw material is rapidly warmed to 15-20                degrees C, anaerobic intracellular fermentation for                5-15 days, daily detection and maintenance of gas                concentration not greater than 98%. The sensory                evaluation of the sample to determine the time of                the can, remove the stem, moderately break,                continue to immerse for 1-3 days, and press the                juice.
   USE - Method for brewing fruity and soft dry red                wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to brew fruity and soft dry                red wine, which expands the raw material source of                the fruity and soft dry red wine, produces three                kinds of fruity and soft dry red wine base wines,                and base wines have rich fruity aromas such as                cherries and bananas, achieves the stability of the                final product quality style and achieves                large-scale operation of brewing production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The turbidity of juice is not less than 50                Nephelometric Turbidity Unit (NTU) , and inoculated                with Saccharomyces cerevisiae EC1118, and                controlling the temperature at 16-18 degrees C. The                residual sugar is not less than 4.0g/L, sulfur                adjustment is 40-60mg/L, and clarification                treatment to obtain the first base wine. The                pre-filled with a purity of not greater than 99.9%                carbon dioxide or nitrogen in the tank, and the                artificially sorted 5-15% grape raw material is                stalked and broken first into the tank, and the                remaining 85-95% of the grape raw materials are put                into the tank when the detection concentration is                not greater than 98%. The anaerobic intracellular                fermentation is performed for 7-15 days during the                temperature control period of 20-25 degrees C, and                daily detection and maintenance of gas                concentration not greater than 98%. The specific                gravity of the bottom of the tank detected is not                less than 1.010, and removing the stem and crushing                the juice. The turbidity of juice is not less than                50 Nephelometric Turbidity Unit (NTU) , and                inoculated with Saccharomyces cerevisiae EC1118,                and controlling the temperature at 16-18 degrees C.                The residual sugar is not less than 4.0g/L, sulfur                adjustment is 40-60mg/L, and clarification                treatment to obtain the second base wine. The                pre-filled with a purity of not greater than 99.9%                carbon dioxide or nitrogen in the tank, and the                artificially sorted grape raw material is placed                into the tank when the detection concentration is                not greater than 98%. The anaerobic intracellular                fermentation is performed for 5-15 days during the                temperature control period of 20-25 degrees C, and                daily detection and maintenance of gas                concentration not greater than 98%. The specific                gravity of the bottom of the tank detected is not                less than 1.010, and removing the stem and crushing                the juice. The turbidity of juice is not less than                50 Nephelometric Turbidity Unit (NTU), and                inoculated with Saccharomyces cerevisiae EC1118,                and controlling the temperature at 16-18 degrees C.                The residual sugar is not less than 4.0g/L, sulfur                adjustment is 40-60mg/L, and clarification                treatment to obtain the second base wine. The                sampling and detecting the specific gravity of the                juice in the fruit is reduced by 10-20 units from                the initial value, and adding the appropriate                amount of the juice after inoculating Saccharomyces                cerevisiae. The appropriate amount refers to 5 wt.%                of the grape raw material. The liquor is circulated                once every 2 days, and when the specific gravity of                the bottom of the tank is not less than 1.010, the                stem is crushed and pressed to extract juice. The                turbidity of juice is not less than 50                Nephelometric Turbidity Unit (NTU), and inoculated                with Saccharomyces cerevisiae EC1118, and                controlling the temperature at 16-18 degrees C. The                residual sugar is not less than 4.0g/L, sulfur                adjustment is 40-60mg/L, and clarification                treatment to obtain the third base wine. The three                base wines obtained are homogenized according to                the first base wine, second base wine, and third                base wine to 30-40%, 30-40%, 20-40%, and then                stabilized, clarified and canned to obtain a fruity                and soft dry red wine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The breaking rate is                  controlled to 30-50%. The degree of breakage in                  the de-stalking is 30-50%. The appropriate amount                  refers to 5 wt.% of the grape raw material.

PN CN112439404-A; CN112439404-B
TI Preparing nano-titanium dioxide loaded graphene            high-activity catalyst comprises e.g. mixing titanium            source with liquid carbon dioxide, hydrolyzing, adding            nickel-based catalyst, introducing hydrogen gas,            heating and reacting.
AU HUANG X
AE HANGZHOU ZHITAI PURIFICATION TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   HANGZHOU ZHITAI PURIFICATION TECHNOLOGY (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2021259928
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-titanium dioxide loaded                graphene high-activity catalyst comprises (i)                mixing titanium source with liquid carbon dioxide,                reacting with water and hydrolyzing to obtain                titanium dioxide, and (ii) adding a nickel-based                catalyst into the above reaction system,                introducing hydrogen gas, heating to 900-1100                degrees C and reacting to obtain final                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                nano-titanium dioxide loaded graphene high-activity                catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves catalytic performance of                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  controlling the temperature and/or pressure of                  the reaction system between step (i) and step                  (ii) thus liquid carbon dioxide is converted into                  supercritical carbon dioxide. The step (ii)                  comprises releasing to normal pressure,                  volatilizing a part of the carbon dioxide and the                  other part of the carbon dioxide forming dry ice,                  then heating to make dry ice sublime. The step                  (i) comprises dispersing titanium source and the                  surfactant into anhydrous ethanol, mixing with                  liquid carbon dioxide and then reacting with                  aqueous ethanol solution. The step (i) comprises                  reacting with water for 12-16 hours. The method                  comprises performing step (i) under ultrasonic                  vibration conditions of 5-8 kHz. The step (ii)                  comprises reacting for 5-10 minutes after                  hydrogenation. Preferred Components: The                  surfactant includes block copolymer poly(ethylene                  glycol)-block-poly(propylene                  glycol)-block-poly(ethylene glycol) (P123). The                  titanium source includes tetrabutyl titanate,                  titanium isopropoxide, titanium tetrachloride                  and/or titanium sulfate. The nickel-based                  catalyst includes foamed nickel.

PT P
PN CN112121807-A
TI Carbon dioxide hydrogenation catalyst used for preparing ethylene, propylene, butene, comprises lanthanium galodinium ferric oxide, lanthanium ferric oxide, iron oxides, and metal oxides.
AU GUO Q
   MA L
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 202101614E
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide (CO2) hydrogenation catalyst comprises components of 1-74.9% lanthanium galodinium ferric oxide ((LaGd)xFeyO3), 2-25% lanthanium ferric oxide (LaFeO3), and balance is metal oxide, where metal oxides are mainly iron oxides which includes Fe2O3 and Fe3O4. The catalyst is applied to CO2 hydrogenation to produce low-carbon olefins.
   USE - CO2 hydrogenation catalyst used for preparing ethylene, propylene and butene.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst promotes adsorption and dissociation of carbon monoxide (CO) to large extent, exhibits good selectivity for low-carbon olefins in hydrogenation reaction, and has potential economic value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The co-action of lanthanium (La) and galodinium (Gd) elements in catalyst provides to improve olefin selectivity. The optimization of catalyst is modified by impregnation of at least one element of magnesium (Mg), manganese (Mn), sodium (Na), zinc (Zn), and potassium (K). Preferred Conditions: The catalyst is reduced and activated at 400-700 degrees C before being applied to CO2 hydrogenation reaction. The catalyst is applied to CO2 hydrogenation reaction, and the reaction conditions are hydrogen (H2)/CO2=1-4, 150-400 degrees C, 2-8 MegaPascal, 1000-4000 per hour.

GA 2020A2378L

   USE - The method is useful in improving decomposition rate of alumina factory.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The generation device for generating flue gas containing carbon dioxide comprises roasting furnace or lime kiln. The generating device for generating the flue gas containing carbon dioxide is used for sending the flue gas from the first pipeline to the washing tower by the fan, sending the strong filtrate of the aluminum hydroxide procedure to the washing tower through the second pipeline, and sending the mixed underflow solution into decomposing process through the third pipeline by the pump. The washing tower has an air inlet at the bottom and a liquid inlet at the top, and the washing tower is spraying type, self-flowing type or baffled internal structure. The bottom stream solution of the washing tower is sent to any decomposition tank in the whole series of decomposition steps.

PN CN111072452-A
TI Preparation of ethylene glycol comprises feeding ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide into circulating jet mixing reactor, reacting in the presence of catalyst, hydrolyzing obtained reaction stream in second reactor, and separating product.
AU ZHU J
   LIU Z
   DONG Z
   GU X
   YU H
   WEI F
   WU B
AE JIANGSU OXIRAN CHEM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202038036J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ethylene glycol comprises feeding ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide raw materials into circulating jet mixing reactor, reacting in the presence of catalyst to obtain ethylene carbonate stream, introducing obtained reaction stream into second reactor to hydrolyze ethylene carbonate, and separating reaction stream to obtain ethylene glycol.
   USE - Method for preparing ethylene glycol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly and atomically economical, and has mild conditions, safe operation, and simple process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The ethylene oxide raw material is in the form of aqueous solution having water content of 10-70 wt.%. Preferred Method: The first reaction is carried out at 80-200 degrees C and 0.1-6 MPa. The second reaction is carried out at 100-260 degrees C and 0.1-3 MPa. The separation of the final product is carried out by feeding second reaction stream into dehydration tower at 100-200 degrees C and 5-50 kPa, introducing obtained product in ethylene glycol refining tower for distillation at 120-200 degrees C and 1-30 kPa to obtain ethylene glycol above the tower, and separating catalyst from mixture for recycling.

AU WANG Y
   KUANG H
   LU X
   MA H
AE SANGZHI XIANGXIMIMA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SANG-Non-standard)
GA 2019076655
AB    NOVELTY - Production of natural honey comprises collecting honeycomb, keeping in sealed space at normal temperature and normal pressure, introducing high-concentration carbon dioxide, fumigating, sealing in space at normal temperature and normal pressure, dehumidifying air in space, cutting honeycomb cover, taking honey, ultrasonically treating, filtering, physically sterilizing at normal temperature and normal pressure, encapsulating, inspecting by lamp, and packaging.
   USE - Method for producing natural honey.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem about serious damage of original quality of honey.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide is kept at concentration of more 98%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sealed space is kept at temperature of 25-35 degrees C, relative humidity of 50% and normal pressure. Fumigating is done for 4 hours. Dehumidifying is done by standing using dehumidifying device and/or scavenger until water content of honey is reduced to less than or equal to 19%. The space is provided with ventilation system. Cutting is done by full-automatic cutting cap device. Ultrasonic treating is done at 25-35 degrees C at frequency of 20-30 KHz and power of 200-500 W for 10-30 minutes. Sterilizing is done by radiation sterilizing, pulse light sterilizing and/or pulse electric field sterilizing. Preferred Components: The honeycomb is overlapped.

Preferred Property: The composition is moisture-curable. Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 1-60 parts mass carbon dioxide adsorbent based on 100 parts mass urethane prepolymer.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108371306-A
TI Preparing complex fruit and vegetable sheets by beating raw materials, treating obtained slurry and subjecting slurry to high pressure carbon dioxide sterilization.
AU YI J
   BI J
   HOU C
   PENG J
   WU X
   ZHOU M
   LV J
AE INST AGRO FOOD SCI & TECHNOLOGY CHINESE (CAGS-C)
GA 201866117U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing complex fruit and vegetable sheets involves beating raw materials, treating the obtained slurry at 35-45 degrees C and 100-500 MPa for 10-60 minutes and subjecting the slurry to high pressure carbon dioxide sterilization.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing complex fruit and vegetable sheets which are useful for improving sleep.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared sheets have high nutrient retention rate, crisp taste and low energy and balance the dietary structure of people with less dietary fiber, vitamins, phenols and other nutrients in their daily diet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The raw material comprises 20-45 wt.% day lily, 20-35 wt.% carrot, 5-15 wt.% banana, 5-20 wt.% millet, 3-5 wt.% whey protein, 2-5 wt.% jujube kernel, 2-5 wt.% lily, 0.5-2 wt.% almond, 0.2-0.8 wt.% salt, 0.5-3.5 wt.% glucose, 0.5-2.5 wt.% beta -glucan, 0-5 wt.% sucrose and 0.5-1.5 wt.% calcium chloride. Preferred Method: The method further involves adding 2-5% probiotic starter and 0.2-1% white sugar to the slurry, adjusting the pH to 3.8-4, fermenting at 28-37 degrees C for 24-48 hours, removing the slurry, adding binder and plasticizer to fermented slurry, forming a sheet, freezing and drying in a glass state, where both the freezing and glass drying temperatures are below the glass transition temperature of slurry. The high pressure carbon dioxide sterilization is performed at 20-45 degrees C and 10-35 MPa. The slurry is dried at 20-100 Pa. The day lily, carrot and banana are steamed at 95-100 degrees C for 10 minutes before beating, the enzyme is protected by coloring and cooled to room temperature using tap water. The probiotic is also added with raw material in an amount of 5-10%. The sheets are prepared by (a) using 3-5 wt.% dry medlar, 2-5 wt.% cinnamon, 0.5-2 wt.% Angelica dahurica, 0.2-1 wt.% elecampane, 0.1-0.6 wt.% galangal, 0.3-7 wt.% Amomum tsao-ko, 1-3 wt.% sealwort, 1-3 wt.% fleece flower, 1-3 wt.% rice sedge, 0.5-3% Sambucus and 0.7-1.5 wt.% goosegrass, (b) grinding dry medlar, cinnamon, A.dahurica, elecampane, galangal and A.tsao-ko into powder, mixing, adding sealwort, fleece flower, rice sedge, Sambucus and goosegrass, mixing, crushing, adding cellulase, adjusting the pH to 4-6, performing enzymatic hydrolysis at 32-38 degrees C, filtering, retaining the hydrolyzate, cooking the enzymatic hydrolyzate at 90-100 degrees C and 1.2 MPa for 20-25 minutes, distilling the cooked hydrolyzate under reduced pressure, subjecting the solid mixture to calcination treatment for 3-6 minutes and (c) adding 2-5 wt.% edible oil and 10-12 wt.% maifanite particles, where the particle size of maifanite particles is 5-10 mm, frying the material, removing the maifanite, draining the solid mixture and adding solid mixture into slurry and performing remaining process.
   ACTIVITY - Hypnotic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI Device that is used for converting first hydrocarbon-based feedstock comprising aromatic compound, comprises fractionation train suitable for extracting at least one cut comprising benzene, and one cut comprising toluene.
AU FEUGNET F
   LAROCHE C
   JOLY J
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 202245986H
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises fractionation train (4-7) suitable for extracting at least one cut comprising benzene (22), and one cut comprising toluene (23) and one cut comprising xylene and ethylbenzene (24) from the first hydrocarbon-based feedstock (2). The xylene separating unit (10) suitable for treating the cut comprising xylene and ethylbenzene and for producing an extract (39) comprising para-xylene and a raffinate (40) comprising ortho-xylene, meta-xylene and ethylbenzene.
   USE - Device that is used for converting first hydrocarbon-based feedstock comprising aromatic compound (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device can increase the production of aromatics and notably of para-xylene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises fractionation train (4-7) suitable for extracting at least one cut comprising benzene (22), and one cut comprising toluene (23) and one cut comprising xylene and ethylbenzene (24) from the first hydrocarbon-based feedstock (2). The xylene separating unit (10) suitable for treating the cut comprising xylene and ethylbenzene and for producing an extract (39) comprising para-xylene and a raffinate (40) comprising ortho-xylene, meta-xylene and ethylbenzene. The isomerization unit (11) suitable for treating the raffinate (40) and for producing an isomerate enriched in para-xylene (42), which is sent to the fractionation train. The pyrolysis unit (13) suitable for treating a second hydrocarbon-based feedstock (30), for producing at least one pyrolysis effluent (31) comprising hydrocarbon-based compound of 6-10C at least partially feeding the hydrocarbon-based feedstock, and for producing a pyrolysis gas (32) comprising at least carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The WGS water gas shift reaction section (50) suitable for treating the pyrolysis gas (32) and for producing a WGS gas (51) enriched in carbon dioxide (CO2) and in hydrogen. The CO2 aromatization reaction section (52) suitable for at least partly treating the WGS gas (51) to produce a hydrocarbon effluent (53) comprising aromatic compounds, and feeding the first hydrocarbon-based feedstock (2) with the hydrocarbon effluent (53).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for conversion of a first hydrocarbon-based feedstock comprising aromatic compound, which involves: (a) fractionating the first hydrocarbon-based feedstock in a fractionation train in order to extract at least one cut comprising benzene, one cut comprising toluene and one cut comprising xylenes and ethylbenzene;(b) separating the cut comprising xylene and ethylbenzene in a xylenes separating unit;(c)producing an extract comprising para-xylene and a raffinate comprising ortho-xylene, meta-xylene and ethylbenzene;(d) isomerizing the raffinate in an isomerization unit, producing an isomerate enriched in para-xylene;(e) passing the isomerate enriched in para-xylene to the fractionation train, treating a second hydrocarbon-based feedstock (30) in a pyrolysis unit (13) to produce at least one pyrolysis effluent (31) comprising hydrocarbon-based compound containing from 6-10C feeding, at least partially, the first hydrocarbon-based feedstock (2) and to produce a pyrolysis gas (32) comprising at least carbon monoxide, CO2 and hydrogen;(f) treating the pyrolysis gas in a WGS water gas shift reaction section to produce a WGS gas enriched in CO2 and in hydrogen;(g)processing partly at least the WGS gas (51) enriched in CO2 and in hydrogen in an aromatization reaction section (52) to produce a hydrocarbon effluent (53) comprising aromatic compound, and feeding the first hydrocarbon-based feedstock (2) with the hydrocarbon effluent (53).

PN CN114176203-A
TI Preparing storage-resistant braised lotus root comprises keeping braised lotus root product fresh with preparing preservative comprising e.g. glucose oxidase, glucose, chitosan, xanthan gum, vitamin B2, bergamot extract and grape seed extract, and adding lotus root in tea polyphenol solution.
AU OU S
   ZHAO P
AE HUNAN DONGTING MINGZHU FOOD CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202243127N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing storage-resistant braised lotus root comprises keeping braised lotus root product fresh with a combination of prefabricated preservative and fresh-keeping technique, which specifically comprises (i) preparing preservative comprising glucose oxidase, glucose, chitosan, Tween 80 (Polysorbate 80), xanthan gum, vitamin B2, bergamot extract, grape seed extract and deionized water (remaining amount), (ii) washing lotus root to be processed with clean water, removing precipitate and sundries, and spreading sliced lotus roots, (iii) pre-soaking lotus root after being air-dried in tea polyphenol solution I, adding lotus root in tea polyphenol solution II to soak, adding oxidized starch and polyethylene glycol, dispersing, (iv) washing lotus root soaked in tea polyphenols, frying and soaking in preservative and frying, and (v) adding brine material and water into brine pot, heating to boiling with open flame, adding brine, adding lotus root, and controlling marinating temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing storage-resistant braised lotus root.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: ensures taste of lotus root on the basis of guaranteeing taste of lotus root; and improves the fresh-keeping period.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing storage-resistant braised lotus root comprises keeping braised lotus root product fresh with a combination of prefabricated preservative and a fresh-keeping technique, which specifically comprises (i) preparing preservative comprising 0.01-0.03 wt.% glucose oxidase, 1-3 wt.% glucose, 2-3 wt.% chitosan, 0.05-0.1 wt.% Tween 80 (Polysorbate 80), 0.3-0.5 wt.% xanthan gum, 7-9 wt.% vitamin B2, 3-5 wt.% bergamot extract, 1-3 wt.% grape seed extract and deionized water (remaining amount), (ii) washing lotus root to be processed with clean water, removing precipitate and sundries on surface and inside, draining and slicing, uniformly spreading sliced lotus roots on metal mesh surface, and using air dryer to air dry lotus roots to 85-90% of quality of original lotus roots, (iii) pre-soaking lotus root after being air-dried in tea polyphenol solution I for 3-5 minutes, then adding in tea polyphenol solution II to soak for 10-15 minutes, where tea polyphenol solution I is a tea polyphenol solution whose mass fraction is 0.3-0.5 wt.%, and the tea polyphenol solution II is a tea polyphenol mixture solution with a mass fraction of 0.3-0.5% tea polyphenol solution, adding 70-80 g/kg oxidized starch and 3.5-6 mg/kg polyethylene glycol, and ultrasonically and uniformly dispersing, (iv) washing lotus root soaked in tea polyphenols with clean water to remove surface adhesions, frying for 10-15 seconds, cooling to room temperature, and soaking in a preservative for 5-10 minutes, where preservative soaking process is a low-temperature pressure-regulating soaking in an airtight container, and the temperature is controlled at 0-3degrees Celsius, pouring carbon dioxide gas into airtight container, controlling carbon dioxide pressure to maintain between 120 kPa and 140 kPa, and frying again for 15-30 seconds after soaking, and (v) adding brine material and water into brine pot according to proportion of brine, heating to boiling with open flame, turning off flame and adding brine into the interlayer marinating boiler, adding lotus root processed in the step (iv) together, and controlling marinating temperature in marinating boiler to 75-85degrees Celsius and marinating time is 30-45 minutes to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the slice thickness when sliced lotus root is 8-12 mm.Preferred Method: In the step (ii), when ultrasonic dispersion is performed to prepare tea polyphenol solution II, controlling the ultrasonic frequency to be 35-45 kHz, the power is 200-300 W, and allowing to stand for not less than 2 hours after ultrasonic dispersion treatment. In the step (iv), using refined sunflower oil for frying, and the oil temperature during frying is 150-180degrees Celsius. In the step (v), heating the picked out braised lotus root and adding to stirring device, and adding seasoning to stir to obtain seasoned braised lotus root with richer taste.

PN CN112875737-A
TI Manufacturing high-purity medicinal calcium carbonate useful as e.g. raw material of medicine comprises e.g. mixing high-purity fine natural calcium carbonate powder and precipitated calcium carbonate, precipitating and removing impurities.
AU HU W
AE ELECTRICAL APPLIANCE CO LTD (ELAP-Non-standard)
GA 2021642657
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing high-purity medicinal calcium carbonate comprises taking high-purity fine natural calcium carbonate powder (FGCC) and high-purity precipitated calcium carbonate (PCC) as raw materials, uniformly mixing in a certain ratio to obtain high-purity calcium carbonate, crushing and classifying, preparing high-purity precipitated calcium carbonate is a product prepared by a chemical synthesis method or a carbonization method, using chemical synthesis method, removing key process impurities in the raw materials, removing impurities when the carbonization method is used and method of removing impurities is to select a purer calcium oxide raw material, adding water to digest it into calcium hydroxide, precipitating and removing the impurities with a hydrocyclone, passing carbon dioxide gas to produce high-purity precipitated calcium carbonate product after drying in 2 hours using flash equipment.
   USE - The calcium carbonate is useful raw material of medicine, food and cosmetic.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures adjustable granularity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing high-purity medicinal calcium carbonate comprises taking high-purity fine natural calcium carbonate powder (FGCC) and high-purity precipitated calcium carbonate (PCC) as raw materials, uniformly mixing in a certain ratio to obtain high-purity calcium carbonate, where weight percentage of the arsenic salt contained in the medicinal calcium carbonate is less than or equal to 0.0002%, and the weight percentage of the mercury contained is less than or equal to 0.00003%, the weight percentage of manganese contained is less than or equal to 0.01%, the fine natural calcium carbonate powder FGCC is prepared by a dry crushing and classification process, and the raw material is food-grade natural calcium carbonate GCC, with a fineness of 400-600 mesh, and crushing and classifying to obtain natural calcium carbonate fine powder with different fineness levels of 400-1500 mesh, preparing high-purity precipitated calcium carbonate PCC is a product prepared by a chemical synthesis method or a carbonization method, using chemical synthesis method, removing key process impurities in the raw materials, where impurities include iron, iron, lead, lead, arsenic, arsenic, and magnesium, magnesium, mercury, Hg, these elements form a precipitate under the condition of pH 8-14, the resulting precipitate is filtered and removed by activated carbon for many times, and the raw material after removing impurities is subjected to a synthesis reaction to obtain a high-purity precipitated calcium carbonate product, removing impurities when the carbonization method is used and method of removing impurities is to select a purer calcium oxide raw material, adding water to digest it into calcium hydroxide, precipitating and removing the impurities with a hydrocyclone, passing carbon dioxide gas to produce high-purity precipitated calcium carbonate product to obtain the final PCC intermediate product after drying in 2 hours using flash equipment, where FGCC and PCC are mixed in a certain ratio, and the mixing weight ratio is FGCC:PCC=X:Y, where x = 20-100, and y = 0-50, precipitating processing of the food-grade natural calcium carbonate GCC, fine natural calcium carbonate powder FGCC and precipitated calcium carbonate PCC in a 300,000-level clean area that meets the requirements of the GMP National Food and Drug Administration's drug production quality management specifications. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for high-purity medicinal calcium carbonate prepared as mentioned above, where average particle size of the medicinal calcium carbonate is 2.0-35.0 microns and the bulk density is 0.4-1.7 g/ml.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process for producing precipitated calcium carbonate by chemical synthesis method comprises selecting calcium ion-containing compound is calcium oxide, calcium chloride, calcium nitrate or calcium hydroxide, removing impurity, selecting carbonate ion-containing compound after impurity removal from sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, ammonium carbonate or ammonium bicarbonate and the reaction pot, stirring is carried out for 15-50 minutes at a reaction temperature of 20-90 degrees C to obtain PCC precipitation, washing generated precipitated calcium carbonate, centrifuging, dehydrating, drying, sieving, and mixing. The process for producing PCC of precipitated calcium carbonate by the carbonization method comprises passing the carbon dioxide gas produced by the lime kiln or the carbon dioxide source into the calcium hydroxide solution after impurity removal, where calcium hydroxide solution has a pH of 10-14 and is in the air and carbon dioxide under stirring at 15-70 degrees C, completing carbonization reaction in the carbonization tower to form calcium carbonate precipitated PCC, washing generated precipitated calcium carbonate, centrifuging, dehydrating, drying, sieving, and mixing to obtain a high-purity PCC product. Preferred Components: The weight loss on drying of medicinal calcium carbonate is less than or equal to 0.5%, the weight percentage of copper contained is less than or equal to 0.001%, the weight percentage of fluoride contained is less than or equal to 0.003%, the weight percentage of sulfate contained is less than or equal to 0.2%, the weight percentage of phosphate less than or equal to 0.02% and content of calcium carbonate in the fine natural calcium carbonate powder FGCC is above 99%.The fineness of the fine natural calcium carbonate powder FGCC is 400-1500 mesh. The calcium carbonate content of the precipitated calcium carbonate PCC is above 99.0%. The fineness of the precipitated calcium carbonate PCC is 200-400 mesh.

TI Electrode of chemical cell used as photocathode            for photoelectrochemical cell for fabricating electrode            of electrochemical system for catalytic conversion of            carbon dioxide, comprises substrate, conductive            projections, and nanoparticles.
AU ZHOU B
   KONG X
   GUO H
   MI Z
AE UNIV MICHIGAN (UNMI-C)
   UNIV MICHIGAN (UNMI-C)
   UNIV MICHIGAN (UNMI-C)
GA 202111426T
AB    NOVELTY - Electrode of chemical cell (102) comprises                substrate have surface; array of conductive                projections supported by substrate (120) and                extending outward from surface of substrate, each                conductive projection of array of conductive                projections have semiconductor composition for                catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide (CO2) in                chemical cell; and multiple nanoparticles (124) are                set over array of conductive projections, where                each nanoparticle of multiple nanoparticles have                metallic composition for catalytic conversion of                CO2 in chemical cell; each nanoparticle of multiple                nanoparticles has size at least order of magnitude                smaller than lateral dimension of each conductive                projection of array of conductive                projections.
   USE - Electrode of chemical cell used as                photocathode for photoelectrochemical cell for                fabricating electrode of electrochemical system                (all claimed), and used for catalytic conversion of                CO2.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode of chemical cell helps in                improving optical and electronic properties between                planar silicon and tin (Sn)-based cocatalysts due                to unique geometry of nanowires and strong charge                carrier extraction effect; achieves superior                performance; achieves suitable balance of                sufficient catalytic sites, effective sunlight                harvesting, and effectively sized Sn nanoparticles                with high activity; increases current density;                achieves low-cost, high-efficiency, and robust                artificial photosynthesis for production for solar                fuels and high- value chemicals from CO2                conversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a electrochemical system, which comprises                working electrode configured in accordance with                electrode, and further comprises counter electrode;                electrolyte in which working, and counter                electrodes are immersed; and voltage source, which                applies bias voltage between working, and counter                electrodes, where bias voltage is set to level for                conversion of CO2 into formic acid at working                electrode;
   (2) a photocathode for photoelectrochemical                cell, which comprises substrate with light                absorbing material, which is configured to generate                charge carriers upon solar illumination; array of                nanowires supported by substrate, where each                nanowire of array of nanowires being configured to                extract charge carriers from substrate, each                nanowire of array of nanowires, which comprises                gallium nitride; and mutliple nanoparticles                distributed across each nanowire of array of                nanowires, each nanoparticle of multiple                nanoparticles, which have metallic composition for                catalytic conversion of CO2 in photoelectrochemical                cell into formic acid, where each nanoparticle of                multiple nanoparticles, which has size at least                order of magnitude smaller than lateral dimension                of each nanowire of array of nanowires;
   (3) a photoelectrochemical system, which                comprises working photocathode configured in                accordance with photocathode, and further comprises                counter electrode; electrolyte in which working                photocathode, and counter electrode are immersed;                and voltage source, which applies bias voltage                between working photocathode, and counter                electrode, where bias voltage, which is set to                level for conversion of CO2 into formic acid at                working photocathode; and
   (4) a method for fabricating electrode of                electrochemical system, which involves growing                array of nanowires on semiconductor substrate, each                nanowire of array of nanowires have semiconductor                composition for catalytic conversion of CO2 in                electrochemical system; and depositing multiple                nanoparticles across each nanowire of array of                nanowires, each nanoparticle of multiple                nanoparticles have metallic composition for                catalytic conversion of Co2 in electrochemical                system, where depositing multiple nanoparticles,                which comprises implementing number of                electrodeposition cycles, where number of                electrodeposition cycles being set to ensure each                nanoparticle of multiple nanoparticles, which has                size at least order of magnitude smaller than                lateral dimension of each nanowire of array of                nanowires.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view and block                diagram of electrochemical system.
   Chemical cell (102)
   Light (118)
   Substrate (120)
   Nanowire (122)
   Nanoparticles (124)

PT P
PN CN111253884-A; CN111253884-B
TI Use method of micron calcium carbonate in sealant            comprises e.g. using calcium oxide, distilled water,            modifier, hydroxy silicone oil, methyltrimethoxysilane,            catalyst, sieving calcium oxide free of impurities,            taking distilled water.
AU LI L
AE GUANGDONG XIANGLONG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GDMY-C)
GA 2020536570
AB    NOVELTY - Use method of micron calcium carbonate in                sealant comprises using 15-20 pts. wt. calcium                oxide, 18-25 pts. wt. distilled water, 3-5 pts. wt.                modifier, 8-13 pts. wt. hydroxy silicone oil, 30-50                pts. wt. methyltrimethoxysilane, 2-3 pts. wt.                catalyst, sieving calcium oxide free of impurities,                taking 7/9 of distilled water, adding distilled                water with a temperature of 65-68 degrees C in the                temperature-increasing water bath, adding calcium                oxide, mixing at a speed of 300 rotations per                minute through a high-speed mixer for 35-40                minutes, stirring material and processing to obtain                a slurry, adding 2/9 distilled water to the slurry,                and the temperature of the distilled water is 13-14                degrees C, performing the mixing work at a speed of                360-370 ml/minutes, spraying carbon dioxide at the                speed of the rotation to obtain calcium carbonate                after carbonization.
   USE - The micron calcium carbonate is useful in                sealant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method : increases tensile strength by                1.53 MPa, increases the control of its particle                size, and elongation at break by 120.7 wt.%;                increases the tear strength by 15 kN/m; improves                strength and improves resistance to breakage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use method of micron calcium carbonate in                sealant comprises using 15-20 pts. wt. calcium                oxide, 18-25 pts. wt. distilled water, 3-5 pts. wt.                modifier, 8-13 pts. wt. hydroxy silicone oil, 30-50                pts. wt. methyltrimethoxysilane, 2-3 pts. wt.                catalyst, (1) sieving calcium oxide free of                impurities, taking 7/9 of distilled water, adding                distilled water with a temperature of 65-68 degrees                C in the temperature-increasing water bath, adding                calcium oxide, mixing at a speed of 300 rotations                per minute through a high-speed mixer for 35-40                minutes, stirring material and processing to obtain                a slurry, (2) adding 2/9 distilled water to the                slurry obtained in step (1), and the temperature of                the distilled water is 13-14 degrees C, performing                the mixing work at a speed of 360-370 ml/minutes,                spraying carbon dioxide at the speed of the                rotation to obtain calcium carbonate after                carbonization, (3) warming the calcium carbonate                obtained in step (2) to 71-72 degrees C, adding                modifier, and performing stirring treatment at a                stirring speed of 520-550 rotations/minute, (4)                grinding the calcium carbonate processed in step                (3) through a three-roll grinder, collecting after                grinding, drawing through vacuum pump and 30 mesh                screen, and sending to the dryer to dry it, after                drying, subjecting to high-speed grinding treatment                by a high-speed ball mill, so that the particle                diameter is less than 60 nm and the surface area                reaches 20-25 m2/g, and obtaining modified                nano-activated calcium carbonate ultrafine powder,                (5) treating nano-active calcium carbonate                ultrafine powder obtained in step (4) with stearic                acid and drying at 115-118 degrees C, taking                hydroxysilicone oil with a viscosity of 20000 CP                and the treated nano-active calcium carbonate for                high-speed stirring treatment, stirring at 30-35                rotations/minute for 125-130 minutes, (6)                processing calcium carbonate mixture, grinding by a                three-roll mill, and after grinding, adding                methyltrimethoxysilane and a viscous catalyst and                stirring, (7) vacuuming and stirring at the speed                of 27-30 rotations/minute after the treatment, and                realizing the stirring to 70-80 minutes, completing                the stirring, packing into PE packaging                tubes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The modifier is an                  aluminate coupling agent. The catalyst is a                  palladium catalyst or a titanium catalyst.                  Preferred Method: In the step (4), drying                  treatment of calcium carbonate, activating nano                  calcium carbonate after the moisture content                  after drying is less than 0.2% , if the moisture                  content is higher than 0.2%, repeating the drying                  treatment through a constant temperature drying                  box. In the step (6), performing the stirring                  treatment at a speed of 25-30 rotations/minute                  for 30-40 minutes. In the step (2), the injection                  speed of carbon dioxide is 110-120 l/minutes. In                  the step (1), keeping the temperature of the                  stirring chamber at 70 degrees C during                  high-speed stirring. In the step (5) adding,                  colorants or fragrances in stirring                  treatment.

PN CN111233015-A
TI Recycling deactivated iron oxide/nickel oxide/aluminum oxide catalyst involves recovering aluminum oxide by preparing sodium aluminum oxide and washed inactivated iron oxide/nickel oxide/aluminum oxide catalyst.
AU DI W
   SI Y
AE LIAONING PETROCHEMICAL COLLEGE (LNPC-C)
GA 202051906A
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling deactivated iron oxide/nickel oxide/aluminum oxide (Fe2O3/Ni2O3/Al2O3) catalyst involves recovering aluminum oxide (Al2O3) by preparing sodium aluminum oxide (NaAlO2) and 100 g of washed inactivated Fe2O3/Ni2O3/Al2O3 catalyst, and adding sodium hydroxide solution with a mass concentration of 15 wt.%, and the molar ratio of alumina in the deactivated Fe2O3/Ni2O3/Al2O3 catalyst to sodium hydroxide is 1:2.02-1:2.1. The solution is stirred and heated to boiled, kept warm for 30-35 minutes, filtered, and the filtrate is NaAlO2 solution, and the precipitate is nickel oxide and iron oxide. The obtained filtrate NaAlO2 solution is placed in a container, passed carbon dioxide (CO2) into the container until white precipitation no longer occurs, filtered, and retain the precipitate. The precipitate is burned at 950-1000 degrees C for 60-70 minutes to obtain alumina. The obtained precipitated nickel oxide and iron oxide are placed in a container, added a sulfuric acid solution.
   USE - Method for recycling deactivated Fe2O3/Ni2O3/Al2O3 catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to recycled deactivated Fe2O3/Ni2O3/Al2O3 catalyst in a simple way and easy to operate, and recovery rate of iron (Fe), nickel (Ni) and aluminum (Al) in the deactivated catalyst is high, and the three metal elements Fe, Ni and Al are completely separated, and all of them are recycled, no pollutants are generated, and they are safe and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling deactivated iron oxide/nickel oxide/aluminum oxide (Fe2O3/Ni2O3/Al2O3) catalyst involves recovering aluminum oxide (Al2O3) by preparing sodium aluminum oxide (NaAlO2) and 100 g of washed inactivated Fe2O3/Ni2O3/Al2O3 catalyst, and adding sodium hydroxide solution with a mass concentration of 15 wt.%, and the molar ratio of alumina in the deactivated Fe2O3/Ni2O3/Al2O3 catalyst to sodium hydroxide is 1:2.02-1:2.1. The solution is stirred and heated to boiled, kept warm for 30-35 minutes, filtered, and the filtrate is NaAlO2 solution, and the precipitate is nickel oxide and iron oxide. The obtained filtrate NaAlO2 solution is placed in a container, passed carbon dioxide (CO2) into the container until white precipitation no longer occurs, filtered, and retain the precipitate. The precipitate is burned at 950-1000 degrees C for 60-70 minutes to obtain alumina. The obtained precipitated nickel oxide and iron oxide are placed in a container, added a sulfuric acid solution with a mass concentration of 20 wt.%, and nickel oxide in the deactivated Fe2O3/Ni2O3/Al2O3 catalyst 1.01 times to 1.05 times the measurement relationship between iron oxide and sulfuric acid. The solution is stirred and heated to boiling, kept warm for 40-50 minutes, filtered, kept the filtrate, and the filtrate is iron sulfate (Fe2(SO4)3) and nickel sulfate (NiSO4). The Fe2(SO4)3 is separate, and then transferred Fe2(SO4)3 and NiSO4 into a container, stirred and heated to 85-95 degrees C. The pH of the solution is adjusted to 1.5-2.5 with 10 wt.% ammonia water, kept warm for 30-40 minutes, filtered and separated, and the filtrate is NiSO4 solution. The NiSO4 solution is placed in a container, heated the NiSO4 solution to boiling to evaporate the solvent, and stopped heating when a crystal film appears on the liquid surface. The solution is stirred to disperse the crystal film into the solution as a crystal nucleus, and cooled the solution to room temperature, and nickel sulfate crystallizes out of the solution, and after filtration, nickel sulfate crystals are obtained.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of alumina to sodium hydroxide in the deactivated Fe2O3/Ni2O3/Al2O3 catalyst is 1:2.02. The stoichiometric relationship between the reaction of nickel oxide and iron oxide with sulfuric acid is 1.01 times.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN110898215-A
TI Preparing anti-tumor vaccinating agent used in preparation of antitumor drugs, by mixing immunomodulator with RIPM1640 medium, resuspending microvesicles with sterile physiological saline, incubating and centrifuging liposomes.
AU ZHAO H
   WANG L
   ZHANG Z
   ZHAO B
   WU L
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 202026259Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing anti-tumor vaccinating agent based on cell microvesicles, involves culturing tumor cells in RIPM1640 medium containing fetal bovine serum, mixing immunomodulator with RIPM1640 medium containing fetal bovine serum, adding the medium to adherent tumor cells, irradiating the tumor cells with UV light, incubating tumor cells, collecting the supernatant of medium, resuspending tumor cell-derived microvesicles with 100-500 mu l sterile physiological saline, dissolving 2-3 mg (2,3-dioleoyl-propyl)-trimethylamine (DOTAP), 6-7 mg dioleoylphosphatidylethanolamine (DOPE) and 1-2 mg distearoylphosphatidylethanolamine-polyethylene glycol 2000-maleimide (DSPE-PEG2000-Mal) in 15 ml chloroform, performing sonication of the solution for 15 minutes, adding 1-2 ml phosphate buffered saline solution, incubating at 37 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain liposome membrane, incubating with liposomes loaded with adjuvant, and centrifuging the liposomes.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing anti-tumor vaccinating agent which is used in preparation of antitumor drugs. The tumor is ovarian cancer, breast cancer, lung cancer, gastric cancer, colon cancer, liver cancer or melanin tumor, preferably melanoma (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to perform, improves tumor microenvironment and immune response of organism. The agent has high yield, good universality and improves bioavailability of adjuvant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing anti-tumor vaccinating agent based on cell microvesicles, involves culturing tumor cells in RIPM1640 medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum at 37 degrees C, saturated humidity and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, where the tumor cells are chosen from ovarian cancer, breast cancer, lung cancer, gastric cancer, colon cancer, liver cancer or melanoma cells, mixing immunomodulator with RIPM1640 medium containing fetal bovine serum, adding the medium to adherent tumor cells, irradiating the tumor cells with UV light for 60 minutes to stimulate apoptosis, where the immunomodulator is used for treating ovarian cancer, breast cancer, lung cancer, gastric cancer, colon cancer, liver cancer or melanoma, incubating tumor cells in 37 degrees C, saturated humidity and 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 12-24 hours, collecting the supernatant of medium after the treatment in the collection, collecting the immunomodulator by differential centrifugation of tumor microvesicles, resuspending 1x 106-1x 107 tumor cell-derived microvesicles with 100-500 mu l sterile physiological saline, storing the product at -80 degrees C, dissolving 2-3 mg (2,3-dioleoyl-propyl)-trimethylamine (DOTAP), 6-7 mg dioleoylphosphatidylethanolamine (DOPE) and 1-2 mg distearoylphosphatidylethanolamine-polyethylene glycol 2000-maleimide (DSPE-PEG2000-Mal) in 15 ml chloroform, performing sonication of the solution for 15 minutes, adding 1-2 ml phosphate buffered saline solution containing 2-4 OD adjuvant at pH 7.4 after spin-evaporating until there is no chloroform, incubating at 37 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain liposome membrane, where the adjuvant is aluminum salt, protein, nucleic acid adjuvant, lipid-containing and mixed adjuvant, using carbon power of 200 W for 15 times, each time for 3 seconds after collecting the liposome membrane to obtain liposome loaded with adjuvant, incubating 50 mu g tumor microvesicles, in which 250 mu g of immunomodulator is loaded, incubating with liposomes loaded with 0.3 mu g adjuvant at 4 degrees C for 12 hours, and centrifuging the liposomes at 4 degrees C and 14000 g for 60 minutes to collect precipitate to obtain antitumor vaccine combined with microvesicles and liposomes. The tumor cell microvesicles loaded with immunomodulators are connected stably with liposomes loaded with adjuvants on surface of microvesicles to form antitumor vaccines. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the antitumor vaccine based on cell microvesicles prepared by the method, which is in preparation of antitumor drugs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves culturing murine-derived B16F10 melanoma cells in RIPM1640 medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum in incubator, mixing immunomodulator nanoferric tetroxide with RIPM1640 medium containing fetal bovine serum, adherently growing murine B16F10 melanoma cells, performing UV irradiation of murine B16F10 melanoma cells for 60 minutes, incubating murine B16F10 melanoma cells for 16 hours, collecting the supernatant of the medium after the treatment in the collection step, collecting the immune load by differential centrifugation tumor microvesicles of the regulator nano-iron tetroxide, resuspending 5x 106 tumor cell-derived microvesicles with 100 mu l sterile saline, storing at -80 degrees C, dissolving 2.2 mg DOTAP, 6.6 mg DOPE and 1.2 mg DSPE-PEG2000-Mal in 15 ml chloroform, performing sonication for 15 minutes, after spin-evaporating until chloroform is completely absent, adding 1 ml phosphate buffered saline with pH 7.4 containing 2OD CpG-ODN adjuvant, incubating at 37 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain liposome membrane, using carbon power of 200 W for 15 times, each time for 3 seconds after collecting the liposome membrane to obtain liposomes loaded with adjuvant CpG-ODN, preparing microvesicle capture liposomes to prepare anti-tumor vaccines, and centrifuging the vaccine at 4 degrees C, and centrifuging at 14000 g for 60 minutes to collect the precipitate.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostactic.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

PT P
PN CN110558446-A
TI Natural plant extract composition used for poultry feed additive, comprises Selaginella moellendorffii ethanol extract, Chinese actinidia root aqueous extract, Allium macrostemon volatile oil and Platycladus orientalis volatile oil.
AU GONG Y
   SUN R
   WANG H
   JIN H
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 2019A8649Y
AB    NOVELTY - Natural plant extract composition comprises Selaginella moellendorffii ethanol extract, Chinese actinidia root aqueous extract, Allium macrostemon oriental arborvitae aqueous extract, A.macrostemon volatile oil and Platycladus orientalis volatile oil, and is prepared by adopting a conventional wet granulation process to prepare particles. The S.moellendorffii ethanol extract is prepared by taking S.moellendorffii, drying, crushing, sieving, placing in a round bottom flask, adding ethanol, heating to reflux in an electric heating sleeve, combining the filtrates, recovering ethanol, concentrating, decompressing and drying to obtain dry extract, crushing and sieving obtain the extract. The A.macrostemon volatile oil is prepared taking A.macrostemon, drying, crushing, sieving, placing in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for extraction using 6 %mass 70% ethanol, extracting and collecting volatile oil.
   USE - The natural plant extract composition is useful for poultry feed additive (claimed) for inhibiting bacterial infection, regulating gastrointestinal function, increasing appetite, and promoting growth of bird.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Natural plant extract composition comprises Selaginella moellendorffii ethanol extract, Chinese actinidia root aqueous extract, Allium macrostemon oriental arborvitae aqueous extract, A.macrostemon volatile oil and Platycladus orientalis volatile oil, and is prepared by adopting a conventional wet granulation process to prepare particles. The S.moellendorffii ethanol extract is prepared by taking S.moellendorffii, drying, crushing, sieving, placing in a round bottom flask, adding 8-10 times 75% ethanol, heating to reflux in an electric heating sleeve for 3 times, combining the filtrates, recovering ethanol, concentrating, decompressing and drying to obtain dry extract, crushing and sieving through an 80 mesh sieve to obtain the extract. The Chinese actinidia root aqueous extract is prepared by taking Chinese actinidia root, drying, crushing, sieving, adding 12 times 50 degrees C distilled water for temperature soaking overnight, adding 0.1% cellulase, adjusting the pH to 5 with dilute hydrochloric acid, maintaining at 50 degrees C for 2 hours, putting the mixture in an electric furnace for boiling for 2.5 hours, filtering, heating to concentrate, drying, crushing and sieving through 80 mesh sieve to obtain the Chinese actinidia root aqueous extract. The A.macrostemon oriental arborvitae aqueous extract is prepared by taking A.macrostemon and oriental arborvitae at a mixing ratio of 3:2, adding 12 times, 10 times and 8 times distilled water to electric furnace, respectively decocting for 3 hours, 2 hours and 1 hour, filtering, combining the filtrates, heating to concentrate, decompressing and drying to obtain dry extract, crushing and sieving through 80 mesh sieve. The A.macrostemon volatile oil is prepared taking A.macrostemon, drying, crushing, sieving, placing in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for extraction using 6 %mass 70% ethanol, extracting at a pressure of 20 MPa at 45 degrees C for 2 hours and collecting volatile oil. The P.orientalis volatile oil is prepared by taking P.orientalis, drying, crushing, sieving, placing in round bottom flask, adding 2% complex enzyme, adding 10 times distilled water, adjusting the pH to 4.5 with hydrochloric acid, placing in water bath kettle for enzymatically hydrolyzing for 4.5 hours, transferring into a microwave extractor, setting microwave power to 800 W, and collecting volatile oil, where the complex enzyme comprises cellulase, pectinase and papain at a ratio of 1:1:1. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a use of the natural plant extract composition involving adding 0.05-0.1 wt.% natural plant extract composition to poultry feed additive in poultry feed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The plant extract composition comprises S.moellendorffii ethanol extract, Chinese actinidia root aqueous extract, A.macrostemon oriental arborvitae aqueous extract, A.macrostemon volatile oil and P.orientalis volatile oil in weight ratio of 7:6:7:2:2.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Gastrointestinal-Gen.; Anabolic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
