PT P
PN CN110563258-A
TI Anaerobic hydrogen production and standard discharge treatment of pig farm wastewater involves precipitating wastewater, adjusting pH value, adding dominant hydrogen producing strains, and feeding obtained hydrogen to absorption tower.
AU ZHANG D
   NIU X
   MA Y
   WEI M
   DU C
   ZHANG Z
   HUANG Z
   LIN Y
   LV Y
   ZHANG H
   CHEN F
   LIANG X
AE UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEMICAL TECHNOLOGY (UNGP-C)
GA 2019A8661B
AB    NOVELTY - Anaerobic hydrogen production and standard discharge treatment of pig farm wastewater involves passing pig farm wastewater into sedimentation tank for pretreatment, precipitating, discharging the obtained sediment and recovering, and feeding the obtained supernatant after precipitation to the adjustment tank, adjusting the pH value of the supernatant and feeding to the anaerobic hydrogen production reactor and performing heat pretreatment, using sterilized pig farm wastewater as a culture medium, separating anaerobic activated sludge in the reactor, culturing dominant hydrogen producing strains in sterilized pig farm wastewater comprising 0.5% glycerol, adding the obtained culture to the anaerobic hydrogen production reactor, feeding the hydrogen and carbon dioxide gas produced by the anaerobic hydrogen production reactor to the absorption tower, and feeding the discharged fermentation liquid from the anaerobic hydrogen production reactor to the aerobic biochemical tank.
   USE - Anaerobic hydrogen production and standard discharge treatment of pig farm wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of improving anaerobic hydrogen production efficiency by more than 40% and hydrogen purity by more than 90%, reducing carbon emissions by more than 80%, and saving aerobic treatment costs by more than 30%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Anaerobic hydrogen production and standard discharge treatment of pig farm wastewater involves (i) passing pig farm wastewater through the grille and passing into sedimentation tank for pretreatment, precipitating, discharging the obtained sediment and recovering through the bottom of the sedimentation tank, and feeding the obtained supernatant after precipitation to the adjustment tank, (ii) adjusting the pH value of the supernatant in the adjustment tank to 4.5-5.5 and feeding to the anaerobic hydrogen production reactor which is filled with the remaining activated sludge having a volatile suspended solids of 6-8 g/L from the sewage plant, performing heat pretreatment at 70 degrees C for 0.5 hours before inoculation and stirring for 20 minutes every 1 hour, (iii) using sterilized pig farm wastewater as a culture medium, separating anaerobic activated sludge in the anaerobic hydrogen production reactor from the dominant hydrogen producing bacteria under anaerobic conditions, enriching and culturing dominant hydrogen producing strains in sterilized pig farm wastewater comprising 0.5% glycerol at pH 5.0 under anaerobic conditions, adding the obtained culture to the anaerobic hydrogen production reactor with the anaerobic sludge, mixing with anaerobic sludge, stirring for every 1 hour and leaving still for 48 hours, draining the supernatant, (iv) feeding the hydrogen and carbon dioxide gas produced by the anaerobic hydrogen production reactor to the absorption tower from the top, absorbing by the alkaline absorbing solution and passing the purified hydrogen to the gas collecting bag, (v) feeding the discharged fermentation liquid from the anaerobic hydrogen production reactor to the aerobic biochemical tank, passing the effluent from the bottom of the absorption tower to the aerobic biochemical tank, and passing the obtained effluent to the mixed sedimentation tank, reusing the water in the mixed sedimentation tank or extracting water after adding phosphorus removal agent to remove dissolved phosphorus in the water, returning a portion of the sedimentation sludge at the bottom of the mixed sedimentation tank to the aerobic biochemical tank and the anaerobic hydrogen production reactor, and discharging a portion of the sedimentation sludge. The pretreatment step is carried out by adding magnesium salt and phosphorus salt in a molar ratio of 1.0-1.3:1 to wastewater, stirring for 10-15 minutes, and adjusting the speed to two-thirds of the original speed. The magnesium salt is magnesium chloride, magnesium sulfate or magnesium oxide. The phosphorus salt is monosodium dihydrogen orthophosphate, sodium hydrogen phosphate, trisodium phosphate, potassium dihydrogen phosphate, dipotassium phosphate or phosphoric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Process: In step (ii), the pH of the supernatant is adjusted using industrial sulfuric acid or hydrochloric acid. In step (iv), the pH of the alkaline absorption liquid is 8.5 or more. In step (iv), the mixed liquor suspended solids of activated sludge in the aerobic biochemical tank is 3000-7000 mg/L, and the hydraulic retention time is 4-6 hours. The hydraulic retention time of the precipitation tank in step (v) is 2-3 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The phosphorus removing agent used in step (v) is a saturated solution of aluminum sulfate, aluminum trichloride, or ferric chloride.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC D04-A01B; D04-A01F3; D04-A01J; D04-A01P1; D04-A02; D04-B07B; D04-B07C; D05-A04A; D05-C; E31-A02C

PT P
PN CN110372106-A; CN110372106-B
TI Comprehensively treating mixed sewage by hanging            biological slow-release bag at sewage outlet, placing            linoleum to artificially plant ecological base,            extracting sludge, adding microbial inoculum and            introducing zooplankton in river.
AU ZHOU C
   ZHENG H
   LI Z
AE ZHOU C (ZHOU-Individual)
   YUTAO ENG TECHNOLOGY XIAMEN CO LTD (YUTA-Non-standard)
GA 201992227E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for comprehensively treating mixed                sewage involves hanging a biological slow-release                bag at the sewage outlet, placing linoleum at the                sewage outlet of domestic sewage to artificially                plant ecological base to form a bio-array to                supplement microorganisms, soaking the ecological                base with microbial solution to provide a carrier                for microorganisms, pre-treating the river                pollution from the source, extracting the sludge at                the bottom of the polluted river section using the                sludge cleaning device, where the sludge cleaning                device sinks the device to the bottom of the river                through heavy blocks, pumping river bottom sludge                to the ground with water pump, adding microbial                inoculum into the river, decomposing sludge into                nitrogen, carbon dioxide, water, establishing an                ecological floating island for the production,                reproduction and habitat of plants and                microorganisms in the periphery of the river and                introducing zooplankton, fish animals and benthic                animals in the river.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensively                treating mixed sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method cleans deep sludge in the river                channel, hence improves the sludge treatment                efficiency and ensures that the sludge becomes                loose in the pipeline, and the pipeline is not be                easily blocked, hence improves stability of the                sludge cleaning device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for comprehensively treating mixed                sewage involves (a) hanging a biological                slow-release bag at the sewage outlet, placing                linoleum at the sewage outlet of domestic sewage to                artificially plant ecological base to form a                bio-array to supplement microorganisms, soaking the                ecological base with microbial solution to provide                a carrier for microorganisms, so that the microbial                flora in the tributaries can be bio-enriched on the                ecological base surface soaked with the microbial                solution to form a biofilm, extracting the                pollutants in the sewage through the life                activities of microorganisms, which absorbs,                assimilates, decomposes and removes the pollution                impurities in the water and pre-treating the river                pollution from the source, (b) extracting the                sludge at the bottom of the polluted river section                using the sludge cleaning device, where the sludge                cleaning device sinks the device to the bottom of                the river through heavy blocks and pumping river                bottom sludge to the ground with water pump, (c)                adding microbial inoculum into the river,                decomposing sludge into nitrogen, carbon dioxide,                water, and then degrading and digesting the sludge                and (d) establishing an ecological floating island                for the production, reproduction and habitat of                plants and microorganisms in the periphery of the                river, introducing zooplankton, fish animals and                benthic animals in the river and establishing                artificial landscape wetlands near rivers to                strengthen ecological environment protection. The                sludge cleaning device has box structure and blocks                symmetrically arranged on both sides of the top of                the box structure. Gas tanks are symmetrically                arranged on both sides of the outer wall of the                box. An air pump is provided on the outer wall of                the upper end of the box. The air pump is connected                with the air storage tank through an air flow                channel. A sludge discharge pipe is arranged at the                center of the box. A spiral pipe is arranged at the                bottom end of the sludge discharge pipe. A sludge                suction pipe is set at the lower right corner of                the box. The suction pipe runs through the box. One                end of the suction pipe in the box is connected                with the bottom of the spiral pipe. The box is                provided with a propeller on the outer wall of the                side close to the sludge discharge pipe. The                propeller is located between two gas storage                tanks.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: An aeration bladder and                  aeration tube are arranged at the bottom of the                  box. The side of the aeration bladder near the                  suction pipe is connected with the aeration pipe.                  The other end of the aeration pipe is connected                  with river water. A metal block is provided on                  the outer wall of the spiral pipe. The metal                  block is fixedly connected to the outer wall of                  the spiral pipe through a spring. The vibration                  of the surface of the spiral pipe can drive the                  metal block and the aeration bladder to be                  squeezed. The movement trajectory of the sludge                  cleaning device is in a bow shape, and the river                  sludge is cleaned layer by layer through the bow                  shape trajectory. At least two UV sterilization                  lamps are arranged symmetrically on both sides of                  the spiral pipe and a heat-absorbing airbag is                  arranged below the ultraviolet sterilization                  lamp. The spiral tube is made of transparent soft                  material. The light emitted by the UV                  sterilization lamp sterilizes the pipe wall of                  the spiral pipe and the heat generated by the UV                  sterilization lamp is absorbed by the first heat                  absorption airbag. An arc claw is provided at the                  bottom end of the outer surface of the box and                  the arc claw is hinged to the box. The top of the                  claw is located below the nozzle of the aeration                  pipe. The head of the claw is fixedly connected                  to the box, and the bottom end of the claw is set                  to be tapered. The side of the first                  heat-absorbing airbag remote from the connected                  is provided with a second heat-absorbing airbag.                  The second heat-absorbing airbag is provided with                  a concentrated saline solution and a                  heat-shrinkable substance. An exhaust pipe is                  provided at the bottom of the second                  heat-absorbing airbag. The top of the exhaust                  pipe is connected with the second heat-absorbing                  airbag, and the bottom of the exhaust pipe is                  connected with river water.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); F08 (Flexible sheet materials - consisting of polymer-coated fibrous web, including end products not classified in other sections (D06N).)

PT P
PN CN109692148-A
TI Three-dimensional network graphene-halloysite airgel composite material comprises both nanoscale and microscale pores.
AU LI H
   SUN C
   LIU H
   WEI D
   WANG J
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201941804H
AB    NOVELTY - Three-dimensional network graphene-halloysite airgel composite material comprises both nanoscale and microscale pores, where the nanoscale pores are 10-12 nm on average, and the micrometer pores are 20-22 mu m on average, the preparation of composite material comprises e.g. adding halloysite nanotubes to mixture of deionized water and ethanol, ultrasonic dispersing uniformly to obtain halloysite dispersion, adding sodium styrene sulfonate, polydivinylsiloxane, initiator, and furosemide to mixture of deionized water and ethanol, stirring uniformly, adding obtained solution to the halloysite dispersion, ultrasonic dispersing, vacuuming, washing the product, dispersing in water, polymerizing, washing and dispersing into water to obtain dispersion.
   USE - Used as three-dimensional network graphene-halloysite airgel composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has 600-608 m2/g average specific surface area; realizes release of drug nifedipine loaded in the mesopores first as the soaking time increases, and as the soaking time was further increased, the furosemide drug loaded in the micropores was subsequently released, achieving the effect of one administration and multiple administrations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Three-dimensional network graphene-halloysite airgel composite material comprises both nanoscale and microscale pores, where the nanoscale pores are 10-12 nm on average, and the micrometer pores are 20-22 mu m on average, the preparation of composite material comprises (1) adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes to mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, ultrasonic dispersing uniformly to obtain halloysite dispersion, adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.05-2 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.5 pts. wt. initiator, and 0.05-10 pts. wt. furosemide to a mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, stirring uniformly, adding obtained solution to the halloysite dispersion, ultrasonic dispersing uniformly, vacuuming, maintaining the vacuum, then returning to normal pressure, and repeating vacuuming steps for three times, washing the product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, raising the temperature to 70-80 degrees C in water bath to initiate polymerization, and the polymerization reaction time is at least 50 hours, washing and dispersing into 100 pts. wt. water to obtain dispersion, the polydivinylsiloxane has number average molecular weight of 500-5,000, preferably 1,000-3,000, the mole percent of vinyl content (i.e. the ratio of the number of moles of vinyl to the number of moles of monodimethyldivinylsiloxane throughout the amino group) is 0.1-5% of amino mono-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane or amino-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane, (2) mixing 0.5-10 pts. wt. concentrated sulfuric acid and 0.1-15 pts. wt. graphite powder, and adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. potassium permanganate, placing the above mixture in an ice water bath and stirring for at least 12 hours, adding 50-250 pts. wt. water, stirring uniformly, adding 350-550 pts. wt. water and 8-55 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide to the above solution, stirring uniformly, washing the obtained product until near neutral, adding 100 pts. wt. water to the product, and the ultrasonic dispersing uniformly to obtain dispersion, and (3) mixing the step (1) and (2) dispersions, adding 0.01-2 pts. wt. initiator, and 0.01-10 pts. wt. nifedipine, stirring, raising the temperature of the water bath to 70-80 degrees C to initiate polymerization, after the polymerization time is at least 60 hours, adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. ascorbic acid to the reaction solution, and continuing reaction for 10-40 hours, placing the product in carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extraction apparatus, and supercritical drying for at least 1 hour at temperature of 30-60 degrees C and pressure of 5-15 MPa using carbon dioxide as medium to obtain three-dimensional network graphene-halloysite airgel composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide or azobisisobutyronitrile. Preferred Method: In the step (1), adding 1-10 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes to mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, ultrasonic dispersing uniformly for 1 hour to obtain halloysite dispersion, adding 1-10 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.1-1 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. initiator, and 0.1-5 pts. wt. furosemide to a mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, stirring uniformly for 10-60 minutes, adding obtained solution to the halloysite dispersion, ultrasonic dispersing uniformly for 30 minutes, vacuuming, maintaining the vacuum for 1 hour, then returning to normal pressure, and repeating vacuuming steps for three times, washing the product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, and during the polymerization, prepolymerizing the mixture at 70-80 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, raising the temperature to 70-80 degrees C in water bath to initiate polymerization, and the polymerization reaction time is 12-24 hours, it is sequentially polymerized in a constant temperature water bath of 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C, and 100 degrees C for 2-8 hours, washing and dispersing into 100 pts. wt. water to obtain dispersion. In the step (2), mixing 1-5 pts. wt. concentrated sulfuric acid and 0.5-10 pts. wt. graphite powder, and adding 1-10 pts. wt. potassium permanganate, placing the above mixture in an ice water bath of 1-3 degrees C and stirring for 1-10 hours, adding 100-200 pts. wt. water, stirring uniformly for 1-4 hours, cooling to room temperature, adding 400-500 pts. wt. water and 10-50 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide to the above solution, stirring uniformly for 1-6 hours, washing the obtained product until near neutral, adding 100 pts. wt. water to the product, and the ultrasonic dispersing uniformly to obtain dispersion, in the step (3), mixing the step (1) and (2) dispersions, adding 0.01-1 pts. wt. initiator, and 0.01-5 pts. wt. nifedipine, stirring for 1-10 hours, raising the temperature of the water bath to 70-80 degrees C to initiate polymerization, after the polymerization time is 12-24 hours, sequentially polymerizing in constant temperature water bath at 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C, 100 degrees C for 2-8 hours respectively, placing in 80-200 degrees C hydrothermal kettle, reacting for 1-10 hours, adding 1-10 pts. wt. ascorbic acid to the reaction solution, and continuing reaction at 20-100 degrees C for 10-40 hours, placing the product in carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extraction apparatus, and supercritical drying for 2-4 hour, preferably for 3 hours at temperature of 35-55 degrees C and pressure of 7-10 MPa using carbon dioxide as medium to obtain three-dimensional network graphene-halloysite airgel composite material. The inner side of the wall of the halloysite nanotube has positive charge, and the outside of the tube wall has a negative charge, the sodium styrene sulfonate added in the step (1) has negative charge, and the sodium styrene sulfonate is adsorbed on the inner wall of the halloysite nanotube by electrostatic action, the polydivinylsiloxane, initiator and furosemide added in step (1) are also dispersed into the hollow structure of the halloysite nanotubes, the hollow structure of the halloysite nanotube provides microporous structure for the three-dimensional network of graphene-allotite composite aerogel material, after vacuuming and washing, the polydivinylsiloxane is copolymerized with sodium styrene sulfonate to form crosslinked structure inside the halloysite nanotubes, loading furosemide in the halloysite nanotubes, in step (3) causes polymerization of the vinyl functional groups on the polydivinylsiloxane located outside the hollow structure of the halloysite nanotubes under the action of an initiator, in order to form three-dimensional network structure between the halloysite nanotubes and the polydivinylsiloxane, the concentrated sulfuric acid and graphite powder added in step (2) react with potassium permanganate to form graphene, and the graphene sheet layer is successfully constructed into a network pore structure, the three-dimensional network structure and the network pore structure constructed by the graphene sheets form a three-dimensional network pore structure, the three-dimensional network pore structure provides a mesoporous structure for the three-dimensional network of graphene-allotite composite aerogel material, and the nifediquinone is supported in the mesoporous structure.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B03 (Other heterocyclics.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.)
MC B04-C03B; B04-C03F; B05-A01A; B05-A03A1; B05-B02C; B05-C06; B07-A01; B07-D04D; B10-A09B; B10-A15; B12-M02G; B15-C00; A02-A01; A02-A02; A04-C; A06-A00E3; A08-S02; A10-B01; A11-A03; A11-B15C; A12-S05L; A12-V01; A12-W14

      CN105565774-A   UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ)   CHENG L, LI J, LIU H, LI H, LI Y, CHU P

PN CN109529942-A
TI Asymmetric selective catalyst for catalyzing asymmetric hydrogenation of ketoesters to chiral hydroxy ketoesters, obtained by introducing a one-dimensional spiral coordination polymer between layers of exfoliated hydrotalcite nanosheets.
AU AN Z
   HE J
   WANG W
   WANG D
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2019311610
AB    NOVELTY - Asymmetric selective catalyst, obtained by introducing a one-dimensional spiral coordination polymer between layers of exfoliated hydrotalcite nanosheets, is claimed. The stripped hydrotalcite nanosheet has a basic ionic group, the metal element in the nano hydrotalcite comprises a divalent metal and a high-valent metal ion, and the high-valent metal ion is a trivalent or/and tetravalent metal ion; and the molar ratio of the divalent metal to the high-valent metal ion is (2-4):1; one-dimensional spiral coordination polymer is a one-dimensional spiral coordination polymer composed of a metal ion coordinated with an amino acid, and the metal is preferably platinum, ruthenium, rhodium, iridium, nickel, and the molar ratio of the metal to the amino acid is 0.1-10:1.
   USE - The asymmetrically selective catalyst is useful for catalyzing the asymmetric hydrogenation of ketoesters to produce chiral hydroxy ketoesters (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the asymmetrically selective catalyst, comprising: (i) dissolving metal salt and amino acid in a mixed solution of ethanol, deionized water and pyridine, where the total metal concentration is 0.01-10 mol/l, and the volume ratio of ethanol, deionized water and pyridine is (1-100): (1-100): (1-100), then, drying in a drying oven at 60-180 degrees C for 1-5 days to obtain a crystal, which is a one-dimensional spiral coordination polymer, (ii) dissolving the metal salt of of the stripped hydrotalcite nanosheet in decarbonated water to form a solution A containing a divalent metal and a high-valent metal ion, dissolving the precipitant in the decarbonated water to form a solution B, where the precipitating agent is sodium hydroxide or ammonia, under the action of nitrogen, mixing the solution A and the solution B, and vigorously stirring for 0.1-120 minutes, transferring the obtained suspension into a hydrothermal kettle and crystallizing at 60-180 degrees C for 1-48 hours, completing crystallization, centrifuging the suspension, washing with deionized water to remove carbon dioxide until the supernatant was neutral, and washing once with ethanol, vacuum drying to obtain hydrotalcite powder, the hydrotalcite is added to the stripping solvent, preferably at a concentration of 0.1-20 g/l, the stripping solvent is water, n-butanol and/or formamide, passing nitrogen for 0.1-15 hours, the stripping solvent is placed in a shaker and shaken at a vibration speed of 100-300 revolutions per minute for 1-48 hours to obtain a uniform transparent colloid, i.e., a stripped hydrotalcite nanosheet, and (iii) mixing the stripped hydrotalcite nanosheet and one-dimensional spiral coordination polymer and adding into ethanol, where the mass ratio is 0.01-100:1, and the metal concentration of the one-dimensional spiral coordination polymer is 0.001-10 mol/l, adjusting the pH to 7-14, reducing with a reducing agent under nitrogen atmosphere, where the reducing agent is preferably hydrazine hydrate, formaldehyde, sodium borate, and reacting for 1-64 hours, washing the resulting precipitate three times with deionized water with carbon dioxide and once with ethanol, and drying the catalyst in a vacuum drying oven at 100 degrees C for 2-48 hours;
   (2) use of asymmetrically selective catalyst for catalyzing the asymmetric hydrogenation of ketoesters to produce chiral hydroxy ketoesters; and
   (3) method for catalyzing the asymmetric hydrogenation of ketoesters to produce chiral hydroxy ketoesters, comprising dissolving benzoylacetate and the catalyst in ethanol and passing hydrogen to carry out a reaction at 25-200 degrees C, 1-10 MPa, for 1-48 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The divalent metal ion in the hydrotalcite nanosheet is magnesium, zinc, nickel, manganese, copper, cobalt, cadmium, calcium, ferrous, and the high-valent metal is indium, iron, gallium, chromium, cobalt, lanthanum, manganese, tin, zirconium and/or titanium. In step (ii), the total concentration of metal ions in solution A is 0.1-1 mol/l; in solution B, the concentration of precipitant is 0.1-1 mol/l. The hydrotalcite is added to the stripping solvent, preferably at a concentration of from 0.1-20 g/l.

PT P
PN CN109279594-A; CN109279594-B
TI Preparing porous carbon with controllable adjusting nitrogen doping amount useful as adsorbent material, electrode material and catalytic material, comprises pre-treating the raw material and freeze-drying.
AU HAO J
   XIA H
   GUO Q
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
   UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 201915893L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porous carbon with controllable adjusting nitrogen doping amount comprises (i) cutting raw material into pieces, washing for many times with distilled water, and naturally drying; (ii) freezing in liquid nitrogen, and freeze-drying for 48 hours; (iii) allowing to stand for many hours and filling the pore structure in the material with urea solution by capillary action; (iv) adsorbing urea solution by sample in liquid nitrogen to freeze, and freeze-drying for 48 hours; (v) placing the freeze-dried sample in tube furnace, closing the furnace, and filling tube furnace with high-purity nitrogen gas at gas flow rate of 100 ml/minute, exhausting the air in the furnace chamber for 20 minutes and heating to 500-800 degrees C, preferably 650 degrees C for 60 minutes at a rate of 5 ml/minute; (vi) removing unreacted urea on surface of material by hydrothermal washing, and removing soluble impurities in material by washing with distilled water and ethanol many times and drying for many hours.
   USE - The porous carbon is useful as adsorbent material, electrode material, catalytic material, and as carbon dioxide adsorbent material or lithium ion battery anode material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porous carbon is suitable in the fields of adsorption, energy storage and catalysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing porous carbon with controllable adjusting nitrogen doping amount comprises (i) cutting raw material into pieces, washing for many times with distilled water, and naturally drying; (ii) freezing the pretreated raw materials in liquid nitrogen, placing in freeze dryer, and freeze-drying for 48 hours to obtain freeze-dried sample; (iii) placing the freeze-dried sample in a specific concentration of urea solution, allowing to stand for many hours and filling the pore structure in the material with the urea solution by capillary action; (iv) adsorbing urea solution by sample in liquid nitrogen to freeze for many hours, taking out frozen sample, placing in a freeze dryer and freeze-drying for 48 hours to obtain a nitrogen-containing molded sample; (v) placing the freeze-dried sample in tube furnace, closing the furnace, and filling tube furnace with high-purity nitrogen gas at a gas flow rate of 100 ml/minute, exhausting the air in the furnace chamber for 20 minutes and heating to 500-800 degrees C, preferably 650 degrees C for 60 minutes at a rate of 5 ml/minute; (vi) removing the unreacted urea on the surface of the material by hydrothermal washing, and removing the soluble impurities in the material by washing with distilled water and ethanol many times and drying for many hours. The biomass or biomass waste as raw material and the porous carbon material has a three-dimensional interpenetrating pore structure with microporous, mesoporous and macroporous mixed distribution, and has sp2 hybrid carbon skeleton and delocalized pi bond. The porous carbon retains the inherent structural characteristics of the biomass, and the internal pores of the biomass are not destroyed. The biomass is preferably cotton, apple and celery. The urea is a nitrogen source, and the carbon dioxide released by the decomposition of urea is used to make pores and increase the specific surface area and provide a basic site. The nitrogen content is controlled in the porous carbon by controlling the concentration of the urea solution. The urea is introduced into and remains in the porous carbon pore structure by a freeze-drying process. The nitrogen atoms in the porous carbon are doped in the carbon skeleton, and the additional lone pair electrons of the nitrogen atom provide negative charge for the sp2 hybrid carbon skeleton and the delocalized pi bond, which enhances the electron transport rate and catalytic activity. The nitrogen doping content is between 2-40%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The freezing of raw material is carried out for twice, pre-frozen by liquid nitrogen, and re-frozen in a freeze dryer at less than -50 degrees C. The molar concentration of urea solution is 5-19 mol/l. The potassium hydroxide and potassium carbonate added when the method is annealed to assist the pore formation. The method regulates the amount of nitrogen doping by the concentration of the urea solution when the nitrogen content is regulated, then boric acid and ionic liquid added to prepare boron and nitrogen and sulfur and nitrogen are co-doped with porous carbon.

TI Method for purification of hydrogen in hydrogen liquefaction process, involves performing first-stage purification treatment, and secondary purification treatment of gaseous hydrogen followed by thermally regenerating second adsorber.
AU SHI H
   NI Z
   YAN Y
   WU H
   GUO D
   DU H
   HUI Y
AE ZHANGJIAGANG FURUI HYDROGEN ENERGY EQUIP (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018887206
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen in hydrogen liquefaction process, purification method involves performing first-stage purification treatment of hydrogen by compressing the raw material hydrogen into gaseous hydrogen with pressure, and adsorbing the oil in the gaseous hydrogen through a net oil adsorber, so that the volume content of the oil in the gaseous hydrogen does not exceed 10 ppbV, catalytic refining of gaseous hydrogen to remove oxygen impurities in gaseous hydrogen, drying the gaseous hydrogen to make the volume of moisture in gaseous hydrogen not exceed 1 ppmV. Secondary purification treatment of gaseous hydrogen is performed. The hydrogen is stored in the liquid hydrogen storage tank, first low temperature unit conversion adsorber is composed of the first adsorber and the second adsorber, and the adsorbents in the first adsorber and the second adsorber are all capable of thermal regeneration, the gaseous hydrogen followed by thermally regenerating second adsorber.
   USE - Method for purification of hydrogen in hydrogen liquefaction process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables purification of hydrogen with better purifying effect and safety performance, no solidification impurity in the liquefaction process to block various gas and liquid passages and pipelines in the hydrogen liquefaction device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrogen in hydrogen liquefaction process, purification method involves performing first-stage purification treatment of hydrogen by compressing the raw material hydrogen into gaseous hydrogen with pressure, and adsorbing the oil in the gaseous hydrogen through a net oil adsorber, so that the volume content of the oil in the gaseous hydrogen does not exceed 10 ppbV, catalytic refining of gaseous hydrogen to remove oxygen impurities in gaseous hydrogen, so that the volume content of oxygen impurities in gaseous hydrogen does not exceed 2 ppbV, drying the gaseous hydrogen to make the volume of moisture in gaseous hydrogen not exceed 1 ppmV, drying obtained gaseous hydrogen temperature controlled at a dew point temperature of -100 to -70 degrees C, adsorbing carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and high boiling impurities in the gaseous hydrogen by an adsorber capable of removing high boiling impurities to make carbon dioxide in the gaseous hydrogen. Secondary purification treatment of gaseous hydrogen is performed by entering the gaseous hydrogen after the primary purification treatment into the heat exchanger in the hydrogen liquefaction cold box for pre-cooling, and entering the first low-temperature unit conversion adsorber for secondary purification after the secondary purification treatment, the gaseous hydrogen enters the reactor in the hydrogen liquefaction cold box to carry out the gaseous hydrogen intermediate conversion, and entering the liquid hydrogen liquefaction cold box to perform the liquid hydrogen positive conversion to obtain the liquid hydrogen and the liquid. The hydrogen is stored in the liquid hydrogen storage tank, first low temperature unit conversion adsorber is composed of the first adsorber and the second adsorber, and the adsorbents in the first adsorber and the second adsorber are all capable of thermal regeneration, the gaseous hydrogen after the purification and pre-cooling treatment is subjected to the adsorption treatment by the first adsorber, followed by thermally regenerating second adsorber when the gaseous hydrogen after the primary purification and pre-cooling treatment is subjected to the adsorption treatment by the second adsorber, and purifying low-temperature gaseous hydrogen by compressing low-temperature gaseous hydrogen gasified in a liquid hydrogen storage tank into low-temperature gaseous hydrogen with pressure, and low-temperature gaseous hydrogen having pressure sequentially enters a heat exchanger in a hydrogen liquefied cold box after the reactor is output, supplying heat exchanger and the reactor with a cooling amount, and entering low-temperature liquid hydrogen output from the reactor the third adsorber for adsorption treatment.

PN CN108654347-A; CN108654347-B
TI Method for recycling and utilizing rare earth exhaust gas in fluoride system, involves collecting tail gas, collecting gas into filter, chemically absorbing fluorine-containing gas followed by drying and continuously capturing fluorine ions.
AU LIU Z
   LIN H
   WANG Z
   ZHAO J
   HE J
AE JIANGSU JINSHI RARE EARTH CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201883728V
AB    NOVELTY - A rare earth exhaust gas in a fluoride system recycling and utilizing method involves collecting the tail gas generated during the rare earth electrolysis process by using device, and collecting exhaust gas into filter or sintering plate dust collector through collecting pipe. The separated rare earth dust is contained in container, and the remaining fluorine-containing gas proceeds to the next step. The fluorine-containing gas is chemically absorbed into tower. The generated lithium fluoride solution is collected into a circulating pool, and the pH value of circulating pool is tested during the collection process, and lithium hydroxide solution is added to the circulating pool to adjust the pH to 3. The valve slowly releases the supernatant, and after releasing the supernatant, the remaining solution in the circulating pool is subjected to multiple solid-liquid separation and water washing, and the precipitate is collected followed by drying and continuously capturing fluorine ions.
   USE - Method for recycling and utilizing rare earth exhaust gas in a fluoride system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing carbon dioxide emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A rare earth exhaust gas in a fluoride system recycling and utilizing method involves collecting the tail gas generated during the rare earth electrolysis process by using the gas collecting device, and collecting the exhaust gas into the bag filter or sintering plate dust collector through the gas collecting pipe that generates the negative pressure by the fan to perform the preliminary separation of the rare earth dust and the exhaust gas. The separated rare earth dust is contained in a container for recovery, and the remaining fluorine-containing gas proceeds to the next step. The fluorine-containing gas is chemically absorbed into a spray absorption tower in which the absorbent is lithium hydrogencarbonate, after chemical absorption, lithium hydrogencarbonate is chemically reacted with fluorine ions in the fluorine-containing gas, and the reaction is expressed as given in the specification. The generated lithium fluoride solution is collected into a circulating pool, and the pH value of the circulating pool is tested during the collection process, and a lithium hydroxide solution is added to the circulating pool to adjust the pH to 3, and controlled by the water level set on the side wall of the circulating pool. The valve slowly releases the supernatant, and after releasing the supernatant, the remaining solution in the circulating pool is subjected to multiple solid-liquid separation and water washing, and the precipitate is collected when the pH of the washing liquid reaches 6-7, and the precipitate is subjected to preliminary drying and dehydration treatment, and dried at 105-110 degrees C for 3-7 hours, followed by drying at 420-450 degrees C for 3-10 hours to obtain the finished lithium fluoride, the washing liquid is introduced into the circulating pool for reuse, directly introducing the exhaust gas chemically absorbed by the spray absorption tower into the lithium carbonate liquid pool, where the exhaust gas neutralizes the carbon dioxide obtained by the chemical reaction and reacts with the lithium carbonate in the lithium carbonate liquid pool to make lithium carbonate, and is converted into lithium bicarbonate with high solubility, and the chemical reaction, and injecting converted lithium hydrogencarbonate solution into the spray absorption tower for recycling as a shower liquid, and continuously capturing fluorine ions in the fluorine-containing gas.

PN CN108576355-A; CN108576355-B
TI Buccal tablet comprises bracken extract, lactose, mannitol, starch, citric acid, menthol, silica gel, aspartame and magnesium stearate.
AU MA B
   SU S
   ZHANG T
AE UNIV BAISE (UBSE-C)
   UNIV BAISE (UBSE-C)
GA 201879874A
AB    NOVELTY - Buccal tablet comprises bracken extract, lactose, mannitol, starch, citric acid, menthol, silica gel, aspartame and magnesium stearate. The bracken extract is prepared by (a) selecting fresh and tender bracken, (b) soaking bracken in sodium bicarbonate solution, washing with water, soaking the bracken in distilled water, filling carbon dioxide gas and pre-treating bracken, (c) drying pre-treated bracken, crushing and sieving material, (d) mixing bracken powder, distilled water and absolute ethanol, homogenizing material, ultrasonically treating and filtering material and (e) adding absolute ethanol to first filter, sequentially concentrating and filtering, mixing filter residue and absolute ethanol, centrifuging to separate precipitate, and freezing precipitate, vacuum freeze-drying to obtain polysaccharide, subjecting second filtrate to rotary evaporation, reduced pressure and drying to obtain flavonoids, mixing polysaccharide and flavonoids, crushing and sieving material.
   USE - As buccal tablet.
   ADVANTAGE - The buccal tablet is prepared by simple process, and comprises bracken extract content of less than 0.001 mu g/g, polysaccharide content of 14.2-14.7% and flavonoid content of 6.4-6.8% and has excellent sensory effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Buccal tablet comprises 10-50 pts. wt. bracken extract, 15-30 pts. wt. lactose, 30-80 pts. wt. mannitol, 10-20 pts. wt. starch, 0.5-2 pts. wt. citric acid, 0.1-1.5 pts. wt. menthol, 1-1.5 pts. wt. silica gel, 0.1-0.8 pt. wt. aspartame and 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. magnesium stearate. The bracken extract is prepared by (a) selecting fresh and tender bracken as raw material, washing and drying material, (b) soaking bracken in sodium bicarbonate solution at 35-40 degrees C, ultrasonically treating for 10-15 minutes, removing and washing with water, soaking the bracken in distilled water at 65-70 degrees C and vacuuming to 0.01-0.03 MPa, filling carbon dioxide gas to a vacuum of 0.08-0.1 MPa, rolling for 15-20 minutes, draining and pre-treating bracken, (c) drying pre-treated bracken at 40-50 degrees C for 40-48 hours, crushing and passing through 60 mesh sieve, (d) mixing the bracken powder, distilled water and absolute ethanol in a ratio of 1:25-30:20-25 (g/ml), homogenizing the material at 30-35 degrees C and 15-20 MPa for 15-20 minutes, ultrasonically treating at 25 degrees C, 100 W and 0.2 MPa for 35-40 minutes and at 30 degrees C, 150 W and 0.3 MPa for 30-35 minutes, continuously adding 86-88% ethanol to mixture, ultrasonically treating at 60-65 degrees C, 180 W and 0.5 MPa for 45-55 minutes and filtering material and (e) adding more than 90% absolute ethanol to first filter, concentrating and filtering under reduced pressure to obtain filter residue and second filtrate, mixing filter residue and absolute ethanol in a volume of 1:3-4, leaving for 8-12 hours, centrifuging at 5000 revolutions per minute for 20 minutes to separate precipitate, and freezing precipitate at -80 degrees C for 12 hours, vacuum freeze-drying to obtain polysaccharide, evaporating second filtrate under reduced pressure to remove ethanol and drying to obtain flavonoids, mixing polysaccharide and flavonoids, crushing and passing through 80 mesh sieve. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing buccal tablet, which involves (i) weighing above-mentioned raw materials and mixing to obtain premix, (ii) adding 70 vol.% ethanol to premix, granulating, passing through 30 mesh sieve, drying sieved material at 40 degrees C for 3 hours and passing through 30 mesh sieve and (iii) mixing coarse particles obtained in step (ii), magnesium stearate and silica gel, and tableting the mixed material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The buccal tablet comprises 35 pts. wt. bracken extract, 20 pts. wt. lactose, 50 pts. wt. mannitol, 15 pts. wt. starch, 1 pt. wt. citric acid, 1 pt. wt. menthol, 1.3 pts. wt. silica gel, 0.5 pt. wt. aspartame and 1 pt. wt. magnesium stearate. The concentration of sodium bicarbonate is 1-3 %mass. The polysaccharide and flavonoid yield are 14.2-14.7% and 6.4-6.8% of bracken powder. The content of bracken extract is less than 0.001 mu g/g. Preferred Method: In step (e), the vacuum freeze-drying is performed at -40 degrees C and vacuum of 50 Pa. In step (iii) tableting is performed by rotating at a speed of 30rpm and filling at a pressure of 20 KN to a depth of 11 m.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN KR1872254-B1
TI Surface finishing of concrete structure, comprises e.g. removing the laying, impurities, and applying laying material as putty to flatten the base, and applying styrene-butadiene emulsion, acrylic emulsion and finish coating composition.
AU YUH J H
   CHOI D H
AE YUH J H (YUHJ-Individual)
   CHOI D H (CHOI-Individual)
GA 2018541236
AB    NOVELTY - Surface finishing of concrete structure, comprises (a) removing the laying, impurities, or deteriorated portions of concrete structure and applying applied laying material as putty to flatten base, and (b) applying styrene-butadiene emulsion, acrylic emulsion and finish coating composition to prevent penetration of water, carbon dioxide, chlorine ions, or harmful substances on the treated surface, to increase the adhesion with the adherend or to strengthen the surface layer to form a surface protective/reinforcing layer, (c) repairing cross-section by using a self-leveling mortar or cross-sectional repair material on the surface protective/reinforcing layer, and (d) applying the finish coating composition on the upper restored section, and curing. The finish coating composition comprises inorganic filler and performance improving agent. The inorganic filler comprises calcium carbonate, talc, sodium carbonate, mica, titanium oxide, vermiculite, aluminum nitride and silicon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for surface finishing of concrete structure (claimed) including concrete slab, factory floor, parking lot, warehouse and road facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has excellent adhesion with the concrete structure, prevents cracks, and has improved self life due to excellent abrasion resistance and impact resistance, where self life can be improved by blocking corrosion, ultraviolet rays and other deterioration factors of concrete.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Surface finishing concrete structure, comprises (a) removing the laying, impurities, or deteriorated portions of the concrete structure and applying the applied laying material as putty to flatten the base, and (b) applying styrene-butadiene emulsion, acrylic emulsion and the surface finish coating composition to prevent penetration of water, carbon dioxide, chlorine ions, or harmful substances on the treated surface, to increase the adhesion with the adherend or to strengthen the surface layer to form a surface protective/reinforcing layer, (c) repairing the cross-section by using a self-leveling mortar or cross-sectional repair material on the surface protective/reinforcing layer, and (d) applying the finish coating composition on the upper restored section, and curing. The finish coating composition comprises 5-70 wt.% inorganic filler and 30-95 wt.% performance improving agent. The inorganic filler comprises 5-95 wt.% calcium carbonate, 1-30 wt.% talc, 0.1-25 wt.% sodium carbonate, 0.1-25 wt.% mica, 0.01-15 wt.% titanium oxide, 0.01-15 wt.% vermiculite, 0.01-15 wt.% aluminum nitride and 0.01-15 wt.% silicon dioxide. The performance improving agent comprises 15-85 wt.% styrene-methyl acrylate copolymer, 5-30 wt.% polyester polyol, 1-25 wt.% polytrimethylene terephthalate, 0.1-20 wt.% petroleum resin, 0.01-15 wt.% glycidyl methacrylate, 0.01-15 wt.% carboxymethylcellulose, 0.01-15 wt.% ethyl-butyl acrylate copolymer, 0.01-40 wt.% curing accelerator including hexamethylene diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, 2,4,4-trimethylhexamethylene isocyanate, xylene diisocyanate, toluene diisocyanate, 4,4'-diphenylmethane isocyanate and/or phenyl isocyanate, 0.01-20 wt.% benzoyl peroxide as reaction initiator, 0.01-10 wt.% polyacrylic acid, 0.01-10 wt.% f titanium dioxide as pigment and 0.01-10 wt.% silicone oil-based defoaming agent. The composition has abrasion resistance of 0.06-0.08 mg/mm2 measured according to KS F 4041 and KS F 4936, neutralization depth of less than or equal to 0.001 mm, chloride ion penetration resistance of 16-20 coulombs, moisture permeability of 18-22 g/m2 degrees C, and bond strength of: 1.62-1.73 N/mm2 after standard curing, 1.55-1.7 N/mm2 after accelerated weathering, 1.6-1.71 N/mm2 after warm, 1.61-1.68 N/mm2 after alkali resistance, and 1.59-1.66N/mm2 after salt water.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN107988152-A
TI Preparing active ingredient having antioxidant effect by preparing human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, performing centrifugation, evaluating antioxidant effects of fractions and isolating fractions having antioxidant effect.
AU DENG Y
   WANG Y
   JIANG Z
AE WUHAN BEIDU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201836627D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing an active ingredient having an antioxidant effect from a human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell culture fluid involves (a) preparing human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by (i) collecting umbilical cord after normal delivery or cesarean section under aseptic conditions, and storing at 4 degrees C using preservation solution, (ii) cleaning the umbilical cord, (iii) processing the umbilical cord, and (iv) isolating human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, and (b) collecting human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell culture fluid at log phase, storing at 1-5 degrees C, subjecting the collected cells to ultracentrifugation, performing centrifugation at 1-5 degrees C and 2000-5000 g using ultrafiltration tubes of different specifications, collecting different fractions by filtration for antioxidation experiments, evaluating the antioxidant effects of the various fractions and isolating the fractions having a molecular weight of 10-30 KDa with an antioxidant effect.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing an active ingredient having an antioxidant effect, which is useful for preparing medicines, healthcare products, skin care products, and other household chemicals (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, convenient to perform, cost-effective, and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing an active ingredient having an antioxidant effect from a human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell culture fluid involves (a) preparing human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by (i) collecting umbilical cord after normal delivery or cesarean section under aseptic conditions, and storing at 4 degrees C using preservation solution, (ii) cleaning the umbilical cord by removing the umbilical cord from the preservation solution, placing the umbilical cord in a sterilized container, adding 40-70 ml physiological saline solution, rinsing the umbilical cord for 20-30 seconds, transferring the umbilical cord to another sterilized container, adding 40-70 ml metronidazole solution, maintaining for 5-7 minutes, washing the umbilical cord using 40-70 ml physiological saline solution once, and placing the cleaned umbilical cord in a disposable sterilized Petri dish, (iii) processing the umbilical cord by cutting the umbilical cord using forceps and scissors to obtain meat-like tissue, transferring the meat-like tissue into a centrifuge tube, adding suitable amount of phosphate buffer, centrifuging at 1000-1500 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, removing the supernatant, adding 10-15 ml culture solution, dividing the tissue suspension into two culture flasks, and incubating the culture flask at 37 degrees C for 3-5 days in presence of 5% carbon dioxide, and (iv) isolating human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by discarding the culture medium, adding TrypLE (RTM: Highly purified cell dissociation enzymes), immediately incubating the mixture for 8 minutes, waiting until the cells are completely detached, adding stop solution to the medium to blow out the cells, centrifuging the mixture at 1000-1500 rpm for 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant, adding new culture medium, and incubating the culture flask at 37 degrees C for 3-5 days in presence of 5% carbon dioxide, and (b) collecting human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell culture fluid at log phase, storing at 1-5 degrees C, subjecting the collected cells to ultracentrifugation, performing centrifugation at 1-5 degrees C and 2000-5000 g using ultrafiltration tubes of different specifications, collecting different fractions by filtration for antioxidation experiments, evaluating the antioxidant effects of the various fractions and isolating the fractions having a molecular weight of 10-30 KDa with an antioxidant effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The method further involves freezing the fractions having an antioxidant effect separately to obtain a freeze-dried human umbilical cord powder with an antioxidant effect.

PN CN107935258-A
TI Black and odor water treatment device comprises            coagulation and sedimentation system or air floatation            system, electrolysis system and sludge treatment, where            coagulation and sedimentation system comprises            coagulation reaction tank.
AU ZHANG S
   WANG F
   ZHAO Y
AE BOYING XIAMEN SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BOYI-Non-standard)
GA 201832783W
AB    NOVELTY - Black and odor water treatment device                comprises coagulation and sedimentation system or                air floatation system, electrolysis system (3) and                sludge dewatering system (4). The coagulation and                sedimentation system or air floatation system                comprises coagulation reaction tank, sedimentation                tank or air floatation machine. The black smelly                water body is raised to the coagulation reaction                tank by sewage, and 3-80g coagulant and 0.1-1g                coagulant auxiliary agent are added to coagulation                reaction tank for coagulation reaction to generate                phosphate precipitation to remove phosphorus from                water body.
   USE - Black and odor water treatment device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Black and odor water treatment device                comprises coagulation and sedimentation system or                air floatation system, electrolysis system and                sludge dewatering system. The coagulation and                sedimentation system or air floatation system                comprises coagulation reaction tank, sedimentation                tank or air floatation machine. The black smelly                water body is raised to the coagulation reaction                tank by sewage, and 3-80g coagulant and 0.1-1g                coagulant auxiliary agent are added to coagulation                reaction tank for coagulation reaction to generate                phosphate precipitation to remove phosphorus from                water body. The insoluble organic matter,                petroleum, animal and vegetable oils and other                organic matter are adsorbed into the sedimentation                tank or air floatation machine for solid-liquid                separation, and sludge slurry is dehydrated into                mud in the sludge treatment system and into                electrolysis system. The electrolysis system                comprises electrolysis machine, DC power supply and                automatic control. The chlorine, hydroxy and oxygen                produced by the anode during the electrolysis                process react with organic matter and ammonia                nitrogen in water to produce carbon dioxide, water,                nitrogen and nitrate, removes organic matter in the                water and reduce chemical oxygen demand, biological                oxygen demand and ammoniacal nitrogen. The hydrogen                ion generated at cathode reacts with nitrate,                organic matter and ammoniacal nitrogen to produce                carbon dioxide, water, and nitrogen. The free                radical generated by electrolysis oxidizes and                decomposes organisms to release phosphoric acid in                organisms such as DNA and RNA into inorganic                phosphates. The free radical generated by                electrolysis decomposes organic phosphorus into                inorganic phosphate. The sludge dewatering system                comprises sludge pump, concentration tank, and                dewatering machine. The sludge is pumped into                sludge concentration tank through a sludge pump to                concentrate and then dehydrated into a sludge block                through a dehydrator. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a method for using a black and odor                water treatment device, which involves:
   (A) dividing or partitioning black odor water                body into a section of water body by rubber dam,                and pumping water from downstream of black odor                body to coagulation reaction tank;
   (B) pumping black water to reaction tank by                adding 3-80g coagulant per cubic meter of water,                reacting for 3-15 minutes, adding 0.1-1g                polyacrylamide, stirring slowly for 3-5 minutes to                promote the formation of amaranth flowers, and                separating clear liquid and mud into sedimentation                tank or flotation tank;
   (C) entering clear liquid enters into                electrolysis machine, where working voltage for                electrolysis is 10-380V, voltage between two                electrodes is 1-10V, current density is                10-600mA/cm2 for a duration of 1 second to 1                minute;
   (D) flowing water body into upstream for                multiple times to obtain a clear water body;                and
   (E) pumping slurry that is separated by                coagulation sedimentation or flotation into a                dehydrator and dehydrating it into a filtrate and a                mud block.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of black                and odor water treatment device.
   Electrolysis system (3)
   Sludge dewatering system (4)
   Water pump (11)
   Concrete deposition machine (12)
   Sludge pump (41)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The electrolysis                  machine is provided with a power source and an                  electrolysis cell. Electrodes in electrolysis                  cell are graphite, titanium, iron, aluminum,                  zinc, copper, lead, nickel, molybdenum, chromium,                  alloys, titanium electrodes coated with noble                  metal oxides or nano-catalytic inert electrodes.                  The surface layer of nano-catalytic inert                  electrode is coated with a noble metal oxide                  inert catalytic coating layer with a crystal                  grain of 10-60nm, and substrate of nano catalytic                  inert electrode is a titanium plate, other metal                  plate or a plastic plate. The coagulant is one or                  combination of two or more of iron salt, aluminum                  salts, polyaluminum, and polyaluminium. The iron                  salt is iron sulfate or ferrous sulfate in an                  amount of 3-20g/m3. The aluminum salt is aluminum                  sulfate or aluminum chloride in an amount of                  30-80g/m3. The polyaluminum is a polyaluminum                  chloride, a polyaluminum sulfate or a                  polyaluminum silicate in an amount of 30-80g/m3.                  The polyiron is polyferric chloride, polyferric                  sulfate or polysilicate iron in an amount of                  10-40g/m3. The coagulation sedimentation or air                  flotation system comprises a dosing device that                  adds sodium hydroxide or sodium carbonate to                  adjust pH of sewage to 7-9 when pH of water body                  is less than 7.

PT P
PN CN107667777-A
TI Cultivating Pleurotus eryngii using cultivation            frame, inoculating fungi into culture medium,            transplanting fungi, spraying water, cultivating up to            certain length, maintaining e.g. temperature, chopping            collected fungi, and extending rod.
AU ZHANG L
AE CHONGQING WEIDEXIAN AGRIC DEV CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201814663U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cultivating Pleurotus eryngii                involves using cultivation frame, inoculating the                fungi into the culture medium, placing the fungal                bag in cultivation room, transplanting fungi,                opening the sealing end of the bag, placing the                frame in the cultivation room, sliding the culture                plate into the fixing hopper, spraying water in                cultivating room, cultivating P.eryngii up to                certain length, maintaining e.g. temperature, then                chopping collected P.eryngii, and extending the rod                out of the cylinder for pushing outwards.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating                P.eryngii.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce the workload of                workers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for cultivating Pleurotus eryngii                involves (a) using cultivation frame comprising                main frame portion comprising frame and several                placing plates fixed on the frame, opening fungal                bag, inoculating the fungi into the culture medium,                injecting liquid strain culture into culture                medium, and re-sealing the fungal bag, (b) placing                the fungal bag in culture room for 4-7 days until                mycelial growth is observed, continuously culturing                for 4-5 days, detecting once for every 12 hours,                and once each 6 hours after 6-7 days, and                maintaining culture chamber temperature at 8-15                degrees C, (c) transplanting fungi, making multiple                small holes on the surface of the fungal bag,                opening the sealing end of the bag, placing the                frame in the cultivation room, where the fungal bag                from the higher end of the placing plate is placed                on the placing plate, sliding the culture plate                into the fixing hopper, placing fungal bag,                cultivating until the mycelia grows up to 4-7 cm in                length, spraying water fog in the cultivating room                to maintain humidity at 85-95%, illuminating at                400-500 lx and then maintaining carbon dioxide                concentration at 2500-4000 parts per million (ppm),                (d) cultivating P.eryngii up to a length of more                than 4-7 cm, increasing the temperature by 1                degrees C for every 2-4 days until the temperature                in the room reaches 15-17 degrees C, maintaining                illumination density at 800-900 lx, and carbon                dioxide concentration at 5000-7000 ppm, and                detecting the length of P.eryngii at least once a                day, where the length is more than 15-18 cm, and                (e) opening the switching on the motor and fan in                the frame after 75-85% P.eryngii of 15-18 cm in                length is observed, then chopping collected                P.eryngii, and extending the rod out of the                cylinder for pushing outwards, where the push rod                of the cylinder pushes the fungal bag from the                higher end of the placing plate to push the fixed                hopper. The placing plate is inclined. The frame is                provided with truncated cone-shaped fixing bars and                strip-shaped racks which are sequentially arranged                from top to bottom on the placing plate. The rack                is fixed on the frame. The fixing hopper is fixed                on the placing plate which is provided with several                vent holes, where the fixed hopper close to the                rack has smaller diameter at one end. The main                frame portion is equipped with sliding mounting                seat capable of sliding along the rack. The bottom                portion of the mounting seat is fixed with a                rotating gear meshed with the rack and a stepping                motor which drives the rotating gear to rotate. The                mounting seat is provided with a pushing mechanism                and a cutter for cutting P.eryngii. The pushing                mechanism comprises fan and cylinder which are                fixed on a mounting base, where the fan is                connected to a cylinder. The piston of the cylinder                is fixed with a pushing rod which pushes the fungal                bag in the fixed hopper out of the fixed                hopper.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  placing the fungal bag in sliding chute. The step                  (c) and step (d) involves maintaining oxygen                  concentration in cultivation room at 1000-1500                  ppm, spraying water away from the fungal bag, and                  avoiding water accumulation on the surface of                  P.eryngii, where the temperature of the water                  sprayed in the cultivation room and the                  temperature inside the cultivation room are the                  same. The step (e) involves chopping edible                  P.eryngii and moving the mounting seat to the                  position where the rack extends beyond the                  placing plate.

PN CN114478249-A
TI Improved method for producing dimethyl carbonate            comprises e.g. separating carbon dioxide in raw            material gas, adding rich liquid with dimethyl            carbonate synthesis catalyst group reactant, sending            reactant to heat exchanger, heating heater and sending            to reactor, and forming reaction mixture.
AU WANG J
   WANG W
   SONG W
   YANG P
   YANG H
   YANG X
AE SICHUAN TIANREN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202269544Q
AB    NOVELTY - Improved method for producing dimethyl                carbonate comprises sending the raw material gas                containing 5-80% carbon dioxide to the absorption                tower at a pressure of 0.1-10 MPa and 10-80degreesC                after detoxifying components, using the compound                formulation methyldiethanolamine (MDEA) solution                and methanol to absorb carbon dioxide in the                absorption tower in a ratio of 1:0.1-10, separating                the carbon dioxide in the raw material gas,                separating the reaction mixture in the flash tower                to obtain gaseous carbon dioxide, sending the                solution containing the catalyst to a heat                exchanger and a heater through a pressurized cycle,                separating the dimethyl carbonate and purifying                before being sent to the outside, and                simultaneously realizing the production of carbon                dioxide and dimethyl carbonate from the feed                gas.
   USE - The method is useful for producing dimethyl                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces energy losses for MDEA                regeneration and dimethyl carbonate                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improved method for producing dimethyl                carbonate comprises sending the raw material gas                containing 5-80% carbon dioxide to the absorption                tower at a pressure of 0.1-10 MPa and 10-80degreesC                after detoxifying components, using the compound                formulation methyldiethanolamine (MDEA) solution                and methanol to absorb carbon dioxide in the                absorption tower in a ratio of 1:0.1-10, separating                the carbon dioxide in the raw material gas, where                the gas from which the carbon dioxide is separated                is called decarburized gas, which is sent to the                next process, the solution that absorbs carbon                dioxide is called rich liquid, adding the rich                liquid with dimethyl carbonate synthesis catalyst                group called reactant, sending the reactant to the                heat exchanger, heating the heater to                140-249degreesC and sending to the reactor, part of                bicarbonate in the rich liquid in the reactor                reacts with methanol and carbamate to generate                dimethyl carbonate and water, forming reaction                mixture, sending the reaction mixture to a flash                tower, separating the reaction mixture in the flash                tower to obtain gaseous carbon dioxide, which is                directly sent out or sent to an absorption tower in                a pressurized cycle, where the methanol and MDEA                aqueous solution is called lean liquid, sending to                the carbon dioxide absorption tower in a                pressurized cycle, sending the solution containing                the catalyst to a heat exchanger and a heater                through a pressurized cycle, separating the                dimethyl carbonate and purifying before being sent                to the outside, and simultaneously realizing the                production of carbon dioxide and dimethyl carbonate                from the feed gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material gas                  includes blast furnace gas, converter gas,                  calcium carbide furnace gas, boiler flue gas,                  methane combustion gas, pressure swing adsorption                  hydrogen production tail gas, and other carbon                  dioxide-containing gases. The toxic components                  include hydrogen, chlorine, sulfur, phosphorus,                  fluorine, bromine, ammonia, methanol, etc., which                  are harmful to the dimethyl carbonate catalyst.                  The reaction mixture is circulated in the reactor                  using a circulation pump to increase the                  production of dimethyl carbonate.

TI One-step deashing activation of hydrothermal coke with carbon dioxide combined biomass by hydrothermal process comprises e.g. mixing biomass with water, placing mixture in the reaction kettle and passing carbon dioxide to the kettle.
AU DONG X
   GUO S
   WANG H
   WANG S
   SUN R
   SONG G
AE NANJING INST TECHNOLOGY (NANJ-C)
GA 201914704W
AB    NOVELTY - One-step deashing activation of hydrothermal coke with carbon dioxide combined biomass by hydrothermal process comprises (i) mixing biomass with water, placing mixture in the reaction kettle, passing carbon dioxide to the kettle, pressurizing to carry out a medium pressure hydrothermal carbonization reaction, (ii) passing carbon dioxide into the reactor, increasing the pressure in the kettle, heating reaction, promoting the conversion of alkali metal in biomass to the corresponding salt removal under high temperature and high pressure, promoting oxidative etching reaction of carbon dioxide and generated hydrothermal coke, increasing the porosity of hydrothermal coke, and (iii) cooling, hydrophobic the obtained solid-liquid mixed product into the extrusion dehydrator and then separating liquid product obtained by dehydration separation by concentrating to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for one-step deashing activation of hydrothermal coke with carbon dioxide combined biomass by hydrothermal process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), biomass is rice straw, wheat straw, millet stalk and/or corn stalk, and biomass has a length of 1-10 cm. In step (iii), obtained hot coke ash K content is less than or equal to 12 mg/kg, sodium content is less than or equal to 4 mg/kg, and pore surface area is greater than or equal to 785 m2/g. In step (iii), product includes formic acid, methanol, potassium formate, sodium formate and/or calcium formate. Preferred Method: In step (i), reaction pressure is 4-7 MPa, reaction temperature is 250-290 degrees C, and reaction time is 100-200 minutes. In step (i) and (ii), heating rate of the reactor is 10-20 degrees C/minute. In step (iii), squeeze hydrophobic using a screw extrusion hydrophobic machine, where power is 7-30 kW, and screw speed is 16-8 revolutions per minute. In step (iii), performing concentrated separation using a nanofiltration membrane, where pore size is 1-2 nm, molecular weight cut off is 40-900, and operating pressure is 3-10 bar. In step (ii), pressure of the reactor is 7-22 MPa, reaction temperature is 330-350 degrees C, and reaction time is 200-400 minutes. In step (i), mixing biomass and water in a mass ratio of 1:5-50.
