PN WO2022051598-A2; WO2022051598-A3
TI Making solid inorganic carbonates and solid inorganic bicarbonate compound by capturing, converting and storing carbon dioxide, by reacting at least one precursor compound and at least one carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive.
AU GADIKOTA G
AE UNIV CORNELL (CORR-C)
GA 2022346428
AB    NOVELTY - Producing at least one solid inorganic carbonate compound and solid inorganic bicarbonate compound by capturing, converting, and storing carbon dioxide, comprises: providing at least one precursor compound; providing at least one carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive; and reacting the precursor compound with the carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive under conditions effective to produce at least one solid inorganic carbonate compound.
   USE - The method is useful for producing at least one solid inorganic carbonate compound and solid inorganic bicarbonate compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: exhibits cost-effectiveness and efficient heat management; provides useable and economically viable technologies for tackling and handling the escalating problem of global warming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for producing the solid inorganic carbonate compound and solid inorganic bicarbonate compound by capturing, converting, and storing carbon dioxide comprising the precursor compound, the carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive and a reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precursor compound is: a non-carbonate metal species; a solid precursor compound or a liquid precursor compound; silicate compound, an oxide compound, a chloride compound, a hydroxide compound, a nitrate compound, a nitrite compound, a sulfite compound, and a sulfate compound; a calcium-, magnesium-, lithium-, or aluminum- bearing silicate or a calcium-, magnesium-, lithium-, or aluminum- bearing oxide; calcium oxide, calcium silicate, magnesia, lithium chloride, magnesium chloride, calcium chloride, sodium chloride, heterogeneous mineral and/or rock matrices; and coal, fly ash, steel slag, minerals, mine-tailings, industrial residues, or rocks. The solid inorganic carbonate compound comprises magnesite, hydrated magnesium carbonates, calcite, vaterite, aragonite, calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate and lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, cesium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate. The surfactant is a cationic surfactant. The solid inorganic bicarbonate compound comprises calcium bicarbonate, magnesium bicarbonate and lithium bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, cesium bicarbonate, ammonium bicarbonate and/or carbonic acid. Preferred Method: The step of reacting the precursor compound with the carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive occurs at a temperature below 100 degrees C, where the reacting occurs at a temperature 25-90 degrees C. The step of reacting the precursor compound with the carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive occurs at a pressure of 0.1-10 atm. The reacting occurs at atmospheric pressure under continuous stirring conditions. The continuous stirring conditions occur at a rate of 250-350 rpm. The reacting occurs for a period of time of 1-15 hours in a single step. The reacting occurs in at least two steps and carbon dioxide capture is decoupled from production of at least one carbonate compound. The carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive is loaded with carbon dioxide. The method further comprises: producing water soluble carbonates via anti-solvent crystallization, where the water soluble carbonates are sodium bicarbonate and potassium bicarbonate; providing at least one additional solvent; regenerating the carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive; capturing carbon dioxide via the carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive under conditions effective to form a carbon dioxide loaded state of the carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive; providing a carbonate compound size modulator, where the size modulator is a surfactant; and producing at least one acid and at least one base by electrochemical decomposition of waste water. The carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive changes from a carbon dioxide loaded state to a carbon dioxide release state upon converting carbon dioxide to at least one solid inorganic carbonate compound. The carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive repeatedly captures gaseous carbon dioxide and releases carbon dioxide to the solid inorganic carbonate compound in a loop process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further comprises: many water soluble carbonates produced via anti-solvent crystallization; at least one acid and at least one base produced by electrochemical decomposition of waste water; at least one pH probe; at least one sample withdrawal port; and at least one temperature sensor, where the reactor controls particle size of the solid inorganic carbonate compound. The reactor comprises a rotating inner chamber. The reactor comprises a fixed outer chamber. The reactor comprises a first inlet, and the first inlet is positioned to accept carbon dioxide loaded capture solvent or capture additive. The reactor comprises a second inlet, and the second inlet is positioned to accept at least one precursor compound. The reactor comprises an outlet, and the outlet comprises an exit stream for the solid inorganic carbonate compound.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive comprises an amine, an amino acid, an amino acid salt, an amine functional group for carbon dioxide capture, or a carboxy functional group for carbon dioxide capture, where, when the carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive comprises an amine, the amine comprising monoethanolamine, piperazine, diethanolamine, 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol and methyldiethanolamine. The carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive comprises an amine, the amine is not monoethanolamine, where, when the carbon dioxide capture solvent or capture additive comprises an amino acid or amino acid salt, the amino acid or amino acid salt is a glycine compound. The glycine compound is glycinate. The glycinate is sodium glycinate or potassium glycinate. The concentration of the amino acid or the amino acid salt is 0.1-20 wt.%. The concentration of the amino acid or the amino acid salt is 0.1-10 M. The concentration of the amine is 0-50 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of experimental materials and methods that do not support the claims.
DC E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN KR2334339-B1
TI Producing catalyst for decomposing carbon dioxide involves preparing solution by adding metal oxide to solvent, mixing rhodium into solution, and drying solvent in mixed solution, where mixing is performed by ball milling,.
AU NAMSUNGCHAN
   JANG J T
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2021D99961
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a catalyst for decomposing carbon dioxide involves preparing a solution by adding a metal oxide to a solvent, mixing rhodium into the solution, and drying the solvent in the mixed solution, where mixing is performed by ball milling, and the metal oxide comprises of metal complex (I). ABO3 Where, the element A is strontium, and its portion contains or does not contain barium, calcium, and magnesium, and the element constituting B is Ferrous, and its portion contains or does not contain zirconium.
   USE - Producing a catalyst for decomposing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides carbon dioxide, which has has an oxygen atom vacancy by reduction process of metal oxide, carbon dioxide can be efficiently decomposed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for disjoint device for disassembling to the carbon monoxide of carbon or the carbon dioxide using the carbon dioxide catalyst for decomposing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide is the Strontium ferrite (SrFeO3) of the perovskite structure. The organic solvent is selected from alcohol, glycol, and carbonate. The hydrogen in which carbon dioxide catalyst for decomposing is dissociated on the surface of rhodium shows the hydrogen outflow (H(sub)2(/sub) spillover). The method is progressed in the continuous flow system of the carbon dioxide where the reaction pressure less than 0.1 barg. The conversion rate disassembled to the carbon monoxide is 80 % or not less than 500-700 degrees C temperature range of the carbon dioxide.

PN CN114259962-A
TI Carbon dioxide conversion device under microwave action comprises carbon dioxide conversion chamber, which is set into conversion chamber upper cavity and conversion chamber lower cavity from top to bottom, and gas distribution structure.
AU MA Y
AE SHANDONG LVMAN SHENZHOU ENVIRONMENTAL (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022580415
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide conversion device under the action of microwave comprises a carbon dioxide conversion chamber, which is set into a conversion chamber upper cavity and a conversion chamber lower cavity from top to bottom. The conversion chamber upper cavity is provided with a material inlet. The conversion chamber lower cavity is provided with a height. The bottom of the conversion chamber lower cavity is provided with a gas distribution structure, which is connected with the carbon dioxide gas inlet source. The top of the cavity on the conversion chamber is provided with a carbon monoxide gas outlet. A microwave heating device is connected with the carbon dioxide conversion chamber. The ratio of the height and the diameter of the lower cavity of the conversion chamber is 4:1-10:1. The ratio of the conversion chamber upper cavity and the conversion chamber lower cavity diameter is 1.5:1-4:1.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide conversion device under the action of microwave.
   ADVANTAGE - The device improves the conversion rate of the carbon dioxide, improves the gas yield of the carbon monoxide gas, and reduces the operation cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide conversion device under the action of microwave comprises a carbon dioxide conversion chamber, which is set into a conversion chamber upper cavity and a conversion chamber lower cavity from top to bottom. The conversion chamber upper cavity is provided with a material inlet. The conversion chamber lower cavity is provided with a height. The bottom of the conversion chamber lower cavity is provided with a gas distribution structure, which is connected with the carbon dioxide gas inlet source. The top of the cavity on the conversion chamber is provided with a carbon monoxide gas outlet. A microwave heating device is connected with the carbon dioxide conversion chamber. The ratio of the height and the diameter of the lower cavity of the conversion chamber is 4:1-10:1. The ratio of the conversion chamber upper cavity and the conversion chamber lower cavity diameter is 1.5:1-4:1. The conversion chamber upper cavity and the conversion chamber lower cavity height ratio is 0.3:1-0.5:1.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for converting carbon dioxide by using the conversion device.

TI Device for electrochemical treatment of organic            wastewater and reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises            membrane reactor, direct current power supply            controller, gas separation system and gas distribution            system.
AU MEI Y
   CHEN J
   ZHAO M
   YAO J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG SHUREN COLLEGE (UNSR-C)
GA 2021E95652
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a membrane reactor, a direct                current power supply controller, a gas separation                system and a gas distribution system. The diaphragm                reactor is consists of an anode chamber, a cathode                chamber and an ion exchange membrane (3). The anode                chamber comprises an anode chamber shell (1), a                first sealing cover (12), an anode (4) and a                stirrer (9). The pressure release valve (10) is                fixed on the first sealing cover. The cathode                chamber comprises a cathode chamber shell (2), a                cathode (5) and a second sealing cover (13). The                cathode chamber shell is hermetically connected                with the second sealing cover. The second sealing                cover is provided with an exhaust port (11). The                gas separation system (14) comprises a carbon                dioxide adsorption chamber (15), an oxygen storage                chamber (16), a carbon dioxide desorption chamber                (17) and a carbon dioxide storage chamber                (18).
   USE - Device for electrochemical treatment of                organic wastewater and reduction of carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces the emission of carbon                dioxide while treating high-concentration organic                wastewater, obtains products with high added value                and realizes the treatment and recycling of the                wastewater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for electrochemical treatment of organic waste                water and reduction of carbon dioxide, which                involves: adding high-concentration organic                wastewater undergoes an electro-oxidation reaction                in the anode chamber of the electrochemical                treatment reaction device, and oxidizing organic                pollutants in the wastewater to carbon dioxide by                the anode, and performing oxygen evolution reaction                to generate oxygen. The carbon dioxide and oxygen                produced in the anode chamber are separated and                collected by the gas separation system,                respectively. The collected oxygen enters the                oxygen storage chamber, and the collected carbon                dioxide enters the cathode chamber through the gas                distribution system, and is electroreduced to                organic matter by the cathode. The current density                of the electro-oxidation and electro-reduction                reactions is 2.5-50 mA/cm2.

TI Producing fuel comprising renewable hydrogen by            removing biogas having methane and carbon dioxide to            give upgraded biogas, transporting upgraded biogas to            hydrogen plant, processing upgraded biogas and            purifying syngas or stream.
AU FOODY P J
AE IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
GA 2022D22585
AB    NOVELTY - Producing fuel comprising renewable hydrogen                and fuel produced from renewable hydrogen,                comprises: providing biogas, where the biogas                comprises methane and carbon dioxide; removing at                least 50% of the carbon dioxide from the biogas,                thus producing upgraded biogas; transporting the                upgraded biogas to a hydrogen plant; processing the                upgraded biogas at the hydrogen plant to produce                syngas, where the processing comprises providing                the transported upgraded biogas as feedstock for                methane reforming, and the syngas comprises carbon                dioxide and hydrogen; and purifying at least one of                the syngas or a stream derived from the syngas to                produce a hydrogen product comprising renewable                hydrogen, and providing a quantity of the renewable                hydrogen for at least one of (i) use as a fuel or                (ii) producing a fuel.
   USE - The method is useful for producing fuel                comprising renewable hydrogen and fuel produced                from renewable hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides renewable hydrogen from                biogas that has a negative carbon intensity and is                transported to at least one hydrogen plant, and                carbon dioxide from both the biogas upgrading and                the hydrogen production is captured and                stored.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing fuel comprising renewable hydrogen                and fuel produced from renewable hydrogen,                comprises: providing biogas, where the biogas                comprises methane and carbon dioxide; removing at                least 50% of the carbon dioxide from the biogas,                thus producing upgraded biogas; transporting the                upgraded biogas to a hydrogen plant; processing the                upgraded biogas at the hydrogen plant to produce                syngas, where the processing comprises providing                the transported upgraded biogas as feedstock for                methane reforming, and the syngas comprises carbon                dioxide and hydrogen; and purifying at least one of                the syngas or a stream derived from the syngas to                produce a hydrogen product comprising renewable                hydrogen, and providing a quantity of the renewable                hydrogen for at least one of (i) use as a fuel or                (ii) producing a fuel, the process further                comprises: (a) capturing and storing a first                quantity of carbon dioxide, the carbon dioxide in                the first quantity removed from the biogas; and (b)                capturing and storing a second quantity of carbon                dioxide, the carbon dioxide in the second quantity                removed from at least one of (i') the syngas or                (ii') a stream at least partially derived from the                syngas, and the quantity of renewable hydrogen has                a carbon intensity less than 0 g carbon                dioxide-eq/MJ, and the carbon intensity is                dependent on steps (a) and (b).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The providing the quantity                  of the renewable hydrogen comprises providing the                  renewable hydrogen for producing a fuel. The                  process comprises providing the quantity of                  renewable hydrogen as a feedstock for a fuel                  production process that comprises hydrogenating                  crude oil derived liquid hydrocarbon with the                  renewable hydrogen to produce the fuel. The                  process comprises providing the quantity of                  renewable hydrogen as a feedstock for a fuel                  production process that comprises hydrogenating a                  renewable feedstock comprising renewable oil                  and/or renewable fat. The quantity of renewable                  hydrogen has a carbon intensity that does not                  exceed -20 g carbon dioxide-eq/MJ. The storing                  the first quantity of carbon dioxide and storing                  the second quantity of carbon dioxide comprises                  geological sequestration. The capturing the                  second quantity of carbon dioxide comprises a                  carbon dioxide removal comprising cryogenic                  separation and vacuum pressure swing adsorption,                  where removing at least 50% of the carbon dioxide                  from the biogas comprises removing 55-90% the                  carbon dioxide from the biogas. The capturing and                  storing a second quantity of carbon dioxide                  comprises separating carbon dioxide from hydrogen                  in the syngas. The carbon dioxide in the second                  quantity of carbon dioxide consists of carbon                  dioxide captured from the syngas. The carbon                  dioxide in the second quantity of carbon dioxide                  comprises carbon dioxide captured from the syngas                  and from the flue gas. The biogas is derived from                  manure comprising swine manure and dairy manure.                  Preferred Components: The second quantity of                  carbon dioxide comprises at least 50% of the                  carbon in the transported upgraded biogas                  provided as feedstock for the methane reforming.                  The transporting the upgraded biogas to the                  hydrogen plant comprises transporting at least a                  portion of the upgraded biogas as a fungible                  batch in a natural gas distribution system. The                  transporting the upgraded biogas to the hydrogen                  plant comprises transporting at least a portion                  of the upgraded biogas as a segregated batch. The                  methane reforming is steam methane reforming, and                  the portion of the upgraded biogas transported as                  a segregated batch is fed to the steam methane                  reforming burners. The second quantity of carbon                  dioxide contains &#8815; 75% of a total                  amount carbon dioxide produced from hydrogen                  production. The biogas is landfill gas or is                  produced from the anaerobic digestion of organic                  waste. The methane reforming is steam methane                  reforming, and at least a portion of heat for the                  methane reforming is produced from low carbon                  electricity. The biogas is from multiple biogas                  sources.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of general                preparation that do not support the claims.

   ROSENSTIEL A
AE DEUT ZENT LUFT & RAUMFAHRT EV (DELF-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Production of organic compound involves (a)                producing a synthesis gas in a thermochemical cycle                process, (b) subsequently converting synthesis gas                into organic compounds in a synthesis process, and                forming a gaseous hydrocarbon (I) as a by-product,                and (c) using the gaseous hydrocarbons in the solar                thermochemical cycle. The product stream is a                mixture of organic compounds (II).
   USE - Production of organic compound from synthesis                gas for producing short-chain hydrocarbon as                by-product, reducing agent or fuel e.g. carbon                dioxide-neutral fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is effective and efficient, enables                production of organic compound capable of recycling                the remaining gaseous by-products, and increasing                the yield of desired product to 58% or less.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of organic compound involves (a)                producing a synthesis gas in a thermochemical cycle                process, (b) subsequently converting synthesis gas                into organic compounds in a synthesis process, and                forming a gaseous hydrocarbon of formula: CnHm(I)                as a by-product, and (c) using the gaseous                hydrocarbons in the solar thermochemical cycle. The                product stream is a mixture of organic compounds of                formula: X-(Z')n'-Y' (II).
   n=1-4;
   m=0-2n+2;
   Z'=(CH ) or (CH=CH);
   n'=2-50, preferably 5-50;
   X,Y'=-CH , -OH, -COOH, -NH , -O-K'W';and
   K'W'=alkyl, aryl, or 1-5C heteroaryl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The solar thermochemical                  cycle process is performed by (a1) providing                  water and/or carbon dioxide, (a2) bringing water                  vapor and/or carbon dioxide into contact with a                  redox material, whereby the redox material is                  oxidized and water and/or carbon dioxide are                  reduced to carbon monoxide and/or hydrogen, (a3)                  separating the synthesis gas, and (a4) reducing                  the redox material and separation of the oxygen.                  The oxidation of the redox material in the step                  (a2) is performed at 1000&#176; C or less,                  preferably 500-950&#176; C. The reduction of                  the redox material in step (a4) is performed at                  900-1200&#176; C, preferably                  1000-1100&#176; C. The method further                  involves thermally utilizing the gaseous                  hydrocarbons in the thermochemical cycle process,                  and storing the gaseous hydrocarbon before                  subjecting to the thermochemical cycle process.                  Preferred Components: The redox material is                  chosen from ferrite, cerium oxide, doped cerium                  oxide, zinc oxide, manganese oxide, lanthanum                  oxide, metal-iron oxide of formula:                  (M1xM2yFe3-x-yO)4(III) and/or mixtures, and/or                  perovskite and brownmillerite comprising metal                  oxide compound of formula:                  A(power(m+))B'(power(n+))Ox(IV). The redox                  material is in the form of monolithic, preferably                  mobile elements.x=0-3;y=0-3 (excluding 0);(x+y)=0-3;M ,M =Cr, Mg , Ca, Mn, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Sr,                  Sn, Ba, Cd or Pb;A(power(m+))=alkali metal, alkaline-earth                  metal or rare-earth metal;andB'(power(n+))=transition metal or main group                  metal.

PN DE202022104257-U1
TI Seal of rotary kiln and heat exchanger of plant            for producing cement clinker, has loose contact seal or            seal forming gap that has housing which is connected to            one of system elements, and housing which has gas            supply line.
AU 
AE KHD HUMBOLDT WEDAG GMBH (KLOH-C)
GA 2022C44557
AB    NOVELTY - The seal (100) has a loose contact seal (40)                or a seal forming a gap. The contact seal or the                seal forming a gap has a housing (45) which is                connected to one of the system elements (10,11).                The housing has a gas supply line (50). The gas                supply line consists of a corrugated pipe                (51).
   USE - Seal of rotary kiln and heat exchanger of                plant for producing cement clinker (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ingress of false air into the kiln system                of the plant for the production of cement clinker,                which is operated under negative pressure is                prevented. The absence of nitrogen ensures that                unnecessary nitrogen compression work is avoided,                so that the energy balance of the manufacturing                process remains economically tolerable. The gas                mixture that consists exclusively of carbon dioxide                and water vapor is created to minimize nitrogen                contamination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a rotary kiln of plant for production of                cement clinker;
   an use of seal for sealing transition between                rotary tube and rotary kiln inlet chamber and/or                between rotary tube and cooler head housing;                and
   a heat exchanger.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the perspective views of                the rotary kiln and seal.
   10,11System elements
   40Loose contact seal
   45Housing
   50Gas supply line
   51Corrugated pipe
   100Seal

TI Metal powder cored welding wire useful in root            welding, filling welding and cover welding of            high-grade steel pipelines comprises carbon, silicon,            manganese, phosphorus, sulfur, 0.01-chromium,            molybdenum and nickel.
AU CAO Y
   ZHANG Y
   YANG Y
   PENG R
   XUAN H
   ZHOU J
   LIU K
   WANG X
AE PIPECHINA GROUP SOUTHWEST PIPELINE CO (PIPE-Non-standard)
   NAT PETROLEUM & NATURAL GAS PIPE NETWORK (NAPE-Non-standard)
GA 2022C4823P
AB    NOVELTY - Metal powder cored welding wire comprises                0.04-0.1 wt.% carbon, 0.2-0.7 wt.% silicon, 1.1-1.4                wt.% manganese, 0.005-0.01 wt.% phosphorus,                0.005-0.01 wt.% sulfur, 0.01-0.05 wt.% chromium,                0.01-0.35 wt.% molybdenum and 1.2-1.9 wt.%                nickel.
   USE - The wire is useful in root welding, filling                welding and cover welding of high-grade steel                pipelines (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal powder core welding wire can adjust                the viscosity of the welding pool, the pushing                force of the electric arc can uniformly push the                melting pool towards two sides of the groove. The                welding seam tissue has a large amount of acicular                ferrite, so as to have excellent mechanical                property. It has low diffusion hydrogen content and                less harmful impurities, at the same time, reducing                the diffusion hydrogen and harmful substance that                can reduce the influence of welding worker health.                It can satisfy for welding process requirement, and                the welding forming quality is very good, the                splashing is far less than the solid welding wire,                adapts to carbon dioxide as the environment of                protecting gas, so to increase the penetration                ability and melting depth of the arc.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for welding long transmission                pipeline.

   BROACH A
   BLUM R
AE AIR-CLENZ SYSTEMS LLC (AIRC-Non-standard)
GA 2022B0548Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an exhaled air collector 4001                with an electronic display screen, where an outer                front surface of the screen acts as a blocking                surface. The surface blocks, deflects, or directs a                mixture of exhaled and room air towards exhaled                suction intakes, where the intakes take 80                percentage or more of the blocked, deflected, or                directed mixture. The intakes move the mixture                towards or into an air purification chamber that                cleans and exhausts the mixture of the air. The                chamber is located behind, located beneath, located                beside, and located above the collector and the                screen.
   USE - Exhaled air collecting and cleaning system for                capturing, isolating, transporting, and destroying                airborne pathogens and contaminants and/or                pollutants.
   ADVANTAGE - The increased ventilation resulting in reduced                pollution, pathogens and lower carbon dioxide in                the air can improve job and/or academic                performance, productivity, and attendance. The                device provides the ability to capture, isolate,                transport, and destroy airborne pathogens and                contaminants and pollutants, and signifies an                improvement over currently available heating,                ventilating, and air-conditioning (HVAC) systems,                HVAC purification systems, and HV AC air handling                systems. The system or device can capture and clean                exhaled air prior to it dispersing within the                room's indoor air.

PT P
PN CN114956197-A
TI Preparation of spherical cobalt carbonate used for            lithium ion battery material anode, involves adding            cobalt solution into ammonium bicarbonate solution,            heating and dispersing, and flowing sodium and cobalt            solutions.
AU ZHANG W
   HUANG Y
   ZHENG J
   LIAO X
   RAN J
AE QINGYUAN JIAZHI NEW MATERIALS RES INST (QING-Non-standard)
   GUANGDONG JIANA ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGXI JIANA ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B5349A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing spherical cobalt                carbonate, involves (a) adding cobalt solution to                ammonium bicarbonate solution to react to obtain a                mixed slurry, (b) heating the mixture and                dispersing the cobalt small core in the mixture,                and (c) simultaneously flowing sodium carbonate                solution and cobalt liquid in the dispersed mixture                to make the cobal carbonate small core grow to form                spherical cobald carbonate. The mixture is a                mixture of cobalt-sulfonate solution, cobalt                chloride solution, ammonium chloride solution or                cobalt dioxide solution.
   USE - Preparation method of spherical cobalt                carbonate used in lithium ion battery material                anode used for manufacturing mobile phone and                notebook computer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively control the shape                of the cobalt carbonate, and can obtain the                non-agglomerated spherical carbonate with different                grain diameters. The precipitated cobalt liquid                substance is simple and easy to process. The method                does not need to add ammonium carbonate solution in                the carbon ammonium solution growth stage, and the                sodium carbonate precipitated carbon, thus the                whole preparation process is less and the                precipitated liquid is less than half of the pure                carbon ion ammonium precipitated system, which can                effectively reduce the production amount of process                carbon dioxide and ammonium bicarbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   a spherical cobalt carbonate; and
   a lithium ion battery anode material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                preparation of spherical cobalt carbonate.
DC E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); ; T01 (Digital Computers); W01 (Telephone and Data Transmission Systems); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PT P
PN IN202211039815-A
TI Battery blast prevention system, has independent            cell that is noncombustible and standalone from device            or automobile battery being monitored, and sensors and            microcontrollers for monitoring temperature and size            variation of battery.
AU AHUJA P
AE AHUJA P (AHUJ-Individual)
GA 2022B2309W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an independent cell (1) that is                noncombustible and standalone from device or                automobile battery being monitored. A three-sided                piezoelectric container (4) contains a highly                pressurized powdered or gaseous fire retardant for                electrical fires. Sensors (2) and microcontrollers                are fixed inside a casing (6) for monitoring                temperature and size variation of a battery. A                tearable film (5) forms a side of the piezoelectric                container. The rupture of the film results in the                release of the retardant from the container and                engulfs the entire battery proactively and                completely with a retardant eliminating the scope                of combustion. The retardant is selected from                carbon dioxide and powdered sodium bicarbonate. The                piezoelectric material in tearable film includes                polymers such as polyvinylidene fluoride                (PVDF).
   USE - Battery blast prevention system used in                vehicle. Can also be used in mobile phones,                remotes, flashlights, electric cars, industrial                helmets and in aerospace industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures that not only circuitry of                the device is protected but it also protects the                user from getting physical injuries. The rupture of                the tear-able film material is avoided and the                change in the battery is sensed by the sensors due                to an increase in temperature or deformation, while                preventing the explosion or any other kind of                damage to the device or automobile.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                battery blast prevention system.
   1Independent cell
   2Sensor
   4Piezoelectric container
   5Tearable film

   USE - The heat-resistant polylactic acid composition                is useful in technical field of biodegradable                polyester.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat-resistant polylactic acid composition                significantly improves crystallization rate and                heat resistance of polylactic acid plastic                products. The raw material source of the polylactic                acid nucleating agent is natural ingredients, which                can be naturally degraded into water and carbon                dioxide, which is non-toxic and harmless to the                human body and environment. The method is simple                and economical, can effectively improve                crystallization rate and crystallinity of                polylactic acid, and enhances the heat resistance                of polylactic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Heat-resistant polylactic acid composition,                comprises 100 pts. wt. polylactic acid, and 0.1-3                pts. wt. polylactic acid nucleating agent of                formula (I).
   M = zinc ion and/or magnesium ion.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing heat-resistant polylactic acid                composition comprising (i) drying polylactic acid                and the polylactic acid nucleating agent in vacuum                oven at 50-80 degrees Celsius for 12-24 hours, and                premixing them after drying, and (ii) placing mixed                material in internal mixer, where internal mixing                temperature is 165-180 degrees Celsius, the                internal mixing time is not less than 10 minutes,                and the rotor speed is 15-30 revolutions/minute to                obtain heat-resistant polylactic acid composition,                where mass ratio of the polylactic acid to the                polylactic acid nucleating agent is                100:0.1-3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: (I) is prepared by (a)                  preparing mixed solution of ethanol-distilled                  water, and adding mixed solution into the first                  reaction vessel and the second reaction vessel in                  2 pts. wt., (b) adding metal carboxylate into the                  first reaction vessel at 10-30 degrees Celsius,                  and dissolving it completely under the action of                  mechanical stirring, (c) dissolving mandelic acid                  in second reaction vessel, (d) slowly adding the                  mixed solution in the second container dropwise                  into the first reaction container, heating up to                  75-90 degrees Celsius, and refluxing for 5-8                  hours, (e) cooling to room temperature, suction                  filtering under reduced pressure, and washing the                  filter cake with absolute ethanol, and vacuum                  drying at room temperature to obtain a white                  powder i.e. (I).Preferred Components: The particle size of                  the polylactic acid nucleating agent is not                  greater than 100 nm. The metal carboxylates are                  at least one of zinc and magnesium acetates,                  oxalates, malonate, succinate, glutarates and                  adipates. The molar ratio of the mandelic acid to                  the metal carboxylate is 2.1:1-3:1. The volume                  ratio of the ethanol to distilled water is                  15:1-40:1, and mass ratio of metal carboxylate to                  ethanol-water mixed solution is 0.01-0.03. The                  mass ratio of the ethanol-distilled water mixed                  solution divided into the first reactor and the                  ethanol-distilled water mixed solution divided                  into the second reactor is 6:1-15:1. The                  configuration of the mandelic acid is at least                  one of L-, D- and D, L-.Preferred Composition: Heat-resistant                  polylactic acid composition, comprises 100 pts.                  wt. polylactic acid, and 0.3-1 pts. wt.                  polylactic acid nucleating agent (I).

PN CN114164682-A
TI Preparing solvent-free polyurethane synthetic leather comprises e.g. mixing hydroxy component, isocyanate component, water and catalyst, reacting to obtain bubble-containing melt, coating bubble-containing melt on first substrate and attaching second substrate, and curing.
AU BAI X
   WU L
   CAI H
   QIAN J
   QIAN H
AE SHANGHAI HUIDE SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202242165V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing solvent-free polyurethane synthetic leather comprises (1) mixing hydroxy component, isocyanate component, water and catalyst, and reacting in a reactor with a stirring device at 80-100degrees Celsius to obtain a bubble-containing melt, where the bubble-containing melt contains not only urethane, substituted urea and carbon dioxide, the reaction product of isocyanate, hydroxy, and water, but also remaining unreacted hydroxy, isocyanate, and water, and (2) coating the bubble-containing melt on the first substrate with coating thickness of 0.1-3 mm, maintaining the temperature of the coating knife at 80-100degrees Celsius, after the coating is completed and attaching the second substrate, and then curing at 120-140degrees Celsius to obtain the solvent-free polyurethane synthetic leather.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing solvent-free polyurethane synthetic leather.
   ADVANTAGE - The method coats a bubble-containing melt with higher viscosity, the body strength of the bubble-containing melt is high, the cell wall strength is high, the thickness loss after coating is also less, and is not easy to collapse the foam during the foaming reaction, which is conducive to the formation of more and larger cells and has strong sensuality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for product, which is solvent-free polyurethane synthetic leather prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The mixing and reacting is as follows: reacting for 3-5 minutes to obtain a bubble-containing melt with a viscosity of 40,000-100,000 mPa.s/80degrees Celsius, and when the temperature of the bubble-containing melt is 80-100degrees Celsius, cross-linking and solidifying into an insoluble and infusible solid within 20-30 minutes.Preferred Components: The first substrate is release paper, and the thickness of the aqueous polyurethane resin film attached to the release paper is 0.01-0.1 mm, or the thickness of the TPU film is 0.03-0.2 mm. The second substrate is base cloth for microfiber, genuine leather, or synthetic leather. The reactor with a stirring device is a reactor with a screw extrusion device, preferably single-screw extruder and twin-screw extruder. The coating knife is a coating knife covered with polytetrafluoroethylene material. The molar ratio of NCO in the isocyanate component to the hydroxy component and hydroxide in water (calculated by consuming 2 moles of NCO per 1 mole of water), i.e., the R value is 1.05-1.3. The ratio of the mass of the water and the mass of the hydroxy component is 0.1-1.5:100. The ratio of the catalyst mass and the hydroxy component mass is 0.04-0.2:100. The hydroxy components are: the number average molecular weight of polyoxypropylene diol is 1000-4000, the number average molecular weight of polyoxypropylene triol is 1000-4000, the number average molecular weight of polytetrahydrofuran diol is 1000-2000, the number-average molecular weight of polyester polyol is 1000-3000 (the hydroxy functionality of the polyester polyol is 2-3), and/or the number-average molecular weight of polycarbonate diols is 1000-2000, and mixture of small-molecule diols in ethylene glycol, butanediol, and/or diethylene glycol, the mass of polyols with hydroxy functionality greater than 2 accounts for 10-40 wt.% of the hydroxy components, the mass of small-molecule diols accounts for 5-15 wt.% of the hydroxy components, the polyester polyol with hydroxy functionality of 2-3 is an adipic acid type polyester polyol, by adipic acid and small molecule diols in ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol, butylene glycol, methyl propylene glycol, and/or methyl pentylene glycol are reacted, and prepared by adding trimethylolpropane. The isocyanate content in the isocyanate component is 6-15 wt.%, the isocyanate component is composed of polytetrahydrofuran diol with number average molecular weight of 1000-2000, polyoxypropylene glycol with number average molecular weight of 1000-4000, data molecular weight of 1000-2000 polyester diol or number average molecular weight of 1000-2000 polycarbonate diol and/or and MDI, and the carbodiimide-uretonimine modified MDI is prepared by reaction, where the ratio of the mass of the carbodiimide-uretonimine modified MDI and the mass of the MDI is 5-15:100. The catalyst is mixture of metal catalyst, foaming catalyst, and thermosensitive catalyst. The mass ratio of the foaming catalyst and the metal catalyst is 20-50:100. The mass ratio of the thermosensitive catalyst and the metal catalyst is 10-30:100. The metal catalyst is mixture of bismuth carboxylate and zinc carboxylate in a mass ratio of 0.125-0.25:1. The foaming catalyst is bis(dimethylaminoethyl) ether, pentamethyldiethylenetriamine, trimethylhydroxyethylethylenediamine, and/or N,N,N'-trimethyl-N'-hydroxyethylbisaminoethyl ether. The thermosensitive catalyst is 1,8-diazabicyclo-bicyclo(5,4,0)-7-undecene, N,N',N''-trimethylaminoethylpiperazine, and/or N-MethylN'-hydroxyethylpiperazine.

AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting design data of high sulfur-containing collecting and conveying pipeline, medium component and basic parameter of operation data. A corrosion rate is calculated in the high sulfur-containing collection and transportation pipeline based on the basic parameter collected. An association rule between the corrosion rate and a sensitive parameter of a corrosion main control factor is established. The corrosion sensitive parameter map of the high sulfur containing collection and transport pipeline is drawn based on an association rule determined. A value of the main control factors is read from a specific position in the pipeline from a line distribution map. The value of a main controlfactor is substituted at the specific position of the pipeline to determine the corrosion level of a high sulfur carrying transportation pipeline.
   USE - Method for evaluating corrosion sensitive parameter such as temperature, hydrogen sulfide content, carbon dioxide content and other corrosion sensitive factors of high sulphur-containing gathering pipeline.

PN WO2021247587-A1
TI Waste processing device comprises ion generator, furnace chamber, heat exchanger, pollution control system, and chimney.
AU WEBB T R
   CHAN Y
AE WEBB T R (WEBB-Individual)
   CHAN Y (CHAN-Individual)
GA 2021E0401F
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a furnace chamber (104) configured to thermally decay the waste by combining the waste with a product of an interaction of the ionized gas and heat generated by the furnace chamber. The furnace chamber transfer excess gas of the product to the heat exchanger. A heat exchanger (106) configured to cool the excess gas and transfer the cooled excess gas to the pollution control system (108). The pollution control system comprises a wet scrubber system (112) configured to remove heavy metals and acid gases from the cooled excess gas to generate scrubbed excess gas. A fixed bed coke system (114) configured to detoxify the scrubbed excess gas by converting carbon monoxide, water, and steam in the scrubbed excess gas to carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and removing remaining acid gas, a remaining heavy metal, and a remaining dioxin from the scrubbed excess gas. The chimney (110) configured to transfer remaining scrubbed excess gas that is detoxified out of the device.
   USE - Device for processing waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The device effectively processes the waste in a simple and cost effective manner, and prevents the formation of dioxin, furan, nitrogen oxide, and sulfur oxide in the waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for processing waste.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the device for processing waste.
   Waste processing device (100)
   Furnace chamber (104)
   Heat exchanger (106)
   Pollution control system (108)
   Chimney (110)
   Wet scrubber system (112)
   Fixed bed coke system (114)

PN CN113639933-A
TI Air leakage detection method, involves connecting protective sealing door with door frame and door plate, attaching periphery of door frame to wall body, fixing door plate in door frame, and checking position and degree of color change on seal strip.
AU LIU C
   LIANG Z
   ZENG L
   LIN Z
   LIU X
AE GUANGXI KENING CIVIL AIR DEFENSE ENG PRO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D3124N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves connecting a protective sealing door with a door frame and a door plate. A periphery of the door frame is attached to a wall body. The door plate is fixed in door frame. An exhaust pipe is opened when the door board is closed. A reaction gas is injected into a chamber through an air inlet pipe. The reaction gas can react with a powder color changing agent on a sealing strip so that a corresponding position of the sealing strip changes color. A position and degree of the color change on the seal strip are checked to determine a position of air leakage. Determination is made to check whether a gap leaks air and an air leakage position.
   USE - Air leakage detection method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method accurately reflects the leakage position of the protective sealing door and its structure. The powder color changing agent is easy to change color with oxygen or carbon dioxide. The method is convenient for the operator to analyze. The sealing strip is pre-assembled with adhesive. The reaction gas is convenient to prepare. Separating storing sealing strip and powder color-changing agent helps to prolong storage period. The gas leakage detection method can accurately reflect the position of air leakage.

AB    NOVELTY - The method (220) involves capturing a rechargeable alkaline manganese (RAM) cathode of an electrochemical system decay product and using the decay product as an oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) catalyst in an air electrode. PN WO2021160235-A1; AU2020429156-A1; CN115038814-A; KR2022141815-A; CA3167827-A1
TI Production of oxygen-containing gas product            involves subjecting anode raw gas to catalytic            conversion of hydrogen to water while obtaining            intermediate mixture depleted in hydrogen.
AU PESCHEL A
   HENTSCHEL B
   PESCHELL A
   HENZSCHEL B
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 202195716H
AB    NOVELTY - Production of oxygen-containing gas product                involves subjecting a feedstock (1) containing                water to electrolysis (E) to obtain a raw anode gas                (2), which is rich in oxygen and containing                hydrogen, and a raw cathode gas, which is low in                oxygen and rich in hydrogen, partially subjecting                the raw anode gas to a catalytic conversion (C) of                hydrogen to water to obtain a mixture (4) with                depleted hydrogen content, returning a portion (p1)                of the mixture (4) to the raw anode gas downstream                of the electrolysis (E) and upstream of the                catalytic conversion (C), and using at least a                portion (p2) of the mixture (4).
   USE - Production of oxygen-containing gas product                e.g. water, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen-rich gas                used in air separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that undesirably high                hydrogen concentrations are not reached, and                reduces the concentration of hydrogen in the                corresponding parts of the system. The hydrogen                concentration is reduced in such a way that an                adiabatic temperature increase in the catalytic                conversion is limited to a desired value. The                cost-intensive use of an isothermal reactor concept                can therefore be dispensed with.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of the production of oxygen-containing gas                product.
   Feedstock (1)
   Anode raw gas (2)
   Mixture (4)
   Gas product (10)
   Residual gas (12)

TI Breathing device for use in magnetic resonance chamber inspection, has breathing valve whose valve blocks air supply port under expiratory pressure when patient exhales, where exhalation port is opened when patient exhales.
AU CUI L
   ZHOU L
   ZHU R
   HUANG R
   SHAO M
AE UNIV ANHUI MEDICAL FIRST AFFILIATED (AFHP-C)
GA 2021964513
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a simple breathing device used in magnetic resonance chamber inspection, comprising an oxygen storage bag, a gas storage valve, an air inlet valve, an air bag, a duckbill valve, an air pipe; connecting pipe respiratory valve; one end of the respiratory valve is connected with the connecting pipe; the other end is used for connecting with the respiratory system of the patient; the respiratory valve is formed with a gas supply port and a exhalation port; the air supply port is intercepted on the respiratory valve; the exhalation port is connected with the outside from the side of the respiratory valve; when supplying gas for the patient; The valve of the breather valve blocks the exhalation port under the air supply pressure, and the air supply port is opened; when the patient exhales, the valve of the breather valve blocks the air supply port under the expiratory pressure, and the exhalation port is opened. The device has simple structure, convenient use, and it not only can ensure enough oxygen conveying, but also can avoid carbon dioxide retention caused by long breathing pipe, it gives attention to partial respirator function, at the same time, it avoids the problem that the transport respirator is expensive and cannot enter into the magnetic resonance chamber to check; it has two advantages, it is economical and practical.

PT P
PN TW734614-B1; TW202211802-A
TI Fresh-keeping device for use in fresh-keeping system, has raw material storage area including gas escape port, where carbonic acid-based material is placed in raw material storing area that passes through ice storage area opening.
AU KAO Y
   CHAI H
   CHEN J
AE FISHERIES RES INST (FISH-Non-standard)
GA 2021D9472P
AB    NOVELTY - The present invention is related to a freshness preserving apparatus having an ice storing section and a raw material storing section. The said ice storing section comprises a storing opening and an ice material is preserved inside the ice storing section. The raw material storing section comprises a gas releasing opening and an alkaline material is preserved inside the raw material storing section. When the freshness preserving apparatus of the present invention is placed in a closed environment, temperature changes of the said closed environment will cause the ice material to dissolve and transform into liquid. The liquid will further flow into the raw material storing section and react with the alkaline material producing carbon dioxide gas (CO 2). The CO 2gas escapes from the gas releasing opening of the raw material storing section to fill the whole closed environment. If the closed environment has stored with fresh foods, the CO 2gas could create a freshness preserving environment for the said fresh foods. The present invention is successfully turned the concept of controlled atmosphere storage into a modular apparatus for commercially uses. By the transforming of liquid and ice of the ice material at different temperatures, the amount and time of CO 2gas can be adjusted to provide a long-term and continuous preservation effect for the fresh foods.

PN CN112679717-A
TI Preparation of multi-purpose self-emulsifying            anionic unsaturated polyester carbon fiber sizing agent            by cooling unsaturated polyester reactant, heating with            double-end epoxy resin and tetrabutylammonium bromide            and adding triethylamine.
AU YANG C
   WANG X
AE JILIN QIANREN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 2021492640
AB    NOVELTY - A multi-purpose self-emulsifying anionic                unsaturated polyester carbon fiber sizing agent is                prepared by adding binary fatty carboxylic acid/or                anhydride, alicyclic dibasic acid/or anhydride,                dihydric alcohol monomer and maleic                anhydride/maleic acid to four-mouth flask with                mechanical stirrer, thermometer, water separator                and reflux condenser, polycondensation reacting                under nitrogen or carbon dioxide protection,                heating to 200 degrees C until polyester acid value                is reduced to 39-40.5 mgKOH/g, cooling unsaturated                polyester reactant to 30 degrees C, adding                double-end epoxy resin and tetrabutylammonium                bromide (TBAB), continuously heating reaction to                110 degrees C until acid value is reduced to 10                mgKOH/g, adding methyltetrahydrophthalic anhydride                (MTHPA) into flask, slowly heating to 130 degrees                C, cooling to 25 degrees C, continuously stirring                and adding triethylamine (TEA) for 30 minutes,                dropping deionized water into four-mouth flask, and                stirring for 60 minutes.
   USE - The method is used for preparing multi-purpose                self-emulsifying anionic unsaturated polyester                carbon fiber sizing agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                application of multi-purpose self-emulsifying                anionic unsaturated polyester sizing agent in                sizing modification of carbon fiber surface                comprising drying carbon fiber at 110-170 degrees C                after sizing, free radical grafting reacting double                bond of self-emulsifying anionic unsaturated                polyester with carbon fiber interface,                self-crosslinking reacting, and improving interface                performance of carbon fiber/vinyl ester resin                composites.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The binary fatty                  carboxylic acid/anhydride is 1,4-succinic acid,                  succinic anhydride, 1,6-adipic acid and                  1,10-decanedioic acid. The alicyclic dibasic                  acid/or anhydride is methyltetrahydrophthalic                  anhydride, methylhexahydrophthalic anhydride and                  hexahydrophthalic anhydride. The molar ratio of                  binary aliphatic carboxylic acid/anhydride to                  alicyclic dibasic acid/or anhydride to maleic                  anhydride/maleic acid is 3-7:1. The dihydric                  alcohol monomer is ethylene glycol, diethylene                  glycol, triethylene glycol, propylene glycol,                  dipropylene glycol, tripropylene glycol,                  butanediol, hexanediol, neopentyl glycol and                  1,4-cyclohexanediol. The double-end epoxy resin                  is bisphenol A type epoxy resin, bisphenol F type                  epoxy resin, hydrogenated bisphenol A type epoxy                  resin, hydrogenated bisphenol F type epoxy resin                  and 3,4-epoxycyclohexylcarboxylic acid-3 ,4                  -epoxycyclohexyl methyl ester. The                  self-emulsifying anionic unsaturated polyester is                  viscous liquid polymer at room temperature, and                  has glass transition temperature of lower than -                  20 degrees C and solid content of 40%.

PN CN112626029-A
TI New transgenic modified Daudi cell useful as            inactivated transgenic modified Daudi cells as            trophoblasts for natural killer cell expansion and            culturing, prepared by e.g. obtaining reconstructed            plasmid vector and recombinant lentivirus.
AU GUO Z
   ZHANG D
   GE S
   XIE S
AE SHENZHEN CELL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
   BEIJING CLIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202142175D
AB    NOVELTY - Transgenic modified Daudi cell, preferably                modified with a target gene fragment which                expresses mbIL-21, is new.
   USE - The transgenic modified Daudi cell is useful                as inactivated transgenic modified Daudi cells as                trophoblasts for natural killer cell expansion and                culturing (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Daudi cells produces pure natural killer                cells with an amplification factor of over 1500 and                frequency of CD3CD16+CD56+ of more than 90%, and a                cytotoxic effect on K562 or other cancer cell                lines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing transgenic modified Daudi cell;                and
   (2) application of transgenic modified Daudi                cell as inactivated transgenic modified Daudi cells                as trophoblasts for natural killer cell expansion                and culturing, comprising adding mononuclear cells                and trophoblast cells into the natural killer cell                culture medium and co-culturing at 37 degrees C and                5% carbon dioxide according to the number ratio of                mononuclear cells: trophoblast cells = 1:0.25-5 and                co-culturing comprises adding natural killer cell                culture medium in equal volume every two days until                the cell density is greater than A, when the cell                density is greater than A, the total number of                cells: trophoblasts = 1-10:1 ratio of supplementary                trophoblasts and adding natural killer cell culture                medium in equal volume every day until the 21st day                and stopping where A is 6x 105 cells/ml.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  transgenic modified Daudi cells comprises (i)                  inserting the target gene fragment into the                  plasmid to obtain the reconstructed plasmid                  vector; (ii) recombining the reconstructed                  plasmid vector into a lentivirus to obtain a                  recombinant lentivirus; and (iii) transfecting                  Daudi cells by the recombinant lentivirus to                  obtain transgenic modified Daudi cells, where the                  target gene fragment comprises a nucleotide                  sequence and/or a corresponding complementary                  sequence for expressing mbIL-21, where the                  nucleotide sequence used to express mbIL-21                  comprises fully defined 1236 nucleotide sequences                  as shown in (SEQ ID NO:1). Preferred Method: In                  the step (ii), the specific recombination method                  of the lentivirus comprises adding Dulbecco's                  Modified Eagle Medium (DMEM) complete medium into                  the logarithmic growth phase of the lentivirus at                  a concentration of 0.1-0.8x 106 cells/ml, mixing,                  placing it in a 37 degrees C incubator until the                  confluence of the lentiviral cells reaches 60-70%                  to obtain the lentivirus suspension, adding the                  reconstituted plasmid vector into the lentivirus                  suspension in an amount of 1-6 mu g/ml, mixing                  well, placing it in a 37 degrees C (5%) carbon                  dioxide incubator for 6-12 hours, discarding the                  old DMEM complete medium, adding fresh DMEM                  complete medium, incubating for 24-72 hours and                  collecting the supernatant. In the step (iii),                  the specific transfection method of the Daudi                  cell comprises adding serum-free medium into                  Daudi cells at a concentration of 0.2-5x 106                  cells/ml, adding the recombinant lentivirus,                  mixing and culturing in a 37 degrees C (5%)                  carbon dioxide incubator for 24-72 hours to                  obtain transgenic modified Daudi cells. In the                  step (iii), the method comprises forming added                  recombinant lentivirus by concentrating the                  supernatant, adding 1-10 times the volume of DMEM                  complete medium to resuspend, where the specific                  concentration method of the supernatant comprises                  filtering the supernatant into a centrifuge tube,                  centrifuging at 10000-12000 revolutions/minute                  for 5-20 minutes and collecting the precipitate,                  where the volume ratio between the recombinant                  lentivirus and the serum-free medium is 1:3-50                  and the gene expression of the transgenic                  modified Daudi cells is above 80%. The                  inactivation method of the transgenic modified                  Daudi cells comprises irradiating with gamma                  -rays at a dose of 50-200 Grays for 15-40 minutes                  or selecting mitomycin (C) with a concentration                  of 10-200 mg/ml for 30-150 minutes, where the                  mononuclear cells are mononuclear cells isolated                  from peripheral blood or cord blood. Preferred                  Components: The target gene fragment also                  expresses at least one of Interleukin (IL)-15,                  IL-21, 4.1-BBL, Cluster of Differentiation (CD)                  86, CD314, CD16, IL-18, IL-12 or CD14. In the                  step (i), the plasmid is pCDH-WA6299, where the                  reconstructed plasmid vector comprises fully                  defined 8982 nucleotide sequences as shown in                  (SEQ ID NO: 2) and the lentivirus is 293FT                  cells.

PN WO2021048849-A1; US2022344674-A1
TI Aerogel catalyst used e.g. as oxygen-reduction            reaction catalyst, in fuel cells, and lithium-air            batteries comprises central transition metal complex            having macrocyclic compounds as ligands and            crosslinkers use in oxygen-reduction reactions.
AU ELBAZ L
   ZION N
AE UNIV BAR-ILAN (UYLB-C)
   UNIV BAR-ILAN (UYLB-C)
GA 202126858M
AB    NOVELTY - Aerogel catalyst comprises a central                transition metal complex comprising macrocyclic                compounds as ligands and cross-linkers for use in                oxygen-reduction reactions.
   USE - The catalyst is useful as oxygen-reduction                reaction catalyst, in fuel cells, lithium-air                batteries, carbon dioxide reduction reaction,                electrolyzes and sensors (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: provides highly active                atomically dispersed oxygen-reduction catalyst with                ultra-high catalytic site density and metal                content; has having high site density and large                surface area; and has high catalytic performance,                while preventing aggregation of macrocyclic                compounds in reaction mixture prior to their                polymerization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing aerogel catalyst comprising (a) heating a                reaction mixture at 60-160 degrees C to obtain a                gel, the reaction mixture comprising a solution                comprising monomers of pyrrolic or a pyridinic                compound and transition metal ions, where the ratio                between the metal ions and the monomer in the                reaction mixture is 7:1-10:1, and a solution                comprising a cross-linker, the molar ratio between                the amino of the monomer to the aldehyde of the                crosslinker is 1:1-1:4, washing the gel and (c)                supercritically drying the gel using high pressure                drying liquid to obtain an aerogel and (d)                subjecting the aerogel to heat treatment of at                least 500 degrees C for at least 1 hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aerogel catalyst                  has a catalytic site density of 1x 1019 to 1x                  1020 sites cm3. The transition metal comprises                  iron, cobalt, copper, nickel, vanadium,                  manganese, chromium, ruthenium, rhodium, iridium,                  osmium, rhenium, molybdenum and/or tungsten. The                  macrocyclic compound is porphyrin,                  phthalocyanine, phenanthroline, corrole or                  bipyridine. The aerogel catalyst has a metal                  content of 2-9 wt.%. The aerogel catalyst has a                  Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of                  150-700 m2/g. The catalytic site density is 4x                  1019 sites cm 3. The metal content is 6.5-9.0                  wt.%. The crosslinker is terephthalaldehyde                  dissolved in dimethyl sulfoxide. The washing gel                  comprises exchanging the solvent of the gel to a                  washing liquid. The prior to exchanging the                  solvent of the gel to a washing liquid, the gel                  is immersed in a fresh solvent of the same type                  as the solvent used to dissolve the metal ions,                  monomers and crosslinker, once daily for four                  days or until no residues of unreacted molecules                  can be detected in the solvent. The washing                  liquid comprises acetone, methanol, ethanol or                  isopropanol. The drying liquid is liquid carbon                  dioxide. The heat treatment is low temperature                  heat treatment (LTHT) at a temperature of                  500-1000 degrees C. Preferred Process: The step                  (a) comprises (i) heating a reaction mixture                  comprising a solvent, monomers of a pyrrolic or a                  pyridinic compound and transition metal ions at                  about 60-160 degrees C for 20-60 minutes, (ii)                  adding a solution of a cross-linker to the                  reaction mixture, and (iii) heating the reaction                  mixture containing the crosslinker at 60-160                  degrees C to obtain a gel. The final                  concentration of the metal ions in the reaction                  mixture is 0.25-0.3 M. The final concentration of                  the monomer in the reaction mixture is 10-200                  mg/ml. The pyrrolic or the pyridinic compound is                  in the form of a free base or a hydroxyl                  analogue. The solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide. The                  LTHT is carried at a temperature of 800 degrees                  C.
   EXAMPLE - 15 mg Iron(ll) chloride tetrahydrate were                dissolved in 250 ml dimethyl sulfoxide, followed by                the addition of the monomer for the polymerization,                the monomer being free base porphyrin, 10 mg                5,10,15,20-(tetra-4-aminophenyl)porphyrin. For the                purpose of metal insertion process, the solution                was heated to 80 degrees C and stirred for 30                minutes. To form the gel, a solution containing                terephthalaldehyde as the cross-linker dissolved in                dimethyl sulfoxide with a 1:2 molar ratio of amide                to aldehyde. 250 ml of the crosslinker solution was                added dropwise to the porphyrin-iron solution. The                new solution was heated to 80 degrees C and stirred                for 10 seconds, after which the solution was                incubated at 80 degrees C overnight. Once formed,                the gel was washed by immersing it in fresh                dimethyl sulfoxide once a day, for four consecutive                days, to remove unreacted chemicals until no                residues were seen in the solvent above the gel.                Then, the gel was washed with acetone in order to                replace the dimethyl sulfoxide in the pores.                Finally, the gel was dried using carbon dioxide                under supercritical conditions in a critical-point                dryer to obtain aerogel catalyst.

PN WO2021050369-A1
TI Autonomous comfort system for use in building has processor that analyzes registered image to determine values of temperatures at temperature points, and determines adjustments to operation of comfort device based on temperature values.
AU GHAHRAMANI A
   LEVINSON R M
   MIN S K
   CHEN K
   WANG A Y
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 202126854A
AB    NOVELTY - The autonomous comfort system includes a visible camera that produces a visible image. An infrared camera produces an infrared image as a temperature map. One processor executes code to cause the processor to perform image registration between the visible image and the temperature map to produce a registered image having facial features with temperature points. The processor analyzes the registered image to determine values of temperatures at the temperature points, and determines adjustments to operation of a comfort device based upon the values of the temperatures. The operation of the comfort device is also adjusted.
   USE - Autonomous comfort system for providing acceptable thermal environment in building.
   ADVANTAGE - The system may also use pose estimation to filter images and improve the accuracy of comfort prediction. Providing a weak air movement around the breathing zone significantly reduces the carbon dioxide concentration in the breathing zone of an occupant, improving the well-being and productivity. Only when the system identifies facial landmarks in the image will the server initiate image registration. This will prevent unnecessary computations from being performed in the absence of an occupant in the visible camera s field of vision.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the autonomous comfort system.
   Ronotic system setup (10)
   Client (12)
   Fish-eye camera (14)
   Cloud server (16)
   Stepper motor (32)

TI Refinery retrofitted with hydro-processing (HDP)            catalyst recovery system, has sulfur treatment process            (STP) that is configured to combine first and second            gas streams, and to remove sulfur dioxide and hydrogen            sulfide from combined stream.
AU SEAMANS J
   MCHUGH T
   VISIOLI B
AE POROCEL INT LLC (PORO-Non-standard)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 2021279451
AB    NOVELTY - The refinery (100) has several sulfur waste                treating units that are configured to heat the                sulfur-bearing waste, which produces a first gas                stream comprising sulfur dioxide. Several                hydrocarbon treating processes produce a second gas                stream that comprises hydrogen sulfide. A sulfur                treatment process (STP) is configured to combine                the first and second gas streams, and to remove                sulfur dioxide and hydrogen sulfide from the                combined streams. The sulfur waste treating units                are selected from the group consisting of moving                belt regenerators, fluidized beds, rotolouvers,                rotary calciners, and rotary kilns. The first gas                stream further comprises carbon dioxide, water, and                nitrogen.
   USE - Refinery retrofitted with hydro-processing                (HDP) catalyst recovery system.
   ADVANTAGE - The additional sulfur recovery unit (SRU)                capability is used to offload existing SRU                workload, which increases the overall capacity of                the refinery. The roasting process removes most of                the sulfur and carbon from the catalyst, and                eliminates any self-heating hazards associated with                the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Description: An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included                for a method of retrofitting a processing plant to                configure the processing plant to treat a                sulfur-bearing waste.

TI Fuel cell catalyst support pressurized production method involves heat-treating acetylene black support, and making acetylene black support into fuel cell catalyst support comprised with specific surface area of determined value.
AU KI J H
   KIM S
   SUNG M
AE VINATECH CO LTD (VINA-Non-standard)
GA 202118585H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves additionally heat-treating the acetylene black support at a set temperature and time to adjust the component ratio of the skeleton portion and the base portion. The acetylene black support is placed in a rotating cylinder and the rotating cylinder is rotated at 1 to 100 rpm and the component ratio of the skeleton portion and the base portion adjusted in the second step is not changed. The acetylene black support is rotated, while continuously moved and rotated up and down in the cylinder. A 100% carbon dioxide atmosphere of 1.5 to 10 atmospheres and 90% water vapor atmosphere of 1.5 to 5 atmospheres is created in the rotating cylinder, and the acetylene black support is heat-treated at a temperature range of 600 degrees C to 1100 degrees C in the rotating cylinder. AU DENG T
