PN WO2020247886-A1; US2020386155-A1; US11193421-B2
TI Method for increasing efficiency of combustion in turbine, involves compressing reduced temperature turbine compressor inlet oxygen containing stream efficient than compressing oxygen containing stream to operating pressure of turbine.
AU YOUNES M
   HARALE A
   DUVAL S
   SHABBIR G
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2020C30083
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves expanding in an expansion unit a pressurized fluid stream to form an expanded, cooled fluid stream. The heat between an oxygen containing stream and the expanded, cooled fluid stream is exchanged to reduce temperature of the oxygen containing stream to a reduced temperature and create a reduced temperature turbine compressor inlet oxygen containing stream. The reduced temperature turbine compressor inlet oxygen containing stream is compressed to an operating pressure of the turbine, where the step of compressing the reduced temperature turbine compressor inlet oxygen containing stream is more efficient than compressing the oxygen containing stream. A fuel in a combustion chamber of the turbine is combusted using the reduced temperature turbine compressor inlet oxygen containing stream. The pressurized fluid stream comprises compressed carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for increasing power efficiency of combustion in turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The reduction of the overall compressor inlet air temperature increases compressor and turbine efficiency and reduces emissions. The compression energy required decreases as colder air is compressed, and thus, the overall efficiency of the system increases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for increasing power from or efficiency of combustion in a turbine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system exhibiting expanded, cooled fluid recycle to control inlet air temperature in a gas turbine.
   Carbon capture system (100)
   Turbine system (102)
   Heat exchange unit (108)
   Combustion chamber (116)
   Fuel inlet (120)
DC Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M))
MC Q52-A03

AU NOWOTNI D
AE DAIMLER AG (DAIM-C)
GA 2020317177
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring one engine oil pressure (p) at oil temperature (T) on a component (20) of an internal combustion engine (10) supplied with the engine oil. The parameter value which characterizes at least one state of the engine oil is determined, based on the at least one deviation.    USE - Method for checking engine oil during operation of internal combustion engine for motor vehicle.

The pulse width modulation signal and the pump speed represent variables are measured particularly simply and precisely. TI Method for determining whether manhole event has            occurred for underground utilities, involves obtaining            sensor reading from a sensor installed inside            underground vault, and removing sensor drift when            sensor reading includes sensor drift.
AU BERTINI G J
   ARIGALA S G
   FORD W P C
   ROBERT J R
AE NOVINIUM INC (NOVI-Non-standard)
GA 2020301653
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining a sensor reading                from a sensor (128) installed inside an underground                vault (112), determining whether the sensor reading                is indicative of an alarm state, obtaining multiple                confirmatory measurements and determining whether                the sensor reading includes sensor drift based at                least in portion on the multiple confirmatory                measurements when the sensor reading is indicative                of the alarm state, establishing the alarm state                when the sensor reading is determined not to                include sensor drift, and removing the sensor drift                when the sensor reading is determined to include                sensor drift.
   USE - Method for determining whether a manhole event                has occurred for underground utilities, such as                water, sewer, natural gas, electricity, telephone,                cable, and steam.
   ADVANTAGE - Since drift is independent of dilution,                actively altering the dilution rate can test the                hypothesis that the alarm condition is the result                of a sudden drift. Regarding determining an amount                of water being transported from the vault by                evaporation, there are reliability and convenience                benefits in keeping manholes dry. Carbon dioxide,                propane, and butane are examples of materials that                are safely and easily stored as liquids.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for determining whether a manhole event has                occurred.

   KLEE G

   WEISS TECH GMBH (WEIS-Non-standard)
GA 202026656E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adding components to a container (11) in the ratio of their respective mass fractions to the refrigerant. The refrigerant blend is formed in the container. The temperature and the pressure in the container are set by a control device (21) such that the refrigerant is present exclusively in the gas phase or exclusively in the liquid phase. The components are configured to remain constant during removal of refrigerant from the container without further components to be fed to the container. The refrigerant is comprised of a relative carbon dioxide equivalent.
   USE - Method for providing zeotropic refrigerants.
   ADVANTAGE - The component is no longer necessary to add with extra effort, and a much more precise blend composition of the refrigerant can be achieved on removal from the container. Dispatching and transporting of the cooling circuit is made easier, since the cooling circuit can be filled with the refrigerant before transported, regardless of the type of transport. Demixing of the refrigerant when the refrigerant is removed from the container can be prevented.

   (1) a device for providing zeotropic refrigerants; and
   (2) a refrigeration machine.

PN CN110967465-A; CN212341201-U
TI Multifunctional control device useful for mudstone engineering strength deterioration mechanism, has multifunctional cylindrical device main body to control air temperature, humidity and chemical gas content, and adjust air composition.
AU LIU Y
   KANG H
   SI L
   JIAO J
AE TIANDI SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHLY-C)
GA 202028877B
AB    NOVELTY - Multifunctional control device comprises a multifunctional cylindrical device main body (1), a reaction base (2), a first layer control plate (3), a second layer control plate (4), a temperature control storage chamber (5), a mudstone sample placing platform and a sealing layer. The bottom of the reaction base is sealed. The reaction base is used to contain water or solution for producing chemical gas. The first layer control plate and second layer control plate is formed with multiple flow hole. The top of multifunctional device body is provided with a sealing layer comprising a sealing layer through-hole. The multifunctional device main body is used to assist controlling air temperature, humidity and chemical gas content, and adjust air composition. The chemical gas comprises sulfur dioxide produced by sodium sulfite and sulfuric acid, carbon dioxide produced by calcium carbonate and sulfuric acid, or nitrogen oxide prepared by reacting copper wire with concentrated nitric acid.
   USE - The multifunctional control device is useful for mudstone engineering strength deterioration mechanism.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has simple structure, is convenient to operate, can approximately simulate the atmospheric system through the combination of different temperature, humidity and chemical gas concentration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the multifunctional control device.

   Reaction base (2)

PT P
PN CN110934932-A
TI Plant extraction liquid injection used for preparing medicine for treating blood cancer and granular cell leukemia, comprises mongolian dandelion herb, radix notoginseng and plantain extract.
AU ZHAO X
AE ZHAO X (ZHAO-Individual)
GA 202026488G
AB    NOVELTY - Plant extraction liquid injection comprises 21-35 pts. wt. mongolian dandelion herb, 16-29 pts. wt. radix notoginseng and 8-12 pts. wt. plantain extract.
   USE - The plant extraction liquid injection is useful for preparing medicine for treating blood cancer (claimed) and granular cell leukemia.
   ADVANTAGE - The injection invention is made of pure Chinese medicine, safe and reliable, and has no toxic and side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the injection, involving (a) separately weighing mongolian dandelion herb and radix notoginseng, washing, pulverizing using a physical crusher, sieving using a 100 mesh sieve, irradiating under sunlight for 4-6 hours, and irradiating under UV rays for 0.5-1.5 hours to obtain a mongolian dandelion herb powder and radix notoginseng powder, (b) separately adding the mongolian dandelion herb powder and radix notoginseng powder to an extraction equipment, and adopting a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction method to obtain a mongolian dandelion herb extract and radix notoginseng extract, (c) dividing the plantain extract into two portion, and mixing with the mongolian dandelion herb extract and radix notoginseng extract to obtain a mixed liquid (A) and (B), (d) combining the mixed liquid (A) and (B), adding them to a closed container, pressurizing to 16-21 Mpa, and ultrasonically treating at 67-81 degrees C to obtain a semi-finished liquid, and (e) filtering the semi-finished liquid at 82-96 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The injection comprises 28 pts. wt. mongolian dandelion herb, 23 pts. wt. radix notoginseng and 10 pts. wt. plantain extract. Preferred Conditions: In step (b), the extraction pressure is 7 MPa, and the temperature is constant at 30 degrees C. The density of the mongolian dandelion herb extract and radix notoginseng is 0.448 cg/cm3. In step (d), the ultrasonic frequency is 800 Hz.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

AB    NOVELTY - The plant cultivation device (100) comprises a                light source unit (120) that includes a first light                source which emits stimulation light in an                ultraviolet (UV) region toward a plant and a second                light source which emits background light toward                the plant. The background light has a different                peak wavelength than a peak of the stimulation                light. The second light source emits the background                light during a light period and stops emission of                the background light during a dark period. The                first light source emits the stimulation light for                a predetermined period of time during the light                period, and a cumulative amount of the stimulation                light emitted from the first light source is 0.036                Joules or more per day.
   USE - Plant cultivation device for culturing                plants.

The cultivation device increases the content of                fragrant materials in the herb by increasing                production of the secondary metabolites of the                herb.

A venting gases pathway is                extended from the cannula or the surgical cavity to                the venting component. PN CN110812410-A
TI Topical essential oil composition for treating alopecia, and promoting hair growth, comprises cacumen platycladi essential oil, Gleditsia sinensis essential oil, Zanthoxylum bungeanum essential oil, and radix salviae essential oil.
AU MIAO M
   PENG M
   TIAN S
AE UNIV HENAN CHINESE MEDICINE (HECO-C)
GA 202018625H
AB    NOVELTY - Topical essential oil composition comprises 4-6 mL cacumen platycladi essential oil, 4-6 mL Gleditsia sinensis essential oil, 4-6 mL Zanthoxylum bungeanum essential oil, 4-6 mL radix salviae miltiorrhizae essential oil, 4-6 mL rose essential oil, and 2.5-3.5 g borneol, obtained by dissolving and uniformly mixing the components. The cacumen platycladi essential oil is prepared by crushing cacumen platycladi into 10-20 meshes, sieving coarse powder, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, where the weight-volume ratio of liquid to material is 1:10-20. The weight-volume ratio refers to solids in g, liquids in mL, extraction temperature of 42-50 degrees C, and extraction pressure of 20-25 MPa. The separation temperature is 50-60 degrees C, separation pressure is 4.5-5.5 MPa, and extraction time is 1.5-2.5 hours. The preparation methods of the rest other essential oils are all the same as those of cacumen platycladi essential oil.
   USE - Topical essential oil composition for treating alopecia, and promoting hair growth (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The topical essential oil composition is rich in raw materials, simple in preparation method, easy to produce, convenient to use, and good in effect, and can be effectively used for treating hair loss and promoting hair growth, is safe, and the curative effect is stable and reliable with economic and social benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Compositions: Topical essential oil composition comprises 5 mL cacumen platycladi essential oil, 5 mL Gleditsia sinensis essential oil, 5 mL Zanthoxylum bungeanum essential oil, 5 mL radix salviae miltiorrhizae essential oil, 5 mL rose essential oil, and 3 g borneol.
   ACTIVITY - Endocrine-Gen.

PN WO2020021583-A1
TI Dual energy core system for micro-cogeneration systems, has polymer electrolyte membrane (PEM) based fuel cell that comes into operation when demand for electric energy exceeds availability provided by solid oxide fuel cell (SOFC) apparatus.
AU PICCENNA N
   AL ABBAWI S H Y
AE PICCENNA N (PICC-Individual)
   AL ABBAWI S H Y (ABBA-Individual)
GA 202009570C
AB    NOVELTY - The dual energy core system has double co-generation system that uses natural gas to derive the hydrogen contained in it and to produce electricity and heat through the use of fuel cells. The fuel cell based on the SOFC technology operates continuously. The fuel cell based on PEM technology comes into operation when the demand for thermal and electric energy exceeds availability provided by SOFC apparatus and satisfy demands of users. The entire system is governed and controlled by programmable electronic devices connected through an internet connection to control and maintenance unit.
   USE - Dual energy core system for micro-cogeneration systems.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy conversion efficiency of more than 90 percentage is achieved, so as to save energy consumption and the health of citizens. The emissions of nitrogen oxides, sulfur oxides, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide deriving from the production of centralized electricity and thermal energy for use through the combustion of natural gas is reduced. The emissions of fine particles generated by the combustion of natural gas of fossil origin are reduced to values close to zero. The PEM cell works at a temperature close to 80 degrees C and, being of a polymeric nature, is not affected by the switch-on and switch-off phases, reaching the operating condition within a few seconds.

PN CN110484036-A
TI Processing technique of stainless steel pipe involves using stainless steel plate for plasma cutting, forming mold, welding, performing heat treatment, pickling and processing, and performing pipe fitting detection and coating protection.
AU KONG J
   KONG Z
   ZHANG X
AE WENZHOU FUTIAN STAINLESS STEEL TUBE CO (WENZ-Non-standard)
GA 2019A0393R
AB    NOVELTY - Processing technique of freezing stainless steel pipe involves using stainless steel plate for plasma cutting, heating cut stainless steel plate at 160-220 degrees C and placing in reflection furnace to be pressed to form mold. Welding is performed using carbon dioxide gas shielded welding at 260-370 degrees C. Heating, heat preservation and cooling are performed to eliminate work hardening, residual stress and metal deformation defects generated during forming process at not less than 723 degrees C. Surface of pipe fittings is pickled using sulfuric acid solution at 10-30 degrees C for 15-25 minutes and using special machine to complete welding of pipe fittings tolerance processing of ends and structural dimensions. Shape, size and surface quality of completed fittings are inspected. Coating protection is applied to surface of stainless steel pipe, coating includes first pressure-resistant coating, second antifreeze coating, third thermal insulation coating, and fourth antirust coating and thickness of layer is 0.1-0.25 mm.
   USE - Method for processing stainless steel pipe.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts advanced mechanical equipment, and enables to prepare stainless steel pipe that has reasonable structure, dimensional accuracy of up to 3-5 level, surface roughness of as low as 10-1.25 microns, good compatibility between the components of the coating of the pipe wall, greatly improved compressive strength, frost resistance and rust resistance of the pipe fittings.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first compression coating comprises 8-10 pts. wt. polytetrafluoroethylene, 8-12 pts. wt. molybdenum disulfide, 2- 4 pts. wt. methyl isobutyl ketone, 1-4 pts. wt. xylene, 20-25 pts. wt. ethanol, 21-23 pts. wt. phenol resin, and 4-6 pts. wt. benzene solvent. The benzene-based solvent is methyl amyl ketone solvent. The second antifreeze coating comprises 20-25 pts. wt. polyurethane resin, 10-18 pts. wt. epoxy resin, 16-20 pts. wt. polysiloxane, 10-12 pts. wt. modified starch, 1-3 pts. wt. acrylate, 2-3 pts. wt. ethyl acetate, 1-3 pts. wt. graphene, and 4-6 pts. wt. anti-freezing agent. Preferred Conditions: The graphene is selected from graphene microchips. The anti-freezing agent is mixture of inorganic anti-freezing agent and organic anti-freezing agent in mass ratio of 2:1. The inorganic anti-freezing agent is calcium chloride, and the organic anti-freezing agent is glycerol. The composition of the third heat-insulating coating component is 30-40 wt.% titanium dioxide, 22.5-27.5 wt.% silica and 37.5-42.5 wt.% silica sol. The fourth anti-corrosive coating comprises 0.5-2 pts. wt. inorganic titanium source, 0.5-2 pts. wt. citric acid, 1-3 pts. wt. inorganic calcium source, 0.5-2 pts. wt. curing agent, 0-0.2 pts. wt. dispersing agent, and 0-0.2 pts. wt. binding agent. The curing agent in the anti-corrosion coating is polyamide, and the bonding agent is bisphenol A epoxy resin.

PT P
PN CN110368904-A
TI Preparing solid porous gas adsorption material involves adding organic amine cross-linking agent, mixing with solvent to form dispersion, and mixing dispersion under low temperature freezing, under freezing conditions.
AU LI Y
AE ZHEJIANG YUEWEI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201990357V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing solid porous gas adsorption material involves adding the organic amine cross-linking agent, mixing with a solvent to form a dispersion, mixing the dispersion under low temperature freezing, under freezing conditions, the cross-linkable functional group of amino and crosslinking agent in organic amine in cross linking reaction to form ice gel has cross-linked network, mixing the ice gel which is dried to obtain the solid-state porous foam material, where organic amine includes one or more of amine-containing polymer, and polyamine.
   USE - Method for preparing solid porous gas adsorbing material can be widely used for absorbing one or more of carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur oxides, nitrogen oxides, formaldehyde steam, the gas adsorbing material can be used for realizing efficient chemical adsorption and physical adsorption of the gas from air, industrial exhaust gas, automobile exhaust or natural gas; after the gas adsorbing material to adsorb gas, can be regenerated by heating, including a steam heating, electric heating or solar heating (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare solid porous gas adsorption material has simple preparation process, low device requirement, low production cost, is safe and environment-friendly, is wide in application range, does not need any other carrier ,amino high loading quantity, high adsorption capacity, high absorbing efficiency, regeneration, the amine is not volatile, low loss, has good thermal stability, and good circulation stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyamine is one or more of pentaethylenehexamine, tetraethylene pentamine, triethylene tetramine, diethylenetriamine, six vinyl seven amine, tri (2-amino-ethyl) amine, tris 2-(methylamino)-ethyl amine, 2-(aminomethyl)-2, methyl-1, 3-diamine, 1, 4, 7,tri-azepinecyclononane, nano-particle diamine, aminosilane, and amino-modified. The diamine is preferably ethylene diamine, propylene diamine, butanediamine, pentamethylene diamine, hexamethylene diamine, piperazine, the aminosilane is preferably (3-aminopropyl) trimethoxy silane, (3-aminopropyl)triethoxysilane, N-(2)-3-aminoethylaminopropyl trimethoxy silane, N-(2)-3-aminoethyl aminopropyl triethoxy silane, phenyltrimethoxy silane, and ethylene tri-amino propyltrimethoxy silane. The amine-containing polymer is one or more of polyvinyl amine, polyethylene imine, polypropylene imine, polyamide-amine, poly(allylamine), and chitosan. The cross-linking agent is a compound capable of reacting with amino or hydroxyl to form cross-links, comprising one or more of a polyhydric epoxy compound, monobasic aldehyde, polyatomic aldehyde, polyamide polyamine epichlorohydrin, polybasic acid, carbodiimide, and polyhydric isocyanate, where the polyol epoxy is preferably epichlorohydrin, glycerol triglycidyl ether, trimethylolpropane triglycidyl ether, 1, 4-butanediol diglycidyl ether, 1, 6,-hexanediol diglycidyl ether, diglycidyl ether,ethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, propylene glycoldiglycidyl ether, polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, polypropylene glycol diglycidyl ether, 1, 2, 7, 8, epoxy octane, bisphenol A diglycidyl ether, N, N, N', N'- epoxy-propyl-4, 4'-diaminodiphenyl methane and sorbitol polyglycidyl ether. The mono-aldehyde is preferably formaldehyde, acetaldehyde and propionaldehyde, the polyatomicaldehyde is glyoxal, succinaldehyde and glutaraldehyde, the carbodiimide is preferably 1-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)-3 carbodiimide, ethyl N, N' diisopropyl carbodiimide. The solvent is one or more of water, ethanol, methanol, acetone, isopropyl alcohol, butylalcohol, isobutyl alcohol, tert-butyl alcohol, 2, butanol, ethyl acetate, tetrahydrofuran, preferably water or ethanol. Preferred Conditions: The content of the organic amine in the dispersion is 0.1-50 wt.%, and the content of the crosslinking agent is 0.1-10 wt.%, the content of the solvent is 40-99 wt.% preferably, the content of the organic amine is 2-20 wt.%, the content of the crosslinking agent is 0.4-5 wt.%, and the content of the solvent is 75-95 wt.%. The dosage ratio is from 20:1-1:10, preferably, the ratio is from 10:1-1:1, more preferably, the ratio is from 5:1-2:1. The gas adsorbing material prepared by the method has a shape of a block, a powder, a film or a fiber. The gas adsorbent prepared has a density of 0.01-0.6 g/cm-3 and a porosity of 40-99%, the density of the gas adsorbent material is 0.05-0.5g/cm-3, and the porosity is 50-95%. The amino group content is 10-20mol/kg.

PN DE102018109104-A1
TI Gas treatment plant useful for separation of water            vapor from gas mixture, such as damp fumes of food            industry and laundries, comprises membrane module with            microporous or mesoporous separating membrane, which is            permeable to water vapor.
AU WOHLRAB S
   STOHR M
   WOTZKA A
   ERICH E
   BOBACK R
AE UNIV ROSTOCK LEIBNIZ-INST KATALYSE EV (UYRS-C)
GA 2019862857
AB    NOVELTY - Gas treatment plant comprises a membrane                module with a microporous or mesoporous separating                membrane, which is permeable to water vapor.
   USE - The plant is useful for separation of water                vapor from a gas mixture (claimed), such as damp                fumes of food industry and laundries, heat recovery                in hot drying processes in building materials                industry or ceramic industry, steam stripping in                chemical industry or water vapor regeneration of                activated carbon. The plant is useful for biogas                upgrading plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant adopts membrane separation processes                which ensure separation of water vapor from                comparable gas streams at a high temperature level,                has stable membrane for the components of the gas                mixture, has sufficient thermal stability,                maintains high temperature in the water vapor                obtained as permeate in order to enable heat                recovery or further use of the hot water vapor,                overcomes condenser cooler systems and improves                energy efficiency of industrial plants and purity                of carbon dioxide, separates heat carrier water                vapor from the off gas at the highest possible                temperature and overcomes systematic limitation of                capacitor, correspondingly constructs                amine-containing water vapor released under                pressure, so that a driving force is already                present for technically meaningful and economical                membrane operation, reduces total energy                consumption by using the hot water vapor and                removes impurities, so that the membrane is not                damaged.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The separation                  membrane is a zeolite membrane, a silica membrane                  (both preferred), a carbon membrane, a metal                  oxide-based membrane, a metal carbide-based                  membrane, a polymer, a metal nitride-based                  membrane or an inorganic separation membrane. The                  zeolite membrane is a MFI-type zeolite membrane.                  The separation membrane is selectively permeable                  to water vapor. The plant has a membrane module                  downstream of a heat recovery, in which the                  resulting as permeate in the membrane separation                  stage water vapor can be introduced. The gas                  treatment plant comprises a amine scrubbing                  module comprising a desorber for recovering an                  amine scrubbing, where the plant is designed such                  that it forms as permeate in the membrane module                  accumulating water vapor in a unit for generating                  of steam for the desorber of the amine wash can                  initiate. The desorber comprises a cascaded                  desorber assembly with decreasing pressure                  levels.

AU KAKUWA T
   ZHANG J
AE PANASONIC IP MANAGEMENT CO LTD (MATU-C)
GA 201983431G
AB    NOVELTY - The SOFC system (100) has a modifier (6) which is provided for reforming raw materials to generate reformed gas. A cell stack (4) generates power using the reformed gas and power generation air. A material supply device (10) is provided for supplying the raw material to the modifier. A water supply machine (12) is provided for supplying reforming water to the modifier. An air supply device (11) is provided for supplying the air for power generation to the cell stack. The water supply machine starts supplying the reforming water to the modifier, after igniting off-gas by an ignition device (8). The air supply device is controlled to increase the supply amount of the power generation air stepwise, while controlling the supply amount of the reforming water stepwise after the reforming water is started to be supplied by the water supply machine.
   USE - High-temperature operating fuel cell system e.g. SOFC system and molten carbon dioxide fuel cell system (MCFC) in power generation unit.

PN CN109942111-A
TI Treating potassium tetracyanonickelate in electroplating wastewater, by introducing ozone, oxidizing potassium tetracyanonickelate, adjusting pH, adding divalent iron salt, drying residues, screening powder and soaking magnetic substance.
AU ZHOU W
   LIU A
   LIAO K
   CHEN X
AE HUNAN TECHNOLOGY INST (HNTI-C)
GA 2019584486
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating potassium tetracyanonickelate in electroplating wastewater, involves (i) introducing ozone into the electroplating wastewater and oxidizing potassium tetracyanonickelate, so as to realize cleavage and oxidation of total cyanogens, (ii) adjusting the pH value of the wastewater to be alkaline and combining nickel with hydroxyl group to generate a black precipitate nickel(II) hydroxide, (iii) adding divalent iron salt to the wastewater and aerating, (iv) adjusting the reaction temperature and the pH value of the wastewater, (v) after reaching the set reaction time, filtering, drying the filter residues to obtain black particles, weighing and grinding the black particles into a powder, and screening the powder into magnetic and non-magnetic substances using a magnet, (vi) soaking the magnetic substance in an acid solution with the pH of 1 for 24 hours, washing with distilled water and drying, and (vii) recycling the magnetic substance.
   USE - The method is useful for treating potassium tetracyanonickelate in the electroplating wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes the oxidation of most of the cyanogen in potassium tetracyanonickelate to nitrogen and carbon dioxide by introducing ozone, and the nickel ion is precipitated, and enables heavy metal ions and ferric salts in the wastewater to generate stable ferrite coprecipitate by virtue of a ferrite method, so as to achieve the purpose of removing heavy metal ions from the wastewater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method involves adding trivalent iron ion to step (i), where the molar ratio of the trivalent iron ion to the nickel ion is (2:3)-(1:1). Preferred Component: The ferrous salt in step (iii) is iron(II) sulfate. Preferred Conditions: The ozone concentration in step (i) is 2-4 mg/l. The pH value in step (ii) is 11-12. The amount of iron(II) sulfate added is 2-4 times the amount of the nickel ion in the wastewater. The aeration time in step (iii) is 40-80 minutes. In step (iv), the pH of the wastewater is adjusted to 9-11. The reaction temperature for adjusting the wastewater in step (iv) is 50-80 degrees C. The set reaction time in step (v) is 45-90 minutes.

PN WO2019066187-A1; KR2019051636-A; ID201907393-A; KR2117864-B1; VN71198-A
TI Producing filter medium used for filtering liquid            in hydrocarbons generated in chemical plant comprises            preparing fibrous filter medium, exposing to plasma to            activate surface with hydroxy group, and coating e.g.            amine-based compound.
AU LEE S J
   CHOI Y T
   HAN D K
   JEE K Y
   HAN D G
AE KOREA INST IND TECHNOLOGY (KTEC-C)
GA 2019330844
AB    NOVELTY - Producing filter medium comprises either: (a)                preparing a fibrous filter medium, (b) exposing the                fibrous filter medium to plasma to activate surface                of fibrous filter medium with hydroxy group, (c)                coating amine-based compound on surface of the                fibrous filter medium activated with the hydroxy                group to form an amine coating layer, and (d)                coating a fibrous filter material activated by the                hydroxy group with fluorine silane-based water                repellent agent to prepare filter material coated                with super water-repellent layer; or (a1) exposing                the fibrous filter medium to plasma to activate the                fibrous filter medium surface with hydroxy group,                (b1) coating fibrous filter medium activated with                hydroxy group with fluorine silane-based water                repellent agent to form super water-repellent                layer, and (c1) coating the super-water-repellent                layer with epoxy polymer to form oil-resistant                layer.
   USE - The filter medium is useful for filtering                liquid in hydrocarbons generated in chemical                plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical. The filter medium                has large contact angle, hydrophobic and oil                resistance, can chemically absorb carbon dioxide,                and increases oil resistance and flocculation                effect of liquid phase.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                filter medium comprising a fibrous filter medium, a                super water repellent layer provided on the fibrous                filter medium, and an oil-repellent layer provided                on the super water repellent layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine-based                  compound includes alkyldiamine,                  triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine                  and/or polyethyleneimine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The step (b) comprises                  (b1) introducing gas into the fibrous filter                  medium surface to reduce pressure, and (b2)                  generating a plasma to activate the surface of                  the fibrous filter medium with hydroxy group. The                  step (b) or (a1) is carried out at voltage of                  10-500 W for 0.1-300 seconds under argon and                  oxygen atmosphere. The step (d) is carried out by                  condensation reaction between hydroxy group and                  fluorine silane-based water repellent agent.                  Preferred Components: The fibrous filter material                  includes borosilicate glass fibers,                  polypropylene, cellulose diacetate, poly(ethylene                  terephthalate), nylon, polyvinyl chloride,                  modacrylic and/or acrylic. The fluorine                  silane-based water repellent agent includes                  fluoroalkylsilane,                  (tridecafluoro-1,1,2,2-tetrahydrooctyl)trichlorosilane,                  heptadecafluoro-1,1,2,2-tetrahydrodecyl-tridecafluoro-1,1,2,2-tetrahydrooctyl                  trimethoxysilane, or                  heptadecafluoro-1,1,2,2-tetrahydrodecyl-tridecafluoro-1,1,2,2-tetrahydrooctyl                  triethoxysilane. The epoxy polymer includes                  diglycidylethylene glycol, sorbitol glycidyl                  ether and/or (epoxy propoxy)propanediol. The                  filter medium comprises an anchoring group which                  is formed by polymerization reaction between                  fibrous medium and super water-repellent layer.                  The oil-repellent layer comprises                  diglycidylethylene glycol, sorbitol glycidyl                  ether and/or (epoxy propoxy)propanediol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109433032-A
TI Preparing zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 film            useful e.g. for separating gas, comprises e.g. applying            zinc oxide sol onto inner portion of treated tubular            carrier, wrapping, placing in film forming solution,            crystallizing, and cooling.
AU LI L
   LI J
AE UNIV HUANGSHAN (UNHS-C)
GA 201924540E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing zeolitic imidazolate framework-8                (ZIF-8) film comprises (i) cutting tubular carrier                into short tubes of 50 mm, and subjecting the                carrier short tube to multiple ultrasonic bath                treatments; (ii) taking zinc acetate, ethanolamine                and ethylene glycol methyl ether at a mass ratio of                1:2:16, firstly adding zinc acetate into ethylene                glycol methyl ether, stirring at 50-90 degrees C                for 10-50 minutes in water bath, dropping                ethanolamine until zinc acetate is completely                dissolved and stirring for 10-50 minutes to obtain                a zinc oxide sol, taking is carried out at the zinc                oxide sol and stirring at room temperature for                10-30 hours, and storing at dry shady place; (iii)                applying the zinc oxide sol onto the inner portion                of the treated tubular carrier obtained in step (i)                by a sol-lifting method; and (iv) wrapping the                tubular carrier with a polytetrafluoroethylene tape                and placing vertically in a film forming solution,                crystallizing at 80-180 degrees C for 2-10 hours,                and cooling at room temperature.
   USE - The film is useful for separating gas i.e.                carbon dioxide/dinitrogen separation and                hydrogen/dinitrogen separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The film has good compactness and continuity,                stable skeleton, and good gas separation effect                with less impurities.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier is a alpha                  - aluminum oxide ceramic tube. The molar ratio of                  film forming liquid comprises 1 (zinc                  chloride):0.65Hmin:1 (sodium formate):450                  (methanol). Preferred Method: The method                  comprises soaking the carrier short tube in 0.1 M                  nitric acid solution for 10-20 minutes after                  ultrasonically treating, taking out and soaking                  in 0.1 M sodium hydroxide solution for 10-20                  minutes. The method comprises washing the carrier                  with alkali and placing into deionized water for                  ultrasonically treating again, taking out and                  placing into 50-100 degrees C oven for drying,                  placing into a muffle furnace and calcining at                  300-600 degrees C for 2 hours, and cooling at                  room temperature. The coating of zinc oxide sol                  onto the inner portion of the tubular carrier is                  carried out by firstly connecting the tubular                  carrier and an injector with a                  polytetrafluoroethylene pipe, absorbing zinc                  oxide sol through the injector and statically                  placing for 10 seconds, rapidly pushing out the                  zinc sol before the zinc sol passes through the                  outer portion of the tubular carrier, rotating                  the carrier, coating uniformly the zinc oxide sol                  onto the carrier, repeating the operation after 5                  minutes for lifting the primary zinc oxide sol,                  placing in a constant temperature drying box at                  100 degrees C for 1 hour, taking out the tubular                  carrier and cooling at room temperature,                  repeating the pulling operation again, placing                  into a muffle furnace, heating to 400 degrees C                  with a heating rate of 1 degrees C/minute, and                  calcining at 400 degrees C for 2 hours. The                  method comprises placing the carrier short tube                  into methanol solution of 0.1-1 MHmin before                  placing into the film forming solution,                  activating at 50 degrees C for 0.5 hours and                  naturally drying. The method comprises naturally                  cooling the carrier short tube at room                  temperature after being crystallized in the film                  forming solution. The method comprises cooling                  the carrier short tube at room temperature,                  washing using ethanol, and drying at room                  temperature for 24 hours.
